text: worst morning service ever. rude! my drink is never correct and i have to end up going inside to get the correct one i paid for. the drive through line takes foreverrr. won't be back i'll drive to carmel to get my fix now

this is an ok starbucks, but the job got done. depending when you come parking can get hectic because the lot is small. sometimes it's hard to find a table on the weekends. however, my order came out good, despite them never calling my name to tell me my sandwich was ready so it was kind of cold by the time i ate it. otherwise, the staff seemed pretty friendly. despite it being late in the morning i felt like there was still a decent amount of food items left as some starbucks run out quickly. i'll probably be back.

slowly but surely i'm going back into a normal routine. and part of that normal routine sometimes involves a drive thru for coffee instead of a scale, grinder, and french press. this starbucks location has been closed for about a month due to the pandemic and prior to this it was probably my favorite local 'bucks. so i'm glad to have made a return today. the baristas are generally very friendly and always very efficient. they do a great job keeping the drive thru moving quickly. it's particularly important that this drive thru moves fast because this parking lot is a nightmare. my coffee order was placed and received in a total of 1.5 minutes. i mean...that's impressive. the cafe is very small and so this is an in-n-out location primarily. i'm glad to see the baristas are all wearing masks right now and that they have strict procedures to maintain social distancing compliance. also super cool to find that starbucks has extended redemption period for birthday rewards! i got to use my birthday reward to treat my husband to a ~ridiculous~ ~indulgent~ ~dessert coffee~ (iced venti caramel macchiato) weeks after my bday. that's right, babe, you just got callllllled out. **please note my husband is still a manly dude that mostly drinks black coffee. he appreciates the subtle nuances in beans. he is a particular connoisseur of beans from latin american countries and his favorite way to enjoy coffee is in an aero press. but yes, he digs an iced caramel macchiato sometimes. it's fun to feel safe going back to starbucks again!
had to have my daily starbucks! it's not for luxury it's a necessity!! love this store since it's so close to my house. clean store, yummy drinks & a nice crews! until next time,...probably tomorrow!
they used to be so great, now the cafe is consistently closed, and mobile order is usually unavailable. expect long wait times no matter what time of the day you come

i visit this starbucks a couple times a week--i only use this one because of convenience on my way to work. it is consistently the slowest starbucks i have ever visited. it often takes 10 minutes or more in the drive through. they are in absolutely no rush. in addition, half the time i order they have run out of whatever i'm ordering. if you are crunched for time, you may want to drive out of your way to visit another--you'll probably save time in the long run.

one of my favorite starbucks around. lots of parking, lots of places to sit (especially outside) and love the leather chairs and fireplace inside! the baristas are always friendly, shockingly unlike some other starbucks. always clean too!
i like this starbucks because it's generally less busy than the others in the area. i normally don't have a problem getting a table and the wi-fi usually works.

i love starbucks. loyal customer since their first startups. however, i am consistently disappointed with this location. they are extremely understaffed. this morning, it took a total of 20 minutes, to order and receive my coffee. i am aware i can do mobile order for quicker turnaround. however, it baffles me they don't hire more staff to aide in expediting customers wait times. the staff behind the counter is doing the best they can, overwhelmed and swamped with orders, even snapping at customers. it's a fantastic location, which draws many customers, seemingly at all hours of the day. hire more staff to accommodate the demand. otherwise be prepared to wait in packed room with college kids on laptops, and irritated job goers patiently waiting for their morning buzz.
great location. friendly people. they remember me and my family. even what drinks we like. that's awesome.

i felt like this was a starbucks claim at a trailer park! very entertaining staff! if you want a show i'd recommend going in and watching the tattooed toothless baristas trying to figure out how to brew coffee. don't order from here they don't know anything!

everyone is complaining about the parking. me too. the issue is not just that there's only a tiny lot, but that the surrounding streets all have parking bans or permit parking only, so there's nowhere to go for overflow parking. the parking lot is too tiny to reasonably fill this coffee shop, assuming about 2 people to a car. there's ample seating but barely any parking. there isn't even any metered parking nearby. but to add insult to injury, this place isn't exactly easily accessible by any form of transportation. i'm a walker, and a septa user. this location is along a big street with fast traffic, and the lot is between the sidewalk and the door...not friendly to walkers. it's very near the septa station but the walk from the station to the starbucks isn't a friendly one. other than that, this place is decent. nice employees, decent decor. but seriously...i'd like to see them do something about making this location more friendly to people trying to actually get to it, whether by car or by foot.

i love this starbucks. it is great for studying and all of the baristas are really friendly and kind. i love coming here to work on homework and anything else i need to do. i would definitely recommend this starbucks to anyone in the area

this is the worst starbucks. the last two wines i have been here they have screwed up my order. last time they didn't put syrup in my frap and this time they gave me a mocha frap instead of a salted caramel. not again! wasted 11 dollars on something i didn't like or want!!!!

i absolutely hate writing bad reviews. i feel like i always give an establishment multiple chances before i would ever resort to this. but avoid this location at all costs. there clearly is a problem at this location considering the only thing they are consistent about is being understaffed. i feel bad for the managers and the baristas because i'm sure the turnover rate at this location is high and probably the problem is lying somewhere else. i don't know, but it would be really nice to actually get decent service at my local starbucks. today the line was wrapped around the building per usual and i wanted to go inside to buy a few cups for gifts. that didn't happen because they had closed the establishment due to being understaffed. speedway starbucks .... please do better!
this starbucks location is very slow compared to all other locations i've been to. they need to have a little more urgency because their drive thru lines get insanely backed up.
nice location in an affluent shopping center that is always crowded on weekends. clean and comfortable, medium sized with seating and a retail area. (not a teeny one and not huge).
great place to work! t he barista are always nice and provide excellent service. makes you feel at home. love this place!!
coffe is good but the black-haired girl behind the counter is a total troll. seems friendly enough with her co-workers but rude to the customers. 01/01/2015 @ 1745

for as busy as these folks are they have great poise & attitude while they grind and serve away to tourists, locals, employees and outside panhandlers sure its sbux and the rep that comes with it but philz, sightglass or blue bottle is over 200 miles away. if hub was closer for me there'd be one less review. the weekends are a mess so come earlier or later the inside of this smaller franchised space is gorgeous with a tip of the cap to the construction mgmt team. i like the pictures of their humble beginnings storefront on pike place meets virginia street and the ceiling resembles the fox theater in oakland a bit daily drip seems to have blonde, dragon and pike in the rotation as one is also able to get their la boulange fix
good central west county location. would like them to remain open past 8:30pm. 'outdoor seating' means 2 benches out front.

i have to say i was ignored never looked at or even made eye contact with. made an order, and listened to a man barista talk about his makeup and outfit for the weekend to his coworker on their little headsets while he completely messed up my order of iced coffee...enough said, wasn't a difficult order if you get my drift. i don't mind that he ignored me but i do mind thats the reason he messed up my order.
usually starbucks is pretty good, but this one was awful. they messed up my order twice, and i never get anything complicated just a white mocha. i'm very disappointed
this place is just horrible i can't think of a reason why it's so big coffee is disgusting horrible service never again

just as a previous poster mentioned, the wait time is long. we waited in the drive through for approximately 14 minutes to get two coffees. when we got our drinks, both were done incorrectly and one drink had more mocha on the outside of the than the inside. you could see fingerprints on the cup from the mocha mess. i'm really blown away that we were given this cup with the mess all over it. we had to ask for napkins and then another cup to put over top so we wouldn't get the mess everywhere. ultimately, we threw it away because it tasted terrible. i think the staff may be new or maybe they're not thoroughly trained. in any event, horrible experience. i don't recommend this starbucks.
the atomosphere is nice and the music is good for studying. it can get loud sometimes when a large group of people come in to study. however, the internet is terribly terribly slow.

7 am saturday, relatively quiet, i ordered. triple shot grande cappuccino so it would taste like coffee. it tasted like 100% milk. i asked for a new one. she said sure and made me wait for 7 other people and when i spoke up she said, oh someone else must have taken it. i was standing there the whole time, so no, that's not it. this starbucks is totally hit or miss on quality. i have had too many misses. i will not return.

i came for a strawberry acai with coconut milk ( the pink drink) in the morning and it was extremely busy. but when i got my drink, i realized i was charged wrong and my ticket didn't include the coconut milk, so they had to remake it. it wasn't a huge problem for me, but i'm glad the staff was still nice and didn't get an attitude about it.
register lady said they don't do french presses any more. at least the pour over was hot. everyone knows the french press is tastier!

the new barista, a young african american woman, was very pleasant, great smile and very personable. however, this place always looks like it could use a good cleaning. before we could sit at the large bar height table i had to ask for a wet towel to clean off the spilled coffees and food crumbs. i don't mind this but i have a problem with it when the baristas are standing around talking so very loudly that their voices are overpowering all the other customers in the store. they could be attending cleaning opportunities.
very large plenty of study space friendly attentive staff very very very cold staff said they have no control over the heat. everyone had their winter coats on. i had to leave it was so cold.

i've been to probably over 100 different starbucks. this particular starbucks is the most friendly and consistent i've been to. the other day they gave me my order for free for a christmas present. that's great customer service. it's located in the hyatt so it's quite busy during conventions, but the speed of service is great. they always have enough staff, and they are all friendly.

coffee coffee coffee... we need coffee. so i might be a little hyper for this review as i just had a coffee with three shots of espresso in it but i am i high energy person with a high energy job and a type a+ personality so coffee is like mothers milk and this place has that milk for me. (insert breath) so i came here and while i am usually on the go and running to work my 12+ hour days with a coffee float on my energy drinks, this place helped me s-l-o-w down. ok. this is cool like most starbucks but this one is a bit more comfortable than most. it has a big outdoor seating area. it is in a quiet and upscale community. there are also a bunch of nice shops around it which adds some nice ambiance. it is just a nice place to chill and relax unless you like espresso like me and then it is a great place to recharge.

i'm giving this starbucks a two star only because the set up and slower staff make the line consistently long. they make decent starbucks drinks, but if there's a line, it will take you a bit longer to get your drink. if you're rushing to get on a flight, do not go here unless there's no line.

what a horribly misplaced edition to the neighborhood. i even hate looking at it when i'm driving by. this is not a great new addition to the neighborhood. it's commercial greed at it's finest and takes away from local businesses who are intricately involved in the immediate community. their coffee tastes like burnt toast, their food comes from a box and is microwaved. what a disgrace.

i liked to stop by this starbucks when i was doing some shopping. some of my favorite starbucks concoctions: - nonfat extra foamy misto with sugar free vanilla - americano with caramel drizzle (the caramel sauce that usually goes on frappuccinos... so rich and gooey!) - coffee light frappuccino - green tea frappuccino a good place to meet up with friends too... yum! i would definitely hit up the local coffee shops as well but have nothing against the ever-reliable starbucks!
in line right now for drive thru. it's going to clear 30 minutes fir a coffee. going to be late to work. crazy.....

while the customer service is up to starbucks standards the drinks are not! in the handful of times i have gone to this location i have never once received the correct beverage. i gave them the benefit of the doubt before but after the last visit, where the barista asked me how many flavor shots were in a venti iced coffee, i will be avoiding this location at all costs.

this location is dirty, the staff is consistently rude, and they put zero effort into preparing your order. i've tried this location several times, but i've given up hope. many more attractive options in the area.

disrespectful employees. yelled and threaten to call the cops on my fiancee because all she was asking if the drink that the barista has made was hers. she asked 3 times and everyone of the employees ignored her. the barista gave her an attitude once he gave her his attention. my fiancee tried to speak with a manager but none of the employees wanted to cooperate to direct her to one. one of the employees claimed that he was the "manager" and refused to give her the district manager information. a scene has already started in front of the many customers that have been or was being served. he ended up giving her the "manager" number which my fiancee called and when she asked who she was speaking to, they hung up on her. the managers number ended up being the store phone number. nothing was resolved. never go here unless you're looking to fight with the disrespectful employees.

i come here regularly before work. i enjoy the setting, even when it gets busy. but, the staff is inconsistent. there's one girl that works on saturday mornings at 5, and every time, she makes me feel like i should leave. (really short hair...) doesn't say "good morning" or "hi" back... won't even look at me. i tried to engage in conversation once with her, "how are you?" and she scolded me for expecting her to put the honey in my coffee. no one ever told me, until that morning, that it was wrong and against health code. i certainly don't mind putting it in myself, but the honey packets aren't out for customers. she went off about how wrong it was to put the barista in that position... i just smiled and said that i would be happy to do it. however, all other baristas put the honey in. in fact, when i asked to put it in myself because of what she said, they laughed and said she's wrong. i would give this place 5 stars, except for the way she affects the environment.

i've been to this starbucks a few times...i'm definitely a starbucks aficionado, lol! david, the barista from latimer is here! there's another barista from walnut st., a friendly bunch! they have ample seating here too which is great, plenty of tables and chairs. i would give 5 stars but i think it's kinda lame that they want to hold onto your id or keys while you borrow the bathroom key, i just think that's kinda dumb. i don't understand why they can't just have a keypad on the door and give you the code like other starbucks do. i don't like having to leave my personal property with a barista. if it weren't for that issue and the line getting backed up, i would have given 5 stars. i will keep coming here occasionally but it's not the closest one to me, so i'll visit the bellevue and latimer more often since they're closer. still, good starbucks!

i went in to get coffee, i was yelled at for not wearing a mask. apparently there is a mandate which is not a law. even someone vaccinated i found out, manager grabs a mask violently from a worker and places it in my face. my hands are shaking at this point, for someone struggling with a medical condition, just because you cannot see it and assume i'm just being difficult. she kept saying you can either do it the easy way or not get served. i don't feel good about this, my hands are still shaking and am having trouble breathing now with how she screamed at me.

cashier seems nice enough, but the young black girl who fixes the drink has an attitude, and made a mess of my drink right in front of me. considering the location, i would think they would have a much friendlier staff.

this is a typical starbucks. nothing special. can't figure out why all the neg reviews but my main gripe is the parking situation. this location is fantastic! on the way to work for a lot of people off a main thoroughfare. and it's great that they put a drive-thru in to make it easier but there are definitely some logistical issues that weren't addressed in planning. the drive-thru isn't long enough to accommodate all the traffic so cars end up backed up onto that main thoroughfare and it can cause a lot of honking and not so happy people. the line moves pretty quickly for the most part but it doesn't take long for it all to turn sour. if even two to three cars end up in the street, it's a commuting nightmare. on the other hand, the parking lot is so small and hard to get in/out of that people inevitably end up parking at one of the nearby local businesses and, i'm sure, making those owners angry. the servers here are fast and friendly. i don't drink coffee so i couldn't tell you about that but they brew a pretty good earl grey.
so rude. not a friendly bunch to start your day with. additionally weird music playing. i would recommend a neighborhood coffee shop.

the incredibly friendly and attentive staff here bring the rating up to three. if it were not for them, i'd give it a one, because this is not a particularly enjoyable starbucks location. st pete, doing its best to be a 'big city', has a decent number of homeless folks in the vicinity of this starbucks, and they are constantly coming in, spreading their belongings out, coughing without covering, etc. pinellas already has a hep a issue, so i'm sorry, but i don't really like homeless people in my chosen eating establishments. the bathroom has a pointless combo lock on it, and i say pointless because as we all know starbucks policy is to no longer ask if someone is a paying customer, so they just give the code out to anyone who asks anyway. that of course has lead to a disgusting bathroom i won't use. next up, the non-homeless expensive condo population of st pete, who are also in close vicinity. that brings in a combo of either yoga pants and 'service animal' dog as an dress accessory, or male with airpods talking about their next awe-inspiring business deal way too loudly. finally, the place is kept ice cold and music loud, probably to encourage people to not stay too long.
nice starbucks in a nice little plaza. great staff, and fast! as a starbucks should be!

we just stopped here (7-15-17) at 7:00 am for our fuel to get us going on a long 14 hour drive ahead of us. i wish i would have gotten the employee names, but hopefully leaving the date and time will help so they can be recognized! all 3 staff on hand were incredibly kind; each individually wished us both a great day and a good morning. the drinks were even better than normal, and the whole establishment including the restrooms were very clean. thanks for the pep in our step, starbucks!

starbucks continues to be the bane of my existence. the thorn in my side. the ultimate example of everything that is wrong with chain restaurants, and the suburbs. while i do enjoy their coffee and willingly pay five dollars a cup for coffee, water, and milk. i would much prefer going to a mom and pop establishment. however, the problem with the suburbs is there is a severe lack in mom and pop coffee shops. but i guess the upside to chain restaurants is that you can always expect the same quality where ever you go. all my ranting aside, this is a good starbucks. they have a nice outdoor patio, my order is always correct, and they have a drive through which is always convenient. be warned, however, this drive through with its proximity to rt. 1 and the turnpike, can get very crowded in the mornings.
probably the friendliest starbucks i have ever been to! i walked out there with a smile on my face. such a pleasant way to start my day!

they are taking covid-19 protocols seriously. they added plexiglass by the cashiers and they are enforcing everyone to wear their mask properly. they opened up about 3 tables recently. their bathrooms are still closed. the staff here is really friendly and they're all hard workers. when they are busy, everyone seemed very focus and organized. for how busy they are, they are very efficient. for the amount of people they have, they get the drinks out quickly. if you come here during the weekdays and if it's around 7am to 8am, i suggest to do a mobile order. order 15 minutes in advance before you arrive, to ensure your drink is done when you get there. sometimes they get slammed and there's usually a really long line in the drive thru. if you weren't aware, you can also add a tip on the app, by viewing your receipt. select the dollar amount and click done. i didn't know this until recently. i think starbucks should ask if you'd like to leave a tip before checking out. this way, it's visible to everyone.

i don't understand why they use online ordering for!!! i ordered 15 min before i arrived, 2-workers, no line! they haven't even started my one drink! i guess this what happens when u have poor management!

i've been here twice, and each time my drink has been wrong, when it's marked correctly on the cup. each time i seem to have someone at the register who also can't find buttons or has a hard time just ringing the order in for one drink. if you are looking for efficiency and accuracy, god forbid you are in a rush, i definitely wouldn't come here. the staff are super nice, they just need to actually do the job correctly and this place would be great. might need to improve training.

they always get my order wrong!! i usually order a hot espresso drink and substitute coconut milk for regular but add whip. somehow the whip never makes it on the drink. sometimes the drink comes out as a frappuccino!! i've ordered both through the app and in the store and it's always wrong. what i love about starbucks is the ability to create your own drink. i hate visiting this location because i have to double check everything. and when you are spending almost $6 for a drink you want it to be right

my friend and i met here for coffee. the cashier was extremely rude. she was cussing as she rung up my friend and snatched my card from me when i was paying. needless to say, she is always rude. that can't be good for business. lets also talk about the guy who took my drink order. it seemed to me like he was hard of hearing. i said "skinny caramel latte" maybe 5 times and he still wrote it wrong on the cup and before i was even telling him additional things for my drink and he gave it to the barista as i was telling him that i wasn't finished. i went over to the barista and told him what i wanted and he was very nice and apologized because he saw what had happened. we couldn't see a manager on duty to tell this to, so i figured a review would do.

i typically come through the drive-thru and have good experiences, always fast and accurate service, but had a great experience at this store today and i had to share! my friend and i are both partners and have been through so many stores (and have a habit of watching and analyzing who's on bar)! the baristas at this store at every single position were on point today and so so kind!! they were operating effortlessly and quickly and were kind and accommodating to everyone in the cafe while also managing the drive thru. my complicated-a** friend even got a drink that he never gets right at other stores, and he said it tasted just like he made it for himself. overall 10/10 experiences, todays morning crewe really made our day and were doing it right. will be returning forever and ever!

this starbucks is very similar to all others but i find the employees a little less friendly here. it may be that most of the patrons are here studying or working so it's a more serious environment. it might be that everyone is just hard at work. i just don't get a warm and fuzzy feeling here at all. people seem a little stoic. my coffees here have been delicious, and the staff is very efficient. i'll be back.

this location is a bit odd because of the design of the building/parking lot. the drive thru is usually very busy and because of the design, the line literally wraps around the building. this also makes it risky to park in the three spots directly in front of the building because you can get blocked in. they renovated the interior in 2019 and made the seating area smaller. location is the main thing this place has going for it, otherwise you should go to the other o'fallon location on us highway 50.

not much to review ... it's a starbucks; what can you say, except to be glad they have one in the mall! you get your drink, and hope it's up to par. if not, they'll make you another one. so relax, and hope they get your drink right. also, prices are a little higher than other stores, the barista couldn't explain why. but that's ok- maybe they're a privately operated. there are few tables and chairs, so it is convenient to stop, have coffee, and strategize shopping plans. they use the app, and it's convenient.

i've gone to this starbucks for almost twenty years now, and though i've aged and some things have changed, the service here 3 years ago went down hill. many of us went to this location because it actually was fun, but time moved and so did alot of the people, and starbucks pretty much exploded in the last 15 years or so, being that they are everywhere. we still think of this place, but 3 years ago is when it started going down hill will service. that is sad. but it is most likely confounded, as mentioned. locations are everywhere now, and people move on. ~peace thomas :)
fast service and the unicorn frap is fantastic!! the drink is extremely refreshing and not too sweet.

the lady works in the counter for making drinks in the morning on sunday seems to be very mean or racist. i said to the male employee that i wanted the drink to have no ice, and he wrote it on the cup. it was understandable that she mistook it for having ice. but after i asked her to take the ice out, she didn't even say a word with the mean face and took the ice out silently. i said thank you to her, and she still didn't want to say a word to me with the mean face. but she said "have a great day" with a large smiley face to the white customers before and after me. i'm wondering if this is the overall starbuck employees' attitudes to people with different color.

i am not a starbucks person but i do come in for tea sometimes. i have often noticed when in the broad ripple location that they have the absolutely nicest/best team. they are so happy, polite, professional and make you feel so warm and welcome. i like this location so so much. i come in when my daughter is at dj class. good stuff.

i was running a little bit late today so i stopped in at starbucks to grab a quick oatmeal for breakfast. i was the only one in the store. it took them about 15 minutes to make the oatmeal.... running later than i would have liked i ran to work open up the packet of blueberries was hit with such an overwhelming stench of rotten fruit. looking down on expiration date it's well over a month and a half old. i know it's really my fault i picked to go to starbucks over literally any other place and i will not be making that mistakes again

one of my favorite starbucks places! the workers here are wonderful and the service is even more amazing! came in for coffee and for some reason my starbucks app would not pull up so i can redeem my free drink. the girl who was at the window told me not to worry and have a wonderful day. just amazing customer service!

my friend used to work here she always told me never to go here because they do not wash there stuff daily, a pond in the dishwasher and mold where it shouldn't be. said it was absolutely disgusting never go here unless you want to take a sick day at work

what a complete shit show! staff sucks accept for sarah. they have let all of their seasoned people go. drive thru sucks! the rookies/snowflakes could care less about serving their customers. i met the jackass district manager. her head is so far shoved up her own ass she has no clue on customer service. the ukrainian mafia has moved in and are allowed to smoke outside the front and side door. they intimidate a lot of customers. i was told we allow it because they tip well. none of the mainline mom's driving their range rovers or suburbans have any clue on how to drive in the drive thru. so, if you don't have time to waste for the douche bags in devon, take a quick trip and go to target. it is always empty.

what can i say? it's a starbucks and if you've been to one, you've been to them all. my "go to's" for starbucks are always the peppermint mocha frappucino or the pumpkin spice frapp, when it's in season. i stop in here when i am looking for a dessert type of drink and i don't think i have ever actually had real coffee here, now that i think about it. this is a nice location that usually has people coming and going all day, since it's located on a busy part of manhattan.

so... five employees... one working as a cashier, one making drinks one squatting down i assume looking for some thing, one sticking his butt in her face laughing then going on about "blah blah blah bullshit, blah blah blah bullshit", and the final one stuffing his face with food and visiting each individual employee. on a positive note, they got the order right so i guess that deserves one star at least. ‍

i give this place 2 stars for its service only, which is friendly. however it gets only 2 stars because they don't know anything about coffee. the customer behind me asked what the difference is between a regular drip coffee and an americano. the barista looked at her for a long time with a confused look and then told her that it was a "darker coffee with more caffeine because it has espresso in it." if you know anything about coffee, you know he's wrong on all counts... didn't expect much, but misinformation is worse than just saying "i don't know."

in the middle and late 1980s, this particular location was a café paradiso. i was more of a tea drinker than a coffee drinker then, but every once in awhile, i went to café paradiso for their snacks, baked goods and an occasional cup or two of coffee. it was a nice place and i liked it a lot. then, one day when i was working and going to classes at a nearby university, café paradiso was gone; it was converted to a starbucks. and it's still a starbucks. my café paradiso was gone! quel horreur! so now, it has continued to be a starbucks ever since then, and it's not too much different than all the other starbucks locations. some have outside seating, and others don't have outside seating, this one has outside seating. the interior must have been remodeled because it's dark like everything is mahogany wood or made to look like that and depressing inside. i'm not sure what they were trying to accomplish with that, but for me, it doesn't work. it doesn't feel inviting and i didn't want to stay there very long. they offer typical starbucks stuff. they were limited on the starbucks merchandise like cups and mugs. they had food offerings like made up sandwiches and what seemed like limited baked goods. parking is a problem now because the formerly free spaces along wydown are not free anymore. they have an electronic pay station where you have to pay for the parking now. i don't know why they changed it, but it is now really inconvenient to park there now. those electronic pay stations are a major pain in the ass because they take coins or cards, and you have to put in your plate number in addition to paying with either the card or coins. i hate them. around the corner are the old fashioned parking meters which i prefer over that damn pay station. so just beware of that and you will get a ticket if you don't pay. i won't be back anytime soon.

headed for flight and stopped as i normally do. line was 40 people deep, wrapped around the store next door. stood for 15-20 minutes and did not move. no coffee is worth this aggravation. they need a better system for folks who just want a cup of black coffee.

had a bad experience at this starbucks. made a mobile order from the app and chose the location. it took my money. we get to the front only to find out that this location does not except mobile orders. it sent it to the next starbucks. the barista said to call the other location and cancel my order and get a refund. after ringing a very long time with no answer i hung up and called back. more waiting but when the gentleman answered he said he couldn't cancel my order. they have no control over mobile orders. now i have to take my time to call the corporate office and try to get my money credited back. they should not have locations listed on the app that don't accept mobile orders!!! what a mixed up way of doing business!! actually not this locations fault i guess , but they knew it was an issue and has been and haven't corrected it !!

this has to be the worst starbucks in idaho. i went through the drive thru on one of those rainy days, ordered my $7.00 coffee and left only to get down the road to take a sip of the wrong coffee. i drove back to starbucks, went through the drive thru again and explained that i had received the wrong coffee. they apologized and said that they would make me another coffee correctly this time. well, my story should end here but it doesn't. they hand me the coffee and while i'm at the window i take a drink, it's wrong again! at this time i just ask for my money back, after 5 minutes they finally give me a card with a $7.00 credit on it to use at any starbucks. i end up going to another starbucks where they know how to make their product only to find that instead of a $7.00 card i was given one with the value of $4.00. that is actually consumer fraud and i will never go back to that starbucks again.

i know most of the reviews are of the drive thru... i always like to walk into a starbucks, mostly for the fact that i put a lot of sugar in my coffee and you never get that with a drive thru. the inside is nice and clean, and i never have any problem making an order. it is served up nice and quickly and i go about my day. i enjoy it, no reason to hate.
i like this location because the drive thru line is efficient and tricks you into thinking the line isn't as long as it is. quick service& delicious coffee.
our favorite starbucks we ever regularly went to, they are always fast, super polite, and never mess up drinks. shoutout to alex, the best barista who always knew our drinks when we came

agh. meh. mmmph. and other shoulder shrugging sounds. a.) it's your standard starbucks as far as menu, decor, etc. go b.) the free wifi is less than weak. double "meh" c.) i get that the suburbanites have few other coffee shop options, but holy shiza this place is jiggity jammed. d.) friendly service e.) it gets "the job" done
this is a pretty standard starbucks. it's close to work for me, and the staff is very nice. it's really, really small.

love this starbucks. the crew working is so kind and fun (usually) and they want to get your day started right! thankful for starbucks black coffee not breakfast and lattes or tea for lunch. up for something different, try a matcha latte. up for something new, try their nitro brew. up for the most delicious tea, try peach tranquility! and if you aren't feeling the best, ask for the medicine ball drink -- it's delish! love starbucks for its typical consistency

it's new and so are all the baristas. but they are super nice. and i can tell they actually hired some people from the neighborhood. that made me smile. psalm, is my fav. the artwork by b mike is also a plus. if i ever have a reason to sit in inside i'll grab a seat by the window and gawk at that.

1-1-15 just went in for a chi ice tea to start the new year. asked for soy milk and extra cream. once i got my order i asked for a bit more cream since the server squirted a little cream. she then gave me the worst attitude you can give a customer. then i asked for a staw and she tossed it at me like i was a dog. they need to retrain this girl on service. never going to thos starbucks again. extreemly rude girl employee.

i have to downgrade them as a result of the past few years of service. the drive thru has substantially slowed down. the past few visits, scattered across an assortment of times, have all resulted in extended wait times. when the lines are long outside i'm happy to get out of the car and go inside. even inside seems to have taken quite awhile. the priority seems to go to the drive thru, though when you are in the drive thru it doesn't seem like that. a recent order was requested with "no whip" and delivered to me with whipped cream on top. that easily doubled my wait time since i asked them to remake it. i will give it an upgrade for last nights purchase. the wait time seemed far longer than the usual extended wait. when i drove up to the window to pay my drink was handed to me and the employee said "no charge since it took 17 minutes." thank you for this freebie but i have sat for just as long before with no change in billing and usually an incorrectly made beverage. i am sure these are a result of the surge in people in the area as of late but they should be adapting for that staffing wise. i tend to chose another location or another chain as a result of these issues.
stopped by for a cup of coffee. couldn't wait for pike coffee to brew so i chose the other type. a sip as soon as i drove off, coffee is warm to cool. so disappointed. thanks for ruining my morning
it is what it is, the staff suck and the coffee is good, you need to put up with all the bs from the staff. believe me they will frustrate you over a cup of coffee!

rude & inconsiderate staff. after waiting 20 min in line, we ordered lunch. one of the drinks was super sweet and my son needed some water. i walked up to the barista to ask for a glass of water and she rudely told me to get in line. so i waited another 15 minutes to get a glass of a water. i'm glad we're just visiting, our starbucks at home is very welcoming.

not sure what was happening today but, there was a total of 5 cars in the drive thru, including me. it took 32 minutes to get my order in my hand. i wanted to back up and leave but, someone had pulled in behind me.

came in the drive through today to order a dragon drink with foam on top. i guess they forgot to turn the mic off because they started laughing at what a ridiculous drink it was and how "people just order things just to order it" maybe you should double check if your mic is off next time mrs. haylee

i'm surprised the ratings for this starbucks are so low. the baristas have been nothing but friendly. i'd give this place five stars if the first floor wasn't so cramped up, if going to the second floor didn't mean the temperature went up 10 degrees and i lose cell reception, and if the first floor had window shades so i don't go blind sitting there as the sun rises in the morning.

awful coffee! ordered a hot coffee with a cold foam topping. i have ordered this multiple times at various locations and never had an issue. i ordered through the app and picked up at the drive thru. was never told they made it an iced coffee up til i was at the pick up window. when the young girl handed me my drink and i noticed it was an ice coffee i asked her why my coffee was made are used instead of hot like i ordered. she proceeded to tell me that they cannot add the cold foam to a hot coffee. i told her i have had this ordered at various locations and had no issue. she never offered to remake the coffee nor apologize.
quick coffee stop in the new msy terminal, concourse c. the the waiting in spacious, with lots of seats nearby.
pretty annoying waiting 15 minutes for coffee and then when youre halfway home and try your coffee its wrong . cant say this is the first time either

my local starbucks. great staff, very friendly. the store is a bit small if you plan to hang out and do some work / have a meeting, etc. but, they have made the best out of the space. it is also right next door to the best doughnuts in town (nicola's), so grab a starbucks and some nicola's doughnuts and you are set!

this is an alright starbucks location. it's close to the parkway, so it can be pretty busy. the service here is nice, but i don't enjoy being cramped, pushing myself to the window so people can walking past while i wait for my drink to be made.

i would have given this place a 5 star but everytime i order the green tea frap it taste like a vanilla bean frap. but its still pretty good and can't complain. the service here is amazing. its always busy and so hard to get parking if you have to drive around a couple of times. wish they had drive thru... lol. the spinach wraps also very good. love love it.

lovely to look at, but impossible to love. the setting alongside lake afton is as pretty as a postcard, but the 3 car parking lot is ridiculously inadequate. it makes a difficult traffic location that much worse, forcing people to focus on parking rather than driving. double and illegal parking happen every day, creating a dangerous condition. amazing that the yardley government permitted star$$$ to do this. the coffee is the same as everywhere else, which to me is poor to average for the basics, but decent if you fall prey to the insidious upsell pressure to go latte or capp in order to be able to imbibe the burnt tasting brew. the facility is pleasant once inside, with windows facing main street or the lake. it is also a front row seat to the near miss accidents that routinely happen there.

we decided to stop and get some coffee on the way to the lake. it's monday afternoon and we don't normally go to the lake, however the power company is working on the transformers and so we have no power. kids are going to do their studying at the lake. there was about 15 cars in line, now that we can't go inside, we have to compensate for long lines in the drive-through. the light went fairly fast. the girl who took our order was polite as usual as well as repeated my order back to me perfectly. that's one thing that's nice about starbucks, they never make you feel bad for having long more specialized requirements for your coffee. but yes, you do pay for it. one thing that irritates me is the price has gone up since last time i was here. and this time of economic crisis, starbucks it's not reducing the stress by providing a little discount on their overpriced drinks. they actually have increased. $5.90 for a vente mocha with caramel. my usual order is a vente mocha, extra hot, no whip cream or foam, add caramel syrup. today the flavor was good, however it was not extra hot. the line was just too long for me to go back and have this fixed. so, next time i am reminded to test it before i leave the drive up window. previous review: i had this review sitting in my yelp and forgot to publish it so i included it. today was 3 star, with the previous one star average is two star. going through drive-through today. the girl on the microphone was very polite and friendly sounding. there was one car ahead of me. the wait was extremely long. i have no idea why. i sat behind the car at the window, and the car behind me stuck for over 10 minutes. when the person came to the window to give the guy in front of me his order, there were two drinks. there were three girls inside talking, and i could only see one customer. i had to wait about seven minutes for my coffee. i decided to take a picture of the lobby to show there was no one in it, when the manager came up and told me i was not allowed to take pictures. i let her know that i did not take a picture of any people, as well as i would never put someone's picture on yelp without their permission. she seemed quite distressed, maybe she had a feeling that the review was not going to go well... who knows? it seems kind of ridiculous to wait in line for nearly 20 minutes for one coffee. so yeah, i'll give this place another shot sometime and see if it's consistent or not but as for now... might want to avoid this place seeing that the workers prefer to talk over customer service...
this is a great starbucks! large inside area and large area of outdoor seating with an umbrella. there is also a convenient drive thru and lots of parking. the staff is friendly too.

i always order the same thing at every starbucks i go to and the quality is always always the same...except for here not only was the wait stupid but the place is filthy which makes you think the sanitation of the blenders ect is the same. i could have gotten better service and better coffee from mcdonald's. i ordered a vanilla bean creme for my boyfriend and it's just an expensive fast food milkshake. the last one he had was delicious from the lemmon valley starbucks so idk what the deal is with this place in particular

my girlfriend and i decided to come to this location to enjoy a strawberry refresher over the other locations because this location is usually so spot on. however, we order two venti refresher just to be told that they are out of strawberry juice. ok that happens, i guess, so we change to passion tea when we are told that they are out of lemonade. seriously?!?! we decide to do do the new cold brew iced coffee and what looks to be a chocolate scone. i hand the heated pastry bag to my girlfriend sitting outside and ask the barista if milk comes in the coffee. she tells me know but that they could add half and half. i ask for a splash the barista hands me two drinks what look closer to white mochas than coffee with a splash. my girlfriend then notices that the barista gave us a chocolate croissant. i went to point it out but the girl walked away as i was talking to her about the mistake. my girlfriend then noticed that the scone we wanted was no longer in the case. we wouldn't have ordered food today, but the scone was something new for us to try.

i usually love coming here and they haven't messed up my drink. but whoever was working drive thru on new year's day around 11 am was definitely not even listening to my order. i ordered a venti iced vanilla green tea latte. she repeated it back without saying iced, so i reminded her again that i wanted iced. she told me my total then i proceed to pull up. she hands me a hot latte. i tell her it's supposed to be iced. she hands me an iced mocha -____- this point, i was just fed up and was going to be late if i had her fix my drink for the third time.

ok i frequent this particular starbucks, from work, yeah-i know-its starbucks!!!but sometimes you get shitty employees with 'tude!! here-you will not get that.oh contrare mon freire, the people that work here are great, from the girl who takes my order, to one makes my coffee or whatever i get!! eye contact, friendly, just genuinely nice. sweet little asian lady takes my order, how cute is she!!! but my drink always comes like the coke commercial" a coke & a smile" , this here, a coffee & a smile" that's what i'm talking about.

located just inside the tar'gét store. this starbucks is just what you need when looking for a caffeine fix during a toiletry reload day. it has plenty of seating which overlooks apparel, trinkets, the front entrance doors and cart hub. my barista alicia was super nice and crafted my drink to perfection. starbucks reward cards are accepted, pastries on deck, lots of ceramic mugs, travel tumblers, bags of coffee and more. ~christy
pretty busy place with longer than average wait time... happened a few times. drive through is ok.
i just got a bagel with cream cheese and the cream cheese was awfully sour. i'm guessing it's spoiled. i was going to give it to my kids, but i'm glad i tasted it first. this is not ok.

starbucks is starbucks, my coffee was good- but for an order of two croissants, a blonde roast and a chai tea latte- it should never take 15min. funny enough, it was an honest mistake, but after you make a mistake, you apologize. i think the sticker got stuck to the apron of the barista so she didn't know what i was waiting on. after i politely reminded her that i had another drink, she looks down and sees the sticker and makes the drink. a nice acknowledgement- sorry for the wait, or "i'm sorry", or "oh, excuse me."

i am done with this store!!! i have called and called, over and over! corporate knows the problems this store has with the training issues but jessica the manager is either oblivious or has an extreme turnover rate that makes teaching impossible. waiting 15 minutes for coffee is bs especially when you place a mobile order and they don't start working on it until you get there. and there are 4 people working with no one there. seriously no one in the drive through and no one in the store. they have to be the single starbucks issue in the whole region. for the love of god get it together

i went to e. hillsborough ave location near my home, i ordered a small size pineapple matcha tea for 1st time. usual preference is matcha frappe. lid was not properly put on and drink spilled out, when i checked, the lid had a split. paid 4.79 for two sips that was leftover with mostly ice. cup seemed smaller than usual small size and wh8at i did tatse of this drink was just okay. very disappointing! however 5* drive thru staff, he was very professional.

great location, right next to rumba. we came here to have a drink while waiting for rumba to open. as i stood in line, noticed a fly inside the cake cabinet next to the counter. it was going to town, taking turns landing on all the cakes. i mentioned this to the cashier, who opened and closed the door to the cabinet several times. he did not reach in to try to get the fly out, and the fly ended up untouched and unbothered. the cashier walked away and started mopping the floor. i picked up my receipt from the printer as the buffoon cashier forgot to give it to me. however, my drink was good and satisfyingly cold.
i ordered three drinks. they were all made wrong the first time. the second time two were made wrong. the third time, they were still made wrong and i gave up trying.

an average of 3 stars after 2 visits. so convenient that they have a drive thru and the line moved rather quickly. great that there are two exits to either go east/west on route 38. we sat inside second time around and it was empty on a saturday morning. it was clean except for spills on the sugar/milk table (didn't know what to call it, sorry). minus 2 stars because they did not have cold brew and i did not really care for the baristas being petty towards one another. they were lacking professionalism. i don't really care to hear them bicker about who get's to go on break first. this chatter is unnecessary to hear 1st thing in the morning sans caffeine. please don't make your patrons feel uncomfortable.

four stars for being a starbucks with a drive-thru, but minus one star for being a pain to enter/exit. this location is right near a roundabout, and there isn't a clear way to enter, so i ended up driving a block past it and circling around. once you go through the drive-thru and exit, then that's when you finally see where the entrance is. fyi: the entrance is off of keystone.
they will never get your right !!!! worst starbucks in tampa . i have a milk allergy and they always mess this up even when i tell them this ! no foam? you will get extra !!!!
hot food takes forever. every time!!! i just don't understand. so many people working. is there just one oven?

why not have this group trained by the paoli folks? they are friendly and efficient!!! the drive thru was full, so i pulled in to park as the lot was empty. thought i could just run in and run out...no drinks, just a spinach wrap. devon is friendly, too friendly. there were 2 people in front of me...one was ordering when i arrived...4" later, he left the register area! then the woman in front of me ordered and there was more clever conversation. all this chatting and 2 more people behind me waiting. i was present to retrieve my wrap,thankfully. the girl was so busy talking, she never got my name. grrrrrrrr! when i pulled out of the lot, the last car in the drive thru lane when i arrived, was in front of me leaving! come on people, chat when it not busy....but if you have a line, let's move it alone. its called customer service! ask paoli, they can do both!

i recently came to this location and ordered a double espresso with a splash of milk. when we received the drink, it was cold. we went back in to get it replaced with a hot one and the workers claimed it was because of the milk that it was cold. when the remade it, with comparably the same amount of milk, the thing was actually hot. which leads me to believe it was a cup of some old shots of espresso they had sitting around. i'm glad it was replaced, but the deceit, customer service and quality of the original drink is offensive. any who, was happy to let this go. but today i came to this business and saw that they are completely closed, doors locked, over half an hour before the listed closing time on the door and online. sooo yep this location is caca, avoid at all costs.

3/01/19 - eddie's last day. he has been a plus plus for this location. he will be missed immensely. ;( 2/12/19 - so disappointed to hear that our wonderful asst manager eddie the arizona pavilions location will be leaving us. 1/11/19 - very disappointed that they didn't have my favorite sandwich today, chicken and artichoke 11/20/18 - so disappointed today. not one sandwich in the cooler at 11:15 am on once again we are here. our favorite place. love our baristas. jessica, treigh, caleigh, alex and taylor. if i've forgotten anyone please forgive me. so many great people work at this location. :) and of co

i seriously love this starbucks. i actually prefer it to the regular one in shiloh. the staff is amazing and friendly. always make me what i want, how i want it. also they are rarely out of what i want. a huge plus... it's inside a target.

the service was really nice. very fast no wait at all hardly. i also had to purchase 13 5$ gift cards for a business event and the cashier made my experience so pleasant she was so kind! i appreciate this level of service.

i came in to this starbucks and their mobile ordering had gone down, so there were a handful of angry patrons. the staff here handled the rude crew with grace and patience, and kicked into high gear to get everyone taken care of. not every patron was grateful for their hard work, but i hope the staff knows that it didn't go unnoticed. it's almost like people get crabby without their coffee! :)
sweet staff! long lines but it's constantly moving. food and coffee always yum. good location by the freeway.
hard to find. filled with kids climbing on the tables. not a seat to be found. loudest starbucks i have ever been in. and this after the circuitous route to get here. walked in, walked out

it's 6:53 am supposed to be open at 6:00am no staff in site not the first time they have not been open on time. heading across the street to the diner for my go-go juice at least i know someone will be there to make it.
great drinks and food. awesome/ cozy place to come and get work done and even meet up with friends!

first time at this location, well it is brand new. stopped in to take advantage of double star day and some pumpkin spice goodies! got a grande psl and a pumpkin cream cheese muffin. i got the muffin warmed and it was perfect for dunking in my latte. tasted sooooo good! my latte was hot and delicious with cold whipped cream on top. very clean and spacious interior. i sat in a counter height chair down at the end of the prep area, very comfortable!
this starbucks is utterly disgusting; truly gross. it was infested by gnats which meant that everywhere we went had bugs. my food had gnats in it, the bathroom had gnats in it.

i am a permanent fixture in this starbucks! my morning commute would be absolutely horrific if i did not stop here! there is no drive thru here, so i have no idea why there is a picture of one under this location. the baristas here are excellent!! i come here so much that i don't even have to tell them what i want! it's hilarious!! depending on the time you get here, this location can have a line. i came here on a saturday morning at 10am and actually left because they were so busy. i felt violated like i own the place. hahaahaha!! *i come here all the time, who are these strangers clogging up the place?* the service here is excellent! but, some days there is a long line, most days i get in and out and there's no way to tell when. you won't lose by coming here, that's for sure!
i was unaware that it is apparently bad manners to call and order a box of coffee the day you want it. the staff was unbelievably rude.
what a mess!!! the drive through line blocks the entire parking lot. i couldn't back out of my spot and then couldn't exit because cars were blocking it.

lady at the drive thru was real sweet. girl on the mic wanted to argue what a black eye is. just run a quick google search. it's coffee with shots of espresso, not sweetened coffee. i'm done with this starbucks. y'all are terrible.

it pains me to write this review but time and time again i've been burned by nasty drinks at this location. where's the consistency? the interior is beautiful, i used to love coming to this location when i worked in the camino real marketplace. every morning i was greeted and they knew my drink. now they don't, that's understandable. my drink is a white mocha, hardly a difficult one. why doesn't it ever taste like it is supposed to?

7:50p on a thursday night and the place was closed. no sign. waited behind a car in drive thru for several minutes. they left. i pulled to window and it was a ghost town. .disappointed. dissatisfied because no hours posted. not your typical starbucks experience.

service not great. had to keep asking for my pastries long after getting my coffee. mocha super sweet (?). coffee prep station very messy. very long lines to drive up extending onto busy bay st. large dining area but over air conditioned so we ate outside.
the barista was very patient and helpful. even when i asked to have the whip cream taken off they still hd a smile. totally recommend this starbucks out of many ive been to.

very disappointed in my experience there. i bought two caramel frappuccinos and they were nothing but ice and foam when i got home i had to brew my own coffee to add to it. i will not go back to that location
slowest starbucks ever.... three years i've been coming here and they never disappoint... guaranteed time to get a latte ... 8 minutes. i will say the people who work there are nice but come on
new location is open - avoid it. inside is dirty. the service is pissy. and the drinks made taste awful.
this is by far the slowest starbucks. it's not just once in awhile, they are consistent. it's as if the manager is never on duty and the workers give zero f@#ks.

i frequent starbucks often. i have crohns disease and their chocolate banana protein smoothie makes a great breakfast replacement meal without upsetting my stomach, however this starbucks location can never get it right. the few times ive asked them to make it, it always comes out very bland... equivalent to the taste of milk, bananas and ice. it's very disappointing and seems to be the only location that serves it this way. this morning my husband wanted iced coffee and this particular starbucks happened to be closest to where we were. he ordered a mocha frappuccino with a shot of espresso. he too was very disappointed with the flavor. he said it tasted as if they were skimping on using all the ingredients, much like my smoothie. if i'm going to pay the same price regardless, i'd much rather go to a different starbucks location with better quality.

before getting on the pa turnpike early this morning, i made a short detour onto horizon boulevard to get some java and a small bite for breakfast. when chik-fil-a was not yet open, i drove through the take out window at starbucks next door. i liked the convenience and time savings of a drive thru, but it seemed like the order taker was also the barista as well as the short order cook. so, she looked rather busy, and in need of assistance. still, she kept her composure and got everything right. as usual, the coffee had a burnt and bitter taste, but the extra milk requested helped to soften the harshness. i asked for their sausage, egg and cheese sandwich on a croissant, but was told that all these meals are pre-packaged and microwaved just before sale. i decided to go with the english muffin as the only option. i don't like what microwaves do to eggs and bread, so the meal was at best, average. nothing i would want a second time. hopefully, cfa will be open next time i find myself in a similar predicament.

i made a google account just to review this place, more of a yahoo kind of guy lol. this starbucks should get zero stars if it was an option. the drive through never moves fast enough, incredibly slow. and when you get to the window they take ur card and give ur drink and slam the door. so if ur taking all this time, and not treating your customers well what the hell are you doing? also found out from sitting in the cafe early mornings, how much behind the back talking there is. i have to sit outside now, because it bothers me so much. looks like adults but feels like it's run by a bunch of teenage high school girls. i've worked at millions of customer service jobs growing up, and understand what it's like to close and open. but while i went up to use the restroom & ask for the code, the reason they don't move fast enough is apparently because of closers. just grow up & do your job. incredibly unprofessional, lower your voices and prioritize things your customers can hear. will be calling corporate about the interesting choices of words i hear around 9-10am.
they are on the slow side here. not the worst but not the best sigh. i am frustrated by my most recent wait so i just can't say more.
didn't have my typical order of blonde roast, but employees were friendly and fast. location was in the lobby of our hotel, but clean and bright.

i go to this starbucks frequently, usually always pleasant. however, this time around i go there to get lunch. ate the pb&j sandwhich and cheese stick then i noticed the cucumbers. they were covered in mold. the best by date was 5/10 and it seems that they weren't checking their food case. the drive thru guy was in a rush and it was pretty busy. but, i do pay to have food that's fit for consumption, and this is just concerning. it worries me that they aren't cleaning the machines that brew the coffee properly either. i'd say, check you drinks and food before consumption here.
just your basic starbucks but in a mail. they weren't open when i went by first even though the updated hours said they should have been. employees seemed like they didn't care.

this starbucks has one key feature that puts it on my regular itinerary. it's near where i sleep and therefore can provide an early morning double espresso when i'm too lazy to crank out some coffee on my own. honestly, i've been to so many starbucks that i can't really tell them apart anymore. why is there not a yelp badge for this amazing accomplishment hello!? yelp! i want a badge now. ok. maybe this is the caffeine talking.
it's starbucks, and i'm not a hater. but this place was understaffed and kind of dirty.

i'm convinced that the people that work here just make whatever they think you should drink rather than what you actually order. i especially love when one certain condescending employee informs me that "skim milk beverages do not come with whip" after requesting no whip. oh but it does...every.single.time. the other day, i ordered 3 items and every single item was either made incorrectly or completely the wrong thing. also, the drive thru....so slow. glacial, even.

love this location. it is by far the best. i love how big the inside is allowing us customers to have a nice comfortable seat anywhere. me, my boyfriend, and my sister went here and we all ordered off the secret menu and the cashier was very nice and took her time with us. i forgot her name but she is super nice and did not get anoyyed by us ordering from the secret menu. this is our go to location.
the amount of taylor swift i heard in this store in one hour on the loud speakers felt like a depressed teenage girls room after her first breakup.

we frequent this location. (3) times this week we have sat in a very long line only to find out they ran out of tea refreshers. could you possibly put up a stationary sign indicating this? so frustrating & waste of time. even before the holidays, this location frequently runs out of bagged coffee & stoppers. this location could be managed better.
i frequent this location weekly. extremely friendly staff, consistent drinks, and quick service.
it's in a mall but we never could find it! called and called and called yet they nevet answered the phone! really poor!

i'm not a starbucks regular, but sometimes, a girl's just gotta have a frappucino on that mountain drive up to tahoe to keep her awake. i generally prefer to come in and order so i can order my drink, pay for it, then run to the loo or use the free wifi while it's being made. multi-tasking mode complete! the staff here are super friendly and nice, which is a bonus.

my coffee is always great! at times it does get very busy but the staff is fantastic and quick at getting drinks out. i've never had a problem with my orders. love it here. now if only they could get a drive thru :)

the partners out here are super great! my family is visiting from california! i just so happen to be a partner from california. the partners out here are amazing and i love the fact that this starbucks happens to be around the corner from where i stay!! nelly nel was the first to show that good old louisiana southern hospitality, mo and sarah were awesome as well! thank you guys for making my experince here an fantastic one!!! #tobeapartner!!!

fastest starbucks i've ever been to and i have definitely been to more starbucks than i care to admit. seven of us rolling through the drive thru, everyone ordered and they not only got everyone's order correct, it was all ready when we got to the window!! we were super impressed!! enjoy!!

wow! almost 20 minutes for a basic latte and iced mocha?! not even a "sorry about your wait". only 1 barista and 1 check out person. gotta blame management for this one. won't be back to this starbucks if i can keep from it.

it's nothing extraordinary but it did provide me with much needed caffeine and sugar, and for that, i will remain eternally grateful. the baristas here definitely take their sweet old time but in the end, i did get my soy latte.

clean, straight forward starbucks. for some reason the green tea does not taste like the ones i get in ca. i know, i know this is blasphemy for a corporate chain ....... i'm so sorry. i guess the problem is me, i am monotone - i just get one drink over and over.

i so badly want to give this location a good review but i have visited too many times and been disappointed-have to say that this starbucks has such a high turnover and maybe this accounts for their inability to make a consistently good drink, specifically their lattes. i really think it's the lack of training. almost every other location makes their drinks consistently, but at this one, they either let the espresso shots sit too long ( causing a burnt taste) or fill it overflowing with milk (which dilutes the taste) or serves it lukewarm. they are also 5x slower than any of the other locations in town. whatever it is they are doing, more times than not, i walk away frustrated and vowing never to go there again.

stay the hell away from this place they have a psycho batista working there a light skin black or hispanic man trying to kick in bathroom door several times while i was using the bathroom crazy thing they have employee bathroom i was nervous i called 911 they never came after 20 mins i confronted the nut he have feminine voice if o c him in the street that's it for him fyi rittenhouse sq police don't care about black people i bet if i was white they would've came smh this happen at 230 pm today sunday stay away
the location is very nice inside and the coffee is great a usual! the only downside is the awful parking lot and drive thru lane. wish they could fix that part.
i have always enjoyed this spot. the staff are nice and the place is clean. plenty of places to sit and the music is pleasent. i would recommend this place to anyone.

the staff here had gotten pretty consistent; there was a huge turnover rate but the workers have been constant factor so that's a big plus. the manager here seems really really invested in what she does and i love that passion. even though starbucks is a corporation, i can see that the manager here really likes what she does and she seems pretty well educated on coffee (i've seen her so seminars and stuff). the biggest thing i have against this place is they run out of things which is fine if it's towards the end but, it's been pretty frequent that they will run out of things in the menu by mid-noon. i understand that coffee is popular but that being said... once or twice i understand but this many times this early? overall though, horsham location wasn't the best and i preferred to drive out to other place even though this place is the closest to me. i like it now; staff is good, coffee is ok (aside from what i mentioned).

never coming back here again. i've tried many times. i'm the only person ordering a drink., a ice coffer. three people are working, talking. it took 10 min and there was no one in front of me. this wasn't go too. i'm not someone who writes a lot of yelps. the last few month when i come in i always order a ice coffer. all they do is scoop ice, walk over to where the ice coffee is in a big pitcher and pour. why must i wait 15 minutes for that when people in front of me who order warm coffee gets it made right away. this place what is my go to starbucks but now i'd rather just make my own and save myself the trouble of waiting forever when it only takes 15 seconds to make.

this starbucks messes up drink orders frequently. oftentimes the taste is off for hot drinks. also, they have been terrible at following through with mobile orders every time thus far. once i heard an employee say, "we have mobile ordering?" get with it. today i ordered a frap through through the app and it arrived 19 minutes late!

i didn't realize that the stores at the mall don't open until noon on sundays, so i got there before the stores opened. luckily, starbucks opens earlier, and i stopped by to get a coffee while i waited for the stores to open. just a starbucks, but it was big enough and had seating. what else do you need in a starbucks?

the store was clean and employees were friendly. coffee from this location is as expected from a starbucks chain, it tasted fresh and was made to order. they weren't real busy due to the recent snowstorm so we didn't have to wait in line and our coffee and food came out fast. the cranberry scone we had was tasty as was the caramel latte. my only complaint is the space is quite small and not good for groups, especially when the 2 larger spaces in the back were occupied by a single person in each while the tables for 2 were all empty. so the 5 of us had to take up 3 seating areas in the middle of the place. it is conveniently located right off the freeway and not too difficult to get back on in either direction. the lot was car friendly, meaning there weren't any steep ingress/egress conditions to deal with, nor any gargantuan speed bumps. would i go back? -yes, as long as we have a smaller group.

it's starbucks, home of over priced, over roasted coffee. and apparently when you ask for a chai with 2 xtra pumps you get a chai with 2 shots of espresso. that is not a good thing. oh well, live and learn.. *chuckles*

this is the best starbucks around. warrington consistently messes up orders and huntingdon valley shopping center location runs a little slow. i loveee the baristas at this location. they're all incredibly friendly and make the best drinks. if you aren't 100% satisfied, they go out of their way to correct the order and make up for any errors. great starbucks, indeed!

hands down the best looking starbucks so far! the store totally exposes it's city's jazz scene with trumpets hanging on the ceiling, musical notes on the walls, music, etc. this location has a huge seating area as well so it's great for people!

holy macanoli!! this is easily the busiest starbucks i've ever seen. it's small and the line is backed up to the door. the drive thru is 10 cars deep. there are 8 baristas with well differentiated task priorities who fill orders remarkably efficiently. though there are easily 6-8 customers awaiting orders at the pick up counter for in store and on line orders, it is not a long wait. i'm impressed. quality is the standard i've come to expect from the starbucks brand. well done!

took a little coffee break coming from hearts castle back to l.a. this place was busy but the staff was very friendly. coffee was delish as usual. bathroom is clean. and it's easy access from the shopping center where it's located.

great starbucks: fast and good service. my coffee always comes out right. it's super convent when you have shopping at target to legends to do. if they is a long line it moves fast and you get your coffee within minutes. didn't give 5 stars because a they always seem to be out of something.

the ladies that work at this location are so rude!! this is the third time i went through the drive through and the baristas were not nice at all. i ordered a venti iced tea lemonade, nothing fancy and the young lady that took my order didn't even greet me when i pulled up to the window. it felt like i was inconveniencing her by just being there. maybe she was having a bad day but since this is the third time i've had a bad experience there, i'm convinced the workers are just rude.

this is a very packed starbucks right off the freeway in my hometown. on weekdays you can count on everything being good--the baristas take a lot of care with the drinks. my wife is very picky, always asking for extra hot, and this location gets it right. on weekends the backup teen crew is less strong, slows down the drive-thru at times and makes the occasional mistake. overall the coffee is done well here. not enough seating. people like to hang out at this location, and it's hard to get a seat except when it's nice outside. parking--just not enough spots and very poorly designed.

my barista jp put too much ice in my drink. 95% ice 5% pinkity drinkity. was disappointed in the amount of drink and the lack of berries. he also called me amy (which was not my name on the cup) but he was nice. the other girl gave me a straw when he didn't, thanks hun! overall it was a good experience. thanks jp and girl with glasses!

my brother and i went here and our coffee was just okay. i had better a different locations. it was almost empty inside but the guy making our coffees was taking a very long time. then a lady he was friends with came in and he took even longer to make them. he forgot to put the vanilla syrup in one coffee but corrected this. he also almost spilt the second one. we probably won't be returning to this location.

this is my favorite starbucks in center city. service is fast and friendly. i don't go to starbucks more than twice a month, and the cashier in the mornings still remembers my name and my order. i'm always very impressed with her recollection. occasionally, they run out of spinach and feta breakfast wraps, so get there early to make sure you get the food you want.
claimed a star reward by ordering a java chip frappuccino at the drive thru here. i was very disappointed with the watered down taste - mostly ice and only a few chips.

every time i come to this starbucks i'm in line for 10-15 minutes. i barely even get to the ordering speaker in a timely manner. they really don't have a good system here and it's really this starbucks
i think i'm going to love this location. clean and cozy and right next to whole foods. yes and yes!

this is seriously the worst starbucks iv ever been to. i ordered myself a vanilla chi latte no hot water and my son a kids hot chocolate. well we get down the road and he takes a sip and said it was water. i then grabbed it from him to try and sure enough tasted like water with ice and chocolate syrup. my vanilla chi was just a chi no vanilla. i went back and they remade his drink and filled up the kids cup not even half way. i'll never go back to this location.
don't wait in the rediculous line at this store, order on the mobile app and you'll be in and out, just about as fast as drive thru. sb coffee is good every time though.
employees turn people away before posted closing time. as a former starbucks partner, this goes against company values.
