i was staying at harrah's for an extended weekend and was thrilled to learn that there is a starbucks in the hotel, in the west tower, on the 2nd floor (near the elevators & hash house a go go)! i ordered coffee for myself and family members every morning we were there. it was always moderately busy, just like any other starbucks, but the baristas were friendly, quick, and made everything ordered spot on. if you want to get total rewards credits for your purchase provide your tr players card prior to paying (i had zero issues with this). this is a wonderful convenience for people staying and playing in harrah's or around. this is not a standard starbucks with seating and i wouldn't expect that from this type of property, real estate is a commodity, there are a few tables, but this isn't intended for a hangout spot, grab your coffee and go! overall a great experience!
30 minute wait in drive thru (5 cars were originally ahead of us) ....ordered two white mocha s. ...both were terrible. worst starbucks i've ever visited.

this location as others mentioned is very hot or miss. it's the closest of the five in the area to me, but also the one i frequent the least. it's a mall location so those of you who may hang out for business or for meetings may find space and noise louder than usual. the staff is usually friendly but not as 'warm' as some of the others. it feels less cozy and more about customer turnaround as a result. the plus side, never had a problem with drink orders done well!

not sure what's up with the low rating for this location. every time i go my order is ready as scheduled (mobile user here) and if i don't feel like waiting in the drive thru, a quick hop inside to the pick up area and i'm on my way. saturday afternoon around noon, it's usually busy. located in the rouses/planet fitness parking lot.about ten minutes from where i work. friendly, courteous staff.

we walk in and the four employees in here are all working the drive thru and standing around looking for something to else do then we walk up and order one of us heads to the restroom (you need a code 51111) and the floor is filthy the paper towels are gone and/or on the floor with with four something to do don't you think they could replace the paper towels, so i tell one of the baristas and she said "ok i'll let them know" as if they have a janitorial staff! after another guy uses the restroom and comes out with dripping hands in front of everyone and asks them for towels they finally replace them but don't do anything else to clean up in the nasty restroom. and this is when brand new places bathrooms go down hill fast!. the restaurant area was also never tidying up during our visit. 7 at night, not busy, no one swept, pushed in chairs. 4 employees, so sad!

if it ain't broke, don't fix it!! if it's broke fix it asap. waited 18 min for a tall americano, about 15min to long. absolutely no efficiency to get their customers a cup of coffee. i finally walked out empty handed after 4 people came in after me and left with their orders. worst customer service but then again it's starbucks. if your time is valuable, don't waste it going here.

filthy! i would rarely leave a negative review , but this place looks like it hasn't been cleaned in months. the floors look absolutely disgusting, everywhere ! dust and hair balls built up under tables. the scum and junk has built up on the the floor edges. i have never in my life seen a starbucks in this state, i was shocked honestly. i asked for the manager. he said 'i've been gone a few days , yeah , i noticed it too.'. no way this got like this in days , you can tell it's just the way this store is run. badly.
i was having a pretty terrible day got some awful news and somehow the workers here managed to have me leaving with a smile... driving away i was a little speechless but in a good way! thanks!

great place to get a coffee fix and read the news of the day! the lattes are to die for‼get the non fat venti it is only 150 calories. that is just a 2 mile walk tonight to lose it‼‼‼but it is worth it
place is dirty. tables and chairs and floors dirty, makes you wonder about food and drinks. bathroom was filthy also. pretty gross.will definitely visit a different location (of the hundreds around).

while the parking lot here is an absolute nightmare at certain times during the day (lunch especially), they actually have some of the best staff out of any of the starbucks stores i frequent. they're not only friendly and attentive, but they make good drinks and manage to keep the store clean while doing so. i was particularly struck one day when a very demanding woman came in looking for bagged coffee for a friend. they didn't have what she wanted in the retail selection, but the manager went in the back and was able to get what she wanted from what i assume to have been the store's supply. they just seem to go above and beyond there.

pros: - baristas are super awesome and very helpful when it comes to drink choice! - very quick service. - clean. - stable internet. cons: - limited parking space. notes: - be careful not go up the hill to fast so you do not damage your car. - be careful parking, great deal of traffic in that small little area.

congratulations. you just discovered the slowest starbucks in the united states. if you want to wait 25 minutes to get 2 cold shots of espresso dumped into a plastic cup, then go here immediately. the employees here are so inefficient they will take as long as possible to prepare your beverage. and then act like they worked so hard at it.
their recipe for large peach green tea lemonade is apparently 1/4 liquid and 3/4 ice. i finished my large tea in a few sips. not worth $4.19
best costumer service ever! super quick and accurate with orders. everyone is so cheerful there. definitely my favorite starbucks

pretty fast service (even during a very busy day) and decently clean. unfortunately due to starbucks regulations, when i asked to try one of the teas, they couldn't give me a sample but hey i guess with a chain we can't be too picky. they could have been a little bit more informed on the coffee and tea choices. when my boyfriend asked one of the baristas which of their coffees has the most bold flavor, she answered "sorry i don't know that answer, i don't drink coffee" hmmm what?! how are you a barista with no opinion in coffee because you don't drink it. ouch that was not a good thing. my boyfriend eventually got a coffee that was not nearly as strong as hoped.
my drink is always made exactly the way i want it! i also like their selection of desserts as some locations do not offer such variety :-)

i'm not the biggest fan of starbucks coffee, but it's fast, convenient, and there are locations everywhere, so i opt for starbucks somewhat regularly. this particular location isn't my favorite, but it is closest to my house. the parking lot is tight and kind of a pain to navigate, and there's often a long line at the drive -thru that spills out into broadway, which is a little bit terrifying. it means that you can't even get into the broadway driveway from around 7 to 9 am. the baristas are generally students and although there are some helpful ones, some of them seem like they can't be bothered. i ordered an iced coffee this morning (which literally involves pouring some pre-made iced coffee into a cup and topping it with a lid). the guy who rang me up put my cup on the counter and it just sat there while the three baristas on duty chatted in a circle for two minutes until i spoke up. i got a weak "oh, sorry" from one and the other two laughed. not impressed.

came here on a friday night. not terribly busy. the staffs are busy talking among themselves so the service is slow and chaotic. no thank yous no hellos. drinks are meh. nice location. decent number of seats. tables are mostly kept clean.
this may be the worlds slowest starbucks.... 7 baristas on the floor and i've been waiting 15 minutes for an iced tea...

nothing like going to starbucks and waiting 40 plus minutes for 2 cold brews to only see a 4th employee (army print crew neck) come out of the back and distract the other 3 workers (who were working hard).

i ordered my coffee. i was the only one at the drive through. when i got to the drive through window, it was closed shut, which was strange because i'm use to starbucks employees greeting you at the drive through window. i didn't think anything of it. i waited for my order. the flamboyant young caucasian man greeted me and apologized for the wait, other young caucasian female employees were in the back laughing, i paid and received my coffee. again, i didn't think anything of it. i didn't finish my coffee and poured out the rest before throwing the cup in the trash. in the bottom of my cup was a bug. if you're asian, do not get your coffee from here. these starbucks employees were definitely targeting and being ignorant.

the baristas here are outstanding! they're friendly and make grabbing coffee here very pleasant. and yet the parking is so atrociously awful i often drive to this location, see that there is no parking, and then head somewhere else for coffee. there is no place to wait for a spot, and i've even been double parked into a spot before. it's just a disaster

service was good but i ordered a tall latte and it tasted like a cup of warm milk. there was only the slightest hint of coffee. i know a latte has a lot of milk but i have ordered lattes at other starbucks and have never had one this bad. i updated my review from a 1 to a 3 because the staff fixed my drink without hesitation when i voiced my opinion of the drink.
best starbucks i've been to in a long time! so cute and everyone is so nice and knows exactly what they're doing!

pulled in through drive through, gave my order drove around and my drink was alreaaadyyy ready. literally in 30 seconds, wo supppper fast! i tipped well because let's face it, this process usually takes no less than 15 min.
we mobile ordered and our food and drinks were ready asap. this location is so nicely kept. the staff is always great.

it's starbucks, but anything in cwe has to carry its own swagger, and this little spot, despite being near coffee cartel, manages to squeeze together plenty of seating in a small spot and has some great service. it's also conveniently across from the chess club and right next to the chess hall of fame.

the employees at this starbucks are awful. simple requests made drinks worse. all i ask was for to shake the drink.. one dude said he did when clearly the green tea sat on the bottom of the cup. and the girl dumped out the drink out of anger, and remade a drink that was no bueno.

one thing i know about starbucks in this location is that there a lot of mistakes with order. i ordered iced green tea latte but instead they gave me a hot green tea latte. last time i ordered tall iced white chocolate mocha but instead they gave me a different size, which is grande. great place to study, but i don't recommend ordering coffee at this place.

this new drive in location is beautiful with marble countertops and a sleek modern vibe throughout. they do still seem to have some of the same issues as they did across the street where your order is not always correct and the employees are not friendly. i ordered almond milk in my decaf and got regular. the employee who waited on me didn't say thank you or even look at me during our transaction. not your typical starbucks.
great staff all around in the evenings. very patient with the toughest customers. thank you for an enjoyable evening.

nice location inside of the hilton hotel on clearwater beach. that is the only good. women at the register did not know what was included in certain beverages, and when i received my brown sugar oat milk shaken espresso, it was made incorrect. after notifying an employee about my beverage having zero oat milk or brown sugar, an employee with a nose piercing that was rude with an attitude, insisted that it was made the correct way. so in the end i paid nearly 7$ for a drink that is normally 4$ (quad espresso on ice). look elsewhere for coffee in clearwater.

believe it or not this is my second visit to this starbucks that is within a quarter mile from where i have called home for a few years. not a coincidence, i have only been in any starbucks a handful of times, business meetings and a couple times meeting friends. why... i flew at least 4 times each week during a long portion of my career with waste management, usair, american and united were serving starbucks coffee onboard every flight, flying first class i was never far from those brewers cranking out coffee pre flight and again during. i tried it a couple times. after all i have volunteered and worked at rescue squads and at trash companies where the pot and brewer were often dirty and the brew sat in the pot for many hours (the wawa brewmasters just seized) so i have had some non gourmet cups. maybe it was the fact that the lavatory drain pipe and the fresh water fill pipe are only inch's apart and the same gloved hand connects the clean and the dirty when the airplane is serviced between every flight. all this considered i did actually like the blonde roast at starbucks and always had good experiences. this visit is my first to review one and at 11 am no blonde roast available while sitting at least 4 guests asked for it and were told we don't sell to much of that. my pike place cup was good and service was good. i did see it obvious that drive thru was a priority in service many customers waited minutes to be served as drive thru flew thru. it was a rainy day but the bathroom was dirtier then you would expect and a very detailed cleaning obviously hasn't been done in a while corners of the floors and such. it was well stocked and in general it is a nicely appointed store. the traffic flow around the store is weird the two store stop mall should be facing the side or toward the main strip of stores. the sidewalk is too narrow to even walk on outside the fence out front. very small interior store but very large restrooms.

we are here every saturday morning. today we had the misfortune of coming when the manager mark was manning the order counter - truly, one of the most unpleasant people i've ever encountered. he went off the rails when i asked him to warm up a cream cheese muffin just a tiny bit, something they have done at every other starbucks the many times i have ordered this. it's one thing to nicely say "sorry, i can't do that" but he was loud, belligerent and incredible rude. other guests were staring. we won't come again when he is here! (one of the other employees quietly apologized to me for mark's behavior!)

consistently terrible service. a few weeks ago i placed a mobile order consisting of a few cold items from the case and a warm sandwich. the whole point of placing a mobile order is that so you can just run in and grab it quickly. the only thing that they placed on the pick up counter for me was my sandwich(which was hard like they used one this someone didn't pick up instead of making a new one because i was there only a few minutes after placing the order) and my drink. i asked the shift supervisor where the rest of my order was and she said yeah it's in the case just grab it.i have never had a starbucks make me collect all of my mobile items. then when i asked for a bag she acted like it was the end of the world. then today i went to pick up my mobile order through the drive thru because i had a little extra time and when i got to the window they didn't even know what my drink order was.. but they had my food? so? after telling them what my drink was they made it incorrectly not once but twice and the girl must have thought i drove away the first time she handed me the drink because she came back tot he window and said sorry for the wait the person in front of you was complaining about everything. wow... maybe you should verify which customers at the window before you open your mouth. someone made my drink incorrectly twice and i'm the burden? would never go here again if it wasn't the closest one to my work.

i worked at starbucks for close to four years, and that was across 3 stores. this one, without a doubt, takes the pillars and core values that are giving to us at starbucks into heart. the people work like a team, and solve problems like a team. they always have a welcoming smile and a warm hello waiting for you. not once has our order been wrong, or our food cold. even at peak time the service was fantastic, never having waited for more than 5 minutes. i wish my store was half as good as this one.

i couldn't give this place one star because i normally get decent service here. i am not sure if this is a holiday staff issue or what, but they were way off today. the line was not super long, but it definitely was not being managed well... by the time i finally got to the front of the line, i was looking at the cashier's back. there was no acknowledgement of me at all. i know the cashiers are generally charged with the task of preparing the baked goods, so i was prepared to let this slide. either way, it would have been nice to get a smile, a "hi, sorry can you give me just a second...?" or something along those lines. but i got her back. then another employee comes to the cash register. i didn't assume she was taking over and she wasn't... she was just clocking in or out i believe. either way, she also did not offer any type of acknowledgement to me, the customer, and when she was done with whatever she was doing at the register, she also turned her back to me a proceeded to do something other than provide customer service. then the two of them started talking about topics that had nothing to do with their job, which would have been fine, had they offered some level of customer service. there were two other young ladies working on what looked like the drive thru orders since they were at the other end of the bar. although they offered a few glances my way, they did not smile or speak to me or any of the other customers who were standing in a line that was really starting to build up at this point. i took a few minutes to take in what was happening, then i gathered my things and left. i am sure the two young ladies are completely oblivious to the fact that they just lost a customer because they didn't acknowledge me in the first place.

*no drive through, the closest starbucks with one is a few miles south at 10002 n dale mabry hwy. i frequent this starbucks to sit and get work done. the staff is always friendly. it may take a bit to get a table inside on which to work, they only have six tables: 2 four-seater high tops, 2 four-seater regular height tables and 2 two-seater regular height tables (i'm here right now lol.). they have outdoor seating on two sides of the building and all of it is under an awning. i also put in a fair amount of orders here through the mobile app. unfortunately they don't have a drive through and since this location is in a strip plaza parking may be a tad challenging if you're trying to just run in and grab a mobile order drink from the designated counter area for them. it's not that bad though. the bathrooms have been clean here every time i've used them. well, the mens'. can't speak for the womens' (would i tell you if i could?). overall a solid 4/5 starbucks. a drive through would probably make it 5/5 but short of a location change it ain't gonna happen.

great service at this location. it's the usual fare but the menu is better detailed and the service polite, pleasant, and not perfunctory. it has a drive through and on this sunday afternoon, the line was six or seven deep. there are plant of outside tables but you are on busy route 38 so i'm not sure how relaxing that would be.
i still and will always say starbucks is overpriced and over rated, but the coffee is still good. the people that work here care about their jobs and are super nice.

just now (sitting in parking lot) was here. walked in, no line stand by the register and wait to place my order. cashier (shorter brownish hair) is having a conversation and completely ignores me then turns to me half smiles and finishes her conversation before bothering to take my order. (sorry to interrupt). i pay with card and she doesn't give me my receipt. i ask for it and she says "oh do you want your receipt?" um yes ... so she hands it to me like i'm being a bother. what ever happened to customer service?

i was in a really desperate place mentally this morning as i pulled into the drive thru of this starbucks. the encouraging signs that read "you are enough," "you are worthy," and "be kind" paired with the friendly voice through the speaker made all the difference in my day. thank you for putting so much kindness into the world. even though you don't always see it, it helps people. 8/19/2021

i was originally going to keep this short & sweet. you know, an espresso with 2 lumps of sugar and 2 stars. then i got to thinking about starbucks. they were the originators of the chain specialty coffee shops. they have grown by leaps and bounds and managed to make money. they are a fortune 100 company that has stayed independent, with out being franchised. new opportunities are available for having a starbucks, inside a business, hotel or hospital. i think starbucks is grossly overpriced. why do i say that? if i order an espresso in a fine dining restaurant, i pay that and more. i am unusual in the fact that i love coffee, good strong colombian coffee, period. i make that at home, so i do not see the sense in buying it out. i also have an espresso machine. i am not into lattes or cappuccino's. they have a new line of hot chocolates, that are sure to be a hit. i have had the opportunity to try many of their sandwiches, which come into the starbucks, already made. the ones i have had are very good. i also love their breads. the multi grain berry bread with egg salad is exceptional. their muffins are wonderful. but, they're still overpriced! .

i have been to this location a few times..all i can say is when it is crowded u risk the chance of being cheated.. i ordered a frapp.. large.. my mistake, i drove off should have gone back..was pressed for time.. ended up with nothing but chocolate syrup and ice!! .almost 6 dollars wasted!!! because they want to rush your drink!!

update: this is literally the worst starbucks ever with the absolute most awful staff to exist. i just waited in a 4 person line for 10 minutes watching an employee use his bare hands to maneuver pastries. save yourself your time and money and go to united by blue for better coffee and nicer people.

it's always busy which doesn't bother me, however service over the years as gone down. yesterday i went in to pickup a mobile order and i saw the man put non fat milk in my drink that was supposed to have almond milk. i informed the barista who looked annoyed and said i was lying. her co worker who put the milk in told her i was right and he did put the wrong milk in. the manager i assume was sitting on the bar side and went back over to help them and re make my drink. i am just frustrated by the lack of an i'm sorry we will remake it no problem. if i am paying $10 for a coffee it should be made correctly. this location is close to my night class and work or i'd honestly go elsewhere.

everyone that works here is very friendly. i love coming in in the morning to get my morning coffee and sometimes a nice pastry item. i spent a lot of time here at night to working on my homework and everyone, no matter what shift, is always so friendly and eager to be helpful. this starbucks is always clean. it is a little small but there are plenty of places to plug in your laptop if you need to.

what a shining example of how a starbucks should be ran. 2 days in a row, the drive through was quick and the people friendly. one girl started up a quick friendly conversation while we waited for our coffee at the window. super sweet girl (i didn't catch her name, but she has a couple tattoos on her lower arms). i truly wish all starbucks operates this efficiently and this friendly. thanks!

i'm not sure if this is the one that is in the terminal, but if it is samantha was so incredibly sweet and patient and took the time to make me a decaf right before closing, even though her colleague was rude enough to ask right in front of me whether not they had an of time before closing... when they still have plenty of customers left in line that they needed to attend to. just as a sidenote, i had already stopped by earlier to ask for one and said i would come back closer to when i boarded thank you for being so sweet and having such great customer service, the coffee was awesome!

this is my main starbucks! i try to go here every morning. sometimes i'll do order ahead and i've never had a problem! they are typically always busy . i will say i've had hit or miss days with my drink but i am also keto so i get over it if it's not perfect. but 4/5 mornings i go it's usually super good and just what i need to get through my day

this is our second time here in two days. to best describe it layout wise, it's long and narrow. the whole layout of the prep and kitchen area is a lot different from any starbucks i've been too. yesterday, when we came at 10am, they were struggling to stay afloat with the massive drive thru lane and the line inside. we had ordered a bacon gouda sandwich and were told after ordering that they didn't have any left. we had also ordered a warm plain croissant and when my niece bit into it, it was still frozen in the middle. so she was gifted a chocolate croissant to make up for it. today, we ordered 2 bacon gouda sandwiches, mocha, java chip frappuccino, strawberry green tea & a passion fruit lemonade. they were quick and efficient and got everything right. they didn't have any passion fruit for the passion fruit lemonade so we opted for strawberry instead.

friendly staff. very open. felt very safe. they delivered it with smiling eyes. unfortunately they were out of oat milk so i couldn't do half coconut milk half oat milk it's great they are offering more vegetarian options. i will be really excited to see it completely vegan breakfast sandwich someday.

went in for a coffee, while there were 3 people behind the counter, besides the drive thru person, i was left standing there for over 3 min. if you are going to stand in a circle engaged in conversation while clearly seeing a patron waiting then i will drive out of my way to fenton, never an issue there.
slow slow slow, never enough people working, brewed coffee never ready in the am. they really need to get it fixed here. the location is great but corporate needs to get on the manager

the hot americano's (extra shot) here do not taste the same as a corporate starbucks. as an example, compared to the circle center monument corporate starbucks store. i'm unsure if it's the machine type that does not properly brew the espresso beans, or the bean they're using? i can tell you this though, the machines they're using is not the same as a corporate store! suggest a little extra 10 min. walk to this store. on 55 monument cir., indianapolis, in 46204.

i'm a starbucks fan. while i've never been a huge coffee drinker, when starbucks arrived in town that all changed. yes, i know they're a tad overpriced. yes, i know the coffee can be a bit bigger at times (though i always think this comment is typical of someone who hasn't really experienced the variety that starbucks offers). i don't care. i'm a starbucks fan. i'm a fan of the coffee. i'm a fan of the culture. i'm a fan of their devotion to customer service. i'm a fan of their recent decision to pay for college for employees. this particular location, however, is not my favorite starbucks location in indianapolis. while they can still be mighty friendly at times, especially in the morning, it seems like far too often i've either experienced indifference or employees that lack the knowledge i usually associate with well-trained starbucks employees. as a member of their "gold club," which you can equate to a frequent flyer in their rewards program, i'm fairly knowledgeable about their offerings and i expect their employees to know more than me. at this location, that doesn't always happen as was the case during my visit this evening. i arrived to take advantage of a reward that i had received called a "lucky dozen." the starbucks website encourages you to tell the barista you want to use your "lucky dozen bonus" so they'll know to look on your card and use it. when i announced this to the barista this evening, i got a puzzled look and a "what?" while eventually a supervisor did get things figured out and it did work out, this isn't the first time at this location that i've had difficulty receiving rewards. as an added bonus (sarcasm noted), i also received the wrong receipt. as a chain, i'm a starbucks fan. in the case of the starbucks at 56th & emerson, it unfortunately drops down to a "like" with occasionally great service, occasionally indifferent service, and a lack of knowledge that puts a damper on the usual warm and friendly starbucks customer service that keeps me coming back.

called at 5:59 a.m. to order box of coffee to go along with pastries for a board meeting. (i actually was only going to order the coffee but the associate asked if i wanted to add pastries--yes!) my order was taken by a pleasant and helpful young woman named emily. it took all of two minutes. awesome!! the dunkin donuts across the road won't even answer its phone or return calls--so it's starbucks from now on.
slow service , not enough parking. need another register and to always attend both registers. on average, it takes 12min for a small pike - should never take that long!

bonus points to this starbucks for being located inside of my hotel (new orleans marriott). i was able to visit each day to get my daily iced green tea craving satisfied. the cool thing about this location is that the barista told me i could bring my cup back for 60 cent refills! score! i was ready to take complete advantage of this until housekeeping tossed my cup out while i was out of the room. grrrr!
where is grant in the mornings!!!! my friend and i love him and he's never there when we go anymore:(:(:(:(:( grant if you're reading this switch your schedules back to the mornings please

i have been here 15 times since i fly in and out all the time, and this is, by far, the worst starbucks ever. one time they are out of what i want, one time they argue that they can't give me the drink i want (even i get it everywhere else), today they charged me for some other drink which cost $2 more. it's truly amazing how bad they can always be.
this location is awful. you have to double check everything in the drive through. these people could fuck up a wet dream. if you want sbux, find a different location.

i would visit the old location at least twice a week. the staff there was great! they were friendly and always helpful. most of the drinks i ordered were made perfect. however, i have had quite a different experience at their new location. i often feel like i am bothering the staff when i place an order (even if it is a simple as straight coffee). most seldom smile and as mentioned in previous post, they mock customers or other employees. i have had positive visits with a couple employees that still work there from their old location. not a go to place anymore. it's worth the drive to main street lansdale location where the staff there has always been great!
the brownsburg starbucks is my favorite! not only is the coffee and the food great, but the service is amazing! i always feel like i am being greeted by friends! such a friendly crew!

one of my "regular" starbucks locations. i've used both the drive-thru and counter service and each time my order has been correct and prepared quickly, the service is friendly and helpful, and the location is clean and welcoming. also - mad props to my brother in unusually colored car love who was making eyes at my gorgeous celestial blue mazda (my car knows he' hot and enjoys the attention). rock on, sir! why be beige/silver/white/back/gold when you can be electric blue or metallic purple or coppery orange?
the coffee was good but i was waiting for 15 minutes for food i ordered a half hour before i came

love this sb location. my favorite part is seeing my baristas and getting my happy greetings from them! love michelle, nick, brady, hailey and several others. i hate that i can't recall all their names. my drinks are always made quickly and accurately!!

how hard is it to take an egg off of a pre made sandwich? must be extremely mind blowing hard. out of the 15 to 20 times i've ordered this sandwich they have managed to screw it up multiple times. now i will admit it falls on me to check my order before i drive away. but if i ask the person accepting my cash if there is an egg on it and you tell me no. i do not want to be surprised by an egg that i did not ask for. this is not easter and i am not a child. panera will now get my business at least i can get what i asked for and it is correct!!!

i've been to many a starbucks in my time, so i have quite a bit to compare a visit to. the service in here was great, a tad slow, but okay nonetheless. the issue i had with this trip was my visit to the bathroom. after i got my key from one of the baristas, i opened the door to see the nastiest bathroom. there was toliet paper all over the floor, the trash can was overflowing, and the whole place had an air of dirtiness. i felt like i needed to sanitize my skin after using that bathroom. gross!

this area needed a reliable starbucks! it's got the perfect atmosphere to sit down and do work without being too overwhelmed. the whole team always does an excellent job preparing our drinks. maddie, zac, and emily are always there when i'm there and they are really friendly, provide great customer service, and know my drink by heart! keep it up guys!

we like coming to starbucks. we buy our coffee here for at home, so when we feel lazy, drive thru here we come. good service, helpful and inside always has a nice vibe to it. always great in our opinion. and we love the dark roast blends. can't remember what we bought last time, but its been our morning coffee.

this starbucks location has simply become more and more infuriating. i like coming here after i do laundry to watch some netflix or surf the internet during loads....unfortunately the internet connection at this location is such an embarrasment, i am very surprised that no other reviews have mentioned it. there might well be no internet - i can't watch netflix, it takes 60 seconds to load a standard website, videos don't work, its absolutely pathetic. i am actually writing this review from the pj's next door. perhaps nobody else has complained? when i look around i see everyone on their computers in word documents but nobody trying to actually use the wi-fi so perhaps that could be why - but to the owners, simply pay for more/better bandwith, it's worth it despite your obvious conclusion that somehow its not. i will not be returning unless the internet actually works. village coffee on jefferson also never has this problem ,but again i'd love to come back since everything about this starbucks is great but for the nonworking internet.
the worst. can i do zero stars? orders take forever to fulfill. they are almost always out of certain pastries and cake pops.

best place to grab a coffee in chestnut hill. im not sure why butt hurt drama queen liberal from good old flint,mi is so upset about @erica a. but she needs to go back to her home state. the staff is super friendly and show they do care about their customers. would highly recomend stopping buy while you window shop in lovely little chestnut hill area. #phillylove #starbucksrocks

i am downgrading this store because for whatever reason the management cannot figure how to keep it clean during busy times. the response "we just got slammed" is no excuse. i like most of the staff, and they work hard and are friendly, but the store has to be kept clean.
they plated my cheese danish - the first of 8 starbucks in the area to do that. great service and very friendly people.

stopped in on a sunday afternoon out of sheer boredom. service is friendly and accurate but the tables need to continually be cleaned. someone was sweeping and cleaning when we first arrived but within 15 minutes nearly all tables needed a good wipe down. as a tip, the new chili mocha is really tasty. very happy i tried it.

they dont listen to customers. this was my go to starbucks for so long. i go here multiple times a week. they have been messing up my order for so long now but i keep giving them a chance because i think they will get it right. i only get an iced coffee with 2 pumps of white mocha.... how hard can it be? today the barista decided to add 4 pumps or maybe more. usually when i ask for 2 pumps of anything it says it on the sticker but not today. there is literally a mountain of white mocha because they decided they wanted to add more. can't even drink it now because it is way too sweet.. listen to your damn customers!!!!

meh, way too busy. i go there when i don't want to get out of my car and go into a good coffee shop. i either get really nice baristas or the complete opposite. good place when your craving a sugary coffee beverage.

i live nearby so this starbucks is very convenient to where i live. most of the baristas know me by name which is a nice personal touch. i'm in every morning to get coffee, sometimes a paper, rarely something to eat other than a banana, petite scone or biscotti because i'm watching my diet, lol! the pour-overs here, which i often get, think half caff and decaf pour overs, they're great and far superior to 16/walnut! having come here for a long time, i've gotten to know various baristas and i really do miss david, he along with other baristas are often very friendly, which is so nice. i also miss josh who left last year! john, the manager gave me lots of free boxes when i moved a few blocks and i really appreciated that! i bought a few starbucks gift cards for christmas gifts which were real hits! i'll be back tomorrow morning! :-)
i don't like the borritos because mcdonalds has better donuts

the most charming (i.e., least corporate) of the area starbucks thanks to a repurposed interesting building and a trendy neighborhood location. it needs more seating and easier parking, but it's still the most pleasant starbucks in new orleans.

the building is nice. the baristas are kind. furthermore, there is n't a plethora of people so if you want to get work done then definetly come to this starbucks. it isn't as popular since the storm because it took a while for it to get back into its regular hours.

i love starbucks. a lot. but this drive through on the weekends is so incredibly slow. i don't know if they're understaffed but sometimes a car won't come out of the drive through for over 5 minutes. sometimes it seems almost 10.

it takes a lot for me to give a starbucks a low score but this location is not my favorite. i ran into an issue where one of the drinks i ordered required "steamed lemonade" the barista was very rude when replying that "we don't steam lemonade" and when i asked why he simply asked the manager. the manager stated it was a "health code" violation and stated they couldn't make my drink. they didn't offer to sub the lemonade for anything or even to not steam the lemonade, they just moved on to the next customer. i contacted their customer service and they said there's no reason the lemonade couldn't be steam and apologized profusely for the situation. to this day i still avoid this location, it's worth it to drive further down the road.
located in a strip mall in montecito it's a larger sbx - the emplyees are genuinely nicew and the clients are kooky!

i opted to go inside this starbucks location today instead of going through the drive thru since the line was so long. it was during happy hour and bogo handcrafted beverage. i purchased two grande irish creme nitro cold brews ------ delicious! sometimes they are better than others and today they tasted superb! i also got a brownie for 50 points. this location has been remodeled (i believe) and it is very nice inside. there were many seats & tables available. parking was sparse as is typical with most of the starbucks i have been to. the baristas today were extremely friendly.

spanking new starbucks, open less the a week. it has everything you expect from a starbucks, and that's good... people are friendly and helpful coffee is good, cold brew is on tap..... big store with plenty of seating both inside and outdoors they have a drive thru as well...

really love the location of this starbucks. decided to switch up my usual by trying the vanilla sweet cream cold brew. i definitely would prefer the coconut cold brew over the vanilla! this starbucks is pretty open and always has a moderate to smaller crowd. if the line is backed up here their is another starbucks walking distance in the market district grocery store across the way!

horrible service!!! lack of communication! i went to this location today (the day after christmas) and was surprised that there was only one person in line as this location is usually always busy. i waited patiently as the woman in front of me was asking a bunch of questions about the syrups and sugar free items. i glanced at the board and noticed a huge sign advertising a free drink when you sign up on the starbucks rewards app and register your card. i waited until the woman in front of me finished her order and when it was my turn i asked the cashier about the advertisement. i asked, "if i sign up right now, will i receive a free drink?" the cashier acted as if she didn't know what i was talking about so i pointed at the sign behind her and stated my question again. again, she had a puzzled look on her face, then another worker chimed in and stated that i would receive an email of some sort with a coupon. i said, "okay, i'll step aside and register now." as i sat in a chair right by the register i noticed a young woman and a homeless man. they were talking to one another, and to quickly sum up their conversation, she was in there to buy him (the homeless man) a drink and a sandwich; which i thought was a great gesture for someone in need while most people are celebrating the holidays. the young woman ordered the drink and food and i hear her tell the man that everything is paid for and that the worker would call him when it's done. she left the store after that. i'm pretty much done registering my card on the app and waiting for an email with my coupon. as i wait, one of the workers yells a woman's name and is holding a food bag. i just observe for the moment and say to myself "that was clearly the girl's who bought the sandwich for the homeless man." i began to feel a little antsy because it started getting crowded with people and i still had no email. i then hear the homeless man (he was speaking loudly every time he spoke) he's asking a cashier about his drink and food. mind you, there are now two new cashiers at the register. i see the situation escalating because the cashier explains that he did not ring the man up and he would have to wait a second (as he was ringing another customer). the homeless man just says "what do you mean? it's already been paid for. the girl, the girl bought it for me! a hot chocolate and a sandwich." i no longer could just stand by. i got up from the chair and i asked the male cashier where the female cashier went who was just on the registers. i said "i saw everything, a young woman was in here and she bought him food and left!" and as i'm explaining this the female cashier peeks her head around the corner and i point stating "she rung her up" (as i did this, i see the food and sandwich sitting off to the side together, unclaimed). i was just fed up, i yelled "there's the food right there! what is the problem? why are y'all not communicating? this is horrible service, i'm leaving". so i left, with no drink and no further explanation of this supposedly free drink they were advertising. i hope that man was able to get his drink and sandwich, he clearly needed it. it's really a shame that the young woman took the time to help him, trying to do a good deed for someone, and the workers there just totally ruined everything. i will never return to this location, it's not the first time i've had a bad experience.
great staff in a very busy part of town. good job sbux.

i have to strongly disagree with the other negative reviews of this starbucks. we are frequent customers here and of the dozens upon dozens of drinks i've had here i've never once had one wrongly prepared. unlike many starbucks, this place never burns the milk and never fills up a significant portion of your cup with foam even if you order a latte with no foam. i've ordered numerous special requests and my drinks have always been prepared properly and quickly. service here has always been great as well, with the employees all quite friendly. honestly, this starbucks is much better than the standalone one across mccarran, so if you need coffee go here, not there!

one of the best things that this starbucks has going for it is its drive through and friendly service. it's fast and efficient. depending on how crowded it gets, the line can get ridiculous because of how the traffic can flow into the strip mall that this starbucks is located in, which can be a pain to get into if you happen to be on the opposite side of the street and trying to cross traffic. if you chose to go inside, parking can be tight, especially during the the morning. if there is no parking in front, you can park behind the store itself and walk a little. the inside has a few tables to sit, but space is at a premium, especially when crowded. the line for orders can sometime merge together. when the weather is nice, there is a small patio with a few tables and chairs that allow you to watch pendleton pike traffic while enjoying your caffeine.

i love that they have a drive thru and the loughborough commons starbucks and i like the bistro sandwiches. but, they are always out of stock on menu supplies. very inconsistent in regards to inventory at this location.

not the greatest service at this location, particularly in the drive through. i just went through the drive through, and the cashier not only got my order wrong, she then gave me change for a $20 instead of the $50 i had given her. when i pointed that out and in a nice way asked for the rest of my change, she had attitude with me. she then gave me $5 too much change. as i hesitated for a few moments before driving away so i could double check the amount and give her back the extra, she opened the window and quite rudely asked twice "what do you need now?" i should have just said "nothing" and let her discover her mistake the hard way, but i didn't and still gave it back to her. unfortunately, this is not my first such experience at this location.

this is an awesome starbucks. huge and spacious. this is the one at 700 canal where the st. charles streetcar turns from canal onto st. charles avenue. it is lakeside of rubenstein's clothiers. enter by the front corner doors. the st. charles side is for exiting only. the line forms by the case where all the delicious pastries and such are. the staff and baristas are amazing and awesome. this is generally the location i visit most frequently. i love my starbucks!!!

service sucks, came here multiple times and was constantly disappointed. this time the lady seemed to get frustrated with me because of how i was pronouncing the word "acai". she bluntly corrected me which was annoying. at least be nice about it. another time, i got an iced caramel macchiato. it was absolutely horrible. the barista probably was drizzling the caramel in my drink for a good 15 seconds. i would sip clumps of caramel....it was so bad
fast, clean, not over crowded, professional area, lots of outdoor seating and plush chairs inside. great for business meetings.

if you've been to one starbucks, you've pretty much been to them all. that said this starbucks has some of the friendliest staff, and plenty of room to sit and grab a drink, work on your computer, read a book or even people watch :)

have been here a number of times, and not overly impressed. the staff usually seems to not know much about their own products and my drinks get messed up often. i would rather go to a local place instead!

i never thought to do a review for a chain like this, but i've come to really appreciate the quality this store offers. the employees are always pleasant, and orders are made swiftly and correctly. i had gotten used to the quality at the location on valrico & 60 which is... subpar. i now drive the extra 15 minutes to go to this location instead because the experience is unparalleled!

loved this location, it is such a neat building and in such a neat area. service was great and the atmosphere was fantastic. wish all starbucks were modeled after this one in terms of the layout of the inside and the design of the building. super neat place

love starbucks. just not this one. my wife went there last night through drive thru. she asked for sugar, they said they were out of white sugar. brought it home to me, thank you sweetie, and it tasted like warm, milky water. i called, deema said ok to get new one......tomorrow. i went back today, still no white sugar. i parked, went inside, and there was plenty of baggies of white sugar. is laziness or lying sop for a. $5.00 cup of coffee? cmon people, you can do better.

the drive-thru line at this location is always long, but because i expect it at this location and know to give myself the extra 10-12 minutes that part is irrelevant at this point. after waiting a decent amount of time just to get to order a female in an annoyed tone says "what can i get you?" now it may be petty of me, but at every other starbucks they usually say "welcome to starbucks what can i get you?" or "how are you? what can i get you?".. i felt very rushed to give my order & i barely had time to finish ordering my last drink when a total was being given.. i apologized and asked her if i could change the size on the last one and she recited the total. i verified that she heard my size change and apologized again and once again a very annoyed "yes" thru the mic and that was it. i get up to the window and a girl with a matching voice and name tag that said "cassie" is at the window.. once again no customer service, no nothing. recites my total, scans my phone and closes the window without a word being said. i wanted to use a reward (which had she not just scanned and closed the window i would've had the chance to tell her the first time). she opens the window, hands me a drink to which i explain i wanted to use a reward and she says she needs to rescan my phone. hands me the other 2 drinks and closes the window again.. still with no words exchanged. i didn't even get the chance to tell her one of our drinks was the wrong size and i was so disappointed and frustrated it never got fixed. i get that it can get busy and hectic, but this is the only location i chronically have customer service issues with, but this experience was worse than normal. if this location wasn't a block away and most convenient with having a newborn i wouldn't even go to this one. and as a matter of fact.. i feel that after this experience i'll be going back to other coffee shops that appreciate their loyal customers. unfortunately, i know this review and experience won't be address, but maybe by reading it you can save yourself the long wait (inside and drive they) and the snobby experience.
they have the nicest workers, i didn't know how to order my coffee an what lingo to use ,and all workers helped & found me my perfect cup

lovely new brentwood location. easy access off hwy 64. in the lower plaza next to cyclebar. drive thru available, outdoor patio and plenty of seating inside. staff is decent but could certainly use a little spunk & a few smiles to go with a morning cup of joe. merchandise, snacks, nice background music and most importantly caffeinated beverages vailable. therefore i'll likely return at some point. ~christy
this starbucks is the best! kaitlyn rocks it out and makes some mmmm mmmm good coffee! hazel dell crew, salute!

i like starbucks...i am not a die hard fanatic for coffee with a lot of calories, and tend to prefer what i make at home, however i do receive gift cards quite frequently and i love it because it's a treat for which i don't normally indulge. this location is close to home, but it is located right in the middle of a busy triangle intersection that has three stop signs that are all pretty much ignored by 95% of those who happen to pass by. there is no drive through, parking can be a bit of a challenge, and it is almost always crowded. the inside is on the smaller side for the amount of traffic this place sees daily. none of this should be attributed to the fast and pleasant baristas and environment you typically expect at a starbucks, but it all adds to the overall experience. i really want a starbucks this close to home, but i wish even more that it were somewhere - anywhere - else than in this specific spot.

stopped by this starbucks before a trip to the dmv. my order was made perfectly and the girls that worked there were nice. the only problem was the cleanliness. i went later in the day so i understand it being a bit messy but it wouldn't hurt to wipe down your tables and check your bathroom every hour or so. i did like that this location had ample outside seating, as well as a counter top seating section. overall the look was very modern. definitely better than other starbucks i frequent.

i don't give many 1-star reviews, but this place is the slowest starbucks...wait, let me rephrase...the slowest coffee shop i've ever been at in my life. cashier/manager was pleasant, but it took fourteen minutes to get through the line as they had one inept barista working

worst sevice ever and i love starbucks. yesterday i went to this location and forgot to order my carmel iced latte with almond milk because i'm lactose intolerant. my fiancé reminded me when i got my drink that i forgot to get my milk alternative and so i apologetically asked the barista to make me a new one because i forgot to order it with almond milk and i get really sick. she starred at me with the drink in her hand for a good minute with the most attitude and asked me how i forget i'm lactose intolerant..... obviously that isn't what i forgot then she made me get in line to pay my $.60 up charge which is fine but the cashier said there was no up charge on milk alternatives for iced coffee, the barista then proceeded to yell no she has to pay. then when i finally go my new drink she didn't even make me a carmel iced coffee!!!! it was straight espresso and almond milk.... no carmel squirt which i'm convinced she did on purpose! i will never be going back there.
this is a wonderful starbucks. only one with a drive thru anywhere nearby. the employees are second to none. especially dan. he's the man! only problem is it's slower than most.

when you have a drive thru line out to the street, you don't sit here hanging out the drive up window socializing with every customer. its discourteous to everyone else in line, especially when people downtown are trying to get to work. this place has numerous other faults as well for such a busy location. they don't fill up the drinks all the way and they've forgotten my food order inside twice so i had to wait double the time when it was already way too long. i'm done with this place. there's so many other good coffee shops in town to give my business to.

probably the biggest starbucks in the touristy area of new orleans . stop by at night to get their special new orleans mug as a souvenir. be prepared for a lot of homeless people, slow and confused service, i paid for my mug and the guy forgot to give it to me and walked away. there's trash everywhere on the floor and on the condiment bar. for a upscale starbucks they need to step this up.

the starbucks on the corner of 109 st. and jasper ave is a busy place, both in the restaurant and outside the window. just a short hop from the alberta legislature, i'm sure this starbucks is popular with government types. it's also quite close by another government employee haunt, ricky's grill. the ambience is not too bad. it's nice and bright, with windows going up 15 feet. no worries about a dark, dingy space here. all in all, it's a pretty solid location. if you're worried about noise, plug in your ipod and pretend the rest of the world doesn't exist. it's fairly laptop friendly here, but there are no large tables, so workspace is a bit limited. i took transit here, but the parking lot is free, but limited to 90 minutes. i pushed it when i parked the other day, but i don't want the hassle like i had at northgate. impark runs the lot. there is some outdoor seating available in the summer months, but i'm not sure how many seats there are. there's a lot of traffic here, so it'll be noisy! busyness - for the early morning, it's not as busy as i'd figured it might be, but it's not far from grant macewan university and the alberta legislature, so i'm sure it sees a lot of traffic from there and the other office towers close by noisiness - combine a tile floor with hard surfaces, and you have a loud space. it's not too bad while i'm here, but i'm sure when all the tables are filled it can get quite noisy. ambience - it's a comfortable location and not a bad place for a quick meeting. power - i'm sitting tucked away in the back corner where there are power outlets directly under the bench seat. another bench is also suitably equipped. if you're prone to wetting your pants, i wouldn't recommend these seats. free wifi - it is available and worked as advertised. staff - the staff here seem energetic and friendly, as is usually the case. seating - ample seating is available here. the tables are all on the small side, so if you plan on gathering many people for an all-day, starbucks fuelled programming session, find somewhere else, or occupy many tables. the tables are small. the seats aren't too bad. there are two benches with power outlets underneath. there are 31 seats, but the benches can squeeze in more (shower first please!). security - if you've read my other starbucks reviews, you've probably gathered i'm a bit paranoid. you're right. laptop thieves are everywhere! take the appropriate precautions. i'm kind of a fan of going with an inexpensive laptop for public work. while i may like a macbook, i'd have it chained to me in public. could be a fashion statement.

i'm not going to tell you anything about starbucks you don't already know. but i regularly stop into the edmondson pike location, and have never been let down. it's not the sexiest sb i've ever seen, but in my experience the service is good, the coffee is hot (or iced, whatever), and the wi-fi is free. there is plenty of outdoor seating, and the parking lot is huge.
i really like this location. the staff is nice and helpful. i have never had a bad experience and it is a typical starbucks. it is just a normal experience...

its a good 'bucks, a little busy with people at laptops, but i can't complain because i often do work there too. its the place to have your office. they have a few more items than some of the local smaller locations. they also have a drive through.

you always expect it to be bad at an on-campus starbucks but this one might just give others a run for their money. i have had extensive waits whether it be 30 mins for a cold brew that i have preordered through tapingo (without cream, might i add) or for some hot tea which i don't get until the five people in line after me have already received their lattes and frappes. the staff is also very rude and have a lack of understanding. do yourself a favor and skip this location.. order ahead to the library location or take a walk to university instead. anything. is. better. than. this!!!

1 star because i can't give 0. i never rate places negatively. this starbucks is terrible. since this is about the 8th time in the last 2mos i got my very simple, literal 2 ingredient drink (iced coffee, milk, no sugar) made wrong, i made sure said no sweetener like 6 times to the cashier. when she repeated it to me, i said it again. after 30 mins of standing in there, i made the mistake of rushing out and not trying it before i left. do you think there was sugar in it when i finally got it home? the wait times are outrageous and the drinks are consistently wrong. i understand people being overwhelmed and making mistakes. once again i never give bad ratings. but a place selling $4 coffees simply cannot be this bad at coffee.
i can only imagine the lines here with this location between penn and chop. luckily i've been going at off peak hours so i haven't had to wait. lots of seating here and friendly baristas.

i'm a big fan of starbucks, but this particular store m is the only starbucks i go to that doesn't have my mobile waiting for me when i come to the door. everything just takes longer here. not sure why, since they have more baristas than other places.

found a hair in my muffin, when asked to speak to a manager they made me wait twenty mins and when i went up and asked again a man who was standing right beside the girl i asked in the first place who watched me sit and wait said he was the manager and then proceeded to tell me that basically it ws a mistake and what did i really expect him to do. awesome place not.

i stopped at this starbucks in a state of latte-emergency. i desperately needed a caffeine pick me up and this location at 5:30 pm on a friday was nearly empty so i got in and out quickly. staff was friendly and restrooms were clean (enough). no issues. thankful they are here.

the best customer service ever!!! ms.debra, marty, kelly, and greg are awesome!!! everyone knows what i drink and it's ready at checkout! they always greet everyone with a smile and a pleasant greeting!!
love this bucks but the blonde with the red lips has a little bit of an attitude she might need to check....
went for a venti javachip on my lunch break, around 12:45pm on a monday. there was a long line, slow service and they got my order wrong. boo!
great service and the coffee and pastry hit the spot for an early wake up to fly home. the line is long. may be time for a larger starbucks!

i have tried to write this review three different times this morning. this particular starbucks has one particular employee that cannot put the lid on the coffee cups correctly. last friday as she handed it to me the lid came off and i ended up with hot coffee all over my hand all over the outside and inside of my car. needless to say i said some very colorful words. i was not very happy. today which is again friday, and again the lid came off and once again my hand was burned i have a mess in my car. i give up i've never had a starbucks cup lid come off. and to top it all off i asked her at the drive-thru speaker to repeat my order back to me and she informed me that she could not do that because she was getting food for another customer. actually told me that she would remember my order to make it good enough. i don't want you to make it good enough i want her to make it the way i asked her to! i think this young lady needs some serious training. not only on how to put a lid on a coffee cup. she also needs extensive customer service training. the attitude this morning was terrible. i go to the drive up because it's supposed to be quicker apparently not where she comes from. it's really disappointing that one employee can make the entire shop look bad. i may have to reconsider a different coffee shop in the future.

review is based on drive-thru only. the baristas are always super friendly and they always get my order right. the line can be a little long in the morning but everyone's getting their coffee on their way to work so i get it. i don't get starbucks unless i have a giftcard. i have much better coffee and lattes at other local places and i'd rather support a local business if i'm being honest.

of the local bux, this one is great for two reasons: drive-through service and very speedy service (a necessity with a drive through window). the beverages are consistently good, and i have never encountered a surly barista here. i do not find this location terribly hospitable for lingering because the seating is very limited.

every time i go here they made my order with sugar and claim that they don't add any. i'm very very sensitive to sugar and ask every time. i've gotten it from other starbucks correctly. here i always have trouble! the blonde woman is always rude to me also

starbucks is starbucks... unless it's this starbucks. the friendliest, fastest starbucks service around. if nothing else, go just to talk to dan in the drive thru. his energy is so contagious, you may not even need any caffeine!

same great coffee as every other starbucks. the location is great, right smack dab in the middle of suburbia. they recently expanded the dining area which made it nearly double the size it originally was, which is awesome; however, there is still a horrible parking lot situation and drive thru. i often place my order on the app and like to run in and grab my drink off the counter (usually much faster than the drive thru) but the problem is there's never anywhere to park to run inside. it's a shame because i really like this location. the drive thru doesn't help either, it often wraps around the building through the parking lot, only to block more of the parking spaces. i think the solution is building another location on hazel dell, north of 146th st. if anyone from starbucks corp. wants to chat about exactly where i'd like them to build, let me know!
the partners here are just very cool. i will definitely come here whenever i'm in the neighborhood for my yummy venti salted caramel mocha frappuccino! yum!
happy staff. running special during the time i was in st. louis. quick service. will walk the extra blocks to visit this location.

being a non-coffee drinker i have no issues going to starbucks with my friends. they seem to like the coffees and teas. i have been to several starbucks in the past and the food has always been good, i was not impressed with the food here. the service was very good and the people were friendly.

great service wonderful customer service i normally get hot chocolate and banana nut bread today i had the double chocolate chip cookie..warmed up of course. .it was mouth watering and delious.i want more..

excellent service ! such nice and friendly staff. this is the actual way my drink is supposed to come . if you take a look at my last starbucks review my cup was half empty while this being the same order and it is completely full !

staff was very kind and respectful. the only thing is, that the drink i got i barley got any liquid. thats my fault i guess.. either way this place is pretty good and i can tell they care for their costumers that come through to get something delicious. honestly i would come here again thank you!!

i am a lover of starbucks and going on here is always comfortable. it is big enough to sit down and have a nice chat over coffee and efficient enough to quickly run in and grab the drink of your choice. staff is very friendly and when you are a regular they often remember your name and order. every now and again you will find a bad apple but also will find those that go above and beyond to make sure you are a happy customer. if you love starbucks, this is a great location.

computer isn't able to properly process payments through the app. don't ever have problems using my starbucks app but came here two days in a row and suddenly i'm having those issues. don't have problems at elmwood location or at a covington location in which i frequent often. won't be back.

dear starbucks in suburban square, come on. are you really so hard up on cash that you need to charge for wireless? and why are your tables so small--and round, to boot--that they discourage camping out and working all afternoon? i must admit, i'm irritated. i love my frappuccino lights in all of your flavors--i'm a loyal customer, i swear--but i also love coupling said drinks with long afternoons of steady work, preferably in an environment devoid of teenage girls screaming about lip gloss. just saying. love, shawna

i would just like to say a big thank you to every member on staff at the starbucks. thank you for letting me order drinks over the phone when i'm running late, thank you for caring for plus boxes out to my car, and thank you for not judging me when i show up in nothing but my pjs. you're an awesome team and you all rock.

very frustrating waiting for my first cup of coffee in the morning and all 4 ladies behind the starbucks counter in terminal a are more interested in talking to each other than doing their job. once my coffee was finally made the barista didn't even make an attempt to give it to me. she set it on the counter as close to the edge closest to her as possible and then turned away to continue her conversation. i hope they don't actually get too busy this morning because folks could be waiting a while for their coffee and if you're one of those people that need your first cup pronto then you may want to pick it up somewhere else. thankfully i am not! the upside? i am sitting here now in the airport enjoying my caramel machiatto and cinnamon bagel. yum!

i'm from out of town and this was honestly the rudest and worst service ever. they could be less interested in doing anything for you. try not to interrupt their convo by ordering your drink...ridiculous.

this starbucks is in a cute little part of webster groves next to gazebo park, where events and farmers markets are held in summer. it's just like any other starbucks but this is right next to my work so i go there all the time, usually around 2:30-3pm when i hit the afternoon slump. the staff are friendly and always prompt with my order, and they rarely mess it up. my go-to drinks are the white chocolate mocha with non-fat milk and no whipped cream, as well as the java chip frappacino, skinny and without whipped cream. the clientele at this location are a mixture of business people, hippies, young teens (nerinx hall, an all-girls high school is just around the corner), university students (webster university is within walking distance), artists, entrepreneurs, and stay-at-home moms. you really get a mixed bag here. i typically don't sit and stay to drink my lattes, but when i have in the past, the furniture is comfy, with a variety of seating choices for individuals or small groups, and the place is kept clean and orderly. it doesn't wow my pants off, but it maintains the high standard of starbucks that i expect. a solid standby.

most starbucks are fairly standard since it's a chain but this is the one i seem to frequent the most. there's nothing about this starbucks that makes it stand out aside from its location (right on the corner of jasper and 109ave). in the summers they set out patio sets, which makes it a great place to just hang out, have a coffee/drink and just people watch or read. the menu is the same as any other starbucks and they have the same products. the barristas have always been super friendly and willing to fix a drink if there is something wrong. i think the only thing i would change about this starbucks is that i wish it was bigger and had more spaces inside to sit. since its so central, it's usually busy and at peak times it's quite difficult to find somewhere to sit.

this starbucks staff had to be one of the most unorganized and unprofessional staffs that i've ever seen. there was no type of communication amongst themselves or to customers walking in. it is unfortunate because the products we bought were actually well-made. that is not a saving factor for terrible customer service though. i will try to give this place another assessment a little later on to see if they fix this. update: since i've been back, they must have taken a note of the issue because the drinks/sandwiches have been marvelous and the service has been prompt! they've completely turned around my negative experience from previously and i was happy to see their staff has been improving their communication. great job, guys.

this is the slowest starbucks i have ever been to. and i have been to many many starbucks. i have come here twice and waited almost 20 minutes in the drive through. the first time was during the morning rush so i can understand being busy. the second time was a sunday afternoon. not sure what's going on here but something is off at this starbucks.

in general, starbucks' are slow with drive thru service but this one is reallyyyy slow. i think i've been waiting in this line for at least 30 minutes now and it's 9:30pm on a saturday. don't come here if you're in a hurry lol.
this location has greatly improved over the past few months. they always make my order right and the line moves much faster. i appreciate management taking action and improving the service.

ugh!! just ate (breakfast?) at this particular place. i asked to order the sausage sandwich with chicken sausage instead of pork and the clerk looked down her barista nose at me and said she could not possibly do that unless i purchased two sandwiches. well. i discovered why, the sandwiches are pre-made and they just nuke them. i got a mocha ( which is what they are know for right?). double ugh. she must have been pissed at me for asking for a substitution and stuck her bitter finger in it to stir it because i have never had such a bitter cup of coffee in my life. i've been to starbucks before....i even went to starbucks in seattle, so i know what it's suppose to taste like and baby that ain't it!

i have stayed at many hotels in my life. of those hotels, several have had a starbucks inside. but this is the first hotel i have stayed in that had a full service starbucks and that is a nice perk. when people visit new orleans they often have a tendency to overdo the partying. that leads to dehydration, food cravings and the need for a massive influx of caffeine. i am not saying that happened to me, but this starbucks was a very welcome addition to my stay at the hyatt. since it is part of a massive chain, you already know what they have here. the sandwiches are a great snack or meal, and the coffee does contain caffeine. service was friendly, but a bit slow. this could be because of the massive crowds so i cut them some slack, but it does bear mentioning. if you stay at the hyatt, come here. if not, it probably is out of your way and you don't care. move along to my next review.
the workers are very nice but sooooo slow. it is consistently the slowest starbucks i've ever been to.

this is ridiculous. i come to this starbucks all the time and and these past few weeks i've had countless problems. i ordered a pink drink with dragon fruit inclusions and this is what i received. the coconut milk won't mix with the refresher and i got 2 pieces of dragon fruit- literally. the staff used to be very considerate but seriously how do you mess up one of the most popular drinks and still serve it ?!
i owe starbuck's coffee shops for my undergraduate degree. i just moved to town and i really like this one. i love the post-it board and everyone is very friendly. i will going back for sure!

staff is very efficient and know their stuff, the downstairs is a bit crowded but they have a very large upstairs that makes for a great place for a book club or poetry reading. i used to live around the corner from this starbucks and it was the only place i could go, very early in the morning to sit and read or write in peace. the upstairs windows are a great people watching spot for the loony tunes that frequent south street.

bad experience with the cashiers and all here. very rude, careless, loud, just lack of service and no knowledge of service, and overheard making racist jokes and impressions. the drinks they have no knowledge of and carelessly makes it. hire new staff.
the outdoor summer seating just boosted this rating up all the way! great comfy spot to chill and enjoy their coffee! my go to starbucks in the area!
a whole month after a storm and they're still opening and closing at ridiculous and inconvenient times. very poor quality of service even when they are open.

in general the move away from the train station was a very positive one. allowing for a great space to sit and relax or work. ample parking. they now have a drive through and still seem a little "challenged" with that new addition but all in all a great place.

this is by far the worst starbucks that i have ever been to. i tried for the fifth time today to order at this location because i was being optimistic and thought maybe the first four times were duds. every coffee that i have ordered either tastes like milk or water with a splash of coffee. my order came out with a hair in it, and the employees made a new coffee and sincerely apologized. i was very satisfied with the customer service, but once again the drink was not the drink i ordered.

we came in and ordered an americano and the shots felt a little weak to us. julia, the barista, actually was the one to first comment on it before we drank it. she asked us to sip it and see if we liked it. we both agreed it was a little off. she apologized and then she remade us a drink on the house and made our whole day. thank you for the amazing customer service julia. we will be back!!

the good: nice location. outdoor seating is way cool. near the clearview mall. friendly baristas. they have stock, when others don't. near a gang of eateries. love the bar style window seating. the not so good: i find this location to be a little more stuffy than others, stuffy as the patron wise. i don't care for the drive thru, it's space deficient & a little hard to navigate. parking is very limited. easily missed when driving, seems to be just stuck in a 'tiny, oh this will do' space. but i'm glad they had my eggnog latte on tonight... tis the season.

not impressed with the drinks here. the peach green tea lemonade tastes like water, but for $4.05. but the air conditioning and wifi were fine so i'd recommend it as a study spot. next time i'll stick to black coffee though.
i love the location. the staff is friendly! service is good. no complaints at all. it's starbucks quality.
though plenty of staff, few waiting on customers so very slow and rather surly service. they do not actually stock items listed on their menu board.

the staffs are super friendly, i've been coming here for 4 yrs now, they make the coffee right, and quick. just one small problem that it is very hard to find parking in the morning around 8-9:30 am. overall, it is a good place to work, study, meet with friends. they have plenty tables.

i am not a starbucks aficionado but i've been to this location a couple of times. the baristas are very friendly and the noise level isn't too bad -- probably my favorite thing compared to the typical starbucks.

horrible service! rude! make their own rules. they denied a starbucks promotion, even though they are a participating store. corporate should definitely come down and assess these employees and managers!
