nice staff, nice facilities, but sloooo service i have ever received in a starbucks! i ordered a grande iced coffee (read not at all complicated) with no one in front of me and just one guy behind me. there were 4 staff (yes four people matched to two customers) and somehow it took us both 6 minutes to get our drinks. not the end of the world but not very considerate.

gosh darn lousy experience...dirty interior, xtremely slow drive-thru, no smile employees...nine of them behind the counter. what a disaster of an operation and certainly not well managed. tried the drive-thru first and line wasn't moving, so we went inside. chaotic looking scene, with full garbage bin overflowing the counter top of the cream/sugar area, dirty floor, littered table tops. two employees at drive-thru window busy talking to each other, not doing anything?! employee at inside register must have been new. hesitant on questions, taking our order, etc. asked for no whip on cold drink, got about half normal amount on top anyway? all this at 11am on a sunday. we were not impressed. too bad they've got a monopoly here...a little competition would do us all good.

wow- i'm no starbucks expert, but they're usually nice. this one is gross. for starters, upon entering, at least 7 employees were buzzing around, but no one was taking orders. the woman who finally assisted me was very nice & helpful. drink was fine. i needed a space for some computer work. had to put napkins down as a barrier between my computer and the table. the bench to sit on is completely broken, dented in, and uncomfortable. only use the bathroom if you are wearing a hazmat suit. i would definitely not recommend this place unless you use the drive-through.
always friendly, fast service and never a problem with my order - even though there's regularly a line for the drive-through. it's the one starbucks location i know a line shouldn't mean a long wait!

as a weary lonely female traveller i came to this starbucks to get a caffeinated drink to last me on my drive home (about 2 more hrs) and empty my bladder cause i feel safe at starbucks and it was is stated to close at 9:30 according to the app arrived at 9:15 and doors were already locked up and why is that. close at your scheduled hours according to the official app.

i've been here several times. i've found the staff to be very friendly and i have never been disappointed with what they've prepared for me. the free wi-fi is very nice and handy. if you are waiting in line, check out their community bulletin board. just be careful as you pull in and out of the parking lot. it can be alittle tight.

honestly this starbucks is pretty good. for anyone complaining about waiting in line, keep in mind you're on a college campus with like almost 35,000 kids all trying to wake themselves up with coffee. if you're patient, it's fine! also, try going at like 0:30-0:45 time period since most kids get off of class at like the 0:50 time period and that's when lines start to form. also this starbucks is awesome be sure to ask for a receipt for most drinks because if you come back later in the day you can get a refill for only 50 cents!

very rude! use another location!! i have never had more poor service in my life, women working were rolling their eyes as if my order was an issue. customer service is your job!! as a former server for over 7 years this was unacceptable to me! i will be advising everyone i know to use other locations.

we ordered two pumkin cream cold brews and they where really watered-down. after trying to explain to the baristas she said "maybe all the coffee is down in the cup and your just tasting the sweet cream" after i stir it up it still tasted like water and pumkin cream. we informed her about it and she never once offered to switch our drinks or even a refund. so after making us look stupid infront of others customers,we politely left the drinks on the counters and left. we will never go back to this starbucks.

i honestly feel bad for the employees working.. it's 5am on a sunday morning and you only have 2 employees behind the counter? that's ridiculous.. they need to give their baristas more support.. i'm writing this from a line that's 15 deep.. might have to forget about getting my coffee or miss my flight. horrible scheduling on the managers part but the employees that are working this morning are polite and courteous nonetheless so kudos to them.

i normally go to this starbucks before i start my shift at a nearby hospital, so i stopped in last night and had a pretty terrible experience. when i walked in, there were three visible employees: one talking to customers, one making drinks, and one standing and doing seemingly nothing. the store was not busy at all so i was not at all prepared for a poor experience. i was eventually able to place my order and i ended up waiting 15 minutes for an iced coffee. the might not seem like a lot of time but this was close to 7pm on a monday night and i really only had 10 minutes before my shift started i watched the one barista prepare someone else's drink very sluggishly while he complained about his commute to another barista. he was much more focused on his conversation than his professionalism. as my patience thinned, he started working on my drink. at this point i've waited 12 minutes for my coffee and my shift was about to start in a few minutes. so i told him that i had no choice but to leave because the service was just way too slow. another barista came out of nowhere and instead of apologizing for her and her teams unprofessionalism, she told me to order my drink on their mobile app before coming into the store. while that may be a good suggestion for others, i don't believe that i should be concentrating on my phone while driving to starbucks. the coffee itself was pretty terrible. i ordered a hazelnut iced coffee with almond milk and it was not mixed properly so there were little flecks of "almond milk" throughout the drink. i could've used an apology for the extremely slow service but i guess i'll just save myself some time and money and stop at dd next time!
worst starbucks ever slow service and wrong items and drinks after 40 minute wait got to be better i'll be back at pj or cc

so... i don't really use yelp anymore, as i find it too accommodating to the chronic complainers out there that want to hear themselves rant about the minuscule details in nearly every review. today though, i had an experience that i feel needs to be shared in as many avenues as possible, so i am writing. i drove into tucson today for a quick 2 day business trip, and decided i needed a coffee on a cold day. i popped into the local starbucks expecting the consistent product i can get from them in any city. and i got it. what i wasn't expecting was the wonderful hospitality and friendly approach of tom (apparently the manager). i sat for 10 minutes and tom warmly welcomed every guest that entered his little coffee shop. he knew no less than 50% of the clientele walking in the door by name and held meaningful conversations with them. i know it's not the craziest thing in the world. but, its so nice to see someone actually care about their customers and offer a genuine caring experience to them. thanks tom. keep up the great work!

pretty average starbucks. i asked for an unsweetened, no ice, lemonade-iced tea. i received a full of sugar, iced tea. not the end of the world but as the only person in line, i was hoping for a bit more attention to detail. the pastries were stale on a sunday evening... if they're not fresh don't put them out!

i'm sorry; i know it's still covid and they're a new franchise who may be training more folks, but this starbucks is the worst i have ever experienced in town. the people are so nice, but there is a massive disconnect between ordering and receiving your coffee/drinks. even the busiest shops in franklin/cool springs turn around customers quicker with double the crowd. we've tried to order in-store, online pickup, drive thru - every option comes with a minimum of a 15-20 minute wait as they all run around and forget who's order is actually finished and who's needs making. really want to like this spot, but with other coffee options nearby, can't see us keeping up with a place that holds no value on decent service. hope it improves!

i am not a fan of starbucks except for a few favorites so i only go when there is nothing else around. that is the case with this location inside the westin hotel. the staff is always friendly and makes a really nice breakfast sandwich. the coffee here seems less burnt than other starbuck locations, so i give them some high marks for that. i also like the early hours that they open (6 am). when you have no real choice, this place is worth going to. as for the small location, there is nothing wrong with going outside underneath the umbrellas and taking in the fresh air at one of the many hotel provided tables, you just need to head left towards the beach and you will see them. they also accept american express which many places in tama do not.

this is a reliable starbucks. it has moderate seating, not as much as some locations, but at least 3 tables and several relaxing chairs. it has a drive through- which in st. louis we know that not all do. it is always clean and i have never had any issues with orders.

this starbucks is hit or miss when it comes to making drinks right. i go here often as it is the closest starbucks and i always order the same thing for my wife and i, but two out of three times they tend to get the order wrong and completely botch the drinks entirely. it's especially terrible if you order by drive thru, and it's only slightly better if you order inside. on the plus side, the employees are always friendly and welcoming.

this is the worst starbucks i've been to. it's a disappointment coming here before work to have your order constantly messed up. on their iced beverages, they will fill up your cup with 75% of ice esp. if you get a venti. i usually get a venti cold brew coffee but with this location i always have to ask for no ice. they can't even make a simple iced carmel macchiato right. i ask for 5 shots of espresso & my drink was a light carmel color. i ask for almond milk and they put more milk than it should be & there was no carmel! im mad because i used my starbucks reward on this drink. always ask for your receipt at this location because they will over charge you. they don't care because it's a drive through. welp, only the one star because it is a drive through starbucks. i'm sad that i have to write a bad review on my favorite place but i'm fed up with this location.

made a stop here before heading back from a weekend get-a-way. there's no drive-thru which i didn't like. there is a parking lot and the starbucks​ is located at​ the end of the first little strip mall surrounded by other little shops. this location was nice and clean. the service was nothing less than very friendly. also,​ this location is located up the street from usf which i'm sure can become pretty packed with college students meeting and studying. the bathroom was also nice and clean. if i'm ever in the area again i'd definitely make a stop for my favorite cup of brew.
this is my go to spot for a caffeine fix...lol everyone who works here puts forth an excellent level of customer service.... keep up the good work my friends
i think this location is better off for not having a drive thru. the inside is very echoey, so if there are a decent number of people inside it's not a good place to have a conversation.

i go to this starbucks all the time, most of the staff are very helpful and useful and even offer discounts when they are available, everytime they make a drink it always comes out just about to perfection, they make it better than some of the stand alone starbucks locations. this starbucks is a smaller one from being inside a target, but the staff is always courteous, working and won't keep you waiting.

wow. the drive thru was crazy long and there's very little parking. i parked and walked in can can't believe those people wait in the drive thru that long. i think it was a bank a long time ago. shared with jimmy john's.

drop your (friend, sibling, loved one etc) off at work at 7:30am, drive around awhile and end here. order a chai latte (or your own poison), and a crazy good zucchini muffin. then, devour the muffin top and the rest, while picking out the walnut gems which is strangely like panning for gold. repeat when your budget allows.
this is the worst starbucks ever. i get starbucks every day and this is literally the worst one i've been to. every time they take forever and make my drink wrong.

everything about this is standard in regards to quality of the product. the staff could be more pleasant but it really variates depending on the shift. all of that aside, the remodel stole the old comfort this starbucks always had in its atmosphere. it has an uncomfortable feeling now and they removed a lot of seating so it's prone to getting more crowded and it won't be a great place to study or relax like before. but if you're just stopping by, it'll suffice.

this is one of the worst starbucks i have ever been to. who knew that you would have to mobile order an hour before you wanted your starbucks. i just waited 30 minutes to get my hot tea. they never printed out the mobile order receipts, people were coming and going who were in line. i believe starbucks noted that mobile ordering is for people who are in a rush, they just made me 15 minutes late for class. this starbucks needs to get it together or they are going to lose customers.

i've been into this starbucks once before, but only briefly. i'm impressed by the space as it has a high ceiling, lots of windows and decent sized tables. it's not a residential area, so it seems like most of the patrons drive here. it's a well laid out, comfortable place. busyness - for the middle of the afternoon it's fairly steady and a bit of an older crowd. i guess there must be no schools nearby. noisiness - not bad. about average for a steady starbucks. the drive thru keeps them busy evidently. the music is definitely set to background level. i like that. ambience - typical for starbucks with modern, coffee-shop decor. power - power is available to pretty much all the seats, except the center ones. wouldn't have far to stretch your power cord though. free wifi - available and works well. no connection problems. a touch slow though. staff - it's adequately staffed with the usual, friendly staff you expect at starbucks. seating - there are 17 seats inside and a patio with about 10 seats. there are two comfortable armchairs at a table and the square cafe tables are big enough you could probably fit two laptops with mice. five of the chairs are at a bar, and i've never been a fan of them. in all the starbucks locations i've been to, i'm not sure i've ever seen anyone actually use them. security - seems fine. not a bad area of the city, but you know the drill.
great staff and yum yum for my tummy coffee. fav is the white chocolate mocha all around

i love starbucks and one with a drive thru. there were 4 cars in front of me and they went by very quickly. less than 5 minutes in the drive thru and the employee was super nice. i order the egg white bites and they were delicious. whoever though to use the label to help hold down the spoon is a genius. i don't like having lots of loose things in my hands and that was innovative to me which i appreciate. thank you!

redesigned store...comfy and trendy seating and a buzzing staff that, although new to most regulars, they're trying to make everything awesome for you the first time. there use to be music playing by way of their starbucks spotify playlist, but not here. and the wifi...don't even bother to connect to it. lte with 2 bars is faster most times. seriously need a reconfigure to ensure it can handle customer load regardless of how much content is being streamed. but today, my drink was good...i got a smile... and it was just a normal experience. nothing more or less.

normal walk-in starbucks... staff was very friendly... drinks were made fast but with care... had a great convo with the bristia about differnt coffee's we liked and she suggested some of her favorite that i might like...

best customer service. quick. super friendly. more than happy to help you. best coffee. knows their menu inside and out. hand down best starbucks. keep it up ladies!!! and thank you so much for my coffee (my soul) every morning.

walking up to starbucks at 3 am some weeks ago, from a full table outside, i heard, "vagina!" "pussy!" "pussy foot!" "chlamydia!" ...wtf? a minute later, i realized that they were playing a game like taboo or catch phrase. apparently, they're always there. good times. though i still wonder what that word was... yes, i'm writing yet another 5-star review for a starbucks. i mean, it's hard to give a place that's open 24/7 any other rating. plus, they've got cute, attentive baristas ready to serve you a tasty drink any hour of the day! you can't beat that. definitely my starbucks of choice when i'm in tampa! sidenote: if you're going to sit outside, use bug spray. in the scant few minutes that i sat outside to people-watch, i got four mosquito bites on my leg!
this is the best starbucks in reno! i'm in and out of there quickly and the drinks are amazing! not to mention the baristas are some of the best. i won't go to any other store! :)

we stopped through this starbucks drive-thru location on an afternoon. we only wanted coffee, apparently in the afternoon the only thing they brew is pike (must not be a heavily trafficked starbucks to have such limited options. they had just ran out of pike and had to brew another pot. the woman was very apologetic and gave us our order for free, we just had to pull up and wait about 4 minutes for the pike. as we were not in a hurry to be anywhere this was not a problem, however if you're trying to get somewhere quickly i would not recommend stopping at the drive-thru as it did take awhile.

always a great starbucks! their team is super friendly and knows many regulars by name. they always seem to get drinks out quickly no matter how many people are in line and the store is always clean and well stocked. they even have cold brew and nitro cold brew on tap!

5 people in line took me 30mins to get one cup of coffee. i ordered three different things off the menu and of course they were out of all three. this isn't the first time, nor the second time this has happened. hence the review. i have been to starbucks all over the country and have never had this poor of an experience. do not recommend this starbucks if you are hungry or thirsty.

i was working in the area and stopped here to caffeinate myself for my 90 minute drive home. if you're not college student age (the place was packed full of kids doing their homework, which i respect), you will pretty much be ignored at this starbuck's. my 20 year old colleague was proof of that! i saw him get treated better than my 40 year old self and my 50 something colleague. i was holding an empty canister of half and half to alert the staff that it was empty and needed a little for my americano and the cutesy young blonde working looked right at me, and continued her conversation with her coworker until another coworker from the back noticed me and took care of the situation (maybe i should've asked my 20 year old coworker to get me the creamer, i would've gotten it quicker) the fact that there's a vons in the same shopping center with a starbucks in it should make these people give better service so the clientele doesn't go walking to vons for their coffee fixes!! i have a feeling that's what anyone over the age of 30 does in this neighborhood!!!
love this starbucks. i usually come here after school with some friends and all of the baristas are very friendly. they enjoy having conversations with you while they make your drink.
they play great music at this starbucks. really good service. comfy chairs inside and a nice outside seating make it a little nicer than all the starbucks in this area, but it is still just ok.

i love starbucks and always get the same thing. i am glad to see a starbucks so close to home so i don't have to drive all over to get to one. the only thing that has always been an issue of mine with starbucks is they're prices since they are a little high. other than that i love them.

today, i had a horrible experience at this starbucks. i'm going to call out the particular shift because i think managers should know what their barista's are doing. i was in around 11am on sunday. the barista started my drink, and another customers drink. during this time she decided to walk to the back..get a mop..proceed to mop the floor...and than put the mop back...finally getting back to finish off our drinks...really????? horrible customer service starbucks....while i will be back...i hope to get a barista who actually cares about their customers.

i love starbucks. it's my go to coffee destination. i won't, however, be coming back to this location. i'm lactose intolerant and love the bux for the delicious soy milk. i ordered a soy latte. the barista confirmed that it was soy. i've not eaten anything else all day and have been violently ill. clearly the barista put milk in my drink. i called the location to complain and they couldn't be bothered other than "let us know if you have to go to the emergency room"... really?? i'm so disappointed and still sick.

i go to this starbucks everyday, and staffing has gotten so terrible that i won't be able to continue using this location. i just sat in line for my coffee for 47 mins. almost an hour for a cup of coffee. i have been a daily starbucks customer for nearly 20 years and this level of customer service is no longer to the standard i expect when spending almost 8 dollars on a cup of coffee. i have continuously tried to show a level of grace, because i am aware of the nationwide staffing shortage. however, i believe starbucks needs to step up and make some changes. for starters i believe after waiting 40 mins before even reaching the register, without them even beginning to make my drink it should of been comped. my complaint is less with the few employees working hard today trying to do there best. and more so to do with the management that approved this schedule, and the hire ups for not offering incentives to staff there locations. when choosing starbucks, i pay more then the standard coffee shop because i expect not only consistency but a level of competence that puts you above your competition. but, these days, starbucks is really dropping the ball.

slow drive through service. sunday morning - wanted ti stop for a coffee before church. drive through set up won't let you leave - we've been stuck here for 21 min. please fix this! finally got to the window and the order was wrong. drinks, food, everything was wrong. had to pull up and wait for them to bring out.

decided to go to this location this morning not a good way to start my day. nate wasn't very friendly but rather sassy. i always start my morning with starbucks and get my usual so i know how much it is to the penny. when he told me the total i told him that it was incorrect. instead of asking someone else he says ,well this is what is coming up and completely disregarding my comment and charged my card. not quite the way to start off my weekend . i will try and avoid this location with that.

this starbucks is close to my house so it's convenient but the time it takes to get through the drive thru is ridiculous. it's usually pretty slow, so i go with patience but today, 8:30 am on a sunday, 6 cars in line and it's taken me 30 minutes to get through the drive thru! crazy! however, the baristas are friendly and the drinks are perfect. this would be the perfect starbucks if speed was improved. honestly, i should have used pre order but i didn't realize i would be in the line this long.

had to update my review because the service was so good today. not sure the barista's name, but he is always so kind to me when i come in! always greets me! i wanted a chocolate croissant, but noticed all the pastries were out of the display and sitting out on the counter so i asked if there were others and he took a new one out of package for me. i ordered my drink wrong and didn't realize until i got it and he was so nice about having it remade. i ordered an iced green with no water and meant to do classic syrup instead of cane sugar (since they changed it). my tea tastes perfect.

great drinks like any starbucks and great customer service, but the bugs flying around are so annoying!!! i can't study/do work in the store, i have to sit outside, because the gnats/flies are so present.

located in the corner unit of a strip mall just off causeway blvd, this is a convenient place (for me) to get a quick caffeine fix. this location is an average starbucks. they are quick at herding people through, and i like that. one thing i don't like, is the men and women share a restroom. there can be long lines at times. also, because of their location, this particular starbucks does not have a drive through window. other than that, it is your average expensive high calorie coffee joint. it's kept pretty clean, and the service has always been friendly. there has always been plenty of seating, both inside and out. who doesn't want a caffeinated milkshake in the mornings?
all staffs who were friendly and helpful with fast order to take out. nice services and very good.
this is legitimately the slowest starbucks in america. maybe don't have 1 barista working during the 8am rush hour on a friday.... i waited 25 minutes for a latte.

great friendly service. one of the better locations i have been to. patio seating outside where you can sit with your doggie and soak up the florida sun! this place has been our coffee spot as we transition into our new home here in tampa, florida!

my guilty addictions include the spinach wrap and skinny cinnamon dolce latte. i need a part time job to fund the daily purchases of these items! these two items pair well and make my tummy very happy!!!

this starbucks is in the front of the parking area of town and country crossing. it sits near fed ex and the veterinary office in a strip of about five businesses. this location is great because it offers a drive thru unlike the other two starbucks in town and county. they also have plenty of parking on a surface lot. this location has always been fast and friendly. my last visit i enjoyed a cold foam cascara nitro. i really enjoy how smooth and silky this drink tastes. i love all the cold brews and cold options...starbucks keeps pulling me in. i will return.

i took my dad to starbucks for a coffee however there was not a place to sit together because everyone in there had a computer and took up 4 seats per person with their backpacks books and crap all over the tables. wtf!!! this crap is out of control real customers do no have a place to sit and enjoy coffee that's crazy that they have to take over the entire seating sections.

i try to avoid coming here as much as possible because it always feels so frantic for no reason. the one in the cherry hill mall faces a lot more crazy circumstances in a much more confined space and they still manage to function without making the guests feel just as anxious. i've had my order forgotten, once had an employee ignore me as i waited in line and proceeded to ring up another employee who cut in front of me to purchase her lunch instead, and have experienced ridiculous wait times during slow hours. the upside is that it's all made well and there's two guys that work there who actually have an understanding of good customer service.
solid, dependable, predictable coffee. not the best, but certainly very good.
starbucks products are great! this store doesn't have the friendly service like most. i will come here again if i need coffee and am in the area, but it's nothing special!

love, love, love, love this location. 99% of the time i use the drive through, and am greeted with a perky voice in the box and a smile in the window. obviously we all know starbucks prices sort of suck, but when that grande iced skinny vanilla late touches my lips, i know it was all worth it (lol). anyway when i did go inside i was surprised by the nice decor and comfy seating! also has some of the fastest wifi around! no joke! i will say the layout of the parking lot kind of sucks, so if you park and go inside you better not be in a hurry because chances are your car will be blocked in by everyone in the drive through lane.

long overdue full stand alone starbucks on the northwest side!! replaced small starbucks that was across 1st. new, clean, spacious, tables to work, seating to meet friends, staff friendly snd helpful and great drivethru. take care in the parking lot however-while parking slots seem to accomodate, the traffic mapping is tight and intersecting with queue to drivethru, pedestrians, and entry/exit traffic.

as we speak i'm walking away from a starbucks who not only had the doors locked and closed ten minutes before closing time, but a starbucks whose employee straight up ignored my knocks on the door. unimpressed.

why go to starbuck's when you can support local coffee shops? this place literally doesn't need to be here, and the workers are clearly not from the neighborhood nor do they live here. here for supporting workers but this is isn't the hot take.

horrible service. ordered on the app and came to find my order missing. no apologies whatsoever. in fact, the barista acted like it was my fault that my order wasn't there. not sure what's up with your attitude. but if you were going to treat people this way, then maybe you don't belong here in the first place.

i came in here for a little post exercise treat. this store is a little smaller than other starbucks, but i thought that made it feel cozier. there's a long snack bar counter facing the parking lot, and the traditional comfy area with tables. the employees were really friendly and everything was made fast and correctly. even my boyfriend's blonde roast that had to be done specially had hardly any wait time. this starbucks is definitely one of the better ones i've been to, so stop in if you're in the mood for some yummy caffeine.

ordered 7 items from the drive through just now. noticed that they gave me a bagel mislabeled in a bag that said it was my breakfast sandwich. i went in to switch it out which was easy. didn't realize until we were much farther away that they also forgot to put in our two cake pops. tried to call and at least change the amount we were charged on cc but no one would answer the phone. are you guys just throwing random things in bags?

never had poor customer service from any other starbucks. one of the employees who took the order was very impatient. and whoever made the drinks did not know how too. went back to redo the order and they argued that they were right and that the person who made them was a manager. horrible!

brownsburg finally has a new starbucks--which has had no problems finding customers. the new location on the northern side of the freeway has several noticeable improvements over the old one. first of all, the parking situation is way better, with a circular set up for easy access. second, there is more space in the interior for hanging out. it is way less cramped. and way less noisy. the crowd here seems less uptight than the group who frequent the older branch. because its new, there is an efficient and fresh-looking checkout area. no exterior seating, though. i like this option at various times of the year. service--friendly and error-free so far
poorly run. always a long than normal wait. subpar drinks. andrea is terrible. the workers seem overworked and are not friendly

typical starbucks as far as coffee drinks go. i prefer this location in st. pete however. the staff here is always friendly. i've yet to get any attitude from anyone. always very clean when i'm there. i love that they have a bar that you can sit at or tables and chairs. plenty of places to hook your computer/smart phone up to. any time i go through the drive through, they seem to move the line along fairly quickly. this location is very close to tyrone square mall and many stores/restaurants. not a fan of their "plain" coffee however (and that's at any starbucks). every one i have tried is super bitter. love the white-chocolate mocha and other drinks though!

i have been waiting several years for a starbucks to open in this location. right on the corner of terrace road and 101 avenue. so i waited a while after it finally did to post my review. now first off, i do love starbucks, but i am not one of those customers who are in there every day, or necessarily even every week. and i am not happy with every starbucks i go to, especially drive thrus. as i am one of those people who never get their coffee the way the recipe is made. that is one of the reasons i like starbucks, it is never a problem to ge my drink customized. i find that most of their coffee drinks are too sweet for my taste so usually get them half sweet, and often have to customize the milk due to dietary concerns. and due to chronic migraines & doctors orders have to have decalf now :( i have never had a problem with any of these customizations when i have gone in to a starbucks, however, drive thrus can be another story. speaking of customizations, their grande hot chocolates have; 4 pumps mocha & 1 pump vanilla. try it with 2 pumps mocha and 2 pumps vanilla, it's good! everyone i have suggested try it agrees. this starbucks has been open for a few months now and i am very happy to say that each time i have been in, the service has been great. i have been in at different times of day (but not early morning yet). they have been not so busy to very busy when i have been there and the staff have always been pleasant. this is something to say as many of them are new to starbucks, every time i have gone in i was happy to see that they are getting busier and busier as this means (i hope) they will be around for a good while to come. i was disappointed that they have extremely limited equipment for sale as i was looking for a burr grinder and they did not have any. but this was probably due to the fact that they are so new and have not established any retail sales yet.

the staff this morning was extremely rude. when asked who was here for mobile orders a few people said their names and the girl shouted one at a time. a gentleman came in without a mask (by mistake) and the girl shouted you can't come in here without a mask so get out !!! who the hell does she think she is, never going back to this elitist dump !!!

starbucks is starbucks, but the people who work here really make it. it feels familial when the employees get to know you. i'm a regular in the morning during the weekdays, and there are a lot of other regulars who make it feel warm and friendly.
capricia is the best she knows just what i'm going to order and sees me coming lol love her
your employees need to wash their aprons! some are so disgusting. otherwise service was okay.
pricey for a starbucks...although i do have to say i very much appreciate the fact you were open on christmas day. my family thoroughly enjoyed it. thank you.
this is the nicest starbucks. wow! what a friendly staff. i absolutely enjoy having my first cup coffee at this place. it's a true definition of five starts customer service.

this is without a doubt the worst starbucks ever i have yet to ever get a drink order correct. its so simple: venti vanilla latte almond milk no foam today no vanilla i wa the first or second person thru the drive thru i had to repeat the order twice , we had a whole lol momnet about no vanilla creamer at home i mean seriously at $5.50 a cup get it together. i usually drive tot he 71st and bindford location because the location by me on 56 st. is so slow and they always alway always mess up something in the order or you have to repeat it 3 times. get it together

this place is ridiculous. seriously why pay 5 bucks for coffee when you can get it for a dollar at mcdonald's. not to mention snobby people. some customers and some employees. i don't care if you are called a barista or not to me you are a coffee pourer. don't make me feel like a low ball because you sell expensive coffee!! (end rant)

this location almost never has nitro, does not matter what time of day, and they are often out of ingredients. i can never get what i want from this starbucks location! i have stopped attempting to order from this location all together! additionally the service is pretty terrible for starbucks standards, especially since they moved locations from the northwest corner of first and oracle. it seems that they did not prepare for more volume at the new location despite being a bigger store and adding a drive through. it usually takes at least 10 minutes if you order in person or through the drive through no matter the time of day, their printer always jams so even if you mobile order, your chances of actually getting your order are a crap shoot, and on the rare occasion they actually have nitro, my order is never right. very disappointing seeing as i am paying almost $7 for a cup of coffee!

i was over on this side of town to shop at the local winco last friday. there was a queue going all the way around the building like no thanks i will pass! i noticed this starbucks across the street, so i thought what the heck why not treat yourself since you wasted a ton of gas coming over. i noticed they had a drive-up window, but the line was long. i went in and they were very nice to me. i got my usual order. i get a skinny vanilla latte. i found it interesting how different the order tastes depending on who is making it. i found it super annoying that it's not the same every single time. like it's a basic recipe for goodness sake. when i go to a juice bar and make a order, it's almost always tastes the same. i know it's silly to be annoyed but i just don't get it. that's why i love my coffee at home! it tastes exactly the same every morning. i also think it gets pretty expensive to get it everyday... the waitstaff guy was super friendly and accommodating to me. so i want too disappointed that the coffee was different tasting once again. home is better :) cute location and clean, well organized store.

one of the worst starbucks i have been too! whether in the morning or afternoon there is always only one person making drinks and 6 other people doing nothing. i seriously question the management at this location, and wonder if the staff just do what they want. if you ever get upset about having to wait 20 mins for your drink they look at you as if they have the hardest job in the world and how dare you question them. what a joke!!

we stopped in two times to grab tea/water and use the bathroom. the staff is very nice and the store is clean and well stocked. the women's restroom was down and had to use the men's which needed a bit of cleaning. good location for a pop up wedding parade as well as we saw one each day.

we come to this starbucks every time we are in town it's always a great atmosphere but, today it was not. i'm assuming it's the store manager not wearing her apron and has the nastiest attitude i've ever seen. she's giving the most horrible customer service to every single customer apparently because the morning shift did not stock prior to them leaving. she's letting out her frustrations at the customers. i ordered my drink at 1.31p and just now got it at 1.54p and it was made wrong. when i mentioned it to her all she said was "my bad" no i'm sorry no i'll remake for you, nothing she just turned around threw the drink away and had another associate make my drink. this is not what you should expect when walking into any starbucks.

the starbucks locations in tampa bay are below average. usually they are mobbed and have below average staff in terms of drink-making skills. this location is no exception. if you want a large starbucks to hang out in, check out the one on mlk jr. blvd. in tampa. it's never mobbed and staff are nice.

update: omg. i'm just done with this starbucks until they figure out that whatever they are doing is not working. i get it that it's in a busy location on a main street but all the more reason to make sure that things are running efficiently. the lines are forever long and sometimes the people on the registers are sooooooo freaking slow! beyond slow. the other day i stood in line for more than 10 minutes...i was third in line..! it's not like the line was out the door...i was third. then it took forever to get my drink...i drink black iced coffee...that's it. it just seems ridiculous to me that this store can't seem to get with it. it's a great store...the location is picturesque and perfect, it's comfortable and spacious, but if your in a hurry then it's not a good place to be. i live around the corner but i get in my car and drive 5 miles to the location in flourtown because they are quick and friendly and their employees always seem happy to be there. the chestnut hill location just needs an overhaul badly. also....think that mobile ordering would help this situation..!!!? nope! you can mobile order, then come in and find a place to wait and stand in the way while you wait and wait for your mobile order to be done. or you can wait a bit until it might be ready but then come in and find that because they didn't mark things not available in their system they don't have a single thing that u ordered. so now you have to re pick all your stuff based on what they do have. i've had to do that the last two times i mobile ordered which is supposed to save time. it doesn't at this location. i think i'll keep going to flourtown for a while and maybe during that time they will figure things out in chestnut hill.

6/26/2018: when traveling somewhere around the planet, there will be several starbucks franchises practically everywhere in every city from reno, carson city, minden, california, new york-all fifty us states, canada, alaska, singapore, philippines, south korea, macau, hong kong, japan, thailand, malaysia, europe, etc.. i like the starbucks' ice cream scooper i bought in beverly hills ca a few years ago; however, while traveling to lost wages, i put my prized starbucks traveling, stainless steel, coffee press on a table, and i turned the other way. . . ppppppfffffttt~gone! the nerve of people taking my coffee press inches from my elbow! well~it's time buying a new cold coffee beverage container . . . i just wish the one i bought today was safe keeping hot coffee inside but, oh, well . . . not. and~the number one reason i keep returning to starbucks: drumroll, please . . . peppermint coffee with a double shot of espresso surely makes me into a nervous chihuahua~lol! arf-arf-arf!!!!

starbucks always leaves a good impression on me. the service is as good as to be expected (sometimes there is a line). there are usually good sale items. everyone is pleasant and the quality is always good.
best starbucks in nola i have been a regular there for years now. everyone is so nice at it.

only been here a handful of times. the oatmeal and breakfast items are good and the blonde roasts are never burnt. so by chain coffee store standards, this starbucks deserves 4 stars. for me, i don't think there are many tables in open areas for studying especially because i don't like the claustrophobic tables in the back by the restrooms, but they have a cute little area with couches if you want to catch up with friends!
the staff is very sweet. they offered ice water while we sat and was very nice. really welcoming aura and establishment.
this place has the a/c on freezer cold. bring a sweater. everything else seems normal.

we t to this starbucks 3 mornings in a row and had a great experience every time. it is nice to be able to come in from out of town and be able to get coffee without an issue. the people who work here are very nice. i didn't have any issues. i came here every morning with 9 girls and 4 parents. we were in and out within 10 mins. when we completed mobile orders, they were ready when arrived. the other nice thing about this place is the little sitting area off to the side. it is quiet and open, so you can sit over there and complete any work that you may need to. i was pretty impressed with this location. between the physical location and the people, it is a great combination. hopefully we are back next year and can visit again.

great starbucks location, friendly staff (most of the time)...nice place to get coffee and relax or chat. can be crazy busy at times, making service less than ideal. still my goto place for my morning caffeine fix.
this place is fabulous. i am in love with the frappucinos. java chip is the best. or i get the banana chocolate viviano if im feeling healthy.
dont bother coming here if youre expecting your drink to get made correctly or even taste good at all. oh and asking for a caramel drizzle = give me diabetes! and the wait is crazy...

i'm a fan of starbucks, like any other red blooded american. but this starbucks, is my little piece of heaven. it's not the biggest, the location is not the most ideal and the parking sucks, but the people are extremely nice. this is my go-to location for my starbucks. b. major grade: b

i stopped by as i was leaving target i was thirsty so decided i would order a drink. the reason for the one star review is the starbucks is very busy and only had one person working i waited in line and then had to wait an additional 10 minutes before the lady could take my order then when she did take my order she was rude. they also need to check their expiration dates on their evolution fresh drinks as many of them were expired and usually i wouldn't mind but they are very perishable items. then i got my drink and there was barely and fruit pieces in it. probably one of the worst starbucks experiences ever.

ok every other day it's a 5star rating. however today was less because of how the drive thru barista acted when she got my drink wrong. i go through the drive through and order as a creature of habit and say the same thing every time so i know it was not something i had left out. when i got my drink it did not have cream in it and she said "well it doesn't come with cream unless you ask". i said i did say i needed cream in my drink and did not hear her not say cream when repeating my order because she said it so fast. i am getting ready to provide customer service for 8 hours at my job and i really did not appreciate the attitude / arguing before 7am in the morning. might be awhile before i go back to starbucks and might need to head back to dunkin'.

mobile ordering sucks. my sandwich was cold by the time i received my drink 15 minutes later. which by the way was wrong. then i was handed 2 drinks without any indication of which was made correctly. uggggggg
i just want to say thank you to the gentleman working the drive through today at 1:51 on 1/21/2019. small gestures go a long way. you are a badass sir!

first try at the blonde espresso and i can say i liked it. it has a sweeter and smoother taste to it, caffeine wise i cant really tell if it has a stronger kick. as for the rest it was normal but i like that starbucks is trying out new things with there coffee even when they have 800 different things you can get and then customize on top of that (not complaining). as always using mobile order has made picking up coffee on the go so easy and probably the best feature.

first starbucks app order for my daughter today and all went wrong, then got worse. she ordered the mango dragonfruit refresher with strawberry added (realized she ordered that extra once i returned home). i went to pick it up for her, because i'm a good dad, and was handed a "clear-ish" looking drink with no explanation. so i asked, "isn't it supposed to be kinda pink?" was told they're out of dragonfruit but it wouldn't change the flavor at all. here's from their website... "this tropical-inspired pick-me-up is crafted with a refreshing combination of sweet mango and dragonfruit flavors, handshaken with ice and a scoop of real diced dragon fruit." turns out that dragonfruit must provide some dragonfruit flavor!! called them once i got home about the missing strawberries and another worker told me again, after saying i wasn't charged for extra strawberries when my receipt clearly showed i was, that it didn't matter about the dragonfruit and if i drove back to the store she'd refund my 50 cents. that's not the way to correct this. the right answer would've been to refund it through the app or take my name for a future credit, not have me drive back to get a refund of 50 cents because you forgot the strawberry in my daughter's dragonfruit drink...minus the dragonfruit!!! we'll go elsewhere.

ok this is kinda a word location. i've seen several starbucks now that seem to be part of a bank drive through. this one is no different, hence the pics. service and drink were good. just puzzled by the bank drive through feel. lol

free standing starbucks. the parking lot you pull out of is like on a hill, so be careful not to scrape the bottom of your car when you pull out. basically like any other starbucks. no problems, and i think they now have free wi-fi. you can also sit outside if you'd like.

great starbucks. nice people, and they prepare the drinks well. a lot of starbucks have stuff that's kind of watered down, fortunately that isn't the case here. the building itself is pretty clean too. they're also the only place that spells my name right.
when i am in town, this is my place for espresso. curtis even knows what i want without asking, triple shot on ice. my go to coffee shop when i am here in nola

you know, not all starbucks are created equal hence why they have 4 on each corner of the street. you don't like one, go to another, right? well, i would unless i was at a conference and not trying to walk in the rain. so, the only good thing about this starbucks is that it's connected to the convention center via a skywalk. so, i trudge over there each morning and order the same thing, soy latte no foam. no biggie, no major frills but i guess these baristas do not understand no foam nor filling it up to the top. i normally order a tall and have gotten used to the weight of my drink. so when i order a grande, due to the long conference, it should weigh much more since it goes from 12 to 16 oz. it should but it doesn't. in fact, i think it almost weighed less if that's possible. i opened my cup to take a gander and the first thing i noticed was how low the foam came up my cup. i would say it was about an inch below the top. then, why is there sooo much foam? i understand that soy is different and it will foam later once it settles but seriously, i do not think this barista did anything different from normal. so, i went back up and asked her kindly to add some more soymilk since it was not full. well, she did but she filled it with the cold soymilk. so i asked if she could steam it first. hmm...so when she does pour it in, i swear she poured in about 4 oz more of soymilk. so, if you must get to a starbucks and you're at the convention center and want convenience, this'll do.

updated review. only three stars today as the pastry display case was pitiful, to say the least. the barista explained to me that they are having great difficulty getting their pastry orders, especially the pumpkin scone order. the starbucks advertising for the season talks up pumpkin, pumpkin, pumpkin, and yet they have nothing in their stores. someone has obviously dropped the ball major big-time on this one! nice sb's. clean with friendly baristas. ample parking out front. only drawback is it has no drive-thru. conveniently located in shopping center with a grocery store.
great service and coffee ! it's like they opened an airport in my neighborhood coffee shop, starbucks at the airport, nice !

fast service with polite staff. my order was correct especially because extra carmel is the key and they did just that. this location sits nestled between a business building and cvs. there is not lots of room to drive with the surroundings businesses but you still are able to maneuver just be mindful of the traffic.

i am a longtime starbucks custom and have visited multiple sites across the us over the years , i stopped in to this site this am to get a hot brew and use the site connectivity. expecting the best i had become accustom to, i unfortunately experienced more of the worst in a possible shop visit. coming in to the shop, no warmth in any kind of a greeting, i had to wait for a barista to help me (was first in line at the counter). i typically am a simple order guy, tall coffee with cream .. simple right ? 9 out of 10 times the barista says let me get that poured up for you .. and seconds later i have a hot cupper. not so here .. it took nearly 5 minutes to get my simple order i got a cool cup :( no "thank you", no attempt to get to know me at all (very cold setting) i was just another face asking for business and interrupting a busy drive up customers who ordered had to wait .. too long saw a mobile order customer come in and get "no order" which in watching took on a trend, saw multiple orders done incorrectly and the waiting queue continued :( what comes to my mind as i watch the workers here "the old video of who is on 1st?" watching the crew they seem to be disinterested in customers on the whole (there were a couple of team members who were there because they truly wanted to be), on a 1-10 have to sadly offer up a 3 here .. needs attention and leadership with a passion and vision. will not revisit ..
this starbucks is over priced and gets very crowded. they also don't let you use your starbucks reward stars .

i can't believe i'm reviewing starbucks but here i am. this place is a panic attack waiting to happen. it's always so damn crowded with little seating. you literally have to steal seats sometimes. you're also rushed ordering because of this. the service is decent though and well, it's starbucks.
once again this location had no via decaf in stock. now i realize they may do many things right but if they don't keep all their products in stock, for me, obviously, they are worthless
friendly staff. can't beat $1 drip coffee refills when you bring your mug.

i love starbucks! this one is absolutely horrible though. i thought they all went to "sensitivity training." i think they forgot to invite the employees from this particular starbucks. the store manager that works the morning shift, is by far the rudest manager that i have ever encountered at any starbucks.

not a fan of this starbucks. i would go as far as to say it is the worst in in indy. the staff is slow and unfriendly and the location always seems drab and dirty. there are plenty of other locations just wish they would redo the staff and interior of this one because it is closest for me.
they are super nice and fast! just stopped in and the baristas are sweet, clean store and fast service! drinks made perfect. ali was a sweetheart.

two words...barista baby! so you think starbucks coffee is bitter? wrong...it's better and you wanna know why? the obvious of course is that you've gotta start with some pretty awesome coffee beans...after that it's all about the barista. doesn't matter which starbucks i visit...whether it's a drive-thru, in a mall, airport or target. those baristas are highly trained professionals who know what they're doing. i've been to many other coffee houses and time and time again, my #1 complaint is lack of consistency. i mean it's not like they don't have great coffee, but please make me a non-fat latte that tastes the same all the time. until then, i remain a starbucks devotee...peace out.
fucking idiots only filled my drink up halfway. i ordered a chai tea latte with light ice ??? not light drink dumbass. and it still has a shit load of ice

i love starbucks ice coffee but this place is so packed and so slow and so tiny. and parking is hard outside. i just avoid this place. there are so many wonderful starbucks nearby and this place is super annoying and i have been here many many times before realizing that it's the same every single time!!
ok. service. not a corporate location, so they don't accept starbucks coupons. not cool. :-(
best most favorite coffee including coffee beans to purchase for home brewing (unground of course!) we like their pikes, veranda (blonde) and guatamala/antigua.

i go here all the time as it is right near my work. christina, seth, dante. . . the entire staff is awesome and more than happily indulge my indecisiveness when i want to try something different. yay!

the reason for two stars for this location is the horrible parking and ingress/egress design; it is easily the worst starbucks i have encountered from that standpoint. the primary entrance is a single entry/exit on central ave but with a pointless and stupid triangle curb built in the middle as an effort to force people to only enter when heading westbound, and only exit via a right turn west bound. of course, this being florida, no one listens to any traffic rule, so judging by the deep gashes in the curbing, many people turn in when heading eastbound anyway, don't see the curb and then run over it. additionally, i routinely see people leave with a left turn even though the curb is supposed to prevent that, along with the sign that says right turn only. anyway, that's not the worst part of this location, it's once you get in the lot where things get really bad. since the location is small and only has one primary entrance/exit, the drive-thru backup often snakes through the lot and out to central. this means anyone who tries to park in a space, has to wait in the same line to get to a space, and if you're in a space trying to leave, good luck, you could be there five minutes or longer while the morons in the drive-thru line keep inching and inching so as not to lose their space, rather than letting you out. complicating matters further, there's an entrance from the alley behind the place where people in the know try to sneak in from. this results in two queues of cars trying to get into the same drive-thru, and people fighting with one another via their cars if they didn't think the other car was waiting as long as them, and thus not entitled to enter the line in front of them. finally, if you are there during busy time, leaving the parking lot after successfully navigating the drive-thru can be just as difficult because the turn to get out without smashing into the curbing can be quite difficult if there's a line of cars blocking half the space you'd normally use to make the turn.
this is consistently the worst starbucks. the wait times are always unnecessarily long... like 30 minutes long. it's faster to drive to another starbucks than wait here.

this starbucks is normally on point but a couple of days ago i went to get a drink and waited in the line of the drive through for 25 minutes before i got up to the window to get my order. i was so frustrated that i didn't realize that my order was wrong before i pulled off. i tried to go back so that they could fix it but they had already closed 10 minutes early. online it says they close at 1030pm but the door says that they close at 1000pm.

these guys are on it! fast,friendly, helpful, even smiling baristas. their super busy but work with a much appreciated zin calm. tried new skinny hazelnut macchiato...excellent. carmel version also offered. smaller store with few tables indoor and couldn't get table even in late afternoon. outdoor setting should help once warm weather hits.

the guy taking my order in the drive thru messed up my drink. i don't know if he heard the right thing or not. when i told them that wasn't what i ordered, i heard the guy say "what does she want now?" in a bitchy tone. umm, rude much!

a decent starbucks but with management/staff proplems. 5/17/16 the short, rotund, redheaded am manager fielded a complaint from a customer. i listened. before long, she accused him of making a scene. he did not. his tone and demeanor were perfectly appropriate; she didn't like his having a contrary opinion. last i checked, its a free country in which to have and opinion and express them. 2 weeks prior, i reported a woman taking a bottle of water from a display without paying. the tall lanky male manager thanked me and did nothing. now that may be company policy but... the managers in this store need better training and the insight that loyal customers are the source of their paychecks. 90% of the staff are fine... a few are less than pleasant, slow on the smiles and warm greetings and clearly unhappy with their jobs. the managers should be addressing this but i won't be holding my breath....
are there any adults that work here and can help these kids out? 20 minutes for a ice tea and panini... the drink wasn't even correct. parking lot is a nightmare.

this location is poorly operated and the service is always slow! yes, i understand it takes longer to make some of the specialty drinks but the service here is very slow! this morning there was a long line and a woman complaining that her mobile drink was not ready for pick up! again, forget the sensitivity training and train your employees how to actually provide service at a reasonable pace!!!! the 3 star rating is for the decent size parking lot and decent size indoor/outdoor seating! other than that not much else i can say!!

i did not realize that a starbucks could be bad. the coffee i have had at this one tasted like it was diluted. i thought the first time was a fluke and gave the place a second chance. bad idea! some people (my 93-year old mother, for instance) may like their coffee this way, but this is not what i expect from a starbucks.

this is an excellent store with friendly employees and good service. i visit it almost every day. both inside and outside seating areas are excellent. they have a nice fireplace with comfortable arm chairs around it. the only caution is that it is directly across the street from vanderbilt university, so when school is in session (most of the year) the store is very busy in the morning.

super busy here everytime i come by to grab some coffee. saturday mornings are just ridiculous i can see why the staff is not all smiley and sunny days but hey at least it's busy. it's walking distance for me so hey.
starbucks has a delicious green tea frappuccino! and their coffee is super yum zone!!

we've come to this starbucks early on every saturday for years. we've tolerated the inconsistent service, the cranky manager, the bible group that takes over the entire patio every saturday because we love the location. this morning was possibly our last visit - there are tons of other sbs to choose from. no attempt whatsoever to maintain the patio area - see the picture. we always have to clean off our own table, but this is ridiculous - i'm not going to sweep for them! when someone slips and falls on the piles of leaves they might pay attention. sloppy.

teavana shaken iced green tea every time i come to this location, they fill your entire drink with ice. i take a few sips and the whole drink is gone. it's a total rip off.. i'm certain i could ask for a tall with no ice and it would be more than what i was served today.

always busy and under staffed. 15 minutes in line everyday is ridiculous. if you have a line out the door and a bunch of employees in the back bring them up front to ring. at best a manager should stop and ring.
convenient location. drive thru a plus. baristas are ok; not as friendly as the glen mills location...i miss those guys and gals!!!
really nice manager and staff seating and wall outlets are really not too great but really isn't up to the staff. lots of parking and easy to get in and out.

this starbucks location is very hit or miss for me lately. as mainly a drive-thru patron, i do get somewhat frustrated with the amount of time it takes to expedite orders. however, the most frustrating aspect of my experience is not getting the orders correct and/or having a non-chalant "fast food" type of attitude from the order-taker through the exchange at the window. imo, things started going downhill around a 2 years after opening. it appears that the great quality baristas left, and were replaced with typical fast food employees. let me make clear, however, not all of my experiences have been bad. like i stated in the beginning, it's very hit or miss. i just hope that the franchisee has not gotten arrogant by having a cash cow location, because nothing lasts forever. i'm sure that the new dd down the street is taking some market share. as a gold frequent drinker member, i hope they improve their consistency and customer interaction before a cc's or pj's gets built down the street, and they lose many loyal customers' business (including mine) forever.

convenient location right next to my favorite nail shop. i come every time i get my nails done, which is often! it gets super busy here so be prepared to wait in line. the baristas are super friendly and try to get you your drink in a timely manner. if your drink doesn't come out the way you want, they're really good about remaking it! which is always a plus!
some of the most foul mouthed, ghetto trash, racists you will find. the way they talk about their job and customers (when they don't think anyone can hear), really makes you go somewhere else.

drive-thru is perpetually slow, sometimes waiting 10 minutes plus. not sure if they are understaffed or staff is just slow. other tampa starbucks the staff is hustling during busy times. it's too bad because it's such a convenient location.

starbucks is starbucks. i prefer the locally owned brews, however in a rush you can't beat the perfect same consistent coffee from starbucks. their 2am--2pm treat receipt is a great deal right now. (make a purchase before 2pm then show your receipt after 2pm same day to get any cold grande drink for $2)... and besides the coffee, i am really liking that berry hibiscus tea. i can't get enough of that this summer!
the starbucks is about to lose my daily sometimes twice daily business. mobile order placed at 11:15 not completed until 1127 too much chatter between employees. they are having a great time!

it's exactly like every other starbucks everywhere, except it has the additional excitement of having about half of their electrical outlets in the store, distributed randomly, just not work at all. there's no indication of which outlets work and which ones don't, and the employees have no fucking clue, so if you don't happen to be paying attention to which power icon appears in your taskbar, your computer may just suddenly shut down, losing all the work you've done since you last saved. if they're too cheap to get their outlets fixed, they could spend three buck on this [ ], and then cover the non-working outlets, so that there's a reminder for customers that some don't work, plus an indication of which is which, but that would, like, require that someone test all the outlets, which would, like, be work, so they don't do it. how much do you value that forty-five minutes of work you just lost because they're too fucking lazy to get their outlets fixed? i figure that five bucks plus a loss of almost an hour of work on my dissertation bumps the price to $$$$, hence my survey question answer. oh, and don't think they'll pull one of the five or six people chatting with their coworkers and ignoring the customers behind the counter onto the second register just because the line's out the door.
been there a number of times and they didn't have what i wanted...... sandwiches, lattes, even coffee.

some of the baristas at this location are very rude. the men, i would say are the only friendly ones there. they are good at making drinks but several of the "teeny bopper" baristas in this location lack customer service skills. they need more training in the "friendly"department.

this place is wonderful the staff is quick. this is one of the smaller store but they had 3 people working or even a long line is gone in a few mins. they know their coffee and the store is clean. it's starbuck so the coffee is always good.

i really like this location it's easy to get in and out of. they have a lot of seating inside and outside. no drive-thru but the staff is so awesome it's worth going in. logan is extremely nice he really helped me out this morning when my app on my phone wasn't cooperating.
excellent location! the staff is always courteous and never miss any special instructions. the lines generally move very quick and always a great experience. keep up the great work!

this message is to management, if you even care. for god's sake, please please please hire more staff! it is unacceptable to have three people working in the store at 7:30am when the drive through line is 15+ cars and the line in the store is 10+ people long. it is true that i used to live in the city and am used to a 2 minute visit. spending 10+ minutes in this starbucks just tops the list. i thought maybe it is just a one time thing, but it is consistent every single day and that is why i go out of my way to avoid you, horizon blvd starbucks. i thought a mega giant like you would have some standards...
super friendly folks, always clean and they have the clover system. grab an iced coffee and have them use the clover system, trust me.

this starbucks has great employees especially trevor, jonathan, and patti but i'm not sure if she still works there. these people are great, they are easy to talk to and they are super friendly and they get your order right! i defiantly recommend going to this starbucks for there great service and hard working employees. they deserve a raise!

ugh. from worse to worser... went in this morning for a few drinks. condiment bar was gross; luckily, i didn't need it today. my order (i get this all the time, so i know i didnt mess up): venti no-water chai & a grande extra-caramel caramel macchiatto. first was the chai. "non-fat no-water chai" said the barista. (she's also the manager!) i politely told her i ordered a no-water chai, not non-fat, and she took it back, made a new one. next drink was the extra caramel macchiatto. she called it a "grande non-fat extra caramel macchiatto." i again informed her that it was not non-fat, to which she replied that it wasnt non-fat. and i said that isnt what you called. and she said, rudely, "listen to what i'm saying. it isnt non-fat." ok then... eff this place.

don't hate it but don't love it. i've yet to find a starbucks where they are actually moderate to quick service wise--- and i just mean to take my order- obviously it takes time to make coffee. i went to this location on a saturday to try the new smores frap. there was one guy ahead of me. he was ordering a bag of coffee.... it took the lady a really long time to get him this coffee. meanwhile 3 other employees walked around behind the counter... looking at me but not asking me if i needed help... i continue to wonder why this part of the starbucks chain fails at every location..

i had to sit down and write this review because after a few times of having my order be wrong in the drive thru, i couldn't not say something. i get the easiest order you can get. trenta iced green tea, unsweet, no water. yes, i could go to the one downtown, but it's hard to park, and i have my little one in the car with me and it's just convenient to drive thru, i don't even mind the consistently long waits at the drive thru (20-25 min) because i don't have to get out of my car with a kid in pj's. i do however get frustrated with my order being wrong and then still having to go inside to change it. today they got the teas right, but gave me venti, a few days ago, i got one mango iced tea, and one green tea...a few days before that, i had to go inside because they gave me someone's tea from the mobile order, and she got my drive thru order. i've been to so many super busy starbucks, and they are hustling to get everyone through in a quick manner, and with the right order, this one seems to need more espresso or something to speed them up and also some drilling on getting the orders right in spite of being busy. nothings worse than going through the drive thru and then having to go inside to fix your order. i am very polite, and don't usually make a big deal about it, but this needs to be handled.

this happened a few weeks ago, and i am finally responding. it is consistent with the many one star recent postings about service, and in this case respect for customers. i am an 82 year old jew and a widower, and among other matters going through a difficult transition since my wife's accidental death. i had come into this starbucks in the morning, and really was treated so disrespectfully by one of the blonde baristas. i had not brought my cane with me into the store, but if i had, i am almost sure i would have smacked her over the head with it, and it would have given me great satisfaction. i could paraphrase gore vidal that "once the dog pees on the flowers, it ruins them for everyone." so just one bad barista makes this store verboten to me. i have been a starbucks card holder for many years. also now as an old jew, i can rightfully be very sensitive to even a hint of antisemitism much less pure disrespect. i am being very mild in this review!

i have been visiting this location about two times a week for about a month. the store is a good size with a drive-through. the costumer service is so-so. the purpose of my review is the absolutely disgusting restrooms. i've seem cleaner port-o-potty's. every single time they are dirty, smelly, wet and unsanitary. there is no possible way they are cleaned regularly. i'll be going back to cherry hill where they clean the restrooms from now on.

stopped into this starbucks for octane blast to get me through the morning. i've visited many of these coffee shops and this is the only one that seems to lack a certain level of organization for ordering. i walked in and was totally confused people standing everywhere...literally a line out the door for placing orders and receiving. i ordered a solo expresso macchiato and a blueberry scone. they must have earned it in the microwave, because when i received it it was a blueberry brick. however, the overall experience was a pleasant one. the staff was extremely accommodating...scone was replaced and the coffee got me thru the day.

not liking this location. the staff does not wear gloves when handeling food or drinks. my frappuccino was not up to the same standards of other locations. i was not happy. they took my name when i placed my order but they did not call my name when my order was ready lol. they need to work on a system. my caramel ribbon frappuccino was not good. not crunchy at all. it barely had caramel. i was sadly disappointed.

i've now been here twice for afternoon work sessions. it's spacious, there's a decent-sized parking lot, and the wifi seems to work pretty well. service was friendly, but at the same time, they got my drink wrong (i asked whether they only had white chocolate iced mocha, or whether they had regular iced mocha--and very definitely asked for the latter, but i think asking about the former confused them). also, floors could use a bit of a sweep, and tables--at least in some cases--could use a wipe. i understand sometimes a place gets busy and this isn't always possible, but once the crowd dies down, that's something that should happen. still, it's a generally comfortable environment with lots of plugs; it could just use a bit more curation (and cleaning). all in all, though, this is a solid option in an area without any independent coffee shops open past last afternoon, and i'd certainly return as needed.

went to this starbucks location this morning to order my drink and got the rudest barista i've ever met in all the states i've been to and all the starbucks i have been to this one was just a total jerk!! i didn't even get to finish ordering my drink as he cut me off multiple times. i understand not hearing me clearly but after he ignored my request for light almond milk... i ordered a venti coffee frap with light almond milk and he says "a coffee frap with almond milk ok" before i could even tell him i wanted extra frap roast pumps. so i tell him i want light almond milk and he snapped back well it's a pre measured amount so what you want extra ice????? like no you jerk!!!!! i want extra frap roast but you wouldn't let me speak to even order it!!!!! i ended up leaving because i was so upset about it. i've never been treated that way before at a starbucks. i spend a lot of $$$ there and to be spoken to like that was out of line.

what a wonderful location tucked away off the street, next to the hampton inn and suites! although there was a glitch with my mobil app order, tori the manager, handled the situation with grace and class. i enjoyed the service so much so, i purchased additional boxes of tea and coffee to demonstrate my appreciation of the wonderful customer service. there is a lovely patio location where you can enjoy the sunshine or finish your last chapter of your book!

don't come here unless you are using the drive-thru! i have been here twice in the last 3 days and both times i had to wait over 20 minutes for a toasted bagel! terrible in-store service. coffee also inconsistent in quality and flavor. i observed the staff frantically getting orders out to drive-thru customers while ignoring in-store clients. bad form , bux!

this is a really good location for a coffee shop. the seating area is large and very comfortable with free wifi. there is also a large table with 10 chairs around it and i often see people come in for informal meetings. the staff is always very pleasant - true rarity. a great place to hang out!!!

this review is specifically for january 11,2020. i (a disabled former rn whose boyfriend needs to drive me) go to this specific starbucks 7 days a week and the majority of the time i just say my name at the drive thru and they say "hey heath just pull around we got ur order." on jan 10, 2020 i forgot my phone with my bday free drink on it and instead used my starbucks credit card. i figured i'll just bring my phone on the 11th for the free drink. on january 11,2020, they denied me using the coupon which was still on my starbucks app with the redeem button still active. i told them that i simply forgot my phone, i'm a 7 day a week customer to the warminster starbucks and please honor the coupon. they said there was no possible way to acquiesce my request. considering the amount of money i spend at this establishment, the fact that i was only 1 day late, and the excellent relationships i have with most of the employees, i was very upset that they wouldn't honor my simple request. if the 5-star employees who know me and my dog so well would have been working there on the 11th they would have taken care of me as one usually does to an extremely loyal long time customer. all other days this place would have gotten 5 stars, but it's one star for the 11th of january if that's how they would so easily dismiss a long time loyal customer. on showing up to starbucks warminster on january 12, 2020, jaime the manager apologized said she saw the review and completely took care of me today. i thanked her and we had a lovely exchange. this is the employees that make
