this place awesome. i was so surprised at how fast she saw the above review and when i showed up today at the ordering screen it was jaime and she immediately told me to drive around and she would take care of the issue plus honor my bday drink tomorrow. i purposely go to this starbucks 7 days a week because of people like jaime, john, and all the other employees that know me by name and we always have a brief exchange of pleasantries at the drive through counter. they feel like family and i look forward to seeing them every single day.

i go here when i'm too lazy to get out of the car with the baby. (too bad lazy daze doesn't have a drive through.) the baristas here are always friendly but sometimes too friendly. i know they don't really care how my day is going or what i am up to that day. i especially dislike one particular girl at this location that talks in this high pitched, totally fake voice. really annoying. if you aren't sincere, than at least be real.

i've been here a few times and i keep forgetting to review them. i have never had an issue with this starbucks. the staff are always nice and cheerful. i am somewhat particular about the drink i order and they never roll their eyes or make me feel like a "difficult customer". today, service was fast and easy with virtually no wait. i wish i could review starbucks as an whole company because my experience, no matter which store i go to, is always pretty great.

place is overrun with aggressive homeless ppl. management needs to run them off. have compassion for homeless, but don't want to be aggressively panhandled while i'm trying to eat. i'm a gold card carrying starbucks hard core customer whose been to many locations across the country and the staff at this location is really rude. second visit here they were all talking in front of me, turned their backs to me when i approached the counter and kept taking, and then all walked away and did not take my order. finally one of them who was taking came back and helped me. guy who got my food and drink was rude. won't come back here if in the area.

i've been coming here week now so i thought i'd wait before i wrote this review i come in around noon every day sandwiches are always gone by noon so if you want one get here early----- also can't seem to understand the difference between and iced caramel machitto and a hot caramel machitto since i order iced and they give me hot
we waited for over 55 min this morning at 10am and left without our order or our money back. this place sucks!!!!

average starbucks. i have been through the drive thru and inside many times. parking lot is always full and sometimes i have to park in the neighboring hotels lot. the employees are friendly, however there are always new people here. accuracy of the orders are sacrificed because they are always training new people. i am pretty leinient on my scoring because of that. i am not reviewing starbucks products on this review because i have done so in others. their products taste the same as every other one around.
frappacino was fine, but i asked the barista to wipe off the counter where i was seated and was handed a wipe. not cool!
great experience here. easy walk from tpac. line moved fast. inside and outside seating. i do wish they had stand alone wifi.

also stoked that this is so close to home. i don't use this place enough. it's not too busy during the day and when it is busy the noise level is still moderate. great place to meet people or have a business meeting.

i would stop by this location more often but the parking lot design makes it frustrating. the drive thru line blocks the parking lot. the entrances are poorly designed where cars block each other in both directions. if the flow was improved they would probably ove a lot more cars through. my other issue with this location is the consistancy of the drinks. i usually get a vanilla latte and i often wonder if i got the wrong drink.
my go-to place when i'm at work in west philly. the newest starbucks in the area with friendly partners, fast service and fun seasonal drinks; back-to-school frappucino

edi: i'm making this edit today to say that an employee was extremely rude to me. i sat there for a few minutes, looking at my mobile order. they kept walking past everyone waiting for mobile orders. it was so frustrating and i was about to be late for work (which is why i did mobile order!). i finally asked an employee "can you please just hand me my drink, it's sitting right there" and he was very rude. he threw his hands up and said have a nice day. i was not rude in the way i asked, it took him 5 seconds to hand it to me. this starbucks continuously disappoints me. it does not do well for the starbucks brand. this is the longest line i have ever experienced in a drive through. takes a minimum of 20 minutes no matter the time of day. not only that, but it's so hard to get someone's attention to give you your mobile order if you go inside. not only that, but at one point i ordered a mobile order 20 min ahead of time and it still wasn't ready when i got there. it's just so frustrating.

i mean, it is starbucks so you can't expect a whole lot. when i entered the store it was quite messy and nobody acknowledged me. i stood at the counter for a few minutes before i was asked for my order. i chose a peppermint mocha with whipped cream. the wait time was about 5 minutes, not too bad. the milk tasted burnt however. bleh. i was going to ask if i could have ny drink redone but the barista at the bar seemed in a bad mood so i didn't wanna make things worse.
terribly slow. took over 15 mins for a flat white. ordered it, but left to board flight. keep moving.

racist. a dangerous place for black people. the barista who called the police on the two black gentleman for not ordering while they waited for a friend needs to be fired. you need to trained your employees onbanti blackness.
great place for coffee..at silver legacy casino..lots of choices..real neat staff..people real nice..m

i like that the baristas know their coffee culture and can answer any questions. whoever set up this drive-thru, however, is an idiot. if you come from the cross street rather than entering off brentwood, prepare for some tight finagling (and the blaring horns of irritated, coffee-deprived customers) to get to the lane. and you're going to sneak in a sixty cent surcharge for adding soy milk (no upcharge for dairy) to my coffee? st. louis bread company is a block away with better coffee & no extra fees for those of us avoiding lactose. i won't go out of my way for this one.

be warned. starbucks on saint josephs university is money hungry. they will over charge you for anything if they want. employees always on the phone. not a real starbucks. no customer service skills go to the other starbucks on 46th and city line avenue or on city avenue and monument rd.

the quality is the same as every other starbucks and staff are friendly but the wait time was way more than typical! it was a good 10 to 15 minutes waiting in the drive-through lane... only a few cars in front of me

great staff! always polite and helpful. rarely do they get a drink order wrong. however, space is almost always an issue at this particular location. it's not like the ambience is bad. it's just that a lot of people don't understand how to use proper 'line etiquitte', especially when the weather is cold. there are two entrances to the store, so i think the potential for awkward encounters is high. personally, i've had some people think i was trying to cut them...when in fact, i was trying to manuever the line so the waiting line wouldn't trickle outside. honestly, if you can swing it, i'd go for a different starbucks...if the wait is long. while the staff's attitudes are great, at the end of the day, the environment is way too claustophobic. thus, more often then not, you'll probably get a whiff of some of the negative demeanors that mark certain downtown employees. on the other hand, if the wait is short...then i'd still recommend going here...just because you'll have a great experience with the staff and open window seating (if available).

outside of airport starbucks, this is the most inefficient starbucks crew i have ever encountered. we were stuck using this location for four days during spring break. the crew is too busy talking to each other to focus on the morning rush. line out the door and only one (slow) register open. while one crew member is greeting the customer, another interrupts him to ask for the pastry order (again) because he has already forgotten. never mind me, i'm just trying to order a coffee . . . the two baristas moved like sloths and (worst of all) prepared me a latte vice the cappuccino i paid nearly $5 for. only saving grace is the morning manager with the starbucks tatoo on her ankle (now that's dedication)! she needs to pull the rest of the crew up to her standards or replace them.
absolutely hate this location. however the one by st. joe's university was crowded and i needed to get some work done. it's not the cleanest starbucks nor the coziest but it will suffice.
very friendly baristas. i never had any problems with my order. clean store. drive thru is a plus!

okay first of all... i wanted starbucks, during the corona virus they only had there drive through open and that's fine but the service was absolutely great! shoutout to john!! he made my experience great!!!! awesome attitude!!!! keep up the great work!!!
this is the newest starbucks on the main line! it's the cleanest, has the friendliest, most informative staff, and the layout is perfect for working, studying or doing art!!!

parking is pretty bad here so unfortunately lose a star. and if you are on germantown pk its really only accessible if you are heading east. and the place could use a good dusting and window cleaning. staff is professional and very nice. the coffee was hot and good and the cinnamon roll was light, airy and tasty.

nice that the sbux was in the hotel, not so nice that it smelled like piss every time i walked into the place. i even saw, what appeared to be some hotel staff, walk into the area and ask each other what the smell was, but the next morning when i ventured down, the smell was still there. what didn't help the issue is that the line is not quick nor speedy in any way. funny, since the main population this place serves are business professionals who are in a hurry. the staff did not seem to be with it, and the manager on duty (read her tag), was not friendly at all to the staff, nor guests. disappointing.
its a regular starbucks, nothin fancy. right inside the casino next to the slot machines. was never a fan of starbucks. i was here just because i couldn't find a decent coffee shop around.

i had absolutely no idea that this location was even here until i stopped by cheeseburger charley's. it was a lot larger than i thought it would be. but it didn't seem to have a lot of lounging room. i rate all coffee shops on how good they'd be for studying or reading. they had a lot of seating but it wasn't really a studying environment. i don't think i'll be going back.

such nice service. i didn't hear my name so my drink sat for a few and she offered to make me a new 1 even though it was my fault and still got (actually perfectly cooled). i said no but what a nice offer!

there isn't a lot to say about starbucks so far as their offerings go, because they're pretty consistent across the chain. the only thing to comment on then is the service and the actual store. as for the dresher store, something changed a couple of months ago and all of the faces i had become accustomed to seeing turned over in a time period of what seemed like overnight. the new people are efficient and nice, but they're a little less personable than the former employees. i have not had any issues with the service otherwise -- they've been prompt and accurate with my drink orders. as for the store itself, it's generally clean and there is ample seating upstairs. they have a variety of table sizes as well as the over sized armchairs, so sitting there during a lunch break reading or knitting is very inviting. people seem to "fight" over the outlets as there aren't many available. the parking is horrendous. people make their own spots and park in the handicapped spaces. i've even seen people drive up on the grass next to the dumpsters. some people tend to camp out in their cars there and have phone conversations or check email -- this is not the place for that. people also don't abide by the "one way" directive, so that makes things worse. i would say one out of seven times i go there i give up on finding a spot and drive down the road to the maple glen location. the parking situation is what it is -- it's not a deterrent, but it's definitely an inconvenience.

bout to bring this rating down!!! i'm sorry but i have to... i refuse to rate every single starbucks or chain restaurant i've been to but this one deserves it... im surprised to see all the great comments for the staff because that is my beef exactly! maybe this is why, i tend to go always around rush hour so i probably don't get the happy staff that works in the morning. i have been to other 'bucks before in the morning maybe 8am or earlier and they are always soooooo cheerful and they say please and thank you, have a nice day, they even write ur name on the cup and say sylvia here is ur americano have a great day. and yes, that makes me feel like a million bucks... however, starbucks... why do you choose to put ur crabby staff around 4pm. they should take espresson shots to keep em on their toes. i remember one time the girl behind the counter practically rolled her eyes when i gave her my order.. i wasnt even rude or in a hurry or on my phone or listening to my music.. jeez, lady lighten up! maybe she needs a hug.

stopped in this new starbucks for a coffee after visiting my niece. the starbucks is easy in, easy out, and the people that work there are just fine. i ordered a vanilla latte iced, it took about the time i placed the order until i got to the window to pay for my drink to be ready.

it's starbucks. there is no need to review my caramel apple spice because you should have already tried it by now! this location seems like the only starbucks on this side of town. i pick my grandmother up from church in irvington and i usually stop here on the way home. i have read in other reviews that the wait time can be pretty long, so sunday afternoon is a blessing because i never have to wait. i go to the drive through every time so as not to leave my granny alone. we are in and out pretty quick and i am sipping my fave starbucks drink in a couple minutes after it cools off a little :)

the baristas have no sense of urgency. i need my coffee. this is very urgent. it does get busy from time to time, but even when there's nobody in line, it still takes a good ten minutes to get my drink... but hey, starbucks is starbucks and green tea frappuccinos call my name~
i drive out of my way to go to this starbucks. annie is great and there is a fella inside with blonde hair who makes my drink perfectly every time!
service is okay can get very busy at time. typical starbucks coffee it's okay it's overpriced . hard to find a seat because so many people come in there and sit on the computer for hours
the worst. rude and doesnt know how to ring up a refill. go across the street to the other starbucks.

fast, friendly, drive-thru, new & nitro coffee! i was very ecstatic about this new starbucks popping up close to home. i had high expectations & was not disappointed. this location has an easy in & out access drive-thru & they're one of the few stores in all of tucson for that damn delicious nitro coffee-for now anyway, until other stores rebuild & make room for the machine. the inside of the store is nice & clean with friendly staff. i really love their patio because it has booth-type seating with electric outlets! perfect coffee escape for a student & anyone who truly appreciates the starbucks experience!
while i usually prefer the drive up locations, it's nice to stop in and chat with the barista's.
i always get a frappachino here and it is so good! its a nice little starbucks in indianapolis and real close to my house. it is a great spot if anybody needs to find a better starbucks to go to.

this absolutely sucked my a$$ i ordered a venti and paid for a venti and got a hot damn grande! then i was like at least i still got this burrito and i tasted it and just my luck it was just booty hole another cherry on top was the drive thru window lady.... let me tell you her breath was appalling that's it that's all i have to say.
great customer service! your bathroom was a little dirty though. the floor out in the lobby had drink spills all over it as well. but the customer service makes up for those spills

i love any starbucks, this one has a lot of seats and feels a little cramped in cold weather. in warm weather, sitting outside is a pleasure. comfortable and pretty seating makes it a summer favorite.
2 stars for coffee.. sometimes it's very weak and flaw. 5 stars for customer service. polite & friendly baristas.
always a fan of the very helpful staff at starbucks :) they are always smiling and peppy, no matter how early / late it is.

i love starbucks, & i especially appreciate the consistency in their drinks no matter which location you're visiting. what makes this particular location even better? the staff! i am a drive-thru kinda girl & always drive away smiling :) there is a very nice young man & a couple of darling women i have seen there, each of them very sincere and sweet. i'll be back again and again!
this starbucks is my " i feel crafty" stop. it's right near home depot and pet store. they have great staff. jade rocks the house!. she goes out of her way for her customers. well stocked.

pains me to give such a low rating, but customer service was poor, product average, and as a passionate coffee drinker, it was almost pathetic to even stay for any length of time. wifi was spotty, and to top it off, an employee began dusting right over my head as i was drinking my coffee. i left. baristas were loud and not very friendly. starbucks: you can do much better. i know you can. fail.

i went to the mccarran drive-through starbucks on a pumpkin spice latte mission the other day. having recently moved to reno i found it a bit hard to find. i had to double back and circle around a bit. i know that's my own problem tho. hopefully i'll figure reno out eventually. this starbucks is located in a business park and seems like a pretty business-y starbucks. it's super-nice from the outside which is the only part i saw. a nice, new, modern building. drive through a little confusing too, but hopefully i'll figure it out soon. once i got to the drive through the guy who helped me was super-nice. i got a venti pumpkin spice latte as it was a cold fall day. i love starbucks as a general rule. knowing reno had a bunch of starbucks reassured me that i would be okay living in reno. this was not my favorite location as it was sort of far away and hard to get into, but it was a good starbucks experience overall. when i got to the drive-through window the sweet tattoo-ed hipster handed me my drink. nice hipsters instead of snotty ones are such a new concept after los angeles. this nice hipster neglected to give me a straw. that would have been nice, but wasn't that big of a deal. my pumpkin spice latte was delicious as usual. i loved it. i gave grandma a little drink of it when i got home and she liked it too. i'll probably check out the other drive-through starbucks in reno before coming back to this one but it's nice to know it's there. love that pumpkin spice!

this is not the best starbucks but they get four stars for having quick service and the standard starbucks fare. if you get there early in the morning, there is typically no line at the drive-thru. the parking lot for this starbucks is horribly designed and the drive-thru is even worse. it is especially at the apex of a u-turn. if you have a larger car, it is impossible to get close to the callbox. i have seen lines here back up to ten cars. the wait can be long, so if you are in a hurry, this is not your spot. on this particular visit, i went inside to avoid the drive-thru line and ended up leaving before placing an order. starbucks could probably put another location in ambler. this one just gets too much traffic..

i am a loyal and frequent client of starbucks and have been for years, i travel often throughout pa, northern nj and nyc. often i have business in the 422 corridor. folks - this is by far the worst starbucks i have ever been to. - clueless staff and plain stupid - incorrect orders constantly after explaining a basic black coffee - wrong lids placed on beverages - always long lines when the place is dead, i have seen higher volume stores pump out so fast. - once waited 30 minutes for a mobile order, basic stuff - staff looks like they are on drugs and unmotivated - never make eye contact and nasty always, arrogant and miserable as well. like you are doing them a favor - worst designed drive-thru ever and parking lot configuration - bank set up and people have no clue how to drive in it. i once contacted the regional manager - trina, she is as clueless as the staff, does not respond or care. folks - stay away from this mess, the one in limerick is just as bad - same regional manager, stay away! called corporate several times and requested refunds and made complaints. shut this dump down.
nice place to meet someone or just have some time to yourself... found out about their happy hour that starts at 3p-6p.. missed it!

usually a pretty good starbucks when i come here but, today they were short in the display case of just about everything from sandwiches to pastries. it looked rather picked over like a blight zone! very unappetizing to say the least. there were enough partners in the store that someone could've taken a moment to refill and restock. the staff was very friendly and my beverage came out perfect, so they only get three stars today.

maybe it depends on the time you hit up this starbucks, but my experiences have been pretty great. i tend to go to this starbucks early in the morning, somewhere between 5 and 6:30am. the employees are always upbeat and friendly, especially considering the time. there's never any wait and my ice coffee with soy milk is always made perfectly. it's my once a week surviving summer camp treat.

this location is chronically slow both in the store and in the drive thru. the employees are friendly enough but never apologetic when there is a big backlog. recently i waited more than ten minutes in the store for my drink. i was waiting with lots of other people (some who gave up waiting in the drive thru and came inside to wait). if i hadn't just paid close to $5 for my green tea frap i would have left! the one customer told me to give feedback via their website and i would receive a response and maybe even a gift card. well, i did and i never received a response or gift card. what a waste of my time, yet again! shame on me for wasting my time and $$.

found a dead fly in our latte today! disgusting! obviously the baristas do not double check the drinks hey make! changing to dd! for that price of coffee i expect exceptional drinks! not true at this place! grossed out!

the service was fast and friendly. the sandwich was hot and fresh, however a bit burnt which made the bread hard. the taste wasn't bad after getting through the texture of the bread. the bacon and egg was cooked really well. the drink was hot and tasted great. i loved the cake pops as well. there weren't any napkins in the bad which was a bit annoying. overall i wanted to give a 3 because for $16.02, a burnt sandwich and no napkins is simply average. i gave a 4 because the service was nice. i would recommend for coffee and/or cake pops but not food.
this is one of the worst starbucks in the area. they are consistently slow and regularly screw up your order. avoid.

today i went for my usual coffee and when i went up to the register to order, i found that i had left my debit card at home. the cashier was kind enough to say "don't worry about it we'll take care of it. " and gave me my drink for free... she totally made my day! and because of her i will always recommend this place. :) thank you
the service is always friendly and sincere here. plus, it's not super crowded on week nights. i usually stop here after my usual runs to sam's club and walmart before heading back over the bridge.
ladies were very friendly and line moved quick. spacious location with fair amount of seating. was in and out in no time.
dark roast sucks. i think i'll stick with dunkin' on this one. i love the atmosphere though, i go in with my dunkin' cup and study. food is okay too.
nice place with nice environment. i like my starbucks once in a while. this is a good place to get mine since it's right by my church.

for the purposes of studying and doing work, starbucks is great. they (finally) have free wi-fi, lots of electric plugs, etc. etc. however, as a coffeeshop, starbucks fails miserably. the drinks are overpriced, over sweetened, and the location in bryn mawr in particular has really bad atmosphere. the baristas here have no idea what good espresso drinks are--drinks are way too hot, and there is no difference between lattes and cappuccinos. not only that, there's also a really sleazy male barista who flirts with any female customer under a certain age--you may note that some of the positive reviews here are from women who have received special treatment. parking is awful, and there's only one way in and out. i actually think there's a lot of room for competition in this area, especially since saxby's is just as bad, and milkboy is way too expensive in my opinion.

after spending way too much money at starbucks in my life, this is the one that has made me decide to drastically cut back. after they made my $5 order wrong (pretty basic - mocha w skim milk), i got a condescending lecture about the starbucks vocabulary and how to order properly so they dont get confused. or, i could just go just about anywhere else.

i ordered the green tea latte but with a cappuccino ratio of milk. unfortunately, the cashier wrote, "latte, half foam" so i got a very heavy drink. i explained the miscommunication to the barista, she knew exactly what i wanted and remade it perfectly. 5 stars for the young lady!

drove up to my favorite starbucks this morning in the hopes of using my new starbucks app which i'd downloaded onto my iphone. just needed someone to show me how to use the app. i'd linked it to my paypal but couldn't figure out how to charge using my app. the drive thru guy couldn't help and neither could any of the other workers in the store. they said they were clueless. can you believe that? suspicious. dumb. worthless.

the location is outstanding and the staff is excellent. it is without question the best starbucks location i have visited. granted, i am not much of a coffee drinker and have only been to 4 or 5 locations in my entire life, but this store is at the top of the "world class customer service" list. i am known to have a plain bagel with cream cheese and a btl (arnold palmer). the one time they made my drink wrong (because i was chatting up the young man and distracted him), they offered to remake my drink without hesitation. in fact, he caught his mistake as he was handing me the cup. i took the drink he offered and told him to not worry about it (i felt a little responsible, i think he was new and i was distracting him). they remembered the next time i came in and comped me my drink. i know you have a lot of choices when buying coffee in tucson, but i promise you will not be disappointed if you choose to visit this location. not on product, customer service, or their commitment to our community.

i am always blown away when people i barely know remember me. our cashier not only remembered me, but asked me if i still had partner numbers from the very short time she knew me as a barista. we chatted a little while as she rang up my order for a green tea lemonade and when the barista on bar handed me my drink, it was a green tea latte (the cup markings are super close). i actually would've rather had a green tea latte -- i just didn't want to pay for it. the tea lemonades are cheaper. both baristas apologized over and over and offered to remake it, but insisted it was fine. more than fine, as i said earlier. they personified what it means to be a starbucks partner. sure, they messed up, but they took the appropriate steps to correct it and i left a very happy customer :)

probably the worst starbucks ever been too. took forever walking to even find it. you would think in a touristy town of nashville they would have starbucks on main street. where even many open deserted spots could be. this was all uphill and then very crowded and tiny. the server taking order was nice but girl that made my tea left it to me to add my own raw sugar in tea. usually they always do it. it was so cramped to even wait for order. many groups of people walked up and saw wait and walked away. so losing business.

this starbucks is in the safeway shopping center south of the golden dragon and einstein's bagels. there is outside and inside seating. i went to get the pumpkin spice skinny frappuccino which i have always liked and was glad it is back. the baristas were friendly and very helpful. it took a little bit to get the drink but a big order ahead of me. i enjoyed the frappuccino. it was extra spicy and i like it that way. i appreciate that i can get a "skinny" version and it is just as tasty.

i work for starbucks currently.. and my store usually always gets the drinks correct unless it's a simple mistake of it being unsweetened or missing a syrup ingredient but won't change the drink and we always fix the drinks on the spot if we made a mistake, even during peak hours we make it a priority. one day i bought a creme frap for my co worker who was pregnant and waiting for me at a restaurant, while i waiting on my order at starbucks, i specifically ordered a venti creme frap, so no frap roast that contain caffeine. my co worker is going into her 9th month of pregnancies, and when i received her creme frap which was suppose to be white in color but it was tan brown. i couldn't tell in the dark lighting whether it was a tinted white or a tan which shows there is frap roast in the drink. and i asked them, "are you sure this is a creme frap?" and the two baristas gave each other a look and said "yes" well turns out after 10 mins of walking back to my co worker there was frap roast in it and she asked me not to go back and make a deal out of it but it upsets me because the cup was written correctly! but the barista's f* up! and this is the fourth time they've done this with my orders!! i order about 3 drinks each time i go but one of the few are always wrong! i asked for caramelized honey frap! i get caramel frap! there is a huge taste difference! i understand it gets busy but i too am a barista for starbucks and i work during peak hours and i make sure i make the correct drink! they write the order correct but however makes the drink never makes it correctly! and it's not even the same person! each time i go it's a someone different who makes it but they still screw up my order..... it's just annoying and since i understand they are in a rush i leave it be but when i asked for creme frap! i expect a creme frap! anything else is okay, but when someone wants a decaf give them a decaf!!
this is a great drive-thru starbucks. great location and friendly servers. met my friends here for coffee and we all loved it. visiting in town from san diego feels like home.
best starbucks in the area! only a drive through would make this a 5 star. friendly staff makes it a great place for coffee.
every time i go here, i receive my order very quickly and the staff is always exceptional! i've never had a problem here.
the staff is always pleasant whenever i come here. which isn't very often. unless it is during the winter season. hazlenut lattes are unbeatable.
friendly staff! quality iced coffee. can't warm a breakfast sandwich up to save their lives.

i go to starbucks very often. i have one near my home on gandy, but after one visit i always favor going to this starbucks. mainly due to their fantastic costumer service. one star employee that i'd love to point out is niki. she always greets me with a smile when i come to the drive-through window and always has the best snack recommendations. she is a pro. i highly recommend coming to this starbucks. thank you again niki.

pretty disappointed with this starbucks. i moved to the north valleys a couple of months ago so i started going to this starbucks. a couple months ago i stopped by close to closing to get a coffee because i was working the graveyard shift. i pulled up to the drive thru, placed my order, then the person who took my order told me "it's going to be a while because the person in front of you literally ordered 15 drinks." closing was in 10 minutes, and there was no one else in line. they had the person who ordered all of the drinks park. what was stopping them from making my 1 drink? if someone orders 15 drinks in the drive thru, obviously they are a jerk and should wait....why wouldn't you make the 1 drink first? the other person is going to have to wait forever anyhow. i didn't get coffee that night, i left. this morning i went to this location to get coffee before work. i waited in the drive thru for quite some time without anyone taking my order. i decided to call the store, while i was in the drive thru and the employee who answered told me it would be about 15 minutes because they were running late. it was 04:45 and they were supposed to be open at 04:30. i'm not up at 04:45 because it's fun....obviously i don't have 15 minutes to wait around for coffee! :) the time i spent sitting in the drive thru could have been spent getting coffee somewhere else. starbucks is my go-to for coffee. it's usually convenient because it's a quick stop on my way to work. i go to starbucks almost daily. as a result of the events that unfolded this morning, i didn't get my starbucks, i am sipping on some crappy gas station coffee as i write this unfortunate review, and i was almost late for work :( not cool starbucks...not cool. my other 97 visits to this location have been good but the fact that you were not there when i needed you this morning gives you 2 stars.

this starbucks should have its license revoked. i think they have a work share program with the tsa. hungover 'lady' wearing sunglasses at the register has all the personality of a tree fern and half the warmth.

look, i typically steer clear of starbucks when there are local options available, but i spent about a month going to this one when my coffee grinder broke...and i like it-- a lot! the employees here are much more down to earth compared to the starbucks by my house. they remembered me shortly after i started frequenting this location and their conversation is pretty genuine. i also felt like they burn their coffee less at this location...or is that my imagination? but the *number one reason* i feel compelled to write a 5 star review for this location at speedway and country club is the fact that they recycle! as explained to me by one of the baristas, recycling is not a corporate mandate at starbucks, rather it is a choice made by each individual franchise. with all the plastic milk jugs and packaging that they receive supplies in, starbucks generates an insane amount of recyclable plastic waste that quite often goes into landfills. not here! if you are going to buy a triple-double soy mocha chocha blended whatsit (or just a good ol' 'cuppa), you should support this particular starbucks, one of the few in town that recycles.

it's a sad day when a starbucks has bad customer service. her name is jo and you'd think with a name like that she'd understand mix ups between erin and aaron but she wanted to blame me for handing me my sausage egg sandwich and nitro cold brew. the receipt was a mobile order and i am aaron, then after her coworkers asked her she tried to blame me even making me go to the trash for the receipt. yes, i had to go into the trash to get my receipt. never again jo, i pray management sees this and other poor reviews and decides to do something about it.
this has got to be the worst starbucks in canada. always new people, the drinks are inconsistent and the service is horrible. definitely needs new management!

i went to this location with my 6yr old daughter she wasn't feeling well that day and accidentally dropped her smoothie on the ground. she felt so bad and started to cry. the staff was amazing they gave her a new one and told her it was ok. they even gave her an extra cute little cookie.
the service was great met my friend there for coffee. it has be come my favorite meeting place. my coffee was very good. smooth and full of flavor.
the baristas are always friendly and it's the speediest drive-thru of all the starbucks drive-thru's i've been to.

nice to have good quality coffee like starbucks at a rest stop but they seem to be pretty slow considering the amount of people working at this store. i guess beggars cannot be choosers here. definitely a contrast from the nyc starbucks stores. my drip coffee was good.

i frequent this bux location at least three times a week, usually by means of the drive thru. i've gotta say: fantastic service and friendliest cashiers. i highly recommend it if in the carmel or zionsville area! :)
great coffee and pastries, they add alot of ice so i suggest asking for less. a little pricey too. but overall great place to stop by in the morning.

i come here all the time. most of the time, the staff is on point! a few times ive had one particular young lady at the drive thru who just doesn't understand coffee, money or customer service. tonight in particular, i ordered a dirty chai with an extra shot and she told me i was ordering a made up drink... ummmm...no. that's a very common drink served at most coffee shops. it's literally on their menu. when she went to hand me the drink, she just shoved her arm in my car and didn't say a word. just hoped i saw her. it's very weird and she needs training... everyone else is great. just not her....
they made they drinks pretty fast, and some of them were friendly. but they didn't have any table and chairs left when we went.

love the staff here. they are always super friendly and fast! they make an effort to learn your name/face and your drink. this is one of my favorite locations because they get my drink right every time. the only issue i have are all the rude high schoolers that come in being loud an obnoxious demanding water after school. to which they still are given ice cold water with a smile. love stopping here in the am between 9am -1pm or in the evening after 5 to avoid the kids. nothing against them, i was in high school once...but i do like to enjoy the café when it's peaceful and quiet. (: plus it's nice to stop in for a quick lunch or a pick me up when i have some grocery shopping to do at the fry's next door as well as the wells fargo conveniently located inside the store. it makes my day easier to have everything in the same plaza.

i recently returned to town after a trip and when i went in to order my drink, the barista made it a point to welcome me back and ask me how i'd been since she hasn't seen me. i know how busy this starbucks can get, so the fact they really get to know and remember their customers is impressive! kudos to their customer service.

i went in this morning and ordered an iced mocha. tatiana greeted my friend and i with a smile and a warm welcome even though it was 6:50 in the morning. brandon greeted everyone with a good morning welcome to starbucks even thought he was prepping drinks. this locations stafff has never failed to impress me. whenever i need starbucks i will go to this location because of the great customer service that i receive from here.

love that they 20 minutes closed earlier than their advertised time and several people were in line trying to get coffee and were completely ignored by the girl inside with a headset. utterly pissed. i need to make a long drive tonight and need some coffee.

this starbucks has very nice customer service. the manager, miguel, is a 5-star manager. he's always in a great, mellow mood. they are very busy place. however, that tells me it's because their good! they have plenty of tables inside and out. my husband and i go there almost everyday so i can start my day off right, with a smile!

i can't begin to express my gratitude to the young lady who held a bag of decaf christmas blend coffee for me. i cannot have a lot of caffeine in my system so decaf is my usual go to when it comes to coffee. she held this bag for me overnight and when i arrived the next morning at the store, she was not there but alex and the gentleman who was also there were very sweet and accommodating. alex told me of another location nearby that might have the decaf christmas blend and wouldn't you know they had it! so now i've got 2 bags of the decaf christmas blend to last me!
rude staff and drinks are not made properly. i have thrown out my drink on multiple occasions.
this is the worst starbucks actually. i come here often and the hours are different every damn day of the week. if you're gonna close at 1pm-3pm everyday just shut the place down.

this location is always so busy....but we like coming here as it's the closest drive-thru for us when we get our starbucks cravings. the staff is always so pleasant at this location and makes an effort to strike up conversation when at the window waiting for drinks to be ready. i am totally addicted to the guava green tea lemonade (no sweetener) and my daughter loves the pink drink (blended). if you've not had the guava, i highly recommend!! i've gotten some of my friends addicted to this drink. i think it's just the best and so refreshing and so pretty! :)

this used to be my go-to starbucks and i'm not sure what has happened as of late but the service is horrendous! the last 4 out of 5 orders have been incorrect. the last order resulting in swallowing a mouthful of coffee grinds only to discover 3 inches of grinds at the bottom of my cup. they are way understaffed and the staff they do have need significantly more training and support. the trash is often overflowing outside.
this appears to be the starbux inside the target. they are remodeling the store and closed it down for a bit but are reopening any minute now. no real issues here - but i come at "off" times.

i don't ever plan on writing reviews for franchises because essentially, you get the same experience as far as food/drink quality and service goes, especially when the aforementioned franchise is starbucks. it's kind of impressive and scary how all starbucks employees (regardless of their make, model, size, shape, sex, skin tone, etc etc) are almost stepford-ish in how nice, considerate and generally chipper they are. a secret (well not so secret anymore) way to test the general amiability of a starbucks barista is to walk in, look stupefied, and then say the following words: "i... don't know what to get. do you have any suggestions?" if they're good, they'll smile and give you some suggestions based on your tastes. if they're great, they'll do the same, but then offer to make you something else for free if you don't like what you bought. this is why i love starbucks and am annoyed by people who hate it. yes, they're a big faceless corporation, blah de blah, but they got so big because they make their customers feel like they're the only ones that matter for those brief two minutes. but i'm soapboxing now, so i'll cut it short. this location rocks because i love how it feels like a library with a starbucks attached. it reminds me of my undergrad days, and even simple things like the bricks in the ceiling, or how there is never a shortage of desks to lay out my books/laptop, or how all of the patrons are considerate and quiet, or even how this location is kind of set a little bit below ground making it feel all... cave-like; all these things just make me love spending time in here. say hi if you see me. i'm the brown guy with the idevice headphones on. there aren't many indians in that location, i mean it's not like there's a really nice private university nearby with indians all running amok. oh wait... ok fine, i'm the indian guy always wearing flip flops; i know most indians have an aversion to flip flops.

great staff, bigger than appears from outside the amount of personal interviews, small office meetings, and real estate closings held in here, just ruin it. from across the small place, with little soundproofing, you hear self promoting projectors and their agendas, more than you want to. fun to meet up with friends, even a cup by yourself, but there is real taking advantage of, particularly this location.

starbucks in the e concourse on mon. mid-afternoon 4/16/18- the best people working, cheerful, made a great shot- in- the- dark, and looked us in the eyes - nice to make a little human connection any day but especially while traveling.

the baristas are very nice, great environment and drinks are pretty good. however they don't seem to know how to make a lot of the drinks on the app. the other thing is they turn the ac wayyyy up and you end up freezing sitting in there longer than 5min.
drinks are good. device is fast and friendly. place is always clean.

i was asked to leave for not having a mask. i was happy to wait for my coffee outside so i asked if they could notify me while i wait outside. they said no i can not let you know when your coffee is ready. they were extremely rude. on top of it they were wearing clothing affiliated with a political movement that was found to be offensive. i would not recommend this starbucks.

ugh,, starbucks app let me down and this store did nothing to help, rather added salt to the wound. while 2 stars is harsh, starbucks is usually perfect and i'm spoiled by their perfection so this slight mishap is so rare it stands out. i came in and not very busy. my starbucks app/card payment that i have been using flawlessly for over a year decides it didn't want to automatically reload my balance. i aksed the girl to reload my starbuckscard and then charge my drink to my card. she charges me full price for my drink by charging it to my credit card instead of reloading my starbcuks card . since there was no line, i was super annoyed that she ignored this 20 second request. to charge my starbucks card. it is less than a dollar but when i come here everyday, it adds up. it says something about starbucks as a whole that this location is considered sub par. pretty impressive record. 1 annoyance out of the dozens of times i've been here. then again, there are starbucks i've been to hundreds of times and they've never had a mishap even this slight. considering we continue to pay these crazy prices for what is pretty much coffee and milk. i'd hope they would have perfect service.

my star rating is low because this is the smallest and busiest starbucks i've ever been to. at any time you would expect to find a working person get coffee you can expect the line is out the parking lot, across the street, and backed up to the light. i can't imagine how much business they are losing simply because of that. if they opened up another location in the area that would greatly help. i'm also not sure if they changed their to go cups but mine are always leaking! i have coffee stains all over my car :( fix the location problem, fix your cups and i'll be a happy camper
i love going to this location. the staff are always friendly they're fast and they all seem to be happy when they are working. probably one of the friendliest in the city.

this is not my first time coming here and they can never seem to get my order correct. i went in a asked for a refund and they didn't even ask what was wrong with my drink. they are definitely lacking customer service! i'm done coming here
did a mobile order and it took 13 minutes to get the drink. store was busy and not running both espresso machines simultaneously.
so confusing, awful drivethru/walk up window set up. if it's busy good luck figuring it out.

very nice starbucks in heart of doylestown. this starbucks sits in a historic building which adds to the cozy inside atmosphere complete with a fireplace. there is also outside seating overlooking downtown. the service is very good and i have been pleased. parking can be tricky with a small public metered lot behind the building. there is plenty of street parking if you can find a spot.
very long wait and the worst frapuccino i ever tasted. big chunk of ice with a little coffee. never going back.

this is really a top notch starbucks! it's larger than most, with lots of little areas for you to hang out with friends, or just sit alone and get some work done. the baristas are knowledgeable, quick, and don't seem to get flustered if there's a bit of a line. there often is, but they get through it quickly and you never have to wait long on your coffee. they truly care about the community as well. i work for a children's theater, and they've welcome us with open arms to bring our kids in to read scripts. there's a super cozy fireplace as well, and great outdoor space for the summer months. this starbucks does a great job of serving you like a neighborhood coffee shop and doesn't feel like a big chain conglomerate store like some of the rest

starbucks is my pick up of the day and by far has the best dark roast when compared to most coffee shops. however, beware of going to this location around 5 pm. i placed an order at 532 for cold brew black venti, paid and revived my order at 550.
smaller cups,yet they are more expensive than the starbucks down the street. i paid 83 cents more here for my usual order.

i place an online order. it was a lunch box, so there wasn't much to do but have it ready. i went there and one of the girls told me your order isn't ready yet, so i told her it's just a lunch box, so she said oooh ok go ahead and grab it. they didn't have the box i ordered, so i chose a different one, and the cashier charged me $3 for it. i was so upset because i used my rewards to pay for the previous box, so i felt deceived and also pay extra for a $6 box. i basically end up paying $9. i am upset not just for the poor customer service but for the extra fee. she acted like if i was trying to steal it. the first girl i understand she was busy making the drinks, so she didn't bother me at all. i mean i can grab a box. however, the cashier displayed such poor customer service. she didn't even attempted to look in the back to make sure they didn't have the other lunch box. she also wasn't even busy. i wasted in line to have her full attention. just upsetting. also, the bread is old, and the box has bacon which i don't eat, that is why i ordered the egg box. $9 to eat half of an apple. great!

i was pretty disappointed to find out that this starbucks is closed all summer after advertising that it would be open all summer online. you have to actually go down to the store to find out that it's closed over summer. they need to update the hours for the summer stating that it's closed as well as the signage around campus saying that it's closed..

terrible experience at this location. they gave away my sandwich to another customer and after informing them that i had not received my sandwich 10 minutes after getting my coffee, they showed no urgency and took another 15 minutes to put my sandwich into the heat....

inside of cafe is so messy, not clean at all,, my coffee, americano,, tasted horrible. which is hard to make it terrible ,, it is just black coffee after all. and my partner ordered caramel macchiato,, it was not any better. horrible as well. waste of money on coffee

no one is looking to yelp to figure out if they like starbucks or not. they already know. starbucks does starbucks well. along those lines, this starbucks seems to be about average. it has all of your standard starbucks products, decent service, large windows and a small area to socialize, work or just relax. i am a fan of starbucks corporate culture, i believe they treat their employees really well and through their efforts they are improving working culture around the usa. but, because i've been in tens of dozens of starbucks around the country and globe i don't tend to rate them 5 stars. a great starbucks gets four, and an average starbucks gets 3. this one is pretty decent- but hasn't yet struck me as amazing. this was my second visit to this starbucks- of which i anticipate there being many. it is the closest to my spring internship. if they impress me further i will update this review.

i love this starbucks! i stop by at least five days a week on my way into work. yes the drive thru does get a little backed up from time to time, but the customer service is worth the wait! this location has some of the best baristas in town!

this place is crowded like most starbucks and the coffee is too bitter for my taste. my main problem here (and all starbucks in general) is the generally slow service. the staff are mostly good people but there seems to be a lot of chaos. it seems like most of the work falls on the barista and the rest of the staff doesn't know what everyone else is doing. go to saxby's instead.

i was really happy that there was a starbucks in the casino. the barista was very friendly. the reason i'm only giving 4 stars is because it was really expensive. i guess harrahs hits them with the resort fee. still love the convenience.

this is a fully licensed starbucks that is inside the giant grocery store. if you've ever been into one of these mini-starbucks before, you know the deal- * they generally have the usual selection of drinks. * pastries/sandwiches are limited. * merchandise is severely limited. compared to other stores in the area, this locations claim to fame is a beautiful outside cafe.

this is possibly the worst managed starbucks that i have ever visited. inexplicable and inconsistent pricing. good customer service is seriously lacking at this particular starbucks. when i brought this to the attention of the store manager, she insisted on blaming everyone else. yet she was the one that charged me differently, on three separate occasions, for the exact same drink. honestly, they really need to find someone to run this starbucks that knows what they're doing.
the wosrt starbucks i've ever been to! 15-20 minutes for 1 frappe!? unacceptable. looked like nobody knew what they were doing, running around looking confused. i will not be back.

first time at this starbucks location and it was an amazing experience - way better than the location closer to my house. 1) it's fairly new! if you're going to try the drive-thru, there is a screen that shows your order. it's great to make sure that your order is accurate. 2) they are fast! there were about 5 cars ahead of me in the drive-thru but i was still out of there in 5-10 minutes. i purposely don't go to the starbucks near my house (of 56th and lafayette) because 5 cars in line there is an automatic 15 min wait. 3) the staff seems so friendly! keep up the good work representing the starbucks brand appropriately.

as always starbucks is great but expensive. i tried the new maple pecan in a venti size, not impressed:( it has a very weird taste...taste like coffee mixed with peanut butter. not a good combination. i personally won't order this drink again but it was a double star day! next time i will stick with my peppermint mocha.

i was relieved starbucks was open on easter! i made a trip in here that sunday afternoon to catch up on work and house hunt online. the barista made my drink very efficiently and there was lots of space to sit down. this is one of the bigger starbucks locations in nashville.

the staff will do literally anything to make your day and be personal. if they ask if you want something made off of their clover press do not say no! so worth it. every penny. i love these people. shantel is the store manager and i wish she was my mom. tony is hilarious and personal, phil is knowledgable and up for conversation, christine is like your best friend, sakinah is sweet and adorable, evan is a polite model, mary knows her stuff and you can ask her anything, darcy makes the place feel like home, she loves the popcorn and you will too. eli knows the best way to add the right flavor or spice to any coffee, let him help you! he helped me find my soul coffee -- verona with cinnamon. come here and be merry!

i always great and friendly service here. my order always taste great. the only downfall is the speed of service. this store is really slow, but due to it's convenient location it doesn't make sense to drive to another store. just wish they would pick up the speed, i've never waited less then 20 minutes in the drivethru!

i'm a former barista, though for a different starbucks, so i tend to judge them a bit strictly. this one makes the drinks correctly and quickly. i'm not always thrilled with the customer service as sometimes they can be a bit rude. but most of them are friendly. i'll go back knowing that my very specific and complicated drink will be made right. for a busy starbucks, they do very well and have their jobs down pat.

often the line is really backed up in the morning and there's no parking anywhere. keep in mind if you're stopping on a weekday morning. as of recently i've noticed if i order a extra shot of espresso it doesn't get added into my beverage and sometimes they don't put my requested soy milk. when i notify them i can tell i don't have the extra shot of espresso they give me attitude and say that the paper says it so the barista must have added it, when it's quite clear it doesn't have more than the usual. getting pretty frustrated since it's the only starbucks on my way to work and this is happening more and more lately.
two perfect drinks after a night of zero sleep. soy misto for me, mango smoothie for the bf. quick service too. thanks starbucks!
pretty appalling. apparently, although i've successful picked up mobile orders from probably 50 different starbucks locations, this location wants you to go inside to get your order. bizarre.

i visited the isla vista, ca store which i understand serves primarily students in the surrounding community. however, as a 53-year-old multi-year gold card holder, i could not have possibly been made to feel more unwelcome. the manager was far too busy chatting with his friends and running around behind the counter doing part of everyone's job in a vain attempt to train, he took only half of my order, left me standing at the counter awaiting checkout while orders from the other register continued to jump in front of my poor cup, languishing unattended beside the register. when the young lady at the other register finally rescued me, she helplessly explained she was "just in training" and promptly put my cup behind several others that had been ordered by customers coming in long after me. more than ten minutes after my initial order, my grande nonfat latte was finally served, about 2/3's full. despite the lack of customers at the time, i had no interest in asking the hapless barista for a redo nor did the manager offer anything but a "your drink is coming" during the entire wait (though at least he pulled himself away from his buddies in the store long enough to dismiss me curtly). unless you're the manager's "bro," don't bother...

last saturday on 12/26/20 i visited this starbucks through the drive thru. my sister got her beverage and i got my standard hot chocolate. there was a bit of a wait but that is standard for any starbucks drive thru these days. the beverages were decent and the location on easton road (route 611) is very convenient if not very crowded. overall, it was a decent experience.

this location is frequently understaffed. however, i order through their mobile app. and i'm able to simply walk in and grab my drink. i love that they offer coconut milk for drinks so you can feel somewhat healthy. i always order the caramel macchiato and it's my favorite drink.
friendly staff, fast service and love the layout! all the greatest things you want from a coffee house. especially if it's starbucks!

quick breakfast before boarding our flight. everyone had on gloves and masks to serve customers quickly. i don't drink coffee, but they have many favors to choose from. i had an egg sandwich with lemonade and my husband and son had eggs, cheese and bacon sandwich. we will return soon.
the people who work here are awesome. it just always seems to take an incredibly long time to get my coffee.

the environment is pretty relaxed and i like that it's big enough for a decent amount of people. but the drive-thru can be extremely busy and the intersection at bloomingdale can be a mess. friendly staff though and it's typical of all starbucks. i return fairly often.

don't get me wrong super friendly service, but almost every single time i come here they manage to mess up my order whether that be making a hot drink iced or an iced drink hot, or not heating up bakery items. you're better off driving to brookhaven. i wish i could give a better review because the staff is so nice and the location is cute and convenient, but for the $$ your order should be right the first time

my secret starbucks place! this is the best bux i've been to in st louis. lots of seating, different types of seats (high tables, couches, normal seats), big tables, very fast google internet, friendly wait staff, very clean, lots of parking. only downside is it closes at 9pm but so does everything else in st. louis. this is the go-to place to get work done, or just chill and talk with your buddies and enjoy some starboox coffee ***oh! the internet is mad fast here!!!!***
worst cup of coffee ever. tastes like it sat in the pot for 6 hours. if you're going to have coffee on brew, please make sure it's fresh. this is gross.
they close at 6pm on june 14 2021 because there is not enough employees or they choose not to work until 8pm. i wouldn't expect it from starbucks. i should choose another coffee place.
they're closed until further notice ): there's a sign blocking the drive thru saying not much more than that, hopefully they will reopen soon and i'll change my stars! i hope y'all are all good :)

the usual! no complaints. got the coffee. although the person taking my order must have been new because she got it wrong. the barista had no problem taking care of it. a typical starbucks and layout!

went through the drive-thru and ordered iced tea and an iced mocha. sat in the drive-thru for 15 minutes, 10 of them waiting for my iced mocha to be made. 7 staff behind the counter, 2 actually making drinks, several playing on their phones, and then after waiting for 7 minutes they asked if it was ok if my iced mocha did not have whipped cream as they were still making it. at that point i needed to leave so i said fine, but it still took another 3 minutes for my drink to come out. as my drink was handed to me i watched a barista put whipped cream on another drink. many young kids playing around and chit chatting, not working. definitely not going back to this location, poor service!

while the parking isn't ideal, the staff are incredible. they're talented and caring! they remember your name and have an interest in serving each customer well. holly is the manager and she's active in the store and does a good job of picking the right employees. the people behind the counter are just incredible. lucky to call this "my" starbucks!

friendly, fast service, drinks are as expected. limited seating that isn't always neat or clean, but remember this starbucks is in a target so they have a zillion people with carts and little kids going in and out constantly. they're very fast and i've never seen a huge or slow-moving line, which is nice because i usually want to get my chai and get the heck out of there. i'd recommend going before you do your shopping if you want to sit and nurse your drink or snack, as the sitting area really isn't large enough to allow lots of carts and bags unless it's basically empty. one or two small bags are fine, but if you've stocked up for the end of the world, put your stuff in the car and then come back in.
the coffee shop was messy. furniture looked in need of replacing. table tops were in need of wiping, trash cans were overflowing.
this starbucks is fairly new and i've only ever gone through the drive thru, but the staff is pretty awesome and efficient. had to ask for a sleeve...my only complaint

the staff here is very friendly and efficient. love the outdoor seating in the loop. sounds odd, but their decaf coffee is best tasting (pike). i order a pike half decaf and it is consistent tasting every time while other stores are not.

stopped at this starbucks on my way to a doctor appointment. i went inside, did not go through drive-thru so i cannot comment on that. i was heartily welcomed as soon as i walked in. it's spacious, with lots of tables, a few couches, and comfy chairs. i got my order within five minutes, it was correct and hot. i worked on my laptop on the table. bathroom was clean. no complaints.

i have only been to this starbucks location a few times and as i recall each experience has been bad. today was the same. i went through the drive through and placed my order of an americano & brownie. when i got to the window the barista held out the scanner to read my card as she was talking on her headset to the next client in the car behind me. she did not greet me - she was busy helping the client after me. once she took a break from the drive through clients after me - i told her i wanted to get my brownie for 50 points. she said ok with a displeased look & said she'd have to void the transaction and ring for the 50 points. that took a few minutes & a manager had to come over to assist with the void (the manager did not greet me either) during the wait time she did not greet me, did not make small talk & was not pleasant in any way. she had handed me the brownie when i pulled up but as yet not given me my coffee. i asked for the receipt when she said she had to void the transaction - and when she got the receipt and handed it to me (she was on her headset talking to a client) she seemed finished with me. i said "i still need to get my americano" - she said very rudely "give me a moment", in fact she'd had several moments. a minute or so later she handed me my coffee without a thank you. i do not plan to return to this location.

i love this place!!! in the month that i got to spend in tucson visiting i wend through the drive through at least once a day and never had any serious issues. there was one time that my order was confused by someone working in the drive through but the staff quickly fixed it prior to it becoming an issue and gave me a free drink because of it. my kids also loved this place, all i have to say is they do something with chocolate milk that is awesome.
worst starbucks ever, customer service is bad they just want you to get out give you the order half way, won't come back to this starbucks again that's for sure
i had the pleasure of stopping here coming back from philly. my drink was perfect, and the ladies working were so sweet.

i had a terrible experience at this location from the moment i walked in. nobody greeted me upon arrival which, if you are busy is understandable, but there was only one person ahead of me and all other customers had been served. i waited patiently till the customer was finished, then was left there while the employees joked and spoke to each other. again, i typically don't fuss about things. i myself have worked in customer service and understand that often, what a customer perceives runs deeper. finally someone steps up to take my order with yet again no greeting. i receive my order and proceeded to add my fixings. the station was extremely low on almost every item. the cream was empty, the trash was filled to the top, there were zero napkins, and the sugar had lumps in it to a point where you could not get any sugar out at all. i had to open the sugar and remove hardened clogs for the next poor customer. this is unacceptable! considering there were at minimum three employees standing around, you would think at least one would take pride in their work. it is safe to say i will not be returning to this location.

this location has the best and most cheerful employee (i believe his name is richard) i have ever encountered, and i go to starbucks regularly wherever i go. if i could nominate a starbucks employee of the year, it would be him. the location is spacious, and a quiet place to do work (thanks, free wifi). i don't feel rushed by employees to leave, even after monopolizing a table for almost 4 hours. clean, comfortable and welcoming! what else could you want from your local starbucks? i've only been to this location once (but will be coming here from now on), but i can already tell there are regulars that come to this starbucks. many of the customers greet one another; one guy even came to pick up his packed lunch that his wife had left for him behind the counter. 5 stars.

this is my starbucks home, and i've never had a bad experience. if there's ever an issue, they happily take care of it. (like with anywhere, sometimes things happen.) for instance, they were out of my preferred pastry recently so i opted for another, and they not only refunded my money on the ones they ran out of, they gave me my second choice for free! i'm not sure where the barista went that automatically knew my order when i walked in, but i'm always treated well and with friendly service. i can always count on this location!

sidenote: the best cup of coffee i've had in cwe is at pi pizza. i think starbucks excels at consistency of product. this location is no different in that the coffee is the same taste as any place else. that said, this is a great location to grab a quick cup and go. ...however, parking is tricky near maryland and euclid so this is easier said than done. i rate this as 2 out of 5 stars comparing it to other starbucks shops in the area. it is small and cramped and has one unisex bathroom which creates waiting for the bathroom an expectation. on a positive note, the bathroom has always been clean. maybe the guy in front of you feels obligated not to be a sloppy mess knowing a cute girl might be waiting for her turn. the business itself does really well given the traffic in cwe and their apple -esque ability to make people feel special while holding the logo on their cup (myself included as i type away at my macbook writing this review...). a coffee shop as a whole and i might give it 3 or 4 stars. the staff is great (as great as all starbucks staff) and you know what you are getting when you walk in the door. but when it comes down to going to a starbucks, i'd rather go to one elsewhere.

ok, so i come to this starbucks often as it is somewhat close to home. i have visited numerous other starbucks over time, and this particular location consistently does iced coffee right! there is no bitter taste, no "americano over ice and "it is just a really great and consistent iced coffee! the people that work in this location are friendly, and always seem to know my name, even without the mobile order. it just seems easy to come here because i enjoy consistency in my order! thank you to the store and all the staff for making the experience pleasant every single time!

i tried their oreo cookie frappuccino, and my husband tried their lemon pound cake.... delicious! the employee at the drive thru taking orders was extremely polite and bubbly, and let me know i could substitute dairy for coconut milk, etc. after i let it be known i'm taking a chance with dairy today.
very nice store. outdoor seating away from traffic. comfortable variety in seating/working areas. friendly baristas.

i had the chance to meet a lady by the name of leilani and the team this afternoon at the glenwood store. the day before i bought a starbucks cup at another location, but it really wasn't what i was looking for, as i visited glenwood starbucks the following day...i realized that they had the cup i have been looking for a while now! leilani was able to exchange the cup i wanted, when other store refused to help. thanks, ladies for the amazing customer service and my amazing guava drink!

service is short - ordered a venti iced caramel macchiato through the app. & the barista interrupted me multiple times - i had basic questions...shouldn't they learn how to help customers with online orders since i'm sure it's majority of there business? this starbucks is just okay - service brings it to 3/5 (should be better imo). this is a big difference between the customer service i get at this location vs. the one on sheldon road, which always goes above & beyond; it's a 4/5 @sheldon as a result. also, parking is really tight, so i recommend the drive through! my previous experiences wasn't really the best (nothing special here), so i don't stop here often at all despite it being on my drive home - maybe i come here only once every 4 months out of desperation.
my favorite starbucks in the area now. they are super friendly and quick. i also like the setup and ambiance of the store. i will have to go more often.

what a poorly run place and the workers have tons of very bad attitude... four people working and i waited 5 mins to place my order and when i asked if i could order i was snapped at and given a horrible look. go anywhere else

there's always a long line wrapping around the building, because the baristas like to screw around instead of doing their job. i know this for a fact, because we go inside instead of waiting in the drivethru. it needs to be fixed.

i love this starbucks. it has a healthy split of clientele with middle age people like to college students studying on their laptop for classes at unr. it's in a good neighborhood northeast of downtown reno off i-80. the employees at this starbucks are very knowledgeable about the drinks and are helpful. they also are very nice to the customers here. the place is very clean. it's usually busy day and nite. there always a few people in here. my favorite starbucks in reno!!

employee friendliness was great and all but when i see three people get their drinks even though i ordered before them, then i ask where my drink is. fourth person gets their drink and i don't have my drink. not cool beans! fifth person that ordered after me gets their drink.... oh now sixth. how unfortunate i had to be somewhere soon. disappointing service today especially when i spend about $15.

if they would stop chit chatting with each other they would probably get my order correct. i go once a week get w scone and a venti sweetened green tea and most of the time they do put the sweetener in but i'm done they were too busy chatting with each other they screwed my order up. that's it for me with this place

every. single. time i come here, the people are friendly and don't rush you to order. grant it, i'm not taking 5 min to order, but sometimes you may want something different. they are always willing to make something for me to see how i like it. i love coming to this location!!!! i do not drink coffee, so they still give me great options!
helpful on the drinks and decently fast in the drive thru. drinks are consistent. convenient location. not sure why others rated low. typical starbucks ;). that's why it's so popular.

you know, when all is said and done it's a starbucks. it checks all of the important boxes. they have a drive-thru and a reasonable amount of parking. they are currently open with limited seating capacity which is nice. but most importantly they have always gotten my order correct. i end up here from time to time and will be back for sure.

this starbucks used to be good, but they never have cold brew anymore. they don't know how to make the pumpkin cream cold brew either. so gross i asked for it to be remade and the cashier rolled his eyes at me and it still came out nasty. please learn how to make the drinks advertised.
incredibly slow service, even for a starbucks. the staff here do not care. if you got through the drive thru, it's a minimum of 10-15 minute wait

amazing experience here with the staff! my lattes were made perfectly! the baristas made me feel a personal connection, almost like i've known them my entire life! 10/10 recommend. my favorite starbucks in town!

the good: consistency the bad: not for people that want to "drink in" on location. in the morning you will wait in line forever as all their workers prioritize mobile orders over people in line. even the "cashier" will work five mobile orders in between every person in line while those in line just stand there and get angrier. two people in line behind me striking up a conversation and complaining about the same thing. they may as well just make it a drive trough or mobile pick up only place..

its like there are two different stores here. the morning crew is friendly and efficient, although swamped with business. but the order is always right and they fly around like you expect. go in the afternoon, the little "manager" walks around doing nothing. the staff moves like snails, and its 50/50 whether your coffee comes as ordered (25 minutes later). my last visit there were just 3 cars in line at the drive-thru. great! a chance for a quick stop mid-afternoon. 14 minutes later i ducked out of the drive-thru and walked in. ummm. no line here at all. 2 people waiting for their orders. must have have been the driver trying to buy a franchise at the drive-thru. a breeze now right? i left 25 minutes later with a chalky, poorly made specialty coffee that the obviously new guy asked for help with on every step. the manager ignored his pleas, leaving the other "kids" to give him partial instructions and frustrate him nearly to tears. all while the the three customers inside watched the chaos behind the counter. poor management to say the least. kudos to the morning crew. shame on whatever you call that circus that goes on in the afternoon.

sat outside for 15 minutes waiting for my mobile order pick-up ($14) and i never saw anyone inside. this is 40 minutes after they opened. tried calling. nothing. took videos if you want to see them. i want my money back right now.

i usually get excited when i find a starbucks. however, today i was disappointed. we walked in to a very long line so i went to my app to order on line and i could not find the store listed. the lady in front of me said, oh their app is not working. then i looked behind the counter and they only had two people working. the guy taking the orders was as slow as molasses. the barista making the drinks was just as slow. made for a long wait. several people got tired of waiting and left. too bad.

tuesday afternoon shift is popping every one is attractive the drinks are always good and service at starbucks is always the best. this is a fairly new location so it's nice an clean and a cool place to do homework or just chill at
