the service at starbucks is amazing! and the drinks are great to. i recommend you get double chocolate chip frap it is amazing. but there are some downsides with starbucks sometimes the floor can be very stick and the tables. that is my review i highly recommended going there. but get it to go.

killing time while waiting for someone, i needed a shot of caffeine as a picker-upper. there's nothing too different from this place from any other of the 3,984 other starbucks within a 50 mile radius. i ordered a mocha frappucino, they made it, spelled my name wrong, it was delivered, i sipped it. i'm a rebel. i refuse to use the words "venti" or "grande" or whatever. i steadfastly ask for a small. in the meantime, i was provided with a nice clean bathroom and a comfortable place to sit and relax until it was time to head out.
the people here really seem nice, but 9/10 they get my drink wrong. i've gotten cold sandwiches, iced drinks with no ice, lattes with no milk...it's just bizarre

i'm a lifelong starbucks drinker, love the place and the happy culture and the service. i went in this morning and the two baristas were major grumpsters. i know it was 6am but man i do not go to starbucks for that sort of attitude. total turn-off and not a great way to start my day, having to put up with extreme attitude to get my coffee.
waited 21 min for my mocha this morning. ridiculous! need new management. no wonder the stock has been dropping.

by far the best starbucks i've been to. the customer service is outstanding and i have never had an issue. i always come in with big orders for everyone back at work and they never even seen stressed with such orders no matter how busy it is. simply outstanding.

close to home, but i usually visit my store in university city on the way to work. so, this store i visit on the weekend when we don't go to regency cafe in landsdowne. stopped today and was reminded how friendly the staff is! for how infrequently i visit this store, the barista remembered my drink. well done!!

i went in to meet a friend, the hot chocolate was good but it took at least 10 minutes to make! my friend who lives in belleville (i am from and live in chicago) said that this location is always slow. if you want coffee but have some place to be afterwards this s not the place for you!

i have been going to this particular starbucks in the morning for almost a year now and they are amazing. i use the mobile order app when i'm about 5 minutes out and 9/10 times it's ready for me to walk in and grab, i have never had to wait longer than the estimated wait time at this locatio. i may be jinxing myself but i have also never had a bad coffee from this location. so to summarize, best coffee, best service and the fastest starbucks i have received since moving to tennessee. keep up the great work.

great since it has a drive-thru. they took quite awhile to make my aunt's white chocolate mocha, but my drink came out quickly. the cashier was very nice though and we chatted while we waited so it wasn't too bad. i just love me some starbucks.
i go to this location when i am in the 109 street area. i wish it was a bit larger as it is difficult to find a place to sit when it is busy.

i went here today and got an iced americano. it was delicious and the staff was friendly. but i just don't think starbucks is worth the high prices. it really adds up. i'm gonna figure out how to make espresso at home, save some money.

they have cold brew. i've been having it daily for a week. one day i had it twice. looking at the amount of caffeine on starbucks site. it has about the same amount of caffeine as a hot coffee. but hot coffee has 1g of protein and 15mg less sodium than the cold brew. i ll switch back to hot coffee, and cheaper too

it's actually a starbucks window in harrahs casino no seating and always crowded with casino patrons. the prices are higher than a free standing store but if you need a coffee fix it's your only choice.

hate to say it but all starbucks are pretty much the same. this one gets an extra star because it's clean, the bathrooms are stocked and the baristas aren't rude. they aren't overly friendly, but i don't expect too much! they get the drinks right, they seem to be well trained and the app makes the purchase process go smoothly! be careful backing out of the parking spaces in the front lot!! there are bollards at the end of the spaces and i've seen quite a few people hit them backing out!

andrea must be having a bad day.. shouted at my daughter when she asked if her drink was correct and then tossed requested spoons her direction.. hey shouting at my kids is my pastime no need for starbucks to get in on the act :)

'we're out of everything! ' seems to be the order of the day. i was disappointed that they display the pepperoni turnover in the display case but when i order it the product is discontinued!? don't display product you don't sell. also all other fold over types are sold out. finally- it doesn't take 24 hours for a gift card to transfer to the starbucks app, it happens right away. i put an extra $10 on my app thinking that's what happened but when i refreshed my app the gift card had applied.

it's a shame that i like starbucks coffee so much because i continually have to be let down by the service here. the drive thru line moves slower than the dmv and inside may actually be worse. can't count the number of times i ordered a food item to be told they're out of it only after i reached the window to pay. to put it shortly this is the the worst starbucks location i've ever been too and they seem determined not to relinquish that title.

the worst starbucks that i've ever been to. super slow and drinks came out terrible. i ordered a grande iced caramel cloud macchiato, and it came out 80% cloud foam, the remaining amount was probably 3 gulps of coffee at best. i didn't have time to return for them to correct the drink, so maybe management would have to learn about it via yelp.

i don't go to starbucks often because it's so ridiculously expensive. this location is nice, clean, and the staff were friendly. there was a wait both inside and even longer at the drive thru. to be honest the wait wasnt as bad as it usually is at starbucks. i am sure i will be at starbucks again every once in a while since i do enjoy their iced mocha frappachino but not regularly since i cant spend $4 on a coffee all the time.

it's usually pretty good here, good service & coffee. this is my sat. morning hang-out, and the last 3 saturdays have been less than paar, i'm sorry to say. they have a new barista who needed reminding of what i ordered, didn't greet me, no "thank you," and barely said anything at all. i found her potentially rude.
absolutely love this location and everything about it. the staff are awesome, truly a great warm welcoming environment.
love the drive through. friendly employees and eager to give my dog a pupa chino the breakfast sandwiches are delicious.

i created this account just to write this review, becca is the unfriendliest person i've ever met. she should never have a job where she would have to interact with anyone. talked down to staff and customers. never will i go back if she is working here.

they coffee here is good, like at all starbucks. the offerings to eat is quite limited. what is astounding is how indifferent the people who work here are to how loud the music is. at times it is impossible to talk to someone on the phone without using the speaker phone. i have had to talk to people who work at various starbucks about this, requesting that they turn the music down, mostly with bad results. they say they will gladly will turn the music down, then they don't. and they will not turn it off. i can only surmise what the reason is for this is: they think they are a music venue, and the music they are playing is simply too important for people not to be exposed to it.

am i dreaming? starbucks opened less than 5 minutes from my house, and its a drive through!!! i'd been dreaming of this moment for all of my suburban mom life (which has been less than 10 months at this point, but still...). dragging a baby out of the car to get coffee in the cold or rain is less than ideal, and dunkin was the only close option. now there's a starbucks drive through right on rt 130! i still miss la colombe (and being able to walk to get coffee) but this is the best option within 5 minutes of my house.

while i dislike reviewing big chain stores, all my experiences at this place warrant a review. every time i have been to this location something has been messed up. two particular instances are what finally got me to make this review. the first being the time i went to pick up a mobile order for a coworker. i arrived and told them what i was picking up, the girl behind the register let me know they had not received any mobile orders. i called my coworker and let her know. she in return sent me a screenshot of the confirmation. i showed it to the cashier and again they let me know they hadn't revived any orders. i asked if i would wait and see it if takes some some to show up on their system as the order was place about 20 minutes ago. the girl apparently in charge of the mobile orders snapped at me and she's been here all day and hadn't received anything and that i was a the wrong location. i read them the address on the screenshot and checked my phones gps but they insisted i was at the wrong location. when i arrived to work, my coworker double checked her order and her gps as well to prove i had gone to the correct location. my coworker headed over to this location to speak with the employees and of course once she arrived her order was ready in the mobile pick up station and no one said a word to her. the second instance was today when i order two iced lattes and instead was given a hot drink. when i brought it to their attention told them it was ok as long as the second drink was iced instead somehow ended up with a frappuccino. i stood at the window in hopes of getting anyone's attention but was ignored. after about 20 minutes of waiting finally someone looked up and and condescendingly asked "what else are you waiting for" as if to acknowledge she had seen me standing there but didn't want to help. at this point i had had it and just left. i get this that it was very busy but honestly everyone working there has ridiculously bad customer service skills. as a 20 something year old who has worked many customer service jobs this is place honestly has the worst displays of blatant rudeness i have ever experienced or seen.
this is the starbucks in the lawrence park shopping center and it is fine. bathrooms are closed due to covid and the service can vary based on who is working. no issues.
fast service, very clean and love the modern look. very accessible, in the corner of a main street. headed here because my hotel offers starbucks but nothing compares to the real deal.

i'm a pretty laid back guy, but i am big on manners and etiquette. when i asked the manager "how's your day going?", his response was, "what can i get you?" the young man also proceeded to call me "dude" multiple times, although this is a word i often use, it is definitely not appropriate for a customer you do not know. i still went back the next day to get some more work done, but found it impossible due to the overly loud, obnoxious conversation of the employees. i'm all for a fun work environment, but please respect your patrons. nice enough people, they just need a course on social etiquette and treating customers with respect.

i would love to try the coconut cold brew but every time they are always out!!! not just at this starbucks, several starbucks i've been to. what's the deal? why can't they order more when they know they are going to be selling it?
what's up with all the private convos and your rolling eyes? customer service means nothing here.

the place is clean, the service was awesome- some of the nicest baristas i've ever had, they were fast, the drinks were perfect and i was pleased. i feel like i'm betraying my normal baristas at the frys location who are awesome-but these ladies need some recognition! great job!

i heart everything about this starbucks. from the location (perfect for lookey-lous) to the friendly staff to the quick drink processing - to the busyness to the hustle & bustle of it all. they handle the load with finesse, grace & class. due to the location of this starbucks, i'm surprised it's not a 24-hr store, or is it??? i'm sure in nyc there's a 24-hr starbucks or even cali. anywho, i digress; but yeah back to this location... my hope is that once you make it here to this location, that you're not dismayed by the parking situation. it can be a bit overwhelming, but the treats that await you inside promise not to disappoint. #windowseatsarethebest
this starbucks is in a great location--right by the southwest airlines gates. the service is fast and usually friendly. they are often well stocked on baked goods and snacks.

if there were an option for 0 stars i would click it. the male worker with blonde highlights at the register charged me $13 more than he was supposed to. he didn't give me a receipt because he literally stole from me, and this store doesn't accept rewards.

everyyyyyy sinngggllleeeeee timmmeeeee i go they mess something up to the point where they mention it at the drive thru "i know this happens often" uh no every time update your mobile app so orders are current so you don't get pissed off people and have to refund the entire order then provide a courtesy " next ones on us" gift card

the best starbucks ever! we have one literally right next to our house but we go out of our way to go here. the service is fast and the workers are always super friendly. if they weren't so nice i'd say they might be robots because they make everything so fast and the drink always turns out perfectly. they don't have friendly robots yet right? even if they are robots its still my favorite starbucks.

starbucks is ignoring the current plastic and waste issues that are all over the news. they have a double cup policy? so when you order a coffee or tea you get two cups. i asked why and even the server agrees that this silly policy is ruining our environment but she has her orders. normally i bring my own cup so i never noticed until today. this is very wrong

i got to starbucks at 6:20 and they were not open yet. i asked the worker who was smoking outside at what time they opened and he said around 6:30 or 7 implying that they did not have a set time for opening and closing. when i have gotten there and it is opened. the overweight white man with a scruff has been ridiculously rude. rolling his eyes at customers including myself. so sad to feel uncomfortable at my local starbucks!!! i think that he's the manager. if you don't enjoy people giving you attitude, avoid this place!

regular starbucks nothing different. however i will day someone else is using their lot because when i went today there was not one spot for me or my gf we had to park across the st which is a pretty busy street. they need to be more strict with their parking lot. they are for sure losing business with a full lot like that. oh i forgot to say that there wasn't enough people there for there to not be any spots

this is my favorite starbucks and i go here as often as i can. the baristas are always helpful and friendly and they fill my orders accurately. the store is relatively new and spacious and very clean. in addition, the customers are mostly nice and friendly. i only wish i lived closer because the starbucks nearest me is always crowded with slow service and less friendly customers and baristas.

this starbucks fucked up my order. i ordered a matcha latte w almond milk. they put way too much freaking powder. like chill. it shouldnt be that hard to make a matcha latte. it's literally powder and liquid. how do you mess that up. i understand that these employees r new but why did you hire them if they cant make a simple drink. fire them. i paid 7 dollars for the most horrendous piece of shit ever. u guys ruined my day, and my appetite.
super nice people, always get my order right and they're fast as hell in the morning. totally my preferred 'bucks!

located under the radar within a target department store, this hidden gem of reno is must-see for anyone looking for a good coffee and even better service. this coffee shop bleeds the kind of atmosphere you thought only existed on television. you can sit back and relax with your coffee for a minute as the world moves around you. a melting pot of people bustle back and forth within the red walls of the store in a rush for the hottest deals as almost character-like employees interact and whip up drinks with a frenzied synergy that contradicts your expectations of what working for a soul crushing seattle based corporation must be like. with dazzling smiles, the baristas address their customers by name. it is clear that the people warmly taking in their morning coffee are regulars. they could go anywhere. so why do they go here? to a chain located within a chain? it can only be because of the excellent customer service. the dynamic dark roast duo behind the counter are named diana and kelsey and between discussing the intricacies of french cinema and canine-american rights, they are delivering the precise orders of their dazzled customers at a lightning pace. curious to see how they would treat a newcomer, i approached the counter and struggled to figure out what to purchase. with a smile, "kelsey" suggested i try a "crystal ball" drink that resembled the bad guy in the last spider-man movie. "diana" interjected and suggested a variation of the drink that was much better. this personal touch and recommendation had me instantly sold. despite my eager enthusiasm, they even offered to let me sample it first, assuring me it was not a problem. it was like they'd known me for years and i instantly understood why so many people chose to visit this location. the drink was made to perfection and in that crystal ball drink i saw myself returning here in my future! the service didn't stop there though. after offering me an ice cold water (they managed to inject the frozen h2o croutons very adeptly), i watched as an errant child projectile vomited all over the wall, his mother nowhere to be found. the girls were on top of it instantly. cleaning the mess and directing customers with practiced ease while calling for assistance with the child, i was stunned by their rapid response. to add to the situation, i watched as a target employee stumbled over and attempted to rattle the girls with incessant questions about a show called "duck tales". with patient looks, the girls answered his questions to satisfaction and it became clear to me that they were truly prepared for anything this red walled hub might throw at them. do yourself a favor and visit this starbucks located within target. if diana and kelsey are making drinks with confidence as cool as the frappuccinos, i can guarantee you'll leave with a smile. ...parking kinda sucks though.

i'm new to the area, but i travel often around the country and have never had an issue with my normal starbucks order. always tastes the same across the board. except here. 3 times in a row my drink was made and it was gross. they are always super slow. so taste your drink before you leave or drive off. they will probably have to make it again.

very busy store in the morning. 6 team members very busy serving orders. bathroom was out of order which was an inconvenience. lot of people waiting for orders. batista yelling people's names and orders. even using the preorder app..... had to wait a long time for drink.
thanks guys! always great to see a barista team that works so well together to get drinks out like rapid fire! thanks so much! can't beat the doppio con pana!

first, i will start out saying that what i'm writing here reflects my views of most starbucks locations that i visit however, in all fairness to the establishment i won't say they're all like this. consistently inconsistent due to time of day, location, and barista i feel. i come here quite often as it's not outside my normal circle of travel. i decided to go with two stars on the rating because i'm not really happy with my drinks here. i feel like the only time my order is correct, is at night when it's slow. i've noticed that the barista's will intentionally make my order wrong if i've ordered something they don't agree with or they feel they are pressed for time. my drink usually isn't filled to the top either. so not only is my drink wrong but it's less than what i've paid for as well. on many occasions i've had to argue to get my drink remade. strange considering "the barista promise" advertised on their cups. all in all the people are mostly friendly but their service and attention to detail lacks. i don't mind waiting for good quality service and a correct order but i get really frustrated with the opposite and an excuse attached. sadly, i've come to expect my order to be wrong at this location. as we all know, it's easy to see our orders being made from the drive thru in any standard size vehicle or while standing inside, my accusations of my drink being incorrect don't come from me guessing. let's not forget my taste buds know too. i don't want this to be all bad so i will end with this- the location has great customer and is extremely clean. about three days ago they did give me two free drinks because i forgot my wallet and the starbucks app wasn't working properly for me to pay using my phone. thank you for that.
i love starbucks.. you could say i'm a starbucks freak.. but this location tends to not satisfy my drink.. 5 out of 10 times they get it perfect..
who would think that we would want a coffee at 5:00 am before our flight?? starbucks still hasn't opened. this is a crap airport with crap concessions.
great service always and long lines. great for them. another location on 54 is badly needed.

ok, so its a saturday evening at 7:15pm, after a cardinals home game and this location is closed, wtf???? and its located in the lobby of a marriott hotel!! and there are no signs with their hours posted on any doors, weird!!! was very disappointed! thankfully their competitor, kaldi's was open!!

the only reason i'm giving this 2 stars is because the service is extremely extremely slow! there were 3 cars in front of me and about a 30 minute wait. then, when we finally got to the window, it was another 5 minute wait. customer service needs to be worked on.

super crowded and busy. this location needs to hire more starbucks employees so that they can be more on top of the game. i feel as if the lines were too long and short staffed. cleanliness was a-okay. could be better. everyone was cranky and employees were making mistakes since there was too many orders and i don't blame them. the lines were incredibly long and people are cranky for waiting.

a smile goes a long way, and so does a lack of one. walking into this starbucks, this past afternoon, i immediately felt like the baristas hated their jobs. a positive is that after they got my order wrong the first time due to the cashier accidentally writing the wrong order on the cup, a barista redid it without hesitation and said i was fine for asking. the drink was delicious. but still, the atmosphere just felt angry because of the lack of eye contact and the r.b.f. in the baristas. probably won't be returning to this location, at least at lunch time, anymore.

fast service, always very friendly. the mall can be a busy place. if the mall is busy you can bet starbucks will have about 15 people in line. i'm not a big fan of starbucks but this location always makes their drinks well and i've never been unsatisfied by their service or product.

starbucks is good as always! but service is not the greatest. i have never waited in line at any other starbucks longer than i've waited at this location. this past time my order was completely wrong, which isn't the first time i've waited 10 plus minutes to get the wrong order
honestly greatest experience ever. hannah and sierra are a hoot! they made sure to make the perfect drink and they definitely did. i can't wait to come back

this starbucks has the best customer service in the area. it is true that they are always busy, but they move people through quickly and with a smile on their face. the manager jamie is always happy to greet you with a smile

terrible baristas. this place was a dumpy little corner off the street with a weird area feel. i wouldn't go in here again if i were dying for caffeine. i just can't believe that starbucks hires workers like this. boy have their standards fallen.
love the atmosphere here. the staff is friendly and drinks are great. i wish there was more parking as the lot is almost always full.

i have made 5 trips here (all mobile orders) and 4 of my orders were wrong and the 1 that was right was only 2/3 full... i'm assuming the baristas don't know how to make the drinks smh i was so excited we were getting a starbucks in algiers but this is a huge disappointment...

horrible customer service every time i come here. they have the most unfriendly staff and could absolutely care less about their customers. if i'm paying $6 for my coffee i expect you to get it right and not be rude. i wish i could say that this was the first time but sadly every time i've had the pleasure of going here they've either messed up my peppermint mocha or been rude. not coming here again! save yourself the trouble!

if you are coming to work on your computer, don't bother. there is very limited seating and it isn't very accommodating. would only recommend this place for sipping on something while you shop at safeway or to take back to your car.
staff members were super friendly here. we were able to relax here after walking around the area. it was also a great place for people watching.

i go to this starbucks fairly frequently, and the drinks are fine but i can't stand that if you just walk in and up to the counter sometimes it takes a couple minutes until you are greeted, let alone have your order taken even though there are a ton of people behind the counter.

the furniture looks like it came out of an old college dorm. not uncomfortable, worn out, uninviting. environment is cold and warehouse like. wifi doesn't work. i ask starbucks person and they say it doesn't work for them either. have you tried fixing it? i ended up going to one on 96th street. much better.

conveniently located next to my place of work and full of the friendliest baristas? perfect. i typically like any place where i frequent enough that people start to recognize either me or know what i usually order. i wish starbucks wasn't so expensive, but that is not any of the workers' faults - i'm sure they wish their paychecks were larger, too. i don't think i have to say anything about the coffee. this location gets full of college students during school season who are faceboo, err... studying. while i would love for you to be open 24 hours, my pocketbook would not.
coolest starbucks i've ever been in. $5 for a coffee is a small price to pay for the super laid back hipster mac book ambience going in this java house.

this is primarily the starbucks that i go to when i drink starbucks. i have been going to this location for over two years and every time i go i received excellent service. the staff is nice and welcoming and always offers you options if you don't know what you want. the drinks are cool and refreshing and i always get a double shot of espresso over ice or a latte on my way to work for a little pick me up. look out online for specials during the pandemic i know they offered specials for nurses and frontline workers. will be back soon!

it seems that the drive throughs at starbucks lately have been taking much longer than usual. this location particularly is always slow in the afternoon when they are the slowest you would think that their service would be better. but i even just went in the morning and the line didn't move for quite a few minutes and i ended up leaving the line.
caffeine not caribe. stupid spell check. well, fast service, friendly and i get fry's points. what more can you want. if you are near, stop on by, they have s few tables and chairs where you can sit.
would it kill this place to serve a better selection of soft drinks that are not coffee?
one of the best starbucks i've been to. the employees are always nice and fun. plus the drinks and food are always amazing. it's always my go to for a coffee shop.

this starbucks is located within the providence town center shopping plaza, so you won't find any drive-thru here. you will find two entrances though, one on either side of the shop. the inside is fairly typical of most starbucks i've been to. in addition to coffee's, tea's, and an assortment of food, you can also buy starbuck's branded items like mugs, coffee, etc. plenty of seating options offering both indoor and outdoor seating and my kids like to sit up by the bar and watch the barista's do their thing! it's also nice being able to stop in here, grab a coffee, and then walk around the shopping center.

not one of the better starbucks. at the times when this place is busy, i understand turnover will be slow, and inaccuracies will be more likely to occur. (i've only had one incorrect drink order, and they were kind enough to give me a "free drink" coupon.) however, there is no excuse for a snails pace when the place is empty. i used to work at starbucks when i was in college (which was, admittedly, a while ago), and they pushed for drinks to be made a lightening speed regardless of the lines (both in-store and at the drive-thru window.) so maybe they don't have that "rule" anymore. because it's convenient to my job, i'll continue coming here, but i do wish they would attempt to pick up the pace.

this is the starbucks i go to most often. it's usually very busy, and the space is l-shaped and narrow. t's often hard to get a place to sit down inside, and there's plenty outside, if that works for you. it usually doesn't for me. why am i giving this starbucks four stars? good service and good location. i often go to trader joe's to shop afterwards. it's often on the way to or from home, and i came here for trenta-sized passion iced teas to counteract dehydration during cancer treatment.

usually make your order wrong the first time, so be sure if you're stopping by that you have enough time to wait for everyone in front of you to have their drink remade, as well as your own. i assume this is why their drive through moves more slowly than any of the others in town. added one star for the convenience of having a drive through.

dead roaches on the floor, rude barrister behind the counter. ended up walking out after waiting 15 minutes and not getting my drink (and there was no one else in the store and the drive through was not busy). the best: the rude employee had the nerve to flip me off on the way out the door. to the short white girl with extremely short black hair, watch out, you never know who a customer may know at the corporate office!!!! just saying.

customer service is usually pretty good, however starbucks says they care about their employees but then start them off at $9.25/hour when the company has the ability to pay a living wage. pay your works more. especially during a pandemic. you are not essential.

pleasantly surprised with this off-macys mid-city starbucks. given the location, i wasn't expecting much to be honest. there was also a starbucks inside my nearby hotel, and i was disappointed with their drink quality. i figured this would be more of the same. instead, i was greeted by a friendly, smiling barista, my drink was made quickly (and correctly!), and i was able to find a seat easily. it was decently busy for a weekday, but it was still comfortable & not crowded. the line was moving & the vibe was relaxed. and that's all you really want from a starbucks, isn't it? besides a good drink of course ....

i have been a starbucks gold member for at least 8 years. as you can imagine, i have been to several different starbucks locations in my life. this location was the absolute worst location that i have ever been to. i visited on december 4th. i entered the drive through line at 4:58pm. there were 8-10 cars in front of me. i did not pay for my order until 5:30pm!!! thirty minutes in a drive through line. i ordered a venti caramel brûlée and it literally tasted like warm milk. no caramel flavor at. all! and it was lukewarm when it was handed to me. i wanted to pay for the person behind me, and the girl at the window was making it difficult for me to do so. i had to pay for the person 2 cars behind me and only after practically begging as i had to ask her several times. there were at least 3 workers in there and the person at the window told another worker to go turn people away. she said "go 4 cars back from the speaker and turn people away." what on earth!? i can't believe i waited thirty minutes (plus the time it actually took to get my drink! i have proof of when i arrived [sent a video to my husband as soon as i arrived in the drive thru line] and what time i paid.) for a drink that had no flavor and was lukewarm. how incredibly awful. i have waited a week to post this because i have had to make sure i am not completely run by emotions in this review. i will be putting this review on every platform that i can. unbelievable.

i work at the va and frequently walk over to this location. today i did the same and when i got there it said "drive through orders only" on the door and it was locked so i tried to order on the app but it said i couldn't order oat milk on the app. i ended up going to the drive through speaker and asking if i could order and she said "ma'am you can't come to the drive though" i said well i tried to order on the app and i can't go inside, and she said "due to unforeseen circumstances we can't serve you and we will reopen the main lobby again next week" ...... so i asked "if i was in a car you could serve me, but because i walked over in the middle of my shift at the hospital and your door is locked for me to go in and order you can't serve me" and she said "yes i cannot serve you" so that makes no sense to me i tried going in-they locked the doors due to "unforeseen circumstances" for this week only, tried ordering through the app which wouldn't let me and they wouldn't take my order. had to walk back over to work without coffee and am pretty disappointed.

take a step back when approaching this historic landmark, first floor roastery, this isn't your regular strip mall 'bucks. this be-seen locale offers the perfect porch viewpoint to look down upon the feeble tourists that keep this colonial town afloat. not for the non-elitist, there's a dunkin across the street for the peasants. enjoy your $4 cup'o'joe in peace and quiet countryside nobles! even with all its bells and whistles, it's still a starbucks. just as upidty at the next. it's in a perfect location for walking traffic. service is quick and polite. make sure you find street parking to save some dough instead of the lot out back. someone is getting rich off that. street parking is free before 9 or 10 am for the morning goers. the historic building adds a nice touch and separates it from the other run-of-the-mill chains in the area.

if this place didn't sell starbucks products and have the starbucks logo all over the place, i'd confuse it for a local, neighborhood coffee shop. the building itself is pretty small and there isn't much seating space, but it's cozy. it's my place for a coffee treat so i'm not sure what's like during the work rush, but i've never had to wait too long to place my order or receive my drink. the sugar area is always clean. not sure how this place will hold up once all the new granary residents are moved in and there's a whole new set of foot traffic, but for now it's a pleasant experience.

probably the best customer service i have ever received at any starbucks ever. i normally don't go to starbucks for coffee as it's a little pricy but my wife and i really wanted to try the irish cream americano which was very good. the person (wish i would have got her name) that was working drive thru gave us the best customer service i have ever received. it took awhile to make the coffees but because she was delightfully having a conversation with us it didn't feel like we waited long. for all starbucks fans i would strongly recommend this location.

nothing wrong at all with this place when i visited it! my daughters and i stopped in for a little treat after spending the day at museums. service was very quick. staff was friendly. i was worried that we would have to go into the constitution center to get to the starbucks (like... i thought it was just part of their gift store or something) but it's completely separate from the rest of the building.

yes in the morning they are super busy so i have to wait sometimes but it's ok. i still love the coffe and wouid come back for it. but it's different today because i got cut by the front door of the store but nobody even asked me how it is. they came out to clean up the blood on the floor but never had a look at my wound. i'm so upset and disappointed about how they treated me. the long wait is ok, sometimes they mess up the order is ok too, but the rudeness can not be accepted.

so, kudos to this starbucks for recognizing demand and having two kiosks in this rest stop. despite the extra booth, they are still incredibly busy. i waited just over 10 minutes for an iced coffee. i felt like they were fairly efficient, although only two of the three registers were open. when you have a 10-minute line, seems like it's logical to open up that third register. not sure that they really needed that guy adding cups to the already full cup stack when there was only one girl making drinks too. they have the cooler out front with prepackaged yogurt, sandwiches and such, and they have pastries, but it does not appear that they do the hot breakfast. all in all, there are some steps this starbucks can take to improve the speed in which it serves its customers.

sad excuse for management. mark needs extensive training in customer service. perhaps some professional help. there with my 3 children after a doctors appt to get a special treat. unfriendly from the start and very short. was putting order in as he informs me my 3 year old is taking chairs. i realize now he's been staring her down angrily. i look over and see her putting empty chairs at a table for us. i said i could handle it. goes on to say no, she's taking customers chairs. informs me i should keep her right next to me. yeah she's 3 and she doesn't listen. she wasn't disturbing anyone and i don't know how she was taking peoples chairs if they're sitting in them. mark has children and informs me they always listen to him. i'm just a terrible mother i guess who can't control a 3 year old. maybe i should get her on some meds. mark goes on to ask me why i want to be rude to someone who makes their drinks. so now you're threatening me. i said i kind of have my hands full and i can't run over to her. well you should keep her right next to you is his response. believe me i wanted her to stay there but she has a lot of strong will and i didn't see the harm in her reserving our table while i'm about 15 ft away. it would have been more of a scene for me to try to get her to stay. i said to him you could have been a little nicer about it. he informs me he is very nice. please don't go to this place unless you have children that act like robots and stay glued to your side. children with any free will are not welcome here. my children were upset by this whole situation. my 9 year old was very aware and was upset with the way he was talking to me and realized this was not the right way to treat someone. she went over before we left and helped put the chairs back. we left without any thing we ordered because i was just so upset with the treatment we experienced. my children and i have been to all the starbucks in the area and have never had an issue and always felt welcome. been to this location twice. in and out first time for a drink and then this experience. the sad thing is this is located in a community with lots of families. you really need to try to be accommodating to all customers. this experience makes me feel really sad about humanity.

super slooooooow! going on 25 min waiting for our drinks. these guys are so smart they are stacking the new cups in the older cups waiting to be made. so the newer orders are made first. these guys are very disorganized. what a sad excuse for a starbucks. not to mention they are out of souvenir mugs....

been to this starbucks several times and am disappointed every single time. i went today and asked for a drink and got the wrong order in the drive through, i then went back around telling them the drink was wrong and they made me the right drink with no issues. however both times i ordered i made it very clear i needed decaf. and both times got regular. the second drink they gave me that was the right order tasted horrible and had no flavor syrup. i have given up trying this place, will be going to other locations from now on.

shtarbucks. guys, i was waiting for my order for some time and had to hurry up with eating because of the flight boarding on my nose. i dropped some food on my pants because of that. i understand some inconveniences might happen in any kind of job. but there were not that many people and still i had to wait more than it usually takes. please take it into the consideration.

i always enjoy my drinks from starbucks (bring back valencia orange refresher). a pet peeve of mine is when someone does not say thank you or you're welcome. when placing orders at the drive up, it would be pleasing to hear them say thank you. they say thank you at the window,but it should also be said when you place your order. why not? it is what starts your day. gratitude is so important!

i love this location!!! really convenient. melissa is my favorite. she always fusses over my pugs when i bring them to the drive though. she even draws puggy faces on the coffee lids sometimes. all the employees are friendly and i can not recommend this starbucks enough.

after recently moving, it is my local starbucks. it's a typical starbucks atmosphere. i have been in several times and have always been greeted with a smile. my classic white mocha has always been made correctly and even stirred so all the flavoring isn't hanging out at the bottom of the glass. i pass three other starbucks on my way to work but i will happily come back to this one.
absolutely the best place around!! the customer service is amazing. only had one bad server, but for the majority of the time excellent service.

this starbucks is right in the lobby of our hotel, which was so convenient. i love starbucks and this one did not disappoint. i got a yogurt and bagel one day, and a yogurt parfait another day. the espresso drinks were on point.

this starbucks location is right in my neighborhood, and i've had my fair share of visits. never anything to critique this store about, until today. i got an email from starbucks rewards promoting the limited-time tie-dye frappuccino, and i wanted to give it a try. i had my sister (teenage years) grab it for me and we'd share while i stayed in the car. it took a while for her to get back, which didn't phase me as this is a popular location. drink was presentable and wasn't bad at all, but this review is for the customer service. on the drive back, she started telling me "if employees don't want to make these kinds of drinks, they shouldn't sell them at their stores?" i told her it was the corporation that decides what drinks are promoted and it's mostly out of stores' hands, but that prompted me to wonder why she asked that. she told me that once she'd told the barista the order, the staff all began to seem annoyed. like i get it, these showy, over-the-top promotional drinks are probably annoying to deal with, but i don't think my sister shouldn't have received the air of annoyance because of that. nor should any customer in general, i believe. it would've been much more professional to keep their discontent to themselves, or at least display it at a time where customers can't take note.
as soon as i woke up...i walked towards the window...pulled the curtains apart...rubbed my eyes....and bammm..starbucks. i knew what i was having for breakfast. woo hoo!

used to be one of my favorites, but a fair number of the staff aren't as friendly as the previous employees. i like their product, but half of the starbucks attraction is atmosphere and if employees are grumpy it ruins the vibe.
worst starbucks, worst staff. it's not clean, they're not nice, and they're not competent. avoid at all costs. the reviews are awful for a reason!

hate this location and absolutely hate the service. they need a entirely new group of employees. who warms a croissant for a second and expect the inside to be warm? man and it's literally across the street from my house but i would rather walk or drive to another location. do better starbucks.

the jw starbucks is rude and unprofessional. ordered a cappuccino and venti water, it was $6, come to find out they charge for water in a cup. i said i don't want the water. after the third time of telling me they need to scan my phone app, i said why don't you train your people to warn about charging for water so i don't have to scan my phone three times. i went back to sit down and some sjw employee says in front of my seven year old that she's going to call security on me. why is a low brain sjw scaring my kid? i contacted starbucks and if they don't make this right, i'm done with starbucks and their sjw nonsense. i spend $200 month in starsucks and they'll let homeless people use their toilet water for free but charge a good customer for a cup of water? i've had enough of this sjw company treating me worse than a homeless person. this jw marriott has nearly convinced me that i'm done with starsucks and their new bonus system sucks as well. i'm really starting to hate starsucks

my morning at this starbucks started out with a venti cup of coffee that had a choking quantity of coffee grounds in it. i then waited five minutes for them to brew a pot without grounds. finally when my coffee was delivered i was informed that there was not one packet of splenda in the store. on top of that, the area near their dumpster in the parking lot is littered with pumpkin sugar cookie boxes. i recommend picassos, it's only two or three minutes further.

this location is literally next door to where i work so i visit often, haha. sometimes i work at 6:30am, sometimes i work at 7:45am. either way, the baristas in the drive thru are helpful and friendly! i believe they recognize me too, haha. anytime i order a food item, they will ask if i would like it warmed up. i also know that my coworkers are treated well when they go too. our drinks are made to order. depending on what time you go, the drive thru can get pretty busy. i personally have not had a problem, but i have driven by and seen a long line before. it definitely depends on day and hour. the medicine ball is a wonderful beverage! made of hot tea, lemonade (steamed) and other healthy items, it helps when you are sick! not a cure, of course, but it will ease your symptoms so you can temporarily function for work. one last thing about this location, there is plenty of space to sit with friends or conduct a coffee meeting with peers! nice little patio in good weather and large sitting room inside! one of reno's best starbucks locations.

some of the best service i have had ever! ordered on the app, it was ready in less time than estimated! then as i'm going in my husband decides he would like something also...i ask if i can have another and they just whip it up! friendly, courteous, excellent service!

today was the first time i ordered from starbucks during quarentine. this location was quick, careful, and friendly. everyone was masked and my mobile order required no contact, which was great. i ordered 3 of the pink-drink alternatives (coconut milk creamed refreshes) for my housemates and all 3 were delicious! most of all, i really happy to see the very clear support for both the black lives matters movement and the lgbtq community. that alone will make me support this store.

at the best of times i am not a starbucks fan but i go with benefit of the doubt and rate against other starbucks. the location is ideal but the service, variety & attitude of staff is ridiculously bad. they really need to employ actual baristas with the wait time and queues that happen here. i was queuing for over 20 mins to get my coffee (standing) and they didn't have half and half which was what i requested and tried to faux make it and it tasted terrible. the staff lied to me and said it was half and half even though i watched them pour something different (and incorrectly - i suspect none of them work for a creamery). i know this seems picky but this is my 4th and last experience here as none have been positive and mediocre is the best it has achieved but generally disappointing.

thursday 1/4/18: my wife, two children and two friends and myself arrived at this location at 7:55pm and purchased $40 in 6 lattes, taxes and tips. there were absolutely zero smiles at any juncture of our experience. we tried to be polite and make small talk with the two employees who served us. they were not having it. after ordering our drinks, we were about to sit down and wait for our drinks when we were practically yelled at that they were closing in 5 minutes so we wouldn't mess their tables up. besides not being friendly or even cordial in the slightest, we were very rudely told to leave at 8:03pm because they closed at 8:00. the manager went to the door, told us they were closed and held it open until we left. i will not visit this location again.

this is the starbucks location that made me want to be a starbucks barista. then i became one, and i had to laugh at my own stupidity. it turns out i didn't want to be one at all, i just loved coffee and pastries. but, that's another story. i love this location because my drinks have always been accurate and the store itself is close to my house. that being said, it's annoyingly challenging to ever get out of their parking lot because of traffic on loughborough. they do not have the fastest drive-thru, but they do have friendly partners and try to move quick. tip: the drive-thru moves faster than if you'd go inside to order your beverage.

i absolutely love this location!! i stop here everyday before work. the morning staff during the week are always upbeat and super friendly. they write little smilies on my cup which makes me smile! they are always so quick, even when there's a mad rush. the weekend crew is a little bit slower and less friendly, but they still get the job done. i switched my starbucks location to these guys about three months ago and i don't miss my old location one bit!

old favorite spot to get coffee. debey has been rude to me on 3 occasions, making me never want to go back. once she ignored me as i waited for her to never look back at me after handing me a half full cup of coffee in the size i ordered. another time i asked for a drink to be split, never was a problem in the last 2 years for this, but she acted suddenly angry i asked and gave me a small hot cup and no lid for an iced drink. i waited for a lid but she ignored me then also. i am tired of feeling this rude person's passive aggression. i wonder how many other have fallen victim to this. she should not work with the public

dumb butch gave me the limp wrist when i handed him my change cause my broke ass did not have bills. i specifically counted and divided my change and verbally told him so it would be a time saver but no of course i'm trying to steal smh i asked for no ice and these bitches in the back ground laughed.
they lost my order and i had to wait 20 minutes. i ended up late for work!! they were not overly friendly either!!

tall ceilings, well lit, lots of action. this starbucks is a great respite at the corner of canal street and saint charles avenue. plenty of seating too. being where it is and what it is, i like sitting here to put your finger on the pulse of what is going on downtown.

outstanding customer service from v.w., came in with my elderly mother, she came out from behind the counter to make sure my mom had a clear enough path to walk to the restroom. very mindful, after that excellent customer service at the counter. eden made terrific drinks for me and my mom, thank you starbucks

my favorite starbucks!!! ...sometimes. i've been coming to this starbucks since it opened, dealing with it's overly-small, pain in the neck parking lot, because it was the closest one to my house. the staff is what keeps me coming back, and no matter how much time passes between my visits, the veteran employees always remember my drink, and genuinely ask how i have been, and what i've been up to. i usually order a grande or venti iced americano, with hazelnut syrup, soy milk, and extra, extra, extra, extra caramel drizzle, and they never disappoint me here, especially with the extra caramel drizzle, so long as it is one of the veteran employees making my drink. they have had a lot of new people come and go, who are typically just... mediocre. they seem unenthused, and never, ever make my drink right. like, really.. how hard is it?! extra caramel drizzle doesn't mean one small drop of it in my drink, especially when you charged me for the extra caramel drizzle... twice!! but, i know my drink is safe when i see one of the regular, veteran employees. i love going in and catching up with them, and they're all awesome. a few of them go to my gym, and we always talk about working out there. it's nice, and adds some personality behind the baristas who previously just made my coffee. now that i have the option of the drive-thru starbucks on the other side of the expressway, it truly says something when i continue to come to this one, with it's horrendous parking. the veteran employees make me excited to return, and pick this starbucks over the new one. the latter consistently does not make my drink correctly, and the cups are always sloppy there, but i'll leave that for the review i'm going to write for them. all in all, this is my favorite starbucks. great employees, good coffee (as long as the right person is making it), with horrendous parking (hence the docked 5th star).

over a two week span they got my order wrong 4 times (out of 10 orders via mobile). i come in fairly early every morning before work when it's not crowded nor busy so i don't understand why they can't or wont, make my drink right.
this is one of the slowest starbucks in the airport system.. been in line over 5 minutes and 3 people have been helped so far... not enough employees working.. there are 4 and one is stocking items..

it really catches me off guard when i swing by this location with about 30 other people in line most of the time, yet somehow the staff here is always as nice and courteous as they can be. this is probably my favorite starbucks around.
turnover rate seems high, so had one bad experience. they might have to pay the employees more to keep them here. i could be wrong, but that is what happened to me.

ordered two drinks from the starbucks in grand sierra resort. one of them still had large chucks of ice that wasn't fully blended. the other was only 2/5 filled, and when we asked why, the worker just told us we could get in line again. unacceptable because we always order the same drink at other starbucks without any trouble or inconvenience. service here is lacking, as well as the coffee. deserves better!

the only reason i rate this store 1 star is because i come in at 5 am, first person in the store, and sit at the handicap table as i have a lot of paperwork to spread out and the small tables are too small, then a worker comes up and tells me, "there is a handicapped customer that comes in and sits at that table" i think, no problem, probably someone in a wheelchair or something, i'll gladly move for them. but no. a tall, very capable man walks in and says i need to move (which i was in the process of) because that's his table. i get you're a regular but i'm still a paying customer who was there before you and you have no physical need for the wheelchair table more than i do, and i don't appreciate the employee telling me where i can and cannot sit. ridiculous

when ordering the cashier told me they only had small lids available, so best to order a small drink. then it turned out they had run out of small cups (??) i ended up with a child size... also, forget about powering up here, the entire seating area only has one working power outlet. i'm left quite baffled by the whole experience...

staff is friendly and helpful, and always get my drink right. there are at times long lines, this usually is at lunch and on weekends, it is a kiosk with about 5 people working at a time so they do a descent job moving the line, but as a frequent patron it is not uncommon to overhear one person order 5 frappachinos for themselves and their whole family. if you are not willing to wait in line there is a coffee shop in nordstroms 1st floor entrance.

i have a love/hate relationship with this starbucks.... hate: i get anixety in the drive thru... the line is crazy and unorganized and i hate getting so mad when people dont follow the proper "drive thru" etiquette. it makes me want to ram into people sometimes which i really dont think is a good way to start off the morning. love: i mean... its starbucks and its a drive thru... need i say more? plus... the morning road rage really wakes me up and the long lines does give me a chance to check my email/groupon/yelp/facebook.. yes the line is that ridiculous. i do go here every morning so i guess it can't be that awful.. sometimes when the line is crazy they do give drinks for free.. which is nice. need a new drink? try .. iced coffee.. non fat milk.. sugar free carmel... its good, low fat and cheap (if you consider $3 every morning cheap for coffee).

i come to this starbucks every so often, and that is for good reason. not once have i come here and actually been greater by a single person. i get to the ordering menu, the same monotone (i cant be bothered with you) voice answers. i feel like i'm burdening them by being there. i order two things every time, i get to the window, one is ready and the other is forgotten. no a smile or a "hi". the only thing that is said to me is "$5.41". i show them my rewards app, she scans it, hands me my one item, starts taking someone else's order, then closes the window. i don't even go back for that second item out of fear that she'll punch me in the face for wanting anything else. i don't write reviews, but i've been coming here for two years and something has to change. it's ridiculous that i go five miles out of my way to avoid this location.
the drinks were fine but all 4 of my sandwiches are cold in the middle. not room temperature. cold.
horrible customer service. major attitude problem. been to many starbucks in us and in other countries definitely do not follow the starbucks great customer service model.
everything great here and it shows. good crowd on an early sunday afternoon, drive thru was easy, my wife's customized latte perfect and so were the crispy sandwiches. very friendly crew!

great product and brand but extremely slow. i go to the one on gandy and by my work on westshore. the speed of the drive through depends on the employee. some are good but most are super slow. very frustrating and making me start to avoid going.

while the workers at this starbucks provide exceptional customer service, i was extremely disheartened to learn that this starbucks severely lacks in its ethics! they have "recycling bins" and trash bins inside, with a recycling symbol indicating the recycling section. while an employee was changing the recycling bag, i asked them if i should wait until they were done so that i could recycle my cup. their response: "we don't actually recycle. there's a recycling dumpster outside, but we actually just combine our recycling bin and trash bin and throw it all in the trash dumpster". i'm thankful for this honest employee, as part of the reason i choose starbucks is for their "support of the environment" and developing counties. this starbucks purposely misleads it's customers and exploits patrons like myself. i will not be back.

this place is super inviting! the staff made it really easy for me to pick an order i like and even offered to remake it if i don't like it so i don't waste my money. my day was been super terrible and the staff had no clue, i have no idea how his day is going yet he put on a polite smile and helped walk me through the menu. scott is such an amazing worker and just wanted to say your effort, although small, was very appreciated! i hope you get granted with happiness and well beings (:
i loveeeeee starbucks it is sooo delicious escpecially the cake pops and frappecinos!!!!

i love starbucks in general, but i particularly like this one. mel is knowledgeable about products and externally helpful. she is a pleasure! my only complaint is the small parking lot and narrow drive thru, but it doesn't stop us from going there.
always enjoy coffee and pastries. great staff cleaned facility which is warm and inviting. heck ya i'll be back
it's like a mcdonald's, but coffee. expect average american coffee and service and you'll be ok.

normally i don't have a problem with this location but today the young lady on the window delayed the line by talking and petting a dog! if you talk that's ok but when you handle food, especially my food, don't touch. to add to the problem she called another worker over and she petted the dog too! i don't know what happened after that because i got out of the line. i hope they washed their hands but i doubt it.

starbucks in general is not my favorite coffee place. in my humble opinion, there are always better places to get coffee. that being said, it is a necessary evil because it is the most accessible place to go when i am in need of coffee and i don't have time to find something else. i have been to a couple other locations closer to my apartment, but this location is my favorite in tampa so far. i like it because of its location--i'm a student at usf, and this location is easily accessible when i'm coming or going from campus. sometimes i stop on the way to school, other time i stop here to drink coffee and do some extra studying after school. starbucks is on campus, of course, but the line to get coffee is always out the door. i also like this location because it is consistent (so far). my current favorite thing to order is the salted sweet cream cold foam cold brew. this place makes it best. the sweet cream foam is the star of this drink. it should be light and fluffy and sit on top of the cold brew. oftentimes when i order this at other locations, the foam goes flat and dissolves into the drink before it even gets in my hands. finally, my service here has always been pleasant and the atmosphere is pleasant. if it's not busy, it's a great place to zone out to some study music and get some work done. however, the inside space is small, so i can see how it might not be the best place to go during busy hours. all in all, i enjoy this starbucks.

wow. worst starbucks ever? i had them remake a drink and they gave me an empty drink! i wanted to go back and get my money refunded but it's inconvenient for me as a customer and they are probably one of the only starbucks that does not have a drive thru store.
although i am not a big fan of coffee, their hot chocolate & sandwiches are definitely worthwhile. the inside of the business is very well decorated, and a nice, quiet place to get work done.

this is just an unnecessary addition to the area. literally there are three other coffee places within a mile that are more interesting and are local businesses. i would much rather support them and i also just like their coffee better. i could understand this place being here if there were no other options in the area, but that is not the case. there are other, better options for coffee. if you want something generic and burnt tasting, then this is your place. otherwise, steer clear.

update - turn on the heat and clean the phuckin bathrooms! it's not enough just mopping the floors. gauddam! gloves mask rag or wipes and wipe the toilets down clean and flush. end of story! excellent study spot!!! spacious and very comfortable. they also have reasonable hours. 10pm close. staff is very friendly. only bummer is, there is no clover at this location.

this morning the problem is the ability to accept payments other than cash. these coffee geniuses don't tell their customers about the purchasing limitations until after one is trapped in a 10 minute drive through. this is simply pathetic customer abuse. is it so hard to show a little common sense and respect towards customers?

the best starbucks in the city! i go about 3 or 4 times a week and i love everyone here. their service is exceptional every single time i visit ! i live on the north east side but i only go to this one that is by my work because they are so friendly and awesome ! and the drinks always taste better, but it could be my biased taste buds ;)

i can see why they have bad reviews, but honestly, they're still new and still going thru the hiring process. i come here about twice a week before work and i have seen a guy get flustered bc he's by himself. which is understandable. so i don't get upset over that. however, this morning there were two new faces. i think the girls name was haley and i didn't catch the guys name but he had that blonde curly justin timberlake hair going on. well they awesome! they were polite, attentive and very quick. they were getting people in and out of there super fast. all the while being nice and courteous. i don't know why people are being so negative on here, they are still new. they are waaayyy better then the one on the icon. which also doesn't accept rewards and stuff. now they are super rude. i would rather skip starbucks then go to them on saturdays. the girls there don't look like they want to be working and they just have the worse attitude. i've given them several chances, hoping that maybe it was an off day, as we all have those days. but nope. that was just their attitude. the only downside to this location is that they're not open on saturdays. hopefully that changes soon. bc i literally live 100 ft from them. otherwise, keep up the good work!

i pass this starbucks on my way to work, i have been going there for 5 years if that tells you anything. why did it take me so long to give them a review? i am not sure, what i can tell you is the service has always been good. sure there have been mix ups but in 5 years i think there have been 2. i am a nurse and "coffee" is very important and when i order my drink i like it to taste how i think its going to taste. i downloaded the app for sb and this has been very dangerous for me. i think i now own stock in sb. if you want a really delicious cup of coffee made just the way you like it, or even if you want to indulge in an after dinner dessert this is the place to stop.
i have never waited as long in a drive thru as i did here. i waited more than 30 minutes

just posting an fyi, you can use your starbucks card but you can't use your rewards here. i've never experienced that so thought i'd share the fyi. they also don't have the yogurt parfaits, salads or sandwiches here. they do have the pastries and breakfast sandwiches.

not my favorite location. ordered my drink in the drive thru. pulled up to the window, no hello, no how are you... i was pouring out a diet soda on the ground. he talked to me as if i were his small child in a rude voice saying "next time can you not do that. it attracts bees and i have to clean that". luckily it was diet and bees don't care about aspartame. i would've been understanding and apologetic about that if he had been kind from the start. i believe his name was andrew. those are the only words he said to me the whole time at the window. yikes.
love starbucks, love their coffee. but crystal was extremely rude at the cash register today. i hope i never come here again if she's working. definitely needs to be trained on customer service.

i ordered hot cocoa and the reindeer cake pop. unfortunately, they forgot to add the whip cream. the cake pop was delicious and i even shared it to my sister. i recommend to order the reindeer cake pop so you can try it your self.
went through the drive thru for an iced coffee. the line was long but the service was quick. my ice coffee was delicious and the barista was friendly.

nothing but terrible experiences at this location! i have never had a smooth and efficient experience while visiting this starbucks, both in the drive thru and in the store. the drive thru is terribly slow with ridiculous wait times of upwards 15 minutes. i've pulled up to the window on several occasions where a barista has even opened the window to greet me or on other occasions they will open the window, process my payment, and then disappear for several minutes without communication what is going on. let's talk about the inside and recap my episode from this morning. there must have been three baristas working drive through, two working the bar, one working the till, and a barista (who i later found out was the manager) was running around providing support where needed. it was absolute chaos. after waiting in line for 15-20 minutes i waited around for my sandwich and pastry. i communicated to the barista at the till that i was going to wait off to the left for my items. i don't think he understood what i meant, none the less i waited for an additional five minutes before asking the barista, who i later found out was the manager, about the status of my order. she stated that they were down at the end of the bar and that they had called my name several minutes ago. i was standing right there. no one ever called my name. i was already late for work and left. as i was pulling out of the plaza, my sandwich didn't smell right. i looked and it was the wrong sandwich. they gave me sausage, which i cannot eat. i had to drive back and run in with the sandwich.the manager first verified the name on the bag before confirming that indeed i was given the wrong sandwich. she re-made the sandwich and asked for which form of payment i used and offered me a refund, not understanding that a refund would be more inconvenient for me as i would have to go back to my car and get my wallet. let's be real, they have free drink cards. she could have provided better customer service. unless this starbucks issues an apology i will not be visiting it anytime soon. what should have been a convenience ended up in a 30 minute experience due to complete chaos behind the counter and poorly run service.

i'm not sure if i could imagine a starbucks that is more sterile. one would be better off going to dunkin donuts or mcdonalds. sure, i'm not a fan of the coffee at dunkin or mcdonalds, but they both have better food (imagin that!) for a much better price.. starbucks is nothing more than a corporate coffee shop with a shelf full of chinese products and coffee that is disguised as good through the addition of large amounts of sugar in the clients drinks. this brownsburg location has two soft chairs with the remainder of the decor designed to get the locals in and out of this retail location as fast as possible. i certainly regret ordering coffee in a paper cup from this place. the staff though was certainly pleasant. please someone open a good coffee shop in brownsburg and hire the starbucks staff.

i live and work around several starbucks and although it may be slightly out of my way at the time, i will always come to the starbucks on 5520 calgary trail. bren, the barista, is an absolute treat. i feel working at a coffee shop you need to be in a positive mood dealing with grumpy bears before their morning coffee and bren really sets the bar. he takes the time to get to know you and always calls me 'friend' which i find makes it more personal! haha... my coffee is always done proper and in a timely manner. not an overly busy coffee shop either so to sit down and get work done isn't much of an issue.

stopped by on my way to work about 8:40am- the drive thru line wrapped around the building, which blocks a couple of parking spots in front. if you are parking and plan to go inside, just remember that you may have to wait for a drive thru car to move out of your way before backing up. service inside was speedy. cashier knew what he was doing and was very friendly. my drink was finished by the another barista and handed to me before i even left the register. my oatmeal was warmed and bagged about 2 minutes later.

the first time i tried to go to this starbucks, as a graduate student who spends most of my time on the center city campus... i couldn't find it. eventually i realized it was tucked away in the business building. after finding it, i can say it's a great location- easy to grab a coffee between meetings or if you have some time to kill. they almost always produce drinks quickly, even when the line is scary long. i've been trying to figure out when the line is long or short, but honestly i haven't been able to figure it out yet- i think it depends when classes in the building are starting, or taking breaks mid-class. i go here about twice a week, and they haven't messed up my drink order yet (good job, starbucks). once they forgot to make my drink, but i complained after waiting about 10 minutes, and they made it quickly with no questions asked. the food options are more limited than other starbucks, and not only do they not have a toaster to toast your bagel (fyi starbucks- toasters cost about $20), but they are usually out of bagels by mid-afternoon. when that happens, no semi-healthy options are left, and i usually default to lemon cake or pumpkin bread. the space can be a bit hard to navigate, since the starbucks is basically jammed into a corner- occasionally causing awkward moments. for example, if a student with a big backpack is standing at the right cash register and there is a line, it's impossible to get from the left cash register to the area where you wait for drinks. i don't care how skinny you are, it's not happening. i'm glad i eventually found this place- it has made my classes and meetings nearby more bearable. minus one point for not having a toaster, and for occasionally being overridden by caffeine-addicted undergraduates.

we love this starbucks and come here every saturday morning. however the recent renovation has replaced the comfy chairs we loved with tall rickety tables. the only way we'll be able to continue coming here is if we can get a shaded table outside. too bad--we loved this place. i didn't see any other regulars there yesterday. fortunately food and service is still good.
ordered a venti got a grande they fixed it by short filling my cup was supposed to be no foam this place is a total disaster

i'm addicted to the 1/2 blueberry black tea 1/2 lemonade this summer. i love it. i get it without the cane sugar and it's not too sweet and very refreshing. i stopped into this location for an afternoon pick me up. the drive-thru is always busy here! i'll be back!

standard starbucks with regards to the interior, albeit slightly larger than most since they have a corner location. barristers have always been friendly towards me (perhaps because i am friendly to them? how novel!). plenty of outdoor seating with ceiling water misters to beat the heat. convenient location too, with plenty of parking & just steps away from pier 1, payless and a gamestop.

these guys love me and i love them too! :-) i come here every weekend. this place is always packed (because it's right across the street from a college). feel free to chat with the staff when it's chill. they're all really cool! i also recommend the lemon loaf... holy cow... so good!
i ordered yesterday and today, both times my order came out good. i ordered the passion lemonade and caramel cloud macchiato. service is good. they are super busy.

i stop in here every morning before work (and have been doing it for about two years now). the staff has always been nothing but friendly to me. i have no idea how they're so perky so early in the morning, but it's refreshing. i always get greeted by name and my drink is started as soon as they see me. overall, i have a great interaction every morning!

i have always had a good experience at this starbucks and especially enjoy sitting at a table outside and relaxing with a drink. i usually order through the app so it's nice to have my drink right there waiting for us. everyone is super sweet to my kids and take time to have a little conversation with them.

this is by far the worst starbucks i've been too. this is the second time i waited about 10-15 minutes waiting for my drink. i had people behind me getting their drinks before i did. only for them to forget about my drink. really? twice!

stopped in harrah's casino for quick a gamble and popped in starbucks for a quick drink. staff very nice and patient as my mom could not remember her order and kept switching it up on the guy. i got a sugar cookie which was pretty good for a packaged item. mom and cheryl got teas and a caramel frappuccino which were to their liking. i got a vente aqua polar freeze which is the best drinks they make :-) a.k.a. iced water.

great customer service!! polly is always happy and cheerful. there's great teamwork in there. a young lady named haley appeared to be under the influence of some type of drug or alcohol or i would have given them 5 stars...

usually pretty good service, pretty good coffee & i like the new pick-up window. just wish there was more space! unfortunately, had a pretty bad experience today with a blonde employee that i hadn't seen before... she got really upset with me for asking for half-and-half and ordering my drink in a venti cup. she claimed that i was "ripping them off" by asking for half-and-half and wasting their venti cups "because [they] run out of venti cups very quickly"... no other employee has ever said anything like that to me plus there's half-and-half out for everyone to use and those things were set as options on their mobile ordering app. someone should really talk to her about this because that was really unprofessional and rude... you could tell that she was stressed out and just taking it out on me in front of other people. very unfair and dampened by day. edit: docking this place down a star as that same issue with the venti cup happened again. if this is because they run out quickly, then they should start ordering more. i've never had this issue at any other starbucks. starbucks should not say that you can choose whatever cup size you want if their employees are just going to put it in another size cup anyway. very unprofessional.
