i'm really kinda bummed to give this place 3 stars after seeing a few 5 star ratings. i had two separate bad visits here. the place is clean and looks great. the staff was ok, nothing overly impressive. i had very long wait times for my order of an iced coffee with cream and sugar... not good. meanwhile two of the other baristas didn't seem phased by the growing line. not impressed.

starbucks is starbucks, you can get the same drink at any location. i come to this starbucks because i enjoy the staff. i especially enjoy the positive energy of a young lady named hailey. she always has a smile when she hands me my drink and seems genuinely interested when she asks me how my day is.
only once out the seven or so times i have been here have the staff been friendly, they are usually not even cordial, just plain rude.

absolutely the slowest service ever! i was second in line and it took them 15 minutes to get me a grande nonfat latte. the store was completely empty besides me and the person in front of me.the store manager just watched while her employees were doing nothing

one of the nicest starbucks in tucson i've been to! i had a great shop back in chandler so was antsy to find another personable location. this crew is great - friendly and work well under pressure. remember, even if you have a simple order there is someone ahead or behind you with a ridiculous request...these baristas do their best to make your experience and drink top notch!

this is the second time i have been to this starbucks and ordered a vanilla cream cold brew which tastes like it has no vanilla at all. this is always my go to drink in the morning and have never had issues when ordering it anywhere else.

compared to other starbucks this one always has the same issue - really dirty, gross restrooms. i stop here on occasion to work before appointments downtown. nothing makes me lose my appetite more for a delicious cup of joe than smelling urine while washing my hands. please fix!!! begging you because you have good wifi. you are kind of my office starbucks.... most recent visit was yesterday 6/28.

so the reason this gets one star is false advertisement. i noticed after i got my holiday drink, on the chalkboard, it clearly stated buy a holiday coffee or teavanna tea and get a $5 gift card. additionally it had a picture of three holiday drinks. so i asked for my gift card. i was then told i had to buy a pound of either. i said that was not how it was advertised on the board. the cashier agreed and said he had in fact been handing them out but had gotten in trouble. he said his shifts after was on break. so i waited. in the meantime, he turned it over to another worker " who knows more about this". i explained to her and she looked at the erroneous board. she went in the back and got the manager, who was snippy and bitchy and said " you'd buy a pound of coffee to get a $5 gift card?" i said " no i've already bought a coffee" as indicated on the board. he then goes around to a stand where there are bags of coffee and says " see the sign says right here, buy a pound and get a gift card" i asked him how would i see that from where i was standing. all that was in my sight was the board in front of me.he just stared at me. i said that was false advertisement. he then quickly walked over and said "well!!!" and dramatically tore the board down and took it in the back. outrageous!! and over a $5 gift card and starbuck's mistake! he should be ashamed!!!

i can't believe i created a yelp account to review this terrible starbucks. i've been to hundreds of restaurants across the country and this is the one that caused me to create a yelp account. this is my 3rd and final time ever stepping foot into this piece of crap. my most recent attempt at ordering a drink in this craphouse failed miserably twice. i ordered a salted caramel mocha frappaccino and got a hot mocha coffee they put in ice (this after they tried to give me a salted caramal hot coffee. this piece of crap was luke warm by the time i took my first sip and tossed it out at the first trashcan i saw. my previous experiences here were just as bad. with 2 customers before me, i waited 10-15 minutes for a freaking coffee. i am not sure if the management at this starbucks enjoys hiring 50 iq baristas (someone needs to support the idiots), but this is not acceptable. not only can these baristas not give you what you ordered, they are rude. you must avoid going to this starbucks at all costs. if this starbucks is any indication of the health of this organization, start shorting the stock now and thank me later. this company and location is complete garbage. i only wish 0 or negative stars were an option. i hope corporate realizes the management at this location is an embarrassment to the brand. fire everyone and start over.
yelp standing up for the cop hating management and employees at this store by hiding negative reviews. i'm sure this comment will be hidden too.

absolutely the worst starbucks in new orleans . store is dirty, unkempt and not stocked with basic supplies i.e. straws and napkins. baristas are rude and can't even get a mobile order correct or complete . save your money and aggravation and go somewhere else , anywhere else. not interested in guest satisfaction at all. avoid avoid avoid .ps french truck coffee just opened down magazine , go there , i know i will.

the coffee is fine, but the early morning staff is horrible. the early morning barista is condescending, rude and just overall nasty. she doesn't make time for the line of customers, but does make time to chat with her friends and talk down to you when you order. overall, good place to get coffee with an attitude.

this starbucks doesn't have a drive through like some of the newer ones in the area but it does have an awesome sit in area to work on stuff with wifi and lots and lots of seating. 10+ tables, a window bar, couch, and a seating area with 6 or so comfy chairs. the staff is awesome too!

this starbucks is small but very cozy. staffed with a caring & generous crew, my experiences here have been wonderful! i usually find myself popping-in to grab an espresso drink, but occasionally i sit down and browse the internet, chat with friends or do some research for work. all together this starbucks is one of those rare gems in a generally sparse coffee desert.

starbucks! it's random that i come to this location but when i do i usually go through the drive thru. when i go through the drive through it's flawless. on monday 11/25/19 i decided to go inside‍.the girl at the register..ughhh let's jus say rude!! aside from placing my order i asked for a large cup of water with ice. sounds simple right... so when i went to pick up my order the guy gave me my food, coffee and a little cup of ice water. since the girl who took my order wasn't busy i went over to her and let her know i asked for a large cup of ice water. her response was.. well you have to let us know you wanted a venti cup.. i wanted to say, really fool i don't work at starbucks to know their terminology! instead i just let her know that whenever i go through the drive thru it's not an issue.. anyway.. she takes the cup pours it into this venti cup n then fills it up with just ice! her attitude was straight rude definitely not what i would expect at a starbucks in cherry hill. so for her negativity this starbucks gets a super

how do you forget one person's drink when you have only one person ordering? starbucks needs to check with this staff. if these baristas keep this pattern they will be called out on it. i have visited so many starbucks and never once i've heard someone forgot a (the only ordering) customer's drink. she bluntly asked me what i was waiting for, to which i should have answered 'your phone number'. the whole staff can not be that senile. starbucks is starbucks, but some location needs training. as customers travel everywhere, managers should be concerned with the brand's image. now, i was trying the new drink and i can't tell if this is the normal taste or not because i can't trust this barista's skill. it tastes very plain (new drink, cascara). what a shameful experience! those managers need to be more mindful and not just drag brands' names in their messy act.
love love love starbucks! the employees here are super nice and friendly! they know your order, they customize your coffee at your request and make super yummy drinks! 5 stars all the way from me!!

i usually go to the starbucks in montecito, but stopped here to avoid traffic on coast village rd one afternoon. i know they don't serve bold after noon, so i asked them to "pour it over". unfortunately, this took 20 minutes and was not really hot when they handed it to me. strike 1. the barista informed me they don't sell the double choc. chip brownies at this location because they don't sell well. i guess that means that i am not the typical carpinteria customer... so i went to the donut shop across the street and bought my brownie. i would avoid this location... the montecito starbucks is by far better and the help is much more helpful with hot coffee.
literally so slow, you would think they would be a little faster with orders. i should not be waiting 30 minutes for a drink.

so glad they moved down the street away from the train station where it was a nightmare to park let alone get back onto lancaster avenue. i fequent this drive through at least 3 days a week and 99% of the time they are quick, kind and drinks are correct. i try to stay away from any business during peak hours but this is my exception to the rule. 2 thumbs up for easy access and convenience.
excellent baristas! a lot better service than usual. very knowledgeable of their products and very helpful. thanks guys!

drinks are always good here -- try the toffee mocha -- but the staff is a little on the raucous side. if you're trying to get any work done, i advise sitting in the comfortable couches toward the front of the store, the farthest point from where the drinks are being made. that is, unless you'd like to be entertained by a dissertation on why one manager is superior to another, and the other nuances of the interpersonal relationships of the staff -- a kind of soap opera, really. as the coffee bean turns...ok, i'm a dork. in fact, takeout might be a better option all around. i would give this location a 4 for takeout experiences.

you know what, this is a place of respect. they are so nice whenever i come to get my coffee. i've gotten it twice now, they're so friendly and it's an experience with them. they greet me by name and they laughed with me and joked around with me! they're a vibe! i hope they get to know i said this. very clean store. you guys are awesome!

if it's one thing the employees of starbucks can do right it's mess up an easy order. for the third time in a row, i've ordered egg white bites. they have given me regular egg with bacon. save your time and money and go to a local family owned establishment instead.

generally speaking, the people working at this starbucks are friendly and get your drinks just like you asked for them, so i've never had any complaint. up to this morning i would have given 5 stars. but this morning the male server who handed out my drink at the drive thru had the filthiest finger nails i've ever seen. no food service worker should ever have such nasty looking hands. i couldn't find a way to comment inside the starbucks app, so here i am. if it happens again i'll stop going to this store.

the service good but here is what i don't like, this is a franchise place meaning there are starbucks all over the country. i should be able to go to one starbucks in phoenix and it taste the same in missouri but that wasn't the case. the store was dirty and the restroom was dirty. i get it when it gets busy that it can cause a mess but when you walk in and people are just hanging around and the store is dirty that's a problem. the macchiato was not good and pretty much tasted like milk. for a 5 dollar coffee i expect for it to taste better. was not happy with this starbucks
covid coffee house pick-up & go this winter seating upside-down #nola_haiku @starbucks #covid #haiku #mardigras2021 #coffeeshop #freret

the staff is horrifically slow. this starbucks is the worst one i've ever been to. food and drinks all taste the same, as usual, but the staff clearly does not care. they're super slow and often get orders wrong. the one thing i like about starbucks is that they are all efficient and everything tastes pretty good. this one is not either of those things.

starbucks at cheshire is one of the best in town. the location is right off the highway, and is very spacious inside. not to mention it is like the inside of someone's living room, which makes it super cozy. the service is always fast and pleasant, and i never have trouble finding seating. many people do come here to work and study. will keep suggesting this as my networking coffee shop before and after work!
i went in here before my interview at home depot. i usually don't buy star bucks cause i can go to dunkin' or even wawa get coffee. the barestia was very friendly.

i love the drive through at this starbucks. i realize that sentence makes me sound like the laziest person alive, but i'm typically on my way to a friend's house whenever i swing by this location and the drive through is just too convenient to pass up. everyone who works there is super friendly from my experiences and the line moves quickly. they even plug your drinks with those little green plastic things so there's no spillage. i just wish there was a more convenient way to get out of the parking lot, instead of having to drive back through the strip mall lot.

i come to this starbucks several times a week, mostly using the drive through but also coming inside often to work. the staff is friendly and knowledgeable. they seem to keep their staff--there's not a lot of turnover and constant training. the drive through is really quick. the amount of seating is pretty good for a smaller sized starbucks. there's a public table in the middle where you can sit and plug in your laptop. it's pretty quiet most of the time. this starbucks has floor to ceiling windows on two sides, which makes it really bright and inviting. but it is so cold in the winter. it's difficult to work with your coat on. overall, great, fast, awesome starbucks. one of my dacori

yeah jeremy, chris, vanessa, and the rest of the crew are the best in all of tucson and i'm not just making it up. it's the best place to go to get your starbucks. i hope their boss see's this because they deserve a raise. they are very special people. if i had a business i'd hire them on the spot!

i am not a coffee fan, but i love the zen tea. i usually purchase it in tea bags and prepare it at home. i decided to stop in and order one directly from starbucks since i had some time to kill before my next meeting. worst mistake ever! they used boiling water, which anyone that knows how to make tea knows that boiling water cooks the leaves and leaves them tasting disgusting and also burns the mouth of anyone who attempts to drink said tea. i ended up throwing away my overpriced cup of bitter water and grabbing my ice water in my car. i felt that there was no point of asking for a manager, they are all in their twenties.. like they care.
busy location, generally friendly staff, inadequate seating, could be much cleaner, a convenient location, but too small.

this was one of the best drive thru starbucks locations that i've been to in a long time. it's perplexing that there were so many negative reviews that i read when i was in search of a local to me starbucks. not seeing any other locations to try, i put on a brave face and attempted to go through the drive-thru. admittedly, it's a rather odd configuration due to space limitations, but truly if that's really the only issue, it should not be an issue. i found the customer service to be five star plus, and who could ask for more?! i was greeted through the speaker box by brittany, who by all accounts was not only super sweet, but very helpful when i asked a couple of questions about a new drink that i wanted to try. when i arrived at the window and flashed my phone app, brittany smiled, asked me how my day was going, and if there was anything else that i would like to add to my order. she did an excellent job of promoting products which i've never had happened to me at a window before. i really appreciated it. she even went out of her way to show me a couple of the new tumblers that they had just received. then to top it off, she very politely reminded me that if i wanted to use any of my stars for my purchase i could do so. great customer service! my drink was totally on point! and when i'm back in the area, this absolutely will be my go to place.

i stopped in for a pound of coffee. justin overheard me tell someone i would like a cappuccino and he said, "well you've come to the right place!". i was skeptical because i have walked away from the last three cappuccinos i have ordered at starbucks. i didn't think he could pull it off but i liked his enthusiasm so i ordered it anyway. it was the best cappuccino i have ever had at starbucks.

of course starbucks is known for its coffee and amazing blends! however, today was my first time trying the breakfast. i ordered the sausage chicken biscuit and it was certainly a treat. i was shockingly surprised at how amazing this breakfast sandwich was. it was piping hot, sweetness from the biscuit and spicy, from the gravy. this's sandwich melted in my mouth. better than mcdonalds, chic-file, and burger kings sausage biscuit. i would rate this sandwich 5 stars hands down

it's super nice having another starbucks on the other side of the highway as it cuts down on time when i'm south of i-80. love the location! unfortunately, when it comes to the food & beverages it's a different story. i've gone in several days in a row since they have opened and have had nothing but problems. to start, i ordered a decaf drink, which was not made as decaf. i have a caffeine sensitivity that causes heart issues for me so after drinking my beverage, i experienced an extremely high heart rate for the better part of two hours. i've also ordered white mocha hot chocolates a couple times and received the white mochas with coffee instead. luckily i can taste the difference when coffee is in a drink, so i was able to avoid any caffeine effects on my heart, but nonetheless still disappointing. in regard to the taste and make of the beverages themselves, i've also been pretty disappointed. any drink containing syrup and milk tends to only taste like milk, so i'm unsure if the proper proportions of each ingredient are being used or if the recipes are being followed correctly. i also believe there is something wrong with their nitrogen when it comes to the nitro cold brew. more than half of the cup ends up being foam, even after the drink settles. the amount of cold brew i receive in a grande is not even enough to fill up a tall cup. lastly, i've noticed that when i ask for extra caramel drizzle, they are only using as much as a normal order and not coating the cup/drink in the necessary places. there has been several times where i will ask for cinnamon to be steamed into milk and it is not being done, the taste difference is something you can't miss on that nonetheless watching the baristas, so customizations aren't always being followed. i've also ordered food several times only to receive the incorrect item. just this morning, i ordered a double smoked bacon sandwich with no egg, only to receive an impossible sandwich. while the customer service is great and everyone is friendly, i question if the baristas have received enough/proper training. i'm in a bit of a rut because i desperately want to shop at this location frequently, as the location is convenient and the store itself is absolutely beautiful and very well kept and clean; however, it becomes stressful worrying about my health when it comes to receiving non-decaf when asking for decaf. it's also such a let down for the place to be as inconsistent as it is. i can understand that things happen, but the same issues occurring again and again every visit cannot be a coincidence. i don't feel comfortable spending $15 on breakfast or lunch, knowing that it will have some issue, and i will not always have the time to flag down an employee for a drink or food remake. this place has amazing potential, but there's definitely some kinks that need to be worked out. i will probably spend the extra time to drive to the starbucks on the other side of the highway as i know the small amount of wait time will be worth knowing i'm receiving food and beverage to the starbucks standard we all know and love.

i honestly don't know how long these baristas have been working here let alone being an actual certified barista but it's just amazing how much they don't know about the drinks. i came in this weekend and got an iced matcha green tea latte with light ice and no foam and when i was handed my drink i was given a white drink with the matcha powder stuck at the bottom and i asked to have my drink shaken and he told me that i had to ask for it to be shaken because it doesn't come that way. i told him that i was a starbucks barista and that they come shaken so that the powder is mixed into the drink. long story short he again handed me a drink that was 95% milk because he just dumped the previous drink into the shaker and so then it was just over shaken milk with extra foam and extra ice. overall extremely disappointed with the service and the quality of my drinks i bought. and this isn't the first time i've had terrible service here. no more second chances for this store.

the weekday morning crew at this store is absolutely the best. they are always friendly and quick, using a style that makes you feel invited and not rushed. they have offered excellent suggestions every time i have asked for ways to tweak any of my favorite drinks. i pass by 2 other starbucks on my way into the office because the service at this location is truly the best.

the starbucks is the best! their singing barista is a sweetheart and they are one of the fastest starbucks drive thrus around! on a busy work morning they brighten your day, quickly give you your coffee and you leave with a smile along with your caffeine fix!

yeah, so i bet seattle is thrilled to hear we all equate a starbucks with their fine, rainy city. with xoconocho two doors down, and the listening room across the street, i'm not sure how this starbucks gets any business. even merridee's down the street offers free wi-fi. enough ranting. the coffee here is identical to all the other starbuck's coffee i have ever tried. if it were not for the urge of people to give me starbucks gift cards and holidays and birthdays, i would not be here.
this is an excellent starbucks location. beth who works here is always inviting and an amazing person. i would highly recommend this starbucks location to all. wonderful new building and very clean.

decent starbucks. we got there 15 minutes before they were closing and it seemed like they were already closed. the food case was empty and the servers seemed bothered that we came in. my colleague had a medicine ball and they didn't have enough tea bags to make it correctly. my coffee was old and i wish they would have said something about getting something other than old coffee.

this is a pretty good sbux. long but swiftly moving line. accurate order fulfillment. ample condiment station. plenty of comfy shmumfy leather armchairs, footrests, and power outlets. what more could you ask for? maybe a lil of that free sbux wi-fi. but no, its in the usf library and guess what? you need a visitor code. oh well. everything else is great.

the barista was making a drink for a target employee and was talking me and a girl got in line the target employer left and she took my order then proceeded to take other orders before starting any. my point is you were sitting there chatting with a target employee you could have done that while taking my order and starting it. it wasnt even that hard an order. waited 15min for an iced tea.

tracy is the sweetest lady there. i go there most mornings before work, and we always chat and talk. my breakfast is always delicious and made quickly. even if it wasnt good, i would still go here just for the people who work here. the customer service is beyond compare.
cameron is a fucking gem! always brightens my day when he is there ! ps. liz and davis and everyone here are troopers
is one of the fast drive trhru starbucks so far

update the damn app! hate it when i decide what i want and order it. then when we get there that item us out of stock! so exasperating. started coming to this starbucks because the other store didn't update their app either. would change if there was another store in the area. grrrr, just update your app when something is no longer available. please! otherwise what is the point of having a mobile app to order ahead? blah!!
i haven't had many great starbucks experiences but this one was really nice and definitely surprised me! great service and i definitely recommend! i'll definitely be going back.
went on wed 11/4/15. great service service as usual. cashier in drive thru was friendly, helpful and a delight! he had great red hair!

i've quite honestly experienced better service at other starbucks locations. that said, i really do appreciate the fact that i can use my starbucks gold card (or app) here just like at any other starbucks location. this location isn't one where you would be likely to sit and enjoy your beverage: instead you are expected to buy one and go elsewhere to enjoy it. on my most recent visit, the staff member serving me didn't understand what i wanted until i rephrased it twice and even after i answered her questions she provided me a slightly different product than what i requested (which really wasn't a problem but it was rather odd). i do appreciate having access to starbucks in this location but i wish the service were better, especially when it is summer and not very busy, and that there were more of a seating area available.

i would like to give much appreciation to this starbuck's team for working so hard this morning. (11/18/21). they were so busy and seemed to have very little staffing, but they were so kind and calm. they gave me a heads up that my drink would take a while to come out and i was okay with that. i just really want to give appreciation to them and for all their hard work. you guys were great!

coming from new york my standards are high... not only is the service slow as molasses but mid way though my iced coffee i almost choked on the baristas turquoise hair!!! i literally had to pull it out of my throat. the most horrifying experience ever! just the thought of it sitting in my coffee the entire time gives me the chills.. on top of the fact i had to pry it out of my throat. the gentlemen before me had returned his cappuccino because he found a hair in his cup as well.i've been coming to starbucks for years and never ever has this happened. being a female i understand that our hair falls out all the time but as an establishment that handles food and beverages this should never happen. i'm so very disappointed!

this is our neighborhood starbucks and i have to give it 5-stars. we never have any issues with our drinks and of course going here for years now, you get to know the staff. we take our dogs here on the weekends, and sometimes during the week, in the drive-thru, and they are very dog friendly. they love to give treats to our dogs and even ask about them if we don't have them with us. it's a great location, i think it's easy to get to, and the drive-thru is a bonus.

this particular location is great because it's right on my way to work but they are so so slow, and the staff isn't very friendly. except for the older lady with grey hair. she's really nice, i think she's the manager. i always end up waiting at least 10-15 in the drive thru and at least 10 mins when going inside...

ok, just putting it out there because every time i go, at least one of the peeps in front of me passes by the microphone to order and drives up tot he menu board. mind you, most places have the microphone to order on the menu board. not at this starbuck's. so...the employee inside is yelling..."i cannot hear you, please speak up, or please repeat that". the person in the car is yelling, "sorry, i pulled too far forward" and is hanging their head out yelling to order. unless you are a regular here...happens all the time. put a big green sign on the black tower to order from that is before the menu board. have it say "order here". we seriously take bets every time we go on the chances of a newbie ordering and passing up and going to the menu board. if i had a dollar....i tell ya. this place is always busy and seems like they just do not staff accordingly. i mean, come on, in this area, you know a starbuck's will be hoppin. anyhew. it is what it is.

this starbucks is decent, not the best or worst i've been too. it's mostly clean considering how much foot traffic there is. the baristas are nice. there's extra seating upstairs, only one bathroom, which considering how large the starbucks is - it stinks. my coffee was okay, nothing spectacular. i asked for lite ice but they didn't really give me lite. overall it's convenient if you're in the area.

i had a meeting today at this location and it was a nice summer sunday @12:30. the place was crowded when i arrived with no available parking. i waited for about 5 minutes, parked and went inside. all of the tables were taken with electrical outlets which was fine because my pc was not totally dead. typical sunday morning crowd. in less than 15 minutes someone left and i was able to charge up. i ordered my drink and took the last 2 napkins. bummer. i wiped the table and in 5 minutes the napkins holders were full. i had to smile because this is great service. the place was clean and orderly. the only pet peeve was the free wifi was spotty and it took several attempts to login. meeting was great and it was another successful starbucks boardroom meeting. :)

dude. can you guys actually do your job. please. i'd appreciate it. i've been sitting here for over half an hour and still no food. i even went up to the counter and one of the employees just stared at me. if only jason was here. he does a great job.
one of the best in the area! excellent service, friendly (and funny!) staff, great environment.

this is a new drive-thru starbucks. so far, while a "cozy" location, it does have a good amount of seating for its size. there's outdoor, patio seating as well as bar, table and chair, comfy chairs, and a long rectangular table with seating. the staff is friendly and they made my drink well. i'm always glad to see the opening of a new starbucks and i bet it'll be a well-used location in no time.

by far my favorite starbucks location in the new orleans area! the service is usually very fast and if there ever is a wait, i'm never in line for more than 20 minutes. every staff member at this location is very accommodating, energetic and friendly! caesar is always a pleasure to see in the mornings when picking up or placing my order. also to mention that this location and their baristas are always consistent! my coffee always tastes very good. thanks n. causeway staff for always making my mornings better!

seriously slow, employees need retrained on how to manage customer flow and satisfaction. i was specifically told that i would have to wait in line between 15-20 minutes before i could be served a black coffee which merely required turning around and pouring a cup of coffee. come on gang. you are surely smarter than this! had to walk away from the counter.
incredibly friendly staff on this brilliant easter morning. numerous outdoor tables that don't feel directly adjacent to road. rambunctous guys playing backgammon. all around splendid experience.

i have went to this location more than enough times that i can say this place is ok. the drinks and food are great but the service is so slow. workers are friendly but it seems they're short staffed all the time! i have went in and went to the drive thu. just be prepared that you may have a long wait and i don't recommend when you are in a hurry

they were out of just about everything and that is not okay. i couldn't get anything because all their options were awful. i would not recommend. service was awful and cookies were not fresh. this is a disgrace. update: i ordered a drink and it took 20 minutes to come. it wasn't even the right drink. starbucks has to get it together.

not the best starbucks. they seem rushed even when there is not many people. as usual this time of year at all starbucks they are out of the ingredients for the special holiday drinks, but they do not make a note on the board or on the drive thru, so you have to go through the order and "sorry out" a couple of times to get an order in. and unlike other starbucks they do not make an attempt to offer suggestions, cut the price, or substitute a similar ingredient, they just leave you hanging with minimal apologies. there is indoor and outdoor seating that his facing a busy street. the location is convenient to some shopping, ie trader joes and bookmans, but not a great location to just take it easy. if you come here it is only, obviously, to get your chain coffee drink and to move on. if you have a minute or two drive a couple miles east to the speedway and pantano store or to broadway and kolb for the recently remodeled store.

this is where my husband and i get our morning coffee when we are in the area visiting family. i always have a complicated order, and it is always met with a smile. the staff here are great, above average staff for a starbucks in my experience. they are super friendly, have never messed up my order, and have been very accommodating. for example, i wanted an egg and cheese breakfast sandwich the last time i was here, and they were out of them. the cashier offered to take the sausage off of a sausage, egg, and cheese for me before it was heated. i've never been able to sit down and enjoy my coffee in-house, as the tables are always full when i stop by. the reasons it is a four and not a five: 1. i always order my drink extra-hot, and sometimes i still get it regular temperature (not a huge deal as i just go home and microwave it), 2. during busy times they run out of different food items quickly, and 3. it is a small starbucks and is always very crowded with a constant line (though i think they handle the volume of business generally well). i'm only picky about a few things, and my coffee is one of them. i always know when i come here that i will get what i'm expecting and will get great customer service (much appreciated, especially before i get my morning coffee!).

if i could give one star. i totally would. i ordered a bagel. the woman grabbed the bagel with her bare hands. i didn't see her wash or hands or put any gloves on. i'm kind of disgusted. with all the food issues going on lately. i would've expected better service and cleanliness.

this is the most personable starbucks crew i've ever met. pretty much everyone is extremely great. the only reason i didn't give a full 5 stars is one employee i believe named sally is extremely rude. i come in almost everyday and am always extremely pleasant to her saying thank you and all i get is a stank face and ignored. aside from that these people are amazing. would recommend highly.

starbucks is starbucks. this store has been pretty decent. major problem - this location is too small. they parking lot constantly backs up and the drive thru blocks cars in their spots because it routes right through the parking lot. the drinks has always been really good coming out of this location though and the staff has always been really friendly. i hope maybe they expand this store or move it to a bigger location with a better design for a drive thru.
customer service was great, mobile order on point. breakfast items perfectly warmed. thanks for keeping me caffeinated.

please update your hours! i've tried to come through between 5:30-6am multiple times when being called into work and either no one is there or, like this morning, one lady is there without a headset and not paying any attention to let customers in.
worst starbucks i have ever visited. they're have a 7 people behind the counter that do not know what they're doing-the line just grows. i've never waited this long for a drink and food.

i rarely visit this specific starbucks location, but whenever i do there seems to be an issue that reminds me of why i don't come here! they're always out of something and seem to barely be capable of putting together a drink correctly. now yesterday was probably my third time experiencing a nasty attitude from one of the baristas. when i ordered my frappe, i specified that i wanted no whipped cream. of course once i received it there was whipped cream on top, (figures). i told the barista i wanted no whipped cream, as if to only remind her of something she clearly forgot, and she tells me, "ok well next time just let us know" (full blown attitude). i'm over this starbucks location!
super busy location. barista forgot about my drink and promptly made it when i asked about it and gave me a free drink coupon to make up for it. great customer service!

rude! the women who work there are extremely rude and inappropriate. i have been there to witness and experience them eye rolling and yelling at customers multiple times. it's a shame because the space is really nice with the fireplace, etc.

stopped by the drive thru this morning, about 6:20, and noticed that the car in front of me was taking an extra long time. the car finally drove off. when i pulled up to order, no one ever came on to ask to take my order hence, why the previous car drove off. i went to the window and no one ever came to the window and i noticed there were people outside banging on the door. the employee inside looked at them and kept doing what he was doing. i went to another starbucks. the employee/s could have placed signs at the drive thru and the door to inform customers why the store wasn't opened. bad business practice!!!!!

before i asked for non fat frappuccino and no whip but they put whip cream on it and then they restarted it but ended up giving me light frappuccino which had the light syrup and tasted really bad i just threw it away. today i asked for non fat latte with no whip the same barista added whip and when i told him i don't want whip he did not do the latte again just scooped some of the whip off the top. when i asked him are you sure this has no whip cream in it he said it is a drink that has a lot of chocolate in it and a lot of fat in it but i assure you it does not have whip cream in it. i know what i'm doing and i calculate the calories of chocolate and everything else. wow lots of attitude for so much money put into a cup of coffee.
insanely slow for any starbucks - let alone an airport one. lines have consistently gotten worse, but service speed as stayed stagnant over the past couple years.

the pandemic really had a negative effect on this once great place.nowadays you never when they are going to open or when they are going to close.the labor shortage has hit this place hard.i do not fault the employees.many are inexperienced with little training.hopefully and eventually this shop will become my home away from home again.

staff here is always friendly and efficient. great location- very convenient and although they don't have a drive-thru here, it is sometimes faster here than other starbucks in the area with a drive-thru. pet-friendly outside and this store is always clean and well-stocked. enjoy coming here.

this is a nice little starbucks! downsides: parking can be tough during peak hours, and the lineup has a terrible set up. positives: quick service, super friendly staff, lots of seating, and a very handy location! i was literally drooling over the amazing selection of baking and other food in the display case, but just went with my standard flat white today. can't wait to try some of those noms!

the barista at the window was very friendly and seemed happy. they also put like alooot of caramel in our drinks since we asked for extra caramel. but they were generous with it and i appreciate that. great location and you can tell they care.

first, the staff was welcoming and efficient. my americano (no extras) was perfectly made. they do charge over $4 for a venti. my issue is that they use the hilton wi-fi which is less than stellar. the signon page would not open on our android phones. it works on windows but even at that, the signal was only two bars and slow.

horrible customer service & to boot $10+ they gave us 2 coffee and milk when we paid for specially lattes....not impressed. i've been a "starbuckets" drinker for years and always looked forward to the holiday flavors. was in a giving mood & decided to treat a co-worker after lunch. ok so she has a complex coffee order (i zoned out somewhere in the cinnamon, triple, no foam, whip...) the gal in drive-through had to ask a few times herself to get it right....no biggie. get to the window and waiting for order i could sense hostility....the gal makes a comment about "head-set not always working" co worker said it's ok you got it right, insert awkward pause, then "you seemed ungrateful" huh?!? i was flabbergasted thought we were the customer and your goal is to get my order that i paid for. i was floored. after a "manager" gets snarky with us and rolls her eyes we pull away at this point scared to drink coffees and realize they just gave us coffee and milk not the drinks ordered. not coming again to this location and suggest unless you are into s&m don't go there yourself. thank goodness the folks at corporate are decent and understanding.

first time coming to nevada, and wanted some starbucks. stopped by the closest one and i ordered a caramel macchiato with extra drizzle. i also ordered some egg/bacon bites. got to the window they didn't have them, and they gave me the option of getting egg white bites (never again). they were not good what's so ever. do not recommend. and they didn't put extra caramel in my drink. couldn't even eat the egg white bites, they were just not it.!!!!

42 people in line and 3 employees. i'm not complaining if it's a $2 coffee. but if i'm paying $7 for my venti iced nonfat upside down caramel macchiato, i expect to have a half decent customer experience. little help here?

this starbucks is connected to the tampa downtown embassy suites. if you are walking into the hotel the starbucks is to the left. there is a row of tables and chairs on the outside and some basic seating inside. you are likely going to sit in the hotel lobbly to be honest which is connected on the inside. it's a nice place. seemed clean and i did not see a bathroom but the hotel has a public restrooms which i assume you would end up using anyway. the food offering was not awesome as they seemed to be out of a few items but they had what i wanted so i was satisfied. i ended up grabbing some waters and a protein box so i was an easy customer. will try some actual coffee soon.

i love this place! great environment to hang out. especially being from a church in the same parking lot, i spend a lot of time here through out the week meeting people, and on days to get away and study. all beverages are rad, and friendly staff. the manager is truly welcoming as well.

nasty help. i stepped inside the door while putting my mask on and suddenly i hear this girl scream put your mask on!!. terrible extremists. i will not be back . you have to give a person the chance to put on the mask as you walk in the door. also place is dirty and maybe they should be more concerned about spreading germs to others. not a nice place to grab a cup. go elsewhere!
i've never seen such fast working starbucks employees! they get the job done and move on to the next in lightening speed. wish they were this fast at my local starbucks.
if you're open until 830o, don't lock the doors at 8p and make people walk through the drive thru to get a drink

awesome new space but terrible service. this place just does whatever they want with your drink and they don't care when your drink was messed up or with the wrong ingredients - it's like you're bothering them. go here for the atmosphere but keep your drink order simple.

the worst starbucks ever!!! for the life of me, i do not understand why this starbucks which is connected to one if not the largest hotel in the city are out or tea, premier sandwiches, and oatmeal. being attached to the marriott - i am positive that the calendar of events and conferences are shared. to make matters worse, they are not sharing with the people standing in line that they are out of almost everything. so patrons are waiting 10-20 min to get served only to be told that they are out of all the meals and even black tea. unacceptable. whenever i think about the worst service in starbucks, this location will always come to mind.

this is my local starbucks and i'm going to reconsider ever going back after dealing with one incredibly rude employee. one more than one occasion the older male employee has denied making decaf coffee. the first time it was in the evening and i was told they only make decaf in the morning. this morning i went in at 9:30am and was, once again, told they do not have decaf coffee and when i asked why i was yelled at by this man and told "we do not make decaf because no one drinks it" despite the fact that i was asking for it. i've always ordered decaf and this has never been a problem. seeing other reviews, where people have been denied other types of coffee, it looks like this guy just refuses to do his job. will never return here. awful customer "service" especially during the holidays. someone needs to get rid of scrooge!

a beautiful patio and outdoor seating to enjoy your favorite starbuck purchase. mature trees, shaded and quite location to appreciate my chai tea. indoor seating for studies or a nice spot to get some work done and the staff is friendly. bring change for parking meters if not on your bike.

i hope people understand that this starbucks is populated by human beings who have been working with the public throughout the pandemic--i hate to see negative reviews when they can't help that their staff is out sick or that they had to close early due to lack of supplies... anyway this is my go to starbucks and i love the people here, always kind and polite even when i forget my order as soon as i walk up lol. you all are doing a great job.
teeny tiny location. quick, friendly service and oddly not busy during the mid afternoon. loses a star for not really having anywhere to sit inside.
always packed, always slow. snobby suburban moms being rude. sucks it's the only starbucks near by.
quick starbucks that has a good staff to move the line... the inside is a little tight but there is plenty of room outside on the patio
my iced caramel macchiato has no caramel? how is that possible? cafe seems to be doing well- lots of customers and friendly team. we just need caramel in the caramel drinks!
worst starbucks i have ever been too every time i've gone service is always good but the drinks are terrible tastes like burnt coffee every time

this starbucks location is in the fred meyer on overland road. at this visit i got a berry refresher. it's was good. i like other locations better. the baristas aren't as peppy here as they are at others but i'm okay with that. overall it's a starbucks and i'm a fan of this franchise.

slowest starbucks ever. i downloaded the yelp app and left this review in the time it took to place my order and get it, 2 cars back from the box. this isn't the first time. very nice staff, and great new location. couches outside are a nice touch. please...pick up the pace!

first of all, let me just put it out there that i love starbucks! i'm originally form seattle, washington & a starbucks girl at heart! i've been coming to this location for almost a year now and have probably come a good 20 times. it's a convenient location in village landing in the same parking lot as save on foods and shoppers drug mart. their drive through is efficient and friendly. the inside of the store is smaller than most starbucks and for that reason they only get 4 stars. sometimes it can be tough to get a seat and it doesn't feel as cozy and inviting as some of my fave locations. the staff are super friendly and cheerful even early in the morning! my heart belongs to starbucks...

the drive through at this location looked really long today so i decided to just go inside. that later proved to be a mistake when the woman taking my order took probably 5 whole minutes to take my order. 5 minutes in the grand scheme of things is not much but when the man next to her is serving 3-4 people before she was even done with my order, it starts to feel long. she was very nice and obviously just new but maybe start your newer employees at a less busy hour than 10am. with that being said, the barista making my iced vanilla coffee did a great job. the best one i have had in a while.
bought a venti coffee and it was barely over half full. the coffee was also made wrong. i worked in a very busy starbucks location for over 2 years . it's not hard to make things correctly

starbucks at a turnpike service plaza where people want to get in and out. two people working on a high travel holiday weekend. i counted 17 people in line. all i wanted to do was purchase one of their expensive ,high profit items- a coffee mug. as the line appeared to be over 30 mins long i asked since i wasn't needing a prepared item could i just jump ahead and be rung out. i was told no. i don't get it! lost sale. !!

the service at this starbucks is below poor, waited 15 minutes for my latte and never got an apology for it taking so long. no customer service at all! very disappointed for a starbucks! in addition everyone working behind the counter had major attitudes, not one staff member smiled, nor acknowledged any of the customers. i thought when you gave your name it was to be called so you know when your order is ready. it was more calling out the drinks instead. 3 people ordered the same thing and we were left wondering whose was who. management need to retrain it's staff at this location.

it's a drive through! it's early in the morning, you're on your way to work and you want to beat traffic. here is the perfect place to get that cup of coffee. staff will make you feel like they care about you. service is quick, then you're on your way. a must for teachers traveling from odessa into tampa. you know you need that 5:30 a.m. coffee fix to sweeten up your day! conveniently located right before the entrance to the veteran's expressway.

you would think that starbucks is a starbucks is a starbucks and that since they are a chain, you could pretty much walk into any starbucks and receive a relatively similar experience. that said, i've been to hundreds of starbucks in numerous states and this location is consistently the worst. i've had issues with this starbucks going back to 2007. for years i refused to frequent this particular location even though it was very close to my former office because for some reason their coffee tasted bitter and they frequently messed up my and/or my boss's order. that said, i recently moved temporarily into a rental in the mira loma area as my husband and i go through the home buying process to move back to our neighborhood in damonte and unfortunately this is the only starbucks on my route from my home to the freeway in the morning. i figured that since so many years have passed, certainly they have had ample time to correct any inconsistency issues, but i was oh so wrong. on at least 3 separate occasions in the last few months i either got the wrong coffee or they forgot to put any flavored syrup in the coffee at all, that's fine, i called they fixed it. it didn't really fix my crappy morning without my caffeine fix, but at least they fixed the issue. today i went through the drive through as i do most friday's and lo and behold, once again one of the drinks had no flavored syrup. it was bitter tasting coffee and milk. i tried to call and the first time they answered the phone and then set it down so i could hear the cashier taking orders and the chatter of customers and staff etc. i sat there for a while thinking someone would get on the line, nope, they just wanted to silence the phone, but heaven forbid they speak to the customer who just paid $5 for a cup of crap coffee i could have made better in my own kitchen. i called back twice afterward and they just let the phone ring and ring. from this point on i will either wait until i get to carson city or go out of my way to go to the damonte ranch starbucks who are always consistent. it's not about getting a replacement cup of coffee, i have the funds to pay for another cup of coffee, its the disappointment of waiting in line, paying $5 for a cup of coffee to brighten up my friday mornings and then getting swill and being inconvenienced by having to call and go back. i don't want a replacement, i'd like for your baristas to pay attention to what they are doing and to be consistent. if they have messed up that many times with just my orders, i wonder how many other customers are consistently disappointed by the lack of accuracy and/or poorly made drinks. sorry for ranting, i just wanted to give the back story on why this is so frustrating, but for one location to have continually screwed up for years is just incredibly annoying and i feel that management should be aware that they have the worst location in south reno or any starbucks i've been through throughout the u.s.

the irvington starbucks is my favorite starbucks by far!!! i come in just about everyday and the place is clean and they have amazing staff!!! i come in so much they know my name and they always engage me in conversation. it's a good work environment and the music is always good here!

definitely not the best location. i was thinking with it being so new, maybe that's what kept everyone continuously slow and rude. i decided to just start going inside due to that being much quicker than the drive thru. there are two blonde guys who work there that are amazing and have a great personality, but every woman that i've come across there is just mean. hopefully it picks up and their attitudes improve..but for now, i'm sticking to my coffee and tea at home.

i have had a 50/50 experience at this location, tough to find your first time. the staff are friendly but sometimes it seems they are a person down. drinks are always as expected but slow on occasion. the location is convenient and the centrally located.

this is my go-to starbucks while i am at work. their employees are always friendly and accurately take your order. no matter how long the drive-thru line the employees handle it pretty quickly. i have on occasion tried their pre-made sandwiches. my favorite is the egg salad, the arugula adds a nice depth to this classic. there is outside seating. overall a great location.
since bookstores and their cafes are dissapering, starbucks is the only bastion left to enjoy a cup of coffee and sit relax, read, chat or peruse your pc.

i visited this starbucks on 9-3-2013 and don't think i would recommend it to anyone else. the tables were dirty. the windows hadn't been cleaned and there were dead flies on the window sill. not a good first impression.

traveling through deleware looking for coffee, this location says they're open at 6 am today....not the case. this location opens at 7 am on sunday. would be nice to have an updated hours listing here to rely on. thanks!

i usually don't comment aloud about poor service at restaurants, etc., but after the consistently bad service i've received at this starbucks i couldn't hold it in any longer. too often there is at least a 15-20 minute wait inside the store only to receive a mediocre drink. earlier last week i went inside after seeing 7 or 8 cars in the drive thru. i stood in line behind one person for almost five minutes before one of the 7 people working came to take our orders. after ordering, i waited another 15 minutes to get my drink because the workers were busy socializing with each other. even then, my drink was incorrectly made. today, i wanted starbucks again as i was on my way to work. this is the closest to my place of employment so i left with plenty of time to make sure i would get my drink. i waited in line after four people, and while that part went fast, i had to wait ten minutes to get my drink. i ordered an iced black tea lemonade and was served an iced black tea. i had no time or patience to tell them that once again my drink order was incorrect. in the future i will be avoiding this starbucks at all costs when possible.

in the past i've been dissatisfied by the service and quality of product but on this trip i was shocked to see that, regardless of the cavalier attitude of many patrons, the barrisa was not wearing her face mask. i let her know that since she's preparing food she should be the most cautious of anybody and i walked out without placing an order because i didn't feel it was worth the risk.

where's the flatware? the only "cutlery" this starbucks has is plastic. and they don't have plates. when i asked for one they had to pull a demi from the display case. i broke my plastic fork on the muffin and then spotted my dress shirt when i had to eat the stale breakfast sandwich by hand. is this where the industry leader is taking us? not to be grumpy (i seem to be writing a lot of sub-3 ratings lately) but this starbucks is located in a business hotel. we bleary eyed patrons in $1,000 worth of dress wear are dodging grease dribbles before meeting clients. (thankfully i remembered to blot not rub!)

starbucks is starbucks. always pretty good. i went through the drive-thru, and was not busy too busy unlike what someone said previously about always being busy. i guess it just depends on the time and day you go. i went on sunday around 2:30. i'm not sure what the one-way issue was from the previous review, but everything i saw was 2-ways (in parking lot, the streets) so this place was easy to find and i didn't have a problems going in and out.

slowest starbucks ever! i popped in for a gift card, decided on a coffee to go as well. time stamp on receipt was 2:17p; did not coffee in hand until 2:24p. the inside service was completely in the weeds; drive-thru a priority for service (average time seemed to be 3+ minutes). i recommend drive-thru if you're in a hurry. knew i should have gone to the one in target...waiting sucks.

there was a memo going around today to women at businesses saying "if yvett r. happens to walk into your establishment today, be extremely rude. extra points for creativeness". wasn't there? (see my best buy review for earlier today) we'd been out practically all day and stopped by starbucks before heading home. now, i'm here all the time and i'd never seen this barista. i was in the mood for one of those cap'n crunch crunchberry frappuccinos. now, i know this has been around for years but i'm not daft and fully realize that not every barista will know what this is, since it's not a real menu item. so i don't order it that way. i just ask them for a strawberries & creme frappuccino with a shot of hazelnut and a shot of toffee (get two shots of each if you're ordering a venti). i was ordering in that exact order when the barista looked like she'd been kicked in the gut and interrupted me. "you want hazelnut in the strawberry frap?", she asked. i said yes and also asked for a shot of toffee. again, "mixed in with the strawberries & creme?". i said yes and she stared at me for a while before shrugging and saying, "okaaaayyyyy. . . ". what gives? i mean, it wasn't her drink and isn't the customer always right? if i wanted the drink that way and i'm paying for it and i'm not asking her to taste it, what difference should it make to her? we were waiting for our drinks while my husband was making fun of how i'd been treated by ladies that day when the guy that makes the drinks called out for me. he read out my drink and asked, "oh, you like the cap'n crunch drink?". a-ha! at least he knew i wasn't crazy. take that, judgmental drink lady. yeah, i'm whining about today but i know and you know that i'll be back.

great staff, very efficient!!! everyone has a great attitude and welding smile, no matter how early it is. i love this starbucks. i go to this location and only this one. the manager and assistant managers are very active with there staff and get involved in the customers.
the cashier was so sweet. she offered me a trash bag to put my pillows in and it helped tremendously! great service!

starbucks in the cherry hill mall is very hit or miss. they're either empty or the line is so long it extends out of the entrance to the macy's it's located in. due to the stand's small size, there are probably 3-4 employees working at the maximum. but even with 2 on the register and 2 behind the counter it's not enough to accommodate the business of this location. also, the space where customers wait to order and wait to pick up their order is cramped. it's not uncommon for people to resort to standing outside of the macy's and try to listen carefully if their name is called, rather than elbowing strangers in the ribcages just to wait for a frappuccino. the drink taste like any other starbucks. the baked goods are usually fully stacked and the employees are very helpful and nice. i give them props for being able to serve their coffee with a smile given the factors that make this location inefficient. i believe the mall would benefit greatly if they simply opened up a large independent starbucks store within the mall.

i really do like this starbucks, and it's conveniently located close to home, but.. it is very busy and that can lead to mishaps with coffee orders and some spillage with unfastened lids. check your order and ensure that the lid is fastened when you pickup!
great coffee expensive prices not such a great place to sit around not as inviting as a stand-alone starbucks

the best bucks. hands down. people only get mad because they are terrible at time management, and can't tell themselves no when they are already late, and then blame it on the minimum wage earning baristas, because the thought of being even remotely in the wrong upends their entire worldview and goes against everything that mommy and daddy told them about how special and important they are. just be nice people. dang.
i am used to waiting at starbucks, but this was an exceptional wait, to get the wrong order, from a rude staff. next time will try the coffee place across the street.
erica at the front needs a huge attitude adjustment just because you hate your job don't take it out on us we love starbucks and come to ours every single day!
ordered online but the workers were super unhelpful and basically ignored me. supremely rude staff. looked at me like i was an alien for ordering an extra drink after passing past drive thru.

knowing how hard it is to find the the you are here city edition mugs i was glad to stop by this starbucks store when we were passing tn and thus adding nashville to my collection! the service was good!

this is my favorite starbucks in town! the staff are super friendly and every time i come and sit down and relax the people around me are always so friendly and the vibe is just awesome. sometimes it can be slow, but what do you expect when there are so many people trying to get coffee in a drive thru at once? love it here :)
not my favorite starbucks location.. the drinks taste different depending on where you go i find. very nice as always!

i don't love starbucks coffee. but i do like this starbucks. the staff are friendly and kind, the patio is great with some refreshing misters, and the interior is clean. when i need a quick espresso on ice, i will pop in here. this was one of the first places i stopped when we came house hunting in tucson, and emma (hey girl!) made me feel welcome and at ease. she always remembers me and says hello, which is awesome in such a busy place. they also have a drive thru, but i always go in for some extra steps.

i am a lover of everything starbucks! however, the biggest downfall of this location is the fact that everyone goes there! i used to attempt stopping at this starbucks in the morning on the way to work and it was a terrible idea. i would challenge myself to get up earlier so i didn't have to wait in such a long line.... still a fail! the worst part is the setup of the parking lot. instead of having one clear cut line, people approach the line from different directions so it seems like you may be getting the short end of the stick (depending on which side you line up on). i have also tried to hop out of my car and go inside.... ironically, it took me longer inside than it would have taken me in the line. nothing against the service but because this is at such a busy intersection, it is never a quick stop!

basic starbucks. i only came to get some quick work done while i was back in my hometown. it's a little bit on the small side and adjacent to a chase bank. there isn't too much seating for work and very few comfortable seating options. because it's located in the parkside mall plaza you get less of an working environment and teenagers or pre-teens coming in throughout the day. avoid if you're looking for a quieter location. spent a few hours here and the bathroom, which was a bit dirty, was nevre cleaned. they could definitely check up on that more often. otherwise baristas are nice and fixed my drink when they messed up my order.

great starbucks. decent size with some seating. right in the heart of downtown by macy's and city hall. they made a great cinnamon dolce latte, but my favorite thing is that they said my name right when they said my coffee was ready! for someone with a unique name i never wait for my name to be said correctly! i was so excited! employees were super nice.

i've been to this starbucks on a few occasions. the time of the day, determines how crowded the place will be. today i went early, expecting a crowd. my expectations were met. the store is very small, so when it is crowded it can be a bit uncomfortable in there. the wait in line wasn't very long this morning. i ordered a venti carmel macchiato with an extra shot of espresso. when it came time to wait for my drink, the line was long. someone before me ordered a bunch of drinks. by the time the barista had my drink ready i hurried out of the store. my macchiato was delicious, however i noticed on the cup my extra shot of espresso was missing. no big deal. as i mentioned, i've been to this location before and they just can't seem to get my order right. overall it was a nice start to my day, minus the missing espresso shot. i will go back if in the area and in need of starbucks, ill just make sure to pay attention to the barista.
really charming location. love the outdoor seating area and people watching in west park village. my drinks have been as expected. mainly the outdoor seating and area is the draw for me.

no reason not to give 5 stars but they didn't have grilled cheese. otherwise, nice people and apologetic. and people, i always love when starbucks has a drive-thru. especially if it's connected to a bank.
it took so long for them to make my tea, a grande tea, that i signed up and made an account on yelp just to give them a bad review before i even got my drink :))

this starbucks is small but the staff are so friendly and the location is always clean. this starbucks has a far busier drive through so if you are looking for a place to sit down and chat, this is a pretty good option. the location is convenient as it is along one of the main roads that enters and exits the shopping center and i'm definitely a fan of their drinks.
the drive thru is ridiculously slow here. 4 cars in line and over 20 minutes on an afternoon-not the busiest of times. they also don't have the nitro cold brews at this location.
first review! the place opened today and looks great. it's about time we had a coffee shop in the neighborhood.

i spend plenty of time here but the service varies on the time of the day. morning hustle is always extremely busy and sometimes slow. afternoon is pretty fast but most people working not in the best moods. i can't complain about the coffee always deliciously made. the spicy chorizo is probably my favorite and my caramel macchiato i wouldn't change for the world. given that it is a drive thru ...i will say i spend way to much time in the in the morning and inside is no better.

this location is amazing the employees are the best of the best top notch they strive to do their best at all times when they are unable it is someone had an emergency or they are low on the things they need to prepare. our orders, please don't take it out on them. they truly try to do their best. they work back to back shifts sometimes as well please don't make cry like a person i know did love and live our best lives now

i always have to order my drink extra hot here. when i do ask for that, my drink is as hot as it is normally at other starbucks. they also feel the need to correct you on the sizes you order. i want a small. other places do that as well, but when they do it at this one, its almost as if they are saying it like they are better than you. i order a mocha with soy milk because i am allergic to milk. at this location only, i can hardly taste anything but soy milk. this is a place where i will stop by, but only if in a hurry or not passing by another coffee shop.

[out-of-towner review] thank you and you suck...are how i feel! given that you are in a convention center, you'd think although it was the weekend, you'd have better hours of operation. conventions run 7 days a week. my convention happened to run sunday - tuesday. so opening day (sunday), everyone was jonsing for coffee all day, but after 2pm you were screwed! it was closed...you could endure the gross humidity and/or pouring rain and walk a few blocks to the marriott starbucks, but theirs closes early too! annoyed...to say the least... but, for the two remaining days was their saving grace. although the line was continuously 40+ people deep, these automons kept it movin'! no lie, they were quick and efficient for a very small set-up. they weren't the friendliest, but they were damn good and efficient with their time...you can't complain about that... if i walk the length of this convention center, you better reward me with a caffeinated beverage. your wimpy hours just won't cut it for all the various people who saunter through here...step up your game, but keep up the good work! it's a catch 22, sorry guys! keepin' convention'er's caffeinated on their timetable...

if you want to find an exceptional starbucks in your area, look no further than this one. the baristas provide excellent customer service. rachael exceptionally stood out and went above and beyond to make sure i got my order right and quickly. there's definitely a certain care that goes on in this place that you can't find at the other locations.

i love this starbucks. they're consistent and fast. the girls (and i say that because i've never seen a guy working) are the sweetest, super accommodating, and go out of their way to take special requests. this is a super busy location during the day during the week, so i recommend mobile order. i've never done their drive thru so can't speak to that.

hillbilly food critic review: location: 1/5 atmosphere: 4/5 food: 3/5 price: 2/5 here is the deal, i've been here several times, and had to leave the line to catch a plane. it is always understaffed. people and employees are grouchy because management doesn't staff it properly. today, the line (again) loops around the restaurant and out the door. poor management.

this starbucks is excellent for evening study sessions it's usually pretty quiet and the baristas quickly learn your name and drink preferences. they are quite friendly even if you aren't a 'regular'. this starbucks has pleasant but not overwhelming volume level for their music. i cannot comment on the service at morning rush times as i'm strictly an evening starbucks customer. the wifi has yet to give me any problems. over all good place to grab a cup of caffeine and crack open a book. parking can be a bit of a challenge as the lot has perhaps 10 spots in it and a drive thru lane between the parking spots and the store. street parking is ample on north & south as well as delmar.
they have updated and now accept the app. staff takes their time to get to know you. drinks are always spot on. see you soon...
18 minutes for an iced coffee? i was the only customer waiting for a long while? and then they screwed that up too? come on, folks. wake up!

an incredible staff at all hours. they are to a man and woman friendly,personable,efficient ,and professional. they remember your name and favorite drink and deliver it quickly. if you miss a day they are genuinely concerned that everything is ok. coming to this store each day is a great experience. highly recommended.

i love starbucks for how they let you customize your drinks to fit your diet. i ordered a custom drink this morning, and as i was pulling up to the window, i overheard one of the baristas as she was making fun of what i ordered and mocking me. i literally never write reviews, but it was incredibly uncalled for and unprofessional. thanks for ruining my friday morning coffee experience.

i love starbucks and this is two miles from my home, but the parking has become impossible. last two times, i've been--no parking. drove across the street to an independent, equally good coffee shop. i want to be a loyal customer, but this location is making it hard. don't do business coffee here anymore either. no quiet tables for two. the staff here is great. the space is clean. but it needs a bigger space and parking lot.

great location (meaning it's right downtown st. pete) and really have to love almost any starbucks! the employees are really nice and does a good job. however it can be hard to find a parking and this starbucks is pretty crowded at most times (downtown, huh). there are only few nice tables and they are taken for hours by people who sip on a drink and study on their computers. so it's a no no for finding a nice table, but yes yes for getting my morning coffee... until i get my own starbucks machine on black friday sale for $59. yeeeey:)

very busy mall location. service people are great, and they do manage to get the customers in and out as quickly as possible. i've stood in line outside in the hall several times. good thing i'm a coffee junkie :-)

make sure you order your coffee drinks non-stop or to-go otherwise they'll send security to ask you to leave or order more from the workers who spend their time talking about their teenage personal lives out loud and cussing non-stop. either that or they just judge people based off of their appearance. either way, not the usual starbucks customer service found elsewhere. also, they charge for refills regardless of your rewards status. just go elsewhere!!!!!
very slow. went to the front of the line about 20 minutes ago and still haven't gotten my drink. baristas making 1 drink every 3 minutes.

i have been going to this starbucks for years and it used to be my favorite spot. over the past year i've witnessed the manager insult and degrade her employees on several occasions to the point where i've almost said something. she isn't friendly to her customers nor was she at all helpful when i had my my computer cord stolen there a week ago. the rest of the staff is fabulous but she's honestly made me change locations and take my money elsewhere.

meh, parking nightmare. there are better locations but honestly, i'm getting a little tired of starbucks in general. anybody tried dutch bros? they're awesome. better customer service (and not because starbucks customer service is bad, but just because dutch bros. is that good) and the best rewards program. starbucks should, at $4 a cup, have a better rewards program for frequent drinkers. i will say i like the ability to sit in the seating area with a laptop so i can hopefully someday be the target for a piece in the onion. i'm sorry, this review sounds so negative. starbucks isn't bad and this location is not to blame for all of starbucks' issues. there are just better, less chain-y options out there.

this is by far my favorite starbucks. i frequent here every morning, and the staff is just an absolute pleasure to chat with each day. they have it down to a science. from knowing the orders of regulars to ensuring there's always coffee brewing, this starbucks is a well oiled machine! if every company had associates such as these, we wouldn't need customer complaint lines! ms deb is the best!!!

i'm not a starbucks fan, but if you're in the willow grove mall looking for coffee, this it. baristas were very efficiently working their way through a fairly long line. place is clean, and it's in the "food court" with convenient seating, pleasant lighting under skylights, clean seating area. very strong dark roast, almost with a burnt flavor. i don't drink it, but shockingly they were out of the cold brew, which must be a bestseller at a place like this.
this is a really nice starbucks. the wifi is strong and there is ample comfortable seating. i like to wait here while my car is in for service at the firestone across the parking lot.

comfortable leather (looking) chairs. it made a good stop for an after movie drink and snack. the croissant was not very good, especially after having a excellent one a couple days before. the oatmeal raisin cookie was pretty good. i am not a starbucks acolyte, and it always seems strange that everyone around me seems to be in this highly efficient and exact routine when i go to a starbucks. they have tazo tea, and the chai wasn't bad.

about a month ago i went i side to purchase a ice coffee. i asked for milk . the girl didn't give me my ice coffee with milk in it and when i saw it i reminded her how i wanted it and she said rudely " there's creamer in the corner " dumb girl. thankfully there was a nicer girl there and i asked her to fix it and she did!

slow, always out of stuff, poor customer service, not clean, not typical starbucks workers. i've never seen a starbucks who always gets the drink wrong. its always too much of this or not enough of that. the location just sucks! taylor is delightful and a pleasure to order from. very well spoken and pays attention to detail!

this starbucks is in a hopping spot right in the middle of the philadelphia premium outlets. so be prepared to wait in line for a while. they do a pretty good job of keeping things moving but it is crowded. they do have some outside seating which is nice. all in all its your average starbucks.
