starbucks never disappoints! love their hot or cold green tea or iced chai tea. their breakfast sandwiches are always good too. not greasy and flavorful. my daughter enjoys their smoothies and rice crispy treats. i also love their chocolate chip cookies--they are huge and they'll even warm them up if you like. baristas are always friendly and quick service. the cafe is clean with seating available. this one is convenient to stop in after a movie, especially on a day like today where it's rainy. they have drive thru also available. if you love starbucks like i do, i highly recommend joining their rewards program and linking a starbucks card to pay via your phone. didn't take me long to make gold status and maintaining that status with all the promotions they have. you also get free rewards after you gain stars as well as a birthday freebie.

update- they still are awful im not sure what progress can ever be made!!! i keep hearing horror stories- its a shame because the district manager is so sweet!!! sadly this store might just need a complete turn over

worst starbucks ever. was driving up to pismo beach for a wedding a few weekends ago. decided to stop at this starbucks since it was so convenient to the 101. big mistake. the ordering system and baristas were overwhelmed with a long line of customers waiting to be served, combined with a ton of to-go orders for people who used the mobile app. our order got lost in the shuffle. after about 10 minutes i asked the barista if our order was in the works. without looking, he responded "it will be ready soon..." i asked again 5 minutes later -- same answer, but still no mochas. after we waited a total of 20 minutes for our two mochas, i asked the barista (who was completely overwhelmed the whole time) what happened to our mochas. he said he didn't know, there was no order for them. he then went ahead and made our drinks for us even though he couldn't find our order. finally, 25 minutes after we placed our order, we got our mochas. we're never, ever going to this starbucks again.

i never was a starbucks groupie.. people have sworn on sb forever and i'm justlike "blah its only coffee" or "who pays $5 for juice?"....i could never justify the prices until....recently. i am now a groupie! i don't have a desk at home so i was kinda looking around for a place to use some wifi and get some work done at a table. rode past this sb and parked in a pretty small parking lot. soft music playing, people ordering their $5 teas and coffee(insert sarcasm here lol)some open tables and just a pretty chill atmosphere. i felt slightly intimidated because i had no clue how to order or even what to order. so i ordered some guava green tea. guy asks me what size and of course i gave him that bewildered, blank look that i'm sure he's used to. he was great though! explained the sizes and how the drink was made. the tea was $4 and took about 6 or 7 minutes to make. i grabbed a table(the chairs are not comfortable at all) and gave it a taste.... i'm pissed nobody made me try starbucks yall! all this time i was drinking crap from dd and always angry. lmaoooooo! my tea was absolutely amazing and i wanted another. next time. next time.
my favorite neighborhood starbucks! they know my drinks and give my foster dog a puppachino, is there anything better? :)

i just moved to the area and today was my 2nd day (sat & sun) going to this starbucks. both days the workers seemed frazzled, with very little direction. some were just standing waiting for direction while the counters were covered with major spills and a mess. others were running around nearly missing each other with beverages in their hands. there is zero communication, especially when they messed up my order and the girl who was remaking was told to go on break and just left in the middle making my coffee and it got thrown out because the other girl didn't know what it was. funny thing is she came back and tried to speak to girl 2 but was cut off and told to just go take her break. good thing i was watching and was able to let the 2nd girl know that she threw out my drink. each day i had to wait for 10 min for my food and drink order. there are spills all over the place and today it just seemed like a bunch of teenagers running the place with no management. good thing there is another starbucks near work because i would not be able to come to this one during the work week.
staff very nice. smile, friendly, and very customer service driven. place is a little dirty most of the time...but beyond that...i will go again for sure!!

yelp keeps suggesting that i review starbucks, so here goes... it's starbucks...there's nothing that i can say that hasn't been said already. except for maybe this: i despise coffee. as my mom used to say, it's an insult to the palate and an abomination to the stomach. so, much to my surprise, they actually have a pretty decent tea selection. not that i'm an expert...i recently had to take up tea drinking after voluntarily giving up mountain dew after a 20 year bender. every once in a while, i have a friend who wants to meet at starbucks and i grit my teeth and acquiesce. i usually order the plain black tea. and, to my astonishment, it's pretty freakin' good! better than the lipton that i get out of the keurig, which isn't half bad either. the other day, i decided to live on the edge and order the chai tea, black. holy s***...what have i been missing? luckily the starbucks chai tea k-cups are available on amazon for a reasonable price. back to starbucks at the park place mall...the employees are super friendly. i'm literally the last person on the bandwagon, i know, but they really seem to go above and beyond. so yeah, go there...like you don't already know.

this starbucks isn't the usual starbucks. they have very few selection. the smoothies was extremely great! my bf ordered an ice coffee something and it was too watered down. i wouldn't recommend this place unless you need something badly.
love starbucks! venti passion tea lemonade with raspberry.....so so good! the baristas here are super nice & know what they're doing.

pissed! i went in today to get a caramel apple spice and a double bacon breakfast sandwich. they were out of the sandwich as it seems they always are, and i received the wrong drink! i got a apple crisp macchiato, and even that had zero apple flavor! i didn't notice until i got home relaxed and ready to enjoy my breakfast. it was a total disappointment.

i have been a starbucks customer for years and this starbucks in centerton square is closest to my work so i go there 3 times a week at a minimum. i have been increasingly disgruntled with this location. the layout stinks, the staff are fast but they seemed to have lost their friendlier baristas, and i hate that i have to ask for milk for my coffee because they only put out half in half...i know that's corporate but when i go to the starbucks on main street they are so much cooler about it. going to avoid the centerton location as much as possible from now on.

i've never had an issue with an location. it's naive to think that a company would embody the hatred and stereotypical nature of many insecure people we share the world with. the locations i visit are polite and want to know my name. also, if you frequent a location, they already know who you are. every company has room for improvement so, i will leave some space here. happy workers create happy customers.

madison b is certainly a sight to see. she rang me up as happy as can be. the way she whipped up my frapp was the bee's knees. no better barista than she. that's my official decree. come here you won't be disappointed guaranteed. madison if you're reading this, my number is 215-237-8433

i ordered ahead at 11:03 am and my drink wasn't ready until 11:37 am. on top of that, i asked for syrup and there is none there. the employees seemed to be fine, but the manager was not prioritizing orders. walk in orders were done immediately and mobile orders were not. poor management, not coming back here again.
one of the slower starbucks i've been to. this location is run by hmshost which is not known for service, speed or efficiency. prepare to spend about twenty minutes for basic beverages.

i feel like such a snot complaining about a starbucks. the staff here is great. my issue here is the guests. there are a couple people that live here. so if you were planning on sitting in one of the cozy chairs think again. they've basically claimed them as their own. take a close look and you can see their ass prints in each seat. seriously. and on a nice afternoon there's usually 5-10 too many highschoolers hanging around. they tend to sit outside around the cold stone next door which is a little bit of a relief. where do these kids get money for starbucks everyday? i was counting change to share a $1 slice at the local pizzeria! but otherwise, drinks made right- every time- also friendly and quick drive-thru.
reiterating my last review...the people are nice, but mobile ordering has essentially ruined this location. no point in going unless you can order and pay ahead.

i hate that this is the only starbucks close to my work because it has the absolute worst customer service of any starbucks i've ever been to. i always order ahead and do a mobile order so that i can come in and grab my drink and go but there have been multiple times where i have to wait 5 minutes for my drink because the dumb ass teenagers who work there are too busy standing at the register doing nothing. once i came in and there were a few people standing by the window where you receive your drinks, so i asked the girl at the register- with short blond hair (who was doing absolutely nothing at the time) if she could hand me my mobile order and she told me to wait at the window with the other people. there was a guy making multiple drinks, so i stood there and waited five minutes and he was too busy to hand me my drink, so i went back to the girl with short blond hair and asked her to hand me my drink. she handed it to me and then proceeded to chastise me about how busy they were. today i went in and asked for my order and the guy handed me my order then ran outside. it was the wrong order, it was for someone else with the same name. i told the girl who was wiping down counters that the guy handed me the wrong order and she said, "oh no." then proceeded to ignore me and continue wiping down the counter. i had to flag the cashier down who was busy taking orders at the register and ask her to hand me my drink. these are only two examples. there are more but this review is already too long.

this starbucks is clean and staff is really friendly. i came here with my sister around 330pm on a weekday and it was a little busy but they did not rush us. our drinks tasted awesome and cant wait to come back.

so this one takes the cake. out of hundreds of starbucks i've gone to over the years, this one is by far the worst service i've ever had. from the "wach yo name?", to the "we don't got the ingredients for that drink (in such a rude way), to them messing up my order, denying it, and refusing to help make my order correct until i get back in line to wait another 15 minutes. you should be ashamed at speaking to other human beings like that, and the higher ups at starbucks should know not to hire people like this.

consistently good drinks and one of the best in the area with getting mobile orders out on time. if you're complaining about this location you haven't been to blue bell or springhouse, clearly, not even close to these baristas!

love starbucks and this location. everyone is really nice and i love sitting here for hours studying. my only gripe is the drive thru. it's completely hidden so you can't see the line before you pull in. once you're in, even before you order, you're stuck, which is annoying when you're running late. it needs to be redesigned to allow people to get in and out in the event of an emergency or if you charge your mind, especially since there's usually a long wait. but overall, love love love my local starbucks!

do not waste your time here! and believe me, you will waste a lot of time. this place is a disaster and an embarrassment to the starbucks name. aside from the terrible location that other reviews have mentioned, the low level of service and attitudes of staff who simply could care less, defy explanation. after a long break, i went back early on a saturday. the drive thru circled around the building as usual so i went in. there were at least 20 people waiting for their orders, only one person at the register, and one person who may or may not have been a manager, who did not look up or get off her cell phone the entire time i was there (approx 30 minutes). the staff moved at a snail's pace. there was a steady line of customers asking about their orders or with incorrect orders that had to be remade which only slowed things down more. the young man at the register was more concerned about making sure his hair was in place. i did not appreciate watching him run his hands through his hair multiple times and then handle pastry orders for customers. i made sure he was not the who touched my food or i would have definitely said something. apparently there are plenty of people who don't mind spending their money here but i will not be one of them.

this was the fastest starbucks i have ever been to. i ordered a pink drink and it was prepared in under 5 minutes. the consistency was great and it was mixed well. the people working were very nice and made sure that they got my order correct. my friends frappuccino also took a very short time to make. they have lots of seating and it's a great place to study or get some work done.

i have been a loyal customer of starbucks for a few years, and over the past year alone i visited starbucks at least 4x every week. i have always been satisfied with my order and the amount of time it took to prepare, however, i am extremely disappointed with their service in the course of the past few months. the store hours have been decimated so much that it is almost impossible to get in there once a week. i have made many phone calls asking for the new store hours but i get different answer every time. the website has not been updated and myself as well as a few others have tried to be flexible and come by when we can, but it is almost always closed. i'm not sure if this is due to short staffing or covid, but you are losing customers and money. please take into consideration everything i have said. thank you.
if you plan to eat/drink inside be prepared to fight/swat at bugs the entire time. gnats and flies everywhere ..it's gross

i absolutely love this starbucks compared to others in the area. its much newer, the crowd is good, wifi is usually running on decent speed and the barista make a pretty design on your latte if you ask them lol! i just love the outside seating for nice sunny days.
quick service, clean interior, and plenty of outdoor seating with many tables and outlets for chargers! great place to do work on a nice day!

i enjoy studying at this starbucks since there are more seats here than at the walnut/34th st. location just a couple of blocks away right across from the upenn campus. the baristas are generally pleasant, not any more or any less friendly than those at other coffee shops. immediately to the left of the main entrance is a section with couches, which are ideal for sitting in while reading a book or relaxing while savoring a cool beverage on a friday evening. the place is open until 11 p.m., which is nice. i live about a 20-minute walk away, but i often come here with my laptop since it is less noisy than other places. this goes for any starbucks, but i recommend getting the following: 1) coffee frappucino (this comes without whipped cream) since it is less saccharine than other frappucinos and it probably has the highest caffeine content. 2) passion tea lemonade (sweetened) - this cool beverage is perfect for the dog days of summer. i get this either in the grande or venti size. don't take too long to sip it or the ice will melt and dilute the syrup. the tall-sized house coffee is just okay and rather bitter in my opinion. if you take your coffee with cream and sugar, it might be tastier since the coffee is so strong and bitter. there is a difference between bitter and dark coffee, however, as can be noted when one compares the house coffee at starbucks with peet's coffee blend. the latter is dark and aromatic without giving a bitter after taste. unfortunately, it's difficult to get access to peet's coffee here. another perk: the place has restrooms which are clean (and open to the public). the music is also enjoyable - it's usually a mix of bossa nova or indie (wilco, emo music, etc.)

i've been coming to this starbucks for quite some time, maybe a year if not a little more and it is the best one around! the staff is always friendly and full of conversation, most of the time i just have to walk in and they're already typing my order out without me having to say a word!
right downtown! cute location: inside & outdoor seating. again, it's starbucks....quality & consistency of product were right on. baristas were friendly & this was a clean location!

it was the iced honey oatmilk latte for me! my friend had recommended i try it and it sounded right up my alley so i headed to starbs the next day to try it out. i was extremely excited that oat milk had been added to the menu! it's sooooo delicious - definitely my go-to drink now - i'm obsessed. my mom was craving a sausage cheese biscuit - so i suggested she try the sausage cheddar muffin. she is extremely picky about her food and when i say she gobbled that muffin down! i think she might have found her new go-to too. and the customer service was top tier! i will definitely be back - specifically to this location. if you don't get anything else out of this review - get the honey oat milk latte and thank me later!

writing this on minute 12 as i sit in the drive through line. for some reason i haven't learned my lesson the other times i've come to this location and had the exact same slow service. officially switching to the one by work. peace!

although i do not drink starbucks often (way too pricey for me), i do like it here. the customer service is top notch! i noticed that they really try to get to know each and every customer there. they are huge on teamwork back there. when one person gets extremely busy, i noticed that they don't even need to ask for help. other team members just step up and do their part. they tend to always butcher my name, but i know they are trying. :-) maybe if i came more often than once ever 2-3 weeks, they would get it right. plus, i give them a thumbs up for knowing so many people's names since the place is always packed to its max.

worst drink i have ever received from a starbucks!!! all i ordered was a hot chocolate (simple) it tasted horrible so i asked for another one and again... horrible!! love wasting my money, thanks bad barista!

honestly this starbucks is so awesome. confused at the bad reviews because i've never had issues. frankly, the drive thru service has been nothing but great. the person taking my order is always so sweet. my cappuccinos are always perfect.

this was a new location that opened up on mlk. which is so close to my job. i am so excited. the only complaint i have (and this is with all the starbucks) is that they have to find the locations with this smallest lot possible. there is no room for anything. however, i will continue to maneuver around these tiny lots to get my starbucks fix.

i love starbucks! however i had a bad experience the last time i was there :,(. i always come in to do homework in the peaceful&quiet environment! although the curly haired freak, the ugly makeup girl, and the budget zoolander kept banging their pitchers, the zoolander dude kept screaming "cheddar" was it necessary? no it was not

it's 3pm in the afternoon and the young moron working the counter tells me, they have ran out of freshly brewed coffee. there is seven people working and they are all dimwits! just think this must the only starbucks in the world that doesn't have any freshly brewed coffee in the middle of the day! unbelievable !

i have been to over 100 different starbucks all over the midwest and south and this is the only one that consistently gets my order wrong.. i,get an iced white chocolate mocha with caramel drizzle, not a hard drink to make, and yet they never get it right.. it is either way too sweet like they added extra sugar or it is soo bitter you can't drink it.. just wish this store was as good as all the other ones i go to in my travels.

good morning yelpers. nothing too long today. only that this location is super busy from about 7:45am to 8:30 so if you have somewhere to be at a certain time get there early or use the mobile application. otherwise the customer service is. great. despite he busyness they still make your coffee with love and care. thank you starbucks staff

i never met a starbuck's that i didn't like. until this one. they were shorted staffed the morning that we visiting. apparently, an employee had wandered off, leaving the other to fend for herself. service was decent considering. our beverages were good, but seriously, i was almost no longer in the mood. that's how long it took to get them.
as far as starbucks go, this one is occasionally my favorite because: 1. they make coffee 2. they are open 24 hours repeat...

the staff is usually friendly & helpful - i had the unfortunate situation to have to deal with a woman named yonnie - extremely rude & unprofessional! stay clear of her unless you want your day ruined.
love starbucks drinks but i will never go to this location again. waited 45 mins in the drive thru only to get the wrong size.

may possibly the worst starbucks i've ever visited. incredibly slow service, typical new orleans attitudes, and always out of product. out of all of my weekly trips out of msy, they had exactly what i order one time. do yourself a favor and get coffee before you fly out.

this starbucks is aways crowded, which isn't neccessarily a bad thing-it is the only starbuck around. i suggest not going through the drive-thru because there is always a long line. just step inside and you will be served much quicker and you can actually meet the people who are making your coffee.

i come here most mornings to get my bacon and gouda sandwich and an iced coffee. this morning i stopped intending to get the same order and the barista told me that the "ovens were down". okay, fine. so i just got my coffee. on my 40 minute drive to work, i pick my iced coffee up from my cup holder and it spills all over my car because she put the wrong size lid on my venti coffee. not having a good morning and now i can't even drink my damn coffee.

griffin quay is by far the best employee there. he is so kind and made my experience beyond enjoyable. even with a big rush, he made sure that every customer was catered to and taken care of. he was so sweet and you could tell he loved his job. no surprise, he got employee of the quarter- he rocks! hope this starbucks gives him a raise because he is running the show! such a great barista, if you ever go here make sure you get your drink made by griffin. he makes it right every single time, best barista ever!

we went here today mostly for the coolness of having a starbucks at the zoo and having a chance to try out the paper straws. all was good! this starbucks functions like any other starbucks, including the breakfast sandwich options, but they serve iced coffee with a paper straw. i've never used a paper straw before today but i'm happy to report it worked just like a plastic one. definitely recommend this starbucks.

starbucks are hard to find in this area. i have to pick a direction and basically go out of my way 30minutes to get to a starbucks. this is the 3rd time in a row now i've had horrible service plus my drink was wrong. i won't be back to this location. i'm not sure what's happened recently to your customer service but your employees seem very inconveniences with having to do their job. i say job bc my requests are basic uncomplicated drinks usually only one drink.

worst customer service ever! good luck getting a consistent drink or even a smile, the manager with the long blonde braids is the worst offender and talked smack about me while i was still standing in front of her. take your money else where!!!
excellent location, clean and app orders are ready promptly. only suggestion would be more consistency in their brewing.

recently waited over 10 minutes for a drink while walking in. rather disappointing. i drove through the next day for an order and the person working the register looked at my twice but didn't open the window until finally the third time. she asked what i ordered and said it wasn't on the screen. she then yelled to one of her coworkers "you didn't input his order!" honestly better off driving to a different starbucks location because disappointing service seems to be the constant.
ahh much better (unlike the 39th street). swift, efficient, clean. the youthberry granita is delicious!

starbucks is a much needed addition to this part of delaware county. it's hopping and with a drive thru too! today is bustling because it's a cheer party. workers are friendly and engaged with the customers.

this is my daily go to coffee spot, the staff are friendly and fast. every starbucks is basically the same, this one is no worse than any other. except the location is ideal for me and the many downtown office workers. the bathroom can only be opened by asking at the cash register, this is due to the central downtown location.

[465] this busy corner starbucks always prepares to order. it's hard to get in /out due to the super busy parking lot. no drive thru. they really need double the size lot. we go when it's not busy .. i'll definitely be back.

this is probably my favorite starbucks in the tucson area. the staff is awesome, the service is fast and the seating is the best. lots of space for studying and relaxing (i never feel cramped unlike other starbucks in the area). i would totally recommend this starbucks!

severely understaffed on many occasions - i waited over 30 minutes for my frap and this is after placing my order. isn't the purpose for getting coffee, to get a daily boost not a daily depressant. good place to go if you're waiting on your wife to shop and have hours and hours to kill. otherwise please avoid. goto the food court instead for a java boost.

the cashier was so nice! we wanted a hot chocolate and the girl literally mentioned every single line they have and could do! it was nice and sincere, something you don't always get when ordering such a simple drink.

this starbucks is soooooo disorganized. the staff behind the counter talk to each other instead of making drinks and leave you waiting at the pick up counter with your foot tapping. i asked what teas i could choose from and the girl just said we have green tea that's all i know. i asked for earl gray and i was the only one customer waiting for drink besides the drive thru line which had 2 cars.... needless to say, i waited for an uncomfortable amount of time to the point where other folks walked in, ordered and got their drink before me. i just asked for tea!! they definitely forgot my order. not cool! i had to grab the girl's attention to ask where my tea was. i said, "do you know how much longer on the earl gray?" and she says, "no" and i wanted to say, "well then who should i ask to get my tea?" but i held my tongue. then i see her drop a random tea bag into a cup and quickly fill with water and push towards me. i thought they typically sweeten with some kind of syrup but i guess not for me or not today. i seriously think they need to hire and train folks not to be so disorganized even when things are slow. just tried the tea and wow it is bland and tastes all wrong. i looked at the tag and it says "english breakfast" so i'm definitely not happy with the service but i already drove away bc i was so ready to get out of there. next time, get the order right and don't forget customers.

thank you, thank you, thank you. i can always rely on this starbucks to serve me well. the staff is wonderful--always smiling and helpful, despite the length of the line. they understand the importance of a properly-made drink, serve it with a smile, and are patient with you when ordering and your brain freezes (duh--havent had my coffee yet). it is much better than the starbucks one block over on walnut (and less crowded with penn students). fun fact: when i was a student at drexel some years ago, rumor had it that reese witherspoon and jake gyllenhaal hung out here while she was filming a movie a few blocks away.

so we came in for a slice of chocolate loaf and a latte .... instead of sitting down to a delicious piece of chocolate-loaf...we get a hard stale piece of cake .... as i return to the counter i see the display piece of chocolate cake is missing .. and i ask the employee "did you take this out of the display case... and she replies " yes but i touched it to make sure it was ok.... of course i flip out .... the sign clearly says inside the case that those things are for display purposes only. those things can be in there for days at a time. and she says to me this one was not that old .... i respond well those sandwiches in there look like they have been in there for weeks so i'm sure they are bacteria ridden and could certainly cross contaminate ... i had my $3.16 returned but how absolutely gross and then, i see the other young lady eating in the coffee station ... literally hand to mouth... and then without washing her hands she starts making someone's coffee order. :(

easy access, getting in and back out onto the highway. my drive thru experience today was swift, courteous, and on point. when my drink was served, i was warned they had accidentally made it "extra hot" and i realized that i had never even thought to order a drink extra hot. i'll keep that in mind the next time i'm ordering a coffee to deliver to someone else.
all i wanted was to birthday cake pops at 5 o'clock on a sunday and they did not have that. they did not have any cake pops to make it worse.

super friendly staff. always nice. i even spilt a little coffee and had to go in to get napkins and i mentioned it, they came out a few min later with a fresh coffee! very nice and considerate. iv been here often, great clean place

i highly recommend that a system be established so that the bathrooms can only be used by paying customers. sitting in the back room i saw a steady stream of non-paying customers creating a line that at time was 10 people long. as a paying customer, when i then needed to use the restroom there was a longer wait than there should have been. thanks.

went with a group of friends during a monday around 4pm. one friend ordered an iced caramel macchiato and there was no extra caramel and the drink was already mixed (the milk wasn't separated like it usually is when served). the same thing happened with another girl who ordered a similar drink. another friend had no caramel drizzle on the top of her drink, even though it was on her receipt. when i ordered the girl informed me that they were out of grande cups. she basically led me to believe that they couldn't just make grande drink in a venti cup, so i had to get a tall. when i got it the cup was only 3/4 of the way full and half of the drink was foam. i couldn't even get to the actual liquid. definitely do not go to this location. i give 2 stars

i thought starbucks had a strict non-smoking policy. it does not. when you arrive at this starbucks on a typical sunday morning, you will be met by 5-10 motorcycles impeding your entry to the cafe, first and foremost. then, if you enjoy sitting outside and taking in a bit of fresh air with your latte, this is impossible. starbucks allows smoking of cigars and cigarettes on their property. this goes on for several hours. every time the door opens, all of this second-hand smoke and worse, rushes in through their front door. so funny to see the "no smoking" signs, as they are completely ignored by management and entirely not enforced. no need to patronize this location, as you will stink like smoked tobacco when you leave.

i am trying to give a zero stars to this location. very very disappointed. we ordered a double espresso, a teavana, a chocolate brownie and a turkey basil pesto...almost not turkey at all inside the sandwich and the total was about $ 18.00. beside that the table was very dirty. not sure what's going on with this location but it definitely needs to be managed in a proper way.

i like local. but, it's the only option out here other than pj's. pj's is local-ish, but their coffee isn't to my liking, so here i am. it's easy to sit here and grab a few moments of zen before my evening class.
the front staff lady was super friendly. i ordered a chai lattee with coconut milk and they were able to accommodate which is fantastic. the store was clean and organized.

was not impressed with this starbucks. the space is rather large but the seating is limited. the overall space was kind of a mess and seemed like it needed a deep cleaning. stuff was just out of place. they also need better training on making drinks. the first time i went i ordered a brève hot chocolate. it was not made breve, and when i told the barista she started to argue with me that it was instead of just making me a new one. the second time i came i ordered an iced matcha, which is one of my usual orders. it was extremely bland tasting and lacked a sufficient amount of matcha. would avoid going to this location unless absolutely necessary.

staff is always very nice here. got out and got me a strawberry açaí refresher which was suppppppper delicious and it was based off a recommendation from the staff! sooo tasty! i needed a little pick me up but didn't want coffee so that was a nice change. this place is always super busy and they have a drive thru which is great! especially if ya have kids and you need that treat!

this review is for this starbucks location compared to other starbucks. everyone knows what to expect quality-wise from starbucks. i'm not a huge fan of their coffee. i think there is much better out there. but, this starbucks in particular, is a very cool starbucks. this is "my" starbucks. (the one near where i live) it's a cool place. there is outdoor seating and plenty of couches, comfy chairs indoors. it's one of the few starbucks that sells wine and beer. this is definitely one of the nicer, bells and whistles, larger, and complete starbucks. my one complaint is that when i order a venti coffee, the lid often leaks. that's more of a "starbucks in general" complaint since the lids are not specific to specific stores. free wifi, just as in all starbucks.
great location and super fast service! ordered through the app and walked right in to pick it up.

average to below average starbucks. two stars because the bar never has milk (skim or whole) and if you need some you have to ask the baristas who subtly let you know they don't like you asking for milk in your coffee as it gives them extra work. for some reason, if you ask, you're given a really tiny amount of milk in a separate cup, not the usual carafe used by other starbucks.

this is the starbucks located near gate e16. i have traveled to many a starbucks from coast to coast and internationally. the ladies that are at this location are not only the fastest i've come across, but also the friendliest. all starbucks should be trained by this team. howard schultz take notice!

why no sign about being closed at the entrance to the drive thru or even the board where you give your order??? i waited behind another car for ever and then pulled up and tried to order not knowing that you were closed. there was a sign on the drive up window but what good does that do? i drove around after finding out that you were closed and told the 6 cars waiting in line about being closed. you are welcome! this was on 8/8/2020 in the afternoon.

the employees are short and a touch rude. their customer service lacks a little in the "i am sorry it is early and i am douche working at a coffee shop kinda way". have a latte and act like you are glad we are spending 10 dollars a drink.

this starbucks is a disgrace. i understand that this is a high traffic area, as it is in the middle of the outlets, but the cleanliness and customer service that i received was unacceptable. the cashier was very nice and understanding, even after i returned to her to change my grande order to a venti, but the brown haired barista was not having it. the first red flag was when she (the barista) jumped in front of the customers to grab a bag of chips, and proceeded to take them to the back where drinks were being made. as she was making the drinks, i noticed that she was not in uniform, and was making drinks very messily. since there was so many people, i understood the rush, but her tone of voice when speaking to her co-workers also gave me a very unprofessional impression. i witnessed her mess up several other customers orders, and was honestly very worried. about 10 minutes later, i saw her begin to make the drink i ordered, and i asked her if it was mine since i noticed it was only in a grande cup. i told her my name, and she said that it was. once i let her know i changed the order to a venti, her response was "great *huff*, well i'm not changing it unless i can find that order." after hearing her rude tone, i responded saying "well i actually have the receipts on my phone." then she proceeded to angrily dump my drink in the sink and kept a dirty look on her face, about 5 minutes later she finally made my drink. i asked her again to confirm if it was mine (since it had no label), and she barely looked up at me. after she finally looked up and said "yes" i took my drink, said my "thank you", and left with no further interaction. honestly, i am very disappointed that this was my experience, as all the other workers appeared to be so kind and understanding.

stopped by the other day 'cuz i was craving a caramel frap..an old high school fave. i'm not a huge coffee person but i do love the fraps here!! as i'm pregnant snd don't typically drink coffee, i asked how much caffeine was in the drink. the first barista didn't know but was honest and friendly and kindly directed me to a second who was able to tell me. i appreciated the honesty of the first and the knowledge of the second. my frap was great and definitely satisfied my craving!
trash was overflowing and my frap took forever to get. my husband ordered a double espresso shot and they served it in a large cup that you'd get a large coffee in.

no bathroom use allowed! customer beware. after buying $25 of items, my 4 yr girl was told to go next door to a competing store by the starbucks supervisor. the supervisor randomly shutdown the bathroom for no reason other than high traffic. the place was packed with 30+ patrons, but the staff shutoff a clean bathroom. don't have accessibility concerns, medical problems, or a parent of children and expect basic human support.

you take time to write a review if is really good or the opposite:(( i like this location, nothing really that can be criticized other then long lines every time but i like the crowd:)) the outside patio is great!!!! but... i only give 3 stars because every time i go they misunderstand my order. now i learned to read what they write on my cup... misspelling my name is fun! but me repeating a few time "coconut milk please" is embarrassing! if i see they put my drink in dairy blender i know they got wrong and i ask again.... some are funny and chat with you, make a nice comment but yesterday both girls(the one that took order and one made my drink) forced me write the review this morning:(( she took a long time to make this mocha frap with coconut!!!! milk... chat with her coworker that was carrying buckets of ice, making jokes between the two, got distracted with so many other moves around, meantime started like few other drinks... literally i saw my drink waiting at every step to get to the next... it was in blender for like over 2!minutes because she served a few other drinks.. she even took time to rinse the blender cup and top before she got a top for my drink to serve it... my drink got to me very watery because it was melting already and not much flavor... i don't think i had all ingredients in it... but i had to run... otherwise i would have told them to remake it... did not risk it for another long wait. what can i say... i will go there again because the nice location and very convenient to enjoy a sunny morning...

beautiful interior, very clean, very friendly staff, they give a very warm environment fast service amazing drinks, pretty good food hard working staff very rarely did i see an employee not working they have an employee faith that's very sweet and helpful was polite and professional and knew what she was talking about. young man not sure what his name was didn't get a chance to talk to him was very hardworking and very set on doing his job keeping the store clean and helping those around him i recommend this place to all who look for a very comfortable and great place to relax and have a great drink.

i've never had this happen before but today i was not happy with my starbucks. i asked for a hot latte and received an iced latte, i bought a chicken wrap box......it was terrible!!! the wrap had barely any chicken it!! while the flavor was good, i had to eat a plain wrap with nothing in it because i was starving and already back at my job. not happy!!!! there has also been times when i have ordered my latte and got back to my job (which is in the same parking lot as my starbucks!!) and when i sipped by drink i could feel how light it was, opened the lid and the cup was just over half full. seriously!!!!!! so now i ask for light whip cream so they don't fill half of the cup with whip cream. last time i checked....whipped cream was just a topping.

i gets coffee here on the reg. service is tight. food is grrrd. drive thru is sick. other patrons are legit. i like this store. just one mega ish that my wife got is their inability to keep cold brew flowin. what's up with that?! besides that, i consider the irvington starbucks to be the best in indiana and i've been to several other ones.

i really like this starbucks location. it's definitely a neighborhood spot for east franklin. if you love nearby it's quite likely you'll run into a friend. typically it's easy to get in and out within 5 minutes. it does get very busy during the early morning hours. it's a great location to meet a friend or colleague unless they are full which happens more often than i'd like. this is a criticism i have have most starbucks. there's never enough seating. the staff is always very friendly and courteous. if you like starbucks coffee, i would recommend this location.

old gum pressed into the floor in multiple locations, dirty mats, coffee isn't very hot. coffee has been spotty at many locations lately,but starbucks is usually very clean. what more will go downhill with these people?

long time starbucks customer. i easily spend $50 a week at various stores and i'm a coffee addict. as they rapidly expand their brand on to every corner in florida though there's been a noticeable downgrade in customer service. this experience at the fowler starbucks was really the nail in the coffin for me and the brand. i showed up to the location at 4:45 am on my way out of town traveling for business. all the lights were on including the drive light. after sitting in the drive thru area for over 5 minutes with no response i drive up. inside i can see two employees who see me but don't acknowledge me at all. i'm clearly there assuming they're open and they just go about their business ignoring me entirely, not even offering a wave or hand motion that they're closed. and i certainly wasn't discreet lol. i honked, shouted, even tapped on the window. they probably didn't open till 5, but they could at least give me some indication of that. or make any effort at all to acknowledge me as a human standing there clearly wanting coffee and thinking they're open... so it's wawa from here on out for me. it's been real starbucks

do not go to this starbucks. i've been here several times and every time i've went they always mess up my drink. i am very specific with my drink and i always get the same thing. somehow they always seem to get it wrong. once i received a drink that was a legit just warm milk. not what i ordered! then, another time the top of my drink was caked with whipped cream and carmel that i couldn't even drink the rest of my hot drink. the staff here always act like they know what they are doing, but they don't! they'll also ask u to repeat four order so many times and still get it wrong! i will no longer be returning.

hate this starbucks. asked the all male barista's several times if they can put the names on the cups. they refuse and say "we will take this under advisement" i had an issue on 6 occasions that someone ordered a similar drink to mine and took my drink then either tasted it or realized it was not there drink. this could be avoided if they put a name on the cups but they are too freakin lazy to do that. so then they have to remake the drink. i don't understand why they only hire men at this location. i feel like i am at a truck stop getting coffee from a vending machine and not a starbucks. who wants a 6-6 big guy 280 lbs giving you a coffee? this is not prison i am not your prison bitch. why can't they hire females to work at this location? coming here makes me feel like in line for prison food. they should relocate this starbucks and move into the local prison. this place always has dirty tables; you have to clean it yourself, this is a disgusting starbucks of truckers wanna be barista's who is anti-hiring females. boy-george village people starbucks. bitch here is your coffee you take it black now. next!

starbucks is starbucks (supposedly) wherever you go, but if you like standing in line for a cup of coffee in a place that is never well staffed, this is your place. too many times i have had to walk out without coffee because i just can't wait 7 min. then, the one thing that really gets me when i order a dark roast i get "we ran out and were brewing. it will be about 5 minutes." so overall, and a bit contrary to the other review, i'd have to give this a very low score because for a place that serves coffee, sometimes they don't have it and they make you wait for it much of the time. they need more staff. 2 people. one making drinks and one running the register in a place that is this busy is bad managerial planning.

every time i go in the drive thru it's pretty slow, unfortunately. one time they were even closed for cleaning and didn't post any signs to indicate this in their drive thru area so there was a line of cars waiting. when i pulled up to the window to explain this to the employees, they gave me a curt response and apparently could care less. they don't seem to want to retain regulars. i suggest using pick up when you can to avoid the drive thru and possibly have better luck.

i came here en route to houston. i asked for a bacon gouda sandwich. they made it, the egg was cold. i asked them to remake it the egg is still cold. at this point i am like wtf. they can keep the $4 how hard is it to reheat a damn sandwich? never getting my money again at this airport. only getting two stars because the coffee is somewhat decent. there was a racist review below in which the user nash m. (who is a negative yelped per his profile) who states the "wrong demographics" works here because the staff is african american. don't pay attention to that, because that comes from a place of godlessness and hate. pay attention to the fact that products were not cooked or made correctly and service sucks. i have been to starbucks in major cities with all demographics: african american, hispanic, caucasian, asian you name it! it is the work culture of that particular location which fosters poor service not the race or ethnicity of the workers. good news is there are multiple starbucks in the airport i went to the one right at the beginning of the c terminal. there is one some ways down you may have better luck with.

the gate a location... literally the slowest starbucks i've ever seen... literally. it took longer to get coffee from that starbucks at 5:30 am than it did to get through tsa. did you get that? it took longer for their "baristas" to make espresso than it did for the federal government to make sure i wasn't bringing a bomb on the plane.
this starbucks sucks it never has anything in stock and is so dirty no caramel or strawberrys and ive visited this starbucks 4 times in the same month not once did they have what i wanted

i walk in they see me, i go pay and then pick my coffee up in two minutes. can't ask for more. one of the guys who is been working there for a while, and is been making my coffee all the time almost, u will be missed buddy. thanks for making my frap always delicious

the change they made with staffs are the best that happened since my last review. they are more knowledgeable and more in tune with the upkeep of this place. baristas are a lot quicker especially during busy hours. so far they haven't messed up my order (not like before) since i am attending classes on base i am more frequent here so i can really tell the changes they've made and i hope this will continue.
the staff are highest quality. if anything is not to your liking, they will make it over. they care enough to do that! they care who you are and what your orders are. and they can converse!!!
i tell everyone that this is the best starbucks in tampa and i will stick to that. they always make my order correctly and are always so nice.
coffee and staff are nice. but the lines here are abominable. plan a minimum 10 mins during the week in the morning.

best time ever!! my friend and i ordered drinks and food #delicious!! butttt we didn't have enough honey...still good tho!! although we didn't finish our homework, a great time was had by all! (except for the friend playing crazy 8s with us) coming back soon!!!!!!!
the staff are all efficient and friendly. they know my drink and always ask about me and my family. jocelyn is an absolute sweetheart!!

i absolutely love this starbucks. i have been going to this location for years and the customer service has always been great. the wait times can get long, but i always know that the staff is trying their best to get everyone their drinks. if i'm in a rush, i always do a mobile order and conveniently enough they just added parking by the front of the store for mobile orders only. the mobile ordering is super easy and i am in and out of the store so quickly. i highly recommend giving it a try!

extremely slow service, the seating area is horrible. one big huge table that makes you feel like your in a middle school cafeteria. the other tables are so pushed together, you have zero comfort space. when you talk or if your on your computer everyone can see and hear what you are doing. above all the cleanliness of this location is horrific. there was actual bugs flying around the store and in the cases! starbucks might have the name, but it is becoming just an expensive, unclean, unorganized flop.
my local starbucks. good space for some reading or studying or a small meeting. lines are never too long. drinks are made in fashionable time, overall great service. definitely recommend.

corporate office needs to evaluate this store! slow service throw drive thru. don't get orders rite the first time, i have been coming to this location nearly 6 years and the last year or so has been with soo many problems occur

i have been to many starbucks locations, and none are as good as this location! none of them! i really do not understand the negative reviews here. every time that i go (which is fairly often since i work across the street) the baristas are always friendly with, not only the regulars such as myself, but everyone in line. the drinks made here are always better made than any other location i have been to. for such a small location, they sure do get that line moving faster than larger locations with far more workers. if you are reading reviews, listen to the girl who visits this location on a weekly basis.

this starbucks is really good. they are consistently busy, and it's fun to watch the staff bustling around filling their orders. today i got my traditional iced chai tea latte, and my wife got a skinny mocha frappuccino. it was the perfect pick-me-up while browsing for shoes at the mall.

this particular starbucks is on the ball every day and i visit coffee shops on the regular. the lady in there by the name of heather is a machine. she even remembered my order from my last visit. now that's what i call customer service. all around i'm going to be making this my regular stop in the mornings. still all of the ladies there are very chipper this early in the morning and my drink is mad perfect. well done.
this isn't a real starbucks. it's in a target and lacks the ambiance and quality of a stand alone. avoid. table was too dirty to even put my sunglasses down on it!

this is my favorite starbucks! the baristas are always friendly, happy and quick with my order. my drink is always made right, no complaints here. special thank you to these baristas: kia, brianna, david, kg (?) and sarah. :) they do a fantastic job and have excellent skills.

i've been to many starbucks. the tea here was the worst. i have gotten the tea infusion and it has been very tasty. at this starbucks it seemed as though there was no infusion. i even returned thinking they had just made a mistake. no apologies just excuses and another tea that was very bland. it would have been a one star but the woman taking the original order was friendly and courteous.

coffee is coffee to me so maybe not fair to judge but this location of starbucks just had too many people that seemed to be using this as their early morning place for hygiene. a small line at the bathroom and the place was a little unkempt compared to the sb i went to about a mile away the previous morning. this is a good location. very convenient and did not have time for the extra distance.
flat white if eve makes it, you will not be disappointed service - friendly cost - same as all starbucks

super super friendly baristas :) the best peppermint mocha's in town and the drive thru is very fast, even in the busiest part of the morning! definitely drive a bit out of my way to go to this particular store when i need a s'bucks!

this starbucks location was very crowded for a weekday early afternoon. primarily outdoor seating available however high tables and a small area with cozy arm chairs was open. i would not recommend this location for a business meeting or quiet place to work.
so, bathrooms closed for paying customer. wanted a large, got medium, asked for large, but when asked in stupid starbucks language i must have agreed to the wrong size. f**k this place

how is it possible that a coffee place is bad? i actually like starbucks but this one does not stand up to others. my favorite starbucks happens to be in a grocery store which is hilarious since they are actually employed by the store. this location i have hit up at least 10 times and each time the service has been less than adequate. too bad because i would gladly keep spending money there.

compared with other starbucks locations, this one seemed fairly new. there were several comfy-looking chairs and the entire place was spotless. the staff seemed a bit amateur. i asked about the ingredients in a pumpkin latte and got a bewildered look from a staffer, like i had just asked a question about quantum physics. after i explained ("you know, is there sweetened syrup? just spices? what exactly gives the latte it's pumpkin flavor?"), she relaxed and ran through the ingredients list, which seemed too sugary for what i wanted (and there's no sugar-free version of the pumpkin latte). i went with a regular latte and the boyfriend ordered a mocha. there were several staffers but the process wasn't as seamless as other locations. still, this is one of the few coffee shops in the area that i could find on my yelp app, so it did the trick.
i really enjoy this starbucks because the staff is so nice and i don't have to worry about my drink being wrong! i've noticed with this one that when the line is long they still are pretty quick!

of the many starbucks i have been to across the us and canada, this one most consistently gets the orders wrong and has the most uninterested and bla se employees i have encountered. there is so little to meet the expectation of an experience here that it is just another place to get coffee. i prefer to go to the starbucks kiosk in target though it is much less convenient.
this starbucks always gives me excellent service. i come three times a week and they workers are so nice

have been several times as my office is nearby. service is generally friendly but slow. i would love to get coffee before my early trains to new york but they are almost never open on time. great location, they just need execution help in a big way.

i have had better experiences with starbucks, so i am pretty sure the problem here is with their staffing. i arrived to a busy store and waited in line for my turn, i placed my order and moved over to the waiting area. while waiting i noticed two other customers, who had ordered after myself get the same drink i had ordered get their before me? overall the drinks are what you come to experience from starbucks, my complaint is about the service. another problem with this store is the way that the its setup, the drive thru is a total mess and often blocks regular parking during peak hours. i have learned to never park in spots close to the building, always park in the spots away otherwise you risk getting blocked in by drive thru traffic!

this starbucks has been terribly designed. the parking lots suck. the drive thru lines block one lot and get so long they linger terry parkway. every time i get s starbucks app reward or discount, this location never identifies it and that takes even longer. the west bank needs more locations!

this place seems so understaffed recently. whenever i go inside to order, the one employee is handling the register, food, 5 mobile order drinks for customers who aren't even there yet, and then eventually..my drink. i'm giving 5 stars for the poor overworked guy behind the counter who manages to maintain a good attitude.

i go to this starbucks because it is the closest one to where i live. i will divide it into general starbucks and the warnings of their specific location (lots of congestion/traffic- can't really be helped). i do not have much to say about the drinks as they are what you find at every location. they are always very consistent here, which is fantastic. i like that they have a long powered table and a plentiful amount of wall outlets. thank you for this! the service here has always been great. sometimes it takes a little longer to get a simple drink out, but it is usually because they are very busy. the quality of the drinks has always been great for me, and i do not have complaints! if there has been a mistake or they have somehow forgotten my drink due to their busyness, they always correct it or get it to me right away (sometimes in a larger size). when they aren't as crowded, the baristas are always friendly and chatty! although starbucks is not my favorite coffee spot, i like coming here to do work due to their longer hours and friendliness once it's a little emptier. the only meh i have about it is that it is always so cold. a barista explained that their thermostat is hard to control, and that it's either extremely warm or cold. but when i have to bring my winter coat and gloves to a florida coffee shop in the summer and when the baristas have to war hoodies and beanies sometimes, it's a little extreme. --- so here are my warnings primarily based on their prime location. these are things that can not be helped, since it is purely because of where this starbucks is situated. for this i would probably give 1 star, but i do not think i can rate something that can't really change unless a massive construction project was done. :p the location of this starbucks is very great for their business. during peak times it gets very crowded. the thing is that their parking and drive-thru situation are both awful during these times. the lot is way too small for the amount of traffic they have, and during busy times i usually have to park far down in the neighborhood by the papa john's (to not risk towing) or on a strange faux-driveway next door. if you are parked in the lot but the drive-thru is packed, it takes a very long time to get out. you will be stuck unless a kind stranger lets you out. if you are trying to go through the drive-thru, due to the location and circulation, on a packed day you will be waiting in actual street to go through the drive-thru. there is not enough space. sometimes if i don't want to deal all of this i drive to a different starbucks location that is not too far down the road and is way easier to park at/drive-thru. sorry! if you are patient (which honestly depends on the day for me), this location is a good spot!

the staff at this starbucks location was very disorganized and inefficient today with all customer orders while i was there. they seemed adequately staffed (5) yet did not seem to know what they were doing. they didn't even have the added distraction of drive-thru services. whoever the manager is needs to address this.

starbucks is good because it's a franchise and it's hard to screw up a business that's already been layed out for you. since the methodology and the ambient are uniform throughout the stores, the one thing an individual store needs to make a priority to not get a bad review is customer service. the barista, cory, took my order as if i were inconveniencing her. if she had even been uninvolved or emotionless while i ordered i wouldn't be writting a bad review. but she outdid herself with her attitude. she didn't make me feel welcome. only reason i'm not giving one star is that i liked the coffee.

i went there and ordered a grande iced cloud caramel macchiato. then, the lid wasn't closed completely, so all the coffee poured into my white hoodie. however, i couldn't use the bathroom at that time because of some problems. i told an employee who works there and her attitude was unacceptable. she didn't say sorry, all she said was " if u want, i can make u a new one" with a disrespectful tone of voice. in addition, she gave me a ton of napkins. could i clean these stains only with napkins? i don't wanna think this way but were they racist me cause i'm asian? that was my first and also last time there. what a bad experience!

this starbucks has improved a lot since it opened. it is a very busy location but they handle it very well. very convenient for us but we still miss the crew at the chadds ford location by whole foods. it is consistent with every other starbucks!! very friendly staff!
worst customer service ever! the guy was rude, made the drink wrong, then tried to tell us our drinks were venti, and they were grande. i think i know the difference. will not come here again.

normally starbucks is great! consistent from location to location right? well sadly not the case. this location seems to struggle with the basics. order a "standard" drink. straight from the menu with no modifiers you should be ok. i say should because some times they end up on the mobile stand when ordered via drive thru. now if you order any drink with modification might as well skip this location. something will be off, wrong milk type or leave out the espresso (not exaggerating). calling won't change much as i've done that. head over to 86th street if you'd like to get what you ordered, if you want to roll the dice come here.
this location is in a weird location and gets very busy!! service is pretty quick. i hate the parking lot abd drive thru layout!

your "third place." it's like your "fourth meal," but with comfort and class. starbucks isn't just where your order your caffeine fix and leave, it's where the baristas know your name, drink and ask you how your kids are doing in school. they are constantly trained to perfection. they want your drink right every time at every location. consistency is key. if it's not right, they will make it so and compensate you for your time and money spent. starbucks isn't all about profit, it's about you. you're the one spending $5 on your daily latte -- it should be perfect and if it's not, you should let them know. i am a little biased, seeing as i used to work for this company, but every time i walk in here, i feel like family and the vast majority of the partners have no idea who i am. i have walked in here having a royally horrendous day and left feeling amazing.

this location is a joke. was here today from 6 -7:30 pm. the baristas were straight up screaming at each other and doubled over laughing because they were pranking each other with drinks made from every pump of syrup they have. they were bragging that they were costing the store "so much money." it got so loud that another customer and i said that if corporate was here, they'd shut the place down. baristas fighting with who has to be on the floor, yelling, trying to calm each other down from the pranks, it was absurdly unprofessional. another customer wished me luck because i was working on my computer while eating a protein box. the place cleared out because the baristas were so out of control. management needs fired here, as does their staff. unbelievable. i recorded some footage after the big prank fest died down. this was 1/100 of the volume it was from them pranking each other.
great service today! my skinny coffee frappuccino w/sugar free vanilla is so delicious

customer service is alive and well at this starbucks location. i had a problem with my starbucks reward card and went to speak to the manager on site, felicia. she took care of me very kindly and graciously. apologized for the mistake. wow... this doesn't happen now a days. love their service and coffee. they will have a loyal customer here

so apparently i've just haven't learned my lesson with these guys. by far the worst starbucks i've been to! so tonight me and my husband went out for a late dinner and afterwards we wanted a drink from starbucks so i looked on my app and saw that one was the only close by that was open. so we took the chance and went and sure enough failed us. i got a venti pink drink and my pink drink was light pink that was it! it looked so plain!!!! why?!? because they didn't add any strawberries! there was so evidence of any strawberries! i'm not complaining about the price because hey i know i pay a lot for a drink but hey i love starbucks so whatever! it's yummy! but anyways if i'm paying that much i should be getting what i paid for! what it's suppose to be! my husband got a white chocolate mocha hot and he said it tasted bland. no flavor! and on top of it i placed the order on the app so we don't have to wait too long. my husband gets his drink and makes him wait another 10 minutes for the pink drink! if i want starbucks i'll just go somewhere else and drive a little further out of my way to get some i guess! ugh so annoying! the worst starbucks ever!!!!!!

love these guys. especially considering how swamped they can be, they're always very professional and pleasant. i'm in there a lot, and whenever they actually get a chance to breathe, they definitely recognize you and acknowledge you in one way or another. very friendly and personable. they're also very on top of their customer service element. i've been in there a couple times when i've asked for a food item that they were currently out of, and whoever was helping me was quick to suggest some similar items i might have an interest in and also offered to discount a more expensive replacement item to the price of the item i originally asked for. the store is also very clean (as are the restrooms) and they have a very nice outdoor patio area. definitely my favorite local coffee stop!

best starbucks ever! the baristas that work here are amazing!! they get my order right every time and even know me as soon as i start to speak through the drive through! kathy is amazing always so friendly! i hate being away from home and having to visit other locations! thanks 4 being awesome! triple shot expresso mocoito with 4 sweet and lows!

how many people standing around does it take to make a strawberry smoothie? there were at least 7 ppl standing behind the counter staring at you first off. i felt like a chicken dinner. end result this was the worst strawberry smoothie ever from starbucks.

whatever guy is working right now.. it's 6pm on wednesday night, 12/6, is the most rude starbucks employee i've ever encountered. i can't believe i'm even writing a review but i've never had this happen at starbucks. i know these jobs are hard to come by as starbucks is a phenomenal employer, so whoever the dude is working the starbucks drive thru on robb needs to at least treat people with some form of kindness. i'm in disbelief..

this i why i love starbucks: first, the coffee cake! the best! good with coffee, but without it, it's great also. second, the frappe chino and teas. they are so flavorful! it also goes well with the food there. also, the service is good, because they get the order right and don't take a long time... (usually). lastly, they are definitely kid friendly. a lot of drinks kids can have and they are delicious . all in all, starbucks is amazing. enjoy!

coffee is a lot harder to find than daqueries in this part of town, so starbucks would have been fine for a fix, this is on the far side of the marriot lobby, went in at 1:30 in the afternoon and they were already closed, wtf?
great pick me up pit stop on a road trip!!! the drinks are on point and the staff is cool. gets the job done.

well, it's starbucks. i like the coffee (if you don't fine, but don't be a hater) and the food too. i just drink brewed so i'm an easy customer. the staff is always pleasant and they do a good fast job. however, i'm in here like 3 days a week and they don't know my name (and never asked)-which is weird for a starbucks. update. they do know my name now.
they fired three good employees never going back... who fired them and whyyy. so lame.

stopped here with my family for a breakfast treat before we did our taxes. the staff were super nice and made us feel welcome in their store. the store is much larger than most and has very comfortable seating. they made the wrong drink, but quickly made a new one and gave the the wrong one too! bonus! overall, i feel this place is a win. we will be back.

ehh, this starbucks is ok. i came here mid afternoon on a saturday and the place was packed. waited in line for at least 5 minutes to order and then another 5 for my drink. luckily, my drink was prepared well and correctly. the service was average, nothing to complain about but nothing spectacular. the barista making the drinks was disheveled in appearance and had liquid all over the front of his apron. granted, i get that spills happen but his apron was gross, he should have changed to another one if he was going to continue making drinks. overall, average experience. this place seems to have pretty good reviews so perhaps i caught it at a bad time.

omg people weren't lying when they said this is the "slowest" starbucks. we waited easily 20+ minutes in the drive thru when there were maybe about 5 cars in front of us. it reminded me of the sloths in zootopia! and on top of that, i ordered a skinny peppermint mocha frap which i'm not sure why, but they topped with salted caramel syrup. the guy working the drive thru window was nice though. i just don't recommend going here if you are in a rush. you'll definitely be late.
really awesome location. not real hard to find parking, and building has kind of an industrial chic flavor. employees weren't real friendly but i'd def come here again.

behind the location at the cheshire inn on western side of forest park, this is probably the most unique starbucks in st. louis. service is quick, the staff are personable (even when slammed under the morning rush) and the atmosphere is a little less corporate in feel. there's little mistaking that you're in a starbucks when you step in the door, but it's also distinctly the central west end starbucks. skim the reviews (or google maps) and you'll find just about all that you need to know for a great visit to this walkable store. there's no dedicated parking lot or pickup spot, but there a few sturdier places to lock up a bike on the block. (drivers do have options though. there's a public garage above the schlafly library on euclid and metered parking along the street. free street parking is often available to the east of the shop on maryland ave and there's a paid parking lot across the street too). there's no drive-thru, but mobile orders are usually accepted. the location isn't that large, but does have a good mix of semi-communal seating, small tables, sidewalk seating and a couple of more comfortable chairs. if there's really any complaint with the store, it's the minor irritation of its tendency to sell out of products, whether it's blonde roast, breakfast sandwiches or nitro cold brew (each of which have been unavailable at least once during my random stops in over the past year). i've never had that affect an app order but, if you have your mind set on a particular item, it's probably best to order ahead.

this starbucks has great location since it is close to my office and i am a coffee person. love the outside sitting area, mostly in the evening because it is too hot during the day. normally, i do not want to write a review for starbucks because of their popularity, however, i just want to bring to the manager of this location's attention about one of the barista there, sam is probably the worst barista that i have ever had at any starbucks location, she lacks of personality and customer service skills. i am working in hospitality industry, too and that is not how customer should be treated. she made my order wrong and refused to make it right, claiming it was my fault. when i asked her nicely to redo it, she rolled her eyes and walked away. i had to ask another barista to get it done. never have this kind of experience at any other location. hopefully, the manager there will address this issue because this location is very convenient for me. if not, i guess i have to give steep station across the street a try.
everyone at this location is really friendly. my coffees have always been spot on. this particular visit, tried the pumpkin spice cream cold brew. i loved it.

i have been to this starbucks three times this week, only to have them tell me that they are out of pumpkin bread each time. each time, they say they "just ran out." they also have pumpkin cream cheese muffins and pumpkin scones advertised; they are out of both of those as well. the staff appears to have zero training and the management is clearly unable to order effectively. this place is a joke. if you want to wait in line for 20 minutes only to be told that they are out of three different things that you try to order, then, by all means, go to this starbucks.

went in around 10 and had the worst service . waited 30 minutes for an iced green tea . watched workers laugh and talk while slowly making drinks . they should have called in more people if they were short handed .

this may be the largest starbucks i have seen east of ohio! at least 5,000 square feet, it feels like a small theater, more than a cafe. plenty a seating with tables, chairs and more relaxed furniture in the corner. easily 15 foot ceiling which does impact the acoustics, but almost feels like being outside. a 50 foot wall long black and white panel that pulls the room together. yet, when i was there at 4:30 on a recent afternoon, the operation was being run by only 3 baristas, including drive through service. impressive that they managed to sweep and clean up the place during their shifts. menu was standard for the green mermaid, although the display case was somewhat depleted given the time of day. i snared the final slice of lemon pound cake - my endulgance for the week. it was as tart and sweet as always. while still not a true fan of starbucks, this place isn't bad.

this starbucks is very roomy and has lots of outside seating. our drinks have been made correctly each time we've visited, however their restrooms are disgusting. i thought it was maybe an off day when we came the 1st time but they have been bad each time we have come. the floor looks like it never gets washed and there are used paper towels and toilet paper everywhere. it makes me wonder how clean their machines are and the hygiene of the staff.
worst starbucks i've ever been to. absolutely ridiculous that there were 3 cars in front of me in the drive through and it took 20 minutes for them to even take my order. not coming back here again.

this is the slowest starbucks drive thru. it's the closest to my home and it is always--literally always---a long wait. even when i'm the second car, it's guaranteed a 10 minute wait at least. you can't get out of line either.

the pricing is what you would expect at the airport. not ever coming to this crappy starbucks again, because if you have to get hot water you have to purchase something. never mind the fact that i purchased something from the stinking place this morning. avoid at all cost.

they have friendly and happy staff here. i think her name is taiesha? she's lovely! i had my non-sweet chai and this was so good! i have tried many flavors from starbucks a while ago when this is the only brand i used to drink. it tastes great when having it once a while. you can't go wrong with starbucks brand. this location is very good too.

this location is fine. it's not spectacular but also not the worst. we typically stop in here because its the last stop before we hit delaware. they don't have any gluten free items and they are a little slow but it gets the job done and you get to have coffee that isn't dunkin or whatever other brand.

one of the worst customer service i have ever seen!!! this girl was so rude to me! she did not greet me at all just stood there staring at me and waiting for me to say something. then took my order and didnt say a word just started dojng her thing and talking to her co workers!! just the worst!! and the drink i ordered was so disgusting!

what a pleasure as emily was at the drive through and as she handed me my drink i drove away smiling because of her great personality!!! look forward in seeing her again at starbucks on los altos in sparks!!!

this starbucks always delivers. they are open late (midnight), with a great seating area inside and an even bigger one outside. a much needed staple in wesley chapel, you often see people socializing outside while studying or working a their computers and enjoying a coffee. they have a drive through with quick, friendly service. i've never had a long wait when running in or going through the drive through. love it!
