least favorite starbucks unfortunately. almost every time i put in an order something is wrong. they forget the little details. venti water no ice- get a grande water with ice. changa bagel extra toasted- barley toasted. wet cappuccino- barely. customer service was pretty good for the most part until the gal told us to move cause we were standing where the mobile orders go. oh well.

i usually never like to write a bad review, but i was not content. a particular employee here, a tall man with long hair and usually wears a beanie, had given me a rude attitude when i was ordering. i always studied inside starbucks when they still had their old location (they had great service btw) but since covid, i have only been going through the drive-thru and ordering through mobile order at their new location by party city. i always get the same order so i know the price of my drink, however, when i finally decide to go inside the new location to study and order my regular drink, the price came to be pricier. i asked the man nicely if my drink should've been a little less compared to the price that he had given me. he then gave me a rude attitude while repeating my order to me. he says "this was what you wanted right? then the price comes to that price." i understand they tend to get busy, but that does not excuse being rude to customers. i work in the food industry as well, and no matter how rough my day is, i still try to give the best service. what i experienced was not good service. i just hope their customer service improves.

this is still my favorite location. just recently the orders have been wrong alot, most recently in the last 2 days. yesterday wasn't a big deal because it was just lemonade missing. today is an issue because i've only had 1/4 of my drink and feel sick because the wrong milk was used. mistakes happen but 2 days in a row and people have severe allergies, luckily i didn't drink it all and it will pass but please starbucks pay attention a little closer. (plus we pay extra for these things) 1 star- total failure of an experience based on this experience would not return. would not recommend. 2 star- below average on several levels, not a total failure. wouldn't seek out to return or recommend. 3 star- average experience, minimum expectations met. nothing that says wow or that stands out. would return if someone else wanted to come, not a regular go to. 4 star- above average experience, will come here again and recommend to friends and family. 5 star- this will be a regular go to and will be highly recommended to people i know. exceeds expectations on a consistent basis.

newly remodeled beautiful store with ample parking. my only complaint was the employees seemed exhausted and didn't want to engage. i also needed a cup of water and waited at the pickup area for 5 minutes without being acknowledged so i just left. hoping it was just a fluke!

purpose of the visit was to pick up an online order. drive-thru was a long line so i elected to go in to avoid crowding the drive-thru. employees are fairly apathetic when you enter. there are two queues - one for ordering and one off mobile pick-up. when in the mobile pick-up queue, they only acknowledge you when...well that never happened. you had to queue in the order line before they react. if the only way the system is supposed to work, then they shouldn't display signage for two queues.
came to this starbucks when we were in philly. it's pretty cozy feeling but i don't like that the line ends up right at the entrance. there isn't a lot of parking outside either.

friendly staff and a tremendous caramel flavored frozen drink! located right in the middle of downtown franklin, and they do a great job of managing orders, keeping up with the population, and blending in with franklin's atmosphere.

i don't know much about other items it offers, but i do love the *iced green tea coffee latte* there. i've tasted this one at bunch of starbucks in philla, and another one is near broad street, temple univ., which is also neat. but i used to go to this one where is near my house to do my reading assignments every weekends, and i'll definitely buy a cup of green tea coffee latte. the only drawback there, i would say, i feel a little noisy if i sit in the upstairs, cause there are always vibrating the coffee machines underneath.. and the seats having good lights are limited, but if you don't work with readings, you can choose the secondaries.

the coffee was good but the staff was downright horrible! they made fun of customers as soon as the customer had walked out of the store, they sat there on their cell phones while i was waiting to order, and while i was trying to give me order i was ignored and had to repeat my order twice because some person had to show another person a thing on their phone. to top it off my husband went to ask where the bathroom was but because the place was old english style he decided to ask in semi old english (old english but understandable) and those obnoxious children waited until he was gone in the rest room before literally making fun of him. the guy with the blond hair (sort of white black almost mohawk sort of thing) was the rudest of the bunch though. we will not be going back ever!

i think i'm being generous with the stars. yesterday i went to this starbucks and got a caramel frap and i tasted the first two sips and after that it was like straight up ice. wayyyyyy too much ice that i had to throw away my 'iced drink'. i was not a fan and i am a fan of starbucks. this sucks because the service was really good so i didn't taste my drink before i drove off or i would have asked them to definitely make it over. honestly the worst drink i have had from starbucks.

this location is always hopping. expect long drive thru lines, but it always moves very quickly. i tend to order on the app, park and run inside. my order is always ready for me. the only reason for 4 stars instead of 5 stars is lately my orders haven't been completely correct. i am guessing it is either someone new or just hurrying. i have ordered tea, stood and waited to later be told they are out of the tea and whip cream when i always say no whip. other than those small mix ups my drinks are hot and fresh or icy cold and fresh depending on what mood i am in.

i've been coming here for sometime now. this starbucks is in a great neighborhood and parking usually isn't that difficult to locate. the employees at this location are very friendly and work efficiently. theirs seating towards the rear of the store which seats a good amount of customers with additional seating at the bar area looking out the window. the store always looks clean and well kept. they also have various mugs, instant coffee, coffee beans for sale. they had a 30% sale for christmas time and ended up purchasing a mug. i liked the decorations they put up for christmas and the music being played was great. i'm happy when i come in here it isn't too busy or i probably wouldn't stay. i feel a lot of people will take away from the overall environment. i feel some people talk too loud and i don't enjoy hearing other people's conversation. i would stop by here if your in the area and check it out.
dropped my teacher's drink in the lot and the staff kindly remade my drink without recharging me. i really appreciated it. thank you.

i could write a 1-star review for every single time i choose to subject myself and go to this starbucks. it's one building away from my office and located in the building where i take grad classes but it is hands down the worst starbucks i have ever encountered. i'll bullet some things for now, and edit them as i continue to have terrible experiences: -baristas have absolutely no idea what they are doing. my order of "grande black dark roast" regularly throws them for a loop. this morning she handed my coffee to me and said "dark roast with room for milk!"... that's not what "black" means.. -they have no idea how to stock inventory. this morning i ordered a chorizo, egg and cheese sandwich; after paying and waiting 5 minutes, i was informed that they were "out of those" and then they listed off the ones they do have. when i said "all of those sandwiches are cheaper than the one i already paid for" they responded with "umm you want a pastry?" ugh -one time i ordered a grande black coffee and a $5 gift card (a thank you gift for a coworker). the cashier proceeded to somehow load the gift card with the $5 and then used it to charge my grande coffee. so before i knew it, i was being handed a gift card with $3.90 on it. what?? i should actually be working right now.. but i'll definitely be back to fill in more horror stories. oh and if you ask why i go back? working 10 hours in a desk job and then going to a 4-hour econ class warrants coffee and when you only get one 10 minute break during class?... yeah, you would go too.
henry was at the window and did a great job customer connecting ! a+ for him and the fast service !

the staff here are really friendly and the service is pretty quick! my only complaint is how long the drive though line is in the morning and how hard it is to get in line coming from downtown. you have to wait for a break in traffic and someone coming east bound to let you into the line. not sure how to fix this though.
love love love coming in here on my most tired mornings to treat myself to the wonderful deliciousness of starbucks. the staff always gets the order right each time i come in here.
this starbucks is way way slow..... i was disappointed in how long i had to wait. the service however was good - the person i came into contact with was pleasant.
was not the service i'm used to getting. if all other stores had personalities like the one i encountered at this one starbucks would go out of business

my wife came in to get us coffee so i can't really judge this place i guess. still the coffee we got was good had no problems with it. the pace is small though and if you don't like dogs probably not the best place for you.

this starbucks was relaxing. i've never been at this location. i had a meeting with someone & i felt like crap! my sinus was kicking my butt that night. thankfully i immediately ordered a medicine ball. oh my, i swear within 15 minutes, i could breathe out my nose. i see why it's called a "medicine ball." in reality, it should be changed to "miracle tea." because it was a miracle that i felt almost 100% better after taking the tea within 30 minutes. the staff was friendly & welcoming.
our 'homebase' starbucks, this is a great location. the staff are all very friendly and just overall good people. definitely a fan all around.

i'm surprised that during a busy time there is only one cashier. the person before me had an item return and it took such a long time. no one said anything to me while i waited and waited. i come here a lot and just expected to be welcomed and not ignored. today austin made my day! he gave me lots of ideas on how to enhance my drink. nice! i feel like an important customer and will be back soon. my cascara latte with an extra shot is perfect! thanks austin!

huge, beautiful! this starbucks has endless inside seating, plentiful seats near outlets and an entire outside porch area of seating! it's always busy, but i still find places to sit. the decor is great and they even have books for you to read and a water jug for you to serve yourself water. amazing! however the best part: it's the only place in nashville and possibly in the entire nation outside of california that has toilet seat covers! i come from san diego, a land plentiful with seat covers and have suffered many moons as i traveled across america and noticed that no where has seat covers. yuck! except for this amazing starbucks. god bless you starbucks and god bless seat covers.

most unorganized and dysfunctional starbucks i have ever been to. as a gold member, nothing makes my day more than my afternoon coffee. workers are rude and appear as they don't want to be bothered, staff is overwhelmed with the massive drive through line and there have been several times they have told me they can't make my drink due to them being out of certain things such as cold brew and sweet cream. not your typical starbucks that's for sure... something is always off at this one! very disappointing!!!

new starbucks that opened today across from publix near henderson blvd. it replaces the store at 401 s dale mabry which is now closed. new prototype design is very attractive with natural wood and sealed concrete floor. the coffee - well you know what that is.......
i ordered the 1/2 beignets from door dash. i received biscuits with no powdered sugar or marmalade with no flavor.
very friendly staff. clean and quick as well.

all i have to say is yum!! starbucks is delicious. their coffee is warm,tasty, and how coffee should be. their passion tea lemonade is sweet and very good. i also enjoy their pumpkin and banana walnut slices of bread. the location is great. i'm fond of starbucks.

what a disappointment i had today in starbucks at this location. after placing my order, i waited for 15 minutes for my drinks. in addition, my order was presented to me wrong 2 times. the waste, the waiting customers, the frustrated starbucks workers.....i'll think twice before i go to this starbucks again.
typical starbucks quality food ok prices but this particular time service was super slow. we even had to remind them in the drive-through of the cake. forgive them though
love the starbucks, the staff is super friendly they always know what i want. green tea latte soy no classic yum!!!!!!
no toilet paper or paper towels in the bathroom on christmas day. yuck.

i stopped here after discovering the soho had sold out for the chris robinson brotherhood, and i couldn't find the public restrooms. the barista(katie) on duty was very kind and engaged me in conversation when i told her i had just driven 120 miles to see the show. i was quite pleased with her customer service and getting me on the list to the show)). if all baristas could be as customer service oriented, the service industry economy would thrive. having grown up in ventura county and frequently going to santa barbara on the weekends i have forgotten what it is like to receive great customer service from a corporate run business.

the worst location i've ever been to! out of the 5 people there, not a single person acknowledged my presence. between that and the blaring music and dirty floors i thought maybe they were closed, and i was completely ignored until the cups were stacked (the customer is obviously not a priority here) after standing at the counter a few minutes i finally get to order. for some reason it took twice as long to make it than it usually does...and it tasted like crap. (i was the only customer besides one guy who was also waiting for his order.) i've gotten the same coffee for 10 years at starbucks so i know when it's not right. i asked for another one and i waited another 10mins for it. they got it right the second time but i was floored that this was an actual starbucks as i have never received such crappy service and have never seen one so dirty. i will never go back to that location..do better st. albert.

this store is awesome. the manager, bennie, is the man! the unfortunate part of this store is it gets slammed in the morning and at lunch being in the business location that it is, so be patient. but they kill it.

first off i would like to say that i (we) visit starbucks quite often but today was terrible. today is st. patricks day, and we are a business, so we decided to place an order, as we do on all holidays. needless to say our employee is still there... 1 1/2 hours later. they lost our order. he kept telling them he needs to get back to work. we have a full restaurant and full lobby. i called them and they said " we will get to this order as soon as we can" . i told him he needed to get back to work...just get our money back but they said they could not do it. i have heard from several other people that this location has went downhill but i usually don't listen to people since i have never had a problem, until now. i just wanted to let you all know that this has upset alot of people. i usually don't write reviews but today i was very upset.

this is consistently the slowest starbucks i have visited. if you're looking to grab something quickly in the morning, do not visit this store. there always seems to be 10 people behind the counter but only one at the register and the line moves very slow. the store is aesthetically appealing inside and some of the employees are very friendly but i find it frustrating how consistently slow the service is.

this is my neighborhood starbucks,which makes it the best!! (for me!!!) i love when i walk in and everyone says hi! or good morning!! they are (mostly) so friendly! i think when they aren't it's because they're either a he trainer or trainee and their mind is exploding. seriously, not sure but i feel like this starbucks is the training center of tampa!! how grueling that must be and they still smile at me! this is a typical starbucks as far as offerings, no wine, appetizers or fancy desserts like other ones i've heard of, but i've had my share of starbucks around the us and europe and have had to return a cup or two for burnt taste, wrong order, etc. not here. i come here 54-5 days a week when i'm in town and have never had a mistake! ever. i'd say parking is the only issue because people don't want to park behind the store - but it's there, so my 5 stars stick!

not friendly, not welcoming. 3rd place environment doesn't exist here lol. connections? where you don't get noticed and your order is taken in a very stale voice. we're they rude? not really, were they nice? also no.

business was slow (due to the time of night) so service was pretty fast. tastes delicious. no complaints. it was pretty late so i feel the barista's quiet/short demeanor is due to her being tired or something. either way, super excited to get my caffeine fix at like...2100-2130.

where to begin.... parking is a cluster at most times and people have no qualms about double parking you in to add to the fun. the staff seems overwhelmed and lately they seem to take orders quickly and fill them slowly which creates huge back ups of people. since it's easy to compare this place to what, a million other of the same format, it's maybe time for management to improve here but something needs an overhaul.

i love this place! staff is amazing and they are pretty quick in the mornings. my "peppermint mocha" is always tasty and hot! :) i've even had one staff member announce my order at the drive through before i even said anything!

customer service is still great. nice employees and my order is always good and correct. i'm updating this review because the drive through wait is horribly long and has gotten worse over the years. i typically can't stop here in the morning because the wait is guaranteed to take ten to fifteen minutes. if they work on speed this review will go up.
drive thru was super quick! took me 5 minutes to get all the way from the road to the window! kara was super friendly at the window asking me about my day!

although having the convenience of the drive thru is nice and most of the staff is friendly, drinks aren't made right. modifications aren't given attention and get completely forgotten. i can completely understand missing something once in a while but it's happened a handful of times. most of the staff is friendly and more than willing to help but i have had an issue with an older woman who i've seen working the drive thru window a few times, has had an attitude the last 3 times i've seen her there.
i like the staff and service but the music is too loud and i always end up having a headache. they need to understand that the place is not a club. hope they address this issue.

ugh. how many starbucks does one town need? answer: zero. avoid this corporate piss palace and support your local mom & pop coffee shop. try reds or muddy waters if i could give negative stars i would most definitely do it.

a starbucks is a starbucks, for the most part. the layout is almost always awkward, like the drive thru is an afterthought. the coffee is better than hotel coffee but not as good as craft. as we were staying at a hotel close by, and the hotel coffee was not palatable, we stopped by for an afternoon boost. service was friendly and drinks were accurate when served. i wouldn't make a special trip, but will certainly get the job done when you need caffeine. if i had the time, i'd have looked for a small batch local shop.

when in the area i visit this location, there is almost always a line fir the drive thru but the cashier is always super warm & inviting. the only issue i have noticed with this location is that they seem to sell out if pastries especially cake pops by the afternoon.

worst starbucks ever and iv been to many..they never greet you ..they always get your order wrong ..then when they have to remake it they're upset. i love starbucks but this location needs real help!!

this starbucks is the best starbucks in reno! i have never seemed to have a problem with parking or customer service here. there is always somewhere to sit here. the wifi always works. i go to different starbucks all the time, depending where i am at in reno. this is the best in reno.

welcome to the schoolyard, where first impressions are all that matter. you trip on your shoelaces running down the hill and forever you'll be known as the kid who rolled headfirst down the playground slope. to that end, i agree with kristine d.'s recent review of her interaction at this starbucks. if this starbucks isn't careful, they'll forever be known as the location with that one employee who finds the most basic tasks too complex to complete. his name, according to the several grumbling regulars behind me in the growing line, is alberto. he will perpetually ask you to repeat your order, to scan your phone again. it's not a bashful, flustered "sorry, i didn't hear you." it's an expectant, dismissive, "again? speak up." there is no apology in his tone, only entitlement, as if by ordering a tall coffee we are standing in the way of his efforts to do as little work as possible. it's always easy to tell when this particular employee is working register - which is all he ever seems to work - because the line will stretch twice as far as the average morning. even his coworkers seem put-out by his enervated and apathetic pressing of buttons and mumbling of orders. "so that's a grande mocha, which comes to a total of..." "no," a customer says, "i ordered a tall latte." the manager in the background rolls her eyes and offers an apologetic smile.

what the heck?! this starbucks location has now entirely blocked off, with their starbucks green cones, campbell entrance and exits! they did have the campbell exit open until now which made it convenient to leave from the drive thru window. makes for one entrance/exit off glenn which becomes quite the cluster you know what especially during busy times. im contacting city of tucson to see if that's kosher.

sterile corporate experience the remodel is all about pushing as many coffee addicted lemmings through the rabbit hole. no light to read, no community bulletin board and no comfortable chairs. get in and get out.

if i could give a negative stars on this starbucks location, i very much would. the kid behind the counter didn't get my name right, ok no big deal. i clearly and politely said no whip because it gets nasty on the bottom. the girl making i watched knowing out of their routine would put it on. sure enough, she reaches for the whip dispenser and i spoke up nicely. she gave me a death stare and plastic smile that made me want to throw my beverage at her. i hope the manager sees my post and will give all of his staff a talking to on good customer service. i could see where i would be wrong if i was demanding and rude, but i was not all. a customer should feel good about their experience. i felt nothing but like an inconvenience. i will never go to this location again.

they were very dismissive, they got my order wrong and when my husband tried to confirm our order, they dismissed him at the drive thru window. when i tried calling the phone number listed for this store, they answered the phone and said it was a cvs drive thru. the second time i called it went to an automated voice message. they did not give me a receipt or ask if i wanted one so i couldn't go back to get my correct order. i've been to this store multiple times and each time they either mess my order up or are rude. there was even one time they messed my order up and when i told them about it, they acted like they didn't. i go to starbucks regularly and i am new to the area. this is not what i expect from a starbucks in terms of customer service and food. not sure if my review will make a difference but it's just sad that we have to continue to deal with this every time.

it is a nice starbucks. the inside has some small more intimate tables as well as a big table if you want to study with a group of people. the service is friendly and the location is nice and safe. it is also open til 8 so it's not 24 hours but at least it's open most of the night.
this is the third consecutive frapp i've had here that is watery. it's literally cold chai tea with whipped cream on top.
the absolute sweetest staff on earth! i just moved in right across the street and i will definitely be here daily.

worst starbucks i have been to. they always get my drink wrong. i complaint several times and went back to give them another chance and my pink drink was red. i typically get the pink drink and if they are out of something. they still make it without saying anything. why? they just want to make the sale and don't care about customers. not going back there again.

great location! however, there are certainly some kinks to be worked out. the drive through line for me has always been prolonged and inefficient. my last two visits have taken 15 min just to place the order, to say nothing of actually receiving it. (it's my own fault for not wearing clothes i can wear in public, haha!). the baked goods are always sold out (but that makes sense based on the volume they're facing). i suggest they retain chick-fil-a's management for drive through! they always have that line hustling!
always the best service during the week in the mornings. the staff is fantastic and friendly. i go mostly to see them and they make great drinks.
don't know why this location doesn't have reviews. it's a clean shop, pretty quiet but uber quick service! and the macchiato was up to my standard!!!

as far as coffee goes it stands by its name and brand image. may be expensive but worth every penny. my favorite part is the extreme consistency they provide you. i can order a drink a 100 times from them and it will taste exactly the same 100 times straight. whereas i can order same drink 100 times from dunkin donuts and it will taste different 95 times with 15 of those times i need to force it down just so my 2.50$ doesn't go to waste. if you are looking for quality and consistency then pull out your wallet and head to starbucks

so we have been here 2 days in a row. this starbucks is very clean. the staff taking orders is very pleasant. bathrooms are a bit messy, but with the time it takes to get drinks, there must not be any time for anyone to clean the bathrooms. it honestly takes ages to get a drink. it took less time to get a drink at the extremely crowded starbucks reserve on christmas day in palm springs than it does to get simple orders at this location. again, if you are going for a pleasant interaction with a staff member and a clean seating area this is your location. but if you need to go in and use a clean bathroom and get your drink quick, you may want to reconsider your choice and go to a different starbucks location.

the sewer gas smell in the drive through lane is so powerful that if there had been an escape lane, i would have taken it. chronic problem, never gonna get fixed. i worked burn pits in the military and walked third world villages that smelled better. you've seen my dime for the last time.
my favorite sbux in stl by far. i go out of my way to go to this location before class and work. they're always extremely kind and the order is always right. jack and kahkee are absolutely great.

patio overlooks a parking lot. however, the beautiful pusch ridge overlooks that stark parking lot. the lines are typically short, since the ov is sparsely populated. it's starbucks-- you know what to expect

i'd like to find out what this building was originally. starbucks is great for repurposing buildings and turning them into coffee shops. good bunch of people work here. always plenty of table space and help. they also have a drive-through for the serious javaheads.

awesome people, great service. myself and coworkers are here just about every day for our afternoon pick-me-up. they always take care of us and we shoot the breeze like we're old friends. make sure you say hi to yon for me!

i really don't like this starbucks. they take forever, when the one just up the street on 82nd doesn't have a problem getting my coffee out in less than a minute. i just don't think it should take 15 minutes to get my coffee.

hello strange starbucks attached to the hilton! you were the closest one in proximity to me to pick up some drinks to go this morning. there wasn't much of a rush, and i was able to get in and out fairly quickly. the staff was nice enough, but not as peppy as some of the folks i've dealt with. also, it was a clean store. not sure if that's because it was early or if this location sees less traffic. if you're looking for a sbux on this side of downtown, make sure you get in early because they have more limited hours. there's free street parking nearby so you're good for up to 2 hours. nothing sucks more than having to feed a meter just to have some coffee.

let me provide you with a disclaimer that i'm not a huge fan of starbucks, so you might see some bias in this review. however i happened to find myself here while visiting with friends in the area, and we came here for an afternoon coffee, or in my case, iced tea. the parking here is atrocious, but what do you want for a business that emphasizes more on the drive through experience? we were however able to obtain a space, despite the fact that it was super busy for some reason. the inside of this place was a madhouse. it was dirty, and smelled horrible. people huddled about everywhere, standing shoulder to shoulder, and not wearing masks despite the fact that the city of tucson had passed an ordinance requiring people indoors to be masked. unfortunately, starbucks must be desperate for business since they didn't enforce the masks. there were people talking loudly on their phones, a group in the corner playing pokemon go, people hunched over their computers, and patrons in booths shouting over the din of those on their phones to the point where me, a deaf man, was able to understand what they were saying. what an awful experience to behold. we ended up going through the drive through instead (against my wishes, as i prefered to go elsewhere). however, to starbucks' credit, they completed my order accurately, and we went through the drive through much quicker than expected.

i try to avoid this location as much as possible. i love starbucks and will frequent many locations but this one is sooooo horribly slow. not sure what's going on with their management here. unfortunately it's one of the closest to me so at times i'm stuck going to it. don't go there unless you want to spend 20+ minutes.

used to be my fav starbucks location. not no more after they forgot to fill my mobile order and made me stand and wait for 20 minutes then instead apologizing & admit that they f*cked up the manager made up excuses. i really hate that! i was pissed to see the customers who ordered inside after i had arrived received their drinks first. they didn't make my drink until i inquired. turns out my order was still in the receipt register and no one bothered to pick it up. one of the manager's excuses was that they don't always hear the machine print because it's so loud. then figure out another system! another thing about this location is the new unfriendly faces. i hope other locations are better as i am a gold member.

my fiancé and i are both in school and we love coming to this starbucks because it has a lot of room and tables. i put one star because every-time we come we have to tell them to turn the music down. the workers are very loud and the music is very loud. this place is no longer our favorite spot because of how loud it is.
horrible customer service. rude. my drink is constantly not made right. they are always out of what you want but they don't tell you until after you paid.

never have i been so angry , i'm usually the customer that sees something wrong with their order and i'll just settle and leave . but this time it was way too early in the morning , and they had the audacity to argue with me . i ordered a vanilla bean iced coffee & i ordered it with caramel drizzle . my brother got off the car to get the order inside and came back , the moment i took a sip from the drink it was like sand i my mouth and i spit it out . it was just sugar . literally sugar . i tried to mix it around , it the layer of sugar was sooo thick it wouldn't mix . there were even spots that were still white because the sugar was still dry . so i get off the car and i tried to be nice i said i ordered that drink and there was just a thick layer of sugar at the bottom . and the guy at the register was polite , until some rude girl interrupted , and said "thats caramel drizzle, that's go you ordered it" um no tf i didn't ? i order caramel , not sugar . and she continued to argue that it was caramel and had just settled at the bottom . i asked her 4 times to remake the drink . i said "okay well if that's how your store makes caramel , then i don't want it can you make me a drink without it ? and she said "well it's caramel drizzle , for future reference that's how it is " . okay ? every other damn starbucks i've gone to gives me actual caramel not a bunch of literal cane sugar at the bottom of my drink . needless to say i will never return here and i hope soon they get new staff , that girl was ridiculous. bad customer service .

this is one of the better ones. service was great and the drinks were well balanced. the iced chai tea was spot on. good place to meet and chat with friends late night since their patio is well lite and you're welcomed to sit after close, but with that, this place is not open late. you have to come a bit early to snag a drink (i think they close at 10pm, not sure).

decent, inside is usually a little dirty with ok seating, staff is hit or miss. the tall tan gentleman is very friendly he should be manager. cause the female manager with red hair can often be very rude at times which shows with some of her female employees which can be even worse . definitely poor management. coffee is usually room temp cause it's old and they do the trick where you slightly lift the coffee container and trick the time to think its a new batch, doesn't work for me buddy. and they need to empty their trash more often usually it's all overflowing like the dump in wilmington . overall i wouldn't relate this store to the starbucks image cause it is a disappointment. but with a little work and more friendly attitudes it could be alot better and even reach 4-5 stars.
dirty place, counters are filthy, baristas are not attentive, all tables needed to be clean
very clean and i asked for extra whip and they gave me that. some starbucks don't. everyone is very kind and amazing drinks!

it's hard to leave bad reviews based off one experience but as i read the other reviews, there seems to be a pattern. this starbucks is beyond slow. with only 6 cars in the drive thru, we waited over 20 minutes, we waited 7 minutes before we even moved to begin with. in terms of the drinks, they're basically out of everything so i'm unable to comment on that. if i come back through here, on a day i have time to wait, i'll update the review to see if it's consistently slow.

this is my most frequented starbucks due to the fact that it is close to my office, but i also really like the staff! everyone who has helped me has been extremely kind! i'm that girl who is known to have full on conversations with the staff members when the restaurant is not too busy! my only "complaint" is that they use stickers to mark your order on your cup rather than in hand writing with a sharpie! the sharpie makes me feel special for some reason. haha!
one of the best starbucks in the area! quick and friendly service with an efficient drive thru. can't ask for much more when i'm on the go!

this place is always busy and packed but despite the busyness service is very fast and the employees are always nice and on point keeping things moving. the couch towards the back of the store is very comfy too. my only complaint is the small parking.

staff is very friendly especially jade... he's my favorite barista! he knows me by name and always prepares my order to perfection. i've visited all the local sb and hands down this one is the best. parking can be a challenge during peak times but well worth it. thank you for the great service sb @ bryn mawr

late in the day business meeting. plenty of space in this small venue for our group of 7. slow service....i mean....s-l-o-w service with a barrista who was very congenial but kept forgetting what the orders were and/or for whom. wrote the order on the cup, but not the name...and at times mixed the cups with another patron's order. the chili mocha frappucino (warm) was excellent. pastries and other finger foods that might have helped our group a bit were sparse and stale. will return if i'm ever back in the area...but hope for better.
ordered a strawberry greent tea infusion this morning，i would said it is the worst drink i have tried in my life so far. it is simply just water with little green tea flavor

i'm not a coffee drinker. imagine my confusion when i was told to buy a traveler jug of coffee and snacks for 4 people at 7 am! the night before, i stopped by this location and mentioned that i needed a jug at 7 am the next morning, but know absolutely nothing about coffee. the barista breathed a sigh of relief and thanked me for ordering the night before and elaborates on the traveler contents. the next morning, i arrived at 6:50 to pick up my goods and was out the door by 7. one barista took care of my entire order. she heated and individually packed and labelled each pastry. the coffee was brewed beforehand, but they did not fill the jug until i arrived, which is fine because my jug is fresh! they did a great job getting me out the door on time with a well organized jug. you have my business next time i visit tucson.
very nice staff, but holy moly is it slow in there. i waited 20 minutes for a simple order.
17 minutes in the drive-through ridiculous i'm going to jupiter donut to get better coffee a better service. 4:53 pm 12-21-18

i come to this starbucks a lot and usually it's good, but the past two times i've gone my order is wrong. i got an iced carmel macchiato and it looked nothing like it usually does plus there was weird white stuff at the bottom. this past time i got a cold brew with vanilla foam and it looks just like a regular iced coffee. there's no foam i don't know if it was mixed in or something but i really don't think it's what i ordered
pulled up to buy and was told i had order online. "ain't got time for that!" this store is off my list.

when you place your order online and people that have ordered after you in line get their order first, something isn't working. i ordered online and 5 people, who ordered after me, got their drinks before i did. apparently, the barista never checked for the online order. wtf?! hey starbucks! don't push online ordering unless your people are trained to check for the orders. again!!! wtf?!

on the door it says that you must have shoes and a shirt for service . unfortunately we had neither . but thanks to the great employee for coming outside and letting us know that we may come inside to order and that people do it all the time . so just incase you're like us , you can go inside .
so fast in and out! chelsea was great taking orders in line to help move the morning rush along!!! all the while with a big smile on her face.

went to get a iced tea and coffee been here multiple occasions all were we'll very fast at service very friendly if there is a slight or even chance of dissatisfaction they are happy to remake what you need with out making faces or acting as if it is an inconvenient thing for them to do. never had any issues here yet great place
super good matcha tea! gabe was extremely kind and hard working to get my drink out fast! thank you starbucks

yes, the parking is a nightmare. although, i discovered that there is parking across the street at the noble train station. that is, if you don't mind running across the highway to get to the starbucks. the baristas at jenkintown are okay. but, the staff at the starbucks in chestnut hill and flourtown are much friendlier and accommodating. they also go out of their way to remember your name and face. also, the clientele who frequent ch and flourtown are way more affable and considerate of others.
this is my favorite starbucks. the staff is always friendly with me. they're knowledgable and the drinks are consistent. i work nearby and frequent this location. i've never had a bad experience.

i was there on thursday, december 8 at 3:14 a.m. the employee, joy, was exceedingly rude and incompetent. she was apparently inconvenienced over my order of a pre-made breakfast sandwich and a latte. rather than making the latte, she handled the next customers. (it was not busy at all). finally, it was discovered that the espresso machine was malfunctioning. no apology. miserable. i regret placing $2 in the tip jar when i paid for my transaction.

this location is super convenient and the staff in the morning have always been super friendly. it's a really busy store, so you should expect a wait. my go to is a caramel macchiato or the cold brew.

today i went to the starbucks on the corner of 12th and market in philadelphia. the reason i'm giving two stars is because before i ordered i asked how the rewards program worked and said i signed up can i get it, the barista behind the counter then followed to rephrase what i just said. not giving me an actual explanation. i ordered a skinny mocha grande with whipped cream and a ham and cheese croissant. my drink took a minute to come out but that was okay because it was an extra kind of drink. when my croissant finally came out the lady just said croissant and not my name so i was confused but i finally just got it. the croissant was cold in the middle. i then asked if they could heat it up. the same lady gave me a new one but that also took a minute. by time i tried the new one it was cold it the middle, worse than the other one. i was down the street and couldn't go back because i had a reservation. the reason they get two stars is because they got the order right.

i've been on a tour of starbucks lately in our region and this one along with another could be considered a favorite. the seating is a tad less spacious than some other of the locations, but as long as it isn't a jam-packed day a person should still be able to find a nice counter-top seat by a power-outlet or a comfy chair. the employees have always been nice to me and eager to discuss what is new or popular (the flat white is an interesting addition to the starbuck's repertoire). the drive-thru is efficient at getting people in line and back out on the street at a good pace too--an important aspect for any starbuck's. overall a great place!

i have never been inside of this starbucks lol. i've only experienced drive through service which is why i'm giving 5/5 stars. i usually stop by this starbucks on my way home from holiday. it's just before the expressway on sr 54 and sun coast pkwy, which is a very convenient location. the drive through moves fast and my order has never been incorrect. kudos to the staff!
they make great mochas here! it's not all gross because it's inside a casino either, it's kept clean and the wait wasn't too long in line.
very nice people working. they went to the back to make sure they were out of something i wanted. also, made my complicated order correctly!! very pleasant.

being that i live around here, this is pretty much my go to starbucks. the inside is spacious with a lot of seats and this place is usually not busy so it is nice and quiet for you to do your work. the staff is always friendly.

this is one of about 6 starbucks locations that fall in my work territory, so i often stop here if i'm in the area and need coffee.i don't usually write reviews on corporate chains because i think they have a fairly good system in place for promoting themselves and that they have sops and protocols in place that pretty much guarantee that a latte in des moines is the same as in boston or mililani or [enter city here] without fail. the only thing i can think to comment on here is: -their bathrooms are always clean -the staff are nice -seating can be tricky -the heating system is on the cool side -the music sucks (i'm not a fan of starbucks playlists, with the exception of their holiday stuff) - drive thru is convenient but can get backed up at times

as packed as this location always is, being in the middle of a business district area, they move remarkably well. the service is as fast as it can be but if you have questions they take the time to answer them. they are kept immaculately clean and as busy as they always are there's always a seat to be found if you want to just take a moment and relax

on my way from new york back home to my new house in pittsburgh i stopped at starbucks. it was one of the few i have been to that is in a strip mall. generally most starbucks are stand alone stores. i had a venti coffee frap with an extra shot and whipped cream. the drink was made perfectly and tasted great. the baristas were fast and friendly and if in the area i would go again to this location. the store had charming decor and plenty of seating. definitely recommend this starbucks if you are in the area.
these guys are awesome! super friendly and super accommodating for unusual requests! the manager here rocks! thank you!

i avoid this starbucks at all cost. i've given them so many chances and am always disappointed. they don't update their menu on the app and i always prepay for a breakfast sandwich that they're out of. and then they don't say anything like i'm just going to drive away and hopefully forget about it?
love the place. only down side is the baby boomers who complain all the time. don't dare try to act like a human being, you are only seen as sub-human trash.
good standard starbuck's service as usual, with the standard menu.

normally very good service and i have been impressed many times. the service is normally spot on but today the lady that took my order was very abrupt and short. maybe i caught her on a bad day or maybe she was just in the weeds but it doesn't. take too long to smile and be a bit more personable.

i'm conflicted about the stars... i don't know if i should give three stars or four stars! i'm not a coffee drinker but the 1st time i was in here; it was great. today... not so much! i'm going to go off this trip for the stars but explain both trips. now the 1st trip it was overly crowded and i was definitely confused on how to order. the guy explained the sizes to me and even helped me figure out the drink i wanted. which was a strawberry refresher, no water, coconut milk, whipped cream and light ice. he was extremely patient, helpful and he is the reason why i came back. now today, it's late in the evening, fairly empty. this should be a breeze. i forgot the sizes and didn't want to say small, medium or large. nor did i see the sizes on the menu. so i ask, what are the sizes? she said "small, medium or large". i'm confused... i thought you all didn't say that!?! she gave me a look and ask what i needed. uummm okay! still very confused. this is starbucks... right?! i placed my small order for a strawberry refresher, no water, coconut milk, whipped cream and light ice. i did say light ice two times. ima say they don't understand what light ice is! the ice was filled to the top of the small cup. so i might've had 3 sips of nothing! ugh! ok. besides this mishap of ice vs light ice. it's a really small location, pretty quite in the late evening, clean and plenty of seats inside and out. at the end of the day... yea. i will go back but asking for no ice... maybe i'll get light ice!
great spot for morning breakfast and coffee. usually very busy but quick moving. overall good service and drive through usually moves well.

all starbucks are not the same. this one is top notch! the staff is very quick and efficient, and has never made a mistake in my app orders... not even once. and after a few days, they even greeted me by name when they realized which order matched me. honestly, this is the best starbucks i've visited in a long while. very happy customer.

drive thru! drive thru! i could end the review there. despite the obvious fact that i love the drive thru my items are always accurate and that sweet drive thru is typically quick. customer service is standard starbucks, no additional frills and i'm ok with that!

it's a starbucks whats not to love. even my auto spell has it memorized and will correct me if i don't capitalize the "s". this location has fast service and plenty of seating. it even has a drive through window for those on the go. 4 stars because i was terrified of the wasp flying around near my table that day.

i love starbucks, but i absolutely hate this one. i have tried to give them a few different chances, thinking that maybe they were just having an off day. but, that's not the case. the employees seem to have their little clique of customers they like. the rest of us they don't care. they don't seem to make the drinks correctly either. the place is always dirty. the counters need cleaned. it's the closest one to us and i will still continue to drive 35 minutes to go to another one. i have lived in other cities and been to many different starbucks. this one is horrible.

this is easily one of my favorite starbucks. whenever i order via phone, my drink is always ready with no mistakes. the staff is pleasant and they work quickly. also, i adore the location since it is near where i work, and it is always on the cleaner side considering that some many students hang out there. although the students are well behaved and quite pleasant, i don't think i'll be there working.

wow, they cant properly fill a cup of coffee.. unless filled to the tiptop as high as possible. the temp of the coffee is also way too high, mcdonalds burn your genitalia's hot. not to mention slow like molasses. terrible.

almost every time (maybe 8+ times) i have gone into this starbucks, the staff seems less than interested in helping me or anyone, they are always chatting with each other or attending to other work seeming related things, but leaving the register unattended. one employee even snapped at me bc i didn't understand that adding a shot of vanilla to something meant it was 'sweetened', dumb i know, but i thought sweetened meant adding simple syrup. live and learn. atmosphere is usually pretty good, big tables for work and play. it's your typical starbucks experience, just not nearly as friendly.
this is a super normal starbucks. nothing really pro or con except he outdoor seating is quite nice.
i went to ruby slipper and sydney's hospitality and irish coffee was bomb asf! i want to thank you for hiring her and giving me an outstanding experience!

alright, where to start.... i love starbucks. i usually visit them in a daily basis to procure my morning coffee and on rare occasions food. however, their corporate model to prioritize mobile orders over in-store orders is atrocious. why? because putting mobile orders first, for individuals not physically in store create a bad customer perception. there is nothing worse than sitting around watching mobile orders stack for people who have not made it to the store yet, while people like me who are currently in the store are forced to wait. i hope that this model eventually changes.

staff were friendly , this location is one of 2 remaining starbucks with a drive through in the area. so dad that most of the others are inside stores like albertsons or target now and that's super inconvenient when you have a car full of kids or running to work and need to grab a cup and go. the only reason we stopped here was that we were at a neighborhood business. cafe mojo was opening next week down the street. i ordered 2 drinks a tall pumpkin spice latte with an extra shot and a vanilla latte with 1/2 toffee nut syrup and 1//2 vanilla ,whip and caramel drizzle. they charged me $1.00 extra for both drinks so just under $10 one drink was ready and was not full all the way and the other they forgot about so i reminded them and then i checked it and it was not made as requested. it was also sitting in their drive thru window line and i was inside? not that big of a deal, but honestly they were not that busy ! i would've asked a customer standing waiting if they wanted something else or if they were waiting on something ? i left and we both tried our drinks ( my daughter who has been a barrista) immediately said this is not even hot enough they must have someone there in training , what a big dissatisfaction and waste of $10 definitely not consistent with other locations. you would think that due to so many of the locations closing and all the cute little boutique trendy hipsters coffee shops in this town that are booming, that the starbucks crew would be on their game.
getting a 5 star here. super nice atmosphere. it's also very neat and clean! polite baristas and delicious drinks.! no complaints on my end i'll definitely be back to this location

wow! some of the most unengaged and self absorbed staff i've ever seen at a starbucks in my life! and, that's been awhile. they'd rather play "where am i going tonight" than help someone looking for serious caffeine. oh, and that lack of attention shows in the bathroom facilities. i mean, beyond... i'll tell you that my mochas were perfect though. but, i attribute that more to my suggestion of adding two mocha shots. it almost made me wish they were automatons, programmed to give a shit about the only customer in line waiting for someone to acknowledge them. signed, not all starbucks are the same

horrible service. went to this location, ordered a white mocha frap with soy, and they gave me a white chocoloate frap with regular milk.... i even asked the "barista" is this soy 3 times, and he said yes every single time. the almond milk latte tasted like absolute garbage as well. shift supervisor was super rude when i called in to complain about this. avoid this location like the plague. the regular milk white chocolate taste like crap, there was no coffee in it, and it gave me the skitters because i am lactose and intolerant. i even asked 3 times and the fool lied to me. what a joke, avoid

there are a couple of nice workers but the manager is a bitchy slug of a human being who shouldn't work in any restaurant or any other customer service based industry. all i wanted was a water and she told me to get it myself and then proceeded to roll her eyes and grunt when i asked for a cup. you work at a starbucks bitch, that's not my fault you're miserable i just wanted a drink ‍

i want to give 0 stars! this was by far the worst starbucks i've ever been to! i wanted a drink before my class so i go in and there are 2 baristas working. no one in line and one person waiting for their drink. one of the baristas turns around to see me, looks at her other person and says she's going to break. and leaves. the other girl continues doing whatever she's doing. 5 minutes later she finally turns around and takes my order. that was okay but the other barista came back and they started arguing over something. the barista left again and then the one who stayed just started doing dishes! while me and this other girl where waiting and a line began forming. super annoying. i ended up leaving because i was going to be late for class. so the entire thing was a waist of time and money. super upset:(

eh, this starbucks is hit or miss. unlike some of the other reviewers here, i've typically had pleasant interactions with the staff as it pertains to personality, but the wait times here are tremendous. they're so slow that the traffic gets backed up well into the street, which, during a morning commute in brentwood, is very frustrating. in short, this is a fine place to go on a slow saturday morning when you can go inside and have time to wait, but stay as far away as possible if you'r eon any sort of schedule.

fyi: though this starbucks sells starbucks coffee, it does not accept the starbucks rewards members card or coupons. there's another starbucks in the macy's across the street on (market) or one at 15th and market or one at 13th and chestnut that you can check out so you can still get your points on your sb card :)

it is your typical starbucks in appearance, so i won't go into that. the employees were nice, occasionally there were some conversations behind the counter that i was taken aback by, which actually surprised me. but my mocha was good and i was able to snag a nice comfy chair with a foot rest so i could kick back while writing on my tablet. the only bad thing about this location is that it was my first time there and i passed by it on manchester the first time. i had to turn on my gps to figure out i had already passed it and turn around. so careful if you aren't paying attention (or to the wrong side of the road, look to the south) then you'll miss it.

i used the drive thru to get a cup of coffee and a breakfast sandwich. it took quite a while to get through the line of 4-5 cars. when i got my order i found out that the sandwich was the wrong one. good thing is, it was still a tasty sandwich.

i wandered into this starbucks because my car was in an auto shop nearby. the service was excellent, particularly the barista patrick, who was friendly, helpful and made me feel great even when i couldn't quite decide what i wanted and then realized i had left my wallet in the car. barista's like him make me feel really comfortable in a cafe. i plan to take advantage of the lovely service and relaxed atmosphere again later in the week.

this is the first time i've been in the starbucks and i've been around for a long time. i just never make it to this little stretch of west chester pike. but since i discovered the bagel place down the road i figured i'd stop in for coffee to go with my my bagel. the service here is not great. they're not rude but they're not really friendly and they are kind of slow. however i have to say that i got one of the best starbucks cappuccino that i've had in a long time. for that reason i gave these folks at four-star rather than a three star rating. parking here is definitely a lot easier than it is at the other havertown starbucks. i don't think this gets as much traffic plus they share a parking lot with the pretzel company so i think that has a lot to do with it.

this place is kind of crap. first of all, the line moves incredibly slowly, in part because they make everyone sign for every transaction, even if it's just for $2. second, they seemed to be out of a lot of things, including basics like light-roast filter coffee, whole milk, and unbleached sugar. it took me 10 minutes to get a cup of drip coffee, even though there were only four people in front of me. there were two people working behind the counter.

in the past week this location has been extremely poorly managed. twenty people wait in line for mobile orders with one dedicated barista while those coming in front door are served by a dedicated barista immediately. dont go to this location!!
relaxing place, i pass everyday. i enjoy the coffee and the people there are really nice.
friendly and fast during a rush. everyone was hustling and they kept the line moving.

this starbucks serves a purpose larger than keeping me well caffeinated... and providing me exercise opportunities (it's within walking distance). but i will discuss that after talking about the reasons people read reviews. it's a starbucks. if you don't like starbucks you won't like this one. i am not going to ding stars for what is and will always be a personal preference (for taste reasons or otherwise). this starbucks has a drive through. it is also a bit larger yet smaller feeling. by that i mean the baristas have a lot more ground to cover, i do not mean there is plenty of room for your massive stroller. sorry but there just isn't. the pathway from ordering to seating corral (there is seating along the way) is just not that accommodating due to displays and that aforementioned seating along the way the associates here will remember your name. i find that fifty fifty with starbucks' employees. they also won't complain if you say the size before iced (one of the baristas at a st louis starbucks told me i was wasting her time by ordering in the incorrect order - yikes). however, they are a bit slower. you can be standing at the register for awhile... there tends to be longer lines. they never have spoons ready (they then have to dig through boxes). but i will accept that and be cool with that as the people are pleasant... and i have learned that rarer amongst drive thru starbucks places. i have never used the drive thru, so can't comment on that. just the people and the service. oh and that thing starbucks brings to an area that isn't caffeination? it brings some weird sense that an area is "okay." i don't prescribe to that, but rising home values after it being built show people do. i think people say to themselves "it must be a nice(ish) area is starbucks would build there" the sociologist in me says that's gentrification in its finest.
this new location is clean and pleasant, but the indoor chairs are unsuitable for larger guests, who have to hope for a comfy seat or go outside and hope for better.
use to like this starbucks but they have went downhill. always getting orders wrong. speak to customers in a condescending tone. very disappointed. do better.

this has to be the slowest starbucks i have ever been to. it is really not that hard to make a drink. they are either vastly understaffed or incompetent i waited in the drive thru for 30 minutes, which is insane. the starbucks near cold stone creamery was so much better, had a higher quality staff and were much faster and friendlier. whoever owns this starbucks needs to start over with new staff, because these people are running this starbucks into the ground.

isla vista starbucks is notoriously trash. never in my life have i received so much confusion from a drunk consisting of two ingredients, milk and an espresso shot. not crazy, right? that's what i thought, until i made the mistake of paying for it and then tasting it. i also got a chicken caprese, but given the general state of things here i honestly expect ecoli from it.
long wait lines and order was wrong. i think i would prefer to go to the smaller coffee shop down the street that cares about my buissiness
this sbux is always busy and loud, but they made a satisfying double espresso for me and the line didn't take long to get through. i definitely prefer the sbux a half a block away on chestnut.

i love starbucks in wesley chapel, florida on bruce b downs. stranded for a place to go and everyone is closed ...the bright green lights shine from the highway and soon you are sitting in a warm cafe with lots of people using computers, drinking hot beverages and eating food. also....i can buy newspapers here from new york! a great place, great staff and great chai tea!!! see you there.

i love starbucks, but this location has the most inconsistent output in terms of taste. i don't know if it's just because they are always so busy or what, but i have 3 standard drinks i rotate and they never taste the same, nor do they taste quite right. they do have very friendly staff, but be warned the lines are often long and service slow.

came into the store and got 3 drinks, 2 apple juices, 4 pastries and reload my app. the cashier was very friendly and patient with us. great experience for a friday evening at 7pm. drinks: tall flat white, tall decaf mocha, and venti caramel mocha pastries: regular croissant, everything croissant (so good), chocolate croissant (all time fav) and chocolate chunk cookie.
what i expect from a starbucks. fast, friendly, clean, and a great coffee! and drive thru for those on the go, was quick. close by several hotels, ours included. yup, going back there tomorrow to.

love starbucks! i go close to every day. excellent service, but they are slower during certain times of the day. if they could find the groove like they have in the mornings, they'd have 5 stars from me. unfortunately, i've experienced the slow service during dinner and later at night and that's when they seem to be the least busy.

this starbucks gets me the jolt of energy i need before i head in to usf for meetings. there are always students studying useful stuff like the anatomy of sharks or differential equations. it brings back memories of cramming facts in to my brain in order to extract them an hour or two later. too bad starbucks didn't exist when i went to school but somehow, i survived without it. considering this location is pretty busy and parking can be a challenge, the staff always seems to keep their smile on which is appreciated.

leaving nashville and heading north, we wanted to grab a coffee before we went too far and had to wait until we hit bowling green, ky. while a turn or two away from the highway, this starbucks was easy to get to and get back on the highway from. it's got a nice private parking lot but it shares a structure with a minute clinic which was a first for me. the staff were nice and answered my questions about some new items easily. the menu and set up was pretty standard for starbucks - minus the fact that the pick-up counter is all the way at the back by the bathrooms. you wait way at the back of the shop and then have to wade through all the shop and the checkout queue which is kind of odd and could be really challenging for a wheelchair user, customers with kids, or someone trying to grab a seat, work, and grab their coffee when it's ready. .

this location is always absurdly busy in the mornings. this is the main reason i avoid it - even though it's on my way to work. typically i'll stop off at a local coffee shop on the westbank, or the marrero starbucks, if i need a caffeine fix before work. it would actually better for me if i purchased my coffee on the eastbank, so that i would still have some left upon arriving at work... but here's why it's not going to happen: if you wait in the drive thru, you're probably looking at a 10-15 minute wait on any given weekday morning during rush hour. go inside? add on at least another 5 minutes. i waited 20 mins from the time i walked in the door to the time i got my latte on friday (needed some extra caffeine after muses the night before). it's not that the baristas are slow. they actually crank out a lot of espresso at a fair pace. they're friendly, too, which is why this gets 2 stars instead of 1. if it weren't for the service, this would defintiely be a 1 star experience. i can't see recommending a 10-20 minute wait for the same beverage you can get at any of their other approximately 28196291 locations worldwide. granted, i'm sure the wait time is significantly reduced at any other time during the day... but the only time i ever *want* a $5 coffee is first thing in the morning.

being located right across the street from the business school and law school is definitely a blessing and a curse for this starbucks. it is always always crowded. which is good for business, but not so good if you want to get in and out quickly. lots of seating, not a lot of parking. the barristas are friendly, if a bit frazzled.
i love this starbucks. they always get my order right, and they remember me when i come in. great place to hang out too if you need to catch up on some business or socialize.
good coffee. generally crowded and a bit noisy.

fortunately i work a very short walk from this place. i come here atleast 4 times a week and everyone is so friendly and service is quick. sometimes it gets really busy, but i just end up leaving and coming back 10 mins later

i like this place it's very quiet and beautiful, i visit regularly because of the less-people. not a lot of people know this location it's under looked. it's place to be if you are looking cool starbucks.

i'm a huge fan of this starbucks. it's the closest one to where i live, and the workers there are always friendly. i always feel like i'm talking to an old friend, even though i typically only go a few times a year (i can't stay away from the pumpkin spice frap...yum!) i've been through the drive thru a few times, and they have doggy treats in the window for your furry friends. how sweet! :)

two to three times a day customer, i don't visit this location often. every time i have come here they are out of the product i want. from berries in a drink to yogurt. staff is very professional and courteous, management dropping the ball.

make my 3 drinks all wrong...... pink drink's color and salty cream taste are all different，feel so angry！！ i will not pick in this store again！not professional，they don't know how to make them in right way.

there is almost always a wait here. the staff here are hit or miss, but mostly nice. they always have at least three people working the bar, but they still run behind. my venti iced latte was good, as it usually is. they also carry hot breakfast sandwiches, paninis, and salads, along with the usual bakery items.

what can i say about starbucks that hasnt already been said? well, this place had a really cool and friendly vibe that made me want to stick around a while and enjoy my slightly burt tasting coffee:-)
usually service is crazy slow but it was better today. however my bagel was stale and the server who toasted it had a major attitude. maybe she knew she was serving me a 2-day old bagel?

so i go in on sunday at around 9:30am. not really busy as there was only one person in front of me at the register. of course this person was an employee of starbucks and the two behind the register seem to be having some issues ringing him up (with his discounts and all). everyone else behind the counter seemed to be standing around talking with one another. though this was the least of my issues....now i have never made gourmet coffees myself, so i am no expert on how hard it is to make some of these crazy drinks....but i find it hard to screw up a simple iced latte. i mean espresso and milk with some ice. did i miss anything?? if anyone thinks i wrong in this "assumption", then please let me know and explain to me what i am missing. my iced latte tasted watered down with no coffee flavor at all. and it didn't even taste like milk. you would think with only two ingredients that you would have some sort of taste from either of them, but not the case. so disappointed in this starbucks location. my recommendation...would be to find another location or different coffee shop.

this starbucks is slow and cramped. they also do not have a drive-through which is annoying. the barista's are so talkative, they cannot focus and they are super rude to each other. every morning, i hear one of the saying " oh, is this your drink, you better put it up." i am thinking, "are y'all really playing this game with 15 people in line and they are more enthralled with the drama of which employee sits their coffee beside them. who f ing cares? they also will make the regular brewed coffee before anything blended, so if you order a frap, you better take a seat, it's gonna be 10-15 minutes. i see people leaving line or complaining about being late for work every single morning. this starbucks will slow you down for sure!
the music is always too loud to have a relaxing conversation while enjoying my drink. always seem short staffed and uptight. great location and patio.

what is the deal with this location?!? while the baristas are always nice & polite and my order is usually correct, i am flabbergasted every time i come here by how long it takes to get through it. inside or drive through, it amazes me every time. it's the only one on my way to work however, soooooo.....

if i could give zero stars, this location deserves zero. you really can't screw up starbucks coffee, but when you have 7 employees working and every single one of them moving as slow as humanly possible - 20 mins to stand in line and make a latte is ridiculous.
an interestingly shaped starbucks location in a strip mall. there is good amounts of seating here, but service is slow.

love this starbucks because there is an outlet wherever you sit. if you sit at the community table or at the bar, the outlets are right under the table/bar, facing down. outlets are on the wall for the rest of the seats. of course, it wouldn't be starbucks if the temperature isn't at freezing, so bring a jacket. there are also lots of seating outside. the baristas here are nice and very accommodating.
love the drive thru at this location. coffee is alwasy what i'm expecting!

this starbucks is conveniently located nearby and has a drive thru yet every time i go there the line is always way too long inside and out and the baristas constantly mess up my drink, and it's not even that complicated. i'm always disappointed and shocked they don't recognize me or my order for how often i go there.

i can always count on this starbucks location to be timely and to make what i'm feeling like that day, even though sometimes i don't know what that is myself. this morning, i was really wanting my go-to hot bevie, but it's technically out of season. "not here, my friend," said the barista today... though not in those exact words. :)

ordered a strawberry funnel cake frappuccino. the barista wiped some errant whipped cream off the bubble top with a dirty rag and handed me the drink. she then proceeded to wipe the counter in front of me with the same dirty, wet rag. gross. emailed starbucks and was credited $10. greeeeat. there's a starbucks a couple of block down, across the street that doesn't contaminate your drinks and let me use points.

really nice people but their breakfast pastries are foul. i've given them a couple of chances but just drew the line when my 'blueberry muffin' came out. it was literally tasteless and so greasy you could barely eat it. i'm not sure you could count this as food.

huge upgrade from the starbucks experience we had at the location in harrah's. our drinks were correct, tasted delicious, and they were so very nice. they were busy, but that's to be expected because of their location. it was strange to have to enter a code on the bathroom door to be able to use the facilities. i thought that was strange given the public ridicule starbucks recently received. a public apology from the ceo recently about how starbucks was started with the idea of people being able to come and rest, visit with friends, etc. here is a thought, don't lock bathrooms.
there is a 25minute wait every time i go through the drive through and 9 times out of 10 they get my drink wrong. the only reason i go back is because it's close to my college.

the only reason this location doesn't have 5/5 is because every night i come in, it's the same manager. my experience at starbucks in the morning and afternoons are always pleasant but as soon as i see the short bearded manager, i instantly want to turn around. i came in this last wednesday just to get some quick drinks and the entire time, he wasn't friendly at all and honestly looked like he hated being there. it wasn't good customer service at all. i could hear him actually cussing! with kids in the store and everything! it was very unprofessional of him but what do you expect anymore when he always seems to be in a poor mood. a managers job is to overlook and hold people accountable but if he can't even watch his own actions, then i'll steer clear of his nights. if he can't even watch his mouth, how do i know he can make my drinks in a safe way? i haven't seen much of the other shift. he was always super nice and smiling! he needs to work more nights so the angry gremlin can get his beauty rest or hit the bars. he seems like he needs it. lol guess i'm back to being a morning person at this location.
no line, clean and efficient starbucks. i find it spacious compared to what we have in ny and they also have a large parking.

i have only had two drinks from here, and both of the drinks tasted horrible. i was so disappointed because i get the same drinks from every starbucks. there is nothing like sipping bad coffee especially when the coffee was needed.

i'm giving this starbucks 5 stars based on the cool staff and the clover machine. no pour overs here, they use the clover machine which is pretty amazing!! the clover is a reverse french press and it makes an amazing cup of coffee!!! perfect extraction every time, and now you can pick whatever single origin coffee you want instead of being limited to 3. this is what coffee culture is about!!! i recommend grabbing a coffee and walking down the street to the bookstore!!

the location is clean. the staff is friendly. my drink has never been made incorrectly. there's a drive thru--but the wait is usually much shorter inside. speaking of the inside, that is my only problem with this place. it echoes tremendously. when the metal chairs grind against the concrete floor, it's awful!

as far as i'm concerned this is the best starbucks on my commute. i pass three and a fourth is a touch past my office. i used to go with my mood and chose whatever one looked the least busy but at 6:30 am none of them were terribly busy. this particular one has made one mistake in approximately 200 visits. that's a darn fine record. this one has a drive through and some comfy chairs and a sofa as well as tons of tables of varying size so there's ample seating. service is very good as is the quality. i spend $4.61 five days a week and almost everyone knows what i'm ordering, my name and no room. so why not 5 stars? the parking lot is a nightmare. if you end up parking just as it gets busy good luck getting out. it's set up in a way that the drive thru when busy can literally prevent every single parked car from getting out. i guess this used to be a drive thru restaurant so they were dealt a bad hand to begin with. oh i just wanted to address the speed issue that seems to ne a recurrence in reviews. generally i think they do fine at this location. i do see one particular "crew" that when they work are significantly slower than other "crews" but it looks to me to be due to not working their strengths not because they are doing anything wrong. btw love ana and sue. they are always cheerful.
