no real complaints about this location as far as the drinks or service. it's just always incredibly packed. you're lucky to find a seat when you go in. best to get your order to go!

i went into this starbucks last evening, to use the restroom. the doors to the restroom were open but i was told the restrooms were closed. i asked if i purchased an item, as a customer, i could use the restrooms and was told i could not. i asked how it was determined that the restrooms were closed, and the barista said he or she determines whether the restrooms were closed and they were closed to me. i went out the door, but promptly returned to ask another question and there was a child coming from the restroom. my only guess, since the store was still open and still serving customers, and a child went in after i was told i could not, is that my brown skin played a part in the decision to deny my use of the restrooms. i intend to follow this up to the highest corporate management. i won't use the barrista's name here but i will use it in every letter and email i write and every phone call i make. this type of response to anyone who walks in a starbucks is humiliating, and is completely unacceptable.

the only starbucks i will go to on lancaster ave. the service is always great and the drive thru moves fast in the morning. orders are almost always correct and the staff is very friendly! great place to study, or grab a fast morning coffee.

one of the fastest most efficient starbucks in indy. i may my use the drive thru and at times the line extends out into capitol. they are so quick though you move right on through. fast and professional. one of the top here.

along with my order, i asked for a separate water. while waiting, i noticed the barista clean her fingers in the water as it was pouring from the tap directly into the cup. not sure what her motives were, but its quite disgusting as well as disrespectful. especially because everything she is doing can be viewed by waiting customers.
this place is a homeless shelter, just happy my local starbucks is not like this or i would never go again.

please get staff better acquainted with menu items! every other day it seems like my drink is messed up and i never have time to change it because i'm on my way to work! i was so excited for this drive thru but i think ima start going back to magazine street
they just can't get it right in the drive threw! every time we go there they either forget the sugar completely or no cream! mind you we are asking for light and sweet coffee!

i really like this starbucks. everyone is generally very friendly and the drinks are always good. my only real qualm is drive through speed can be a little slow. all in all, it's still my favorite sbux in the area.

#218 been here lots of times and they have never got my drink wrong...except for today. it wasn't exactly what i ordered but pretty close and actually was very drinkable. i used to drink starbucks almost everyday but the cost is now prohibitive. this is a good location no major issues.

i frequent this starbucks location. generally quick & friendly service (even from the revolving door of baristas) as i always order through the mobile app before i arrive. docking one star because the women's bathroom perpetually smells like the pachyderm exhibit at a zoo. yuck.

ordered a cold brew. handed me a warm one with zero ice. asked if they could add some ice so that it was at least somewhat cold (since you know, it's a cold brew). she poured out 80% of the coffee and stuffed the rest full of ice. awesome. thanks. i'll enjoy this $4.91 cup of ice. hopefully it helps me get through the rest of my da
mr. paul was very unprofessional by telling "idk what does the sticker say". when i asked if the order pick-up drink was mine.
some of the employees are very rude. no customer service skills. there are a few girls there that are very nice and cheerful. i wish they were all like that!

this starbucks is my regular haunt almost daily. it is a great place to start the day. the staff of baristas is great! they all greet me by name...they all all know exactly what i get and have made before i can get through the checkout line. this location always busy....always clean and always a joy to visit.

small starbucks open 24/7 on the rest area stop. either first or last rest stop area on the turnpike depending on which direction tore traveling. i wanted one dark and one blonde roast but barista told me all they had was pike. mind you it was 6.30am. i was very disappointed. bakery items looked good & fresh and the pike was ok.

clean and with good service. everything is as you would expect. they are not open very late. there are only limited places to plug in your laptop. you could close your eyes, open them again, and be in any starbucks in the area. 100% generic.

update: still terrible and my drink always has too much cream smh... the manager that is there in the mornings (light skin african american) is to blame for how terrible things are in the mornings... she looks miserable and pissed at all times and treats the staff miserably... those girls are never smiling and it's cus she talks to them like they are 12... no constructive criticism or actual managing... just barking out orders with soooo much attitude... i've literally traveled the world and have never been to a more depressing starbucks in my life... once you bring in a manager that isn't miserable and retrain the staff they'll have a shot... i sure hope it's soon smh

it is not the best coffee in town, but it sure is consistent. i love the fact that i can walk into any starbucks in town and get something that i enjoy. my drink of choice is a tall vanilla bean frappuccino with a shot of espresso, or a grande with two shots if i want a boost. give it a shot, it is delicious! anyway, i like this location, check it out!

this is the worst starbucks in this area. this is the second time they were out of everything we wanted. no cream cheese, no blonde roast, no bantom bagels. like really. and it was only 1150 am. they had to go all day.

wonderful starbucks location. the staff is great and always friendly. i mostly go through the drive through and the employees are always sweet and funny. my favorite was when i asked if they made a different type of drink and the response was "heck ya, and it's super yummie" this response made my son and i giggle. nice job!

every morning i get a venti vanilla latte and one out of three times it doesn't have any vanilla in it. when they make it right i enjoy it and have a pleasant experience at this location. but i'm done getting up early to spend $4.95 on an undrinkable beverage before work.

i'm here a few times a week. i would like to give it a higher star rating, but due to the service issues, i am giving it a two. the drinks here are always on point. the lines are long and they rarely are running both registers. on a regular basis there is something missing at the customer prep bar: missing cream, sugar, stir sticks, stoppers.

"cookie monster," or the incredible drive through employee formally known as ryan, made my starbucks visit sunday absolutely spectacular. he was funny, kind, quick and turned an otherwise average visit to starbucks to above and beyond. i believe it's employees like him that make a difference in someone's day by spreading joy. thank you ryan - coooooookies!

one star because they're there. sucks the big one. across the street from my job, and was so excited because the one by usf is awesome. seeing the drive thru had such a long line, i went inside-for quicker service, ya know. but, they had me wait for my order until everyone in the drive thru was served. really?! well, i did, i did.... i threw a hissy fit. what's the use? i'll stop at mambo's. cheaper, faster, tastes better. hey starbucks mlk! you suck!

easily the friendliest starbucks i've ever been to! everything is as you'd expect from a starbucks, so no magical coffee - but i definitely appreciate the kind and friendly staff before my work commute!

went in to get an iced latte and piece of lemon pound cake. asked the cashier if the cake was fresh she said it was, which was not true. i then watched as 6 people who ordered after me got their beverages, some of which were quite more elaborate than mine, were given their beverages. as i was the only caucasian in in the place, including both employees and customers, one has to wonder if there was any racial overtones involved here. at any rate, i won't be returning to this store.
the staff was friendly and the service was fast! the location is conveniently located in the hotel lobby which is great unless there is a large conference or event going on at the hotel.

honestly, i came to starbucks to study. i ordered a regular s'mores frappuccino that took some time because i think the barista forgot my order for awhile so they made it a bit late, but they thanked me for my patience. starbucks has outdoor and indoor seating available. there are sofas, a long table near the window and at the center, and bunch of small tables. there are outlets available near sofas and some small tables but definitely outlets available right under the table near the window. it is a good place to study if you don't mind some background talk. and yes, the frappuccino was good :)

this is a new starbucks in nj on route 38. it's right. txt door to pjs and across the street from the morristown mall. it's very clean and the staff are pretty awesome. i strolled in around 9:30 on a saturday evening after going to a banquet. i wasn't sure what i needed but i ended up with my go to drink a big passion tea. they close at 10pm in saturday evenings and has already began cleaning. the bathrooms were clean and i would recommend this location.

i've visited this starbucks 4 times now and each time something has gone wrong with my order. two times were mobile orders that were not ready when i went to pick them up. the app gives an estimated time, usually of 3-7 minutes, and i pad that time with 3 additional minutes to ensure it will be ready when i'm there...and both times my orders were not ready more than 10 minutes after placing the order. the next time was a walk in order where i wasn't served for more than 5 minutes as i watched an employee put out new mats and two others making drinks for the busy drive through. when i did receive my drink, it was made incorrectly and it took another couple of minutes for the busy barista to have time to remake it. the fourth time was a drive through order and both of my drinks did not have sweetener in them despite my specifically ordering them sweetened. i'm sure a lot of this poor service has to do with adjusting to a new location with a drive through but i can't say that i'll be returning to this location with any regularity.
this is not a starbucks. if this were a starbucks, then i could pay for my coffee with my starbucks stars. as i can't, it's rather a waste of time and effort, and false advertising.

this was the first time going to this starbucks and i have never recieved a poorly drink until today. i ordered a chai latte and my husband got a white chocolate mocha. as we drove away i opened my cup and saw my drink was barely filled 3/4 of the cup not to mention most of it was foam. i decided to check my husband drink to see that his cup was only half filled and the remaining space was just whipped cream. i was seriously disappointed. if we weren't already on our way back home to clarksville i would have seriously asked for a refund because messing up a drink that bad twice isn't worth trying to remake.

the starbucks was small and the variety of items was even smaller. it was also very busy there. the staff did not seem to be fluent in english and wasn't the nicest. on top of that, they also got our simple order wrong.

great service, etc. but when i sit outside i get this desolate feeling of lost-in-suburbia. i typically go on sunday mornings and folks come in with the line occasionally creeping out the doorway. the paintings and photos inside are inspiring. the music is ok, usually. my guess is that it depends on what you're used to while sitting outside. my complaint is that it faces the parking lot so all you see are parked cars, as well as a major car intersection, i.e. lindbergh & clayton roads.

the last four years in santa barbara have been filled with late night papers and early morning exams. this starbucks helped me through each one. what a great environment and the partners working there are amazing! coffee is quality, environment is great, and staff is friendly -- what more could you ask? unfortunately, because it relies on westmont college for a lot of its customers and even employees, the turnover in employees can seem a bit high. but it manages to stay high quality despite the constant adjustments. supposedly there is a huge revamp coming soon! i'm excited to see it.

i guess it was ok, i am just use to the staff at my usual sb's remembering me. spoiled yes. the staff was nice, and the coffee exceptional. i paid with my iphone app. that's always fun. i will go back to my usual starbucks from now on.

i have to say i've developed quite the starbucks addiction lately (i blame the baby-she loves soy chai lattes!) and this starbucks has really been pulling through. the service at the drive thru and inside is always fast and super friendly. top this all off with the perfect drink and food order each time and you've got yourself a great start to the morning!
reliable, quality drinks. comfortable atmosphere. parking can be tough to find at times.

girl in drive through window dropped the two quarters i put in her hands, asked me to get it for her when clearly i had no room to open my car door but i attempted.. ended up just giving her more quarters i had instead. she was not very friendly. i asked for my frap to specifically be made thick with extra ice and it was liquid):

this is a pretty solid starbucks w a great staff. they have never gotten my drink order wrong. the restaurant for the most part is always clean. my one huge complaint about this starbucks is the entrance to the drive up. it tends to become a cluster bc it has 2 ways in which clogs the exit and people will not let you pass to exit nor will they let you alternate to get into the line. starbucks people are very possessive about their place in the line.

i love this starbucks. the seating is big and there are plenty of outlets for homework and bloggers like myself. the staff is friendly and happy to serve. it's 15 mins from my place but it's worth the drive!

i come to this starbucks cuz it's on my way to work. if i don't get my coffee fix i can be a real jerk! the baristas are always friendly whether it's drive-thru or inside. my coffee is always perfect so i can continue on my ride. the parking can be iffy since panera shares the space. but if i'm in need of strong coffee, starbucks is my place!
service was absolutely terrible spent. spent 10 minutes being ignored by a staff before someone offered to take my order.

normally this place is good however they must have been rushing. i ordered a egg white spinach wrap from drive through which i love and it was still very cold in the middle almost frozen. i would've wrote it off as a one off however my spouses was the same. a little disappointed because it's off of the highway and once we got on the road we couldn't go back. the coffee was good.
easily the worst starbucks you'll ever visit. staff is incompetent and detached.
one time they gave me a free frappuccino just because there was a long line at the drive thru. it was a pretty good frappuccino.

i want to first start off day that this store and the employees there are very revolting. the bathrooms were dirty, the bar was super messy and i being the customer seeing that makes me not want to go there ever. i have went there only one time and will never go again due to the fact that there are very rude and unprofessional employees there. this one employee made a huge big deal behind my back and a friend behind our backs. this employee name is xander. he has had made various accusations about me and my friend having a fetish with transgenders. first off that is very untrue and that we had no idea this employee was transgender. he should be employed at this store or any starbucks. he has been so rude at the times when me and my friend was there. aside from that the store was very comfortable to do some studying but besides that xander should not ever be employed at any starbucks. he has worked at the tyrone starbucks and had accused everyone at the tyrone store that they were out to get him. i hope someone sees this and have a talk with this person. warning!!!!! if you go to this starbucks please be careful he may bring something upon you.
i waited for my unfortunate coffee for 40 minutes the barista told me that i should wait more and that i generally want from them. people who came later than me took their drinks a long time ago.
love coming to this starbucks! people are so friendly and welcoming and they always make my perfect cup of white chocolate mocha!

i went today. "mr. smith" did a great job taking my order right away, ringing it in correctly and the order came out quickly. he was humorous and engaging... great personality. he made a connection on what is normally a mundane transaction.
this is my favorite starbucks in reno. not only are the baristas friendly & accommodating but so is their manager - the one w/glasses & short hair.

ok so i am a diehard starbucks fan. i was way excited when they opened this one because it's super close to me and now i don't have to go inside target to get one of my fav morning pleasures. that said, i am really sad to say this starbucks has consistently fallen a short of the excellence starbucks is known for. there are usually extra long wait times (regardless what time you come in), the last 4 of 5 times i've come here they got my order wrong or just given me someone else's order accidentally. i've ordered via the app, walk in and drive thru but it doesn't matter - they are consistently inaccurate. literally every time i've come inside, they were out of raw sugar at the bar, so i have to go to the counter and ask for it. i hope they turn it around because the way it's going now, i prob won't keep coming back much longer. i am not one to complain and i don't put in difficult orders - but there comes a point when even the most faithful customer will get tired and walk away. i'm almost to this point and it sucks. please get it together starbucks. please.

most staff was disinterested, boarder line rude. coffee was ok. not sure why starbucks corp doesn't check reviews and fix problems. luckily there are other alternatives now so it's easy to avoid visiting starbucks again.

edwardsville opened another starbucks next to the high school. smart choice for a location!!. went inside for an evening jolt of caffeine. hubby wanted dessert; real slim pickings on that. he opted for the banana bread ("it's good" he said). no wait for service; lots of customers inside all on their laptops. possibly college crowd since siu-e is nearby too. had to ask for creamer since there wasn't any but that was no biggie. we sat outside and there was plenty of seating outside as well as in. think i prefer this location to the older one.
this was the closest starbucks to where i am staying. service was fast and efficient. there was plenty of seating. this location is at the oxford park centre.
we ordered a caramel coffee and they made it perfect. nothing special, just a run of the mill starbucks.

starbucks is just ok. they never make anything sweet enough for me so i always have to get extra flavors but they are coming up on their food menu which is always helpful. just wish they weren't so pricey. good quick drive thru though.
this location is a convenient alternative to the more crowded one a little further down, sans drive-thru.

this starbucks is so slow. i sit in the drive through for a solid 15-20 minutes every time. the only reason i keep coming back is because there isn't a closer location to me. if you go on your way to work? give yourself plenty of extra time. i understand it's busy in the morning and i'll have to wait a little bit, but not that long. it's only a drive through location so there is no excuse for them to take so long. they never seem to be in a hurry or rush to speed things up.

the service is competent and the food average for a starbucks. however what ruins my experience is this horrible lady in military fatigues who seems to live in the establishment and mumbles something at everyone while giving them dirty looks. let's call her the mumblebum. i was doing work in there, and as i had my laptop out she said somethin insultin. i said excuse me as if i didn't hear her, then went back to work. on top of that she hogs tables. she will put her belongings on a table to save it while she goes outside and sits at another table for a few hours. i think if you ban her for good, your business will dramatically improve.
very busy on a sunday morning...hard for the staff to keep up, but they did their best.

i stopped by at this starbucks location while on the go to work. they are stationed right next to the kroger in belle meade making it hard to miss, great parking right out front. i like to be adventurous in my starbucks order and went with the nitro cold brew with sweet cream...it wasn't necessarily my favorite. it wasn't hot or cold, just a weird in between, and didn't have much flavor either. the egg bites were also just okay, but didn't taste very fresh. it was fairly clean, and there were plenty of members on staff to get speedy service. there were plenty of tables and seats available making it great to get work done or just hang out at.

they served me cold coffee. as soon as it was handed over i could feel the chill. i ordered coffee with cream. come on starbucks, keep fresh and hot coffee ready! you are a coffee company and if you can't serve a good hot cup of coffee you should be ashamed. i handed back to the barista and she replaced it and said the person must have gotten it from a pot that was turned off. why would that coffee be available to be served. i'm a starbucks fan and this was downright disappointing. do better!

nice location, but doesn't have normal hours. it was not open on thanksgiving! when i came the day after thanksgiving, it was open and the employees were pleasant. this location also has some nice sofas and chairs to read the newspaper or a book. not sure if starbucks just costs more in the midwest but it ran me over $5 for a mocha...

holiday coffee beans are currently on sale, so my boyfriend and i decided to pop in and see what this location had in stock. both of the baristas that were there were very helpful. one offered to check in the back because he had a feeling that what was on display was not everything that they had, and he was right! we ended up getting 2 bags of whole bean coffee and are looking forward to enjoying the christmas and holiday blends well into the new year. i would definitely return to this location when in the area!

the drinks are prepared well but the atmosphere is dismal. i live nearby so i tried this location about 4 times so far. i would like to stop by after working 8 hours retail on fri, sat and sun but it is an unfriendly place. i arrived at 7:15 pm but the floors were being mopped so i had to pray that i would not fall as the distance from the sitting area to the front is far. no greeting; just there to take my order. i had to clamber back over the wet floor to retrieve my drink and i do have arthritis...no thank you nor a good evening. why? the starbucks on s virginia is really friendly but a bit funky at times. this location is about as cool as a bank.
excellent baristas! a lot better service than usual. very knowledgeable of their products and very helpful. thanks guys!

blair is an asset to this location. all i have to do is pull up and i hear the following: 'hey lady, your usual, right? venti dark roast with cream, 3 equal and pumpkin spice, right?' yes, ma'am. and she's always smiling and making sure you're smiling as well.
ridiculously slow and then forgot half my order. 40 min it took to stand in line, place order and get my order. will not be going back, this is the 3rd time with the exact same results.

maybe the worst starbucks there is around. they are slow, don't do the drinks in the order they receive them, and sometimes the order doesn't even come out and you have to go back to the desk to remind them or ask them what happened. all they have as an answer is "oh i'm sorry, what was it again?". i just don't get it.
sometimes they are quick and friendly, other times they are slow and stand offish. i don't usually chose this location but when i need coffee... it works!

this is my new preferred starbucks location. the staff are friendly and helpful. i used to work for starbucks, so i know how to craft a decent beverage while still focusing on the customer, and these folks do an excellent job. they really have the 'coffee culture' thing down at this store. customers ask about beverages and receive accurate information. they never roll their eyes at my extensive/strange drink requests and they were great about customizing beverages before starbucks went on the customization kick. if something's wrong, they go out of their way to make it right with a sincere apology. i would enjoy the store more if it had a drive-thru or later hours, but i do think that could change the culture of the store. they're pretty fast here, too. occasionally i meet my friend out here on her lunch hour as she works nearby and we make good use of her hour lunch because everything is quick.

smallish starbucks located on a corner street in old city. it's right down the way from the neighborhood ups store. they take starbucks cards here and have the full menu so come get your favorite coffee flavored treats! quick in and out, but few seating areas here.
amy was completely rude to me and the lady in front of me. if i didnt live so close i wouldnt come back. other ladies were nice except amy.

unfortunately, this starbucks has decided to remove all of their electrical outlets. i've chosen starbucks for years to be able to get some work done while having either breakfast or dinner. i've always chosen starbucks for this very reason. i won't be girving starbucks my business anymore. sad, since this was the very premise of creating starbucks

came in one morning and ordered a venti blackberries and cream frappachino and a morning bun. cashier rang me up and never gave me my morning bun. got back in line to buy a bag of popcorn and get my pastry and i get an "oh you're back" i told her she charged me for my pastry but never gave it to me and all i got was a snarky look. after she all but through my pastry at me i waited at the end of the bar for my drink and started to notice that some of the people behind me in line were getting their drinks before me. while it's one thing if they were getting a regular cup of coffee it's another when they're getting frappuccinos and lattes. after 15-20 minutes of waiting one of the baristas came up to me and asked me what i ordered and if i was still waiting so i told her and apparently the cashier never marked my cup. the barista then made my drink and was very apologetic about it. i also try to help the cashier with the marking on one of the pastry bags because nor she or the manager could figure out what it was and being an employee at a real starbucks i tried to help since i knew the marking and i all i got was a disgusted look from both the cashier and manager. not to sound superior or anything buy if any of these things happened at my store it would not be tolerated at all. since i'm on such a tight schedule in the morning i don't have enough time to go to the one in town or else i would.

ordered a coffee online on mother's day, walked over with my family. knew about the shortened hours since i walked over day before, but still could order online and still said open for grab and go. i checked. long line of cars; truck in the curbside space. both doors locked, still sign for grab and go in window. stood there, no one came to door. called, no one answered. i even took pic because i couldn't believe employees were just ignoring people outside when sign clearly said open for grab and go. truck gave up and got in car line. i walked home, got my car, went through drive thru. they now had written sign up for drive thru only. i cannot believe an employee could not have taken 30 seconds to walk out earlier, before sign was up to say "i'm sorry we're short staffed," hand me my drink and hand drink to people waiting in truck. we were all standing out there for approximately 8 minutes waiting since there was no sign for drive thru only. super poor customer service. and they still gave me the drink that i ordered 30 minutes before, not a fresh one! i will be speaking to manager and contacting corporate. i have been going there since they opened in bardmoor promenade shopping center. they know me by name - about 18 years. for a long time i was there numerous times a week. this location is normally wonderful so i hope the manager will counsel these employees to take the extra 30 seconds to handle the situation properly, not just ignore people and put a sign up later. "oh it's just a few people. they'll just have to figure out to go to drive thru on their own." especially since many people walk over from huge neighborhood next door. i guess if you walked or biked over you're just gonna lose $5. (and this was noon, an hour after they opened. sign was not up till after noontime)

this starbucks looks/feels like you are at an airport. some starbucks are like this now: long and narrow with very sleek sandwich/drink displays; perfect ones to just quickly pick up your coffee (etc..) and go. but, they also happen to have the nicest employees here, especially natalie. she is so genuinely personable and engaging, while also being efficient and quick with your order. super special quality. there are also two big tables here perfectly good for study, meetings, and sharing. little tables too. easy outlets. outside seating. seems to have its regulars here too, so that sometimes makes it feel like a warm community cafe despite the cool atmosphere.

standard issue starbucks. coffee tasted about the same as most of the other starbucks i've been to. the seating area inside and outside is relatively large. no drive thru, prices were about the same, and the friendliness factor at starbucks is generally bordering weird as it is.

3 stars because the service here is dismal at best. i've been to this location more than 10 times because 8th very close to my apartment but if i have the time i head west 6 blocks to the next closest location. no matter what you order or how many people are in front of you you will wait at least 5 minutes for a very simple drink (ie black coffee) and if it's busy i've waited up to 15 minutes. normally i'd walk out but when i'm craving iced coffee magic the more hip local establishments fail miserably every time. the only saving grace to this location is the upstairs area, which is bright, with a good view of south st for people wqtching, and has a lot of seating and tables to read relax or do work.

this starbucks makes a great soy hot chocolate. the staff was friendly and professional as well. the closed dining area could use some cleaning. overall the brand could rethink hair control. it's an industry challenge though.

the baristas at this location are incredibly nice and always manage to get drinks out correctly and fast. they always make my day and give a genuine smile and quick conversation. wish i could give more than 5 stars!!
don't know what coffee this is at nashville international airport, but it isn't starbucks! if you want to be disappointed, come here!

this is a small starbucks in a strip mall on the north side of greenwood. they do have a drive-thru, but it's somewhat awkward to get to. no complaints. it is your run of the mill starbucks. i believe they now have free wi-fi.

i've been coming here for a long time and have seen many ups and downs in both attitude and service. the new manager, fletcher, really seems to have this place going great. it is always a pleasure visiting and the manager and staff really seem to above and beyond to make their customers happy.

so everything about my coffee and the majority of the service was fine, except that the cashier doesn't know how to do basic math. my bill came to $11 and change. i handed her $22. she turns around and throws two dollars into the tip jar and breaks my $20 for my coffees. somebody better go back to math class.

standard starbucks shop but perfect for studying when it's not crazy busy. it does get noisy at times so make sure you bring ear plugs if you need quiet or just listen to music to tune it out. the environment is nice and open so you won't feel cramped (like i've felt at other starbucks shops) which is definitely a big plus. outlet wise, there are a few if you position yourself at the bar or at the larger table.

a couple of the baristas have such a poor attitude, and seem to not want to be there. as a fellow starbucks employee i hate seeing this, makes me feel un-welcomed and un-appreciated. i went with a fellow partner and was given the wrong beverage, with a side of sass. i feel like the mid-shift partners should wear a smile and be more friendly to not only fellow partners but costumers as well.

this is by far one of the worst managed starbucks i have been to. this is the closest location to my house in carp so i deal with it when i'm not going into ventura or sb... the bathroom is always disgusting, the staff when you order is nice but the ones making the drinks are not as pleasant. i always order a matcha drink & they never shake it even if you ask. they serve it all clumped up on the bottom. would be nice if they took a little more pride in their job & cheer up. also a cleaning routine where is the manger at this place can't they see the messy store.

not bad, not superb. just starbucks. i love that there's a drive-thru but the location is off of the street so it's kind of hard to get here. yesterday morning, the lid of my frappuccino was not properly applied to the cup, so it created a whole mess after i pulled off. not cool baristas, not cool. but i've been here several times before then and had no problem.

waking my yelp account up specifically for this review. having spent perhaps too much time at too many starbucks, i can say that this starbucks has the nicest staff and is the cleanest starbucks in all of delaware county, probably philadelphia as well. i've never had an issue with my drink being wrong. never hard to find parking, either in front, on the side. i've never had to park by smashburger but that would be another option. gets pretty crowded closer to lunch. for anyone who has criticized this location, they can go to glen mills, media, springfield, villanova, bryn mawr...or really any other starbucks with an angry disinterested staff that can't be bothered. the brookhaven location feels like it's a totally different company.
great little starbucks hubs and i came across when we were visiting nola! great neighborhood, clean and great service!

this is the only starbucks that i can go to and consistently get my drink made perfectly how i want it . the drive thru seems to move a bit faster in the morning but slower during lunch time! they are patient when my app is taking forever to load and seem to practice good sanitation !
during my visit they were upgrading the drive thru so i can't wait for the update. the store was clean and organized and the service was great.
this is my favorite local starbucks! always get my drinks right, handle long lines efficiently, and great customer service! shoutout to alyssa, she's always been very nice and helpful. :)
great coffee! great customer service!! i highly recommend going here for your next cup of coffee!!!

i think this is the best starbucks in the santa barbara/goleta area. it certainly has the best atmosphere. feels more like the seattle area ones with some care put into the interior. warm and inviting. interesting wall art and a good vibe. a lot of seating options. large area outside. consistently pleasant service. very comfortable, handsome starbucks. open late too, which is much more urban feeling. great! a lot of students study here so it can really fill up.

as far as a starbucks goes this is one of my favorite locations. the downside is no drive through. parking is sometimes a challenge in this shopping center and i imagine will get even worse once the new ross is open. luckily the starbucks is on the other side so there is usually a spot or two open nearby. the staff is always very friendly and more than willing to remake a drink if it is not to your liking. there seem to be a lot of regulars at this location and the baristas always remember their drinks.

i have been to almost all the starbucks in st. petersburg/largo/clearwater area. this is not solely due to the fact that i love starbucks, but because i travel throughout the county for my job and need a place to work in between meetings. this is my favorite starbucks in st. petersburg. most starbucks serve the same drinks/food, so my favor of this one is based on three main reasons. 1. it is in a great central location and easy to get to. a little outside of downtown st. petersburg, you can easily get to 275 or stay on 4th st. to travel to other parts of pinellas county. 2. they have a bar with stools by a window, so there is almost always a guaranteed place to work if you are by yourself. each seat even has an electrical outlet. 3. there is free parking. however, the lack of parking spaces is why i take away one star. sometimes the entire lot is filled. try to avoid those busy times (lunch time, right after work, etc.).

i was extremely frustrated as i saw three orders got processed before mine. mine wasn't even processed until i asked where the heck was my chai tea latte. the guy was like....ummm. it was the person who took my order who was at fault who failed to write down my order..ughhhh. very irritated. this doesn't even deserve a star but i couldn't send it without rating
amazing service and great atmosphere. the manager joe greeted me nicely and i love how they seem to know all the local customers. cheers y'all.

short line on a friday evening and they have my favorite in stock! trento sized strawberry acai with coconut milk. lots of places have been running out with the new trend so i'm glad they have it it cool me off on a hot day in st. louis! this store also has snacks and cups and gifts to pick up. i collect the you are here mugs and they have the st. louis one in stock for under $12.

when you are from seattle, it's hard to have patience for the baristas in other areas of the country... i was the only person in line and my drink still took forever. i asked for blonde espresso but received regular. and there was barely any caramel in my caramel macchiato. not impressed.

store looks nice but can be deafeningly loud - no sound insulation at all. here at after lunch - no rush whatsoever - and every free table is filthy. had to get napkins and literally scrub a table. parking lot is tight. love the chain, poorly thought out location with untrained, inattentive staff. bummer.

not a huge seating area and there was a woman (larger, no name tag unfortunately)who was incredibly unfriendly and rude to me. she aggressively swatted me to the credit card machine to tell me to insert my card.... weird

friendly staff and quick service. clean and well stocked with coffee items and condiments for your coffee. we ordered coffee and two cinnamon twists. so disappointed in the twists. the pastries were hard and dry (except the glaze). ugh...a coffee cafe should always have fresh pastries!!

worst starbucks for service that i have ever been in. a quiet thursday afternoon with just a few customers and three employees behind the counter. even accounting for all of them maybe having had a bad day, there was no excuse for the abysmal attitude and surly service. i asked for an unsweetened passion tea and confirmed that it was decaffeinated - which i was sure that it was since i have had it countless times before.i'm not even sure why i asked but knew that a massive headache would follow if i consumed caffeine. the young man informed me that all of their teas were caffeinated.i looked quizzically at him and asked if he was sure. he rolled his eyes and said he was sure. i ordered nothing and thanked him. my husband ordered his drink and had it slid over to him by a woman who would clearly rather be doing anything else. she made no eye contact and did not respond to his thank you. i then looked up on my phone and confirmed that passion tea is indeed decaffeinated and went back to place my order. i did not gloat or act annoyed in any way but simply confirmed that i'd checked and my unfriendly employee then smirked, did not apologize for having made a mistake and punched in the order. again i thanked him and his fellow barrister practically threw the drink at me and never made eye contact, turning away immediately. rude and unfriendly- i will not go back!

great nola-inspired interior with trumpet-lamps and whatnot (1 star). coffee is good as expected (1 star). but don't try to talk or work here, they love to play ear-battering punkrock music at noon (they dimmed it a little bit after we asked twice).

never thought i would rate a starbucks...but i had to because this one is the best starbucks in the city! located on jefferson hospital's campus. great place to study and relax (separate room). very busy starbucks but the service is quick and super friendly. love it here.

the staff at this location is loud and annoying. they are always so busy talking to each other instead of paying attention to what the customer orders. the staff at ina and oracle is the exact opposite. they are polite, quiet, and always get the orders right.

maybe i came on a bad day, but this starbucks was a bit of a train wreck. everyone seemed to be on their first day of work. cashier messed up everyone's order. simple things like water was overlooked. people with online orders were waiting behind people who waited in line. it was general chaos.

this is one of the best starbucks in tucson. the employees are always cheerful, polite and very knowledgeable of the sb products. andrew, zarin and ammie are always very friendly and cheerful, which is always nice to do business with them. if you are a regular or a first timer here, the employees treat you as if you are their only customer. it gets crazy at times, but everyone pulls together and this makes the flow of orders happen very smoothly. the store manager, lawson, has a great attitude toward his customers and employees. best starbucks in tucson. thank you! always enjoy going to this friendly store.
clean store friendly staff. cheerfully added cash to my starbucks card, and had my food order ready before i finished putting cream in my grande. what's not to like?

i have always been astonished at how friendly starbucks employees are. no matter what time of day, what i order, they are always chipper and energetic. i thought that these traits were required to work at starbucks (unless you are in nyc, of course). i came here at about 5:30 am the other day. this is not my usual starbucks but i was in that area and needed coffee. the employees that i encountered seemed to be angry that i would dare to stop by for coffee and a bagel. in fact, only when the gentleman gave me my coffee and i didn't drive away did he remember that i had ordered a bagel. he then shouted to someone in the main-area that he needed a bagel, thus making my wait way longer than if he had put in the order when i ordered it. i got no napkins with my bagel. perhaps i just came in on the wrong morning.

probably the worst starbucks i've ever been to. although they were quick, they made my first order wrong and when i mentioned it to a barista she told me she made my order that was no where to be found and then when she remade my order in a tall when i paid for a venti with a very rude demeanor. :( sad

this starbucks location is in the the hyatt downtown. it's a major godsend on sundays when the 6th and olive location is closed, _but_ the prices here are a bit higher (about $0.50) more. not sure if that's due to hotel taxes or something, or if they just jacked them up due to a majority of their business being from hotel guests? it's a mystery, because they do not provide an itemized receipt. i'm guessing it's a little of both.

great morning crew during the week . however they are lacking customer service during the afternoon and weekend. today for example i order my regular iced coffee with non fat milk and she hands it to me black. i say it's suppose to have milk in it. .... instead of saying ok let me add milk she argues with me that i never said anything about milk! mam.... come on just add the dang milk why argue over it!?

if i could leave a zero i would. not only cant you be a black male and wait on a friend b4 u order your coffee u have to be in embarrass and be walked out in handcuffs while your friend meet you i've never in my life had to witness something so awful to the barista that throught her life was in danger i hope you never truly are in danger. as a franchise starbucks should have procedures in order what to do when a customer didn't purchase anything and it shouldn't be calling the cops. i'm sad the situation is sad and you guys as a company old those men an apology and the barista should be fired.

one photo is from the starbucks on jefferson and walnut. the photo with the holiday cup is at loughborough commons! starbucks baristas are the best in customer service! they deserve more tips as they provide a needed service for a lot of us. tip: order hot drinks including lattes, short, or tall for the best flavor and longer-lasting heat! tip: frappuccinos have the best flavor as a grande! tip: order short brewed coffee or tea in the app. (get 2 stars per dollar) to build up your stars daily

i always get fast service (unless it's 7am) the people who work here are always very friendly. i did once find a bug in the show case thing, they removed the bug but didn't dispose of the food that was in there.

this starbucks sets the standard for the many starbucks in tucson. the friendly baristas, the quality drinks, and service never disappoint. the location is prime and convenient. the best coffee on the east side of tucson!

they're still getting their stuff together and getting settled in, but it's a great place for hanging out and people watching. not the cheapest place to grab coffee around here, but i still find it hard to resist.

what's going on with this starbucks? i've been coming here every day for 2 years and the service just keeps getting worse and worse!! there used to be super nice baristas here, now they're slow and not very helpful at all.

spacious relaxation area to have coffee. the baristas immediately greeted me and helped me figure out exactly what i wanted. i found it very cute that they had turkeys available to write what you are thankful for :) the coffee was brewed and ready fast!

this is my favorite starbucks in the camden county/ burlington county area. the staff is always friendly and fast, even at 5:30 am. they always get my order right and will even throw in things like extra butter for my baked goods at no extra cost.
i'm not impressed. the coffee is acidic, and the baked goods are dry, bland, and often stale. just not worth the money at all.

starbucks coffee taste like burnt ass. the atmosphere has all the charm of a hospital. why people go here when there are so many awesome local coffee shops in this city with fantastic coffee straight of the barges. the time i went, this place was packed though (client wanted to meet there). i'm really confused as to why... you could go to rue, pjs, cc's or any of the other fantastic coffee places.

some people get coffee every day and i am not strong coffee drinker to be honest but starbucks is the only place i buy coffee when i need some energy. i live close by to this starbucks and the service is always above and beyond, many people probably would say i am exaggerating but the other day for example me and my fiancé ordered two caramel brûlée and did not specify we wanted it cold not hot, when we got to the window the lady gave our hot coffee and we said that we sorry but we thought that it was cold even thought we offered to take the hot coffee because it was our mistake the lady from starbucks insisted in making us a new coffee the way we wanted it. i really really appreciated what they did i mean they did not had to do that because there was a lot of people waiting in line and to take the time to make us new coffee when it was our mistake in order it's just demonstrate the amazing service they have. i just wanted to say thank you so much starbucks for making my experience amazing!
hey guys! thanks for wasting my time by not fulfilling my mobile order this morning. waited 20 minutes before leaving empty handed and with $5+ less on my mobile account hope you do better next time

not up to the high standards of service that most starbucks are know for. i ordered a triple grande caramel mach and when i got it, it felt like only two thirds full. i was in a hurry so i didn't complain as i should have. as i was leaving i stopped to go to the restroom and a pregnant young woman coming out warned me there was no toilet paper. i went into the other restroom and it was also out of paper and dirty. very disconcerting.

this location is good in a pinch. on more than one occasion they've forgotten to make my or a friend's drink or food (even when it's not busy!) and i've had to ask the barista what's going on. they never have blonde roast, which is kind of a first world problem, but come on, people. if you're in brentwood and want starbucks, go to the other one in maryland farms. much nicer, more accommodating.

a slightly below average starbucks hence the below average star rating. they were out of things and it was early in the day, the coffee tasted like mud and the staff was particularly "out of it". usually at a starbucks there's some millennial zombies working who don't know anything and then one employee who knows everything. this location seemed to not have the "knows everything" employee no one could answer my simple questions about the varieties of coffee and a question about the food that should've been easy dance. i asked if they could make me a pot of the blonde coffee and they said "no" because it was later in the day, i often ask them to do that at other starbucks and they have no problem with that at all i realize they stop making the blonde earlier in the day. i was super friendly when i asked and they do it at a lot of other locations so it must be indicative of something else. i hate to be negative, i really do, but the place was also not really kept up. as far as cleanliness goes it was definitely a bit messy and no one was attending to it. there are definitely better choices for your money.

i was staying near this location for a month and my experiences were 50/50 right down the middle.. everytime my order was just one drink.. the service and and drink were perfect. i had received suggestions on how to make my drink better next time i ordered it and recommendations on what might add flavor.. it was great.. but the other half of the time i ordered i always ordered 3 drinks and they were never correct. ever.. if i said white mocha i got a white hot chocolate.. when i ordered my doubleshot i received a mocha.. whats weird is that each time it was a different persons fault.. one time it would be the cash register person writing the wrong order.. and the other time it was the barrista making the drinks.. it was really weird.. i have concluded that it is my fault for these mixups. presumably my stunning features or my impeccable jaw-line distracting said persons to make mistakes. i have resigned to never attend this location again. i apologize for flaunting my essence and impeding your abilities. good day.

the worst starbucks. ive ever been to. no problem with the employees. but, my latte was really bad, spinach wrap was black - it was so burned, there were no newspapers of any kind, there were no coffee stirrers available, internet was beyond slow and spotty.

since i work in arnold, i visit this starbucks nearly 4 times a week. unfortunately, this is by far the most crowded and backed up starbucks in the st. louis area. the employees usually do the best they can, but the shear volume of drive thru, online, and in store orders is very overwhelming due to there not being another starbucks in the area. regardless of the day and time, it takes no less than 15 minutes to wait for my order and sometimes approaches a half hour. starbucks needs to either increase the number of employees on the clock here, or better yet, build another drive thru within a 5 mile radius!

love, absolutely love meeting at this starbucks! i will say however, that the service is very mediocre. they just make your drink and keep it moving. i am the type who likes to be sold or engage with others.

after 25 minutes of waiting in line, the 3 staff members forgot to make our drinks. the head barista, arumi was very rude while talking to her cousins that had just walked in. instead of making our drinks, after i reminded her of our order, arumi proceeded to introduce her family to the rest of the barista staff. there were 12 people in line to order and that did not affect any pace of the starbucks 5 points staff. several people waiting in line began to comment on the poor service. i will not come back to this location ever again. horrible customer service.

i've been sitting here doing work for 2 hours at the bar and really appreciate the service i'm seeing (and that i received earlier). the employees are very attentive and friendly and the manager is currently working with a customer to fix an issue from his mobile order. i watched them politely fix another issue with a customer as well, and have been impressed with their personalities and attention. i especially appreciate that they are not loud or shouting, which is very helpful when people are here doing work. i will definitely be back here in the future because of this atmosphere--some starbucks can be loud and rude but this is one exception! (today at least!) thank you guys!!
parking is horrendous. people resort to parking illegally. difficult to enter and exit the lot, depending on where you're going. very slow service.

the absolute worst sb in the usa!! slow slow slow....... and half the time the drinks taste terrible. i've brought this to corporates attention multiple times. they obviously haven't been able to rectify this. just make sure you have time to wait. and try your drink before pulling away from the window.

this is the most friendly and enjoyable starbucks i have ever been in. even though i live 4 hours a way, my wife and i always make a point to come to this starbucks when were in town for the holidays. i dont know everyone's names, but connor, alex, natalie, and justin are always so positive and welcoming and they even seem to enjoy making my difficult drinks. if you're in santa barbara, this is the one to go to!

incredibly slow. i always regret it as soon as i pull into the drive-through line or go inside, having forgotten how long this is going to take. when i finally get the coffee it's fine, but the service here leaves a lot to be desired.

i love starbucks. i'm loyal to them, and have been going there for years. i have a gold card, and am quite addicted! i'm pretty picky when it comes to location. i've determined that this one is my favorite! there are two newer people who always screw up my order, but it's rectified, and i leave with a great drink. i usually get the same drink, and this location makes it the best. sometimes i go in the morning before class, and everyone is so friendly! i'll be honest, i'm not a tipper, but the baristas in the morning win me over every time!

i'm going to qualify this review by saying we are not huge starbucks people. we are definitely casual customers, who might stop in once in a while for a drink. generally, i find the coffee to be a bit on the overpriced side. but when you're out and about and need a caffeine fix, starbucks will usually be a good bet. this one is located near home for us so sometimes my husband and i slip over here for a drink in the evenings before they close. we've also used the drive thru for quick pick ups here and there. and can i just say? a starbucks that has a drive thru is a wonderful thing indeed. i wish all of them (or at least more of them) had them. as a mother with several small children, drive thru's are always appreciated! this location is easy to find, easy to score parking, the staff is pleasant and friendly, the selection is good, the store is clean and the seating options are plentiful and comfortable. this is a fun place to hang out in, chat with someone and just enjoy a few moments. i can't speak to bringing in laptops and working the day away as i'm not the sort to do that but i'm going to guess it's a good place to get that done too. if you're in the north valleys and need a starbucks fix, this is the place to go.
pretty disappointed starbucks. i ordered a grande chai tea latte this morning & when i opened it up, a quarter of it was empty. well, that sucks. next time can i pay a quarter less of the price?

this starbucks is the slowest in clearing airport lines. i've been flying through bna and using this location since it opened years ago. now, unless you have 20+ minutes to wait, do not stop here. time to move on to other options.

i've been to many starbucks locations, but this starbucks is by far my favorite. the employees are friendly & they make the drinks perfectly every time! i drive 20 minutes to come to this location because it's worth the trip!

horrible! 12:00 on friday, march 16th stopped for coffee, iced coffee and pumpkin bread. they were unable to identify my coffee after every other customer had handled my coffee and then the dumb starbucks barista opens the lid and proceeds to say that's my coffee. no customer service or kindest there. do not waste your time and money.

drive thru service is supposed to be for people in a rush correct?... if you agree with that statement then this location is not for you.... i've tried giving this place a chance over and over again and they take forever! there's always a bunch of people working too but it's like they're all in slow motion. it's taken me 20-25 minutes to get a cup of coffee ...i now drive miles out of the way to go to other locations just to get to work on time. i can't wait until the dunkin' donuts opens across the street.

i had a terrible experience here and will not be returning. i ordered the new iced brown sugar oatmilk shaken espresso and what i received was nothing like it. i've ordered the drink before at other starbucks and it was always made perfectly , but this one was nothing compared to it. it was sooo watery and tasted like milk and water. i even went back in to let them know so it could be re made, still received the same exact drink. i posted a picture of what it's supposed to look like since i had it before and a picture of what i received. two different drinks. do not go here. they do not know how to make their drinks.

visited this location for the first time and really appreciated the assistance from the manager abby. she was really helpful with providing information on their new one step coffee brewer. will definitely recommend this location to others.
stopped by this morning. my drink was alright but the place smelled like burnt coffee like usual. there were actually seats open today. most of the time there is barely enough room to wait.

went to this starbucks on friday afternoon. only a handful of customers there. no one in line. i stood at the counter for two full minutes waiting to be served. made eye contact with at least three of the employees none of which acknowledged me nor said they would be with me in a moment. nothing. finally just sat down with my friend who apparently had fabulous service. i went back to the counter five minutes later and was finally served. starbucks, you need to teach basic manners to your employees.
very long line... service was friendly though. had to partake in a mocha frappuccino. they're really good stuff!

starbucks is always going to be a starbucks. i like it enough; i'm a fan of their seasonal espresso drinks and an occasional iced latte. still, i am normally more local-business oriented, but starbucks is the only coffee shop in new orleans that is open when i leave for work at 5:40....so i am there every time i forget to set my coffee pot the night before. however, this location has gotten my drink order wrong far too many times for it to be alright. every other time i am there in the morning, i get in the car and realize that this is not what i wanted. even when my drink order is exactly what i would like, i discover that it is luke-warm at best. if i order extra-hot, i get burnt milk. i understand that it's early in the morning, but there are always other customers and i never order anything needlessly complicated (today it was a skim carmel latte and i discovered a full-fat mocha in my cup once i'd gotten on i-10). that being said, it is a nice coffee shop to go sit at; there's a patio and enough seating if you are going during prime-tulane time. i've never had problem with the pastries. the service is always friendly and awake when i get there. the holiday red cups make me feel warm and fuzzy.
super friendly service with a smile. i cannot remember the gentlemen's name who took my order, but he was super nice and made my day.

an amazing crew that is serving out great coffee and genuine connections! terrance, emily, hannah, torrance, mo and betina quickly made this store one of my favorites and a great way to start the day. love nerding out on coffee with the baristas!!! 5.5 outa 5.

it's amazing my husband was verbally assaulted by the starbucks cashier yesterday, by the name of kiley. kiley became very aggressive with him, instructing him to not speak and to not continue a conversation with another patron, as the patron indicated she was okay with the discussion, but "thank you" for your concern to kiley. kiley, really mis-read the situation and over stepped her boundaries with insult, aggression and hostility toward my husband. instead of kiley checking in with the patron, she directly and aggressively instructed my husband that: "i am sitting at the table and you must go now" at this time we're not sure, what kiley's motive was, perhaps this women was her girl-friend or friend, but kiley became very aggressive accusing my husband of unwanted behavior. these aggressive accusations, harassment and racial profiling by kiley will not be tolerated, as we are consulting an attorney for possible legal action for gross negligence by starbucks to not stop such destructive personnel defamation in their stores. in the end it's starbucks responsibility and gross negligence in not placing a stop to racial profiling, defamation, random accusations directed toward customers. again, our family will not tolerate rudeness, racial profiling, defamation, random accusations , as we are holding starbucks responsible for gross negligence of their team member kiley.
the angry older woman has a rotten attitude . she behaves as if she s doing you the customer a favor . not spending my money on starbucks again - much less at this place
if you like the caramel frappuccinos don't get it at this location they literally taste like water every time and sometimes they don't even put the caramel. the employees are nice though.
jordan in the drive thru was amazing and the drinks were awesome! coming back again for sure! great customer service!

this place was disgusting. i'd rather drive to rockville road then to get something from here ever again. if i could give zero stars i would! between given me the wrong drinks and old food with a bad attitudes, it's not worth it! my advice travel elsewhere.

overall i've had very good experiences with this starbucks. my only complaint is that they only have small tables and a window bar with stools. i wish they had some comfy chairs, and bigger tables that barely fit 2 laptops. but i realize that it's a small starbucks and i assume they're doing the best with what they have!
no more 24hours???? this location is always the best bet when my gf craves for midnight coffee (weird habit but cute )

this starbucks was pretty good...a bit chilly in the front area, but it's nice that they have an extension cord so you can plug in even when there aren't outlets in the walls. a nice seating area in the back, which was super crowded when we went around 1pm. nice employees, no line, and outdoor seating!

no bananas, no bagels bites, out of over 50% of stuff at 8 a.m (and not because they're busy). manager obviously needs to step it up. they have disco music on at this early hour. not cozy feeling either.

prime location on the way to/from work or accessing highway 40 at jefferson. i appreciate the alternative to the busy deltaco location at grand and 40. interior is a bright, welcoming, open floor plan with plenty of seating once we get back to dining, working, meeting, chillaxing at cafes again. the restrooms are upscale chic as a public restroom can be with the soap/water flow/dryer in a single sink location. this really cuts down on paper towel waste it i like to have a little tissue to open the door after washing my hands personally. service was prompt and pleasant (i was the only one brave enough to walk inside i guess) the drive thru was popping. my coffee was made just as i'd requested and i was on my merry way. oh, plenty of outdoor patio seating and parking available. ~christy

i only like coming here because of the drive thru, however there are always so many mistakes with my drink order and today was the last draw. this pregnant african american lady that was taking my order, handle with every customers money. decided to open a straw and put it in my drink. how disgusting is that. i never asked her to do that to my drink. this is the first time in years that ive been to starbucks that someone did this.
i went on friday. was stuck in line for over 30 minutes with no care in the world. service is bad, manager is bad. please get yourself together.

my wife and i love our starbucks. this location in delran is our closest store so we visit when we can. at home we buy and mix the starbucks coffee and it's just right. when we come in we usually get something special like frappuccino or a teavana tea in the summer. i like a bold coffee and starbucks does not disappoint. being a starbucks member and having their app also gets you rewards from free food or beverages to free music and apps for my iphone which i have found to be nice.
poor service, took twenty minutes to get two black, iced coffees out, and when they came out they were pumped full of simple syrup, took forever and they didn't even get the order right.

good enough. what i would expect on an early travel morning. no wrong done here. just decent lattes at early hours. did the trick. did its job. no harm no foul. keep churning them out. keep the machine running.

i've only ever had one problem at this location, and it was quickly fixed once i brought it to the attention of the manager. the way this store is situated makes it a bit inconvenient for a quick drive thru visit, but the staff are accommodating and the coffee is great!
i went in and ordered a iced chai latte, and got an ice coffee. i left the comfort of my home, to go get the taste of a chai latte, and got a damn ice coffee. highly disrespectful.

this is by far the worst starbucks i have ever visited. the staff has no sense of urgency. i have placed mobile orders and arrived to the store 10 minutes after ordering only to have to wait another 10 to 15 minutes because they didn't get my order started until i got there. most recently i placed a mobile order for a food item and found out upon arrival at the store that they were sold out. this happened twice this week! they don't update their mobile site with sold out items. this is the closest starbucks to my home but i will never go back to that store. i will go out of my to another starbucks.
slowest drive thru. definitely not showcasing their a team, tables are always dirty, trash is constantly overflowing. weekdays, weekends...doesn't matter always the same, dirty & slow!

if you ever are driving by this starbucks, stop by. i recommend it! what i had was the pumpkin spice latte, hold the whip cream. it was great. it came out fast, under two minutes, and service was friendly. this was surprising because there were a lot of customers. i came at about seven pm. if you go around this season, i'd definitely recommend. update: also try the green tea latte.
this is one of the best starbucks i have been to in sparks nevada. they are efficient, friendly and a joy to be around!

perhaps it was just a bad day. my drink order was wrong. i had to send it back. it happens. never mind that i was seeing complicated frappucinos being served to people who came after me in line before i even got my wrong drink order. but that's one thing. i guess you would think i ordered something off the wall. my order... a large half decaf regular coffee. you see, they make you wait until they make the complicated drinks for everyone else, and then they took care of my simple coffee. and when i asked if it was half decaf since they didn't state it, the barista admitted it wasn't and offered to make another, but not before they had to complete one more complicated latte that came after me. maybe it was just a bad day. but they reminded me why it's better to go to dunkin donuts or wawa if you just want the brewed coffee.

preordered my starbucks on my way to work. my card was charged and i came in 20 minutes later to pick up. they said they couldn't find my ticket and to come back in 10 minutes. i couldn't wait 10 minutes, i had to go to work! i called later that day to ask if i could at least get a refund and no one picked up the phone. i guess $15 literally down the drain

recently i've seen an improvement in service. i also have noticed several new employees and so am happy that management must be listening to feedback. if the service levels continue to be positive i will update my rating. keep up the customer focus......it matters!

the coffee here is consistently awful and just not up to the standard of other starbucks. i always get a brewed coffee and it tastes burnt and bitter every time. i can't imagine what the espresso drinks taste like that aren't loaded with milk and sugar. also it has a tiny cramped parking lot that makes it difficult to get in and out of parking spots due to the drive thru line.

this is one of the bigger starbucks in the sb area, and also the one i frequent the most due to its location in the camino real marketplace. it's a convenient coffee stop if you're out shopping in the area (costco, home depot, ross, sports authority, ulta, etc) or if you're waiting for the showing of a movie at the theater a couple doors down. this place is quite popular so be prepared for a longer wait (than other starbucks i've visited) for your drink...it's almost always crowded (a ton of college students studying), there's usually a line, and most seating will be occupied. they renovated a couple years ago so the place has newer furnishings and a slight rearrangement of the layout, but overall it seems pretty much the same as when it first opened. their super friendly staff and prime location keeps bringing me back, even if it's not the coziest of places and doesn't offer super fast service.

i've always been a home brew coffee kinda girl, so it's a rarity when i venture into a starbucks. however, my boyfriend is a huge fan so we decided to go on saturday. after staring at the menu completely overwhelmed for what seemed like an eternity, i decided to try the mocha light frappuccino. it was the most delicious thing i've ever drank. it was a cold, silky combination of mocha and chocolate. a grande only has 130 calories too. in my eyes, that's worth raving over!

great customer service and they try really hard for the volume of customers they get.. would be 5 stars if i didnt have to listen to all the complaining entitled students that expect a drink in 5 seconds when they see a line out the door.. try the french pressed coffee, i didnt even know that was an option until some one at the other store on campus by the library told me about it when they didnt have to, worth the wait.

pay attention- as they can't be bothered to write your name on your cup and use it. this means no less than eight other patrons will touch your order before you realize it's sitting there. also, my valencia orange refresher was approximately 4 sips (grande) and all ice. there's a dunkin' around the corner, do yourself a favor and go there. this place is a circus.

about a month ago we came in and ordered a iced coffee with milk and syrup around 10 pm. the iced coffee was more water than coffee with only about a teaspoon of milk added. i go to starbucks very often and this starbucks location just sucked! the girl working late that night wasn't any help when i told her i've never seen iced coffee like that before, she just looked at me and said rudely, " do you want me to make u another one?" just rude. never again here! save your drive and go to another starbucks

this location is hit or miss. the morning staff seems to be pretty good, quick and know their stuff. the evening staff are rude, slow and unapologetic. i also believe this location is understaffed. the drinks are also hit or miss, never know if it's going to be made correctly. i can tell when they leave the cup of fresh espresso sitting too long because they get so busy in the evening and can't keep up. i highly recommend ordering ahead on the mobile app if you want your coffee within a timely manner....

the worst starbucks experience i've had in a while. no parking at 3pm, lot of people working inside at tables. i had to tell the barista my drink order 3 times (unsweetened green tea, light ice...not that hard!) and requested that they use my sweetener packets and they said it wasn't a problem. i went to the restroom and when i came out, my iced tea still was not ready (no one was in front of me in line and no drive through customers). there were 4 total baristas and they were all talking to each other on their headsets about someone they knew in high school. social hour apparently. when i finally got my drink, my sweetener packets were floating my in drink. i was shocked and audibly said "seriously?!" and they looked at me like i was crazy. when i said my sweetener was floating in it, they offered to remake my drink but seeing as i still had a 2 hour drive, i wasn't interested in waiting to get it remade. needless to say, i will not be visiting this location again.
big fan of starbucks! the avon starbucks employees need to step it up. there is always a line in the drive thru. the bathrooms are a mess. tables are never clean when i go in.

my happy place. parking is terrible & it is hard to get a comfy chair, but every customer is treated like royalty. it is crowded often throughout the day, but you will always get a perfect drink. if you aren't 100% satisfied, they'll make you another. justin is a fabulous barista! he's never too busy to greet you by name & to make suggestions when you want a change. please don't come to yardley's starbucks! enough people already & if you do, don't you dare park in the handicapped spot of you don't have a tag. there are only 5 spots & many of us will call the police to move you! i should mention the regulars all look out for each other. it's an office & second home!

i love starbucks because of their consistency, yet this location needs more training on how to make their drinks to starbucks normal standards. for some reason their espresso is incredibly weak and the color of iced tea... when i asked about why it was so weak they offered extra shots of espresso but again, it barely tasted like coffee. they need help.

these people messed the up easiest drink and were rude about it. i ordered a tall iced green tea with no sweetener. i received a cup filled above the brim with ice and then filled in with tea. i pointed out that there was practically no tea in the cup, they said it's fine and moved on. i guess i just paid $3 for a cup of ice with a splash of green tea. i'f i lived in tucson, i wouldn't come here again

oh, starbucks. tampa's embarrassing coffee culture means that starbucks is usually the place that you have to go for coffee. i sincerely miss the quiet coffee shop atmosphere. now you have to endure your overpriced, burnt coffee in a loud, obnoxious space. i have liked this particular, sem heights starbucks, but it has become unbearable. the parking lot is poorly designed (as many drive-thru starbucks parking lots are), and it is so loud inside. i like to read at coffee shops, but it's impossible to do here.

not sure what's going on at this starbucks. maybe they're numb from dealing with tourists. i knew they were busy so i mobile-ordered my cold brew coffee. the app said 1-4 minutes. i got there and there was a line that grew as i sat and waited. everyone got there drinks and more people arrived. they got their drinks. i checked for my drink. no. more people arrived and they got their drinks. finally after 15 minutes (i looked on my app for the order time) i asked the "barista" if the was a mobile order for "richard". she looked at the cup sitting atop the espresso machine, curtly replied "yes." and turned away. i waited a couple more minutes and left. someone else could have my coffee. a simple "oops, sorry" could have turned the situation. apparently to much effort. after seeing the other reviews, i realized it was less an employee problem and more a manager one...
one of my favourite locations, they always say hello as i enter, and goodbye when i leave. they've learned my first name, and always remember i have an allergy to dairy! what more could i ask for?!

i really dislike this starbucks location. they never put whipped cream on my drinks when i always specifically say i want whipped. the drinks taste the same as every other location, besides the missing whip. their drive thru is also terribly designed. if there are more that 3 cars in line you have to wait in the road or middle of the lot and hope no one cuts in front of you.

i'm writing a review and a little tidbit from the review prior to mine. this location is wonderful. the crew here is always so nice and helpful and all the drinks are wonderful. lots of parking and easy on and off access to the freeway. by the way, carpinteria has two starbucks... the other location is located in the vons grocery store located on linden avenue. check them both out, you get excellent service at both locations
