just knowing that you're among the rich n famous in montecito is a thumbs up. its a great feeling right?. then you add in their awesome outdoor patio with wi-fi, tables & chairs.. now i can relax , stress free, while i sip on my coffee and surf the web.. oh yeah!! now we're talking! great service, great location. i give this place 4 stars!!

absolute worst and most pathetic starbucks i've ever experienced. for context, i am 18 years old and have lived in bordentown for 15 years, and have gone to this specific starbucks for after-school treats since i was a child. last year my friend and i went to this starbucks at nighttime and she ordered a vanilla bean frappuccino. she was given a literal glass of unflavored ice. it was the texture of a frappuccino but there was no flavor. we tried giving it back and asking for a new drink but the manager insisted that we were wrong somehow and had to be persuaded to take it back and make her a new drink. months later, i ordered an iced coffee, only to discover burnt pieces of black plastic floating in it. one of the machines had malfunctioned. recently for some reason they have refused to open the front of their store and have been "drive-through only", which i think is absolutely ridiculous. they also are terribly slow because they have a shortage of workers, which i know because i asked a drive-through employee if i could apply online, to which she responded "oh yes, we are very short on workers!". i have applied twice, and have heard back zero times. now i work at chick-fil-a, a franchise which actually provided me with a punctual response to my application and offered me a job. i went to this specific starbucks again last week for a drink and a lunch and i was in the drive-through for 39 minutes (i was looking at the time). it took 39 minutes to go through the drive through because they have a severe worker shortage, refuse to accept applications for some reason, and also do not open the front of their store.

i've never been an a1 fan of this location but it is conveniently located right off of highway 55 and at the tip of carondelet park, so whether i'm on foot (jogging) or in transit via car; this subpar starbucks is the closest within a 10 mile radius. but i do not like the service; most (not all) of the barista's have zero personality, no people skills and lack tact. the straw today was when i asked for a willow on clover and the drive-thru guy replied, "what?" to my order. i repeated myself sloooooo and he said, "oh no we don't have a clover machine here. i damn near pulled off but i didn't want to be rude; besides i'd already been in the waiting line for a few minutes and really wanted a cool drink. i settled for an iced black coffee and vowed to never come back; no matter how convieniently located it is.

rude staff. i waited in line patiently behind a group of people and when i finally made it to the front of the register, the barista proceeds to take the order of a man to the far left of the counter. the barista tells me that i'm not in the right side of the line and i would have to go to the end of the line (that had gotten long in the time i waited) towards the left to be served. i had already waited in line where it was clearly formed and there was no indication that i wasn't in a proper line already. it was clear that i had been waiting there longer then the customer he had chosen to serve before me, but clearly that didn't matter. i left this establishment after being treated like that. there are better coffee shops that do value their customers and have better customer service.

i go to this starbucks frequently as it's right down the street from my house. for the most part i just go through the drive thru but sometimes i decide to go in and sit down. the people who work there are very nice, but the service when ordering inside is beyond slow. the past two times i went in they just completely forgot to make my order.
the atmosphere is nice but the staff was not helpful and did not take my name. they appeared new or understaffed as they did not know what i asked for. not what i expect out of starbucks.
best baristos and baristas out there. amazing service !!!

extremely friendly! me and my wife and her dog are moving from seattle to nashville and spent the night near the starbucks last night. before hitting the road we stopped here for coffee and breakfast. the two ladies that took our order and gave us our food we're both exceptionally friendly. the food and drinks we're both fresh and the appropriate temperatures. they also offered us a "pup cup" me and my wife both had the double smoked bacon cheddar sandwich. i got some large guava juice thing and my wife got a frappuccino.

i'm happily working my way through the gift cards my students' parents gave me at the end of the year. part of me wants to dislike starbucks and only frequent local places, but honestly i was excited for the excuse to have a frappaccino (sp?). i don't actually like coffee unless it's in dessert form, but i assume that most real coffee drinkers already have an opinion on starbucks. starbucks all over have gone slightly down hill in the baked goods department, but you can still get something decent. although the place is fairly small i had no problem finding one of the comfy chairs for reading. the staff is friendly and seems to know the regulars.
free wifi, free power for devices, over priced drinks. exactly what you would expect.

by far the worst starbucks i've ever been to!! ask for a caramel frappuccino & was given a black coffee. never ever have i done a review so yeah it was absolutely horrible, by the time i realized my order was wrong it was too late. i understand there was a crowd but that is no excuse to totally screw up an order. also my order was left out for someone else to grab because no one ever called my name!! they will never get my business again all customers should be treated with the same respect!

this sbux is attached next to the marriott. it seems to have gone through some remodeling because a part that connects to the hotel had that weird pungent vingegary smell. this sbux is not corporate, so a lot of the promotional events are not available here (e.g., frapp happy hour that happened may 5-14). though the space is small, they could have had at least two more tables--but they don't, so their seating is limited. but you could also sit at the hotel lobby i suppose. their service was...bad. my friend got tea, and they were giving him such attitude when he asked to fill the cup all the way with water. it's just hot water! i didn't think it was an unreasonable request.

located right down stairs from the american airlines club lounge which made it very convenient for us to head down and grab us some of our fav drinks while waiting for our flight. walked right up w/no crowd to place our order -- friendly staff and drinks were wonderful.

yay, a starbucks at the convention center. there were 2 conventions going on and the line was at least 10 mins. fortunately, the staff was organized and had someone down the line to get orders ahead. when it looked like they ran out o f croissant, she told me they had more and pulled them out, $3. my latte was more reasonably priced than at other sbs, $2.75, the pike place coffee was $2.25. the only delay was something wrong with their swipe mode at the register and it wouldn't deduct from my sb gold card so they had to type in the acct number.
i really wish this starbucks was bigger. it's such a nice place to study but it gets so crowded. however, it does have good (working) wifi!
every time i have gone to this starbucks they never fix my drink right and calling them is a joke never answer.
i love this starbucks! best barista eva, i like them when they chit chat with me while i'm waiting ;) have a great day guys!

this starbucks is amazing i'm not sure why other people feel differently. google had the wrong hours posted for when they opened and i got there before they were even open and went thru the drive thru. they literally took my order anyways and made my drink and food to perfection. i wish i got those employees names, but either way hopefully they see this and know who they are. i tried the starbucks down the street and the one i think in marrero and neither compare. the only thing that is a little bad is the lines are always long here, but that isn't the baristas faults! it's busy and they are doing their best. i'm sure they're short staffed as most customer service jobs are! love you guys so much

ok, so most everyone knows what to expect from starbucks coffee as its a large chain. with this said, this location has a few things that set it apart. i will start with pros and go to cons: pros: - interior design: chic and large. i often use coffee houses to do some reading, and this place has plenty of cozy seating and good decor. - parking: i have almost always found parking in their lot cons: - service/staff: not the top notch service i have come to expect from starbucks. there are times i have to wait for them to notice me to get an order in. i have had a few hiccups with getting my drinks right overall, decent experience, but had so much potential as it is one of the most aesthetic starbucks i've seen. still good experience and great place to come and get some work done if you're a coffee shop person
very nice baristas extremely friendly and considerate .clean ,always has my special cake

when i pay for two lattes i expect to get full cups, not here! when i let the barista know she needs to fill the cups she shot me a death look. great customer service, not! she did not even come over & look. just turned back to pouring milk.

i frequent this starbucks as it's closest to my job, however the staff at this particular store are not very knowledgeable when it comes to specific drinks (even though i had the drink with the ingredients pulled up on my phone. candy cane frappuccino: grande= vanilla bean frappuccino w/ 1.5 pumps of peppermint). i also ordered a puppaccino for my dog and both of them looked at me with this look of pure loss as to what on earth i was talking about. then when i explained to them what a puppaccino is they looked at me like i totally inconvenienced them although it was just myself and one other customer in there. pretty sad that i had to explain what both items i ordered contained and i'm not even a barista. and to top it off, the store was out of peppermint syrup so i had to order something different.

what an excellent starbucks. yes, we have quite a few already within a 3-5 mike radius, but this one has the best layout and furniture. according to the barista, they just opened around april 5. high ceilings give it a light and happy feel, especially coupled with the light wood furniture. there's black and gold wallpaper on the side that match the furniture perfectly. with the warm lighting overhead, all of this makes for an overall soft look. there are plenty of seating options! this is ideal for the laptop people and social people. there are around 4 wheelchair accessible tables that seat four people, two very long high tables that seat 10 people each, one high table that seats four, one long couch with tables, around 4 comfy lounge chairs, and stools at the bar where drinks are handed out. outlets are everywhere. what's nice is there's the standard 3-prong outlet but also two spots to put phone charger cords in - great for my iphone and laptop both! customer service seems solid so far. concern: currently the potholes in the driveway from 31 into this shopping plaza is littered with tiny jagged potholes. it's really bad, so drive slow! this is the only reason why this place does not get all the stars - you'd think starbucks would pressure who controls the shopping plaza to make things right for their grand opening month. i've kept it at 4-stars since this is just my first visit, and the pothole issue, and i'll update later if i think it's stellar enough to get 5.

recent turnover rate has been high. i miss all my favorite baristas. but the staff is friendly and tries hard. it would get 5 stars except for the fly problem. it has gotten better but the flies are still around. they keep the air up high so bring a sweater if you plan to stay a while. also, the music is a loud if you sit in the corner.

best service during a packed black friday in the galleria! the line was outside filled with impatient shoppers needing their fix. once you made it inside to order, the employee at the register named "drew" was oozing with happiness and excitement to take your order and the employee "cara" passing out the drinks and food was just as happy, cracking jokes, smiling and giggling! now that's how you run a business! i give this location a 5 star!
the staff was really nice but i waited 25 minutes for my drink that was made wrong so i didn't even have time to get them to fix it bc i spent my whole break here.

um. what happened to the caliber of employees at starbucks? especially with the prices going up! pathetic service, zero attention to detail! they made my iced drink hot and dropped my bagel on the ground... the last bagel. i love to write good reviews but i could not avoid commenting on this. they did apologize several times... there is just a lack of enthusiasm and care that cannot go unnoticed. btw i'm a former barista that happened to love serving customers and providing and above and beyond experience for my guests... maybe my expectations are too high, i'll stick to my new local, mom & pop coffee shop... thank you very much.

holy crap these are the most incompetent workers. how do you mess up a plain bagel w/ cream cheese?! or, no creamer at 8am?! these stoners workers are slow & thats the last time i'm going there. corporate needs to step in on these fools.

being located the the brandon mall doesn't detract from this location at all. it's your average, run-of-the-mill starbucks. during any of my visits, i haven't experienced the slow service others mention. i guess i've just gotten lucky, or maybe i've just been bumped to the front of the line due to my dashing good looks & charming personality. i like to think it's the latter of the two. this location can get busy, due to the location, but as i mentioned earlier, they do good with herding people through. drinks are made with precision, just like i'd expect from any other starbucks location. overall, the prices are high, but it's a pain to make a java chip at home, so i'll keep paying them.

omg! every single time i get coffee it's a 20-30 minute wait just to get through the line- i can't believe it. i thought it was just once or twice and gave them the benefit of the doubt- but no one has that much time to sit in line and wait for a coffee . someone needs to get some sense of urgency

the closest starbucks to where we are staying for gjnc, there is one in our hotel but it doesn't take the app or gift cards. friendliest staff here that i've encountered! service with a smile from every employee here. minus one star for not having mobile ordering enabled though. i live off of mobile order bc it's just easier to customize my drinks exactly how i like it. and sad they were already out of some of the breakfast sandwiches, but i'll let it slide bc there are many other people in town for the same thing and likely needed portable breakfast. amazed that they have nitro brew here!
i honestly work at this starbucks, and when i say we do a lot, we do a lot. making beverages constantly for hours straight can be stressful. we try to always make the customers happy i do know that.

before deciding to go to starbucks, i grabbed a cup of coffee from dunkin' donuts the day before. the coffee was very bland and the service was not spectacular...no comparison here with starbucks. upon arriving at this starbucks location, i was impressed by the cleanliness and the fast service this location displayed. this location was very serene and relaxing. it provided an atmosphere where one can write a business letter or even draft a paper with little background noise. some locations allow you to use your laptop but they have background, non compromising noise that interferes with focusing and concentrating on a task. i ordered a tall peppermint mocha that was topped with whipped cream and peppermint bits. this coffee was rich in flavor and very inexpensive. the customer service here was great! i will definitely return with my laptop and order another one of their flavorful coffee drinks in the future. i have been to other starbucks locations, but this one was the best yet, by far.

this is by far the best starbucks in town, i come here to study and it's pretty quiet. there's plenty of seating and is pretty large for a starbucks which is really nice. they have the happiest, nicest employees i've ever met at a starbucks. it's always very clean as well. i always love coming here and it's becoming a regular spot for me.

by far one of the more people friendly and welcoming starbucks i've been to. i'm not a consecutive customer but when i have some extra change or i can't stand another day of trying to choke down office kitchen coffee i'll make a stop in. what really stands out about this place other than the delicious coffee is the gentleman who manages the establishment unfortunately i haven't caught his name but i appreciate the fact he remembers how to spell my name (it's a bit off from how it's pronounced) especially since i'm not an every day customer. it's those little insignificant personal touches that go a long way and are truly appreciated.

horrible customer service. the people working the driveway are always so upset and rude. every single time i order a hot breakfast sandwich they come out freezing cold and they will not give me another or reheat. worst starbucks location ever.
stellar service during a power outage- felt like 100 degrees inside. thanks for hanging in there!

really a no star rating. ordered an iced green tea latte and was made a green tea frap. when i asked to get the drink i really ordered, the barista made sure to let me know how irritated she was with me. seriously! it's not my fault the individual at the register is incompetent and i had to step away to the restroom while you blended my drink. i didn't even ask for a refund of the price difference, but was still treated like i personally attacked her. next time the amazing well coffee place is closed i will bypass getting coffee in fishers.
every friday is decaf mocha latte day. 2 extra pumps of mocha and make it grande please.

people who worked there were very nice and made the drinks in a timely manner and they were also right on the money with them as well, which made it , soooo much better. also there is so seating area in there.
just know if you go through the drive through, you'll wait 30 mins + and honestly, it's ridiculous. should not be this slow especially for just a muffin. this location sucks.

i'm a regular at this location because of the ina construction but now that you can cross the freeway i suggest going to the location in front of target. by far the slowest service ever. every time i go it's 20-30 minute wait no matter if they're busy or not. the drive thru is the absolute worst. went inside to see if the wait was better and ended up standing there for 25 minutes. what a joke it is to have 5 people working the line but having 1-2 drinks leaving the counter every 7-10 minutes because employees are too busy talking amongst each other. seriously, drive to a different location. it's worth the extra drive.

this is my favorite starbucks locations, i come several times a week before work and order on the app. it's always sitting there waiting for me even when i order it 3 minutes before i arrive. the staff is amazingly kind, especially elizabeth who makes my drinks most mornings and always speaks to each customer. little kindnesses like that go a long way in starting your day!

love this starbucks; i go at least a couple of times per week. my drinks always come out on point and the staff is very friendly! it's a little bit small, so finding a table is difficult at certain hours, but that is not the end of the world. the sandwiches and bakery items that i get are always tasty.

my two item order was served incorrectly and with attitude. i always ask for my breakfast sandwich on a plate, and it is almost always served in a bag. besides being wasteful (and face it, starbucks is pretty self-righteous about environmental friendliness), it is represents that getting a very simple order correct is unimportant. i can't imitate in writing the attitude i received when i said the order was to be on a plate, but it was not friendly nor did she offer to make the order correct. i visit starbucks less and less, and this one often reminds me of how employee attitude affects workplace atmosphere and customer satisfaction. i just ate my bacon, gouda, and egg sandwich and it was cold in the middle.

i know, it's a starbucks. i promise, though, that this one is better than most. the baristas are friendly, quick, and they make a mean cup of whatever you drink. it's not as busy as other starbucks as well, given its weird location. this means that there are usually tables available with outlets. that's a huge plus for a freelance programmer like me. plus, their google wi-fi is ridiculously fast. this is usually due to the low number of people in here, but man, it's faster than my cable internet at home most of the time. needless to say, i come here very often and i stay for hours. i'm a fan.

this starbucks is located in a publix. i believe it used to be a safeway. the location is new and updated. the storefront inside the publix is small but they offer everything from drinks to pastries to sandwiches. the prices seem pretty comparable to an actual starbucks. plenty of parking in the shopping center as well. the staff was friendly and nice. we just got bacon gouda breakfast sandwiches but they were great and nice and warm. i would come here again.

the sad part this review, i am the duke and i normally do a mobil order and the last two times i decide to order and i have realized this the slowest store out of the two hundred fifty store and stands i have visited, from reno to washington, to las vegas, san diego, to denver, to houston etc...... the staff was extremely weak and lack customer service skills. the fact the young blond hair lady (girl) was awful! it's a starbucks, everyone on staff in the building should be able take orders and make drinks, if they can't get rid of the dead weight! took twenty minutes to get a coffee thru the drive thru. this store should rename it as the drive and wait not the drive thru! poor experience use mobil at this store or go to a different store!
slowest starbucks ever!! i've been to this location multiple times and it has never taken less than 20 minutes to get a drink.
they just shove that pike down your throat. pour over request greeted with a smirk and wait 15 minutes

pros: the staff here are super friendly and maintain their composure during very busy times. cons: it often takes 10-20 minutes to get a regular or iced coffee if you order at the counter. also, some of the staff seem to have a knowledge gap when it comes to drinks and such. one guy was unsure how many espresso shots were in a drink and another person didn't know which sugar free syrups you had and just said "i think it's only vanilla" he didn't bother to ask or check. however, the most frustrating thing is the qa with some of the products. the last few times i have been in there the iced coffee was practically see-through it was so light. it seems like they added too much water to it. i've been drinking black iced coffee at starbucks for about 20 years, at locations all over the country, so i'm not just being a karen. i've actually had to ask them to replace it with cold brew, which usually looks better. i want to give them 5 stars because most of the people are nice overall, but some of the service aspects are lacking. hope they fix these things.
extremely understaffed for a wednesday morning at 7:45 am. at least 15 people waiting either a mobile order drive-through or counter order.

find this starbucks nestled in the middle of the philadelphia premium outlets. naturally they're packed weekends. friendly, quick service. first time i've tried a flat white at a starbucks, and it won't be my last. normal amenities for a starbucks. besides coffee, there's food and merchandise for sale. need a bathroom? the outlet bathrooms are a short walk away.
this morning i saw a barista add cold water to my drink because she failed to steam enough milk to fill the cup. appalling. they aren't about the coffee here. they're about moving people through.

i'm not a starbucks hater. usually, i'm happy with pike place roast, as reliable a franchise product as a mcdonald's hamburger. but not today. i'm not sure what happened. based on the taste, i'd hypothesize last night's pot was rewarmed, or fresh water was run through old grounds. the petite vanilla-bean scone was much appreciated, a good way not to overindulge. but the ppr was the worst i've ever had. forced to go to one star -- couldn't finish it, had to pour it out. hopefully a onetime aberration from what has been a fine starbucks location.

the first time she forgot to add my mocha chips so she added them and double blended my drink which is a big no no for me. the second time tho it was spot on. even tho i always order the same thing a tall they were gonna charge me for a venti at this location because "the extra ice was going to make it fill the venti cup" (i asked for a larger cup because of the ice. i didn't realize ice cost money? they let it slide.....this time! whew ;-)

this place is amazing. always so friendly they're always busy but working hard, they know me and my drink by name even though i'm just one of many customers that come in there. it is definitely my favorite location in town

we stopped by this downtown starbucks location early on a sunday morning before heading out of town. the cashier was super friendly and personable, which is always nice. the shop was clean and our orders were quickly served up. there is a drive-thru here if you don't want to park. great visit!

most inconsistent starbucks on the planet. one day it's great and the next day service is awful, they're out of half of your mobile order, and they ran out of venti drink lids. the staff are constantly miserable, and not a soul makes eye contact when you enter their store- forget a greeting. clearly an under managed location, sad for how new it is.

yes, this is a busy starbucks, but even when the line is very long (peak hours) they move people through quickly. they always prepare my drink exactly the way i ask and i have never had a problem with them getting my order wrong. there is one male barista (i wish i could remember his name) who is super entertaining and nice to all of the customers and puts a smile on my face every time i come through the drive-thru. i always hear him say, "there she/he is!" when someone comes up to the window. even though i am so tired when i roll through at 6 am on fridays, this guy cracks me up. i also had a great interaction with a female barista there one time. she was a very nice young woman who accidentally dropped my credit card on the ground next to the drive-thru window when she was handing it back to me. i had to get out of my car to get it because it had fallen underneath the car, which was no big deal to me at all. i kept telling her it was no problem, but she insisted that my venti iced coffee was "on her" that day. overall, a decent starbucks that seems to amazing baristas!
i love the way this works. the design os more like the christmas one it's better than the tampa version of starbucks and it's not that bar in the inside. what i like the most is the parking spot.

manager gave me grief when i asked him to refill the mug i bought at a different starbucks. i have had this $20 starbucks mug filled at several different starbucks in tennessee with no problem but this guy told me it was against policy if i didn't buy it at that store. i didn't want him to go against policy so i drove away even though he said he would do it this one time.

the people at this location were really nice and friendly, and one of them went the extra mile when i requested my coffee be prepared a little differently than usual. i tried one of their bistro boxes, the protein one, and it was very good. i just wished that coffee didn't cost more than a gallon of gas! i figure if starbucks continues to grow, they could start discounting their foods! rd

what's up with starbucks. i stopped going for a while so i could support small family owned coffee bistro shops. recently stopped at several including this one for a protein box. almost all of the starbucks i stopped at was out of protein boxes. when i asked at the one on disc drive why? she told me we were having a nationwide food shortage???? not aware of that. hopefully the hoarders haven't started hitting the stores yet. imo i believe starbucks is beginning the downward path of bring too corporate, failing on supply and customer service

nice establishment but starbucks is what happens when coffee goes corporate. nameless, soulless, over-cooked, & bitter. don't get me wrong--the folks here are indeed nice and it's a good location!--but i would buy my coffee off the back of a cube van from a guy named "hank" if it was done well.

so, we go all the way to this deptford location bc they have made our drinks perfectly to our specific likings/specifications. so i have been giving them 5 stars. well, today i have a problem. not with their staff or the taste of our drinks, as always. but the big thing for me, they gave me this horrible straw. when i started to drink, it wouldn't come thru the straw. i pulled it out and it actually disintegrated!!! seriously. i'm not kidding where are the normal large green straws that i use everyday? yes, we get starbucks everyday. i have to have it, not only bc it's yummy, but bc of my medical situation (chemical imbalances) which requires me to have a specific amount of caffeine. anyway, what the heck is this cardboard straw? starbucks corporation, stock the stores with the green plastic straws please!

was really excited when this starbucks opened because it was so much more convenient than the glasgow location. was hoping as time went on, they would work out the kinks and get in a groove. couldn't have been more wrong. i almost always have to repeat my drink order 3 times because the person taking my order is also making drinks. they are constantly running out of something...brews, cups, etc. today my egg bites came in an oatmeal cup. i thought it was oatmeal so i circle back and go in only to be told "we were out of the egg bite trays..we should have told you". they never have enough staff and the drive thru takes forever. find another starbucks on your route and skip this one.

the place is fantastic! i went here two days in a row while at a course. it is super busy and everyone packs into the starbucks. but let me tell you... the staff are never phased by the big line and by the time you get to the front there as chipper and happy as ever. i couldn't think of a place more busy and so full with that pleasent of staff. if only walmart was this good of an experience i might actually go to walmart. as for my coffee, tea desert. it's the exact same as every starbucks i go to. nothing over the top special but the service is fantastic.
starbucks was hella busy today, but jake kept calm and had the best attitude! thanks jake

bumbling and questioning the customer was my experience with this location. i'm a regular customer but i usually avoid this store. today, my order and payment, process were messed up, and the fix of both were cumbersome. i had already left the store when i realized my food order was wrong. i came back and was questioned if i had really ordered what i wanted. i think the person taking my was very distracted, and i believe that was the reason for the issues. but she didn't really accept full responsibility.
very nice especially on the inside. they also have the latest clover machine and select coffees that you don't always get at starbucks. this is one of my favorite starbucks.
literally the worst starbucks in tampa. inefficiency and wrong orders are all they seem to serve.

nice, clean location. have been here 2 times and the service has always been great, and my coffee beverage made well. tried the new casacara latte which was good! this is not a large place, on the smaller size, but is nice inside and good service.
no restrooms is inhumane. there is a coffee shop in the next parking lot for you and vons has restrooms if you need.
it's fucking starbucks, duh. they gave my dog a cool little pup cup for his frappuccino.
this starbucks is straight. drive thru service sucks, but if you're coming in to study or chat with friends the baristas at the register are a lot nicer than the drive thru folks.
this is a fantastic starbucks!!! 10/10 would recommend. the coffee is great as always and it is very cozy inside. the baristas there are all super awesome as well.

this is my go to spot near my house when i need a caffeine fix! it is a small space in a strip mall in the out lot in front of the target store. the space maybe small but they have both indoor and outdoor seating with a fire place. makes a great spot for impromptu meetings, and those casual cups of joe. i especially love having a drive through!
slightly slow service but very friendly even when they had to redo my order. bought the cutest stl mug! good location. clean

large lobby area and right across from vanderbilt and mellow mushroom pizza. wifi is pretty fast, line is sometimes pretty long, parking is typically not easy to find even in their lot (it's almost always full unless you're the first person there or it's nearing the end of the day!) not the cleanest, but pretty clean! ya know, for a starbucks it's pretty solid!
why did corporate think it was ok to attach a starbucks to a bank??? small cramped crowded drive thru is always packed! no parking! friendly and spot on staff!!!

great place for a morning breakfast and a nice cup of coffee. love the location and the vintage ambience. it feels like an old coffee shop in a little town. baristas are friendly and nice. the only sad thing about this place is that it now closes at 9:00pm. i hope they change that soon.

product is always the same so no reason to comment on it....they can make my tea right,,,too bad half the time i come here i get attitude served with it too. i can do without that first thing in the morning!

the best starbucks i know. the batistas here are the best. i've asked them to just make something up for me several times and each time they've given me something that i thoroughly enjoyed. so i trust them enough to just pay and let them surprise me. not to mention the energetic and friendly vibes they're always radiating. they'll also always make sure that they get your order right. if you don't like it, they're more than happy to redo it to your liking. and this isn't some corporately enforced attitude. they are genuinely willing to redo your order and make it right. the store itself is setup well. it's nice and cozy in the corner and also has good spots to just study or to do work. people are always here chillin. the store is always kept clean. almost every time i come in, someone is cleaning some part of the store. out of all the starbucks that i've been too, this is definitely the most well run. it'll continue to be the one i'll always go to.

first time at this new location and went through the drive thru. no one in front of me but it took almost 5 minutes before someone came to the window to take my money. once she came she was super friendly but the wait was a little surprisingly. i got a java chip with an extra shot and it was amazing as always.
great location and service! barista was friendly, helpful, and made me a great cup of latte.

horrible service. unorganized. slow. no sense of urgency whatsoever. i counted 4 people behind the counter moving slower than molasses. i've been to a lot of starbucks coffee houses, and this one is definitely the worst i've ever experienced. i waited approximately 20 minutes after ordering my beverage and still they didn't have it ready. i had to ask for a refund because i had to leave. they seemed to be upset that i asked for a refund.. like i was the problem?? anyway, i've been to this location a few times and always left unhappy either because of the service and/or poorly made drinks. the reason i had continued to go is because the location is convenient, but i can't sacrifice my time anymore for lousy service. get it together people!

what was once the most inefficient chase bank i ever visited is now the most inefficient starbucks i've ever visited. when businesses reuse an existing structure instead of new construction, it goes one of two ways. it either works perfectly (think hong kong market), or goes horribly wrong. this starbucks is a great example of the latter. on a recent day i realized that i had not yet had by daily does of caffeine. knowing that my addiction must be fueled, i noticed this location on my way to a nearby car wash. as i pulled up i noticed the spot i thought i should enter was marked "do not enter". slightly perplexed, i drove on till i found the actual entrance. i realize this is likely the best use of the space they had, but it was very confusing and somewhat dangerous. pulling in to the drive thru meant passing several parking areas including one with cars parked immediately adjacent to the drive thru. if the drive thru was full, i doubt those cars could have exited their spots. making matters worse was the height of the drive thru window. i guess it would be perfect for someone in a jacked up f-250, but i felt like a little child reaching towards the sky to receive my drink. maybe the ground could have been built up to offset the difference in height, but it was very awkward. design issues aside, there were also service issues. at the drive thru there must not be a camera like most locations. after taking my order, the line didn't move. the cashier asked two more ties if i had been helped. this could be prevented if there was a camera showing the cashier the same car was still sitting there, but that was the least of my concern. apparently the cashier taking orders was unsure of how to disable the microphone so i was able to hear her entire conversation. that conversation was primarily a tirade against someone in line who had ordered some very complex drink. while i agree that people who make ridiculously complex orders should be ridiculed, what if that had been a tirade against me? i would have surely sped away, tires squealing with my finger raised in salute, but it wasn't against me, so i just sat and listened. when i finally made it to the aforementioned jacked up truck height window, my drink was thrust into my hand with nary a word of thanks. in summary, i am not a fan.

as far as starbucks go, this one is just fine. no drive-thru, which you might expect from being on the end of the building, but i don't think the structure allows for it. still, that's a minor complaint if mentionable at all. my only legitimate complaint was the amount of time i had to wait on a weekday afternoon for a coffee (latte), as there was only one barista working the entire show (register, making orders, etc.). others slowly came out of the woodwork and started to help out, but if you are a crew of three or more, why would you leave only one person alone for an extended period of time? bad culture i'm guessing, or just a lack of focus on the customer experience. still, the drinks were fine and the service was friendly. i have no real complaints beyond the first one. i'd come back (of course i would, it's starbucks...)

not only is this location at a weird intersection the drive through also sucks tremendously. if you don't mind waiting 15 minutes or more for your order then you found the spot. so you must be thinking i was there at the wrong time of day, like during morning rush? uh no! this is their mo, morning noon and night.

i tried it this morning and will absolutely never go back. i get better service from the kiosk at target in west clay. i was coming to work from a different direction this morning and not only did they not care when i told them their thanksgiving blend tasted horrible, but the cashier completely drained my starbucks card and screwed up my rewards when i was trying to buy my coffee with a card for my wife. the barista was rude. not my fault the place is so loud and you can't hear well. i've only recently gotten into the starbucks experience, but i know it's not supposed to be this.

i honestly don't know why there are only bad reviews for this starbucks when i've had nothing but positive experiences at this location. i mostly order my drinks through the app and it's always been ready by the time i get there and if i made a mistake ordering, they quickly fix my drink without hesitation. the customer service at this location is on point. i always make an effort to come to this location even if it's a tad bit further because the customer service is so awesome. hopefully everyone else who had bad experiences will have better ones in the future.

i've been to many starbucks and have been generally satisfied with most of them. not this one. the "certified barista," lucy, seemed stressed out and was stressing out everyone else. she argued with me about what breakfast sandwich i wanted (even though i was pointing at it in the display cabinet), snapped at me a couple of times and gave me a cheap plastic bag to hold a mug we'd bought as a gift instead of the official starbucks bags that were given to everyone else. also, our hot chocolates were not mixed well and there was so much syrup at the bottom of the cup. maybe this was an off day, but i don't plan to return to this starbucks again.
always get my drink right. always with a smile. it makes my day when i get my tea in the morning from this place! it is a fave! do recommend it highly!

my fiancé and i visit this location every time we visit my in-laws. over the past 3 years the service has never changed. there are usually 5-6 employees working but there is no sense of urgency. it takes 3-4 times as log to get our drinks than the starbucks standard. the employees are all over the place and do not seem to have any specific assigned station to keep the store running smoothly. if there was another starbucks closer, i would be more than happy to avoid this one and drive a little further for better service.
friendly staff and quick service. extremely dirty bathrooms and dining area. garbage overflowing, tables not wiped down, floors with debris all over, quite gross restrooms.

the manager needs to learn how to manage. employees seem to think playing around and yelling and laughing as loud as they can is acceptable, very childish and unprofessional. the manager needs to understand they are employees not friends. customers have to yell to be heard over the music and employees. takes forever to get your order. very poor service. like all starbucks way way overpriced.
slowest starbucks ever. seriously. 10 minutes to get a large black coffee. and it's like that every single time. why do i keep coming back?!

bathroom was disgustingly dirty and it's only 3pm on a weekday. when i pulled my pants back up, they were wet from in front of the toilet. barista brittany i think with bleached blonde and black roots made my mobile order wrong and when i asked about it, she admitted to making it wrong but said "i asked for many customizations" and she had a friend laying on the pick up area girl talking. i waited several minutes longer than i should have for my drink. i noticed later the light ice was not lite, in addition to missing my carmel drizzle. maybe the health department should check this place out. maybe they need some customer service training.

convenient location located on the corner of st charles and canal streets on the route of the krewe of proteus parade. the coffee shop is spacious inside with lots of fixed seating against the windows and middle section. the order-counter is roomy too. there is an outlet right by the front entrance which is where i plugged in to recharge my phone while i people-watched by the window ¯\_('~')_/¯ so excited that, so far, every starbucks we visited here in the south still offered my favorite chocolate cookie crumble frappe (oh yeah! plus w/ caramel drizzle on top). this flavor is seasonal back home and is not currently offered. restrooms were out of service, not sure if it was for real that they're not in working order or they just closed it down because it was a busy lundi gras and new orleans was packed with parade watchers. it did get a little rowdy during the parade that evening. we should've stayed away from this corner. review #1728
asked for very light ice and this is what i gotvery disappointing! next time it will be with no ice.

this is your typical small starbucks. true, it's not "the thing to do" in the french quarter. but, if you work at the canal place mall, at westin, or in the office tower and frequent this starbucks, like i do, the staff will remember your name and your regular order. that's all i ask for in a coffee shop.

the problem with this starbucks is that they know people will still come in no matter what they do. this is the slowest starbucks i have ever been to, and their drinks are far from the best. if you go through the drive through, you'd better plan to be stuck in place for at least 15 minutes, regardless of how many cars are in line. the dt line goes straight down the middle of the parking lot. the parking lot is tiny, too, so it gets super congested and it's difficult to get out of your parking spot when there are more than 2 cars in line. also, their coffee and espresso often tastes bitter and slightly burnt. maybe they don't properly clean their machines? unfortunately, this location is right next to where i work and i am as caffeine-addicted as the next person, so they still get a decent amount of my paycheck. yes, i'm aware that i'm part of the problem! haha

there were only two people in front of me in line, yet somebow i waited 30 minutes for a medicine ball tea. i watched the girl behind the counter plod along and leisurely make drinks. she was as slow and docile as a hindu calf, and it was kind of mesmerizing seeing how long just pouring a pre-made cold brew into a glass could take her. eventually i had to speak up and ask for my food, because it was sitting on the counter getting cold while our peaceful friend was organizing the cups by size. she must have noticed something wasn't quite up to par because she said very condescendingly to us plebeians that they were understaffed, sorry about the wait, we're all trying our best. we can't possibly know the stress the service industry is going through was the undertone there as she lifted her eyebrow at me. i'm a former kitchen manager though - that line doesn't work when i can clearly watch you perform. maybe try some espresso before your next shift, i hear it's supposed to give you energy.

i am going to parrot the other reviews. the staff was puzzled, busy and irritated about it all. i got my order correct so i am happy for that. i received my drinks very fast, as i should have because it was fairly slow for them. though i noticed the same car in the drive up screen for quite a long time, and was still in line after we left. maybe drive thru isn't their thing? compared to the other starbucks locally its below par. the actual location was spacious and clean. behind the counter things look disheveled and lots of exposed wiring and knocked over wall equipment that exposed older paint jobs. the parking lot is still a huge mess. in its previous forms as a bank it was a horrible layout. come optimistic but come inside

going inside is not bad, nice environment. the wait in the drive thru though, i've literally waited for 20 minutes before. also, all of my other starbucks have switched to no straws while they still use straws for their iced beverages.

hellooooo new, conveniently placed starbucks. fellow sludents and i have been waiting for you for quite some time. first perk: they kept the del taco building, i just love it, it looks like a spaceship. second perk: there is a drive-thru and they made it accessible to the shoulder going on to 64 so you don't have to wait at the stoplights just to get back out. third perk: the interior. so cool. the big "saint louis" light up logo, the all recycled wood surfaces and industrial metals with the starbucks reserve section is sharp. the seating is great: some high top tables, and tables facing the all open window view. in a wrap up: it looks spiffy. and lastly the fourth perk: the service was stellar today. i know it is only the second day but every worker was smiling, working hard, and not complaining. even when it was jam packed (and it definitely was) this morning at 9 am they were bustling about but not snapping at each other while they navigated the starbucks station. i like the atmosphere, the staff, the accessibility and, oh yea, the pumpkin spiced latte. i'm a happy camper.

sweet first to review! having a starbucks in the mall is convenient and a very good business idea. i ordered a trenta peach green tea lemonade. sooo delicious and refreshing. this particular visit i had the privilege of witnessing a rare, random act of kindness from an employee. i wish i had caught his name, but sadly didn't. he went above and beyond the call of duty to take care of a severely habdicapped customer. i am truly in awe of this young man. god bless the few people that are left in the world and if you're reading this, god bless you.

customer service is horrible. i got a drink i get everyday and it didn't taste as it usually does. they weren't accommodating. i was going to have to pay more fir shots to make it taste stronger, even though 4 shots that i usually is strong enough. i had to complain in order to get it made again. disappointing.

feel like i'm in a homeless shelter. was quite disruptive to hear someone mumbling and see him staring at me. there was another homeless man too although not disturbing anyone. staff does not clean up after people leaving tables dirty.

the customer service provided by the brothers and sisters of this location is unmatched. it's always a memorable experience when lenny, kyle and batman are working. nothing but laughs, good energy and good drinks. thank you for going above and beyond for your customers and adding value to their lives. i'm sure i speak for everyone else in saying, we appreciate you!

i have to change my review. we waited in the drive through for 20 minutes. definitely not coming back to this location anymore. even the person taking the order was super slow. who has time for that?! no thanks!

my usual is the white chocolate mocha and it's a hit or miss with this starbucks. with that said, when the drinks aren't made correctly or to your liking, they are happy to remake your drink. if you don't have the time to get your drink redone, then you have the option to call starbucks or go to their site to file the complaint and they will send you a gift card for the inconvenience. also, it's a licensed location which means that you can't use your free coffee reward here.

worst starbucks ever. bunch of rude workers who messed up my order 2 times in a row, no apologies, and they were extremely rude about it. if you want to add more stress to your morning before you board your flight this is the place to stop.

barista was rude & the worst white chocolate mocha i've ever had! will not come back here! don't expect any smiles from the barista, she obviously doesn't want to be here, i'm thinking she hates her job. i've been sitting in here for 30 minutes and have yet to see her smile. ugh!

sometimes you just need coffee. i'd much prefer to give my money to a mom and pop coffee joint, but honestly, $bux will have to do. the service was excellent, the product was perfect. and the company was great.

went there for a quick seasonal drink. the store was filthy including the working area where andre was making the drinks. he was even placing drinks in milk that was spilled all over the work station. andre was giving incorrect orders to customers. we saw orders sitting on the counter for the 15 minutes we were standing waiting for our order. andre seemed liked he was being bothered by the rush of customers and was not even announcing names for people to know when their order was ready. ordered the white chocolately bliss and it tasted like it was just warm milk, absolutely no flavor. very disappointed.

love this place because it's the only starbucks that never gets my order wrong. but today was the day. first time i have ever encountered the most rude customer service. while ordering the cashier interrupts me with "can i get your name first, and repeat your order" after i pay i'm waiting for about 10 mins for my two fraps (on a non busy day) and a barista comes up to me and asks me if i was waiting for something. um yea my order. upon this time i had only received one frap and i was waiting for the other. not only did she accuse me of not paying for two fraps she gives me a different order. i didn't even complain no need to argue with theses people.

i have mixed reviews on this location: 1) the service is soooo slow! they clearly take care of the drive-thru before bothering with the lobby. i was the only one in the lobby who ordered a drink, and i had to wait over 8 mins for my coffee. really!? while 4-5 cars got their drinks ahead of mine. 2) the positives is that my drinks are usually made consistently well. they either need to hire more baristas because this location is always packed and is only drive-thru starbucks in cherry hill for commuters to philly and in the surrounding areas.

i very rarely go to starbucks because i don't typically drink coffee or tea. after visiting this location, i thought that maybe i have been missing out. there is something to be said about enjoying a delicious beverage with your girlfriends while sitting in a comfy chair and having a gab fest! the staff here was very friendly and helpful albeit rather confused on how to work the register, resulting in the preparation of an incorrect beverage. nonetheless, they were very happy to correct the mistake and didn't act at all upset or annoyed. during their down time, the staff was busy cleaning and restocking and all were pleasant toward each other. overall an enjoyable visit. i will be back to branch out and try something other than a hot passion fruit tea!

the drive thru is a thing of convenience. or so i thought. i am assuming the new people are on the drive thru. i ordered a hot chocolate & a chocolate chip cookie. the young man at the window appears with hot 2% milk. and no cookie. so i told him. sir, that's not my order. he reappeared with a cookie. hmmm, i just took the cookie paid and left. maybe things will improve once they get trained properly. on the bright side noone was rude to me. oh it's gonna be a lovely day.

it's a starbucks, so as far as starbucks go, they are top-notch. witty banter between coworkers entertain us customers as we wait for our beverage. even so, the wait was short n' sweet. i enjoyed sipping my drink at the window high-top and people watch. lucas oil stadium is a stones throw away... if there was a wind, a strong wind. but seriously, i can see it from my high-top table. last note: i like my chai like i like my men - stong, smooth, and keeps me up. and my friends, they make a strong chai here. :o)

this is my go to for a starbucks fix! they are pretty fast it's a small location and mostly cater to students and office staff in area including hospital staff. too bad they don't seem to be open at 6am especially if you start the early shift.
very slow, the drinks are always wrong and it looks like it's our fault when we say it's not what we ordered.

if you've been to one starbucks, you've been to them all, when it comes to the food + drinks, prices, and the overall appearance. we stopped by at around 8pm to do some studying + work, and just bought a cookie. it was perfectly toasted and delicious. what i love about this starbucks is there is plenty of seating. there's large shared tables, bars, small tables, etc. there are also outlets everywhere. like some of the starbucks in my area, the staff try to make the atmosphere more welcoming. this starbucks also has a riddle of the day (where you can ask the barista for the answer) and a bookshelf in the back to leave & take a book as well as some games. the parking lot is not proportional to the number of seats in the starbucks, but luckily it was not busy so we were able to get a spot. they also have reserved parking spaces for those just stopping in for mobile orders or anyone who was only parking for 15 minutes. for any night owls that prefer to grind + do work later in the day, i would recommend this location!

ok, i am not one to write bad reviews. especially for chains because i feel like you'll generally lose some quality somewhere along the way. but this starbucks, i just will not ever be wasting my time here again. the past 3 times here, i've had my ordered messed up. first time, i got the wrong sandwich but correct drink. second, wrong drink and wrong bagel. today, wrong drink, right sandwich. in all of this i know i've been "overcharged" in the sense where i should be getting some money back no matter the amount. but i just can't even bring myself to stay there any longer. the person at the window has always been sweet but i honestly would just like a correct order for once.
no point in mobile ordering when you wait over 20 minutes anyway. this place has been a total mess for a few months in the morning. c'mon people.
while they make a decent coffee like most others, i wouldn't recommend coming here unless you want to hear some of the rudest starbucks employees complain about their lives

the staff at this starbucks is exceptional; engaging, friendly, knowledgeable & helpful and, very customer centered. frankly, today, it was like being at the coffee version of cheers while i was at the counter .... the whole staff. it's also more spacious than most starbucks. so, if you're sticking around, it's a comfortable place to be.

manager tay runs a tight ship here with lots of love from employees. they always go the extra mile to help you get the exact drink that you want. the place is clean and spotless. bring a jacket because it's always a bit nippy.

i'm always welcomed when i walk in, and have had mostly pleasant experiences. isaiah is by far the best barista here and why i'm giving 5 stars! i'll even make a point to order when i walk by and see he's working. he goes above and beyond to craft every beverage and is always super friendly. i wish every starbucks had a barista like that!

took 30 minutes to get my coffee. the line was long, but nothing they shouldn't be able to handle before 10am. i also watched them mess up multiple peoples orders. when i walked out i realized they had given me the wrong order. there was no way i was going to go back in and try to get them to fix it after waiting 30 minutes. people have places to be in the morning.
very unpredictable drive thru wait time. sometimes it is normal but other times it is a parking lot. right now it is a parking lot and it looks like about 20 cars. no movement for about 5 minutes
tell me why i asked for light ice and they fill the cup up with more than half with ice. 3 sips and my drink is gone lol thanks!
classic starbucks. very well decorated. it is definitely very spacious and has wifi. service and coffee was perfect!
the chile mocha addiction is real my friends. i can't shake it. glad this is on i-44 on the way out of town.
great starbucks, friendly and helpful staff. they overfilled my drink and there was no milk out but when i went to the counter they quickly fixed my drink for me.

everytime i go to starbucks i always get a venti dragon drink with no ice because i have extremely sensitive teeth . i always get my drink filled to the top with no ice at any other location i go to. i went to this location and the employee told me he couldn't fill my drink up all the way because i got no ice. i will never return to this location i paid $5.38 for a half filled drink.

usually come there often, but service was beyond rude didn't even greet me, be honest didn't recognize the girl serving too, spend most of the time talking to coworker so just left. time is money and if i'm not getting proper service definitely not giving my money.

everytime i go in there there's this girl named stephanie i think (short, dark hair). she always acts super conceited and i hate the environment she puts out. mobile order is impossible in the morning unless someone is out there helping organise... would only recommend this one when it's slow, don't go if you want to get out fast!!!

wow. hire up people. if you have plenty of time and patience, and looking for a blatant disingenuous interaction, then you'll just love wasting valuable time here. 1st time ever i didn't tip at a starbucks.

rude!!!! the girl taking my order was plain rude. i explained to her how i want to order food and a coffee however my card is never accepted at this location. i go to other starbucks' almost everyday. she said "that's not my fault we can't give you anything then" and pretty much shooed me away. i was so upset never coming back here.

i was on vacation for one week in seminole. this starbucks became my morning ritual. me, the dog and my friend would order our drinks and spend more than an hour in the sun at the outside tables. well, i ordered the drink for the dog -- ice water -- which they happily provided. the only downside they didn't have my favorite breakfast item -- the cherry polenta scone. i guess these things vary region to region and i shouldn't eat it anyway. the staff was super-friendly. they remembered my drink after my second visit and i got my free refills for ice tea with no hassles. a plus, a nice clean bathroom. wish i could spend more time sipping tea everyday. but, alas, it's back to reality.

this starbucks is the worst but unfortunately is the closest. i try to avoid going here when i can. they are ridiculously slow, i've even gone to the new starbucks that opened further away from me and they were much faster and apologized for their delay, which wasn't even a delay. on top of being slow, they forget half the stuff you ask for and can't be bothered to give you a bag or napkins. when you go to the location in mt. laurel all the baristas are zipping around and it's like a production, they're shouting orders out, rechecking to make sure everything is there. i went inside to see if it may be faster at the medford location and to see if maybe i'm just being ridiculous but nope saw them moving just as slow as i thought. they need better training!

every time i visit this location i end up waiting anywhere from 10-15 minutes to get my coffee in the drive through. given the fact that this is the only starbucks near cwe that has a drive through i continue to visit it. the employees are nice but the service moves at snails pace.

my favorite starbucks in town! the employees are ridiculously good at remembering names and orders. i took a month's break from coffee and when i returned they still remembered me, which is crazy considering how many people walk in and out of there. coffee always tastes good, and i have never had a bad experience here!

it's not new york speed, but everyone is very friendly, and attentive. the size is decent and outdoor seating is available as well. it also has drive trough option which is convenient in the way to the beach.

great location and lots of seating- more than some other starbucks locations. very friendly staff and it connected to a bank next door. this allowed for even more seating and a conference table. would highly recommend this location

i live in the gulch. and literally every time i go to this starbucks it is terrible service. every time! customer service is awful. they are rude. get drinks wrong. and once when i asked about turning on music (to make things not so awkward when all their customers are complaining about service), one of the girls replied loudly "no, please don't turn the music on!" it makes this area and nashville as a whole look so bad! again, horrible horrible customer service!

i visited this store on 08/28/18 6:30 am , restroom was dirty , toilet papers was all over the floor, there was no soup to wash hand. the girl gave me dark roast coffee tested like water, i asked her is it dark roast, she moved her head up and down, because she was on the phone texting or searching, she was on the phone texting, when i entered the store also. this place needs to be shut down or fixed unless starbucks likes to runes it's name by this few.
this starbucks stands out from others. the employees are always joyful and the coffee is done to perfection. this location also has a very nice and large lounge to cozy up with a coffee.
love this location! always great service, friendly,fast, always get my order right, and drive through is the biggest plus.
i stop here about 3 times per week to get my favorite pink drink. always greeted with a smile and great service !

it's very convenient to have a starbuck drive-thru instead of going into safeway store. we have been here multiple time. order was quick and fast. we usually came here order for our family on saturdays i and my girlfriend yes it does gets packed at drive thru and order didn't not came out what we expected it. something it's miss understanding and yet there's room to improvement.

super-fast drive-thru (even with the kind of wonky layout). staff was personable and friendly from the order taking to the pick-up window. they made me a delicious strawberry frappuccino to go with my banana nut bread - which they offered to heat and did.

i really like my local starbucks. my drink is always made perfect and when there is a line, its pretty fast. the people who work there are also super friendly. i just really don't like the parking lot. when there is a crazy long line the line goes out in to traffic.. so that part really sucks. make sure you don't park by the front door because you may be stuck in your parking spot if a line forms!

went here yesterday for a pink drink. very good service and no wait time or line. parking was easy to find but the space is a bit tight but overall good experience and friendly and very helpful staff.

this starbucks is convenient, but their drive through has gotten really slow. yesterday, i waited 20 minutes before i even put in my order and stood in place without moving for about 10 minutes. otherwise, the service is friendly. they just need a bigger prep area and more baristas and barista stations.
this is the most understaffed starbucks i've seen compares to the amount of customers.

this starbucks is trying it's best. it's drive-thru is probably the busiest drive-thru not connected to a mcdonald's of chik-fil-a in greenwood. they work hard to get your order correct, fresh, and to you and if they are achieving that they are successful in my eyes. they've been of consistent quality as long as i can remember and the pandemic hasn't put changed that from experiences!
it's starbucks, you all know and love it. great seasonal drinks, location is friendly and speedy, for your average cup of joe this is the place.

they are always friendly. i am not a coffee person but do like a good "spot of tea". their passion tea with lemonade is excellent. sometimes their black tea can be bitter. i am a gold member and love the perks that come with that.

the coffee was really good, the grande americano really hot the spot. the sausage egg and cheese breakfast sandwich was a little rubbery, not the best quality there. the crew behind the bar was on the job and earned their tip.
love the whole staff there.they greet you with your name after couple of your orders.they learn your customized drinks and get them ready when they see you walk into the door.they are just amazing.
it's right by my work so i come here all the time. staff here is great, good service. lunch time extremely busy though with a school nearby!

it's a smaller starbucks, that does not have a drive thru but they can really get things out quickly. i order ahead on the app when i am on my way to work (usually about 10mins ahead of time) and 9x's out of 10 they have it ready when i get there. the staff their goes out of there way to learn you name and make you feel like the care. i have noticed they have had the same staff for a while which is nice because they take the time to know you and your order. i once ordered at the wrong starbucks and they made it for me no questions asked, no fuss. this is probably one of my favorite stores. however be care if you go right before the high schools go to school, there is a large group of catholic school girls who go and if you get your in behind them you are going to have to wait a long time

this is the greatest starbucks of all time. the people that work there are super friendly and super attractive. the atmosphere is relaxed and awesome and the drinks they make are to perfection. if you chose to go to any other starbucks then you are just wrong.
i made a special request and the person (isaiah) who was making my drink was extremely rude about it. i know the customer service at other starbucks location beats the service i received here.

this is my favorite starbucks location. yes, there's a starbucks on every corner it seems, but here the floors, tables, and bathrooms are always clean and the staff are very friendly in comparison with the indifference that feels so common to a starbucks visit nowadays. even when my app wasn't functioning one day, preventing me from paying, the barista gave me my drink for free and was so kind about it. it's my go to spot to get my favorite coffees and snacks on the go, or to sit and work peacefully.
your coffee is way to strong omg i rather get the latte but u guess it is kinda ok i lake wawa coffee better though
coffee is good but a grande size is now what a tall used to be. not good--lower the price if that's case. there are many other coffee places near here to visit

starbucks as a whole has gone so downhill it's unreal. first peach green tea is gone, now black tea lemonade is ridiculously hard to find. no consistency. this store is drive thru only now and like 9am-1pm. so now i get to wait 10+ mins regardless of if i order ahead or not.

wow... went in to get a coffee for myself and a puppacino for my pooch. this is the very first starbucks in the country that has ever wanted to charge me $.10 for a dixie cup with whipped cream... for shame! especially considering you lost out on my $10 drink order to make $0.10 on a dixie cup. smart business move!

these motherfuc$ers charge for honey. after spending $10.62 on water, a fruit juice and a venti jade mint i asked for honey for my tea. the challenged barista gave me one honey, i asked for more and she then said you have to pay extra for honey. i'm giving you two right now but you have to pay extra next time. the way i want to curse her ass out right now. a whole venti and two honey unless i paid for extra honey.

a good starbucks. the couple times i've been here the staff has been nice and got the orders right. ambiance is nice too- it has a fireplace, some cushy couches, a couple computer bars and a study room. the pasty case is well stocked and everything is pretty clean

nestled and small, i didn't expect much from this starbucks. granted i'm not a big fan but 1) it was raining and i was cold and 2) i had recently broken my large coffee cup and needed a replacement. so while waiting for a prescription at cvs next door i went in for a basic cafe mocha. they don't short you on whipped cream either. the atmosphere is calm, baristas friendly. today i'm filling a prescription and plan on grabbing a coffee simply to enjoy the quiet. i would never say that of the other starbucks locations i've visited.
starbucks by e29.... customer service is terrible!! rude and slow. if you don't want to work...quit!

i'm in here a few times a week during the morning rush. the manager is almost always working the floor rather than passively hanging around - and she's pregnant, too! the staff is always very, very friendly (even when the manager is not working). keep in mind that this is an extremely popular location, so there is always a line in the morning. but i've never had to wait an annoyingly long time for a beverage, and the quality is always great.

this place is like the fort knox of straws. whenever i am in there i am literally reprimanded for taking a straw! leave the straws out like most other starbucks so then i won't have to reach for them or bother you to get one! on top of that the inventory management at this location is awful. there is no food essentially available after 2-3 pm despite the fact many students stop by into the evening. they don't seem to manage the staff well either. frequently there are long lines and people leave bc it will take too long.
worst starbucks experience. the baristas here need to find a job in something other than customer service.

i wish they had more seating. this place has a great warm & cozy vibe. i'm pretty certain if they had comfy seats it would be hard to get people (me!) to leave. i'm guessing it caters to the millions of people who work here in the comcast building (which totally makes sense) vs the occasional passerby who wants to sit & relax for a while. otherwise, it's a starbucks. just like any of their other places as far as drinks & food goes.

i always have pleasant experiences when i stop by this location embedded within the tampa premium outlets. small corner store centrally located by outdoor seating area, fountains, guest services and foodcourt. this starbucks provides friendly and attentive service, and shorter lines than most. so far my drinks have all been made well. i can't think of any mishaps or misspelled names on my cups. for a small shop there are plenty of places to sit and eat or lounge. not to mention the number of places to sit comfortably right outside. i tend to stop in for a chai latte or light snack while shopping since i'm not a fan of the foodcourt. since i'm a huge fan of the neighboring coach store, this starbucks can't be missed. since tampa locals seem to need starbucks to live, they nailed it by opening one in every mall here. the outlets are no exception.

christmas eve and a very good atmosphere. staff were very busy but maintaining a very pleasant demeanor. taking orders as they came and delivering with a smile. made the stop for spiced chai latte all the more pleasant. and of course the drink was spot on!

great place to stop by while going in or out of whole foods, which is just a few doors down! they had dark roast still brewing when i showed up at 4pm today, which is great, since i abhor the pike place. of course they're always willing to do a pour-over of any coffee you want, but it's always nice to have good coffee already made for you :).

this was the worst starbucks i've ever visited. service was terrible and slow. the staff working at the terminal c location were rude, loud and unfriendly. when you pay $6 for a coffee you expect happy polite service. it took me longer to get a coffee than it did to check in my bag and get thru security. visit another starbucks if you can. this one was bad!

no complaints about the service. i do have to complain about the fact they were more worries about mask protocol then handling problems. the good: got 8 shots over ice and 2 sandwiches. came out with the wrong cup with is fine. sandwiches were quick, nice. as i turned to leave, my cup scrunched upnand i lost a couple shots of espresso. i cleaned it up and went to tell the barista just so they know that there's a lightly wet floor and they could handle it. as i was explaining what happened, my mask apparently got caught on my stubble and slowly lowered below my nose. so a completely different barista who saw me talking decided to chide me about my mask. i'm not against wearing my mask as it is currently required. nor am i against adjusting it if needed. but it is rude to interrupt someone attempting to communicate a situation because you wish them to adjust their mask because it's below their nose.
small sb. took a long time in drive through. didn't seem all that busy but maybe orders were large. very small.

my husband and i are frequent customers of this starbucks location because we live nearby to it. we typically drink starbucks anywhere we go due to the fact that they offer the specialty coffee drinks and we run into little to no problems when ordering. unfortunately at this location, more often than not, we have to send the drink back because it's missing something we asked for. this is alarming because what if someone who was ordering a specialty drink made a certain way due to health issues such as diabetes and their request for sugar free sweetener was ignored? i've never ran into this issue so much at any other location that we have ordered from and hopefully the next time a barista repeats the order back, they actually make it the way they are saying it.

today i went through the drive through before work and i ordered a trenta iced green tea non sweetend. they gave me a venti and i decided to let it slide. i noticed the color of the tea looked weird so i asked the lady "this is unsweetened, right?" she shook her head and said yes. i should have known better and tasted it while at the window, but since there was a line of cars behind me i drove off. once i got to work i tasted my drink and it was fully sweetened and disgusting. i'm really bummed as normally they get my order correct as it's not a hard order. this is an *update* i went back in for another drink and after explaining that they keep messing up my green teas, they gave me a free green tea. they said they are training new people and mistakes have been happening quite often lately. they really do want to make their customers happy and i appreciate them giving me a free drink. thank you!
don't like the environment. get's too crowded and unorganized. most workers just seem over it and not super willing to help. never see the people i liked working there anymore :(

absolutely love this starbucks! the baristas are always so nice! i feel like i've made genuine connections with the employees. tori, one of the baristas made my day by giving me a free coffee. she said "you have a big day tomorrow and i love that you always come in and tell us about you and your life!" so so sweet, and truly made my day! thank you for being such a welcoming, kind, and compassionate starbucks!

let me first say that i have not yet been to a starbucks that wasn't super busy but this one is ridiculous. however, it is to be expected because of it's close proximity to the westgate brandon mall, the amc movie theater and the i-75 exit. when they first opened they were extremely slow getting orders out, especially for the drive thru. i thought i might run out of gas while waiting. they have gotten a little better with time but still too slow. keep in mind the location if you plan to go to this one. you will wait.
