great location for studying on campus. it has a relatively small "starbucks" serving area but tons of space for studying, catching up with folks, and enjoying their fireplace (ok it's gas but whatever).

starbucks usually have their acts together. morning prime time and things are humming. this starbucks, people standing around looking lost. long line with nobody being helped. coffee that is mundane. with so many great coffee shops in town (shout out to handlebar), why did i come here?

i am giving this starbucks location one star for their lack of humanity. here is what happened: as i am waiting for my drink at starbuck's on this cold winter's night, in walks an old man who needs to use the restroom. i sense his need was urgent. and being that it was late virtually all of the downtown establishments were closed. so the counter gal tells this kindly looking old man that if he buys something that she can give him the key to the restroom. i am aghast and speechless at this incredulous lack of humanity (obviously a store policy that the gal felt compelled to enforce). the man quickly says that his companion will buy something. his companion, a young man, possibly his grandson, begins thumbing through his wallet for money. just then the counter gal tells me that my credit card did not go through. i come around the counter again to pay and i see the young man looking through his wallet still. i thought he did not have money so i told the gal that they are with me and to let the man use the bathroom. then the young man says, "no, it is ok. i would like to pay for your order." he hands me a gift card. my order was $10.10! he goes on to say, "it's worth it as long as he gets to use the bathroom." i was incredibly touched by this young man's compassion, so concerned for his companion that he would pay without hesitation $10 to starbucks so that old man could use the facility. then he wished me a merry christmas. this young man blessed two people that night with his compassion: his grandfather and me. as for starbucks, you need to change your store policy. i will never shop at this location again.

we stopped by this starbucks yesterday and had a great experience. we used the drive thru and placed our regular order. the barista taking our order was nice & friendly. our order took longer than expected (an iced latte and a frappuccino). it was okay because the gal taking the order made some small talk with us and made us laugh. i know a new location goes through growing pains and some orders can take longer than expected, but she made it great. we will definitely be back when we are in the area.

the service here is excellent. the parking is still pretty awful - and now there is a "super" wawa next door. nonetheless, i still come in every month or so for a $5 latte. i love it, it's expensive, i feel guilty when i do it - but i gotta have a starbucks fix every once in a while.

this is probably the best starbucks in reno! it's right near where i live and i come here to read or draw because it's so comfy, i've never got anything but positive baristas and my order has never been wrong!

my personal opinion is that espresso during travel adventures is an absolute necessity of life. while everyone else orders caramel macchiato frappuccino half-caf whipped extra hot concoctions from the starbucks corporate lab in seattle washington... i'm more than happy with a double espresso aka doppio. amazingly, doppio is the actual authentic italian word to order a double shot! it works at starbucks and anywhere in italy! however, the coffee quality in italy is quite a bit different and to-go is definitely not an option. at this particular location, you cannot use the app to pay for reasons totally unclear to me. but, cash still works. finally, death before decaf!

this was a perfectly typical starbucks, like 100's of others. they provided me the caffeine jolt i so desperately needed and sent me on my way, buzzing around like a fly on meth. ok, it wasn't that exciting but it hit the spot!

my girls love starbucks every time we pass a starbucks they want to stop. yesterday we were celebrating my daughter's birthday weekend, passed starbucks and had to stop in the drive up to get their favorites. well, to our surprise and disappointment. the cookie and cake pop was delicious as usual, but the 3 drinks were not good at all. they lacked flavor, half the drink was liquid, and the rest taste like slushy ice, with lumpy deflated whipped cream. there was only 1 car at the window, which left pretty quick. we were next, but waited at the window for a very long time. it appeared the barista was making several orders while we waited. now there is a line around the building ‍‍so so disappointed. a total waste of my $22, then they kept my bank card and ruined my daughter's birthday shopping spree because we had to leave the mall to go back to get my bank card in order to shop. i don't recommend 2705 veterans blvd.

this is your run of the mill starbucks, in my opinion. friendly staff, useful wi-fi, great drink selection, cheerful holiday decorations. it's a bit on the small side, but that's nothing major. i have always been happy with the quality of my service and beverage/food after visiting this location. keep it up!

trick or treat! well i got the trick today on halloween. i ordered a pumpkin scone. a caucasian male came out from the back area of the store with the scone in the little brown bag closed. i waited a couple of minutes later and received my matcha tea. when i got home10 minutes later and opened the bag, i found the pumpkin cake to be steal. so steal that the pumpkin scone crumbled as soon as i touched it.. it's a shame they've got a dude in there who clearly knows nothing about customer service and seems to be intentionally undermining good customer service. if i opened the bag and saw and felt that it was steal, the guy clearly had to know he was giving out a steal cake. beware the hefty caucasian male about 5'11 and check very carefully anything he is handing to you.

this is a great starbucks to go to if you are looking for a spacious area to set up work shop. the wifi is pretty good here, i had no issues signing in while i was there. i really like the big patio too! the service here is friendly and fast, plus the drinks are consistent with all other starbucks.

i like my coffee like i like my baristas: tall, dark and one shot of sweetness. this team of coffee always perky, helpful and beyond friendly! never a long wait in the drive-thru or inside. when i ask questions about drinks, they give me a pleasant, helpful answers. what more could a coffee girl want? my tall orders: passion tea with one pump of sweetener (100 cals), vanilla latte with coconut milk (150 cals), regular coffee with half & half and raw sugar (80 cals).

worst and slowest starbucks ever. 20 minutes to get a hot tea. the staff was serving the drive thru and the inside counter was back up 10 orders deep. there were 3 employees working the drive thru and 1 working the counter. i will be looking for another coffee tea place and will not be returning to this location.
you know, i was going to write a bad review about a worker there. ...but decided to take the high road...:)
friendly staff but the drive thru lane is unfortunately extremely slow. i usually drive out of my way to go to a starbucks that is much quicker :-/
great staff. they are super friendly. great indoor and outdoor seating areas. they make the best lattes in town. drive thru is convenient and fast, especially when its raining.
this store has remedied the blocking of doors and early closing of tables since my last review.

pretty solid starbucks. i recently started going to this starbucks on my way to work. they do a good job of really trying to get you out of he door quickly in the morning. even though sometimes the line can look really long, it goes much quicker than it looks it will.

during my travel, this location has been the closest branch to my stay. they have been so accommodating as i am a healthcare worker and i have gone at least three times per week over the past few weeks.their hours have been cut short 10-6:30pm due to the crisis, but i am grateful that they're still open so i at least come on my days off. thank you for volunteers coming from other branches just to stay open for this community!

my friend and i often meet at this location to study. the staff are always friendly, efficient, and the drinks are always ready promptly. the drinks are always tasty and made to order (though i usually don't deviate from the normal with my coffee). the store is clean. it is not the biggest location, but we never have trouble finding a place to sit. definitely recommend this location.
i've been to this starbucks a few times while traveling to my relatives. it is clean and the staff is nice and fast.

i think this location does a great job, much better than royersford with speed, card reloading is never an issue and i know how hard starbucks employees work. my little cousin got treated awful at the blue bell location. kudos to you guys! :) and to the guy who always remembers my name! very nice people.

i've never had one starbucks screw up so many drink orders multiple times! in the drive thru no less. people choose the drive thru in order to save time. having to turn around and go back inside completely takes away from the experience. you've been warned.
no complaints for my drive thru. eveyone was wearing mask and gloves. i got my java chip frappe perfectly made.

i'm a basic bitch that loves starbucks so i will leave nothing positive reviews! service is pretty good for the center city location! it sucks that it's pretty crowded but that's not their fault! bigger location would help but that wouldn't keep me from coming back and getting my ice cold latte!
the nicest workers of any starbucks i've ever been to! always ask how you are at the drive thru and chat with you at the window if there's time. they always make my day!
do not go here on saturdays earlier afternoon because they have only one person making drinks and it took forever!!!! i couldn't believe how long it took to get a frappuccino.

this will probably be the last time i come to this starbucks location. today i got a notification from the starbucks app that if you bought a drink, you would get a free one in the future. so i decided i would stop in, do some work and get a drink but unfortunately i picked the wrong location to do so. the barista had no idea what i was talking about and when i showed her the advertisement on the starbucks app she basically told me that i wouldn't be able to participate in the deal while simultaneously preparing food in the microwave for the drive through customers. she was not the only person working and someone else should have been dealing with the drive through food or she should have finished with the food and then said she could help me, not try and do both at the same time... it made me feel really disrespected honestly. the drive through is extremely favored at this location. if you're going to go here, don't even bother coming inside. i'm pretty sure about 8 cars got served before my drink was made and i'm pretty sure they only started making it because i got up from my work and stood by the counter. once the drink was actually made, it was practically thrown at me with drink coming out of the top of it. the barista said she would get me napkins but at that point i just wanted to leave. the drink wasn't even that great since the whole thing was rushed and the cafe itself was crawling with flies all by the cash register. i ended up just going home to finish my work there... free of flies and bad service. i highly recommend the other westshore location over this one.

i ordered a cloudy caramel macchiato with super light caramel drizzle, the lady gave me it with extra caramel drizzle, i let her know i asked for light and she got mad. i understand the frustration but if i'm paying $6 for a coffee i'd like it made correctly. she dumped it into another cup and the drink was ruined (no longer foamy like it's suppose to be) i will not be returning to this starbucks.
nice location on a sunday morning. friendly helpful staff and spacious layout. a full range of pastries snd sandwiches are available. ample parking too.
drinks are fine, but the service is inefficient, and quite slow. if you are a commuter, consider passing this location on the way to work. it is a ten min it stop for anything that isn't drip coffee.

no drive-thru but the designated 15 minute parking on the front parking lot for your "grab & go" convenience is pretty noteworthy. the interior is a large open space with lots of seating & nooks for studying in solace. there is even patio seating available for nice weather days. service is pleasant and the cafe is both well stocked and well kept. i particularly enjoyed the artwork. ~christy

the ladies behind the counter were on it today. i walked up to a 10 deep line and they cranked through it like pros. not the most friendly bunch, but efficient, and sometimes that is just good enough. star off for the sitting area being a bit messy.

place looked like it hasn't been cleaned in a week and the 2 people at the register had attitudes like i should know the whole menu inside and out. i go to starbucks like 3 times a year. the people making the drinks were really nice

i really enjoy going here, because it is less than a mile away from my house. the service is very good, they are pretty quick about getting orders out, and they have never made a mistake about my order.
fast & friendly. a big plus was they were open until 10pm which is a huge for those working later in the evening. coffee was spot on. not sure what the complaints are all about but i will be back.

fortunately, coffee drinkers are addicts so they put up with a lot to get the coffee. very rude workers. petty. no customer service. they don't even try to be decent. convenient location is best attribute. usually there's parking, but wow, putting up with the disrespect and rudeness of the "help" is very hard. 90% of customers are using apps.
i love this starbucks! the employees are so quick and are always smiling. my son and i frequent this location often and its always a pleasant visit.

this is by far the best starbucks around town, i love it! i pass so many other starbucks just to get to this one. it's like they're all trained well. to treat customers right! i will continue to make this my starbucks! keep up the great work management!

this is a cute little stand alone starbucks (not tucked into a target store like so many) nice decor staff is friendly....i do not drink starbucks coffee but i like that i can get a hot or iced black sweetened tea. found myself chinchillin here with my laptop for awhile one day and it was a nice place to be......

got the chorizo sandy today and it was so disgusting! took the chorizo off and ate it with just the egg because i was so hungry... and now my stomach feels like its being ripped apart. other than that this is a good location, come here quite a bit to get my coffee and cake pop fix.
i don't know why we come here anymore. this location is always dirty.
worst starbucks i have ever been to. my daughter's coffee was so weak, it had zero coffee taste. my husband's decaf was made with instant coffee and hot water. he saw them make it.
waited in line at the drive-thru for 30 minutes at 2:30 pm. no option to leave because of the curb on both sides. absolutely ridiculous. will not return.

i used to spend a lot of money here. worst experience today at this starbucks at hillsborough & memorial in tampa. they ground my coffee incorrectly, argued with me, and refused to correct it unless i bought something else. blew my mind. they were incompetent, rude, condescending, disrespectful and greedy.

you have a very rude (continuously) female barista who must hate her job, or hate life in general in the morning hours before 9am on the weekdays. i've experienced this and have seen her be rude to others as well. she either needs a cup of coffee or a reality check that she's making other people's mornings not so pleasant. makes me want to go to another coffee shop.

i enjoy frequenting this starbucks...when i feel that i've given myself enough time before work to go. it gets super busy in the mornings, even at 6:30. the baristas are always kind, and friendly customers! sure parking is sparse, but this is green hills! lots of space in this starbucks if you're needing me time or meeting a colleague.

overrated as usual, starbucks. service the same as all over the nation. nothing special. new covid19 mobile ordering on door for pick-up is not on monument side, but other door. dumb walk up pick-up system. ordered ahead on mobile app and still had to wait. wtf?!
ordered tall coffee got just half a cup instead. ordered muffin which felt like a couple of days old probably...

totally worth connecting with everyday hot chocolate w/ a shot of espresso make it a purpose to enjoy the tasteful drinks, snack, and much, much more. i really miss the one on fall creak and college. i miss the walk. i u starbucks

i usually love starbucks. this starbucks was disappointing on several levels. the coffee was weak and not as good as other starbucks. it is always busy, which isn't a problem. the ambience is cute and clean. this store has the worst service i have ever seen. on two separate occasions, i had an issue with sam, the barista. the first time she made my drink wrong, argued with me about it, and then threw it in the sink (making a mess) and rolled her eyes. the second time she was condescending and rude. this is unacceptable behavior. as a previous starbucks employee, i will be sure to contact corporate. i would recommend finding another place to go. addiction is right down the street with great coffee, service, and ambience.
slowest drive through i have ever been to. avoid this place. nobody took my order on the speaker, the wait was over 20 minutes for 2 coffees. and... why is the cafe closed? drive through only?

i had such a great experience when i came into this starbucks. i am not usually one to order all the fancy espresso drinks so i didn't know what to order. the nice young lady named kristina was such a help in making my decision and the drink came out great! will definitely be a new fave order :)

it's a starbucks and you probably know what the food tastes like so let's talk about cleanliness, service, and seating situation. on the cleanliness front, this place could do much better. it's a really high traffic store and i think that makes it difficult to keep clean, but i think they could do better. the service is definitely not not the friendliest either. sometimes it may feel like you're asking for a favor rather than paying for a latte. and lastly seating. this place gets seriously packed!!! like outrages lines so get ready to never find seating and get bumped into about 20 times while you wait for your drink. but sometimes you just need coffee and this place will do the trick.

i have to agree that this appears to be a slow starbucks. then again, maybe it was just that they served the wrong drink without a name and then moved on to the next last time i was here. when this was corrected and the right drink served, it was accompanied by a "next drink is on the house" voucher. that was classy, and saved the place from a two-star rating. free wifi as well for what it is worth. that is unfortunately outweighed in my opinion by the store's location--the largest, crappiest parking lot sprawl in all of tucson.
i don't know why i keep doing this to myself. this has got to be the worst and slowest starbucks in the city. these so called baristas need more training.

i stopped by here since i was really craving some iced coffee with the sweet vanilla cream and the starbucks at the mall ran out! this starbucks was pretty standard. i can't not love a place that always is air conditioned, has good drinks, and is ideal for getting work done or just chatting with friends. service here was always great!
this starbucks is by my house and recently opened!! the employees are amazing! coffee is great! they're super sweet to my pups in the drive thru!

great customer service attentive polite!!! worst parking lot ever!! drive-thru blocks parking every second on he morning! why does corporate choose these smaller locations not smart! they have tone thinking cash cow

why is it so hard for servers to get an order right? i ask for coffee and a sandwich on a plate. that is two items. time after time, my plate request is ignored no matter how much i emphasize when ordering. there are only two possible explanations: they don't care or they are incompetent. i guess saving 75-100 bags a year is not an issue at the prices they charge. comments?

first of all i love starbucks. my husband and i were looking for coffee the day we left reno. we figured any starbucks is the same. i was disappointed with this location and had to wait over 25 minutes for two coffees! the way it was set up it was awkward to say the least. it seemed we had to push chairs out of the way to walk out....the one and only door we had to enter. the workers were friendly enough but it just seem to take forever and was awkward.

i stop in this location every day. the employees have great customer service and are all very friendly. they remember my name and my few different drinks that i order. professional service. sometimes this location can get pretty busy in the morning, but they are still pretty quick. just because the line is long doesn't mean that you'll be in it for long.

so this starbucks is right by my home and so i have been waiting to give it a review. i will tell you, the major positive thing is the coffee is always hot and i love hot coffee! the staff is really friendly no matter if you go through the drive thru or walk in, they are terrific! i have had ice teas and hot coffee and for both this place knocks it out of the park. one of the better starbucks in town!

i love starbucks. the selection of beverages that warm your insides just bring me pure joy. one thing in particular that i do appreciate about starbucks is how tailored each individual store is to the location the store is located - this location just so happens to be located in the heart of slu campus and is a pain to get in and out off in a jiff. the service: impeccable the quality of beverages: outstanding the location: just okay.. parking: let's just say you may have a better chance finding a needle in a haystack verse finding a parking spot. the circular building that the starbucks is located in is a part of the community but just not conducive for customers trying to get in and out of quickly. will i still be back? heck yeah, of course as i still need my espresso. cheers,
i arrived 35 minutes prior to their closing time and they were...closed. bummer. i made a special trip for beans in the morning.
there are a lot of local coffee shops that will do a better coffee than these people. don't get me wrong the coffee here is okay but its extremely overpriced compared to local coffee shops.

i have been in starbucks all over the country. media pennsylvania is a funky little fair trade town nestled into the county seat with its courthouse and lawyer class. the starbucks lacks an identity. it is on the outskirts of the town center. it feels less like a coffee house than a strip mall fast food coffee shop. plus the sour smell of old bitter coffee hanging in the air here is killing me. i get the feeling that this place is open only because it can, and no other reason. too bad. starbucks is such a great brand.
quick drive thru service

at the starbucks on 4th and south streets, the baristas are a great group of people. they have to deal with so many different types of people(some of whom are very rowdy) and the baristas are always nice. the company has spent so much wasted money on putting locks on the doors that require combinations. the money would have been better spent for the baristas' pay. no one is refused entry to the bathroom. that's humane. so, why the locks? there is one thing that i do not like is that this is the busiest starbucks for bathroom needs(especially people who are not customers). each other starbucks has two bathrooms. this one, one of the busiest, opens only one of the two that exist in the café. opening the upstairs bathroom would facilitate the use by the real customers who have made purchases.

have been coming here a few times a week for the past two years. it's starbucks, it's the only one close to the hospitals and such here on s grand so it's a popular spot. they do get busy but it's always service with a smile, they have been hiring some new great baristas recently as well! always enjoy time spent here! *update* still coming here, still great staff - kori, erika, ella, miranda, the whole team is just fantastic! also for anyone complaining about wait times, this place gets slammed - it's on s grand one of the busiest streets! perhaps try taking a seat and people watching while you wait, or do a mobile order as you leave the house (make your drink extra hot.)
this starbucks has a great location , a had a really fresh croissant and a coffee latte . the service is good
today i ordered lemonade. there were strange stains on the lid. and on my last two visits the staff was very rude
worst starbucks i've been to! takes forever and the employees seem like they don't have a clue of what they are doing. i wouldn't recommend this particular starbucks.
i like this location because it has a drive thru. it used to be a bagel shop, but i guess a starbucks will do. it does have a bunch of comfy chairs inside.
the staff is so warm and friendly at this location. you can tell they like their jobs.
workers were nice, but didn't really know what they were doing. lots of little errors that make a big difference.

this starbucks is amazing. i accidentally submitted my mobile order to the eagle and chinden store and they didn't even hesitate to remake my drink at their store. super nice and helpful. friendly. would recommend and will be the only store i go to!

not sure what kind of crowd surge they were expecting as they are located across the street from the convention center. thirty minutes in line and no coffee still. seems like an opportunity for work flow analysis and communication with the convention center.

nice location. clean establishment. drinks were made well. they had a good assortment of breakfast items. wait times were longer than usual but this place gets packed. it must be the only starbucks for some distance but close to drury hotel.

my favorite starbucks! stephen and john (sadly john is no longer here, hopefully corp will see this and bring him back) are my favorite...this is only one of two locations that make my drink perfectly...everyone here is always friendly, welcoming and provides great service..special thanks to stephen for always remembering me and my order, it's ready before i even open my mouth..thank you all

came here when one of my high schools students did not want to go into wet seal... so we came here and talked about his college options etc.... the night crew was really nice and they chatted with their regulars while cleaning up for the night... no complaints here

this location is perfect. i wish there were more parking. i stop in for my fav drink. green tea frap. always lots of people. tons of wifi user. the best part is the staff. they are very nice. if their is a new product or promo they always explain what it is and what it taste like. mini cherry pie is really good.

this is a pretty standard starbucks, which makes it pretty good in my opinion! the staff is friendly and courteous, and they make the drinks pretty well. we had one issue once where they rang us up wrong, and they gave us a voucher for a free drink (any size), which was super nice because it was a small error on their part. they try to do the very best they can for the customers, and i appreciate that. this location is super convenient for me. it's on the side of the road that i'm always on when i'm going towards my house, so it's easy to pop in and out when i have to have my super sugary coffee craving quenched. say that three times fast, dare ya! all in all, this is a good starbucks, and i'll be returning a few times a week!

sure it's a global chain of cookie cutter coffee shops, but this one is clear proof that not all are created equal. my mochas are always a little off, the workers seem a little surlier than others, and the place is often packed thanks to it's awesome location leaving long lines. totally skip this place!! surprisingly (hint the massive sarcasm) there's a starbucks a mere block away on chestnut. clean, friendlier, tastier drinks, nicer seating. this is the place to go..

i've been to this location 3 times. each time the service was slow. my last trip i got a trenta ice coffee with 2 shots of espresso and it cost me $20. why? i paid with a 20 and asked the barista to put it on my starbucks card and then take the drink off of the card. he spent a lot of time doing this and then said the register wasn't printing receipts. i've never had a problem before so i told him that was fine and drove off. the next time i tried to use my card i had a 0 balance. where was the $14+ that should have been there? i called the store, the manager was too busy to speak with me and i left my name and number. she never bothered to return my call. that's fine, i won't return to your location, ever. i emailed starbucks.com and got a portion of what should have been there. i'm definitely exploring other coffee options now.
i have always had a good experience here. it's a cozy place to sit and work and the deck by the creek is a plus. always had good service over the years.
always hit or miss, but usually when tia (?) is working my drinks are perfect.
when i get a refresher it's always way under filled and the music inside was cranking. sounded like a club in there. very hard to study.

very friendly staff!! comfortable atmosphere for a starbucks. i don't care for starbucks, but i like that starbucks. i had a caramel frappuccino and the foam was so sweet. terra the cashier took her time with me. i had a gift card that needed to be scanned. she took her time and made sure everything went through correctly, my final bill was .23. stop by for a refreshing coffee drink after riding the street car.
love my frappuccinos... great fast service very friendly through the drive thru.. love being a gold member... get free stuff...
huge starbucks!! nice and cool inside. clean and very pleasant workers! fast service!!

i am a student currently living around the cbd and have walked to several coffee shops around the area. i cannot sing enough praises about this starbucks. this is hands-down the best coffee shop in the city. almost never too crowded and a good chance you have a table or couch to sit on and enjoy your coffee while having a meeting or getting some studying done. some of the most pleasant baristas you will ever meet. check it out!
this place has the worst parking, entrance & exit ~ they really need to move from this location

i just moved to sb two days ago and this was the first coffee spot i chose to study at. the girl who made my drink was sooo sweet~ the way she greets people and interacts with them is so genuine and kind. i got a choc mocha frappe and it was pretty good :) i don't know if this is an ideal study spot though since it's a small place and the workers here talk/sing pretty loudly lol

fast and friendly service. normal starbucks coffee. their location is good and they have a huge parking lot right behind them. so they are normally pretty busy. they also have enough table for different groups of people.

a chain - i know, i know. i can't help but be a fan of starbucks - i would pick the local coffee shop (particularly henry's and clavin fletcher) any day over starbucks, but in the same breath i wouldn't bash starbucks like i would other big chains like walmart. somehow this ginormous business (at least the ones i have visited and especially the mass ave location) has achieved a small town-feel with friendly employees despite its big world reality. some may consider it a marketing stunt, but i can't help but smile every time i see their "create jobs for usa" program. this particular location was remodeled and expanded during the summer of 2012. from time to time, you might find me on their spacious patio people watching and working on one of my latest paint-by-number "masterpieces". my visits to starbucks are few and far between, but the caramel apple chai never fails to do the trick!

this was go too. i'm not someone who writes a lot of yelps. the last few month when i come in i always order a ice coffee. somehow it takes 15 min to make every single time. all they do is scoop ice, walk over to where the ice coffee is in a big pitcher and pour. why must i wait 15 minutes for that when people in front of me who order warm coffee gets it made right away. this place what is my go to starbucks but now i'd rather just make my own and save myself the trouble of waiting forever when it only takes 15 seconds to make.
horrible horrible horrible customer service. this location is more worried about pushing customers in and out that they don't care about customer service.
by far the slowest starbucks i've ever experienced. just waited 20 minutes for two cappuccinos, amidst a crowd of other customers waiting. and it seemed to be business as usual.

got a breakfast sandwich and it was halfway decent. i then had to go out to my car to take some conference calls and these idiots call the cops on me because "they thought i was taking pictures of the drive thru". f#%k this place

this is the second time i have ordered a protein box with fruit and cheese and had moldy/mushy fruit. i'd have gone back but the service took too long the first time and we had already hit the interstate. double check your fruit and they continue to just mess up: gave them another chance because of time and it ends up wasting my $$. lesson learned (again): this is why i have given up on this starbucks. when i use the starbucks on lebanon rd (or anywhere else around here aside from this one) my drinks are usually great. this starbucks continually fails. this is one example. these are both cold foam beverages, the one on the left is the cf cappuccino purchased from the lebanon rd store and the one on the right is the cf cascara from this store. if it is supposed to be a cold foam beverage i think more than a sparse topping of cold foam is appropriate. this is always the standard at this location. aside from the ridiculously long wait time in line, the beverages just continue to be lacking.
wow!! allie, thank you for an amaaaaaaaazing iced macchiato and your amazing smile and personality! bravissima!!

this location is quite hit and miss. i don't recommend the drive thru here plus it's an inconvenient parking lot if you're trying to grab a quick cup of joe. that being said, if you're meeting a friend for a leisurely coffee date, this location might be the right one for you! slower paced and not very busy.
ordered kale and portabella egg bites gave me the tomato ones also asked for almond milk and it's definitely whole milk

so, just to get out there: i have worked at a starbucks and know the 'rules.' and every other starbucks i have been to the baristas very accommodating to my orders and questions. and i know i am in isla vista, so that care-free attitude must have some influence. i have been in here multiple times and wish there was another starbucks within a reasonable walking/biking distance. the baristas here are kinda rude and sometimes act like the world owes them. i order two shots of espresso in my grande to-go iced cup with flavoring/syrup and soy. this is my way of getting a cheap latte. but it's not even a full iced latte because i don't want the full amount of soy. two times i have been there they say "we're not allowed to do that." you're not allowed to make a drink? i even explained that it's not a full iced latte but both times they still charged me for a full latte. it's just two shots of espresso with some soy and ice because i don't like hot drinks. to add to my frustration, when i checked my receipt and noticed the 'mistake,' i took it in about 5 days later and they said they couldn't do anything about it because every day the manager "closes the register." as a previous starbucks barista, when a mistake was made, we always fixed it, even if it was days later. we would re-make the drink or give them a coupon for a free drink. another thing i have never heard of is not allowing someone to not have ice. i have served and ordered 'no ice' drinks i ordered a drink for a friend and they said they could only do half the ice. this establishment really puzzles me. on one occasion (i understand that they were closing, but the hours listed were not the same), when i ordered my drink and noticed that he didn't give me my cup discount he said in a very hidden tone "i gave you plenty of discounts." i made a confused face and just scanned my phone. i am almost out of money on my starbucks card, and when i run out, i don't plan on returning there unless i'm desperate. there are plenty of other great cafes in iv with wifi. hope others have better experiences.
nice starbucks and the employees were all very friendly and polite. they remembered my drink after one visit. definitely gets busy there.

i am a regular at this starbucks and found all the staff to be very nice and friendly except i had an awful experience with an employee today. she was rude and kept interrupting me when i was trying to order, and lied to me about not having any blueberry muffins despite there being 3 on display, and didn't even bother to check. i even called in to check if they had muffins two minutes after i ordered and they did have blueberry muffins! i will not be returning to this starbucks again, thanks isabel, you just lost a customer. :(

the employees there act like they pay for supplies out of their own pocket. if you ask for a drink with light ice, be prepared to get pushback about it and how your cup will not be full since you requested light ice. no other starbucks that i've been to has ever done this other than this, yet they do this every time. paying $7 for a cup filled 2/3 of the way because you don't want so much ice is a joke. not only that but they will close whenever they feel like it & tell you it's because they "were slammed today," even if it's still 15 minutes till their posted close time. i recommend driving 5-10 minutes to green valley or tucson location instead.

i go into this starbucks all the time, because one its close to home. two its convenient. the employees have always been so nice and sweet. this morning they weren't either. i know at times you can get busy but that does not mean you have to be rude to customers. they had forgotten my piece of bread i ordered which was no problem at all, it was the fact that when i had my mom go in and get it they first ignored her didn't ask if she needed help, and then when she had asked for the piece of bread we ordered she got snippy with my mom. there was just no reason for that and i was completely shocked when she told me that because usually they are so nice. and we had just gone in there yesterday morning.

starbucks is my go to for coffee but this location i will not be re visiting. they are not friendly, never smiled and on top of that made my drink incorrectly. it will be worth it to travel down the street for better service.
my favorite drink (white mocha) is always done right at this starbucks. staff here is super friendly.
usually when i go to this starbucks they never get my drink right, but yesterday when i went there they were so much better then normal!!

perhaps i'm lucky because i've never had a subpar experience at this starbucks location inside target. compared to starbucks everywhere else, this one always has the shortest line (at least whenever i show up). it's conveniently located at the grocery entrance. my beverage orders are always made to perfection. the staff keeps the short lines moving, and orders are ready in a timely manner. always a great way to start a shopping spree!
great way to start the day! smiling baristas and delicious coffee!! such a nice place to meet up with friends and enjoy a good cup of coffee!

my friend and i spend a lot of time here because the staff is so friendly. they are always smiling and making conversation and they all seem to enjoy what they do. this is definitely one of our favorite places to visit in brownsburg.
i like starbucks and this location specifically. they have a drive thru. generally are quick. the drinks are made right. cant complain.
can't stand their coffee. it tastes like it was strained through an old sock. i'll stick to tim hortons.

i've been waiting 10 mins for one caramel marchiato and a frapachino not to mention the guest that c a me before me didn't even get their drinks i got mine first. horrible. the drive thru was wrapped around the store

this starbucks it one that is worthy of a review purely because the staff is amazing . being in the customer service business i can tell you that they make my stop here 10x better. on multiple occasions that i stop here, the staff has been so nice especially sharmaine and erica ! you can feel that they are genuinely sweet .although our encounters of me placing my order with them is small it really does change how i feel when i walk out .
so small inside. the staff are very indifferent, we are in plant city people, no room for snobbery!

usually amazing coffee, but the oatmeal i ordered today wasn't made properly and they seemed to use cold milk instead of steamed milk (which is how it comes)- completely ruining the taste. i didn't have time to go back so it kind of ruined my breakfast before work.. not sure what happened here- make sure you guys are steaming the milk!!

we are everyday regulars at different starbucks throughout the tampa bay area. today we arrived at this starbucks and was one of 3 guests in the entire store, and the only guest in line. there were 6 employees behind the counter, doing various tasks. the one task the customer pays them to do however, they did not. and that is acknowledge us and take our order. we stood there like idiots for upwards of 3-4 minutes before anyone even looked at us or took our order. then, and elderly gentleman behind us attempted at a refill, and stood there for close to a minute before anyone assisted him. unreal
yes the coffee is good but an additional bonus is that the employees here are for the most part very attractive. a+

the two times i have visited i have been pleased with the rapid service here at this sb location. the staff are always super efficient and friendly while completing your order, and i have not had any issues with the quality of the beverages we have ordered. this sb gets my approval!!!!!
the baristas are great! service is prompt, accurate and consistent. drive thru operates smoothly with little delay

as soon as i walked in every staff member that made eye contact with me gave me a warm hello. i was actually having a little trouble with my starbucks app today and they were very patient and helpful with that. my bagel came out absolutely perfectly toasted and my frappaccino came out great as well. i would definitely recommend this starbucks.
the drinks were good. but when you order and are charged for a venti i also expect the cup to be filled more then half way.

spacious with decent amount of seating. large parking lot, with additional parking lot available. not sure what it is about starbucks, but there are always massive amounts of flies no matter where you sit. barista was friendly and patient with us while we were ordering.
this place a literally my second home i couldn't ask for a better workplace environment than this!

was here on business so i popped in to the local starbucks on the way to the office. i enjoyed the friendliness of the baristas. when they didn't have the sandwich i ordered they gave me an extra pastry. my caramel macchiato was perfect!

this location is horrible!! you wait in line forever inside and at the drive through! the drive through is set up so if there is more than 3 people in line you are blocking parked cars. the staff can be so obnoxious and rude! forget about ordering a cold drink by the time you get to the window the ice is melted! if you can, avoid this location!

5 stars because i love their morning staff! denise is absolutely fabulous. i am always amazed how she knows so many people's names and their orders. also, they seem to understand the mobile ordering. i have been to several starbucks over the past few months who still do not fully understand that concept. whenever i order on the mobile app, my drink is ready by the time i get there! if you're looking for a starbucks in center city - definitely check out this one!

the usual as to coffee. small selection of pastries, etc. and small sitting area. biggest issue i have at this location is the staff, which seems to change all the time. the current employees do not seem to have much enthusiasm for what they do.

i went to this starbucks today around 10:00 and i got a bagel and a hot coco cause it's 50° out and the bagel was so good so i took a sip of my hot coco and i was like this doesn't taste like hot coco it tastes like milk so i open the lid it's this milk with like brown flakes in it and it was hot milk.

stopped into this starbucks because it's on the way to west chester and has a parking lot (rather than street parking like the one in downtown west chester). they served my tea quickly and efficiently and the salted caramel cake pop i ordered along with it was divine (except for when it fell off the stick). the store was busy but still clean and well maintained. i'll be stopping here regularly moving forward.

had the worst coffee this morning! i order the same drink all the time and i knew it didn't look right... but i was running later than usual and didn't say anything... but my drink was terrible!!! not the way i wanted to start my day! i ordered a grande, it was so light, i'm thinking i was shorted a shot. i used to love this location but i feel like it's been hit or miss lately... and it wasn't even really crowded.

one of my favorite starbucks to go to when i get a chance. they always remember me and my drink. they make it perfect with a hint of love. it's perfect location makes it easy to grab on your way into target and out to go shopping. there is no better way to spend your then drinking a starbucks while shopping and looking around. the only issue i ran into is a new lady working there gave me a hard time about a birthday coupon and she got really sarcastic and rude and didn't even say happy birthday or try to make the situation better. the worst experience i had. other then that everyone else is lovely. also sometimes i feel they are short staffed, most the time i only see one person up front.

usually i'd say a starbucks is a starbucks is a starbucks, but this is my office away from the office. i'm here several times a week and my experience is always great. the wi-fi is reliable and the service is always great. bring your headphones though, the music isn't anything to write home to mom about.

literally the worst drive through starbucks experience ever. it takes so long to get even a single drink, that i was able to put my car into park and slowly write up this particular review. they don't even acknowledge how bad it is or that you've been sitting and waiting for 11 minutes. go literally anywhere else.

this store is dirty and gross. employees are rude and i even saw water leaking from their ceiling onto their coffee machines for hours. dirty!! onto their drinkavoid. go local!!! so many other cheaper and better local coffee places within the same block. update: their management is horrible and they're inconsiderate to their neighborhood
started going there recently on the way to work, baristas are fast and friendly. they had my order done before i finished paying. thanks.

newly opened early 2018, located across parking lot from target, near to fedex and pnc bank. nice large patio and a drive-thru. great staff. i recommend the green tea grasshopper (mint and mocha chip) frappuccino.

this is by far my favorite starbucks location. the staff is amazing and is always ready to answer any questions i have about new items! they never fail to make me an excellent drink super quickly! let's not forget how great mike is! his energy and kind manor is the best part about this store, absolutely love it!
this store is steady messing someone order up. every single time. whether it's forgetting putting cream in my coffee, never mix the iced tea lemonades. sugar just sits at the bottom.

last time i stayed in this area for work (october 2011) this starbucks wasn't here, so i was pretty damn happy to see it when i arrived yesterday. the drive-thru was full this morning at 9, but they moved the line quickly and the barista working the window was super friendly. i'm a big fan of friendly baristas.

a friendly local starbucks near the u of a hospital. the location is small, even for a starbucks. however, they do have an extra seating area outside the store, both on the walkway outside and in the commons of the building area inside. this is essential since its freaking cold in edmonton and outdoor seating is basically useful only 4 months a year. the staff was friendly and the drink order made accurately. i also love the fact my starbucks card is usable here even though i am from the us.

i would have given them a four but the gal that made our coffee wasn't the friendliest which was a bummer because we came a couple days later and the guy working the drive thru was very happy and polite. i guess some people just have there off days, i get it.

the layout of the shop seems better than many, allowing seating without feeling swarmed when a lineup forms. and the service any time i've been in has been prompt and friendly. today i met the barista lindsay, and she was friendly and efficient. even though i was midway into a to-the-door lineup, she took a minute to engage in some friendly banter as she took my order and got my beverage started. the shop is clean, well-maintained, and well organized. oh, and they have a deck patio! not ideal today with the melting snow but great once the snow is gone. i pass two other starbucks to get here, including one that's less than a block from my apartment, and i never regret making the trip.

i am a semi-regular at this location. i often end up here when i have some time to kill before or after shopping at merenda's italian specialties, which is one of the most awesome italian groceries i've ever been to, and which i highly recommend. i like this place, and i obviously keep coming back. i like the ambiance, minus the view of the parking lot, and i like the location, again, minus the suburban sprawl that it lives in. my main complaint about this location is that the staff seem really overworked, and i don't understand why. i suspect it's the drive through that's the culprit. i've been in here time and time again, when there is no line (inside, there's usually at least a car or two waiting at the drive through), and it takes quite some time, often over a minute, for someone behind the counter to even come over to me. for example, today there were five employees behind the counter, no line, very few customers in the shop, and they were all doing things behind the counter in a frenzied / hurried looking fashion. all five of them!!! what the heck are they doing? cleaning things? making drinks for the drive through? i don't know, but i don't get it. maybe i have trouble understanding it because i always order stuff that requires limited effort, like tea, coffee, or a baked good, and maybe other people are ordering ultra-special-involved drinks that require 4 people to expend 5 minutes each just to make the freaking drink. but i don't know, that just doesn't seem plausible. i've been to so many other coffee shops where there are fewer employees, yet the employees like, walk right up when a customer comes up to the counter. some of my favorite coffee shops in west philly have only 1 or 2 employees at a given time, and they handled more volume than this shop (no drive through though). i don't like it. it creates a stressy vibe. when i stand at the counter, i feel like i'm absorbing the stress of the employees. i don't feel like they're really 100% there, when they ask me what i want. like, i'm sitting here in the coffee shop as i write this review, and the employees are constantly running back and forth behind the counter in a frenzied fashion, yet there has been a total of one customer at the counter the whole time. ahh, here two more customers come in, finally. i'm watching the interaction. the employee isn't making much eye contact. the two customers look so nice. the guy behind the counter looks so stressed. oh, here someone comes out of the kitchen...she looks so stressed out too. seriously, starbucks corporate management, do something about this location. the poor stressed, overworked employees...they don't look like they are having a good time, and they're making it a less pleasant place to be for the customers inside the shop. i really want this place to have a genuinely chill vibe. that would be like, really chill. and i'd like that. heck yeah.
stopped here when we passed by to grab some coffees. went thru the drive thru. service was quick and our drinks were made correctly.

this starbucks is not a great location if you come with someone else (or more). there are only about 4 tables inside and they are all pretty small. there are a couple tables outside, but in the summer, who wants to sit outside! there are only a couple of big chairs that you can lounge at in the front. however, even though there are not many tables, i have never had a problem getting one (although a couple times i snagged the last one). there is even a bar that you can sit at and watch the baristas make the coffee (which i find a little creepy, but would be great if you're friends with the baristas and feel like sitting and chatting). the baristas are really friendly. no matter who is working here, i always hear them chatting and laughing. they are pretty quick too. since this is a small starbucks there never seems to be a long line. what is kind of funny is that parking here is the easiest, since it shares a lot with publix and a few other stores. we need to switch this parking lot with some of the busier starbucks locations! there is no drive through at this starbucks.

the parking lot at this starbucks is a nightmare. i am always worried someone is going to hit my car -- or worse , hit me as i'm walking from my car to the building! i'm not kidding when i say the parking lot is scary. it's just as bad if you want to use the drive through option. you have to maneuver your car to go through the lane by entering from one side of the lot and if you don't, you have to do like a ten point turn to get back to the right place so you can turn your car into the drive through lane. it's nuts!! this starbucks is like one minute from my apartment which is beyond awesome but because the parking and drive through are such a disaster, it's easier for me to drive a few more miles to the rock hill location on manchester!! what a shame! also, the baristas here are probably the slowest from any coffee shop i've ever been to! they really take like ten minutes to make a simple tall latte. lmao. it's almost comical. finally, they never have samples or specials. if you want to study or read somewhere or just get a quick beverage go to the nearby rock hill starbucks instead!
i waited 35 minutes for a tea that they ended up getting wrong. when i came in, there was absolutely no one there and i don't understand how they still managed to get my order wrong. what a waste.

this location makes the drink correct every time, so i cannot complain there. but every single time i order light ice in my drink, they fill it to the brim with ice. also, the drive-thru takes waaay longer than it needs to.

pretty sure this place is run by college students who don't actually need to work because that is the type of service you get. often times they are "busy" talking amongst themselves and 3 out of 5 times they forget to make something that i ordered(no joke). they don't notice until i ask about it or realize i am still waiting. often times no greeting even when the store is relatively empty and no "can i help you" even if you're standing in front of the register they will continue to talk to each other until they deign to help you. i try to avoid coming to this starbucks. do not believe the positive reviews. as you can tell they are written by other college students who are probably friends with the kids who work there.

we pulled up at 8:59pm having no idea what time they closed and walked in to see two guys cleaning up the counter area. when i asked if they closed at 9pm fully prepared to walk away, they invited us in to serve us and reassured us it was no problem at all that it was 9pm. wow... this kind of service rarely exists and these two young men are true stars! thanks zachary and carlos!!! merry christmas
starbucks is awesome. i love the food. and drink. they have all the new stuff. i wish there was a more local feel but i understand that's hard.

all starbucks are the same. lots of money for really strong coffee. i get the caramel frap light with soy and no caffeine and sugar free caramel while shelling over $6. what's the point?! i would rather drink my shakeology and make it into my own at home frap!
slowest starbucks i have ever been too in my life. sat in the drive thru line for 30 minutes. do not recommend going here.

came in around 4:30pm on a sunday. steven took my order and he did very well for a new employee. i ordered a twix frapp (since i have a free drink) which is a lot of syrups and stuff in it. my drink came in about 3 minutes and it's blended nicely. they forgot the java chips but i didn't mind it.
great location! the workers are always nice and friendly. there's never a mistake with my order. this location always seems to be busy but there is never much of a wait.
amazing! i believe the girls name is britney. i work next door at ulta in the salon and she always hooks us up with free drinks so nice of her!

i'm really not sure what all of the complaints here are about. it might be because i purposefully try to avoid suburban square during high-traffic times (like the middle of the afternoon on saturday) but i've never had a bad experience. true, the line can be very long sometimes but that's to be expected for a coffee shop in a popular shopping hot spot. in my opinion, the service is very quick and efficient. it'd be nice to see more of the employees smile, but they've never messed up any order of mine. i've come here to work on assignments for graduate school plenty of times. i have always been able to find a seat. plus, there is free wifi. what's not to like?

deserves zero stars. some if you cant make the correct product on the label then you should close the store. i go to starbucks infrequently one reason is their quality is generally low. well they didnt disappoint this morning get to work with a incorrectly made drink. a caramel macchiato should have caramel in it this one had none not just a little it had zero of the first ingredient in the name of the drink. last time i was here same problem that was probably 9 months to a year ago. complain to starbucks they wont do anything and make you go back to the store. inconvenience me even more. this is a change in policy from before they used to take care of you over the phone. the organization does t care about customers. do not go to this store unless you want a terrible drink.
this starbucks is definitely catered to large groups (i.e. study groups, conference groups), which is awesome! ideal ambience for reading and getting your coffee fix!

the refreshers.... so so good and feels lite. i can break a tooth chomping on the ice to get to the dehydrated strawberries floating around in the drink. i also shake the empty cup viciously until every drop is gone. but i don't care. the drinks flavors are on point and it's a nice change of pace from a warm coffee. plus they'll always give pup cups. starbucks is wonderful.

i visit this location on a weekly sometimes daily basis. the employees there are not very customer service friendly. i who work night shift who don't sometimes get up until the afternoon goes there ask for a bagel and a green tea the response is they're out of the bagel and maybe i should get up earlier. today i arrive i ordered five drinks for my family the total is $30 all i had on me was $100 bill i didn't have anything smaller and the cashier acted as if i had committed a crime and she says to me it's a security issue i'm not really sure what the security issue was other than her making me feel wrong for using $100 bill in america. the bank gave me that it was real. very unprofessional in the way they handle things.

this is the only company owned starbucks in downtown st louis, and it is far superior to the others, especially the one on washington. the baristas are friendly, incredibly efficient, and seem to care about the customers. it's actually kind of amazing how quickly they can prepare a drink. unlike the nightmare on washington street, i've never had an incorrect order in this store. and, they stock had breakfast and lunch items as well. the only thing that keeps it from being 5 stars is that the location is kind of small. it's not too small, there always seems to be a place to sit, but its far from the most luxurious starbucks i've ever been to. one of the great things is that when the weather is warmer there are plenty of tables to sit at outside. that relieves any overcrowding, and is actually pleasant considering the giant empty department store you look at across the street. its out of my way to go to this starbucks, but it's worth it because of the service, staff, and quality of drink and food.

came here for my usual iced venti half-caf americano with three pumps of sugarfree vanilla and room for cream. there was like -2 parking spaces so i had to put my hazards on and be "that person" to get my drink since i sure as hell wasn't driving to find a spot for a 5 minute coffee run. cute place, deck looks out on the river which is nice.

at first glance, this store doesn't seem as clean as it should be for this time of day. there were several workers, but only one taking orders at the counter. i would be willing to give this store another chance.
weak coffee...for the price it should have more flavor than hot water. waste of time and money

today i was reminded yet again, why i stopped going to this starbucks. call me optimistic, but i thought i'd give it a second chance. the parking lot is extremely small and crowded even though they "added" some extra spots. the only parking that was available today was in the 15 minute parking spots. some other patrons and i were asked to move our cars from these spots so that others could park there. i don't mind moving my car but there were no other places to go. and the starbucks employee was somewhat rude about it. please do yourself a favor. if you're in jenkintown and you really want a cup of starbucks coffee, just visit the barnes & noble across the street. save yourself the time and stress.
this location rarely has decaf available as a selection. i have tried to order decaf espresso drinks on several occasions. they are either out or are "cleaning the machine".

this is easily my favorite starbucks in uc and possibly in philly overall. used to sit here all day during undergrad to meet up with friends and get hours of reading/studying done. this was always my secret spot during finals, and i even make it a point to visit whenever i'm back in philly :) what makes it different - it's clean and a good size (not too crowded) and the staff is friendly. i also love the location; good midpoint between penn and drexel and close to wawa, sangkee, and other food places if you're looking to take a break while studying. there's another starbucks a block away on walnut but it's usually more crowded (although the walnut starbucks is more convenient for grabbing drinks in between classes). what i like most about this starbucks is that it has plenty of natural sunlight and gives off more of a small coffeehouse vibe compared to other [more generic] locations. a couple of considerations - it gets cold during the winter due to all the windows and the lights are pretty dim in the evening.
great service, especially by a friendly barista named zah who was working fast to help a long with a smile on his face. thank you!!

came to the same location at a different time and the experience was 100% better! patrick, the barista, was super helpful and top notch customer service. i got a vanilla roobois, which is really good and even better with a little bit of milk.
literally one of the worst starbucks ever. 2 stars only because it's busy but that's no excuse for consistently getting people's orders wrong.

worst starbucks in human history. i don't understand how someone can't clearly take a simple order here without screwing it up every single time. my girlfriend even got an uncooked sandwich from here.. i received a drink with 4 add shots in it... not sure if they're trying to kill people or not but i've been here over 10 times out of convenience and no bs, they've only got one of my orders right... today i go inside because my order was wrong once again and the girl making my wrong drink gets an attitude... it's the holidays for crying out loud... be jolly and please serve your food cooked and drinks to order... :)
the employees do nothing but complain about working at starbucks. at least they got my coffee right.

i have been coming here for over a decade. small with the usual expectations. a couple of outside tables are available. wifi is free but you have to ask what network. four bucks for a grande cappuccino..
the barista, mckenzie did such a wonderful job making my drink and it was done super fast! super helpful and kind! love seeing her there!
expect to wait a painfully long amount of time unless you have a mobile order. annoying.

wrong order, burnt tasting espresso, very very rude barista. my expectations are not very high for a place with a drive thru but i went in having a nice day and i left completely irritable. also this is one of the busiest starbucks i have ever seen, and from waiting and seeing the people in line ahead of me it seems like i'm not the only person who didn't like their coffee...!

i went in to pick up some coffee for the morning, coarse ground for my chemex. i picked my coffee, asked for it to be coarse ground for my chemex. i'm okay with her not knowing what a chemex is, but to give me the finest grind, the opposite of coarse, is pretty bad. working in a coffee house, you should know the difference of different grinds. to top it off, she was minimally apologetic for her error and made it seem more like i was bothering her than she was keen on pleasing me, the customer.

serious question. you are right in the middle of philadelphia's "financial district" (so to speak). why aren't you open on saturdays anymore? you've got the five largest accounting firms across the street, working on saturdays. this could be big money for you. it doesn't need to be year-round january to april 15th.

i have been here a few times and i don't know if i've just been unlucky the times that i have come, but the vibe at this starbucks is definitely different from the other starbucks in the area. the baristas always seem unhappy and don't greet you with a smile. the young man behind the counter is unfamiliar with how to input orders into the kiosk and when he attempted to ask the other barista about how to input a snickerdoodle hot chocolate into the system, the barista catie just ignored him. another time i've been here, the baristas were bantering back and forth with each other trying to pass off the task of who would make the frapuccino i ordered for my kids, another time i asked to have a grande drink split into two small cups and the cashier told me they don't do that - where at other starbucks they have no issues doing so. customer service is key and this starbucks seems to lack it. food and drinks taste the same as other starbucks, i just wish they had better customer service and smiled more.
awesome starbucks. drinks always taste good, stores always clean, and the baristas are friendly. really awesome atmosphere too. it's a circular shape which i found cool. i'll be back!
i'm a huge fan of starbucks and this was a great one to stop in. for it being in such a crowded tourist spot the staff was friendly. really enjoyed my latte.

(4.5) ~ very good overall food & service! ** my wish finally came true...a starbucks with a "clover brewing system" close to home!:-) read about the clover brewing system online and order your favorite coffee bean either hot -or- iced. my fav is their aged sumatra:-)

worst starbucks's i've been to in quite awhile. barista was rude. she looked like she has been through the mill. i asked if she could sanitise or wash her hands. she was well ya but whatever. she said if i was that worried i should make coffee at home. a real witch. won't be visiting this starbucks anytime soon. everyone at the majority of starbucks are so nice especially during this time.

this is the starbuck's i come to study at the most. i've been here so many times, this review is long overdue. i love his big it is, sometimes it's difficult to find a larger starbucks. but this one is great! it has large tables and there is always one available. i especially love their couch seating, super comfortable to sit in for a while when i have hours of studying. minor flaw is that the staff here can be hit or miss. i have my favorites, but there are a couple that could be rude. i say this because they are consistently rude. other than that great location!

update: they got everything basically correct. i ordered a venti captain crunch frap (fairly common secret menu buy) and none of them knew how to make it so i look it up then the lady behind the counter takes the phone out of my hand to look at the ingredients. it wasn't busy at all and took over five minutes to make my drink in the wrong size. i told them they looked annoyed but remade my drink. took 15 minutes to make one drink.

this starbucks is one of the best i've been in because the staff is always super friendly, makes their drinks correctly, and the place itself is a smaller shop, so it's a more intimate environment, which is great if that's your preference. anytime i come here, i prefer walking in over going through the drive-thru because the people who work here brighten my day and it's a great way to start before i head into work. i look forward to stopping in on my way to work today.

this starbucks is deceptively bland... until you step inside. its centerpiece is a large wood table (i couldn't tell if it was oak or particleboard), with strategically placed power strips underneath, so that each person at the table could potentially plug in his/her laptop. i actually got a decent amount of studying done there...that is, until the teenie boppers showed up at 8. (a busload showed up at once, got some frozen frapuccinos, and commenced talking & giggling uncontrollably with each other) shouldn't you be out at a party?

horrible, i was told what i said, which is not at all what i said, then get to the window to be belittled by theodore. i've gone here everyday for the past 3 weeks and have ordered the same drink, and was never treated or talked down to like i was today. never again. goodbye starbucks

loved my drink. but the girl making it legit had no idea how to make it and was asking questions on what to do next and how much to use, etc. it really slowed down the time it took for me to receive my drink. ample seating. you will have to pay for street parking if you want to park and drink. they also do not have a drive through

this starbucks is on point when the manager is there, when he's not there it's crap. my drink is consistently made wrong every single time! i've even began asking them to make it right while i'm placing my order so i don't have the line backed up. but the moment i don't mention it, it's back to being made wrong! but this time today i was beyond mad. i made my order for the white chocolate mocha frapp. it was once again made wrong and i told the women in the drive through that it was made wrong and i've had this convo multiple times with her about my drink being wrong. so she had the guy make a new one. mean while she opened the window back up and asked me if i wanted the messed up one as well as the new one. so i said sure i'll take it home. i took it and waited for my drink. then the nasty attitude girl ( not sure her name) came to the window and asked for the drink back so she could dump it even though i was just given it by another employee. she is always nasty no matter if i go inside or out. personally don't think she's a good fit for starbucks! i only come to this starbucks cause it's on my way home or to work. i'm gonna have to find another place to go, i'm am disappointed in starbucks employees! the morning staff are better than the night crew!

when you order a hot coffee drink (hot venti americano - extra shot) at nearly 5 bucks, you kind of want it at the right "hot" temperature. at least i do. this clearly wasn't. lukewarm at best. i brought it up nicely and i was met with an insistent recalcitrant attitude. the young girl offered to add steamed cream which i never asked for, nor did i want. i would avoid this starbucks until they get the issue taken care of. turn up the hot water temp! easy fix.

this starbucks is close to home and the customer service is great! ordering online or in person is quick and easy here! i've never had a problem and am a repeat customer. the surrounding plaza also has several shops and restaurants to explore! great location!
favorite starbucks in the area mainly because of the drive thru. they are always consistent and friendly so i have no complaints.
one of the fast line moving coffee stop ! they are 9 people working inside a small counter ! unbelievable ! good works !!

thank you, starbucks employees, for continuing to let us get our starbucks fix during this hard time! this location is now drive-thru only, allowing us all to stay safe and have minimal contact. the people working are friendly and driving here is my big event for the day during this quarantine! i have been coming here for a long time and they are always quick and efficient, even when the drive-thru line looks super long. i hope they can remain open as long as it is safe for them to do so! i love my starbucks and i love this location!
fantastic barristas that can make anything you like with a cheerful smile.

one of the lady was wonderful! seem like she says "you can make it anyway you want"...something like that phrase, customizing the drinks. i was having a disappointing day, but she brighten it back up. =d . one person can change a bumming day to a great day.
great service. fast! friendly. solid cappuccino. drive thru!

by far the slowest starbucks i have ever been to. eve going inside the wait is longer. not sure their staffing but they have staff that are doing nothing at times. your drink will sit there and just melt. go to the one on grand for better service.

spacious inside, nice outside area, i give this starbucks two thumbs up. i never worry about finding a table at this starbucks. there is a nice long booth seating towards the back that has a couple tables plus chairs lined up along it. this is very effective seating. of course there are some comfortable chairs and additional tables and chairs scattered throughout the starbucks. there are about 6 tables outside. a couple are underneath covering which is a godsend in the summer months. they keep the air conditioning on the colder side, but not enough that you freeze. just be prepared for it to be a bit chilly.

i am big coffee drinker my job requires me to be alert and on the go all the time i've had my moments with one or two workers to say the least but in all honesty this is a great place to go they are pretty busy so i started using the app for the on the go order and that helps a lot but there are tons of friendly faces at this location!!! we are all entitled a rough day an that's what i think happened with the two workers they were crammed for time and extremely busy i give it to anyone in retail because i couldn't do it i'll still to hospice
they made my drink right! 4/5 times starbucks locations make it wrong. ordered on app, was ready within minutes of ordering. no complaints here!
great coffee in wonderful location,grab a cup and stroll the streets of this wonderful town.

this starbucks is the where it's at starbucks. it is smack dab in the middle of downtown st. pete and has competition from three pretty close ones, but that doesn't seem to be a problem, for business is always booming.

definitely not your normal, everyday starbucks. this is unidenified flying coffee experience! even the interior looks different. i'm not sure what it's like in their drive thru, because when i make the drive out here, i have to go inside and sit for awhile. the ambiance is perfect. there's tons of room for the students from slu & surrounding universities to come and study and...*drum roll* they have a clover. oh my goodness, when this store opened with a clover -- i literally screamed. it is the best tasting brewed coffee you will ever have. unfortunately, that is reflected in the price. around $3-ish for a tall? kind of makes you feel bad when you put your creamer in.

terrible service. when i got my drink a lot of cream was spilled on the cap. i asked for a new cap and the barista told me "i already gave you a cap" with an attitude, as if i am the one at fault. definitely would not come back.

quick friendly service! our drinks were perfect. it's not a very big starbucks, it's attached to another building, which i assume is some kind of offices of some sort. but for their location and size they weren't too terribly busy, and i was in and out pretty fast both times. staff was cool too. both times it was different people, and all of them were friendly and joked around/held conversations. will be back to visit this one if given the chance.
good service! kinda slow but understandable because i went there early in the morning and only 1 barista during the rush hours

the slowest starbucks i have ever been to. always 10 cars in line and it takes a good 20 mins to get to the window. and once at the window, my drink still isn't ready. i use to work at starbucks and making these drinks isn't a difficult task.

my husband and i were excited for this new starbucks so one day we decided to go. the staff are very nice and polite. the place is very clean and neat. it has this relaxing vibe. the parking lot is very spacious. the tables and chairs inside are not placed very close to each other. thumbs up for every power outlet for each table. physical setup 100%! :))
