clean and modern decor, very friendly and welcoming staff. drink made right and served with a smile. the only negative is they do not take starbucks reward stars although they do issue them- this is more disappointing because on the pastry case they had a sign saying redeem rewards here.

the drinks are inexperienced and taste awfully. they should be improve more how to make coffee rights. i drinks starbucks a lot and this location i have tried 3 times and never got the right tastes as i expected from difference starbucks places.

outside: stairs and a ramp that go up to the front entrance. outside seats and tables to enjoy the nice weather. inside: typical starbucks feel. this location has a lot of seating, and a big long table in the back with stool type seating. quite a few people on this saturday late morning sitting inside and outside. however, when i walked in there was no line with one guy who came in after me. ordered: yogurt blueberry muffin warmed. she used a tissue paper to grab it so i'm glad to see proper sanitary practices. when it was done heating up, they promptly took it out and placed it on the pick-up table. tall iced white chocolate mocha at first she grabbed as hot and started writing until i asked for it iced. whipped cream on top because i forgot to ask for it without. staff: the girl who rang me up seemed neutral, not friendly, and not mean. i apologized for not telling her it was iced, but she didn't say anything like "no worries that's ok." she just grabbed a different cup with a blank expression and re-wrote it. i thought she would ask for my name to write on the cup, but she didn't. i'm not sure what starbucks policy is on that if they only take names when it's busy or what. seems a bit more personal when they call your name instead of calling out what you ordered. overall: a-ok in my book. my drink tasted good and there wasn't an excessive amount of ice like some places do. if the staff was a bit more up friendly then i'd probably add another star.
each time i come in the staff is friendly and nice. the store is very clean. i like the new lay out of the store.

a full-fledged starbucks inside a jw marriott resort?! couldn't ask for a better combo! it was a nice treat to get a cold frap on a hot az summer day. there is nothing else for miles and miles from this resort as it is in the hills above tucson, but well worth the drive and the bonus was this starbucks. you don't have to be a guest here either to enjoy it and prices were the same as any other starbucks.

this is my favorite starbucks location ever. i come every morning before work. it has a friendly, welcoming vibe, enthusiastic and kind staff, and most importantly the baristas are fast. even when the place is packed. highly recommend.
don't shutdown the clover machine early just so you can get out early. there are far too few clover machines in town. americanos and pour overs are not good substitutes.

went on a bagel run to another establishment , but i had to have starbucks. still no seating inside. this location is geared to keeping drive thru moving, so i think the drive thru is quicker than walking in and getting it at the counter.

a lovely place to kill some time and listen to high character folk music, perfect for our situation yesterday, waiting for a flight. a nice little starbucks, well equipped with power and wifi, a little slice o heaven.

this starbucks is very close to my house and we go often, exclusively through the drive-thru. service varies depending on the time of day/day of the week. we typically go before six am most weekdays (we have to be out and about early). our order is correctly and quickly filled about 85% of time at that time of day. when i go later in the day, or on weekends, my order is right and quickly filled more than 95% of the time. despite accuracy or speed issues, service is friendly **all of the time.

inconsistent drink quality and you all too often get terrible service. most of the staff are far more concerned with their conversations amongst each other and with chatting up their friends who come in to mooch free drinks to serve the paying customers. i can't adequately describe how consistently terrible the service is. loosing orders, charging the wrong amount, having to repeat your order over and over and over again all while you are made to feel you are interrupting the staff's social hour is no way to run a business. until i hear rumors that there has been a massive change in the service, the tech center starbucks is in indefinite time out with me. starbucks hq really needs to step in and deal with the poor quality seen at this sodexo-run hell hole.

i work at the hospital across the street and this is my go to for morning coffee! the employees are all super friendly and take the time to make each interaction personable! my coffee always hits the spot and gets me through my long days!

so glad this is my local starbucks! the staff are always friendly, and the drinks are consistently good. the interior is a bit small for how much business it attracts on any given day, but it's a cozy spot, and it's also nice to sit & chat at the tables outside when the weather is nice.

very nice starbucks located on the second floor of the hospital. good signs once you get inside to the starbucks. don't be intimidated by the hospital ambience, once you get to the second floor and find the starbucks you'll be very happy. large, clean, bright, friendly with plenty of seating both inside and outside the store.

disappointed when i got my vanilla bean frap, i asked for extra carmel and i didn't get one drop of carmel. the person in drive thru really needs to listen to the customers order. and they didn't have enough green tea!

let's face it, it's starbucks. a little bit on the overrated side, compared to the hip coffee shops that are now dotting the reno area. i don't hate their coffee, but it's not my favorite. however, it always seems to be a good choice for meeting up. easy to get to, lots of parking, and plenty of tables and chairs to pass the time idly by. no, i am not a regular, so they don't know me here. and the cashier/barrista made that very clear by depriving me of any semblance of a smile that the others have written about here. but i have to say, when i asked if i could have my cappuccino in one of the real cups, and not in one of their famous to go cups, he indulged me. for that i was grateful. my friend had one of their italian sodas, and when i asked her how it was...her reply: eh. but we had a good chat, so all in all, it was not a complete fail.
efficient, friendly service. always willing to accommodate a special request for the barista. and their skills at an iced cappuccino - heavenly! real frothy foam atop creamy cool espresso.

i love java frappuccino from starbucks, but this one from the wynnewood starbucks was horrible! i went there during the week around 5pm, i don't know if it's always the same lady who works by that time, but anyway, the frappuccino tasted like water. there was too much ice, i couldn't even taste the coffee or the chocolate. i couldn't drink with the straw, i only ate the whipped cream with a spoon. really disappointed!

this starbucks brings me back to the old school days. me and hubby used to come to this location all the time. our all time favorite is the white chocolate mocha. we used to sip on that and chat upstairs at their lounge section very often. this is probably my favorite starbucks since it brung me so much memories. since is my favorite, why 3 stars???? i've been checking in quite a bit lately. their drinks are either hit or miss. for example, i've been getting the matcha green tea latte. sometimes the drink is just right but other times, the barista probably didn't shake it enough...... resulting in "chunks" of powder at the bottom of the cup. sometimes the whipped cream looks so nice while others "cheap/stingy" looking.

when i worked briefly for starbucks (albeit it was a decade ago) my manager made one worker a shift wear a timer that'd go off every 10 minutes to remind us to do a sweep of the seating area to keep it tidy. that should be instituted here. i have never been in such a filthy starbucks and won't be spending any time here again soon.

i came in on the very first cold night of december 2015, to personnel dressed up for christmas as santa's helpers with the grinch's attitude. it was a most unpleasant experience. you see i am not a regular participate of starbucks. i honestly went because it was cold outside and starbucks gets such high reviews, so i was expecting a warm experience. i will in future go to wawa for coffee when i am out and about. i experience the personnel at wawa to be much more heartfelt, friendly and attentive. definitely the staff at this starbucks are naughty!!! the holiday presence is not alive at this location of the starbuck family.
racism is alive and well at starbucks! boycott starbucks!

i go to this starbucks whenever i work at the hospital up the street. not only is my coffee made perfectly every single time, but the staff is always wonderful. compared to other starbucks, this place is a ten! they're very quick and efficient!

i want to give them a lower score but it's my own damn fault. an overly long line for an extraordinarily average cup of anything. not only do they mark their products up 900% but they'll argue over the verbiage of small, medium, and large. it's my own fault that i need a coffee this hard.

sigh, yes, i go to starbucks. given the dearth of places to enjoy a cup of coffee around the area, starbucks is the place to go. what i truly love are the baristas. they are friendly, make an effort to learn your name and drink if you are a regular. they are zippy and if they are incredibly busy sometimes they will comp your next drink. while starbuck's coffee, in general, tends to taste a little on the burnt side, they are consistent with the quality of their service. this location seems to go the extra mile for its customers.
super awesome staff! they are very fast and welcoming. this is the only starbucks i will go to...the others don't even compare.
nice people but slow service. 10 minutes from order to first sip. space is average size for a starbucks, they do have a drive through....good if your in it, but slows down the inside.

busy and central location makes this a coffee stop and social event. this location is open longer than the close-by peets' or the other starbucks, in the la cumbre mall across the street. the staff always keeps a good pace and sets a high standard for the slower starbucks.
love this starbucks! it's close and convenient for me, there is never a line and the employees are always fast and nice, too!
excellent and fast service. the line was insane long but i didn't wait almost at all. the staff was pleasant despite the crowd and they worked fast.

overall, this is a nice starbucks. it does get a little busy, but what starbucks am doesn't? the staff is friendly and the drinks are good. the only challenge is location. this one is on the corner of. amino shopping center and it is a little cramped. overall, pretty good experience.

horrible experience. coffee grounds in a pike and no syrup for a basic favorite. it was mid-afternoon, really?! the barista was in a league of her own. blonde female with glasses, pony tail and headscarf who huffed, puffed & rolled her eyes when asked anything. slid drinks to customers with the obligatory thank you. she was not only rude to customers, but to her colleagues. never going back to this one. you shouldn't either. walk a few blocks to 5th street or across the street to whole foods.

this location is sure busy! i'm here on a holiday monday and for the last two hours all the seats indoors have been utilized as well as the patio. i love the fact that this location has a huge patio with like 6 tables! the staff are competent, the drinks taste like your typical starbucks, so that's all good. though, they lost a star as i cannot find plug ins for laptops.thank goodness my laptop has a long battery life - i'll def come here again to work.

horrible customer service - i keep giving them another chance, but it never improves. waited 10+ minutes this morning for a small latte. and that was before the monday morning rush. ask if your drink is coming up? they'll purposely prep others before yours. a bit too much ego for my taste.

i went to this starbucks today and i waited in line for 14 minutes. when i expressed to the barista my frustration she argued with me and said it had been 5 minutes. great customer service! keep it up! i seriously spend way too much money here to get this type of treatment.

both service and quality are consistently good. i felt like a regular after only a few trips. like any retail establishment, starbucks especially, when they hire a new employee there's a good chance this employee will not know all the subtle nuances of the pricing or understand the strange ordering habits of the patrons.

i remember the days when the people at starbucks were fun, happy, and cheerful people. now, they don't say hi, or thank you, or even smile. not that i'm that old but, ah, the good ole' days. just so i'm clear, the young girl who helped us didn't say hi, or thank you, or not even a smile. really, really starting to hate the entitled employees found in more and more starbucks... and my lemon loaf is hard on one side!!! ugh! lol

i understand everyone is busy for the holiday but get it together starbucks! i'm sorry i don't have a fancy app with money stored on it to pay for my drink like all the other 9 people in line, but when i give you a $10 bill for my $5.35 charge, i don't expect over $2 in change in quarters and other coins. you should probably go check if you have some more ones

i had a mobile order placed and it said it would be ready in 5 to 10. i arrive and the girl says "sorry we were busy talking in the back". i had to then wait another 10 minutes for my order. awful service! you girls are not good employees. it was dead. i was the only person there.

this is my "go to" starbucks. fast drive thru, comfy inside. one time years ago a drive thru attendant handed my hubby a cup whose lid wasn't properly snapped on - yikes! but they've been consistently more accurate since. nice associates, especially if you are going inside to meet a friend, etc. there's nothing especially unique about one starbucks to the next, true, but if you are in franklin - your two closest other starbucks choices either have difficult parking (downtown 5 points) or are always crazy crowded (cool springs, not carothers, beside carrabba's) - so i end up here a lot. a fellow yelper just recently posted her love for a starbucks raspberry mocha, omg. my 2013 diet is in trouble... they always have a nice selection of mugs and bagged beans if you owe someone an easy gift. and who wouldn't love a new stylist travel mug?

i love starbucks, but it shouldn't take 20 minutes to get a drink. also, if there's a line out the door you probably should have more than one person making drinks. someone fast, preferably. head a block over to 19th for faster service.

timing is everything? yup! of course had to stop today to get the free holiday cups! of course you need to get the grande specialty drinks. as we were waiting, the line started to increase.. four cars behind me and three cars in front. hubby uses the app and says we needed to spend $20 to get 75 stars... our purchase total was $10.25... but he always falls for those gimmicks.. like buy one get one free etc.. when i got to the window and showed her his phone, she said the car in front paid for ours!!! perfect timing! thank you to the car in front!

we stopped in here to quickly warm up with a cup of coffee. i always am nice to people serving me, especially in big cities, because their jobs are probably stressful. plus, unless you were raised in a cave, you should just say please and thank you regardless. even with that though, i'm bummed the staff was terse and unfriendly. when i asked if they had decaf coffee they almost seemed angry i asked. soooo awkward. i've experienced better because the layout didn't seem conducive to this location either. for the size of the space, the tables were oddly placed and everyone was packed in like sardines. the people next to us were doing some odd things and we were exceptionally close to them. while the coffee was fine, just consider getting in and out. its the best route!

everyone was so rude here. i work at a starbucks and if i acted the way they did i would have been fired a long time ago! it was only 9:30 am and no one was in there...it was like we were bothering them by being there.
i love going to this starbucks location. wonderful and pleasant crew! they have a drive through also.

one of the few starbucks in carpinteria. it gets crowded in the morning. we did an online app order and walked in on a sunday morning. surprised to see a lot of locals satisfying their caffeine addiction. the cinnamon roll we got was crap but thats true with any starbucks. parking is plenty in the strip mall where its located.

good service! this review is based on a drive-thru experience only. i was served quickly, the staff was friendly and the coffee was very hot and fresh. i didn't get anything fancy today just a cup of pike...

very nice spot. excellent surroundings. nice service. various kinds of baked goods. fast and friendly service. has italian lemonade and cookie. a vanilla late with a madeline cookie. very yummy!! so relaxing and inviting to seat here and do some computer work.

best starbucks around!! even if im at giant down the street that has starbucks inside, i make the effort to stop here instead because the staff is amazing and makes the best coffee! i come here all the time and they all know my name, my favorite drink, and even know if i want decaf/caf depending on what time i come in! they always have bright smiles on their faces, and ask about my day, i can say a few of them are practically my friends i talk to them so much when im there. they will always make the drink exactly how you like and are happy to fix it if not to your liking. best baristas around! that is for sure!

good things about this location staff is all very nice and friendly shop is well maintained they've stepped up their game since my original review. i love the staff here and i'm like norm from cheers when i walk in. they all know my name and tray me like family ( actually better than my family ) once again the staff is all great. good job claudio, shay naynay, vee, wren, super mario (drew), markumark, shamir, aaron, jamie and to anyone i forgot. namaste
new "no straw lid"on my iced coffee doesn't fit! way too loose. of course drink spilled as the lid fell off. bs.
ella and lee were awesome! got a delicious carmel macchiato, along with a carmel cappuccino. excellent customer service and made my morning a little better! thank you guys

attention to detail is poor. went drive thru for iced coffee. let's face it, when you pay 3$ and up for a drink, it better be right and good. starbucks quality is exceptional but that doesn't mean a thing when your drink is filled up an inch from the bottom. also, cream and sugar amounts way off. just a bad product for the cost. heck, mickey d's would have been better for a buck each! if you do go to this location, just make double sure you get what you want. our problem was we went drive thru so no way to fix the problem after the fact.
best customer service! and they were able to handle a large order and got everything right.
never fails how amazing the team is. thanks for flipping my day from stressful to happy. you guys are the best
probably my favorite starbucks in brandon. it's very nice and cozy and a nice crowd. they keep this place up to date and clean.

shout out to my early morning baristas!!!! this location goes out of their way for customers and really makes you feel welcome! they always welcome me by name in the morning and ask me how my day is going. the service is fast and friendly, which is great when i only have a 15 minute break to grab my coffee. when i say they go above and beyond i mean it. i was greeted by handwritten birthday wishes on my drink this morning, which brought a huge smile to my face!!

my starbucks! or at least i like to think so. this place is why i spend a ridiculous amount of money for a cup of coffee. friendly staff, nice place. outdoor seating is a challenge due to binford traffic. inside fills up quickly if your looking for a place to work.
not happy at all they gave me warm and not hot coffee. tf. too pricey to screw that up.

this is my favorite starbucks location in all of sj and philadelphia. outdoor seating is a very enjoyable feature, and the interior is spacious and inviting. have never experienced a bad barista here. there are two bathrooms with average cleanliness. speed of drive through seems on par with other starbucks.

faster than the other starbucks in the area. the espresso is strong, like it should be. stronger than the starbucks on 19 and curlew. the staff was very friendly and the internet was great. i am a coffee girl and when i'm not feeling like i can't breathe in my jeans, i like a good caramel macchiato. this place gets it right. otherwise i go for the regular drip. this starbucks is probably my favorite in the area.

this starbucks saved my family and i from a bad time. after having dealt with horrible service at a local restaurant for my mother in laws birthday, we came here to grab coffee and cut a small cake for her. the staff went above and beyond, especially one of the gentlemen working. he gave us plates and forks and made sure we were ok. they were out of decaf and we came closer to closing and he did not hesitate to brew some for us (it was night time so caffeine didn't seem like a good idea.) our drinks were all great and we were able to share a special moment for a special day in an unexpected place. my respect for the staff at this starbucks for exceptional customer service!

most of the barristas are friendly and competent however there is one female, who makes the absolutely worst iced tea. for a venti 1/2 passion, 1/2 black iced tea she put in about 2 fingers of tea then about a cup of water, then iced and shook it. it barely had any color! when i questioned her on all the water she replied that it is made extra strong and must be diluted. she was not pleasant. the other employees are great but when i see this one making the coffee and tea, i leave and go next door to dunkin.

ok, went back to see if maybe my initial 1 star review was a bit harsh. nope it wasn't. extremely long wait, staff is moving but nothing seems to be getting done. lots of frustrated customers in the store. district manager has let this store go down the drain. sad. you can always go up the street to mama java's for local business, but you will wait there too.

this starbucks is awesome ! personally, it's becoming a favorite! i believe this is a newer location with a newish design, they even have cool plants, curtains, shades and a modern interior which is cozy and inviting. the bathroom facilities are newish too. what i personally like of this starbucks vs other starbucks in the area is they play music that's at a good volume, not too high and it's a smaller cafe so it doesn't get overly noisy like some busier cafes which can become a bit overwhelming. i find that the baristas are friendly and welcoming, positive and in a good mood! this is a nice cafe to relax and grab a quick bite. warm, friendly atmosphere. i enjoy it! wish it was closer to me!

this location is in the middle of a fry's grocery store. it's serviceable. it isn't a fully fledged starbucks store, so i wouldn't expect all the frills that come with it. but if you are looking for some quick pick me up, then this is a good place to go. i almost never go to these locations. just doesn't seem worth it to me. however my girlfriend wanted to stop here for a bit, because she was craving their iced coffee after finishing her shopping. i opted for an iced tea. it was a little overpriced, because they absolutely load those cups with ice, but it was a nice refreshing stop gap in our day.

loyal starbucks customer and this is the friendliest starbucks i have ever been to. hands down! i'm a yelp reader but not a yelp reviewer. so i'm giving a review for this starbucks because that's how strongly i feel about this location.
finally: a starbucks that does everything right. i'm so used to sub-par starbucks places messing up my order. they were nice & everything was extra good.
over priced "high-end" quality. i submitted a mobile order because i was rushing this morning. i didn't check my order until i got to my office. completely disappointed! my "warmed" banana bread.

i frequent this location often because it's literally down the street from my house. i have never had a problem in there, but lately, it seems they got new staff or something because the baristas are extremely lazy! the last two visits, two different baristas mixed two drinks in one blender when the ingredients have different measurements! i watched them do this with my own two eyes! like are you kidding me, each drink is very specific for a reason, that's why we have the option to customize our drinks and for you to totally disregard that is flat out rude!!!!! i need them to get it together, i shouldn't have to stand watch like a hawk to get a drink i am paying for made properly!
forgot my order and took 5 min, it's whatever 2 star from me because they did make up my drink right away when i said something.

cute corner location and friendly service. this was a nice location but nothing to write home about. the location is cute but can be a pain to get to and parking, depending on the time of day is not the greatest. that being said, they have plenty of merchandise and the couple of times that i have been here, they have always gotten my order right.

[945] 6/18/2019 it's located on a busy highway so many people stop for a hot drink. this starbucks is very slow.. i mean very slow... it took a long time to get our order. a few nice outdoor chairs. if i come back i hope the service is faster.

this starbucks is very clean, in a small and nice location, their service is great, the people here always serve with a smile. i came here to get coffee with my girlfriend and they make the coffee perfect every time that i have been here, i'll have to go back and put them to the test again, i know starbucks isn't for a lot of people because they are a little bit higher than other coffee places, but i think the customer service makes up for a lot of the negativity that is given to starbucks, give them a try and if their not for you more power to you enjoy!

this opry mall location is the only oasis in the area. thankfully, it opens earlier than the rest of the mall at 8am. sadly, it is a "large" kiosk and not a full service starbucks store. predictably, it is packed from 8-8:30 as everyone grabs coffee and/or a pastry on their way to work. given the scarcity and popularity, an expansion is sorely needed.

good starbucks - friendly service, fast-moving lines & clean interior. only giving 3 stars because my frappuccinos here have been below par. but for the more simple drinks this is a great option for starbucks lovers.
writing this review leisurely as i sit in drive thru going on 15 min now. way too long to wait for a 5$ coffee. ok. update. 25 min. order was wrong. yikes. at least they gave me a free drink coupon.
asked for a granda latte with 2 inch of room. drink came with maybe a inch. the girl said here is the drink. not sure what he did? refused to correct the issue.

this starbucks is inside of the target in town and country. i was pleased with the service that i received, but i was disappointed that this location does not have breakfast sandwiches. that was the whole point of why i came here before work last week. i was devastated, but my mocha frappuccino was still delightful.

i came in through the drive through and ordered 2 protein boxes and anyone with eyes could see that the boxes were old and obviously have been sitting because apple slices started to turn color and were super water logged? i'm not sure how to describe the unsightly fruit‍ well they said they weren't able to cancle due to covid 19 since we touched them already so we left them on the counter (i mean i don't have a trash can readily available in my car and plus the boxes were dripping water ... i turned them sideways and the fruits water juices just leaked...i showed them and not once did they apologize.) riley and some girl with purple ombre hair were so rude imo, not once did we receive an apology and they acted as if i inconvenience them with my standards. after this review i will actual contact the 800 to officially complain too. i've had a starbucks rewards card since like 2008 and i am starting to think i'm gonna invest in a great at home coffee system. tbh though i was really looking forward to that protein box

your typical starbucks coffee, good, made fresh, various options. i stopped by on my way to work, (it was 7℉) and found out they do not have a drive-thru. bbrrr! still, i got off the car and ordered my caramel machiato. i called the coworkers and asked if they wanted something, one of them flat out said she didn't, as they do not carry lactose free milk. i was unaware of that and i asked the barista, no, they do not carry it! this is just a suggestion to starbucks, perhaps they might want to cater to people who are lactose intolerant.

girl who took my order never greeted me but just stared at me til i ordered. it took her a while to input the order so should've confirmed then that she had the correct order. my chai latte came out a green tea latte. so they fixed it w attitude. my drink cans to me luke warm and i only got more attitude when i asked them to warm it up. it is now warmer but hardly. this location is in the heart of the park where locals and tourists alike go and now to enjoy the holiday events. i can deal with an honest mistake but not bad attitude, and it's sad this is what people get coming here.

awful and rude!! i got there at 7 and ordered a regular cup of coffee to go. got to my car and it was cold! went back in and she was nasty. she ignored me. i had to wait about 10 minutes for another cup. she never apologized. i heard the barista call her name. later that day i called to tell the manager my horrible experience and it was the manager who was so nasty!!!

slowest starbucks in tucson! drive thru usually takes 20 minutes to get your drink and if you think it will be faster if you go inside you will sadly see how wrong you are! starbucks company please open another one right next to this one just to keep up with the customers or maybe just hire more competent workers. how is it possible to wait 20 minutes in the drive thru line and another 10 at the window just for one drink?

i love starbucks, but this location i'm not to fond of, and the reason is because i have come here a few times and my order has been ruined. the parking lot is horrible, the bathroom was disgusting, and how many times can you come to the same spot and they mess up the order. i try to avoid this spot, but i come back trying to give them another chance to not mess up. i still come back to the same problem.
slowest drive through ever. be prepared to wait 10-15 minutes minimum no matter the time of day. pic on a slow day at 2:00 in the afternoon. each stop at the window was like 3 mins. so damn slow.

a one star rating is too generous! this should be a -5 to truly capture the negative experience. the quality of staff has gone down hill significantly over the past few years. on a recent visit, i politely asked for a straw since there were no straws in the bin. the staff person didn't make eye contact and didn't say a word. he went to the back, got a bag of straws and literally threw it across the counter with an indifferent glance. i don't need to pay $5 a drink to be treated this way.

i was ending my stay at the cheshire and needed (yes...needed) some caffeine before an early morning flight. i asked the hotel clerk for the nearest coffee shop and he directed me here. it's starbucks. generally, for me, they're a 3 star, a-ok experience at best. but a few things made my visit to this starbucks to be a cut above. firstly, the atmosphere is darling. comfortable seating surrounding a cozy fireplace during cool weather, and a charming, umbrella donned patio for warmer days. also? the workers. friendly, efficient, and conscientious. beyond their crafting of my beverage, i observed them interacting with other (somewhat particular) java seekers and they were patient and accommodating. finally...starbucks recently rolled out a new beverage. it's a cinnamon almond milk macchiato. and? it is delicious. iced or hot, either way it's just plain good. for the most part, i tend toward independent coffee houses. but, on a day when i needed a quick coffee before catching a cab to the airport, this starbucks sure came through! get to it!

if you're looking to buy coffee beans skip this location. the past three times i've stopped here, they didn't have what i was looking for. thet always say they have the kind i am looking for in the back but come back empty handed.
love this place. very cozy, though i never sit down. i always just grab coffee and go. the staff here is almost always great

friendly and clean. a lady took my drink i'm sure by mistake and they gladly replaced it. there was a misunderstanding on the food item i ordered and again they gladly fixed it. they need outlets along the glass wall. there are limited outlets at the bar and back wall, disappointing due to the actually large store. that's the only reason they went from 5 to 4 stars because service this visit was awesome. what a pleasure.

i made the mistake of walking in to place my order, big mistake! at the time i was the the only person is line and watched as multiple cars placed and received their orders before i received my one cup of tea. the customer who came in after me received his order, had time to chat with the employees and even left as i was still waiting for mine. i order the same item over and over and it's never the same. i don't understand why it's so inconsistent. i'd rather drive out of my way to another location than stop here again.

i go to this sbux maybe 5 days out of 7. the staff is reliable. it is a stop-gap for many employees so you see a lot of new faces. i'm guessing many are mtsu students. there is a group of regular baristas who've been there for a few years so i rarely have to tell them my order as i get ye basic tall dark roast...straight up. they're good people. the owner really should do more to limit the hard surfaces and noise..especially spending a few pennies for plastic caps for the chair legs...geez..simple fix . in late winter / early spring the sunlight can take some wicked angles at certain tables. the only other gripe is a very limited amount of outlets for your devices... again....that's an easy fix if the owner cared. update: since the remodel there are two large tables in the middle of the store with outlets underneath. brand new chairs of higher quality. the individual tables are now a bit smaller so it can be tight with your laptop , phone and coffee but overall the improvements work. i don't like the air-dryer in the bathroom over paper towels but that goes for every place that seems to be doing this. paper towels are handing for opening doors in public restrooms. i'm not ocd about it but i prefer not to touch bathroom doorknobs during cold and flu season for obvious reasons.

i've been coming here for a week now during the interactions 2012 conference and despite he staff being slammed, they have been incredibly welcoming and courteous while serving up my grande morning crack with a reduced-fat turkey bacon muffin. seriously, bravo to anyone who has to work in the service industry during any conference, i'm its never easy to keep a smile on your face yet these folks manage to do it.

it used to be nice to meet friends at a starbucks, sit down enjoy a coffee. now you can't complain about the prices because all are overpriced however when the service sucks and the place smells like it has a dead body in the back that is a deal breaker.

it can get crowded at times, and for some reason they will assign one of the less-experienced/slower baristas during those times. i feel bad for them because they're clearly struggling, and are flustered when people tell them their drink wasn't made correctly. i personally just let it go, but if i was in a rush, or really picky that day, i could see getting annoyed. after all, the drinks are pricey at $4+. i like it best here during a less busy afternoon.
when my order is actually correct, it's great. 3/5 times it is right. staff is friendly just not very attentive to orders.
terrible service at this place nobody cares about you they have only one person to take orders wherase a long line of people waiting for coffee.they really need to focus on quality service.

absolutely love this starbucks! in my opinion it's in the wrong spot. should be more centered in the mall. the coffee is amazing! i recommend the passion fruit sweetened tea or the pumpkin spice latte. the workers are very fast compared to other starbucks staff. the store itself is modern and very clean. it's a little small and lacks seating. but that's probably the biggest store they could get. absolutely love this starbucks! doesn't get any better than this!

i stopped here with the intention of getting in a little reading before i headed home. and although the people that worked there were friendly, and my drink was made correctly, the drink station was dirty, and trash was spilling out of it. the sugar packets seemed like they had been wadded up and shoved in the container. they were completely out of vanilla powder, and there were no chocolate powder or nutmeg bottles at all, just cinnamon. and for some reason, for a tuesday afternoon, it was sooooo super crowded that the introvert in me didn't want to have to sit that close to strangers. unfortunately, late december doesn't usually lend itself to sitting outside. so, i was mildly displeased with my experience.

nothing like a vanilla chai tea latte with oatmeal & brown sugar. hearty and semi-heavy on the tummy. i needed a nap afterwards. their breakfast cage free egg white chicken sausage or turkey bacon is rather tasty as well. stole a few bites from my friends. the wraps are light with low calories so there is no guilt associated with eating 'em. this location is very crowded most times so be prepared for the wait.

we came here tonight for 1/2 price frapps. the guy who took our order seemed nice & knowledgeable. however, the poor guy with red hair looked like a deer caught in the head lights. don't know if he was new, but nobody was helping him at all & it took him about 10 mins to make one frapp. if i were in a hurry & needed to get to work i would've been livid! but that was very unprofessional of his co-workers to leave him hangin!
it's as good as any starbucks..mediocre at best

don't do a mobile order here you still have to wait in the long drive through line to get your order what is the point of this?? took me 15 minutes and i should have been in and out. never coming back!

located inside willow grove mall, this starbucks is like any other free standing store except it doesn't open at 6am. it still opens a little earlier than the other stores here so if you are one of those "walkers" in the mañana then you have a place to get a caffeine fix to keep your energy up. there are plenty of pastries, cups, mugs, bags of coffee, and other starbucks themed knick-knacks to purchase in addition to the standard beverages. there is plenty of seating and there are two single occupancy bathrooms in this store, so you don't need to use the general mall bathrooms to pee from the diuretic you just drank and paid $5 for, nice. anyway, staff is always friendly, drinks are generally made to sarbucks standards, and the place is always clean. check it out if you are at the mall.

i don't know why, but i usually have trouble getting my drink made correctly at most starbucks. i was out with a friend and we decided to stop for coffee here on a whim, and i was slightly worried. but, my drink actually came out perfectly here. the staff were all very friendly, and it wasn't too busy which was nice. if i was ever in the area again, i would definitely consider stopping in here again.

bad!!!! most of my order was right, except for the drink i wanted. i had ordered a salted caramel mocha. i was given a pumpkin spice latte. not what i wanted. i cannot say that i was pleased with my drink. #starbucksfail

i'm a fan of starbucks. i am a huge fan of the new feature in the starbucks app for ordering ahead. you just order, go to the location, and get yours off the counter. efficient. here in #nashville, a couple of local #starbucks spots have awful lot designs. starbucks (west end) and starbucks (dominican). west end has a massive and dangerous drive-thru line sitting in the road all morning. dominican has not nearly enough parking, creating a free-for-all need to invent solutions on the fly. sometimes, this results in a cranky customer yelling at another, like i was yelled at this morning. surely causing your customers to bicker was not part of the brand promise, starcrack. this lot also has a dead-end into the oncoming drive-thru traffic, complicating the discovery of the lack of adequate space even more. (starbucks smyrna is also guilty of this one.) i realize the previous councilwoman from my area of town is credited with securing the starbucks (and neighboring lexus) deal, but clearly the planning was poor. kinda what you'd expect, given her reputation. i was actually going to take this issue on if i had run for council, especially the west end part. so the question is, what are you guys gonna do about it??

i have gone to this location three days in a row and have yet to receive a single cup of coffee. i waited in line in the drive-through for over 10 minutes each visit before leaving. all i wanted was a simple cup of coffee with cream. i guess i will going to the 7-eleven across the street now on.
if i could select 0 stars, i would. the service was absolutely freaking horrible. the servers/baristas super rude!!!! never will i ever come to this sorry a-word establishment ever again!!!

worst starbucks i have ever been to in my life. incompetent staff that just try to run you out the door. the only reason that i have continued to go this one is because it is about a block away from my house.

indigo and i went through the drive thru. i don't remember what i ordered, something like an almond milk iced latte and an iced water. i do remember paying. i handed the girl a starbucks gift card, informed her that there was probably 5 cents on it, and then handed her 6 dollars (my total was around 5.57). she looked at me, definitely called me stupid in her head, and said "i don't understand what you want me to do. your total is 5.58 and you handed me 6 dollars. do you want me to put this on your card?" i said "no, there's 5 cents on the card and the 6 dollars is to pay for the difference. use the card up please". she wasn't rude or anything, it was just a little funny. indigo gives this place 10 stars for their excellent refreshing ice water.
great location. and they have a drive thru. starbuvks is consistent no matter where you go. i like this location due to its proximity to mount rose ski area. enjoy.

i've never seen a balcony within a starbucks before! mind blown!! i wish they had that in my neck of the woods. i would enjoy it so much more.. all the starbucks look the same, but this one is set apart from the rest. the young man who took my order was really friendly and seemed to have a nice personality. i used to work for starbucks and i can honestly say that excellent customer service skills are acquired while working for this particular company, and this young kid seemed to know whats up. as a patron of starbucks' everywhere, its nice to know that they do their best to put a smile on your face.

this review is not for the quality of the product but for the customer service, or in their case- lack thereof. this starbucks location continually has crappy customer service, and from the way it looks- it's all coming from one cranky bitch. i went in one morning to get a breakfast sandwich (ham & cheese artisan sandwich with no egg)...and noticed that another type of sandwich had bacon on it. mm, bacon...right? so i asked if bacon could be put onto my sandwich. the answer: no. let me be clear, it wasn't the "no" that was the problem, per se. it was the snarky attitude of the woman behind the counter. it wasn't 'no sorry' or 'you'd have to purchase both sandwiches'..it was "no" with the implication and attitude that the answer is no because she'd rather go stick her chest out and flirt with that married man over there than dick around with something for me. i prodded as to why it couldn't be done, and got the *sigh* and even an *eyeroll* from the woman behind the counter. how stupid of me for not knowing that the sandwiches comes pre-packaged, right? *slaps forehead* i totally forgot i worked for starbucks last week! i pointed out that if egg could be taken off of a sandwich, then surely something could be put on a sandwich. i also pointed out that i had no problem paying for the other sandwich so that the ingredients could be combined into one. apparently a ham & bacon merger was imposssible for her. when, let's be clear - every other starbucks is capable of doing what she is telling me she cannot do. i am the customer. i did not ask you for anything for free. if you have bacon in the building and i ask for bacon on something- then do your frickin' job and give me the damn bacon! if i ask for a bacon frappacino i expect you to start blending. i have no clue what this woman's issue was...maybe she needs to get laid because her bitchery was off the charts. so i grabbed my drink and told the manager at the time (who is now retired) that i would not be back. and i meant it. until i went back. ugh. i got in a situation where it was either stop there for something, or starve til morning. blah blah blah i go in at 6:20pm and order my sandwich. miss stick-up-her-butt was there and couldn't make my food because she'd turned the oven off. um, why? why did you turn off the oven 40 minutes before you're supposed to close? simple. she wanted to leave and instead of serving customers she just turns off everything and turns people away. lame. . .
never answer the phone, also they are very rude and ugly every time i go there. i don't recommend going to this location.

the line at this starbucks is always horrendously long and today, after waiting in line for 15 min, they're out of cold brew and the strawberry refresher that i ordered is basically water. not to mention the lid had drops of coffee on it. anyone who has ever ordered the strawberry refresher can look at this and tell it's just watered down. it's disappointing when you wait forever, pay almost $5 for a drink, and this is what it looks like.

went in monday night 1/27/2020 around 7:00, and was abruptly greeted by jerry. i was attempting to order a cold brew with almond milk and french vanilla. jerry asked me how many pumps i wanted, which i was never asked so i said i don't want it too sweet. with that he blatantly rolled his eyes and said that's not what i asked, how many pumps do you want...:. once i was given my drink it was slid across the counter, and i went to thank him and all i got was him to shake his head. that is horrible customer service. i will never return to this starbucks. save your money and go somewhere that they welcome and appreciate their guest.

let me boast and brag. i've been coming to this starbucks since it opened. i've seen manager after manager, barista after barista come and go but the level of service and quality has been consistent. i've had my share of favorite managers from paige, dan, to our current. the current barista staff of guys and girls are by far my favorite crew. i'll have my triple espresso with steam cream please!

the "owner" posted a photo of a drive-thru which doesn't exist. i went to this location with a physically-disabled friend because of that photo, only to find out there is no drive-thru. this starbucks is now on my ever-growing "never again" list.
not your best model of starbucks... tiny... teenwage driven and no drive thru... parking is a nightmare lol
arguably the worst starbucks i have ever visited. unfriendly baristas, very slow service, and dirty throughout. do not recommend.

somebody call the health inspector! this has to be the dirtiest starbucks i've ever seen. i realize that most everything by there is prepackaged and wrapped but somebody has to clean up. theres food, dirt and hair everywhere. the baristas are playing games on the phone. instead they should look up and see how filthy their environment is.

the service here is absolutely terrible, i will not return. i have came here like 7 times now and gave them the benefit of the doubt because they are a new location. i've ended up in the drivethru line stuck for 30 minutes 2x when there was only 2 cars ahead of me when 1 order each. it's wild how long it takes to get through when they are not short staffed.

i love this location. it's bigger than their old location down the street. the staff is very nice and respectful. they are fairly fast at making your drink, depending upon how crowed it is. i'll definitely be coming back.
a starbucks. this is a go to starbucks when there are holidays or you are up early. yes they are open real early and often. clean. friendly staff!

by far and away the best starbucks that i go to in the stl area. i work right next door and frequent this location and it always delivers. unlike some other starbucks that i've gone to over the years, this location really goes out of their way to connect and get to know their customers. that speaks volumes to me and is something that doesn't go unnoticed. even on their extremely busy mornings you'll see the staff with a smile on their faces, still making great drinks. great place to grab some espresso and great people working behind the scenes.

wow, this is not a great starbucks! it's location is what it is (forget it if you're heading west on germantown pike -- this is for the eastbounders only). the bathroom seemed old and kind of dirty, which is unusual for a starbucks and the store seemed a little crummy as well. it wasn't terrible -- they made my drink just fine, but it just wasn't as nice as their stores usually are. i guess i go to starbucks with a certain expectation of their standard and this store does not seem to meet it.

messed up on three of my drinks. and then i decided to give them another chance but then they messed up in my drink again! and they're pretty slow as well. but once you complain about your drink they make sure it's complete.

i really like this place. i don't care about the aesthetics. i'm just getting coffee and waiting for my daughter, for one reason or another.i sat in here off and on for hours ,some months ago, when my daughter was auditioning and kept getting callbacks. eventually, and happily, she got the job. yay!! the staff was as anxious as me as i sat there waiting. they are so friendly and upbeat. i'd rather deal w a floor that needs sweeping and has nice, cordial, funny people than vice versa. and the caramel brûlée was pretty good- though i will have to take out a second mortgage to afford another one. starbucks' prices are too crazy, so this is a pure treat. and one more thing. why is the smallest drink a "tall"? it boggles my little mind!

i like starbucks in general. but this location was supposed to be big because of all the high class people. aka rich. i come to this location about 4 times a month. every time we come i cannot find a seat. yes it is big but not bid enough. i like to hag with the rich folks but just to tight with the seats. but even doe 282 los altos is a bit smaller it is all so for the rich but comfortable and a coma dating to all. thank you for your time to read this i hope this will help in the future.

there are actually 3 starbucks on this boulevard, but i always like this location the most. well, maybe 4 if including the one inside lakeside mall. one reason is because it looks more modern and the seatings arrangement aren't odd like the other two. personally, this place has a friendly atmosphere that the other 2 locations lack. i have ordered through their drive way before and honestly it is very slow. so it is much faster that you park your car and order inside. another thing to note, if you are planning to study for a few hours here, please bring a jacket or small blanket. this place is extremely cold and if you ask the employees to turn the ac down a little bit, they won't do it.

i got a caramel crumble ribbon crunch, the first time they made it it barely tasted like carmel just plain ice. asked a second time to remake the drink came out the same. while making it they rushed, and there was no point they weren't even that busy!? the guy making it also had a little attitude due to him having to make it a second time. i do not recommend this starbucks.

terrible service. they are supposed to open for 5am, and for the 4th morning in a row we pulled in the drive thru between 5:05-5:15am, and the two-three women that are supposed to working either didn't acknowledge us or told us they won't be serving until 5:30. needless to say i won't be back and recommend the same anyone trying to go before 5:30am.
no matter how many times i go here, emily always has a smile on her face when she serves customers. i know the job is busy and tough at times, but it never seems to phase her.

so i visited this starbuck a couple of times and forgot why i stopped. i have asked for a hot white chocolate. the server then wants to correct me and says hot white mocha. no sweetie i said what i said and meant. hot white chocolate!!!! meaning it should be what color white!!!!! so when i get it it's like a regular hot chocolate and no taste of white chocolate then i'm trying to explain to him he wants to go back and forth with me over the difference like i'm stupid which only irritated me more. listen! i know you use to serving coffee but not everyone wants/drinks coffee!!!! i'm really tired of having to fully explain the concept of such a simple drink. hot white chocolate!!!!!!

i was so excited that they were moving because that would mean adequate parking and an easier in and out. i didn't realize that meant that the fabulous staff at the train station location would be gone. i've gone in several times and each time the staff has either been rude or messed up an order. unsweetened black tea is not a difficult drink, but they manage to. i'll be going to exton, which is saying a lot because they're slower than molasses in february.
served me cold coffee! i was in a rush and mobile ordered and didn't have time to go back in but that's a disgrace!
very good! customer service was very good. frap wasn't as thick as i like but still good. :-) would stop in again if ever this way again.

this is a great starbucks! i have spent many hours studying here. the staff is friendly and not only ask your name, but really try to learn it! i always find it to be clean and organized, and the coffee is delicious!

i have been going to this starbucks for years on the weekends. i have always looked forward to getting a delicious, hot, brewed coffee with a shot of espresso. however for the last few months it has been terrible. the coffee is now often old, bitter and cold. the barista seems unskilled and unfamiliar with common coffee making techniques. it's very disappointing that i no longer look forward to my weekend cup of starbucks coffee.

this location is terribly understaffed. the line had at least 50 people in it wrapping all the way back to c9. the workers are rude and slow. the restaurant is absolutely filthy! starbucks should shut this location down. it's a disgrace! i'd give them -5 if possible.

it is not the best coffee in town, but it sure is consistent. i love the fact that i can walk into any starbucks in the country and get something that i enjoy. my drink of choice is a tall vanilla bean frappuccino with a shot of espresso, or a grande with two shots if i want a boost. give it a shot, it is delicious! anyway, i like this location, check it out!

why does anyone go to starbucks? slow, dirty. the incompetence of the staff oozes throughout the entire store. so many better places close by. if you want long waits, poor service, dirty store, by all means this is your place. try the mustache cafe for the best in sb.

last time i go to this particular starbucks. woman who helped me took my order and just left the cashier. i stood there for a good few minutes and had to ask the neighboring cashier what happened. cappuccino barely had any liquid - majority foam. go to a different starbucks.

this is the best starbucks i have ever been to! the service is super friendly, and the atmosphere is perfect for studying or for hanging out. they have tons of seating! it is the best. always go here.

so...i love this chain. the variety of quality coffee drinks, the app, the rewards. this store though... need to prepare for busy weekends. i've been waiting for 30 minutes for online order. unacceptable.

very disorganized and incapable of getting basic brewed coffee served in under 10 minutes. stay away if you actually want to get on the move in the morning. other locations will take care of you better

horrible experience coming here. i was told i "shouldn't order" my coffee choice because "it takes too long." my reply was then "oh, is that way there were 6 cars ahead of me because you arnt fast enough?" i will not be coming back. horrible staff and they are extremely rude!

service use to be "5" stars...been 4 times recently, they're always out of the protein boxes and chicken sausage breakfast wraps. i have yet to find they keep these items stocked. the summit starbucks is "5 stars"!

all downtown coffeeshops were closed, so went here. got the nitro cold brew. no ice, slightly below room temperature, and zero "nitro". tasted like very average coffee forgotten on the counter. wife got an iced peppermint mocha that was 90% ice and tasted terrible. both were smalls, and bill was over $10. what a joke.

this starbucks is slow as christmas. i've sat in the drive thru for 10+ minutes on numerous occasions and had to just pull out. another frustrating thing about this place is the hours. they don't abide by their posted hours. ever. that's not a covid thing. they say they're open until 9 but i've been by at 7:30 before and they have said they're closed. horrible starbucks.

hands down, the coziest, most uniquely styled starbucks location in st. louis? the ultimate opposite of the new, drive-thru focused locations that have been opening? this is a corporate coffeehouse that encourages some lingering. the interior is richly themed: an olde english pub, complete with wood-burning hearth, dark timbers and beautiful, stained glass windows. old meets new when it comes to amenities though. mobile ordering is available and quick, the menu and music are the same as at most other stores, and there's wifi. in addition to the two indoor spaces, with plenty of patio tables in front of the shop (there's not much of a view per se, but the option is nice), i really cannot think of a better starbucks for a meet-up.

it's the bogo day, and the wait time was about 45 minutes. everyone works hard and still very friendly. however, my drinks were very disappointed. i ordered extra hot and got them warm. also, the drinks were less than 3/4 full. so i brought one drink and break it into two cups???

went through the drive-through asked for a trenta unsweetened very little ice green tea with two splenda. and what do i receive... a trenta with extra ice green tea sweetened with sweeten low! really!!! so then i went inside and asked the lady if they have splenda and she said they do and they had to remake my whole drink

just stopped by this morning, 8 am on a sunday, and the barista chris was so nice, and pleasant. i love starbucks coffee in general, but i wanted to point out specifically this barista because it is not often you find sweet, people who care about you and your morning! my morning was not going so well, and his positive energy was amazing and made the whole starbucks a better place. thanks chris.
i think i just enjoy the convenience of a coffee drive though. i'm not sure i even like starbucks but i don't mind your frappuccinos in the summer.
a totally fine/normal starbucks. do you have a gift card? why not go here and spend it?

wow, i haven't been to starbucks in months and i just went this morning and the drink was ok at best but the service... i was helped by a lady named brooke and she shined a warm ray of sunlight into my soul and cured my ailments with her warm smile and kind aura. love that, will come again

i have been to all over the country, and have never felt i needed to write a review for one unit now. when my friend and i walked into the place they had a large display right in the doorway [blocking everything, so clearly they would like you to see it.] with their new refreshers in a can. when i picked one up to look at it, the cashier from a distance shouted to us "those are gross. the ones we make are ok, but those ones are gross." ummmm...ok. we approach the counter to place our orders and the girl walks away. we stand there for several minutes waiting for someone to ask us what we wanted to order.. but nothing. there were 7 people working behind the counter. once we did put in the order we ended up waiting15 minutes for it. there were no drinks they needed to make in front of ours. they were all standing around talking. the funny thing there was what appeared to be a trainer behind the counter teaching a new employee to do something. the man that ordered behind us didnt get eye contact, asked questions that were answered with short snotty answers and was treated even worse. we overheard conversations about employees and their medications, weekend plans, hair products, etc. i am still shocked about the lack of proffesionalism and the dirtiness of the store, but what blows me away the most is that the drinks took 15 minutes to make. i live in chicago and watch a team of 2 take care of a line of 30 people with no problems. these 7 people couldn't get an iced drink together. get it together.

easily not only the worst starbucks around, but the worst coffee house ever. seriously, this is the place people go when they want their wet dreams ruined. over half my orders are messed up, but i stop here because it's the only sbux in this area. pretty sure the only reason this place stays busy is because they have a captive audience. the staff is consistently surly and confused, undertrained and uninvested in anything they do. this place is so bad i'm almost ready to quit my coffee. almost.

i am finally sitting down to write a review for this starbucks. sadly, it is by far the worst run starbucks i have been too. first, i would like to start off by saying that literally the only reason i go to this starbucks/continue to go is because there is a drive-thru. and to be honest, their wait time in drive-thru is horrendous (you can even watch the counter at the window when you do step inside.) issues i have with this location are as follows: wrong food given undercooked food given long wait times overcooked food wrong drink order (had this numerous times) cold brew often off the menu (consistently for the last 4 sundays in a row i have gone.--someone is seriously messing up on saturday.) lack of organization/awareness of employee to employee and employee to customer. i am not sure how long this post should be, because i have been going here for months and every time i think, "ugh i can't believe i spent my money at that location, again." i have kids so it's hard because i don't want to have to get out of the car all the time (i know, first world problem) but if the area had any other coffee place with a drive-thru i would be going there instead.

my friend is a healthcare worker who serves trays to 50+ patients in a covid infested hospital, putting herself at risk every day, while being even more at risk due to her asthma. i was very upset to hear she went to this starbucks and they told her the free coffee for health care workers is only for nurses doctors etc, when she is making a sacrifice as well. this was very ignorant. she had to explain to them why she was a healthcare worker when they are the ones offering it?? she deserves better after the risk she takes everyday.

seriously. slowest starbucks ever. it's 9am. on a sunday. 5 ladies working... all moving at a snails place like they haven't slept all evening or something. skipped the drive through, went inside. i was the only one waiting to be helped.. 7 minutes later. yes. 7. i finally ordered my coffee. what the hell??

love this place! they are always so nice and meet you w/ a greeting no matter how crowded it is. i am not a coffee drinker, but the caramel apple spice and crossaint are my weakness. also, during the holidays they have this creamy cranberry bar thing! awesome.

lack of basics including heating (it's cold through out even with several layers on and a down coat; even worse is the doors were swinging open or are left open so any one standing in line or sitting nearby really freezes). also several times i received the wrong order so staffing training needs are an issue. the customer counter (napkins, spices, etc) was often not refilled. staff was very friendly though. we never plan to come here again. more locally owned coffee shops nearby are definitely a better use of time, value and money.

ok now giving this location a 1 star! this place takes the longest to keep the line moving . i waiting 5 whole minutes at the window for them to give me 2 cake pops in a paper sack. young workers seem out of touch with their customers, never happy, not welcoming, i get that they are tired but please ! my drinks never taste good at this poco for some reason , it's like they don't care how your drinks come out

since i was in the area and nearby, i had not been here since the summer i wanted to see what i could get from here today. so, here's my review... positives love the location being in cherry hill, which is not too far from my house and township. as always, great atmosphere when you enter the place. two women conversating in their workout attire, working professionals drinking lattes and specially made drinks, people on their laptops making use of the store's wi - fi, barista's taking orders and fixing drinks for the patrons. very busy this morning but not to where it slowed down customers or orders. staff was courteous and professional and all had positive attitudes. ended up getting a caramel coffee, but it took an unexpected turn... negative: ... my coffee order was black!!! i requested it with milk and caramel syrup. it was black coffee with only caramel syrup. very very hot upon my first sip and was unable to drink until i got home and put my own creamer in it. the only thing that went wrong on this particular visit.
pastries are yummy staff is nice. i have issues with my laptop battery and it was a bit of a chore to find a table for two and an electrical plug, but once we got settled in, everything was great!

a big shout out to the crew at houghton and broadway! they are always so friendly and they make the most consistent drinks of any walk in or drive up i've been to anywhere. it means a lot to know i'm never going to have to return my drink because it doesn't taste right. thanks everyone.

i love the location of this shop. i think the management is super cool and always engaging. because it is off the interstate, this starbucks tends to find more traffic than most around but they handle it with great ease and efficiency. it's a great place to conduct an interview if you can find a table tucked in a corner but it's better suited for more social activities like sitting with some friends and enjoying a delicious coffee. again, love the management and how well this starbucks is run!
to be fair, raising stars back, since have been back several times, and have not encountered the same sour barista. everyone else has earned respect for a job well done!

this may be the busiest starbucks in the united states. it is insanely busy and the staff are working their butts off to keep up. if you are impatient or inclined to complain you should go elsewhere. the team at this place demonstrates remarkable work ethic. thanks for the hard work. my drink was perfect.

this starbucks never gets drinks made to order, the drinks are always super watered down and they never taste good. feels like a "half-assed" starbucks, including employee effort, overall food, and customer service. you definitely don't get what you pay for. go to a different starbucks.

having a full functioning starbucks inside of a hospital was a great idea. there long lines in the morning but it moves really quickly. if you go to starbucks, i would suggest getting the app because you can get points on your purchase and eventually get free coffee. you can't redeem your free drinks at this location.

let's face it, we all kind what starbucks is about. you have so many choices and sizes at a premium price. i am fine with that. their customer service is heralded as top notch. i have found the cool refreshers to be a great option to coffee or tea. the issue lately with the starbucks locations i have visited is that they do not fill the cup. it seems trivial, but when you pay top dollar, you want your beverage filled, not an inch or so from the top. next time, i will complain but it was just too busy. bitching in front of others and backing up the line was not what i wanted. let's hope they browse yelp reviews and get it right!

from an avid starbucks customer, this location is one of the worst. make sure to have plenty of time to wait for your order to come out. it took just a few minutes for my breakfast to come out, but another 25 minutes for my coffee to be made.

it's interesting to read some of the reviews of most any starbucks. my experience at any and all of their locations in six states i've received service from is they provide the best of the best in coffee options. check out the lines for service and your answer should be they offer a great product. you have to wait your turn clowns! anyone ragging on a starbucks comes across as a pretentious putz and a classic whiner and someone i would go out of my way to avoid. okay, that's it, i'm heading out now for a extra hot no whip mocha!

they're so friendly at this location and up until lately my drinks have been great. but the past two times i've ordered a hot caramel macchiato they've forgotten to put in the vanilla syrup. i put some sugar in it when i got home and it was ok, but i should t have to pay $7 for a drink that's made incorrectly. they did fix it, but the second drink i got was still wrong and i didn't have the heart to throw another one away.

really awful service. the women working this morning are so rude. one came out with a sandwich and yelled out the type but not the name. when everyone looked around to make sure they were snagging someone else's sandwich, she starts saying "people looking around all confused like they don't know what they want. this is how people take other people's sandwiches." basically berating us for her error - we give a name at checkout for a reason!
slowest starbucks i've ever been to. consistently, every time, s. l. o. w. food and drinks are good when you eventually get them.
starbucks is a chain sure but the coffee always tastes good. the barista spells my name right on my cup every time i come in.

i ask them to make sure my drink is correct when taking my order due to my dietary restrictions. the did not listen to my order. they had to remake it. while i was waiting for my order to be remade, they had to remake another 3 drinks that were messed up. the lady that took my order kaylee wasn't paying attention to what she was doing and her co workers around her. i've worked in a coffee shop before, i understand it's fast paced however you are supposed to watch around you and not damn near knock drinks out of your co workers hands. i do not recommend coming to this starbucks unless you want to wait an additional 20 min for your drink to be remade if they get it right that time.

this was my go-to starbucks. this morning i got a rude barista who chastised me very publicly for trying to get my own drink carrier. i assume it's a policy that when you order multiple drinks on a mobile order, you should be expected to wait in line and try to flag down a busy barista to get a drink carrier so that you can actually carry your drinks out of the store. you know, waiting in line to get a drink carrier almost defeats the idea of putting in a mobile order, doesn't it? i get the idea of not wasting drink carriers, but common sense and a little customer service would be appreciated. in any event, there are lots of starbucks and other coffee shops around town where you can get better customer service and you don't get a side of attitude with the $5 cup of coffee. i'll gladly go there instead.
don't order a drink with no ice. payed 5$ for half a cup, and got charged extra for ice on the side.
this is a decent starbucks. i was surprised at how large the parking lot was, but every single spot was still taken and i had to wait for someone to leave. looks like they need to expand, to me!

okay. i don't normally give 1* to establishments unless i'm really irritated. just isn't worth my time or that of the place i'm reviewing. this is different. i have had many nitro cold brews at various starbucks in the area. i order a newer item on the menu which is the nitro cold brew. this is essentially a cold brewed coffee from a keg. i tried it and instantly knew something was wrong. it had was lighter in color and at first glance i thought it might have had creamer. i pulled around and voiced my concerns and was instantly belittled for my knowledge of nitro cold brew. it was my fault? in reality, the coffee wasn't as strong as i was used to for a nitrogen cold brewed coffee. point is, if your product doesn't live up to a customer's standards. don't make it their fault. talk to them and make them feel like their opinion counts. peace
have been going here for a decade but staff keeps changing and not for the better. no longer a friendly place have moved on to other coffee shops

have been coming here a few times a week for the past two years. it's starbucks, it's the only one close to the hospitals and such here on s grand so it's a popular spot. they do get busy but it's always service with a smile, they have been hiring some new great baristas recently as well! always enjoy time spent here.

this is hands down the best starbucks in the santa barbara area, and there a lot of them! i go in there many mornings and some nights and even when it's busy, they're cool. gets a little nuts sometimes, though and they go through cream so fast! the bathroom passcode thing is annoying. what happened to the singing barista though?
love their selections of teas and coffee. very friendly. wish it had more seating, great location.

i like this starbucks a lot. it is not huge, but the employees are always friendly and on the ball. the location is clean and the coffee is always ready. i wish they would have a better selection of food as some of the newer starbucks do. the parking lot is shared with several other stores and can be a challenge. seems to be a favorite of college students as well, as there is always a lot of studying going on, which sometimes makes it difficult to find somewhere to sit. tonight i just took my coffee to go, so there was not issue for me.

i was surprised to find poor reviews on a starbucks location, but after a few experiences here, i completely agree. management needs to get involved to stream line and smooth out their work flow. twice, i placed mobile orders where after arriving and standing there for another 5-10mins and asking for help, did i then find out they were out of the particular sandwiches i ordered. if they can't update the app to say they're out so i can't order in the first place, then they should at least place a note there with my name so i can speak with a barista rather than continue to wait. i understand any food industry can get hectic and busy, but you'd think after a while, they would've worked on some process improvements. customer service is not always consistently up to starbucks standards either. too bad.

oh geeessshhh...i was visiting nashville and came here one morning and that older cashier has a cough and when she coughs she covered her mouth with her bare hand...i ordered a pike place and she handle my coffee cup with her bare hand that she used when she covered her mouth while she's coughing...she didn't even excuse herself to wash her hand not she's even wearing a globes or a surgical mask while she's working in her cold...i end up throwing my coffee as i am scared to drink it and catch her cough and cold...forget about this place if they are health hazard to the public...why this starbucks location would not watch out and train their employee working with a cough and cold...

so i went to the starbucks today and i asked for a pink drink with zero strawberries trenta they gave me a trenta pink drink with extra strawberries in it then i went back to get a free remake they gave me a venti pink drink with no strawberries frick you starbucks never go to this one go to barclay rt 70 drive thru!!!!!!!

slowest starbucks ever! if you're in a rush do not come here. there's a line to the door and there's one cashier, one barista, two other staff in the back when they should be taking care of their morning rush traffic! the barista told me that he "can only take one drink order at a time" and walks away when i try to tell him my second drink order!

my wife and i went here this morning. there was an obviously "disturbed" man sitting at a tall table near where we sat down at the large community table. i quickly noticed he was staring first at my wife and then at me. i looked away and when i looked back he was still staring. we moved to a far corner of the store. after awhile he stopped staring and began having a nice conversation with himself and making odd hand gestures. yes he is probably harmless. my main complaint is that when we brought this to the store's attention the response we received was "oh yeah.he is a regular here.its his safe zone and he is harmless" very nice and caring. however i think when you are a paying customer (and we have been going to starbucks for over 15 years here and in seattle) you are entitled to a peaceful environment. i thought the store response was inappropriate and unprofessional. we told the store we will not return and they seemed unconcerned to say the least. i have seen similar situations in our seattle starbucks handled professionally and in a proper safe manner. this place needs some customer service training/upgrades for sure!
