let me start off by saying im not from here. this is my second time down here in the last 4 months, first time at this sb. normally goto the one in the brandon mall. but glad i came in, apparently the guy i think his name was cory now works here. i kno him from the other starbucks. anyway, its been about 3 months since i last saw him & he remembered my drink. now that is what i call customer service
sandwiches are cold a lot! still the best coffee ofcourse.....
nice place to have coffee. very friendly people. very good environment. i like this place.
this is my local starbucks and it's amazing! new staff is super nice!! drinks are yummy!! glad there has been a serious change!! this is what i expect when i visit starbucks!

the baristas are bunch beauties. drinks are always made correctly and make me feel as good as some cocaine on a monday morning. sounds depressing? actually it's the opposite. me and a couple boys last week did a full send and nailed some broads. woke up monday morning did the white and it made me feel like i was born in a new light.

i don't venture to the westbank often, but i was out running errands, had my laptop with me and figured i'd stop in to get some work done. not happening. the music is so unbearably loud that i feel like i've wandered into a night club that serves lattes. the staff is not really doing their job. i understand when you're young and it's slow at work, you want to chit chat and be social with your coworkers. but don't hang out in the back and ignore your customers or be louder than the music while you're playing around. the difference between a national chain like starbucks and a local place is that with a chain you know right off what to expect from every location because they're supposed to be run the same way. if you hate a relaxing atmosphere and just want to party it up while you eat your scone, this is the place for you.

this is one of the best starbucks i've been to in a long time. the food comes out hot and fresh and the drinks are made by expert baristas. the speed is good for the area they're located in and it's amazing!!!!

ordered a specialty drink, one of the featured items. paid & was handed my drink missing a key ingredient- the specialty topping. when i asked they shrugged & said they ran out (sorry not sorry). i wasnt informed ahead that the popular item couldnt be made. i wasnt offered a refund, discount or another item. drink was so overly sweet it was inedible & i love sweets. in addition the store is dirty & smells like a toilet. ripped chairs, dirty floor s. self service area dirty. really smells bad. employee shld b cleaning not yapping on her cell phone.

quality location, quality staff (yes, nate, i'm referring to you!) a bit busy on weekends but that's suburban square for you. only thing i'd change is making it open later, especially on weekends. a girl needs to study!

service may be fast but not so friendly. and can someone please answer me why they charge $.60 for chocolate drizzle on top of my frappé? i will not be coming back to this location. the drive thru was packed but they get cars in and out. there are a lot of workers inside the restaurant but they aren't smiling. i went at 8am and no one seemed to be happy to be there. i give this location 1 star because i frequent starbucks quite often, and i am a gold reward member, but this charge is ridiculous and just made me question my loyalty to this franchise.

the location is prime in the 5 points area. the flow of the store is simple & efficient with this social distancing season. the drinks were good but the service - not so good. after they got one of our drinks wrong we let them know & put it back on the counter. the girl was snippy & told us to throw it away bc there was nothing they could do with it instead of apologizing for their mistake & taking it from us.

my absolute biggest issue with this store is their mobile ordering. what's the point of putting in my order if i still have to wait 10 min for you to acknowledge that i'm standing at the pickup area waiting for my coffee that's cold by the time i get it. put the orders in the same place as the rest and not behind the counter!

the baristas are friendly enough, the coffee is.. well, starbucks coffee, but the internet connection is absolutely dreadful - comparable to a cell phone and what ultimately led me to look elsewhere. there's usually copious amounts of outlets and seating though with about twelve 2-tops lining the giant wall of windows looking out upon latimer st (read: dumpsters and a parking garage). occasionally bums will coddle up next to the window outside and attempt to entertain you with their histrionics. you could add or subtract a star if you are or aren't into that.

i'm not sure why i haven't written a review before. this is my go to spit to pick up my morning. staff know me by name and my order. it's nice to have a real conversation versus knowing people are being completely fake because their boss may be there. they always ask how you are and it always feel genuine. as for the drinks, i'm never disappointed. they care if they get your order right. i order a carmel drink every time and they're pretty good with making sure the carmel isn't everywhere. my drink is always right.
cozy seating. not much in the way of privacy, but peaceful and quiet, with folks keeping to themselves.
starbucks are everywhere but the staff at this location makes you feel like you are in a locally owned coffee shop. tyler always remembers my name and makes sure my drink is perfect. great staff!

i'm confused as to why this starbucks doesn't have a complete 5 star rating! for starters, there's actually a parking lot...in philly! mind you, it is shared with a few other stores and a tdbank, but dont worry, they wont tow your car for parking in their lot for 5 min. to run into the bucks for a morning latte. secondly, this location received a complete reno, not so long ago. the difference is great, and the flow of traffic to the register is much better. the real reason i love this starbucks however is the people. the associates are so nice, know you by name, and can spell out what you want before you can tell them, simply because they remember. because of these reasons, i will go out of my way to stop there, even though there is a starbucks 3 minutes from where i work. :)

i have been to this starbucks on several occasions and have not had very good customer service. maybe it is because i am going thru the drive thru? i always feels like i am annoying them or they are doing me some huge favor. my drink was consistent with other starbucks and service was done in a timely manner.

first time i've ever been in a starbucks where i asked the "barista" if a particular coffee would be good over ice and she replied with a shrug, "i don't know, i've never tried it." i get that not every starbucks employee has tried every coffee they sell, but i can't believe she didn't ask someone else or at least feign interest. it turned out, the coffee was good, and service was polite enough; but this is not consistent with the usual starbucks experience. disappointing.

if you want to head to the worst starbucks in the area, go here. they are literally soooooo slow and incompetent. i feel like no one here had any real training. i honestly feel like maybe macy's just threw some of their employees in there like yeah, figure it out. i tried this location multiple times thinking each time they were having an off day, and nope, they're just rude and roll their eyes when you order your drink. i ordered a triple blonde espresso over ice with vanilla sweet cream cold foam. the barista didn't understand what i wanted, then another barista chimed in and said it was a tik tok drink, and that it's ordered to avoid paying for the "extras".. like i don't understand what i was trying to get free? and i don't even know what a damn tik tok drink is. like girl if you don't want to work there and help the customers then gtfo and go work somewhere else. short girl, heard her say she was from chester county.. that's no excuse to be rude. and they gave me regular espresso instead of blonde!

i am a current starbucks employee at another store and felt the need to say how amazing this store is. the staff are incredibly friendly and it's my favorite place to hang out and get some work done if i'm in the area. i don't understand how anyone could have complaints about this store. every time i come i pick up tips on how to be better at my store and give better service. 11/10

they had some random person standing out side telling everyone they are closed at 1pm when i showed up a min after. you should really update your hours online or something. or maybe just close this location.
you might say that a starbucks is a s starbucks and in many ways you'd be right. the difference here is the staff. super friendly and fun.

every time i order from them they always mess part of the order up. the first time they gave me the wrong sandwich. the second time someone stole my drink so i asked them to remake it and they made me the wrong drink lol. i'm just trying to have breakfast before class and they mess me up every time.

the location is key to the success of this coffee shop. not far from washington's crossing, bristol, morrisville and such. the layout is nice with lounge chairs and a high top bar in the front. tables around the side in the back along with an small outdoor deck. the staff is knowledgeable and are efficient with their sense of urgency to the customer. free wifi (as with most starbucks). i visit a lot of coffeehouses in the greater philadelphia area and this is one of my staples. so yeah i like it because it's solid not necessarily because it's a starbucks.
this is the slowest starbucks ever. there is only one person working and doing everything. i hate this place. stay away

this is a subpar starbucks which likely only survives on the business of marriott guests. i waited 15 minutes for my steamed milk which i had to request twice after the baristas forgot it. my pastry was stale. the bathrooms are a bit hidden. go outside to the hall, turn left passing the guest gift shop, and continue down the hallway.
i like this place! they get orders done really quick, even during frappy hours when they had at least 30 customers at the same time.

i am bias because this is my "closest to home" shop where they know my name often times & my mobile order is always ready when i go pick it up. i know they are a bit slower when it comes to the drive-thru (which is why i mobile order) but my order is right 99% of the time and that 1% they'll fix it. pam talks to me about my dogs (who love getting pup cups here) and every single worker "good morning"'s me. kind and great service! highly recommend the nitro cold brew with sweet cream as well - it is the perfect boost in the morning!
the best starbucks i've ever been too!!! everyone is so kind and they make my mornings better. they sometimes write cute little notes on my cup and it's the sweetest thing ever!!!

this starbucks is always slow. long drive through line that crawls and even in the store the staff is slow and unmotivated. i have been at the drive thru window and heard them talking rudely about customers and staff. they seem super catty and not motivated by customer service at all.

came here on a saturday evening to study and drink some coffee. the staff is great; i have no complaints at all about them. the store itself is a bit cramped though and the parking lot is extremely tiny. i would imagine they get totally swamped during peak times.
nice big tables to study or work. staff is nice and very helpful. outside seating area to sit in aswell.

stopped by for an iced drink today and waited in the drive-thru for 15 minutes. when i finally got to the front they had two people working. the guy taking the drive thru orders grabbed my card and then went and started making drinks for others even though my drink was there waiting. finally the second worker handed me my card. i had to tell her i had a drink there waiting for me. she barely said anything to me and handed me the cup. the least she could have done was apologize for the wait. i will not be going to this site again. with all of the other coffee competition you would think they would be getting their stuff together.

did an online order, waited inside for our order for about 10-15 mins to realize that new people that were walking in to order received their drinks before us. then we realized our drinks were behind the counter top where we could not see them. when we walked up to the counter to tell the staff member that those were ours she was too busy talking to a friend in there, we had to grab another staff member who was making the drinks to give it to us. they saw us waiting for a while even when we became the only ones left in there.

happy vibe at this starbucks! the baristas are so personable. quite often i am greeted by name and my drink is already being prepared. i was particularly impressed with elmer, who gave five star service every time- equivalent to somewhere like the rosewood or four seasons. great place to chill and do work on the computer as well.

i never leave reviews but i needed to for these amazing baristas! every single time i visit this location they are so unbelievably kind and helpful. as some one who also works in customer service it's extremely refreshing to visit this store and get treated like a friend. the sweet girl named sarah mai, i believe, today asked me my name and was so kind. i told her thank you for always being in such a great mood! she said there's too much darkness in the world so we try to bring some light into your day! this is how every establishment should be. they are so excellent!

i think sybille is right, we could all go for a midnight frapp. that being said, i commuted here from boyertown to get coffee. boyertown only has dunkin donuts or turkey hill gas station. the woman who did monthly updates for her reviews was right, there was an older woman. when we first walked in, the line was about 10 people long with just the older woman. we decided to walk around until the line died down (10 minutes). when we came back, the last lady in line was only 3 away from the front, but a younger woman had come back from a break, i guess. all starbucks drinks pretty much taste the same. the madeline cookies we got were stale. went back 24 hours later, same woman working. quicker service because there wasn't a long line. ordered 3 of the same drinks because monica said she consistently had good drinks. they were all the same. lol, great work xo
love this starbucks! i often stop by here on my way to work, they are always fast, friendly, and my drinks are always perfect and delicious!

i go to this starbucks all the time and haven't really had any insane issues that would make me want to write a yelp review but girl what is this lmao. i ordered a cold brew with cinnamon almond milk foam, and i can tell you one thing it doesn't have an impenetrable later of cinnamon on top

i like this starbucks even though i always forget it exists because of it's location. it's in the new shopping center where wegmans is located but it faces 202 and it's not accessible from the road, you have to pull into the shopping center. 202 is also going up a hill in front of it so unless you are looking for it i feel like it's easy to miss. it's a standard starbucks in terms of layout and drive through. it has plenty of parking and a nice outdoor patio. free wifi.
using up a gift card for an afternoon caffeine treat. i haven't been to this location in forever. remodeled and very nice. tons of space with lots of workspace tables and comfy seats for visiting.
every time i get a drink from here it's disgusting. anytime i order online even if i order 10-15 min ahead of time it's still not ready. i guess i've been spoiled by the brownsburg starbucks.
always have friendly staff when i'm here. nice environment in the bywater. nice lighting in the building by the way. nice play for a little meeting.

this has got to be the saddest location i've ever been to. we sat in line (drive thru) for over 20 minutes total. they were extremely slow! i ordered the chestnut praline frappe nonfat with whip when we finally got to the menu. waited another 5 minutes or so before getting to the window, and they handed me a hot drink. when i explained i had ordered a frozen drink, she said (very rudely might i add) "did you not order the nonfat chestnut praline?" i said "yes, it was supposed to be a frappe." she rolled her eyes and slammed the window shut, and started throwing cups around while trying to make it into a frappe. she then asked us to move forward and she'd bring it out. after waiting another 5 minutes or so, she finally brings it out, doesn't apologize. i start drinking it, and it didn't even taste like it had espresso in it. it tasted like it was just syrup, ice, and milk. i'll never come back to this location as long as i live.

i'm not a coffee drinker, so i can't speak to the quality of the coffee here, but stop here often with my husband who loves coffee any hour of the day or night! starbucks has an offer going on right now where if you take in an empty bag of their ground coffee (which we use at home), they give you a free cup of coffee. downside to this location is it can be difficult at times to pull in and out of the parking spaces as it is on the corner spot of a strip mall, and traffic can get very busy at times.

their management of the drive-through line is incredibly poor. i've never seen anything like it at another starbucks. not only do they take a lot of time between making the orders and taking the orders but also have carried on conversations that last over a minute with people that they know that are driving up to the window as if they have no consideration for the amount of cars that are still in line. they really need to address this before it becomes a bigger problem for their establishment.

i used to stop in this starbucks in the montgomery mall every now and then when my friend worked here back in 2016/2017 or when i was hanging out with a group of friends and we stopped here. since my friend left in august 2017, i haven't really been back since. this can be attributed to a few reasons. one is that i'm not a regular starbucks goer, the second is i mainly visited this location to visit my friend and third is i'm not usually in the montgomery mall as much as i used to be. i don't mind starbuck's products but since my friend left it seems the few times i did stop in this location has gone a bit downhill with service and employees. there's a starbucks much closer to where i live (and even a bigger one across the street from the mall) and i'll stop in this one only if it is convenient.

one of my hangout a while i was passing through this area a lot. the staff here is so polite, they remembered me every time i came in. learned about the caramel brûlée with the extra double shot in it, straight game changer. thanks guys ;)
staff super friendly here! good ol' starbucks. no complaints. no seating inside as of today. all staff wearing masks. spacious and clean.

i disagree with kara. this is not the worst starbucks in the area and actually has one of the nicest coffee lounge spaces in town. tall lofted ceilings look out to mt rose and the surrounding summit sierra mall. it's actually a very scenic location. the coffee is like every other starbucks, good but not great.

the service at this location is sooo slow. the barista that took my drink order was friendly. then i had to wait about 15 minutes for my drink. there were 7 people working and only 1 person on bar. whoever did deployment for that shift needs to have a rethink. one of the baristas was moving breakfast pastries from one tray to another. she could have assisted on bar, but instead the gal on bar gets a newbie to help her. she has to tell newbie everything from how to read the sticker to how to make a frappucino. why would you put your newbie on bar during the busiest time of the day? like every other drive thru location, the focus is on drive thru customers as opposed to walk-ins.

i needed something to help sustain my focus through the night but didn't know what to try. i prefer teas over coffee but these past few weeks have been brutal and i've needed something much stronger. i've had quite a few chai lattes over the past few weeks, i tried the expresso and it was a tad bit too strong not creamy or sweet enough. i asked the barista for a suggested caffeinated coffee - one that is "creamy & sweet" and strong enough to get me through the night....it was perfectly made. my drink was amazing! sorry i didn't get your name- blue or green hair female- but want to thank you for making me a creamy sweet coffee. i appreciate your positive attitude and excellent customer service! ***fellow yelpers please please msg me your suggestions on creamy, sweet and strong starbucks drinks- i need to get through the next couple of days!!! thanks!!!

standing at the paoli train station at 6am waiting for my amtrak train, this starbucks is always a welcome sight. having a starbucks next to a train station is always great. the staff here are friendly and bathrooms are clean. (which is definitely more than i can say for the train station) there is a good amount of seating in this location as well. if you miss your train, a good place to grab a drink and kill some time.
pretty empty for a starbucks at 8am. but a larger sitting area than most and they have outdoor seating.

very poorly run starbucks. lazy, disinterested employees. wont make a bulletproof coffee, making up some nonsense about how they are not allowed to, even though all other starbucks do. this place needs to get up to speed...

stop here quite frequently on my way to school or home from work. this location is nicely tucked away in the mason woods shopping center, you can do some shopping, banking and even hit up the 'bucks. there frapps are yum, but i love the holiday season because they have pumpkin spice, peppermint mocha and also their christmas blend coffee, which now comes in via! the staff are incredibly friendly, and depending on the time of day, you may have to wait a bit, but no big deal (unless you are those suit and tie types that have no patience and really need your double soy no foam asap)!

i go through this drive thru every morning on my way to the hospital before my 12 hour shifts. i order an ice coffee extra light on the cream (because other wise it just tastes like milk and i can't even taste the coffee). starbucks is expensive. i could go across the street to dunkin' and get a $2 coffee that's larger but i don't because i like starbucks. anyway, when they hand it to me and it's too light colored i know it's going to taste like milk so i just ask if they could please pour some out and add more coffee to it, that's all. again it's expensive to not be able to enjoy it. this morning i asked her to do this and she had an attitude, turned around to her coworker and started laughing while mocking me loudly. i could hear her from the drive thru window. she brought it back to me, mocked me to my face while handing it back to me. and i left just thinking you know what... all i wanted was an ice coffee that doesn't taste like milk. i have to go to a hospital now where i have to keep patients alive for 12 hours which can be exceedingly difficult and exhausting. the most peaceful and easy part of my day from 5:45am until getting home exhausted at 8:30pm tonight is this coffee. my intentions are never to be difficult.

if parking doesn't shy you away, this location is quite quaint. it has enough little tables for seating outside and inside to allow for a great business meeting or a coffee with a friend. staff is pretty friendly and they haven't got my order wrong yet so got to give it four stars for that! good selection of gift ideas too for that coffee or now available tea connoisseurs!!
fabulous service. the lines tend to be lengthy between classes but the staff is always friendly and drinks are made with care. a great addition to washington university's campus!

i have an apartment almost next door, so i thought i'd go down for my birthday to get my free birthday coffee. well, they don't accept starbucks rewards... meaning no birthday coffee for me here. also, the employees were not very nice, definitely not your regular starbucks employee. i turned right around and walked out. go 3 blocks down to the next starbucks, where they accept rewards and the employees are awesome.

on a cold rainy morning, there's nothing like a starbucks right down the block at 6 a.m. to really pick you up. this particular starbucks was one of the best i've visited in a while. not only was the coffee dark roasted and black enough to kill, but the store was very easy to visit and order from, and the orders came out so fast! i went there with two of my friends who were attending an event on sunday morning with me in the city plaza, and the time it took us to arrive, order our drinks, and take off was probably less than 5 minutes. additionally, the service was simply outstanding! the one-woman show barista- and cashier-in-one in this smaller franchised store, inside the harrah's reno, was so nice and pleasant, and had a beautiful smile on her face the whole time we were there. it was just the thing we needed to get our morning off to a great start. overall, a great early morning experience and one i would gladly opt for again.
clean, spacious and plenty of places to sit, either inside or outside. a little further off the main road than most, so it's not at all noisy from traffic.

it's starbucks, you know what you are going to get when you walk in. apparently this is the place to hang in franklin. if you are there long enough you are likely to a celebrity here or there. and if you are in christan music, it appears to be a must that you sit here and converse and write songs. (not that i am complaining, i actually like secretly listing to your conversations) the staff is really nice, and quick at their work. i sort of feel like you have to be promoted to work in this location, you never seem to see people here brand new off the street. they have never yelled at me because my daughter was running around so my hat's off to them.

this would be my favorite starbucks if it wasn't a gazillion miles from home. as i emerged from the wilds of carson city, escaping my own scene from deliverance, i entered this blissful oasis of civilization. light, bright, clean, and comfortable, this is one of the nicest starbucks around. i found a cozy niche, made some calls, enjoyed my latte and yogurt. as i rested, i reveled in the pleasant environment sans awful music and the feeling of being rushed which seems to characterize many starbucks. they are doing it right here. and, the barista even lent me a pen and gave me blank receipt paper when i needed to take a number down during a phone call. that's what i call service!

i just came out with a big smile on my face. the starbucks crew was on their a-game today. here's how it went: i walked in and was immediately greeted by a girl who asked what she could start for me. i told her i'd received an email about half off drinks but that i'd pull up the email so i could verify. she didn't make me go through the hassle. she already knew exactly what i was talking about and even knew the code for the discount! doesn't seem like a huge deal but to me it was. she's the first barista that hasn't made me do the footwork for her as far as what starbucks' deals are. it was such a relief. and it gets better... it only took the other barista three minutes to get my drink in my hand, even with people ahead of me! and my drink was quality, too; done quick and right. congrats, guys! that's how i wish all of my starbucks experiences could be. there must be something magical about this sunday afternoon crew because they really wowed me! keep up the good work, countryside starbucks. i'll see you again soon.

what is happening with this particular starbucks? i have frequented this location for almost 25 years, yet i have found the service to have really declined over the last 6 months. the employees are friendly enough, but my goodness the service is slow. if you have a half hour or more to wait for a cup of coffee then i'm sure this review won't resonate to you, but for those that are pressed for time and on a schedule, i would suggest another starbucks. hopefully they will become a bit more efficient sooner than later.

this starbucks is in a good location. the problem is the slow service! when you walk in, whether there is 1 person ahead of you in line or 10 people, you can expect it will be 15-20 minutes to grab a simple drink. i don't know if it's poor staffing or something else, but it's insane. i used to go to this starbucks every day before work, and each time i was late for work. i finally gave up. there always looks like there's 3-4 people working in the morning but it's still so slow, much slower than any other starbucks. there seems to be a lack of concern for customer's time--no urgency. the staff seems friendly enough to the locals that they know and like...and they ignore the others. the staff usually talks amongst themselves which is frustrating when you're waiting for a drink and employees are babbling away together. it sends and unprofessional vibe. also, they only have one station for cream/sugar and everything is usually empty and messy. and the setup is ridiculous--where people wait for their drinks is also where you try to fix up your drink. it's a madhouse. they need to seriously redesign that aspect. bottom line: for overpriced coffee, try chestnut hill coffeehouse a block further up germantown. it's quicker and the coffee is better.
great service and awesome coffees!! love to share our christmas spirit! nothing better than creme brulée frappuccino!!
if you're going to go to a starbucks in indy, come to this one. great staff, great execution, enjoy.

i had a few minutes before my flight was called to board. it was super cold and super early, which means i needed a good, strong, and hot beverage in my tummy. i walked the long, boring hallway to score a soy latte, which was practically my salvation for this morning. the employee was super nice and took my order promptly. this location wasn't crowded at all, which was perfect for me since i'm a grouch prior to 11am. my hot coffee was ready in about 1 minute. i walked the long and boring hallway back to my terminal with the holy grail in my hand. the soy latte was perfect. hot but not scalding hot, thankfully. i was able to get my caffeine infusion before the flight was called to board. i'm pretty sure the other passengers were quite jealous of my tonic. it may have something to do with my yummy noises i made as i made quick work of my tasty treat.

best service i ever had!!! manager on duty was more than helpful and she was on point!!! i just think that they get such bad reviews is because people are so miserable that they need to be rude and non understanding toward the fact that a lot goes into keep things clean 24/7 not to mention that they are always so busy which to me means that there are way more satisfied customers than the rude miserable people that post bad reviews!!! keep up the great work!!!

this is probably the worst starbucks i've ever been to. it's a shame that this is the one i now live closest to, because i frequent starbucks. but some of the employees here are so rude that it makes me never want to come back. i have been coming to this starbucks fairly regularly for over a year now. orders are frequently forgotten/botched, drinks have been poorly made on more than one occasion (i'm talking the same old crappy ice in an iced coffee refill or very little ice in a venti latte, to the point where you take a swig of warm latte), and some employees are downright rude. if i had experienced this poor service one or two or three or four times over the course of a year then fine; that can happen anywhere. but i'm talking cross your fingers...you don't know what you're going to get when you come here. you get a bad experience about 30% of your visits here. it's important to note that this rudeness does not apply to every employee at this location, however. i have experience the entire staff there and a handful are nice, cheerful people who do not give the customer any attitude. many, though, are the exact opposite. unacceptable.
so, i was pushed to pay for my coffee by the cashier before i was able to order food and then the line was 20 people long. while i loved my coffee it seems like she could have switched registers?
i love this starbucks! it's got everything - nice barristas, beautiful atmosphere. i come here almost every day and it's always a pleasant experience.

this starbucks is always busy. same people out front with their pets ... a lot of people on laptops ... ultra thin starbucks very little standing room. not much of a place to go for the ambiance, the employees and location makes up for the lack of interior flare. i do like when i complained about some food they gave me another sandwich on the house. amazing customer service.
it's the closest starbuck's to my office, so i keep going back. they don't have the quickest service at this location. the staff is either very friendly or just kind of bla.

seriously the best service i've ever had at starbucks. they made my drink incorrectly by mistake, then corrected it (larger size) and gave me a free drink for the future. thanks for a great morning! :)

so they have 3 people working. only one car in the drive through but i decided to go inside to use the restroom while i waited on my coffee. first their restrooms are closed. second no one said hi or even acknowledged me when i walked in. then the two guys stood there with their backs to me talking while girl was on the other side with her back to me. it literally took 6 minutes for them to even notice i was there. worst starbucks ever

i got in an out of this starbucks so quick! this location is at the omni, so i think a lot of people forget about this starbucks. parking can be such a pain to run in and out of any coffee shop downtown. i shouldn't admit this, but i did park in the no parking/valet section, which helped make this and easy in and out trip. there were a couple guys ordering together in front of me and one lady working behind the counter. once the guys moved out of the way, the lady behind the counter looked at me, but didn't acknowledge by saying hi or i'll be with you minute (why it got 4 stars). she finally acknowledged me and became super talkative when another lady came out from the back. the lady who came out from the back is the one who made my drink (chai). she was so nice and friendly! my drink also tasted great. i can't tell you how many times i go to starbucks and the chai has no flavor and/or is watered down (even though i order it without water). but props to this lady for doing it right!
very quick & attentive dylan was very nice & helped me pick out what drink to order! 10/10 recommend
it's a starbucks in kop what do you expect ... i like the one on gateway shopping center but that's me. love the mobile app,
worse starbucks in the area... they make every excuse to the customer no matter how reasonable the solution is, to do there actual job...

i'm not a coffee drinker but i do love the açaí refreshers on the menu! starbucks never fails me with their customer service and the lines weren't overly ridiculous. the menu is never ending so there is something for everyone to try. the teas are pretty amazing and so are the food options although they are limited. most locations are quiet enough to get a good study group going or small meeting

best baristas in town. they can make whatever you want however you want it. they also make wonderful suggestions if you're looking to try something new. never had an issue with an order. when going through the drive thru the line always moves fast. the team is so pleasant and welcoming. it's definitely my favorite starbucks by far, and i've visited many up and down the east coast!

this starbucks is in a perfect location, with a beautiful building, in a great shopping centre. but unfortunately the coffee giant that i love was unable to capitalize on it's opportunity. the biggest complaint about this store that i have is the lack of comfortable seating. all chairs except for 3 bench seats in the room are uncomfortable metal chairs that don't invite one to stay for more than 10 minutes. so if one is looking for a relaxing atmosphere to relax with a cup of coffee for an hour or two, i would avoid this store. this location has the normal starbucks quality coffee, with some college students (most likely from st joes) running the store. some days they are pleasant, other days they are rude and bitter. i wish this starbucks was better, because it could be so nice and perfect. but i don't have any desires to return to this location unless its just to pick up a cheap cup of joe after chipoltle.

i absolutely adore justin and abby at this location. they are so sweet and kind to everyone every single day, and constantly portray an uplifting attitude. i look forward to seeing them each and every time i come to this store.
i wish i could give this place 0 stars. terrible staff. beyond rude. long wait times.
a bunch of rude college kids rushing your orders. they often get our order wrong. we stopped going there. i'm not supporting any business that won't respect paying customers .
this starbucks has a lot of indoor and outdoor seating which is nice. the staff was friendly and since it is in the lobby of the lovely omni hotel, the environment is lovely.

once again there is a massive line at an airport starbucks. these should always have at least two registers open, plus baristas should be taking drink orders while people wait to pay. why is this so hard?

always super sweet and actually makes drinks in reusable cup and doesn't make it in plastic then dumps it into reusable one also never had a drink made incorrectly, but a great place to spend your time studying or working!!!

this starbucks is very slow. there were 4 people working behind the counter, yet my drink took 15 minutes to make and it wasn't even busy in there. then, when my drink finally was made, it was the wrong drink. i ordered a hot drink and received and iced drink. i'll stick with other locations from now on. they're more efficient! (maryland farms location is always so busy, yet so quick!)
love starbucks but the egyptian taking all over the place and the fight in it wallah... i come here everyday because i love seeing them fighting lol

very good drinks. one problem why they lost so many stars is because the guy behind me got his drink before me. the store wasn't even busy since we were the only 2 ordering. i don't care how nice u r but if u skip me when we both have the same order, no tip and minus stars for u. the price of the drinks r more expensive then the starbucks in vegas. i paid only $4.86 for a grande frap in vegas and $5.04 here. the tax here is lower than vegas too. what a rip off.

i never complaint on starbucks..but this time i have to! mobile ordered: exactly 18 mins till i get my drink when i order 5 mins down the road! i understand theres situation where everyone ordered at the same time...but c'mon, 18 mins for a coffee drink,, i really dont have time for it! whats the point of mobile order anyway...really have to rethink about going to starbucks every single time now. and on top of that..my drink was wrong...didnt follow my order!! ask for no ice, when i get ice..before that i ask gently how long or how many drink are ahead of me when i said i have been waiting 15 mins or more? maybe i could come back bc i have an urgent place i need to be..the barista guy smile and said with a tone "you are not the only one waiting, but the whole franklin town is waiting this morning!" wth...i am still shaking my head with the whole starbucks experience this morning!
love starbucks! always quick when going through drive thru. tried the mini fraps. a little pricey than i was expecting. nice friendly staff.

i just can't say enough wonderful things about this starbucks location and the incredible staff!!! so many times i pull up to the drive thru and all i have to say is good morning and i am almost immediately recognized ..greeted by name and my usual order appears on the screen! when i walk into the establishment i feel like norm on cheers... because 3-5 people shout out my name when i walk in! i feel special there to say the least!!!!! even when they are swamped they move quickly and are so friendly and professional!!!! love my peeps at speedway starbucks!!!!!!!

hey it's a starbucks. hard to tell them apart except for the staff. they do a rotation between newtown and heacock road. starbucks embodies the ultimate mcdonaldlization of society. regardless of your staff you can always for the most part get what you want and it taste fairly similar. i.e. friers on the right levers on the left. so what i'm saying for an organization that is not franchised, who really runs them. typically it's going to be your students and possibly mid level managers. customer service is paramount. i think the idea for many customers is they like to build relationships and see that familiar face at each location consistently of the staff. i've noticed that both customers and employees seem to be discouraged when they are store hopping. it feels good when you walk in welcomed by your first name and they know what your order is. i think that a starbucks cared they would stop the movement and allow those relationships to form. their sales would increase their ability to upsell would increase as well. it's all about relationships folks. i give three stars not because of the staff because of the executive management that does not value this type of relationship. i believe training is the underlining cause. which probably breeds inefficiencies if someone does not want to move as the incentive is to excel to run the store. but this is only for the short term as starbucks has a high attrition rate as this is only a stepping stone.

i've been a loyal customer of starbucks since 2005 and have spent many a pleasant hour there, enjoying a latte. (that's all i drink, because i'm not a fan of their brewed coffees.) several years ago, they started charging for milk alternatives. today, my grande iced soy latte rang in at a whopping $5.37. insane. this location is a mess. the bathrooms are filthy, the trash is always spilling over, and it takes up to 15 minutes to receive your order. the baristas never remember my name, and screw up my order (which never changes) about every third time. oro valley is a bit of a wasteland coffee-wise--maybe that's how they stay in business.
purchased bantam bagels and it was still frozen, but was able to come back the next day and get a new order for free. customer service was great.

this new location is great! very roomy, friendly staff, and always smiling. it's not on the best intersection, but so much better than the speedway location up the street. way too cluttered up there. this has a nice, new, modern vibe. check it out!

5 stars for good customer service and a consistently good product, yet the downside to this location is its drive-thru. one of the absolutely worst that i've ever seen. it is (in my donald trump voice) awfuuuuuuul!!!! if you park next to the building, you are so screwed. get ready to be blocked in for several minutes at a time while calling your boss to tell them that you are running late. the drive-thru has a very unique shape to it, in that it takes up nearly three quarters of the building upon entering from the main road. in addition, the drive-thru backs up so badly that you are better off walking in and making your order. it seems as if there are three baristas waiting to assist you within the drive-thru alone. the drive-thru obviously isn't the fault of the starbucks employees, but it kind of sucks from a customer point of view. its probably best to do what i did while on my second visit: park 15-20 parking rows away from the building so that you can walk inside of the store to purchase your yum yums. whoever designed this placed needs to be karate chopped in the neck. the customers + starbucks employees deserve better than this type of dubious store layout.

for goodness sake! when you have eight orders stacked up, and another eight customers waiting in line to order, please consider cutting out the chit chatting with your buddies, and focus on actually doing your job. employees who are off-duty and visiting... please stop the socializing when your friends are trying to complete orders while customers are standing there waiting, and watching you all banter around. socializing and friendly chatter are great, but, please know when to cut it out.

usually pretty fast and i have never had any problems here. 4 stars for a starbucks doesn't ever make much sense, but this is the most convenient drive through when i find myself driving a lot during the work day. that means i end up coming through this drive through pretty regularly. with that regularity, you would expect at least one issue. never ever! mcdonalds next door is constantly slow, but these workers must actually be drinking that coffee they are brewing, and even if the mcdonald's workers are, that stuff just is not of the same quality, i don't are what the commercials say. the staff is fast and friendly. whether it is a white chocolate mocha frappe with extra shots of espresso or just a regular coffee with cream and some espresso shots, it will do the trick and it never takes long.
starbucks south meadows gets an a. the staff is friendly. they have a nice drive thru and are open late. love the chai cream frappuccino. liquid heaven.

quite possibly the most poorly run starbucks in the nola metro area. not only is it nearly impossible to get into their parking lot and/or find a spot, but once you do, the staff will make make you feel like they are doing you a favor by taking your order.
starbucks always has good customer service! the best variety menu! comfortable and never disappoints#

i'm extremely happy that this has opened in my neighborhood. the place is well laid out with a decent amount of sitting room. it has a community table that's perfect for bigger groups. there is an outdoor area to catch some rays as well the staff here are great and are quick in getting drinks finished. even thought the wifi can be a bit of a challenge sometimes, this is one of my regular hangouts.

ugh. this starbucks is a mess in a lot of ways. the parking lot was so poorly designed, it already creates a plethora of issues for this franchise. the workers work at their own pace. they don't care what the customers think, they just work on their own clock. they are accurate with the order, but how hard is it to take a coffee order? not hard. if you're brand loyal it doesn't matter but if you just like a good cup go to the buddy's brew down the street. always a zoo here.

this starbucks has a great location, right by jefferson and near rothman and in the washington square area. i've often stopped by when i've been at a medical appointment in the area or at the jefferson station which is also close by. they move the line somewhat quickly here, usually are pretty well staffed with baristas, so they're pretty efficient here. the baristas seem nice here and helpful, i don't go here as much as other starbucks near me but i'm glad this starbucks is in the area! good starbucks! i think the seating area could be kept a little cleaner but again great location and friendly staff.

this is one of my favorite starbucks. it is a convenient location. lots of businesses we use are close by it. the one down side about the location is that it's busy so it is sometimes hard to park. the drive thru is accessed between buildings and could be easily missed. the workers, however, are always friendly and helpful. they are quick. they are always efficient. the store is near and clean. i stopped on christmas eve while out running a last minute errand. i was nervous about stopping on a holiday like that. the drive thru moved quick and the workers were more than friendly and made my christmas eve great!
it's ok...the staff here are always nice. sometimes the service is really slow though :/

okay so let me preface this with a positive. mino is an amazing baristo and kind human and he is the reason i come back to this location! something about his demeanor and personality brightens my day! besides that, this location is constantly disappointing me! there's been two instances where i've gotten such poor treatment, granted, it's while they're super busy, but i too am in a service job where i get slammed and am super compassionate and professional with my guests. today, i ordered ahead on the app. when i came to pick up, the woman that handed me drink was super dismissive and mean. i told her i was waiting for another drink as well and she said "i know" and walked away. while waiting, i realized my drink was made wrong and i asked if she could remake it, but the lid wasn't on all the way and the drink fell. she was furious and looked at me like i was an imbecile and i got really embarrassed and just walked out, my sister was in the car and knows i have really bad social anxiety and the drinks were already paid for. so she went in and got the drinks for me. this barista in particular really went out her way to make me uncomfortable and it really ruined my mood and stressed me out. probably will visit during minos shifts and on a slower day but really sad about the service and people they chose to have on their team. i wish a manager would talk to the employees about how they treat guests.

starbucks is starbucks. i always go there when i am out of the country. i am addicted to their frappuccino with espresso shot. this place has really nice staff! free wifi always at starbucks! accepts amex.

this is the most horrible place ever you want to start your morning with . i had the most impatient cashier when i came in .she rushed to get my order even there was no one else in line - and of course - messed up my drink . not just that - i handed her $20 , she took my money , turned around and came back ask me to pay again . i gave her my credit card (it's early in the morning and i'm definitely not a morning bird - can't focus with out my coffee ) - she made the payment with my card . when i asked her about the $20 she clearly yelled at me : " so , you just want your $20 back ?! " i left the store , and i called to talk to the manager . there was the lady told me she was the manager - i told her what going on - by the end of my story , she asked me :" so ...., are you still want to speak to the manager , right ?! " never again !

consistently i was getting a coffee cup that was filled up only three quarters of the way. mind you, i never asked for any room for cream. today i finally sucked it up and asked her to fill it up all the way. i was given the silent treatment. baristas face said it all. sorry, if i'm paying 2$ for a coffee i would like a full cup.

drinks are tasty. staff is hit and miss. most of the time very friendly and helpful. one or two every so often that just make you cringe. not as clean as it should be. always seems busy.(but not a terribly long wait) i'll keep going here as the drinks are good and the experience is usually good!

consistently great starbucks location. even though there's a good amount of turnover, the baristas are always top notch. service is routinely fast and friendly. the thing i like most about this branch is that they take the time to make my drink properly. i often order long shots, but many locations don't run the shots that way, despite my instructions. i'd say i only get long shots about 10% of the time. anyway, here they always let the shots rip for the full amount of time and you can absolutely taste the difference. good job for never cutting corners on my drink. five solid stars from me. i really hope we can move past the pandemic and get back to sitting and drinking our coffee in the shop. it's really a great place to do that.
computer issues, slowed drive thru to a stop. this was at the sheldon store...

this is one of my top starbucks in boise. sure, the parking situation is a nightmare, but the location of this place is convenient and the inside is spacious. it seems almost like it is an old converted burger king with the indoor patio seating that the old locations used to have. it has more than enough tables and chairs and there is separation so that it doesn't get too loud. the outside area is non-smoking... but then again, all of boise pretty much is. it is shaded though and away from the main street traffic, which is bueno. i love the location, the service, and the layout. can't beat that :)
i'd take away a star for the terribly planned parking lot and drive-thru, but it's got plenty of seating and a very friendly staff. definitely one of the more enjoyable locations around.
when you have your fave stick to it i enjoy my venti sugar free vanilla skinny latte and the consistency keeps me coming back! if anyone loves me they know to bless me with starbucks gift cards

this is a starbucks. i am a huge starbucks fan, don't get me wrong. i absolutely love a venti bold brew, black, and their coffee is deep and rich and just like my father forced me to drink (none of that "fru fru stuff"). the thing is, being a frequent bucks-er, there's a few things you come to expect and this location misses the beat on one key aspect. 1) get me my coffee so i can get the f*ck out and on with my day. i have never not seen a snaking line at this place. it is, without a doubt, the slowest starbucks i have ever been to and i grew up in the southwest. good god people, you know your address is philadelphia, right? we are city people who walk at a city's pace who want our coffee at a city's speed. if i wanted my cashier to nurture me and call me "baby" i would drive my way out to the 'burbs. if you're looking to grab a quick cup of coffee on your way to work, find somewhere else, because this place will undoubtedly ensure that you arrive late to work like a jackass, too embarrassed to bring your starbucks cup in with you... i will say, parking is great, location is nice and the interior bodes well to coffee enjoyment. but it frustrates the living hell out of me.

this is my favorite starbucks in town. it's quiet and not too busy. drinks are made to perfection. the baristas are nice and patient. really enjoy being able to get work done here. there are plenty of tables and ample space to spread out. the outdoor area has music but it is on really loud. pets are welcome on the outdoor patio area.

had my 8-shot espresso, some vanilla bean frappacinno (plus a hair caught on the lid halfway inside the drink). i love starbucks and this one had great service. however, the hair killed all the other upsides of this place. c'mon kids. you can have all the friendly and prompt service and a great product but putting a hair in the product you serve is poor service.

on one hand, it's pretty nice to be able to get starbucks at such a central spot on campus. i'm fairly new to the school and the area so there aren't a lot of places that i know how to get to fairly easily to grab a drink or something quick to eat. starbucks fits that bill. i'm not ashamed to say that i love starbucks. i like that i get consistently well made drinks, rewards for drink purchases, and something familiar pretty much anywhere i go. i do have to say that their prices do strike on the high side of things. fine. this isn't a surprise. the surprise was that because this store is run by aramark to some degree (my receipt said aramark, not starbucks) they have jacked the prices up even if further!! if you can believe it! i have a go-to summer drink -- venti, unsweetened, half green, half passion iced tea, no water. they were able to make that for me no problem, but whereas i usually spend $2.81 at at normal starbucks, this store charged $3.95. that's crazytown! i can't say i won't be back, but i'll be purchasing my drinks off campus whenever i can help it.

man, i stop in this starbucks by necessity and lack of other options, but it's just never convenient. the parking sucks, and the baristas are slower than most starbucks. i like the space once i'm inside and have my drink, but overall this is not my favorite starbucks in any way.

20 minutes after i placed my order, i brought to the cashier's attention that i had received nothing. he was very apologetic and gave me a full refund and an upsize on my drink, but that does not excuse this level of service at these prices. however, it does bump their rating from 1 star to 2.

1st and foremost this is one of the most beautiful starbucks, the ceiling is out of this world! we had received $5 coupons when we had checked into silver legacy, so our drinks were basically complementary. the line is always long, but the baristas make it go as fast as they can with out messing up an order. i wish this was my everyday starbucks !

drink was good, but pretty sure i ate uncooked bacon with my bacon and gruyere egg bite :( the cashier told me to wait because he wasn't sure they even had it, then waited 10 minutes (while being on my lunch break) disappoinyin

what a rude barista. my order was messed up & after explaining to the barista, she (mid aged caucasian female) was very upset that cashier hadn't ring up my order appropriately. it was a very simple order. she looked at me such a stranger way like i had done something really screwed up. i just flew 8 hours & this was my first stop in indianapolis downtown, was hoping for a little bit warmer & welcoming response in the cold afternoon:(

just opened thursday, feb 11, 2016. it's already constantly packed solid. being next to villanova university, i'm sure they knew business would be good for them. my biggest gripe is there is no drive thru but aside from that, i'm very happy how centrally located it is. it's close to my house and near lots of things but also there is ample parking. i've never seen so many parking spaces around a starbucks before. getting in to starbucks is easy enough. but this is a very fast-paced starbucks and not relaxing. it's crammed tight with college students, local middle school and high school kids and people on their laptops. the seating isn't comfortable. mostly tables and no comfy chairs. also, there is no noise control. it's very loud inside. this isn't the best starbucks i've been to but i think it fits the area and it's good for what it is. i will be back for sure.

maple is better than magazine. metarie is better than maple. that being said, maple is the location i visit most often as it is closest to my home. i have been visiting this starbucks more so in the past few months. i am tired of paying for just ok coffee at the local chains- pj's, cc's, and on occasion rue and zots. i don't include village coffee, because i only go there for 1 thing. but bad cup after bad cup being thrown in the streets of new orleans by this coffee loving woman, i could be polluting the lake by now with crappy cups of coffee. not so with starbucks. this location is a 4. they, for a long time, would "fix" coffee made by lesser starbucks, (i'm looking at you magazine street). they really have their act together. i actually "crave" their coffees. i also like the treat receipt program which gets you a two dollar medium(there, i said it), after 2 pm that same day. i also use their card, belong in their club, and buy gift certificates on line cheap when i can. there are ways to make this starbucks habit affordable. the one evening they were closed at 9 pm, and needed a fix, i thought about my options in the area, drove down to bk and got a seattle's best. (which was better than some other local places). there are those who will disagree with me. i am ready for that. i was also ready to stop wasting my money at places just because they were local, and not enjoying their products. it all makes perfect sense, it just took a long time.

this is your average starbucks. drinks are good, staff is warm and welcoming. but it's full all the time and there is nowhere to sit. and it's difficult to find a place to plug in your laptop if you want to use the restroom, you need a code to get in. (maybe the homeless people use it?) so busy it's annoying. and you have to pay to park. no drive thru but it does have a walk up window. if you want to get your coffee and go while perusing mass ave, it's perfect. if you want a coffee house experience to hang with friends and talk, or somewhere to get a little work done, this probably isn't your place.

i've been here on multiple different occasions. it's a great location- right by the bank and potbellys and across the street from the wynnewood shopping center. this is a great study spot too because it's a larger starbucks with large tables and chairs. it can really be a hit or miss though because it gets busy and filled pretty quickly in the evenings with students. the service and quality has always been really good so far. stop by and give this place a try!
have only been to drive-thru. great service and there is never a wait here! prices are high but that's to be expected given the company. very friendly and professional staff.. i will come back.
its a starbucks. you know you will get good service and drinks. i had the green iced tea. yes it was good stuff.

this is another good addition to a neighborhood making a comeback. not much point reviewing the coffee here, it's starbucks, you kind of know what you're getting there. the service here has been hit or miss for me since i've visited this location. this one young guy that works the window just is not clicking on all cylinders and has caused some annoyance with his slow speed and unprofessionalism. otherwise it's a good location.

a starbucks is a starbucks, but this is my local one so i wanted to commend them. the standard delicious drinks: finally got that pumpkin spice latte this basic bitch had been pining over. comfy seating where my sister and i lounged for a very long time while no one seemed to care. also "writer working on screenplay/great american novel" desk seating. i can attest their frappuccinos are also sugar-y fat-y delicious. service is quick and gracious. all in all a good go to local coffee place.
got a hot chocolate from there. pretty good stuff. it was a bit crowded, but customer service was pleasant.

this starbucks is painfully slow. i am here at 3:30 on a saturday... and the two people working here are possibly the slowest, most non-urgent people ever. which is fine, but not what i would consider ideal if you're a starbucks barista.... i wouldn't be surprised if this location rarely meets their sales goals... within the 20 mins i've been waiting for my small americano, 3 people have asked for refunds. sos. did i mention the slow barista checked her phone while 10 people are staring at her while we all are waiting for our drinks...

i regret it every time i come to this starbucks. the employees seem to move very slow and appear poorly managed. i also see different people every time, which makes me think people don't want to work here. today i had a mobile order that took 18 minutes to come out. their napkins were completely empty, the trash was overflowing, and the bar was filthy. they also altered multiple customer's orders because they were out of iced coffee. i don't understand how i have such terrible experiences at this location when there are so many employees behind the counter. i love starbucks and will continue going, but it will not be at this location.

this is the starbucks at canal place. it's a lil' location with a few seats actually inside. there are 6 tables on the floor in the middle of the mall to sit at to drink your coffee and/or eat your food. this starbucks closes for business at 2000 hours (8:00pm). harrah's casino is directly across canal and there's another in there open until midnight but it is a franchise. this one the staff is great and they do get you in and out quickly. the starbucks at 700 canal is much bigger and fortunate for them but not for me busier than the other ones. anyway this is starbucks. not much to explain. i do love this location when going to: the theatres at canal place. this is an excellent location.

good location but serious parking issues. not sure why the build these freestanding buildings with so few parking spots! friendly barista and staff, even remade my drink without me asking after he overfilled the cup! i've seen other locations just pour off the over pour. great decor and excellent selection of snack items.

i stopped into this starbucks near closing time, and was told that they were "out of coffee beverages". i ordered a frappuccino so i can only assume that they meant that and not that they were actually out of coffee. it was close to closing time, and its possible that the staff just didn't want to bother, but i had to drive to mt juliet and a frappuccino would have really helped.
coffee is good as expected. one problem though the drive thru is atrocious! every time i come i sit in this god forsaken line for 30 mins. i think i have a problem.

another starbucks. typical drinks, typical snacks, typical decoration. so what makes this one different from the rest? i can point out 2 pros and 1 con: pro #1: location. this is located on a busy corner of one of the busiest foot traffic streets in edmonton (whyte ave; also known as 82nd ave). so the location makes this starbucks a great place to meet up with friends while enjoying an afternoon out and about. another great aspect about the location is the ability to people watch. whether you sit inside, or at a table on the sidewalk, this is a great location to soak in your surroundings. pro #2: i like the bar space set up inside. this is something i haven't seen at any other starbucks. just to the left of the barista brewing centre there is a bar with about 4 stools set up. it is fun to watch the barista's work behind the counter and i love the space available. the round tables at typical starbucks' are quite small if you are going there to do work but this particular bar is very wide which is ideal to spread out books and papers. con #1: i guess this is related to the first pro. the fact that it is located on a busy street means lots of street noise from vehicles driving by and also can make it difficult to find parking. hopefully this encourages people to take public transit but i know that option is not always feasible. overall i do really like this starbucks, especially in the summer while the city is abuzz with all of the festivals taking place.
clean, modern, friendly service. this is the best starbucks location in philadelphia.

it's 10:30 in the morning and the place looks like a tornado went through it...all the tables are dirty, myself and the person at the table next to me had to wipe them off, the bathroom floor is sticky with black footprints all over it, the floor inside the lobby has food and garbage every where. by no means is it so busy that someone could not come out and do some cleaning. starbucks should be ashamed.

at almost 80 years old, my beloved mother had never been in a starbucks and she wanted to try it. we went inside to order so as not to slow down the drive thru. the person who took our order was a woman with short grey hair under her visor and the name tag "jaye." she was impatient when we could not hear her over the ambient noise and through the protective plastic. when my mom struggled to say the order loud enough, jaye actually rolled her eyes at an elderly, first time guest. i stepped in and took over the order. drinks were made properly and were quick, hence the 2 stars. but, the disrespect is unforgivable.

we stopped by here quickly before a dinner reservation, he needed coffee for the ride home and i wanted to try the new crystal ball frappuccino. it was easter but the employees were very friendly. i asked for mine with soy milk and i noticed the employee making it with regular, so i asked for soy again and she asked me if i needed it in a new cup or not (ie, was i very lactose-intolerant or not). as for the drink, it was just okay ... the peach whipped cream was very good though. very tiny location with only one bathroom, otherwise this would get 5 stars from me because of the customer service.
literally the worst drive through ever, it takes almost an hour at times to get through and has to be the slowest starbucks in all of tucson

usually all is well here at this store, and i usually frequent it everyday on my way home. today was different. i stopped in around 6pm and i guess there was training going on where the store cleanliness was neglected, even though there was very little traffic (since the drive thru was closed too). i got my coffee then went for a refill, where i was told that coffee doesn't get refills. this is the first time i've heard this "company policy" since it could "give me a heart attack." the barista really didn't care that everyone else but her has done refills and have me a similar drink without asking if that's what i wanted. it not like i'll never come back, but i'll avoid that barista for sure. so much for the third place.

terrible service and no apology or accommodation for making my $5 coffee wrong- cold instead of hot. instead of remaking it it tasted like they just poured it into another cup and added hot coffee on top of it. mind you it was a latte though. absolutely disappointing, terrible coffee and terrible service. gave them one more chance after this visit just because of their proximity to where i was going, and needless to say worse than the first time and i will never be going back. this place is clearly poorly managed compared to many others in to.

came to starbucks to grab a quick drink but no one seemed to be manning the drive through. i was throughly disappointed and did not get coffee. then unfortunately, i got so tired from lack of coffee that i crashed my car. thanks starbucks.

it's starbucks - it should be the same around the world. however, this is the most sluggishly slow staff i think i've ever seen! the lady at the till took my order wrong and after giving me my total had to correct it based on the wrong size. the lady in front of me is waiting for her drink to be remade as she ordered - decaf - and i still stand hoping the four baristas will get their act together so that i can get on with my saturday! i'll drive to another location next time.

my second visit and the second time i have been delighted with my service!! i love the space in this starbucks! lots of tables and lots of space to have a private conversation. i'm grateful i live near by as this is quickly becoming my favorite location wow i'm surprised at the service deterioration. i arrived at 5 and two people were behind the counter. no one said a word to me. one person was helping outside patrons and the other mopping the floor. i finally asked the person mopping if anyone was servicing inside patrons and she said yeah we'll be with you shortly and continued to mop. finally a third person came from the back and helped me. i frequently go to this location and i'm sad to get lower priority than a mop.

starbucks opened today. i do not like the location but i do like the inside and the patio. i just hate that it is so close to local coffee shops and also it is right on the main road. however, it is only a block from my house so... looks like yet another coffee shop where i will be the duke! *nothing special about this starbucks other than it is new. otherwise it is the same as others.

one of the best starbucks experiences that i have had! i had a free drink gifted to me that i needed to use and decided to stop in to claim it. this starbucks was slammed for some reason at 3pm and there were only two employees running the place. when i walked through the door, i was immediately greeted with a cheery welcome. the two employees were frantically making drinks and running around the counter to hand deliver them all while constantly updating the people standing in line that they had just one more drink to make before we could be helped. i wish i caught the guys name because he was so incredibly fantastic at his job!! i ended up getting an iced caramel macchiato (my go to) and it was delish as always!

over the last several years i have frequented this location because i have family and friends in the area. i am boring, i just get a vanilla bean frappuccino or a hot chocolate with whipped cream. i too am challenges with the beverage size names, and experienced one of the baristas, melissa, who has waited on my many times before, said we also speak english you can use small, medium, large. i thought this was funny because as a non-coffee drinker i am not ordering and using the purpose behind the names and descriptions of the beverages. because to me a grande should be larger than a venti. so, i stick to small, medium and large. ha ha! i have been in the store and in the drive thru, and have always had a very good experience and they have always been helpful. melissa works hard. i have seen her be at the front and at the drive thru at the same time. and she remembers me when i come in to order and i am not there on a daily basis. it is more every few months. so kudos!
twice going here for iced coffee it is either to sweet or not enough. it doesn't come out the same each time this pisses me off my coffee is as sweet as a soda this seams like it was on purpose

not one of my fave starbucks locations. service was slow and i saw a few people doing very little when the inside line up was packed. they need to learn to manage their time better by more useful. long line up inside plus no lineup on drive thru equals go help the inside lineup!!!

nice staff, lots of outside seating on the sidewalk, and inside. i live close, but i am happy to have a neighborhood sbux. would be better if it had a drive thru, but it isn't set up that way, so i forgive them!

8/10 times they are out of the food items i order on the mobile app--- so annoying! they offer a refund but when you're doing a quick pickup it makes the whole thing take 3 times as long and annoys people in line, so easy if they would just update the inventory regularly!

i love almost everything about this starbucks. the layout and space available is conducive to studying and/or meeting with others. service and employees are always delightful. there is ample parking. there is really one sole reason for this 2-star review, their wifi connection is always horrible!!!!! my music stream is constantly cutoff and my i internet connection is lost. every other time i conduct an internet search i get an error message that my connection is lost. it is incredibly frustrating and every time i return to this location i hope it's fixed, but i'm batting 100% here - 5/5 times i've come, this has been the experience.

the clay terrace starbucks is a good representation of the brand, although it is a little small. the service is good and the drinks well prepared. a great stop if you are already here shopping, but not worth a special trip.

worst starbucks i have ever been to!!! please do not go here if you want your coffee order done right. my order was messed up about four times and the cake pop i got was old. the barista was so rude about remaking the drinks, and i get it they were busy, but she felt the need to stand there and argue with me about whether she called out the name of whose drink was whose when she set them out. these workers do not care about their customers. honestly i just should've gotten my money back instead of even getting my drink remade for all the trouble i went through. i usually don't write reviews unless a place is really bad, so trust me on this and do not go there.

i'm happy that there is finally a drive thru starbucks. great location and close to where i live which is awesome. i love the open space inside. there is a mix of high top tables with low tables and sofa seats to sit on. i like how everything is spread out including the food items. everything is not jammed packed together. this location does need to hire more people. they need a person who handles customers ordering inside and one designated 1-2 people handling just drive thru. when i go though the drive thru it's not much of a wait but when i came inside there was a very long wait because the barista was handling inside and drive thru customers. drive thru order where being served a lot faster then the customers inside waiting for their drinks/food. they were out of a ton of stuff because no one has yet to go through the boxes and open them to restock items. i like this starbucks but they really need to hire additional staff especially because they have a drive thru. being that this is a drive thru location they can expect more customers because of the convenience of not having to get out of the car. you need the staff to make sure both customers inside and outside are getting their order efficiently and timely.

slowest starbucks i've ever been to. 20 min minimum for drive through. they need to get someone that knows what they're doing inside and that cares about making the drinks fast, the girls working in the am really do have the " don't care attitude "
beautiful location, sweet employees & quality service! we almost didn't go because of the negative reviews and our expectations were beyond exceeded!

3.5 stars. it's a no frills starbucks. nothing great, nothing bad. busy little location, 2 registers chugalugging, long line, and the highlight, a loft with seating. when in the area, and you must get a fix, this works.
nice little satrbucks in carpinteria. the staff is friendly and they make it a point to remember your name and what you ususally drink
one of the bad apple in starbucks family. very dysfunctional store. multiple mistakes taking orders and long wait time for not particularly many customers. a better training is necessary.

looks renovated quick service i wish i took a picture of my frappachino before i devoured it because it literally looked picture perfect. not only that, but it was perfectly made. nice job starbucks. its rare to find a good one that actually cares about the quality of drinks being served.
clean shop. orders taken quickly and correctly. coffee arrived hot and fast and perfectly made.

horrible customer service. the morning staff at this store is extremely rude. always messes up my order. this starbucks is the closest to my home and now i have to drive further out to a different starbucks to get my drink right the first time and order taken by a decent human being. i refuse to ever give this store business again. do your self a favor and drive those extra miles to a different store you won't regret it.

i used to luv having a coffee in here as i live in the neighborhood but lately it's occupied by homeless people, they literally sit there all day with all their shit around, looks like their base. the management doesn't do anything about it which is a pity because they are losing their regular customers..

this starbucks used to be 5 stars now it really is zero! not sure what changed in the last year but there is no sense of efficiency and they always lose mobile orders or don't receive them? management needs to step in and make some changes.
