whoa! definitely my favorite starbucks. i drive out of my way just to see mckenzie. she always greets me with a smile and most of the time remembers my name and what i like to order.

it's no mystery that starbucks is by far my favorite stop for coffee and other selections that are spontaneous dictated by my cravings at any given moment. i have visited literally hundreds of starbucks' location since they've come into existence and although they all seem to be pretty consistence with the quality of the products they offer; the service, quality of the product, wait-time, cleanliness and accessibility of the location are the five points i will rate all of them on going forward - so to make the point i've recently incorporated a rating system based on this short list to calculate the rating of the locations i visit. i feel all these criterions are controllable by the corporation, the franchise owner or store manager. service - 3 stars quality - 4 stars wait time - 2 stars cleanliness - 4 stars accessibility - 2 stars rating - 3.0 stars

this message is for the management of this location. please, please!! retrain your baristas and take the time actually to try the coffees being served out at this location! you need to be in the customer's shoes, so you know what we are experiencing. i have been coming here since opening, giving chance after chance. i don't know what exactly has gone wrong with this newer starbucks. but every single time i go, there is something wrong with my drink. may i add, i don't order anything complicated. my craziest drink was a simple soy latte, for example. i just went through this drive-through today, and my drink tastes like diluted, watered-down coffee and a splash of soy milk. not worth $6. i've been ordering the same drink for years at multiple locations throughout the usa. but this location can't make the drinks right.

this starbucks is fine. it is, however, small, and it gets very busy at certain times of day, with a lot of people picking up coffee on their way onto the blue route or the turnpike. during rushes, the employees crank through orders more quickly than at a lot of other starbucks, but you can still have a long wait in cramped environs. also, the parking lot is small and getting out of it can get complicated. the product is the starbucks usual and the employees are good, but it's not the fastest or easiest place to stop at busy times and the seating is limited so it's not the best starbucks if you're looking for a place to have a conversation or to sit back and sip your coffee.

a very busy starbucks joint. they seem to get new staff every couple of weeks. they need to do some hiring due to the slow service and amount of customers they receive. place is usually always clean when i walk in. need to start getting the blueberry oatmeal correct. i've left before with a bag with just plain oatmeal when i ordered the blueberry oatmeal. also need to check on blueberry status/expiration date because i received half molded blueberries with my oatmeal.
this starbucks starts sweeping and taking all the food away an hour before closing. i don't like this. why come if they are closing at 10pm
these baristas/managers deserve a raise! they are on their sh*t!! service and drinks are consistent every time!
terrible service. 15 minute wait after placing an order. plenty of people to help, but they were sweeping the floor at 9am.

do i have to give stars at all? this has got to be the worst and slowest starbucks. i used mobile order at 7:15 pm. 15mins later my order was ready. had i known drive thru was gonna be faster i would have done that. it wasn't even that busy. everyone in here was doing everything but making drinks. i'll stick to the starbucks on vets and kingman.
their staff is super sweet and easy to talk to and make drinks within a reasonable time frame. i enjoy this starbucks atmosphere compared to others!

stopped in for some passion tango tea. this is a newer location and a rather larger starbucks however the parking can be limited and challenging. drive through today was lined out onto the side road. no inside seating is available due to covid however they do have some tables outdoors under their shaded area available for sitting and enjoying your beverage and food. masks required and the inside of the store is opened for placing your order. they do have order online and pickup available and special parking available for you if you opt for this. keep in mind that you have to either go in or drive through to pickup your online order. staff is friendly and the drinks tasted great.

this is a great starbucks to come to. i love spending a couple of hours working on my school/personal work. the team members are always smiling and they get my order right. definitely recommend this place.

one star because the parking lot here is terrible. it's that bad. traffic from the drive-thru line backs uo out onto brentwood blvd. when this happens, which is frequently, it's extremely difficult for cars exiting the drive-thru to leave. nearly impossible to get one of the very few parking spaces that they have, and overall just a mess. this place was planned extremely poorly.

this starbucks is really good, i have to say it could be 5 stars. however, it will never be 5 stars because the parking lot has only 5 spaces and one of them is handicapped. which is extremely odd because the inside seating area is massive? the staff of very friendly and the location is awesome! small historic town vibe going on here. it is very relaxing with small outside seating next to the lake/dam that runs through town.

this starbucks is amazing! donna (the manager), nate, gabe, and another girl there that names begins with a c or k (sorry :/) are awesome people! they are always happy, know my name, remember my order, ask me how i am doing, always greet me, and are on top of their game! i spend a lot of hours here studying and they do not miss a beat with other customers! i always look forward to coming to this starbucks! they make my studying experience even better! seeing they are a newer store the internet is a little slower than expected, but i know this will be fixed as the store works out their kinks.

for a starbucks, it's as good as it gets. the location is obviously the best being on high street, but what you wouldn't know is that the staff are always great! the manager/owner is always really friendly and funny. generally, there's always a place to sit, which can be a problem at some locations. and the drinks are always made as ordered, again... can be a problem at other locations. great location, great staff, and consistency.

this is a new place, still working out the kinks, but all things considered it seems to be doing well. clean space, friendly staff, cold and hot drinks, cold and hot food. i can see myself coming back here more often. i like that they are experimenting with having a separate community room for groups to use. i hope that works out well for them.

this location opened in 2018 and is a big step up from the other ofallon location. the seating area is much more spacious and easier to maneuver. the counter is also very large and aesthetically pleasing. the drive thru is standard and usually pretty good. only had one bad experience here when they were 100% out of food because of a labor strike at their supplier. parking lot can be treacherous due to the way traffic moves on and off highway 50. 4 stars because of the starbucks price tag.

let me start by saying i love coffee and starbucks (there's a local shop with better coffee but that's for another review). some starbucks are great with fab baristas and great relaxing atmosphere. camby is not one of them. the seating is tight, often the bar is the only spot to sit. the crew behind the counter do a good job at making drinks and are usually friendly, but they are often rowdy and loud with lots of colorful language. in short, it's not my favorite location, but it's my closest so it'll do.

i think i may have arrived after a mongol attack. upon walking in, there were wet footprints about (it was not raining), one of the employee's check stubs was laying in the entrance (and man, i hope that person was a part-timer). i found the source of the wet footprints being an overflowing and caution-taped off wide-open men's bathroom. coffee was fine, place was dirty, people were nice what i saw. i'll try again some day as it's near where i work. it was typical starbucks employee formation of 3 non-stop coffee-slingers never catching up with the unending line of customers, so i can see why the place was a bit dirty. the choice was a line out the door or stopping to clean.

rude manager or shift manager. dark & dirty. you know it's a bum location when they have code locks on the public restrooms. drinks came out wrong as well. it was snowing, so we were waiting for uber to come. my daughter's and i were standing near the door with masks on. the nasty manager came over and shoo'd us with her two hands and said: you need to leave, you have your drinks! we responded and said: we are visiting, took uber here thinking we could sit in here (another nashville store had in dining) we've called uber and are waiting! she said: you need to leave now! (it was snowing out) i said - no! call the police, but we are waiting 5 minutes for our uber right here! she is a nasty wench!

i like this starbucks because of its location inside silver legacy. being between el dorado and circus circus, it's a nice break away from all the gaming machines and tables. the baristas aren't that friendly. and the coffee drinks aren't as consistent as expected. to top it off, the prices are higher because it's inside a casino. yet, i still frequent this starbucks because i haven't found a great cup of coffee from any casino coffee place.

i love each and every starbucks, and this one i can remember pretty well. the inside isn't as big as preferred, it's actually to small i would say. this could easily be a problem for some study groups or students. they did get my order right and every drink received from them tasted amazing i should add. i will be going back because this location is very close to my house, but i wish the store was bigger.

a while ago, it was a very rare event when i went to a starbucks drive-through. that changed when starbucks moved into the old burger king building near my house. while the service is always prompt and friendly despite the long lines, the quality of what they make can be spotty. i'm a big lover of matcha green tea lattes, and all kinds of brewed drinks. a big part of what i love about brewed drinks is the distinct flavor of what is being brewed. so when i come here, and i order a matcha green tea latte, or any other selection, and i get a cup of warm milk with only a splash of what i actually ordered, it grinds my gears. i get that some of the only reasons people drink starbucks is to show off to the world that they're hip and cultured, but some people actually like the flavor of what's being brewed. i could easily warm up some milk on the stove, put it in a paper coffee cup, and lug it around with me, but that isn't what i'm playing for. if this location fails to quit serving warm milk, i'll have to start going to another location, or coffee place all together. these establishments are far too costly to only serve warm milk.

just your average run of the mill starbucks. there's only a few nice comfy chairs with the majority of seating located outside. it's tucked away in clearwater mall and never gets terribly busy. there's limited parking right outside the store but you shouldn't have any trouble parking somewhere in the plaza. so far all the baristas here have been very genuinely nice which i appreciate. more of a starbucks you'd stop in for something quick than a place you'd spend several hours at.
the hrs aren't correct they aren't open until 9:30 it's 9 on friday and i want my starbucks dang it lol.

worst starbucks ever! do not visit. i wouldn't of even given them a star if yelp didn't make me. i'm here on a sunday morning when they should be at their best and their baristas are nothing but rude. i ordered 2 drinks both of which have had to be remade at least twice. the barista at the bar thinks she can speak rudely to the customers as well. make sure not to speak loudly, otherwise you will get yelled at. if you can skip and move onto the next stabucks i would.
not sure which was most unpleasant: the employee whining loudly about how terrible his life is, the overflowing trash cans, or the dirty floor. not a highlight of the starbucks empire.

i've been coming here week now so i thought i'd wait before i wrote this review i come in around noon every day sandwiches are always gone by noon so if you want one get here early----- also can't seem to understand the difference between and iced caramel machitto and a hot caramel machitto since i order iced and they give me hot update came back today and still sold out of sandwiches ugh absolutely unacceptable

there are a lot starbucks around the area and this location has the worst customer service i've experienced. i'm not expecting to be blown away because it is just starbucks but i also want to be treated with a little bit of respect and maybe get a smile but i guess that's too much to ask for at this location. they also seem stubborn about giving the discounts from the starbucks app this is the second time i've had a run in with the rewards on my starbucks application.

gone to this starbucks two times and have ordered the same drink, a quad espresso. both times it was made wrong and the expresso was bitter. not one of the best starbucks. the employees are nice the coffee just sucks.

someone was kind enough to spend their money buying me a starbucks gift card so all i ask is that my drink be made correctly. apparently, this location does not comprehend the value of a correctly made starbucks drink. i ordered a tall blonde vanilla latte, hot. the first time, they made my latte iced. the second time, they made my latte with regular espresso instead of blonde espresso, and then had the audacity to get an attitude with me because i asked for my drink to be made correctly twice. i also wanted a reduced fat turkey bacon sandwich. i was told that they were out of breakfast sandwiches, but someone in line after me ordered a ham and cheese croissant. i politely brought it up to the barista that i thought they were out of breakfast sandwiches as per what i was told, to which she then replied that they were out of breakfast sandwiches so i asked if you're out of breakfast sandwiches why is the person behind me getting a ham and cheese croissant isn't it considered a breakfast sandwich? then i asked, why is it that a reduced fat turkey bacon sandwich is considered a breakfast sandwich but the ham and cheese croissant is not, to which the barista reply that she doesn't know and that whether or not the turkey bacon sandwich is considered a breakfast sandwich depends on who you ask. then she had the audacity to get an attitude with me yet again saying that regardless they were just out of the sandwich. hi definitely do not recommend this location of starbucks because of the rude barista's, and the horrible communication that takes place. save your money and buy starbucks coffee beans from your local grocers or go to one of the licensed locations like the one in the hyatt regency on perdido. they have great customer service, make great drinks, and have much better communication skills.

this has got to be the run-down and grungiest starbucks i have even seen. the place always seems dirty. the furniture has seen better days. the bathrooms are old, dinghy and messy. i've seen nicer truck stop restrooms. the only highlight is the presence of real artwork on the walls. hopefully someone from sb hq is on this. please shit this one down for some much needed renovations.

this used to be one of my favorite starbucks but since the construction of pyramid and mccarren it is a bear to get in and out of. depending on the time of day it is possible to get in and out quickly even with construction. the manager and the barista's are super sweet and if there is ever an issue they will offer to go above and beyond to make sure you get what you ordered. i have been coming to this starbucks for years. are mistakes occasionally made? yes, but that's true for any job. they have always corrected any error that i have found to my satisfaction. what more could you ask for . i use to know the barista's names here but since the construction. i don't. i almost always use the drive thru.

i'm on weight watchers so i don't waste points on overly sweet espresso drinks too often. but thankfully, starbucks has a pretty big skinny menu. i worked in a sb before and there is a formula for every drink. skinny/light drinks are made a particular way to lower fat and calories. that being said this location almost always has to remake my drink because they just don't hear me when i ask for a skinny/light drink. which makes it take a lot longer. the staff usually seem distracted, frazzled or impatient. i only bother with this store now if i have a gift card, or i just order tea which is pretty hard to screw up
i was just raving about how awesome the starbucks was and they gave us free #kindbars to try. just because we have the best kids in the world! #hastykids @starbucks

this been a regular stop on the way to work for me going on 5+ years now. up front, the three stars are for the staff however, a couple drag the rest down, one with a regularly flippant attitude. another common occurrence is at the point of sale when hot food items are ordered. the patron's name is not asked and when the order comes, a weak callout is made with only the name of the food and consequently not heard in restaurant over the often loud music and sometimes the sound level of the staff having a good time at work... i love seeing happy employees so perhaps this is a fair trade-off. however, a half warm breakfast sandwich tastes like hell. often, they do not have in stock breakfast items like the heated sandwiches. who does the ordering and for that matter, is there a manager even present seeing to these and the following glaring indiscretions? as for the condition of the restaurant, i'd have to say it's a bit dirty and unattended. the window exteriors appear to not have been cleaned since the remodel, the window interiors are not far behind. the tables don't get wiped on a regular basis through the morning as well as the floor swept and debris remaining throughout the morning, it doesn't appear that it's mopped nightly either. the supply station is often out of items as i have observed often on my numerous visits at 6 am, the condition of the floors as described herein, unswept and still showing the signs of the previous business day. i did ask about this once and was told "oh, we got hammered this morning". again, 6 am and i look around seeing that i was one of three people in the place and but two cars in line outside, the staff kids partying it up... really? as said, there is good people working there that take a personal pride in the job they do but all that goes out the door given the aforementioned issues. a manager that isn't busy behind the counter preparing orders to oversee that the place is taken care of would be a start. a good hard cleaning of the entire restaurant, inside and out including the filthy paved areas outside, including the furniture is more than overdue. i suggest steam cleaning and sterilization. can anyone say "reset"?
this isn't my everyday starbucks, but i come here often that they now know me by name, since my work is down the street. the baristas are so welcoming.

i go to this location multiple times a week. baristas are great. coffee is consistent. the drive thru line moves fast. everything you need/want from a starbucks. not sure why other people are giving great reviews and then leaving 4 stars. you people are reaching hard for something to complain about. go somewhere else. it only makes the line shorter for me.
whoever hired the girl working this drive through should be fired! she can't take an order to save her life and got a line up out to the road bc of this moron! seriously wth it's 1pm!!!

i don't know how - but they consistently get my coffee orders correct. i have patroned the rock hill location several dozen times since my morning commute changed about six months ago and while their staff is always ultra friendly - they do not get the exact same order correct. i cannot figure out how the same order between two starbucks in the same zip code (? i think?) can be interpreted so wildly... and this one gets it correct so in the mornings i have rerouted to drive from webster to crestwood because they get it correct... they don't ever act like my drink is irritating to them to help reduce the amount of sugar i am taking in but still getting what i want. they drew a rainbow on my coffee sleeve on nye and it made my day. everyone that has ever met me at the window has always been so nice and pleasant and made conversation if we had to wait. kota is one of the names i do remember (i am bad with names) - the gentleman who is a veteran who works on weekends, as well. always a pleasant experience when grabbing my morning latte.

these are my fave starbucks people! i normally hit to closest one when i need my sbux fix but, if i'm in the area, i will make it a point to hit this one up, these guys are so much fun. it's been a long time since i've come across a consistently enjoyable crew who care about their customers.

when to this starbucks after having lunch at pumpernick's restaurant. there was no line. it was happy hour. my daughter and i were able to get a drink for the price of 1. great deal! my drink was made as a requested. i will continue to go to this starbucks when i have a craving.

they have the clover machine here! baristas are very friendly. i had a nice conversation with andy while he was brewing my nicaragua reserve coffee. learned so much more about the clover itself as well as the coffee i was drinking. clean interior, attractive design, great starbucks!

this place could be better. the seats and tables outside always have bird poop on it and is always dirty. i understand these tables & seats are outside 24/7 but it is clearly unsanitary for you to put food and drinks on infested bird poop tables & chairs. this starbucks puts your name on the cups which is a good thing however once they make your drink they don't say your name they say the name of the drink which confuses customers because two customers could be ordering the same drink. the new hispanic employee making the drinks, clearly english is not his first or second language so when he says the name of the drink you can't understand what the hell he is saying. that becomes a problem. i asked him why he can't just say my name of (aj) that would be 10x easier he said " i only do what i know to do okay my friend" fuckin classy! this is not miami hire people who speak engish. when i was in seattle the head quarters of starbucks everyone spoke english and they called my name each time.

the workers where very rude. i bit my tongue and still said thank you after being ignored for 5 minutes so the employees could talk about there personal lifes. the coffee is pretty much the same at all the starbucks so the worker's make the experienced and they suck at this one.

this starbucks is a mess. from web orders to the check out, nothing is set up to be customer friendly. the web orders are housed behind the counter. that is totally fine, but the employees actively ignore people. what's the point of mobile orders if you need to wait to get drinks in line? next, the barista there this evening was in no rush or concerned about doing anything quickly. he filled up the lids, stared at the blender for one drink, remade the drink, and did nothing else. the cups were piling up. each time i go here, the experience is the same. it's very disappointing.
thanks to the kind gentleman at the drive-thru today! he made my day and the best way to start my birthday count down off! 3 days away! the service was fast, friendly and great!!!

never had anything short of great customer service. my iced coffee is always good and tastes consistent every time. the only thing deterring me from giving it five stars is how long it takes to get a drink. some days the drive thru is very quick, but most days it takes around ten minutes to get through the drive thru. other than that, it's a clean location with cheerful batistas and great coffee.

i rarely give bad reviews. i always wonder is it the day...was it a specific employee but no...it's not. i have been in drive through and sat outside a few times now and find myself going elsewhere for a few reasons. the drive through lines here are terrible even with 4 cars. i have been through many many...lines and gone through faster with a better service attitude that this one. we went in and had slow mediocre experience inside as well. there was one time a girl said she was there working from another sbx helped us and she was wonderful. truly. again, 3 o clock...4 people ahead and i was in the line for 20 minutes plus. the service via intercom was less than expected...and slow on top of it. consistently this has been my usual experience here. sad. i prefer to drive further on 60 for better service and atmosphere. it is a busy location but i have yet to enjoy my experience here. sad.

the crew here are always very efficient! the music is too loud so its difficult to make or take calls. it's frequently too cold. i've brought people here and we have had to leave because the music is so loud. this review is after more than 2 dozen visits so it is not random.
besides parking this place is really roomy and they're always very friendly. i live across the street so i'm here all times of the day and it's very clean.
i've come in here off and on for a few years. every time i come, there are always flies, everywhere. i left early a number of times because of them, today included.
we order two cups of coffee every day. nothing fancy just coffee, milk and sugar. every single time- our order is wrong.

they recently updated this starbucks location. it looks brighter, more open and modern. i don't feel like it added any more seating space, but now there are lounge chairs and some booth spaces as well as a longer table to accommodate individual people working. the two-person tables are round and a bit small if you're both wanting to work on laptops or have books to spread out so keep that in mind. i do like the look of the space and they did fix some of those nasty potholes so the parking lot's improved since my last review.

this is a great starbucks- quick lines, great outdoor seating. the drive-through can get clown-packed during peak times, but that's life. the staff is also amazing- friendly, quick. i bought a mug for a friend as a christmas gift and the cashier gave me extra tissue and a bag to wrap it. they definitely go above and beyond!

this used to be my home starbucks that i stopped at several mornings a week for my standard starbucks drink (iced green tea with no syrup!) up until i moved to west county not quite a year ago. i was in the hood the other afternoon and since i had tried the chile mocha (special fall drink) a few days ago it has been on my mind. i hit the drive thru here at around 5pm on a thursday. there was only one car ahead of me. i ordered a tall chile mocha latte and was on my way in minutes. my beverage was hot and delicious. i'm not sure why this starbucks has a lot of negativity directed at it in the reviews. they retain employees and have a core group of regulars who hang out there in the mornings. i used to pop in when the drive thru was too crowded and i always got a "hello" and "the usual?" from the same girl! don't hate on the south city starbucks...
a great afternoon stop, nice patio outside and beautiful inside seating. the coffee is addictive ! ((( great service too )))

so i bragged to my hubby about the caramel brûlée latte and finally convinced him to try it. i have had these at different locations from nj to ny without any issues. so we tried this one this morning and brought them home to enjoy with breakfast. to my dismay they did not add any caramel syrup nor the brulee topping. what a disappointment as i had my heart all set on that delicious cup. i gave them 3 stars because the young lady at the drive through window was very sweet, so service they had down packed, but consistency in making the order, not so good. so disappointed as this is the closest location to me. will i have to travel further to make sure my order is made as it is supposed to be? in their defense, they did offer to remake them but i would have to make the drive again and was not really feeling like doing that on my day off as i already have a full schedule.

every starbucks location seems to deliver quality food and beverages, so they really only vary on their customer service. i've been to this location many times over the years, but unfortunately, this time was a real disappointment. i went through the drive-through mid-afternoon, which was surprisingly busy for that time of day. when i pulled up to pay for my order, i was shocked to see the staff member taking my payment was only wearing her mask half-on. her nose was completely exposed, and it was barely covering her mouth. given the extremely high covid-19 numbers in the state right now, this is extremely concerning - especially by the staff member who deals directly with customers all day long. the second issue i had was how long it took to get my order and general customer service. from when the order was placed until i pulled up to the window took much longer than normal, even though it appeared the people in the cars in front of me had small orders (i didn't see them receiving tons of cups, just 1-2). there were at least two staff members preparing orders behind the counter (in addition to the one at the drive-through window), and they both seemed to be hustling. i'm not sure how many customers were inside, so they may have needed more help. it was the half-masked woman handling the drive-through who was moving slowly. i saw my drink prepared and set it down for her to pick up - she only needed to grab it and hand it to me. however, it sat there...and sat there... she took more orders from the drive-through... moved around a bit... then finally picked it up and handed it to me - almost like she forgot i was sitting there. the drink was perfectly prepared and tasted great (as always), but was dripping all over when it was handed to me. i had to ask for napkins, and the woman seemed irritated that i asked (even though it was obvious i needed at least one to clean up the cup). i had also ordered a cheese & fruit box, and would normally receive napkins with any food order from starbucks. it may seem like something really little to complain about, but when your drink is dripping on you and in your car, napkins are pretty important. the food and drink i ordered would get 5 stars, but the service was more like 2, so i can only give them 3 stars overall for this visit.

how hard is it to follow a three ingredient recipe? they consistently use the wrong tea bag and this last visit was "the straw that broke the camels back". i brought my drink and told them it was wrong. instead of them correcting the issue and sending me on my way, i had to wait for everyone (plus the people that came in after me) to get their order. i'm done with the big bend starbucks and will drive those additional miles to get better customer service. (ie, target)
this starbucks always has good ring and good customer service my latte was very yummy and had great taste as well as it was heated perfectly thanks starbucks team !

i like this location as it's open late so i can study but it's sooooo loud! between the blaring music and obnoxiously loud employees gossiping and acting like they are at night club, it's too distracting to study. btw i'm not sure who maggie is, but none of the staff like you . so i over heard from their gossip

other than a killer wait at peak times, this is my favorite location on drexel's campus. my orders always come out perfect. the baristas are always super friendly and attentive at the register and when you pick up drinks. i haven't had a bad experience there even when the line is out the door. somehow they are always patient and smiling. not a ton of space with the place usually packed with students but term breaks are never an issue--you can grab a seat if you like or have a quick 10-minute turn-around on your coffee. my go-to drink: chai latte with an extra shot of espresso

came here for a post lunch pre shopping iced coffee. at the end, i was asked if i wanted milk - i asked for a little room for milk and instead of leaving room for me,they added my milk for me.... unless i was at a drive thru, i always expect (and have always had) starbucks to allow customers to customize their drink to their liking - add their own milk and sugar unless otherwise directed. the fact they didn't give me this option irked me a bit- they might have thought they were doing me a favor but they weren't, haha. didn't want to be that person who was picky/wasteful and ask for another cup but i will for sure next time be more specific so they have a clear understanding of what the customer wants.

excellent concept for the grab & go joe lovers. it's a drive-thru / walk-up only location. so avoid congestion and standing in long lines at the expense of indoor seating, chill tunes, work space & merchandise. staff is decent...it'd be pretty difficult to do a poor job with so limited contact. pull up, use your reward card & dash out. easy does it. ~christy

this is sherwood park's newest starbucks and only the second one in town with a drive-thru - that alone should give it a couple extra stars! the decor of this starbucks is laid back and comfortable feeling, with lots of diverse seating options. there is one large, long table that would be perfect for a study group or business get together, as well as single, low chairs. with the distressed tin walls covering part of the coffee shop, you get a definite "kick back and relax feel". the service is great and of course the coffee is... well, starbucks - so how can you go wrong? a nice selection of bakery items and clean, comfortable washrooms make this spot high on my coffee spots list.
no decaf was ready and was told i would to wait for it to be brewed, isn't that the whole point of starbucks.

i frequent many starbucks locations in indianapolis/fishers/carmel area and i'd have to say that i get the best customer service from this location pretty consistently. i always order my drinks via the starbucks app because i'm on the go at all times, which leaves little opportunity for interaction with the baristas. however, i came into the store last week while waiting for my order to be prepared and the barista promptly greeted me as i walked through the door and asked what i was waiting for when i stopped at the mobile pick up area. i told him my name and after reading my drink specifications on the sticker he asked if my preference of soy milk was an allergy or preference. i've never been asked that before, and i rotate through the same few drinks at starbucks, so it took me a second to think through why he might be asking. i told him it was more of a sensitivity to milk than an allergy and he must have sensed i was confused by where he was going with the question so he explained the sprinkles that usually go on top of my drink contain milk and so he wanted to know if they should be left off. again, this is the first time i'd heard of this being a consideration for my favorite drink choice and i was really thankful for the tip and opted to leave them off. this is customer service at its finest and all done with a smile. highly recommend this location!

slowest starbucks ever!!! i was late to an event on saturday while stuck in the drive through for 20mins!! sunday i thought i'd try again considering it's the only local starbucks with a drive through. and the line was around the plaza. and not moving. line inside was just about out the door after weaving in and out inside the store. and then the mobile ordering "wasn't available". it shouldn't be this hard to get a cup of coffee! i drove 5 mins down the road to the other starbucks on g2bay and 19 and i was in and out even with a few people in line. but again, late because of this location. the staff clearly needs more efficient training or better management. i've very rarely had quick, easy or polite service. such a shame bc in a great location.

yay a local starbucks! they are super sweet very professional but they had to redo my coffee as they handed me a grande i ordered a vente. then i got my replacement and the whip cream was missing and tasted bad. the line behind me was super long. i just took my $6.35 coffee and left. it's a new starbucks and they need more training. i'll be back in six months....

this is a crazy-busy starbucks but the baristas are especially friendly. i don't like the store layout - you have to squeeze past mobile order and pick up to get to the registers to order. seating is also tight so it's not designed for guests to linger or have coffee shop meetings.

slowest starbucks i've encountered in the world. passed through nashville international 4 times in last 2 months. twice i waited over 30 minutes in line for a coffee; once i wait for 15 mins but the long line didn't move. the 4th time i didn't try. the coffee, once you get it, is as expected. but they really need to hire more people.
really slow service two people working the whole store you expect more from starbucks . i will say that the staff was nice even under pressure

well, as this is a starbuck on campus for drexel's student, doesn't matter how terrible it is, this is our only choice. nobody is even bother to add this on yelp. try to order something simple, like dark roasted coffee or just tea, so there will be less chance the staff here mess up your drinks.
really great coffee. my favorite is the classic grande iced coffee with almond milk and two sugars! also really love the cake pops and little snack boxes.

in an update to my first review, i returned to this starbucks during the holiday season and went through the drive-thru during the same time of day. the employees couldn't have been nicer. while waiting for my coffee, the man working the window asked how my day had been and laughed with me when i said how excited i was about my snowman cake pop. it's cute and delicious; of course i was excited! while i can't truly compare to my last visit as i went in the store that time, i do believe this location has upped its game when it comes to service. so i have no problem upping my rating as well. nice improvement, starbucks!

today i went in and got the usual, venti mocha. like always 3/4s full. i never like going to this starbucks i just drive to the one on dalmonte ranch. and i work across the parking lot from this starbucks. no other starbucks has done this to me. they always fill it up, and i pay for it. what is this! i only came here today because i had no car to drive to the other one. but i knew this would happen. i shouldn't of went! this will definitely be the last time i come to this location. open up your drinks as they hand them to you and let them know. they only do it with hot drinks and not the cold. cause you can see threw the cold coffee cups.
probably the dirtiest starbucks i've ever been too. this includes rest stops. clean it up. windex the doors. clean the bathrooms. gross

this starbucks is great, the people here are very friendly and the location is a nice place to just sit back and watch the traffic. my friends and i love coming here for our bike meet ups and catch up while watching nice cars drive by.

quick service for being so busy. i lose sleep over the fact that every starbs i've been to gives me a different vsc nitro cold brew. sometimes it's sweet, sometimes its mellow and fantastic, and today it's so bitter my skin melted off my body and fell into a puddle on the floor. i did not clean it up i just went to my car and slammed my coffee. choked it down. i'll try again another time.

a starbucks is a starbucks is a starbucks is a starbucks haha. i come here every morniny after i drop my son off to school before i head to school myself. i avoid the lines inside and hit the drive through. it's right next to game stop and within walkng distance of my gym. the baristas are always friendly like most starbucks employees are. not much to say, although i was a little disappointed they were out of their banana bread.
great and fast service. their chocolate cake pop is to die for. i can eat at least 3 a day, i'm addicted. nice and helpful staff

this is now one of two starbucks in the shopping district (another inside fry's). being my closest starbucks, i have come here numerous times for a hot cup of joe. there's some seating outside and it's small inside, but it's a starbucks, so you know what you are getting. never had a complaint or any issues, just hot coffee and tea!

there are actually two starbucks at this welcome center now. one inside the welcome center and another in a new, stand-alone building (with a drive-thru) closer toward the northbound exit. both locations have the same address, so consider this review to be for both of them. the one inside the welcome center gets 2 stars. the new one gets 4, so i'm averaging them out at 3. i think the reason the new one was constructed is because the location inside the welcome center sucks so bad. it's basically a kiosk that was constructed smack-dab in the middle of all the chaos so you never know where the line begins or ends. it has taken me up to 10 minutes just to place my order here and up to 15 minutes from the time i started waiting until the time my drink was prepared. the new location, however, provided a much more pleasant experience. i was in and out in probably about 2 minutes, but all i ordered was black coffee, so take that as you will. if all you need is coffee and a bathroom, avoid the madness inside the welcome center and go to the new stand-alone starbucks, the goal of which seems to be to pull some of the foot traffic out of the welcome center.

this location is okay. it is one of the few places on cleveland street that are open late in the evening. the rest of the street even on the weekend is pretty dead. we stopped in here for a potty break and for a drink and snack. our drink was fine, but our sweet bread we ordered was pretty stale and cold, blah. maybe it was just bc it was in the evening, and it had been sitting for a while. gross, i guess i learned my lesson from that one. if it is not too busy, and it is the evening, don't order anything that has been sitting out...eek. that's okay. i still like starbucks. i just know better what stuff to get, and when.
new starbucks with a drive thru. busy place, cool hipster types working the window doing the safe distancing thing. great coffee as always.

probably the worst starbucks i've ever been to and i've been to a lot. drinks never quite right and staff who seem to not understand that when you pay almost $4 for a drink - you want it to taste in the ballpark of what you get at other local starbucks. avoid..

we frequent this starbucks because the staff is always very polite, cheerful and seem to all be having a great time! and generally very quick with the service, too. thanks you for your cheer and smiles!!! and the coffee, too!! :)
terrible service. twice i've gone here and they didn't get my order right both times. i refuse to come back to this starbucks again.
thank you thank you thank you! to manager of the starbucks for donating to my classroom for preschoolers with autism! they donated so many straws for a sensory activity and we are so appreciative!
hit or miss depending on the barista that day if you're ordering a complicated drink like a frap. always really busy in the mornings and on weekends. can be slow.

my absolutely favorite starbucks!! it's is always so clean and everyone is so friendly!! if i could give this location more than 5 stars i would!! there is a man working that was so kind to me last week it made my day, i think his name might be joe, but love love love him!!

the staff here is super sweet and helpful, i haven't had an issue with customer service at all. however, i recently have started doing my daily mobile order because my job moved locations, and 4/5 times my order is either wrong or late. it seems like this store is incredibly busy, which is totally understandable, but they also seem to have some very serious staffing/management issues. it's definitely not the employees fault, they are just trying their best, but when i give them 20 minutes to get my drink done via the mobile app and then i show up and it's still not done, it's clear that this store is operational deficient.

this starbucks is closest to me. i gave it one star already because of the cold food and even cold coffee. i'm usually in a rush and i always go via the drive-thru. my husband wanted a breakfast sandwich. i regrettably gave it another try this morning. i didn't bother ordering a coffee, but two breakfast sandwiches. as usual. sandwiches are warm to hot on the outside... cold.. not cool, but cold on the inside. my son has worked at starbucks for - 2-3 years. never at this location. he told me they must be warming the sandwiches straight from the freezer. he said that at the stores he has worked at they take out the sandwiches out of the freezer one day ahead and put them in the refrigerator. that way they are not frozen when heated. i have on a few occasions spoken to the folks at this store about the cold sandwiches. i was advised to order them "open faced" to make sure they were heated thoroughly. but it just doesn't matter. either they really are hard frozen to start with or they are ignoring my request to warm them open-faced. i have no idea. luckily this morning i was going back to my office and i heated the sandwiches in our microwave. but i hate it when i'm on the road and i have to throw it out! well, i gave it another try... for sure, no more.. too bad. the staff is very friendly.

this starbucks often has a line wrapped around the building but they do a good job trying to move folks through as quickly as they can. the logistics are a bit messy, though (not their fault), though when it's busy. don't forget your mask, and don't forget to tip your barista!

this is my cheers! the place where everybody knows my name and is happy that i came. the place where my caffeine fix is handled promptly and with a smile. i have been coming to this particular location for about four years now. although i change up my drink order daily, the baristas are fast. many times the line is long but they work through orders fast and with a smile. i do tend to avoid the starbucks with drive thru, it always seems the cars take priority, so this location works for me! a big shout out to danyelle, tress, jamie and jeremy. without you, my good mornings would just be plain ordinary mornings.
every time i go to this location they never have my mobile order ready always slow and the fraps always look like mess. i wish i could post a pic of this sloppy mess

honestly this starbucks is ok. the employees are all very nice people. however, how do y'all manage to make a drink wrong 50% of the time...especially when you're given the screen shot of the actual recipe. vt black iced tea, 6 pumps raspberry, 6 pumps toffee nut, breve --- that's it. i used to think it was my fault but i show the same recipe to every starbucks i've been to and this is the only starbucks that messes it up. but they do keep the place organized and clean. very convenient location as well. and i also like that they prepare drinks very quickly. i would order on my phone and by the time i get there it's already made in less than 8 minutes. that's amazing considering how busy it can get.

i had a friend order a light frappuccino blended. barista thought that meant to blend it for half the time. i don't know why they would think anyone would order a drink like that, and cant use a little common sense.
i went back today and it's much better. the staff is nicer and very welcoming. i'm glad they made changes because i will keep coming to this one. :)

this starbucks is great! the employees are so friendly and attentive and very competent. you get your drink fast and perfect every time. maddie, stephen, drew, stacy, and brad are the best!! this store is a neighborhood staple. we have been waiting for it forever. yay!
the ladies behind the counter were very friendly. my coffee was perfect and it was pretty quiet :) only negative is the fact there's no drive through.

i love this place. it's very conveniently located to home, work, and everywhere in between. the only thing i don't really like is the parking lot adjacent to this establishment...but hey, that's not starbucks' fault! the caramel crunch frappucino is indubitably delightful!

be ready to wait 45min, line wraps around on to gravois. they closed the cafe so you can't walk in and order nor can you order ahead on the app for pick up. way too busy of a location, they need to do something better.

don't bother coming here. go to wawa for a great coffee and snacks at a much better price and service.. just right in front of this starbucks store. i love starbucks but this specific store lacks management. the employees are always feeling bothered instead of happy and pleasant to service customers.

wow...this is my first experience with an unfriendly starbucks. i was given the wrong order going through drive thru. i went in to get it corrected. i explained the problem and was told that it was rung up incorrectly and was given no apology. they gave me my corrected drink and i was told to go stand in line to get my refund if i wanted to. she took my receipt, and as the last person went through, i was told by "d" that she would get to my refund in a moment. after 3 times of saying that to me, i said that i had waited moments, and she asked what my problem was. i was finally given the difference owed to me. starbucks is usually the top in customer service. this location fails with me.
horrible service. was told by employee to not even bother ordering because it was too close to closing time and she didn't have anything left.

extremely rude staff at the drive through window. they were out of every dessert i asked for and when i asked what they did have the woman raised her voice and shouted at me. she purposefully took her time and made us sit at the window without acknowledging us while she spoke to other people.

driving home to south bend after a day at indy, i was parched. seeing the friendly starbucks logo at the off ramp i jumped off i65 with my mind set on a grande vb frapuccino. all i can say is that i finally found the world's worst starbucks. surly barristas who clearly did not enjoy being there. i brought in my empty coffee cup from the morning run only to find there wasn't even a trash can in the place. i had to dispose of it at the gas station next store. what clinched it for me was after making my way back onto i65, i reached for my drink, took a sip and got nothing but ice and air! they left out the liquid! the drink was nothing but whipped cream and crushed ice! i was frosted to say the least. how this place survived the downsizing is beyond me. never again.
took over 30 minutes for a mobile order. just unacceptable when on our way to work. they need to address this with manpower in the mornings.
i recently had the pleasure of visiting this starbucks and amanda was an absolute gem. the team was cheerful, the wait was short, and the coffee was great!

today, i had a very bad experience at starbucks. my drink was made incorrectly so i went back to have this rectified only to be told how to order my drink next time. thanks, but i'm aware on how to order a drink. no other starbucks gives me issues but today i was basically told i don't know how to order my drink. so ok, moving along. i reorder my drink again as i was instructed to only to have it remade incorrectly again! i can't even believe it! to top it off, the girl, whose name i shall leave out but it's taking everything in me, gave me attitude and was short and cut me off as i was speaking. i will never, ever return to this starbucks again!

i really enjoy starbucks. i enjoy stopping off at them from time to time to relax and clear my mind. although, trying to find a parking space at this location is almost impossible sometimes. with that is added stress which i don't need. what's sad is the location is very convenient for me. i'll be craving a cup of jo as i pass, look at the lot and decide to keep going.

disappointed in this sb. took a long time to get drinks, even with what appears to be sufficient baristas behind the counter. and dirty with trash cans overflowing and tables dirty. it's mid afternoon on a weekend that is slow period. disappointed in this sb.

small, quiet, attached to hampton inns. all the handicapped sports were taken by enormous pickup truck with no stickers or disabled plates, super frustrating. coffee was hot and strong just like my husband likes. i like the free water to drink in the car.

thank you to all the friendly batista's that have served me here at this location dailey for 8 + years. i love you guys you always get it right my 4 shots espresso short shots sweeter this way thanks courtney , ralph lol and all always asking how i am doing and showing interest in my life or just how my morning or evening is going xoxo

this is the starbucks you go to if you're trying to get someone to make you a super complicated drink- the absolute correct way and without an attitude, even in the drive through. i always order off menu extra complicated drinks that literally make these poor people have to do math in order to concoct these beverages and they still do it with a smile.

mobile order not ready. they said it wasn't received. they asked if i sent it to the correct location. i showed them my receipt with the correct location. then they said maybe someone took it. i mobile ordered because i was in a hurry. employees did not offer anything to make this inconvenience right.

i am not a big fan of starbucks, but i am a big fan of coffee. i prefer my coffee to be hot and this is something that this particular starbucks seems unable to manage. if you are staying at the bellvue go across the street to wawa. they know how to make good coffee and it's hot. there is also a dunkin down the street...worth the walk. this place is horrible.

quick service even though they were busy. a good amount of indoor seating. got no problems with the drinks they got a nice stained glass ceiling too and even though the starbucks was in silver legacy casino, the cigarette smoke smell was not noticeable. the best of the experience was the friendly service.

the lesser of the two uptown starbucks locations, based purely on seating. there are lots of great local alternatives that serve better coffee with free wifi. local options close by include: pjs (literally two storefronts away toward downtown) ccs (magazine and jefferson) rue de la course (oak and carrollton)

they are very busy in the mornings, but they get everyone through quickly. even at 7am, they are very friendly to everyone in the middle of rush. drinks are made correctly - unlike some other starbucks around town. even though i get off the interstate to get here, it is worth the extra minutes!
i love all starbucks stores. my visit was fast and friendly. my ice coffee was delightful. thank you for excellent customer experience ' i will return! gloria~
this starbucks location is located within the harrahs casino. it's a normal starbucks, so it's great!

good old starbucks. very busy location at times but they're able to somehow push everyone through in a reasonable amount of time. my drinks are almost always made to my satisfaction. friendly crew here too.
clean and modern. staff is very friendly. thought we'd try this new location and we were not disappointed.

just. so. bad. i have numerous stories of nonsense with this store. but, today's shall be the feature. i order a dark roast drip coffee. literally all that needs to happen is a cup needs to be placed under the spout and then, upon fillage, a lid must be applied. i waited for about 12 minutes for a mobile order. no one checked in. i had to ask twice before the barista realized she was not, in fact, brewing any dark roast. the explanation was that they dump regular coffee after 11am. welcome to crazy town. in summation, be prepared to wait bc this excursion took 20 mins beginning to end.

this starbucks has awesome employees. they demonstrate the best customer service i've ever experienced at a starbucks. friendly, energetic, and very welcoming! i've become a regular and they know exactly what i want: a venti iced white mocha no whip light iced!
great service! considering the line is generally long (students need their coffee), the staff is still very friendly and extremely quick.

my husband and i just drove into town from tennessee, after 12hr - we decided to pick up a keto friendly drink. the cashier at the register was extremely friendly, professional and even offered us a sample of one of the most popular drinks. she has been the best starbucks server ever met, and we drink a lot starbucks! thanks to her, whenever we visit, we'll make to stop by!!!

this is where we always go on early sunday morning work caffeine runs. there's usually not much of a line when i'm there, but i've seen it pretty bad on occasion while walking by. i'll be back many many times for sure!

thought i'd try it out again. disappointed, again. how can there be four employees, in the only one in line, and no one says a thing to me for 5 minutes? i came in the store because the drive thru was ridiculous and long, but still just as frustrating.

i would give no stars if that was possible. this is the absolute worst starbucks ever in the name of customer service/hospitality employees everywhere. we are in the age of customer service, get with the program. worst wait times in the world. and i have traveled the world and gone to starbucks in many many countries.
they never have bananas...clearly the manager here has no clue how to stick their store.

newly opened starbucks. this location is not in the target store, as this business location page was altered to show as. i submitted a change request (business edit), but it has been mixed up. this location has a drive-thru window...great on those cold winter days...especially great when you don't want sb but your wife does!! their staff (barista) were friendly and had quick service. it was exceptionally clean...rightfully so since this is a brand new location. they have a decent outside seating area, definitely useful when the weather is nice and you don't want to go inside to wait in those long summer lines...as folks order them ice coffees!! (is it just me or coffee should always be served hot?? you don't see folks in line at 7-11 to get hot slurpees in the winter, right?? off my soapbox now!!) i'm sure business will pick up for them and this location will be busy since it's a prime location.

i have been to a handful of airport starbucks, and i understand why they overstaff some of them for when the rush hits, but this was a slower day and while a hive can work well with tons of bees buzzin' around these folks need to work on their system. it wasn't busy but the 7 or so folks working behind this tiny hub of a starbucks were all younger and super chatty with each other where it made it difficult for folks to know if their orders were being placed or not, or correctly. i don't speak starbucks and asked for a large, but got a medium and felt like the staff while efficient was being short with the clientele in favor of side conversations and jokes with other employees. i totally understand liking the people you work with but when it's at the expense of customer service, you should probably keep it to the break room.

love that this starbucks is close to my house. they are always friendly. my only issue is they have 4 wall outlets in the entire store. 1 of those is hidden behind the condiments bar, leaving virtually no outlets for the entire seating area other than the 2 comfy chairs. not cool. it's as if they don't want people hanging out or working from there. i probably won't come back here to chill or work. sad, cause it's an easy fix to run a strip of outlets down the wall. please fix.

cockroaches! hello! i tell the staff at the counter every other time i order coffee at this location. after a month, cockroaches! maybe it's the old building or maybe it's all the homeless people that hang out in there. this is likely the last time i visit here until they advertise a remodel or put up a sign on the front doors reading, "no homeless welcome". it's a business not a shelter. buy something or don't enter. it's not a personal shower room either, it's called a rest room.

so, i stopped in here for an espresso hoping to use my $5 gift card for starbucks. no dice. i was informed that this is not a starbucks. of course there are starbucks signs everywhere and the barista was wearing a starbucks hat and apron. my espresso was served in a starbucks cup and cup holder. my espresso was in fact starbucks espresso. had a short chat with the manager who gave me the corporate line of bull - they are not "really" a starbucks, blah blah blah. very disappointing at least the coffee was pretty good. . wonder if i will ever be able to use my gift card? mark.
i love starbucks! this one is in a great location. staff is friendly but the drive thru is always backed up.

i always forget this starbucks is there. there is always seating when you go at the right time of the day. i get my hair done at circa 79 so it's a great location to grab something sweet before getting beautified!
one of my fave locations. always available parking, the baristas are awesome, and this one is perfect to come to and get some work done or meet up with a friend.
starbucks gravois bluffs is my place ! every morn they keep me going w my flat white i love austin

i go to this starbucks mainly because i work and live close. carleon is amazing and always greets me with a positive and upbeat attitude! another barista there always remembers my name and greets me with a smile! this location has some great employees, my business will stay here!

a starbucks like any other starbucks. this is the one i visit when i work in the mall, and there isn't anything special about it, but there's nothing i have found particularly awful, either. i mostly visit here as oppossed to tim hortons or seond cup because of the my starbucks rewards program. the lineups can get pretty ridiculous, obviously, since it's in a mall. they also tend to have only one person on till, even when it does get crazy, which is a bit annoying, especially if you're going there on your break and don't exactly have 15 minutes to spend standing in line to order and again waiting for that order. but most of the servers i've had have been very friendly and good at their jobs. there are one or two there that i dread getting, but that's the same anywhere. my coffee usually tastes just as i expect it to, and only once or twice have they gotten anything on my order wrong (and they are always happy to fix it). a good option if you're in the mall and want a drink or snack. (though don't hold out hope of them having the new-ish bistro boxes in stock - they're sold out more often than not!)

i really like this starbucks. i'm more of a pjs girl but this is where i come for my basic white girl iced chai tea latte with soy. as previous reviewers have said, there's not much indoor seating but plenty of covered outdoor seating. what's cool about this is that you can bring your puppy bff with you! while starbucks policy is that they do not allow pets, you can bring your dog inside to order before heading outside. this is a must when there isn't a drive thru! the staff here is friendly and service is quick. drinks are your standard starbucks quality. stop on by, drop a tip in the jar, and treat your doggo to a puppuchino on the patio!

compared to others this one misses the mark. the brand is supposed to be consistent and nothing could be further from the truth. do yourself a favor and go 1 mile east on watson rd to the one in crestwood. this matches what can be found through the country. no comparison! traveled 150k air miles last year and have been to hundreds of locations. sunset hills is the bottom of the barrel.

typical starbucks here... busy time, and still friendly and efficient. i'm sure you all know the pricing... same as every starbucks. exactly as we wxpected... which is good... nothing at all to complain about. but nothing to really cheer about either.

love starbucks but this location is pretty slow...customer service is nice & pleasant & they still make ur starbucks coffee favs delicious but could use some extra people to deliver beverages a little timelier! this location is also open 24 hrs so great for anytime enjoyment plus it's right off i4 so it's pretty convenient for most peeps on the go...hopefully, if u get lucky u won't have to wait as long for ur drinkable delight!
longest wait. i don't recommend at all. been waiting in line for well over a half an hour. with no movement.

my favorite hot starbucks espresso beverage is the venti carmel macchiato!! i admit i'm very picky about my order. but it's not like it's a .99 cent cup of coffee. i was soo blown away tonight as the barista made my carmel macchiato absolutely perfectly delicious and on point!!! my hubby and i frequent this starbucks location drive thru whenever we can. hubby just orders the venti pike w/cream & 4 splenda, he's not hard to please at all. if i wasn't with him, he'd settle for a cheap regular coffee from the donut place. i, on the other hand, am not a fan of coffee. so, when we are out together, it's starbucks only. lol. i order the hot venti sugar free carmel macchiato with 9 pumps of the sugar free vanilla syrup, extra carmel drizzle in the cup, 3 splenda, 2% frothed milk, just 1 shot of espresso (not the 2 that comes with this) and with a straw to sip from. yes, i know it sounds like a lot but i am a diabetic and i also can't have a lot of caffeine so i have to be specific. 5 stars from the barista tonight, my macchiato was perfection!! thank you so much!

the staff here is very fast and friendly. i was greeted with a smile from multiple baristas and they started making my drink as i ordered and before i even had a chance to pay. my iced tea was ready to go as i finished the transaction and walked over to the bar area so i was in and out in a matter of minutes, can't beat it

i've been to several starbucks all over the states as i'm sure many people have, but this specific location is the best. the crew they have working there is the most friendly group ever. i guess i can only speak for the early morning staff because that's when i go, but i always challenge them with made up drinks and complicated orders and they do a great job. also, while they are trying to figure out my drinks they are good at small talk and don't just leave you sitting there in the drive through like a number. i can't speak anymore highly of this group. keep it up!

i had a great experience with amy this morning. i messed up with payment and she went out of her way to make sure i got some coffee in me. excellent customer service, wonderful experience. thank you again, amy! you made my morning!

so far so good on day 3 of being open. staff is friendly if a bit unsure. the inside is roomy with a wood and brick interior. tones of gray, black and charcoal give the inside a sleek, modern look. my coffee didn't have its sometimes bitter, burnt taste to it. which was great! drive thru service is convenient. this is a welcome addition to my neighborhood!
very busy store you can get and right out! lots of parking in the rear of the building!

i almost granted starbucks five stars because i never actually get coffee or any of the other sugar loaded shitty tasting drinks/baked goods. but the almost five star rating was due to the fact that every single day i bring my own tea packets and they give me a large cup of free piping hott water or when i'm thirsty they kindly give me grande free cups of ice water. thanks for the free hot and ice water starbucks.
fast and friendly starbucks, better than some, on par with others.

this starbucks location can be found on the corner, down the street from the betsy ross house. my husband and i came by here after visiting the betsy ross house last thursday afternoon, around 4:25 pm. there were definitely plenty of people inside waiting for their orders and only one person ahead of us in line. we ended up ordering: teavana shaken iced passion fruit green tea lemonade, $2.75 prior to tax- although my husband asked for a grande size, the employee rang him up for a tall size. i didn't have any since i am not a big fan of passion fruit but he thought this drink was good. mango dragonfruit starbucks refreshers, $4.95 prior to tax- the girl at the register didn't ask for the size but automatically rang us up for a grande size. i thought this drink was refreshing, especially on the hot we were there. i liked the pieces of the dragonfruit, it was nice to eat it after i had finished drinking my beverage. ------------------------ (total= $8.32 after tax) the employee taking our order didn't give us a receipt and when i had asked for one, she said she didn't know how to reprint one out. i am assuming she was a new employee since i saw another employee showing her around and training her. after my husband had initially ordered his iced peach green tea lemonade and paid for our order, the employee told him that they didn't have any more peach tea and to pick another flavor. the employee should have checked beforehand before charging us for the drink. my husband ended up ordering the passion fruit instead. he still thought this drink was good but he had wanted the peach flavor instead. wait time for our drinks was over 15 minutes, which seemed like a pretty long time, compared to other starbucks that we have been to before. a little later, i asked the barista making our drinks for water. he said okay but never gave it to me. i had to ask him again after 10 minutes and he never did. i was rather annoyed at the point. so my husband went up and asked another employee who got us 2 cups of water right away. no attitude and seemed to have a positive manner. if you have a choice, avoid this location. please note that the unisex restroom was decently clean and adequately stocked with soap and toilet paper.

i frequent this starbucks on average twice a week. it really is quite spacious and nice inside, and has tables outside as well. the normal staff is friendly, and the service is good. they quickly learn your name if you frequent the location. every now and then they get an order off (e.g. adding whip cream after asking for none), but they always make it right. having been to quite a few starbucks locations, this one definitely ranks near the top.

my favorite starbucks in town. coffee always turns out delicious even when they are busy. jennifer v. is what made me start going to this location and i'm impressed. i will drive passed other locations to go to this one.
not really much to say... i mean, it's a starbucks. thought i'd throw in a three-star because i love that they have the upstairs. pretty nice view of the shenanigans on south st.

a fairly new starbucks that's still working out the kinks but it's nice to finally have a starbucks option in the pennsport neighborhood of south philadelphia. the service is friendly and the location is convenient but just be advised it's located within a target store.

i've always been a fan of starbucks. this was my first trip to the location in new tampa. the staff was friendly and fast. the products were in line with the usual starbucks quality you've come to expect. the only issue i had was lack of seating. my visit was mid morning on a thursday and there was only 1 table left and it was right outside the bathroom. other than lack of seating, the visit was pleasant.

this is the best starbucks in reno. the staff are friendly and speedy, the space is well laid out for staying and reading, and it has a drive-thru. our running group usually crashes the place on saturday mornings, and we're always met with cheerfulness and welcome.

my first time at a starbucks reserve store. it doesn't seem any different than the regular starbucks except for the upgraded interiors and also, i think that they have more coffee/upscale coffee options which doesn't matter to me since i just drink cold brew. the staff here is extremely efficient, the line up here moves quickly so don't be deterred by the line-up. the only downside to this starbucks was that the ladies' washroom smelled horrendous.
this is "my" sbux. it's busy all of the time so the drive thru can take for.ev.er. i have never had a poor customer service experience besides the wait and i'm hear nearly every day.

worst starbucks drink i've ever had. ordered the nitro cold brew with cold foam. first off the coffee was warm. normally there's ice in my cold brew or it's extremely cold. this was unacceptable! it was warm and the "cold foam" was warm as well and very thin. just terrible. my fiancé ordered it and when i realized the disaster we didn't feel like going back in traffic to get back to the location. disappointed. spent almost $6 on one drink and can't even finish it because it's not very good at all.

having spent my life in the pnw, and last 20 years in seattle - moving to philly area and trying to enjoy starbucks does not work. this starbucks has horrible service. so slow and staff not aware of customers (only each other and their conversation) and not able to do more then one thing at a time. have been to enough starbucks in my life to know how quickly and smoothly it should work. just one person in front of me - six staff - why it takes 7 minutes is beyond me (i timed it, after spending what seemed an eternity in line to even place my order). and why they are stocking napkins and straws - and not checking the creamers (of course empty - there's another minute). sad thing, this is par for the course here in philly. haven't found any that provide the level of service i've become accustomed to in seattle. none. bad training here? or just bad people that don't care? and that's sadly why i end up at dunkin donuts or panera most of the time even though i am a self professed starbucks addict.

this starbucks is pretty clean with a couple of tables to the side. it located at a very convenient location next to hup and chop. they are pretty fast with completing orders, with 3 people working on drinks. the ambiance is pretty nice. however it's pretty busy with people coming in and out. if you are looking for a place to complete some work, this is also a good location because the tables are off to the side away from the crowd of people flowing in and out.

starbucks is always a great backup plan when your local favorites are closed or not in the area. i usually go for a grande coffee with 2 add shots. simple but great combo...my wife likes the scones and i will usually for an oatmeal with fruits and nuts. overall nice and clean place with nice and friendly staff.

well sorry to say that the staff isn't as friendly or patient as other locations. the refrigerated cases are dirty saw flies walking around it could be from the door being opened often but my suggestion would be get a closed case. coffee was good so hope to see it improve.
my go to coffee spot in isla vista. the employee were super nice and all had smiles on their faces. great place to study also! 10/10 recommend

this starbucks has a lot going for it. it's super-clean, and service is fast & friendly. there's plenty of seating, incl a few outside tables on the "deck" overlooking the pond. very nice. i know some have complained about the limited on-site parking here, but there's an easy solution, unless you are in a big hurry. if driving, as you pass the starbucks and it's small parking lot on your right, go one more block, and make a left onto penn valley drive. there is ample free parking on the street there and you're only a block away! if you can't walk a block to get your coffee, well hey, maybe you shouldn't be having that vente double mocha praline snickers bon bon double whip frappaccino, eh?

my little sister and i frequent this location and we love it. we always go through the drive thru and we have never had a problem. the people who work the drive thru are extremely polite and nice. great job to whoever does the hiring there. the coffee is delicious as it is most starbucks. quick and friendly service every time always equals a 5 star review from me! great job!

look for what it is, this starbucks is great. located on the first floor of the marriott, this coffee house serves hotel guests, office workers, city hall employees, septa employees, and tourists down to see reading terminal market or city hall. this all translates into foot traffic and high volume. short of adding a wawa style self order kiosk, this place does all it can to maintain a high level of customer service. i've seen in 4 occasions free samples offered to entertain guests waiting for their orders to come up. that said, as mentioned by other yelpers, save yourself the time if you're in a rush and order on the app. the drinks are usually served properly here. there is seating available however i wouldn't recommend planning on getting work done here. it's kind of cramped, and you're better served by visiting the starbucks on chestnut if you're planning on studying or working remotely. but if you just need a place to warm up or scratch your eyes open while catching up on emails on your phone, this venue can accommodate you. for the volume of people, these guys do a great job of staying cheery and making customers feel at home. if you want in and out service use the app.

lemme get the bad out of the way, this starbucks is no longer 24-hours. with all the other economy cuts made by many corporations, it could have been worse... but it's still a loss. the good? there's still live music nights on friday and saturday! yes, at a starbucks. the crew still rocks shop, although they have a new manager that you get the feeling is not as hip as the one who made this place what it was. many of the regulars, from germans, to italians, to asian to suburbanite christians, make for some awesome coffee conversation. the coffee, however, is still starbucks. that means get an espresso, as the talent of the barista actually makes a difference with these drinks, whereas the drip may be as bad as a fast food chain these days. hands down, it is amazing to find this place in south jersey. it's no longer the haven for those of us who work thru the nights, but it is an impressive step and an accomplishment for starbucks.
