it's cramped in here. space is limited to a couple tables and when it's busy - especially in the morning - it's a pain in the butt to navigate around everyone since there's no room for the line to form. that's one star. i won't bore you with the details, but another star taken off because one of the baristas was giving me issues with redeeming a reward. he was also busy chatting with another person while the line continued to grow longer and longer...

everyone who works at this starbucks seems to be having the worst day of their life every time i come here. i've had nothing but bad experiences every time, but i keep coming back because it's closest to my apartment. i've never had a drive thru experience where they didn't get something wrong and then get mad because i asked them to fix it. seriously, it's happened like six times and i have a very common, uncomplicated drink order.

can't live without my coffee fix, usually day and night! white mocha is my drink and they do it up right!!! i really don't like the drive thru set up at this one, but they are friendly and get my drink right.

i love this starbucks!! i travel for work, so i go to a lot of starbucks all over the us & i have to say that this location is one of my favorites! i usually order the same drink but occasionally i want something different. they are always helpful. when i redeem my rewards they are just as helpful & always polite with a smile! my latte is always perfect too! my drink is a grande triple shot latte so i know how it should taste. some locations don't get it right but this location always gets it right! however, there is one employee who is not as nice and helpful as the others. she wasn't helpful when my starbucks app was not working. not that it's her job but she acted as if it was an inconvenience when i asked her about it, even though i had my cash in hand because the app wasn't showing any money. i've had the same experience with her on several occasions.

always a pleasure to get a cool drink or some warm coffee related drink from starbucks can't go wrong when they have what you're looking for lol... i understand that all of the shortages are causing problems for their business as well as others so i can't complain.

this place is still open, the lines suck but i talked to a manager and its one of the busiest sb in the nation, so that is expected and they do such a great job for the amount of people that are in there. if i didn't have to deal with all the entitled students that think they need a drink in 5 seconds after ordering when they see a line out the door i would give it 5 stars, but the crowd is what is a downfall. but the staff and product is great. i love getting drinks and going to read in the library.

this has become my new starbucks now that i've moved. i'm into it. everyone i've met has been really friendly and chipper even though i am never there later than 6:45am. they are also great at handling long lines and getting people in and out with the correct order. very important! only complaint - whyyyyyyyy would you build a starbucks with no drive-thru??!?

ok--- i stop in several times to eat or drink coffee at starbucks before my kiddos have a game at the nearby police athletic fields. today, i had the worst sandwich (not the employees fault). starbucks has decided to make a breakfast wrap and instead of a tortilla shell, they utilize flatbread. well, when you place eggs, steak, and vegetables on flatbread--- it makes the bread soggy. i would strongly urge anyone to stay away from this sandwich. so, the two stars is for the sandwich not the place.

i came here to pick up a order that i made online but when i got there there was nothing waiting for me. the absolutely amazing barista asked me what i ordered and instead of sending me away to the other location i actually ordered, she fulfilled the order and i'm very appreciative!! thank you so much!! now i am on time. thank goodness because i accidentally ordered at a location 20 min out of my way.

i would give them 4 stars if the barista knew how to talk to strangers... i asked her a quick question, more like advice, on if a certain frappucino would be good... her reaction was as though i'd asked her to do a handstand and sing the star spangled banner. drink was great, though. dirtier environment than desired, but since it's within a target, i suppose that's forgivable to a degree.
monday morning crowd appeared to be fairly well handled by this crew... it was crowded i skipped all but the coffee as it is fast and efficient....as always they are consistent!

i only jumped on yelp to write that this is the only starbucks that has, two times in a row, given me a venti java chip with a single shot of espresso, when i asked for a venti java chip with an extra shot. bruh, whaaaat the heck.

i don't usually have coffee at starbucks but when i do, i make sure it's reserve beans on a clover machine. stay thirsty, my friend! (apparently this is the only starbucks in the state with a clover machine. google it and try it, you will be addicted. columbia beans are great and a good value)

one of the cleanest, organized, friendly and prompt starbucks i have ever stepped foot in. i was nervous to order in store due to time, but they were really fast. the quality of the food was superb. i ordered the egg sous vide and a toasted white chocolate mocha. it was all very delicious.
large dining area, quick service, friendly staff at this location. if you like starbucks, this is a good one.
the workers here are friendly and welcoming. they get your order write and make it exactly how you want it.

the absolute slowest drive-thru service one can find at any starbucks. i've spent over ten minutes waiting at the window at least three times now. i would be understanding if they said something like our computer is operating slowly, but instead i'm left sitting and waiting without any heads up as to what would be slowing down the process. the store is clean, well kept and a great place to work from if you can work remote. so two stars for that, but three down because of the drive-thru service is so bad that i don't believe starbucks as a company would be proud of it. (note that i'm not saying time in line waiting, but time spent literally just sitting at the window)
service today was horrible. the weekend staff at this store have consistently been horrible. my coffee order was correct but did not taste correct. . they were very light on the mocha.
went to the summit mall starbucks and nika has the best customer service! she had a bright smile and so accommodating! thank you !

this is a solid starbucks, but not really one of my faves given it's location. the three stars is based on location alone. it sits outside the mall, no entrance from the interior. the staff is consistently fantastic... and always makes a solid drink (and they do the flat white like it is supposed to be done.) it has a lot of seating which is nice and a little outdoor seating area as well.

the good: it's right near campus so i was expecting a little bit of a wait. they handled 15 customers in less than 10 minutes which was terrific :) the bad: i ordered the breakfast sandwhiches with bacon and it unfortunately was cold in the middle :( i would expect such a large chain to have things figured out with regards to heating times. what's more disappointing is that it's not the 1st time but rather the 3rd :/

pros- they had good service and free samples ( i had a chili latte of some sort that was quite interesting) cons- the parking is difficult and limited. also, it is basically located a few yards from a busy highway on ramp, so getting out of the lot into traffic is a little busy at times. i just went out the exit towards the local roads to avoid this problem.

i'm not going to write a review based on the starbucks products, because i don't know anyone who hasn't tried them for themselves. so i will just review this location. if it were just starbucks coffee: fast, fresh, in my mouth immediately i would give it 5 stars because i love that stuff. the mark down of 1 star is due to the speed: i go through the drive thru on a regular basis. when i go i see maybe a car or two ahead of me and have never waited less than ten minutes. i even got to the front a couple of times and saw through the window they had one person taking orders from both the drive thru and the in store guests.... seriously? the other star is for the weird experiences i have had inside. i have been inside only three times and every time i have heard the manager/supervisor whatever she is, yelling at the employees. she could just be a loud person, but it's a small place so really no excuses for this. i feel like i saw all of the patrons kind of pause in their coffee sipping and just look around when she started yelling. i live in the north valleys and prefer to go to the lemmon valley starbucks any time i can.

if you go on a cold day, prepare to drink your latte 'iced'! everyone around me is trying to bundle up to keep from shaking. the seating is also limited for such a busy location. if you have anything more than a very brief meeting, find another cafe'. everything else is your typical starbucks cookie cutter experience.

the employees at this location are a disgrace to the starbucks brand. i've been to countless starbucks locations and the employees are angels. nope not at this one. i walked up to place my order and the cashier said, "yes??" uhhhh okay sorry am i bothering you, miss? made me feel so uncomfortable to order. the moment i started to order she walked away to do something else and then came back with a miserable smirk on her face. in addition to all of this wonderful stuff, the prices are significantly higher than other locations? not sure if the cashier charged me wrong but this was an awful experience. won't be coming back to this specific location.

no drive thru here, but that's okay, what this location lacks for in size and convenience is surely made up for with the amazing staff. i came in on a sunday morning to get my caffeine crave; and this location didn't miss a beat as it relates to service and the quality to come to expect from starbuckies. this is truly your friendly neighborhood starbucks. note: parking is a bit of a a tight squeeze and there is plenty of customer seating outside. cheers,

this is about equidistant to the one on route 70 by barclay towers for me. i like this location over route 70 because it's really easy to get in and out of and the parking isn't too bad for a starbucks. there's no drive through but i never use those, this may be a drawback for others though. they fill mobile orders pretty quickly. unfortunately, this time the order was not what i paid for. i ordered a triple espresso, which means there should be 3 times the amount of espresso relative to the amount found in a single shot. as you can see, i paid for a triple and still got a single shot anyway. on top of this, my boo's iced caramel macchiato got the opposite treatment, it was overfilled! after i put the straw in and out a few times and sopped up all the overflowing sticky "coffee" (i say that in jest as i don't believe these drinks count as coffee), it was dry enough to bring out to the car. i complained to starbucks online team and they did credit my account $6, which is approximately what i paid for everything. when you come after 3 pm, they have a promo where you buy one espresso drink and you get a second free. that deal, along with the credit, took away the sting of what was not the most pleasant starbucks experience this time around. they'll get another chance because after all, it's starbucks and there is no locally owned cafe i know of in cherry hill.

love the regular staff here ... always pleasant and rarely have to wait for my morning coffee. i've been to some starbucks that were not up to snuff, but this one always brews the coffee just right. the early morning crew is a+++++. great location, parking abounds and there's always the philly paper or nyt to read on the big, comfy couch.
best starbucks crew in the area. always friendly with a smile even when busy. too bad they are temporarily closed for a few days. go to the rt. 70 barclay farm sbs. there are nice there too.

why such a low rating? location, location, location. each knocks off one star. it's a real shame that there are no cafes or coffee shops in kennett square that are open past 2 on sunday afternoon: that's the only reason i headed out to this corporate chain situated in an ugly, suburban strip mall. i feel dirty spending my money in a place like this, like i'm playing a role in wrecking the structure of american society. that said, it really wasn't that bad. starbucks is starbucks. i had a decent cup of tea and a tasty, but overpriced baked good. that's par for the course. staff were friendly, and the atmosphere was tolerable. i found the christmas themed decorations a little bit lame, and this atmosphere is a bit colder and less cozy than some other starbucks i've been in, but they're all more or less similar. get it together kennett square merchants...you gotta have something open in the downtown every day of the week. when i'm in town, i'll go there, guaranteed. maybe the mexican bakery (which was open) could put some tables out; they sure have room for it in their large storefront: i'd rather give them my money than a starbucks in some butt-ugly suburban shopping plaza that's just enriching some sleazy developer.

i understand this is a busy location, like many others in the area. but the wait time is uncalled for. the staff is always nice, which is why i gave it 3 stars. if this was based on time it'd be a 1. i go out of my way to the park st. location so i won't be late to work! put it this way, if you're looking for a cool place to chat/lounge/study with great customer service, perfect. but if you are in a hurry, try to find another location.

i know you never expect good service in an airport, but this was terrible! the staff were unprofessional arguing with each other and then talking poorly about the customer in front of us while she was standing right there.

we have lived in 6 different states in the last 10 years, and this is our 2nd time in o'fallon. this drive thru by far is the slowest out of all the starbucks we've been to. hoping it would have improved since 2015 but it hasn't. took over twenty minutes to receive our mobile ordered drink. why is this location so bad?!?

this is my favorite starbucks around butler university's campus radius. the baristas are always so friendly and are great at making suggestions. the line typically isn't long and they accept online orders so your stuff is ready when you get there. great neighborhood starbucks.

so straight off i'm going to say i'm not a big fan of starbucks. i really only end up coming here with my wife when she is in need of a coffee fix. this is a pretty standard starbucks. the one thing to note is that the location is a bit tricky to get in and out of. there isn't much parking, and brentwood boulevard can get quite congested. i would recommend only coming here in off hours. if you come during peak times you make get a bit frustrated by the traffic. since it is a small shop, it is also better to take the drinks and go. i would not recommend planning to do any work or reading here. it works best as a to-go spot.

beautiful store. but no priority to mobile orders & reward members. frustrating when you are in a rush to be somewhere and still have to wait as long as everyone else whom did not preorder their drinks. this is an issue in my opinion and i will plan on using another location because of this. do love the coffee though
truly the nicest baristas ever. i love going to this location whenever i need a liquid pick me up and a snack. gorgeous decor inside. always clean.
please redesign this location drive thur parking is awful. also order a cappuccino and it barely full, when i specified fat free milk & sugar free syrup. so what happened to three quarters or so??

staff worked slowly and managed their workflow poorly. they took several orders from different customers and started making them all, but they waited to give the orders until they completed everyone's. the cashier took our order and my wife wanted to change the size of her drink. the cashier became irritated with us for changing. this would have been understandable if we had paid already but all she had to do was change the order, but she was clearly bothered by this extra work we demanded of her. lots of untrained and inefficient staff with poor customer interactions.

it's a starbucks with a drive thru which is nice and they have a nice seating area inside as well. the downside is that the parking lot is very cramped and tough to find parking and the service is disgustingly slow. on a number of occasions i have waited for 20 minutes to get my drink and then they even made it wrong. if they figure out the service issues i would rate this a 4 or 5 stars.
going going gone! sorry to see this starbucks close. it did not have the largest layout but it was a great place to answer emails over a cup o' joe.

this location is pretty nice. narrow entrance so the line formation can be sloppy but the seating is pretty scattered. the window seats is a must; you get to enjoy people watching lol. there is an upstairs seating area but that's mainly for students anyway.

this is my favorite location! i love adam, ana lee, jasmine, and several others...i'm really bad with names and have a hard time remembering all. but pretty much all of them know me by name lol :) they are all awesome! drinks are always on point, no matter how crazy mine get!! they are real and are not fake. and handle rude people really well for what i've observed!

slowest starbucks i've ever been to! and i visit at least one daily, no matter where i am. the wait was ridiculous every time we went, regardless of if there were only three other people in there. went to this one more than i checked in, and never waited less than 20 minutes. with pretty much no other customers. this one was closest on the drive back to our condo after a days excursion, so we still went there, but i would have chosen another if it would have been an option. probably won't be back for the wait alone.
ordered puppacinos for two puppies, along with a flat white for me. dogs enjoyed theirs a whole lot. thanks!!

this is a great starbucks. the staff is wonderful. plus its less than a mile from our house! this is just to give another shout out to the wonderful staff at this starbucks. you are so nice and helpful and appreciated!

this starbucks is consistently great. i registered a gift card online, so i get free soy and free syrup. if you get a tall misto, your total is somewhere around $2 and it tastes just as good as a latte. there are definitely ways to save at starbucks. a few weeks ago, they had a buy one get one free holiday drink promotion. anyway, my drinks are always consistently the right temperature, and every drink tastes like it is made from an experienced barista. if you ask for a triple-shot venti peppermint white mocha and a scone, your order is going to be around $8, so be careful! but the staff has always been friendly to me, and most of the time i am in and out within five minutes.

obviously, this has all the same offerings as a regular starbucks. but, because it's in a penn building, there are some pros/cons: pros: - lots of seats and study space - nice outdoor seating options - dining dollars from penn meal plans can be used - convenient campus location cons: - not allowed to use mobile app for payment - they don't accept any starbucks deals or coupons (however, they do participate in some campaigns such as frapp happy hour.) - few outlets for laptops despite large study space - gets busy/full during school year - staff can be rude/sassy, atypical of starbucks (you can't even get a glass of water)

the parking lot is a joke might as well park somewhere else and walk over. the drive thru takes forever and block in all parked cars. plus they're always out of stuff. come on starbucks add another location so this isn't my only choice
i get starbucks everyday.... friendly staff most of the time quick service drink is usually made to perfection just the way like it with mobile ordering a added bonus

slowest starbucks i believe in philly. they are in dire need of understanding time/order management and to understand if they have product in stock. service is slow and all over the place. i would recommend doing a mobile order to save time.

came to this starbucks and i will definitely be coming back. i frequent starbucks in different cities vet often. i love that they have a larger patio to sit outside, a drive thru, and a very convenient location to easily get in and out. ample parking as well. the service could of been quicker on a not too busy saturday mid-afternoon. but i didn't mind waiting a little bit longer. but my dog thoroughly enjoyed the puppychino from the barista and meeting new customers that walked by. i will be coming back soon! great experience here at his location

my service was fine but there's a pet store next to it causing the lobby to smell like dog. no one seemed to have a problem with the smell but i don't have a dog which made me nauseous to the smell. again service was fine.
the drive thru trash can is back after 3 years!! the drive thru moves a little slower these days but the consistency of the coffee has improved. i know this was a concerted effort on starbucks part.

worst starbucks location i've been to so far. i tried it 3 days in a row. different people working each day. each day the people here seemed borderline hostile. the coffee was awful on top of it and not what i typically get at a starbucks. next time if i have a choice between this place and a plus, i'll go to a plus.
could not accommodate my wife and me at holiday time despite the long drive-thru line.

this is the starbucks near me, i visit it often!!! really crazy i don't drink coffee. i'm a fan of the strawberry refresher with lemonade no water, i enjoy the protein boxes, i love cookie crumble frapp, and list of other things!!!! chris the drive thru person with the wonderful accent is always so nice!!!

typical starbucks, big and roomy, always a place to sit... plenty of parking too. however, they rarely get my drink correct. and it seems to be happening more and more often, and it's making me not want to go there anymore.

this is the starbucks inside of target. they were rude and forgot to put the caramel in my caramel iced coffee, when i asked if they wouldn't mind putting it in (hence, it's what i ordered) the girl making the drinks got an attitude. it's not been the first time.. so here i go... *posts review*

counterintuitive to logic, parking and going inside when there is a long drive thru will not save you time on getting a cup of coffee...at least the third or fourth time i have gone inside with only two people in front of me and they forgot to pour my coffee. i waited patiently and nothing. finally had to ask barista where my coffee was. hey corporate coffee take a clue from mcdonalds and get your systems in check. this location hit or miss for service with a smile
same as any other starbucks. the service is very pleasant here. the atmosphere is comfy and clean

this is the best starbucks closest to me, unfortunately. my drinks are always consistent but when i pull up to the drive thru, which is quite often to pick up my mobile order, i cringe when the older gentlemen answers the drive thru order. today i receive my third lecture from him on how to properly order my drinks on the mobile. today's lecture was longer than normal because apparently there was a situation with order prior. so i had to yet again wait for my coffee and be lectured by this man! i thought i left my father in ohio, clearly not!! note to all: if you don't properly order your mobile order, be prepared to be lectured! dad works here!!

i have a confession to make. i like starbucks. i mean, i do prefer unique, independently owned cafés. still, if i see the starbucks logo on the horizon, i usually "need" coffee in a classically conditioned response. the thing i like about them: you can typically count on a consistent drink. it tastes the same from coast to coast. however, i stopped at this location for a quick caffeine boost before taking my kiddo to play. the service was just not friendly. the cashier's eyebrow was lifted because my sister took an extra minute to decide on a replacement drink when her initial request was not available. the cashier was very impolite. there were probably 6-8 other employees working. they were also unfriendly...and slow. our drinks took longer than usual despite the lack of any other customers. unfortunately, when they were completed, they were not made correctly. we debated whether or not to return to the counter, and decided that having paid for a "specialty drink," it was worth a replacement or refund. long story a little less-long, they were extremely rude about that too. i'll avoid this location in the future.

rude!! absolutely the worst customer service. i am a former barista and shift supervisor. i understand the rules and that you can never turn a customer away. customer infront of me ordered her breakfast and drink and she only had a $50 bill...technically were not allowed to break it, however as a former shift manager i know for a fact it can be done instead he tells the lady sorry i can't help you then turns to the barista and yells stop making the drink she doesn't have the money to pay....yes she did! how humiliating! i'm up next to order and they were so unorganized. i had multiple gift cards on my phone to use up one with .06 another with 2.34 and lastly a new one with 10.00. he was so impatient and asked me if i had enough to cover my drink...yes sir i do like i stated i have a couple gift cards i'd like to use up first. next, my drink was made wrong....and when i told the barista she grabbed my drink and slammed it into the trash. when she gave me my corrected drink she was not nice about it. only starbucks near my work...

another consistent starbucks. the girl at the register was very friendly which i don't see as much at airports so thank you. i love that no matter where i am i can count on the consistency of starbucks.

your water filters needs to be replaced. i have had cold-brew black on two occasions. out of a several month period. both did not taste good. then tried your water, from the filter. it tastes like typical philadelphia tap water. not very good. you know that taste. get your filter cleaned/changed, etc. filtered water is essential for good tasting coffee. coffee is 98% water. i imagine other drinks may not be affected as much. but certainly your hot coffee that you guys brew in mass amounts behind the cashier probably would. frappachinos, caffe macchiatos, drinks like that probably would be to overpowered by the other flavors in there to notice. or you may not notice if you add flavors or sugar to your drip coffee. i have had better cold-brew from other locations. it was black, nothing added. their water tasted fine. i would recommend getting on this asap. friendly staff, little slow. not too bad in regards to experience.

the cashier had an attitude and got my order wrong. the drinks i got weren't made right. the mocha frappuccino tasted like pure milk and sugar somehow and my strawbery acai tasted like the water that was used to wash strawberries. i love starbucks but this is not good

ran through the drive through and ordered a cappuccino with an extra shot. the guy taking the order was efficient, but friendly enough. the cappuccino was piping hot and the milk was flawlessly steamed without being scorched. the cup was filled the rest of the way with a nice, frothy top that was perfect. nice, quick customer service, well made drinks, and a covered, but open patio for warmer weather would bring me back to this location.

ordered my drink via the starbucks app and waited for maybe 3 minutes inside at the mobile order pick up spot (maybe 7 minutes since ordering) before someone asked if i had ordered online and what i got. turns out something glitches in the system and they didn't get my order until just a minute or so before they asked me what i was waiting on (so while i had been standing there waiting). they quickly started working on my order and got it out within a minute or so. wasn't their fault their machine didn't spit out the order in a timely manner, but i ended up spending 10 minutes waiting for my drink when the store/drive through were practically empty. staff were ok but seemed frazzled with 4-5 of them behind the counter and very little going on in the store.

i love this location. i grab my caramel macchiato and head to my favorite spot, the long couch in the back, the only drawback is there isn't always a table available when needed. hey it's kinda expected because of location though. the staff has always been friendly and the bathrooms pretty clean. quick fyi: when trying to sit and study at times there music is pretty loud so if you need/prefer quiet this may not be your spot.

standard s'bucks product and service, with a friendly staff. lot (and different kinds!) of seating that will really look great once covid stuff is over with. definitely one of my favorite s'bucks interiors to date.

i came to this side of the mall on my way out & ordered my drink, kind of taking a chance bc i have a particular starbucks that i go to. skyler made my drink & it was amazing! that's aside from his pleasant attitude and big smile. he was friendly and definitely had me coming back the next day for him to make my drink!
the whole point of using a personal cup is so that i'm not generating waste by getting a disposable cup. you are starbucks, figure out a better way to measure!!!!
typical starbucks coffees and treats. what makes this store stand out is the terrible upkeep of the restrooms. disgusting!
very snobby barista, god forbid you ask for your drink to be made properly and/or ask for a condiment that is empty...maybe her nose piercing is too tight

i come here once in awhile when i'm in that area but, when i do. i get greeted by my name and the only two are patrick and stephanie i believe her name is pretty tall red head. both are very pleasant know my favorite drink and just very friendly. i love starbucks and these people work hard making all the different flavors of coffee and tea. i normally come afternoon to late afternoon. i really never get a bad drink it just took some time to know me and what i oder. thanks keep up the great work!

i like the set up of this starbucks and it's usually full of people reading, studying, or chatting. it's location is convenient in that it's near the green hills mall and shopping areas, but beware of the traffic to and from the place! use the light to get out if you need to turn left. service: speed (good), quality (good) taste: green tea frap (really tasty)
consistent starbucks food and great staff. a great place to have coffee and watch the neighborhood regulars. come here every morning when i am in philadelphia.

summary: run-of-the-mill starbucks with great baristas! i usually stop by here a couple times a week on my way to work or on my way home. it's right next to the station, the baristas are very good at their job, and the wait never goes longer than 2-3 minutes. i'm definitely willing to wait 2-3 minutes for my drinks. the only reason that i would ever not go to this place is if i was in the mood for regular black coffee. if you're in this area, check out the little coffee shop on the inbound platform (going towards philadelphia). tip: this place is open pretty late, but make sure to not miss the last train back to the city while chillin' and talking to the baristas (been there, done that). it ain't fun (or cheap).

they sold me not one, but two moldy protein boxes and that's supposed to be a mango dragonfruit drink. "we're out of the dragon fruit but it's just the same stuff." server said. but if i'm paying for a $5 drink, i want the fruit. and i want my overpriced apples to be edible.

one of my absolute favourite starbucks locations in edmonton. staff is well trained and always carry themselves professionally. drive thru is fast, efficient and is great for me as i am usually grabbing and running. starbucks has some amazing products and this locations provides fast, friendly and genuine service.

anyone who has read my reviews knows that i'm kind of a starbucks junkie lol so it's only right that i visited the starbucks located in this hotel that i was staying in for a few days. i went there twice and the service was slow both times. there were several people working behind the counter and i wouldn't quite figure out why things were moving in slow motion. but on the up side, being that it was located in the hotel, it was very convenient and each time my caramel macchiato was right on point so i guess it was worth the wait

i've never written a review before but my experience at a drive through in this location was excellent! the representative yadisha was super helpful and even explain throughly the details of how to order the drink my husband and i enjoy to ensure i don't have any question the next time. thank you yadisha!!

a starbucks without cold brew is like mac without cheese; senseless! dropped in this morning for a cool drink. i waited at the counter unattended for nearly 5 minutes while the only visible employee made drinks. he glanced over after a while and said he'd be right with me. i continued to wait... wait... wait... i was the only customer in line by the way. eventually a young lady appeared out of the back and asked what she could get for me. when i asked for a cold brew she stated they were out which blew my mind. how on earth does starbucks not have cold brew on-hand at 10am???? this is a clear cut case of poor store management. i won't be returning to this location no matter how desperate i may be for my caffeine fix; this says a lot about my level of disappointment.
loud blaring music at 9am. i feel like i'm in a nightclub. none of the customers like it. but the employees are having a good time.

how on earth does a place that is so slow stay in business? average wait time in the drive through line is 21 minutes. and not to mention that these slow pokes cause the lines to run out into the road and jam up traffic every morning! just wait. all we need is one other coffee shop to open up, and no one will continue to come here.

this is one of my favorite starbucks locations - it's large, has ample indoor & outdoor seating, generally good customer service and delicious coffee. i like that they always have nice displays of their coffee cups and other merchandise you can buy - i have purchased a few gifts for fellow coffee lovers here! good place to study - with headphones on - and always love the free wifi!

starbucks is my go-to place to work and kill some time for really one reason: free refills on tea and coffee (for members). this location is the only starbucks in the area, but it is definitely not my favorite in tampa. they are always crowded, never feel like they have enough seats or tables. the outdoor area feels like it is sitting in the parking lot, and about half the time in the afternoons they seem to be out of something...sometimes more than one. they are super nice here, and the problems aren't enough that i don't come when i'm in the area--but if i'm heading elsewhere, i would far rather be at the kennedy starbucks. this one meets my caffeine needs, but it isn't the most relaxing of locations!

always tend to stop here every tie i am on my way to work and i haven't had a bad experience i guess that why i am the duke of this great sb location!!!!! even at 5ish in the morning the staff is bright and cheery going that extra mile to make that first cup of java in the morning just right. i look forward every morning to know that they get my morning off to a great start.

the line in this store is in an interesting spot. no big deal, i just thought it was awkward. the first thing i noticed was the lobby, it was huge. i've never seen so many tables and seats in a starbucks. i like it! i ordered my drink as a barista stocked the cold case. i remember saying, "excuse me" when i was loading the case and needed to get by customers but that was lost here. the barista who took my order was friendly and nice, as was the one who made my drink.

i stopped here june 24 while heading to my gate. the line was super long. when i finally got to the counter, there was only 1 register being used. omg!!!! i figured, ok my caramel waffle cone frappuccino would be worth the wait. not!!! it took a while for them to make it because i believe they didn't know how. it was watery; hardly any flavor. surprised!!!! i never experienced that at a starbucks. very disappointed. i will not go back. if the line wasn't so long i would have returned it; that's how horrible it was.
the location is convenient and in the morning the outside area is nicely shaded. service is okay.

it's always busy but there's lots of comfortable seating the baristas do the best they can but it's the only coffee shop in the busy grand sierra casino. it's a great place to work because of all the comfortable seating and you get to watch people play slots.

it is always something at this starbucks. yesterday and today it was a lecture about requesting a straw for my drink. they have started putting sippy cup lids on the drinks. the lecture has something to do with saving the planet. the girl that told me this yesterday was so rude and awful that i shouldn't have gone back today. i did. my mistake. got the same lecture in a gentler tone. i find it hard to believe they are trying to save sea life when they are still using plastic cups and even bigger plastic lids. not to mention the straws come wrapped in paper and my mouth is only touching something i touched not 2-5 starbucks employees.

this starbucks takes forever to serve people and get them through the drive thru. 10-15 minutes is the amount of time i'm usually sitting there. needless to say i no longer frequent this location like i used to. it's faster and easier to order on the mobile app and just go to the closest store and walk in and walk right back out.

this location is very hit or miss! you'll either drink the best coffee you've had in your life or drink a cup of bitter funk! the only way you can really guarantee your coffee will be drinkable is if you order something that's loaded with sugar (which is gross anyway). other than that, this location isn't the most consistent!
really good coffee and food but the drive thru workers are always rude and they always get our order wrong.

friendly staff and i often will stop here before work and get a coffee and it is my go to location if i need to pick up a gift card. staff has always been super friendly and nice to me. there is a decent amount of space if you want to stay in their lobby or on patio to work or read. the only downside can be the drive thru line - not the service - the customers, and maybe the person that designed the parking lot. the cars always end up blocking the road with a long line into the drive thru during peak times. no fault of the staff - these are the folks heading into the offices that are in the intech park area with morning rush and group coffee orders. during these peak morning rush times i avoid the drive thru and order inside.

these guys are fantastic - extremely busy, but always friendly and willing to go the extra mile. today they remade the hideous mistake of a latte i'd had the misfortune to receive at a different location. wish i'd gotten the manager's name... but thank you, cp mall starbucks! you made (or re-made) my day!!

one of the better starbucks locations. i walk there several times a week so my review is based on multiple visits...not a single incident as many reviews are. the staff are great...they work as a team and seem to enjoy being there. after a few regular visits i was surprised and pleased when my order was already in progress even before i placed it. good job guys...keep it up!

i needed a late afternoon fix and slipped into this store because it is the closest to friends with whom i hoped to connect for dinner. there was an unbelievable downpour and it seemed prudent to get off the road and "just chill!" this starbucks is like hundreds i've been to all over the country -- cozy, modern, busy and predictably, friendly. i didn't experience anything above and beyond and i didn't observe anything starbucks wouldn't be proud of... what i saw was exactly what starbucks set out to create: a comfortable refuge from the rest of the world where you can get a good cuppa joe, maybe something to nosh, and either get stuff done or "just chill" with (or without) friends.
they always make my drink perfect! my moms on the other hand, it takes the right person to beagle to make an extra dry cappuccino, but this store usually knows exactly the way she likes it!!!
after dark, starbucks corporate's new policies have turned this place into the mos eisley catina .... (look it up) my kinda place ...

i like starbucks, however, this location is one of my least favorite, due to the parking lot/drive thru situation. however, i was in the area and too early to show up at a meeting, so i stopped in. the drive thru took 13 minutes from when i pulled in until when i pulled out. i would give that a 1 star. i am giving 4 starts to this location because of the employees. each one i saw/spoke to (i was in the drive thru line) was pleasant and friendly. they all seemed happy to be there and like they truly liked what they're doing.

already reviewed. i guess there's two at the same location. but i do love this location... mainly because there's a sunroom, a huge patio, and my big pet peeve is finally being heard by some of the coffee shops and that is: give me some comfortable chairs! not everybody comes by themselves to study or get on their laptop. some people want to visit and sit on a comfortable chair that doesn't hurt their bones! thank you!

i went in today for a pick me up in the afternoon. i got an i sweet tea no water added so very dark. after the ice melted and i was towards the bottom...i noticed the huge black dead fly. my cup had a lid and i was inside my home. i'll never visit starbucks again.

open 24 hours unlike to locations on kennedy and westshore, but the parking is extremely limited and you're usually parking on a side street. staff have always been nice to me, and this location is the most convenient to my home in vm ybor and it's relatively quick and easy access off i-275.

i visit here very often since it's the nearest starbucks to my place. parking is usually easy after 10am and it's free. the best part of this starbucks is they have great customer service. some staff members are super friendly, like joseph and curtis, it's always a pleasure and heart-warming to have their service. however, every once for a while, they will have few new faces who serve people with cold faces and rarely respond or reaffirm to customer's saying. that really make big differences comparing with my regular experience there. i wish those staff can pick up the basic etiquette to work with customers more since i wish to feel like coming home in my neighborhood. the ratio of chance for me having cold interaction is 1 out of 5, so i give them 4 stars.

probably the worst java chip frappe i've ever had, i am still currently sitting here and trying to sip it down, but like their coffee usually is, it tastes incredibly burnt, and it's over syruped. but mostly, i can't get over this burnt coffee taste, and as i sit here writing this, the entire coffee shop has the "something is burning!" odor, and we are leaving soon. my husband and i ordered a tall (12 oz. these size names, still are so ridiculous) java chip frappuccino, a venti (20 oz.) cold brew, an old fashion donut, an apple fritter donut, and a tiny oatmeal. i've had a few bites and a few sips of my apple fritter & jcf, i am already struck with a nauseas wave & syrupy burnt taste in my mouth and my wallet is about $20 lighter. i instantly regret coming here instead of black crow coffee in st. pete, but we needed something in the tampa area quick. alas, we are still in search of a decent coffee place up in the tampa bay area. laura served us and she was helpful, no notorious starbuck 'tude, friendly service on this late tuesday morning. we wished we could have just paid after we ordered but she didn't tell us the total & take our card until after everything was made, warmed up, and served, but we were holding a lot of items and even though it's a small detail... we wanted to just pay after we ordered and sit down to wait for our names to be hollared out like normal. we almost left due to lack of parking, there are barely a few people in here but the lot was completely full. still searching for a better place, but it's hard to come by in this area, so we settled for this starbucks. meh, me thinks not for next time.

let me start off by saying this place is very very busy. you will need to bring your patience with you. they are understaffed, not the fault of the workers. hopefully someone will see this and do something about it. waited 30 minutes in line for coffee. yes that's a long time. they need more staff. the staff they have for the volume of people they get doesn't match. staff is amazing and friendly(with the exception of the two ladies that just left when their teammates needed them the most...smh. we watched them just walk away and never communicate to their teammates where they were going). we have been at the hotel for three days now and they work non stop alllllll day! probably the hardest working folks i've seen at a starbucks! these lines are crazy. their little shop is clean and organized. people walk away happy with their coffee and no one in line has complained about the long wait. i hope these folks are getting some amazing stock options. someone needs to acknowledge this store and staff. i can't say that enough. special shout out to gracie that was making all these drinks alone and rachel for holding down the front and orders. the other two chicks...well they would be better off counting stars for nasa. i feel sooo bad for these two girls. starbucks, time for a secret shopper at this place!

there are several starbucks in the airport but i am referring to the one located outside the us airways gates. i was treated to rude and disrespectful service. i had a reward on my card and attempted to use it, when the cashier didn't know how to work my phone, she gave it to another worker, who took my phone and attempted to enter my card into the machine. she turned me away immediately and told me there was no money on my card, umm lady i have 5 different cards you just happened to enter the wrong one. she gave me this look at service as if i was not good enough and after a red eye i really wasn't having it especially since i did not get any coffee, because i refuse to buy coffee from such rude service, hms host will be hearing from me.

if you can get a parking spot, this is a pretty decent starbucks. the one at kroger isn't that great and the green hills starbucks is a zoo 24/7. the line moves relatively quickly and my drinks seem to come out great.

2/12/19 - so disappointed to hear that our wonderful asst manager eddie at the arizona pavilions location 1/11/19 - very disappointed that they didn't have my favorite sandwich today, chicken and artichoke 11/20/18 - so disappointed today. not one sandwich in the cooler at 11:15 am on once again we are here. our favorite place. love our baristas. jessica, treigh, caleigh, alex and taylor. if i've forgotten anyone please forgive me. so many great people work at this location. :) and of co

can't believe that i haven't taken the time to write a 5-star review about this location thus far; that is, it would have been a 5-star review up until my latest visit last weekend. let's first talk about the 2-ton elephant that i just plopped into the middle of the room: this used to have about 6 super-comfy chairs in which to come here to relax and drink in. the seating really set a wonderful tone as the awesome corner neighborhood coffee shop, in this quiet section of rittenhouse. but the comfy seats are truly gone, and replaced with the typical wooden chairs and tables. granted, there is a bit more seating for this busy location now; but this quaint neighborhood spot just became a bit more sterilized. so there's that. now on to the great things about this location: it's still a great neighborhood coffee shop for this quiet section of rittenhouse, with ample (now more) seating, has a great open atmosphere, it's one of the very few area locations open through 10pm, and it may well have the coolest baristas in town - they've been the nicest, down-to-earth gals around (i'm sure there's guys too, but i haven't seen any thus far). so this is still my go-to location for when i want to get out into my neighborhood; but it's now less cozy than it used to be, is all.

i went to and still live by galena high school, so i've been to this starbucks a lot. depending on the time of day you visit this starbucks, they can be very fast, or sort of slow. in the mornings you have a 60% chance of getting in and out of the drive thru quick, and in the afternoons/lunch time like, 65%. but, their new employees make it worth it. they are always so sweet and courteous, you can't be mad. i've become a regular there, so i might be bias, but they hardly ever get my drink wrong (partially due to the new employees, it used to be really bad) and i can always count on them to be smiley and polite.

sooooooo slow. i thought the couple times i had come in they were just short staffed or busy but no, they are consistently slow. if you're ever in a rush don't go to this location, and if you do expect to wait 15-20 minutes for your drink.
it's decent enough, overpriced and sometimes the drinks are great and sometimes just subpar.

great location! clean, well lit and lots of tables and seats to work. the workers are friendly and professional. they had no problem making me a new drink when something was wrong. the parking lot is a little tight but not a huge issue.
love my local starbucks! friendly service and pretty consistent in terms of quality. i recommend their refreshers and bagels! thanks so much for making my mornings better!

this is the first time i have visited this location. when we entered at 12 noon, there were 3 'barristas'' talking loudly together. they took our order and delivered it ok but when we went to the condiments table, all of the carafes were empty. we brought them to the counter where they refiled them and handed them back to us for us to replenish the table with instead of making the effort to bring them out themselves. it was extremely noisy in the shop even though there were never more than 2 or 3 other customers besides ourselves there at one time. but the noise level rose even further when one of the barristas decided to rearrange the chairs and instead of picking them up and moving them, she dragged each one of the five chairs across the floor to it's proper place. we remarked that dragging was really noisy and asked her to please pick the chairs up and offered to do it for her but she continued to drag them. whereupon, we took our coffee and moved to an outdoor table which was dirty with the remains of previous customers. when we took our trash back in before leaving, the three of them were still engrossed in lively conversation with each other.
so much better inside than the old location. staff is always friendly, and drive thru is the fastest i've ever been to

mobile ordered 5 minutes before i arrived. five minutes in store and my drinks aren't ready. nobody else is in the store waiting for drinks, only drive through. not what i would expect when mobile ordering!

i know, this is just another old starbucks however, the staff here is amazing! super nice folks and very friendly. not sure if the holiday season was affecting them but they were great! olivia was making samples and asked which drink sounded best to try. i opted for the peppermint mocha. long story short, the staff here is awesome. you will enjoy talking with them.

this starbucks does a great job of getting you out the door quickly with the resources they have. it's quite a bit faster than most starbucks that i frequent, and i've come here when there was a line of 3 or 4 people waiting for their drinks. don't plan to sit and work for 8 hours, the location is very tiny and there are 2 tables in the whole place. also, the bathroom is locked, so don't plan on stopping in to use the bathroom, paying customers only! probably the most efficient starbucks i've visited!
ordered a iced white chocolate moca just wanted to try something new.the drivethrough was fast and the order taker was friendly.

i have a good friend who works downtown and when we want to get together for a quick coffee over his lunch break this has been our spot for about a year now. unlike alot of places downtown it boasts a good sized free parking lot making it easy to get in and out of. naturally however when you have a good location it draws a crowd and this one can get quite hectic especially in the mornings and at lunch time. seating is limited at this location (probably one of the smallest stand alone stores i have seen) which i don't think makes alot of sense for the amount of business they do. it's still a good location though,no matter how busy they get i have always gotten my drink quickly. drinks are pretty standard no matter which starbucks you go to and i have no issues with quality here. i come back here due to the warm personal attachment i get from being able to meet with a good friend however there are so many coffee shops downtown you wont miss out if you never make your way down to this one but there is no need to go running and screaming in the other direction either. just your everyday run of the mill three star experience.

ok, as a long time starbucks fan, i am a bit bias. boise is where i began my addiction to coffee, and this "open late" starbucks definitely had a hand. the baristas here are nice, and this location has a drive thru. lately they re-decorated for bsu studiers, with more plugs and lone seats. i personally miss the comfy chairs, but am happy it is open late.

although i find the adjoining space next to old national bank a bit peculiar, it definitely helps to open up the space and provides more seating options. just seems like a security risk having a bank wide open and exposed like that but that's just the "what if" analyst in me. nice spot to get some work done and it wasn't as noisy as other locations i've been to. but what gives about the limited hours the blonde roast is available? is this a morning coffee only? menu is a bit misleading i must say :/ kinda like jimmy johns still listing sprouts on the turkey tom 'which. false advertising but i digress. caffeine + wifi= productivity!

i love this starbucks in the heart of westchase. the staff is always very friendly and the ambiance really gives you a feel of the neighborhood. the outdoor seating area is pet-friendly so i often see owners with the cute dogs outside mingling on the weekends. there is parking within the complex and it's also friendly for those jogging or exercising in the area.
this starbucks was ok. small, cramped and people lounge everywhere so there was no where to sit. the staff seemed gossipy and distracted. not my favorite one! i will stop here on an as needed basis.
this location deserves 5 stars, but it is always so busy that it takes 15 minutes to get your order. the baristas are always fantastic and on point so if you can spare the time, treat yo self!

this starbucks is located inside of the sheraton new orleans. if your in town for a convention its a great spot to grab coffee and a snack. the downside is they are very slow at making drinks and the line usually has at least 15 people in it. i am not exaggerating. if your not staying at the hotel i would suggest you go down the street to pj's coffee for a quick morning pickup. i've been going to this starbucks for three years because i've attend a conference there all three years. this starbucks is here strictly for convenience.

so far so good at this bucks. i was so excited to see another one with a drive thru...right next door to my dentist...it'll be hard to say yes to fluoride knowing that i'll want a latte or a peach green tea lemonade right after my cleaning. now i have a starbucks in every direction that i leave my home. this can't be good for me! so, you know how they say sometimes you can go to a starbucks and someone in front of you anonymously pays for your order? it happened to me for the first time at this one! it really made my day, so i ended up paying for the dude behind me to keep it going. what was cool is i was trying the everything croissant for the first time (and it was just as good as it sounds) so my order along with my go to drink (toffee nut mocha) was a little more than my normal spend of no more than $5. i've only been in the drive thru, but already my service has been fantastic. i can't wait to go spend a little time inside when i'm not running around as it looks like a big one.

even though starbucks is a chain it is still often a good environment to be in. the staff at this particular location is always super friendly, and always give personal recommendations that could make you're drink better. they always make my drinks good.

of all the starbucks in town, this is my go-to location. it helps that it's the closest location, but really it's about the staff. for the most part everyone that works here is very friendly. i've gotten to know a few of them over the years, and it's nice to go somewhere where you are recognized. i've gone to various coffee shops around town, and starbucks is the most consistent in the way it makes its drinks. i just wish the atmosphere was more unique and had more character.

this is small location but both times i have been the staff has been awesome! always super friendly, sincere, and most importantly quick! they could have stocked the front cooler a little but i did take into account i came in towards the end of the day. the best part is it's located inside kroger so i can have my coffee and shop too! now we just have to get kroger some carts with cup holders . worth the stop.

why you guys have to have a bad attitude? the amount of loyalty i have to starbucks is ridiculous but i am brand loyal. this store may have been having a bad day yesterday but seriously...? each barista was surly, curt and didn't have any people skills that the service industry expects. i can forego being accosted by a smoking homeless guy as i try and enjoy my coffee outside... but you guys have jobs. if service and being friendly isn't for you... get out. plenty of other great starbucks stores to choose from fortunately.
good customer service i went in yesterday sunday the 26 and tatiana made us feel very welcome !
waited 15 minutes for drinks while staff went to the back leaving one person upfront. got drink and it was nothing but foam worse starbucks.

they are always closed! no reason just hit or miss if they are closed. it's 1045 am and cones in front of the drive thru and they are locked. this is the 10th time i've been so irritated that they decide to close shop. get new managers!

wtf are you you looking to me for? it's just a starbucks fcs. wrong! this is not just any starbucks. you've got the best staff in the chain right here. you'll sometimes hear the service is slow. that's because every sum 'b in pinellas county starts lining up at the window at 4:50 am for coffee. there's near accidents and angry exchanges every morning as caffeine addicts jostle in and out of fourth street cueing up for their first fix of the day. nearby there exists a half dozen other very good places to get a cup of coffee and even better donuts. but those other places don't make you feel as good about being up that early and starting your day. when these folks meet you at the window it's with a smile like my daddy used to get on pay day when he brought momma home his check. they're still giving the best service.

i won't bore you with the review of the food or drinks, because a starbucks is a starbucks (love it or hate it - i happen to love it.) i will review the service, because it needs some work. i've given this location so many chances. so. many. chances. just once it would be nice to be greeted at the drive thru window with a "how are you today?" or anything other than the amount of money i owe. yesterday (after waiting over 10 minutes for my drink with only 2 cars in front of me) an apology for the wait would have been nice. i paid my money, the employee handed me the drink and said nothing at all, not even after i said thank you. it was bizarre. this happens every single time. i worked at starbucks a loooong time ago when i was in college, and i used to laugh at people who would pass 3 starbucks to come get coffee at our store. but now i see why they did that; it's because of the service. i'm pretty much done with this location, and luckily it won't be too far to find another one.

i'm actually an employee at this particular starbucks and just wanted to shed some light on some of the reviews and comments and concerns of our fellow customers. mobile orders: i want to start off with our mobile ordering system. now typically with this of course it is meant to be quicker and your order is meant to be ready at your time of arrival. im aware that the app gives you a time in which your order should be done, ignore this. it is not accurate and doesn't account for the large volume of mobile order drinks that we receive at one time. majority of the time 10-15 mobile orders come though at one time. we are the busiest in all of tampa so sometimes there will be a wait. we (most days) receive enough at one time to stretch to our drive they window! yikes! i know but trust your barista is busting their butt to assure we get your order our to you asap. 9/10 if your order is missing it is because you have not hit the very last button that finalizes your order and sends it to our location. if not it may be an error with our machine and simply letting your barista know at the bar that your order is missing we will be more than happy to make your drink on the fly. it's never a problem. our baristas bust their butt daily to assure you all are taken care of the best way we can! bathrooms: secondly i noticed a review saying we lock our bathrooms. we do not lock the bathrooms. if the bathroom is locked, that simply means it is currently occupied. reusable cups: while we do fill reusable cups, there is a standard in which drinks need to be made. if you have a sparkly reusable cup, it will not have the line measurements we need by standard to make sure we are giving you the proper proportions to build your beverage. while some are upset that we still use a cup other than your own to build the beverage, customers in drive thru expect there beverage to be ready upon arrival at the window. if our line is wrapped around the building and we have to wait for you to come to the window, take your cup and then make your drink, it not only will hold up the line but will make you getting your beverage a longer process. coffee: during the morning we have all three coffees brewed. we have pike which is our everyday blend, our dark roast which we switch every so often with a new flavor and our blonde roast. obviously during the early morning hours demand for brewed coffee is much higher, therefore having all three coffees brewed is necessary. at eleven is when we cut out the dark roast and the blonde because the demand for hot brewed coffee at that time isn't as high. your barista will let you know (if it is after 11) that we no longer have blonde or dark, and then ask if you would like our house blend of pike instead or wait about 3-4 minutes for a fresh pourover of dark or blonde roast coffee. we completely understand that we are a coffee shop therefore we should always have coffee, but we have found that we waste tons of coffee brewing all three that aren't as popular during later in the afternoon than our house blend coffee. why brew something hardly anyone is ordering, which thus creates waste. hope this has helped some. if you have stopped coming to this location months ago i encourage you to give us another try as we are under new management.
standard mini starbucks but with the worst customer service imaginable. lazy workers that don't want to wait on customers.

horrible- i waited 45 minutes for a white chocolate mocha and a strawberry refresher. they ended up giving me a hot chocolate instead of the mocha and they filled my refresher to the top with ice. never again.

this starbucks was clean, and service was friendly and quick! the very big downside to this location is the parking lot. there is a double-sided, one-way lane that hooks to the left into the oncoming lane of a two-way road. the space is very tight, and if the two cars parked at the end by the store aren't parked far enough into their spots, you may have to do a bit of neck-craning to make sure you're going to make it out without a scratch. otherwise, i like this sb and would go again.

wazzzzup fam? ill cut to the chase. so starbucks gets alot of the hate cause the white girls are like "omg cupcake yay" but in all realness its pretty swag. some of thier drinks kinda ehhhhhh and i dont really like the actual coffee (find it acidic and bitter like) but they frapachinos/tea be killin. shoutout @chaitealatte aka my lyfe. the cake pops are cake... on a pop. hwat mmore do you poeple want????? its so good like i could probs live off them. tbh i probas alredy do. k so starbucks = starswag peace hommies conan outtttttttttttttttttttttttttttttttttttttt

when a friend wants to meet the best choice for me is starbucks the seating is very limited so i was happy to snag an open table! my drink of choice is a sugar-free vanilla frappuccino during the pandemic i can appreciate the measures starbucks has taken to ensure the environment is sterilized, safe, and efficient w/amazing customer service! coffee makes me happy
amazing staff, great view in the outside seating area. i love the people and pet watching here, it's like my second home when visiting my parents in the area :)
slowest service i have ever experienced. can't believe starbucks allows their brand to be used by this hyatt operation. 10 minutes for 5 people to get through.

relatively speedy, efficient starbucks location with a few dings for occasionally screwing up charges on a car full of people, but otherwise competent. be forewarned: the entire local business community and government office complex swarm this place during morning rush and right after lunch, and the ingress/eggress paths were not designed to accommodate that volume of vehicles. it turns unto a rat's nest real fast here.

is convinced that a carmel macchiato is made with additional vanilla?! 3 employees adamantly tried to convince me i am out of my mind... everyone in there was still hungover at 9am sunday morning. you are going to this place cause of the convenience of your hotel but i'd try one of the local shops if i were you

i was going to preface this review with a defense of starbucks, but changed my mind and decided to not get into the politics and simply stick to reviewing. (though i will say - politics does influence where i will spend my money.) in some ways, a starbucks is a starbucks. at least if you are at a company store and not a licensed location (of which there are thankfully few.) however, in the world of "the same" this location stands out as wonderful. the baristas are uniquely friendly. they also do seem to try harder to be helpful than at other locations. i have on a few occasions needed to speak to a manager, not for problems, but to get information, and the woman who seems to be the store manager is knowledgeable and responsive. she also seems to have a good rapport with the staff, which may account for the quality of service. the store, and specifically the restroom, is always clean, and when i have found it lacking in a paper product, they are quick to fix the problem. although the lines can be long, service doesn't dawdle. on the other hand, when it isn't busy, staff is happy to chat about coffee, or movies, if you are interested and don't interfere with their work getting done. there are plenty of tables, and four areas which have power outlets, which is more than most locations, so it is truly possible to be there and get work done. it does get crowded at times, especially during exam weeks at drexel and penn, but i have only once, out of many visits had to leave because i couldn't find a seat. this is not the only starbucks in philly that i like, but it is my favorite! on a company-wide note - they now use no high fructose corn syrup in anything. desserts are back on my menu!

this starbucks just moved "down the street" from the paoli train station (where there was no parking) to here - where the old burger king used to be. i have not yet gone into the store, but i used the convenient drive-thru today and got in & out quickly. glad there is parking!

very busy it was almost 15 minutes from getting into line before i had my iced caramel macchiato. not a problem, just killing time while others shopped. the order taker was upbeat and not at all judgmental when i didn't know how to pronounce my drink. i don't do coffee places often. the guy at the other end was all business trying to keep up and doing a good job of it. there is seating and i scored a small table easily. my coffee was good, easy to drink, and i was finished in less time than it took to get it.

this is a really busy starbucks in the mornings! even when i order my drink ahead of time they always have it done within the estimated time the app gives you! i have never once had to have them remake my drink they always do an amazing job and customer service is phenomenal here!! period, it is always great... be patient because this location is super busy in the mornings monday through friday. they are quick and efficient with a smile!
be careful! i got robbed here! don't leave anything when you go to restroom. starbucks does not help you at all

the staff here is very friendly and polite. the inside of the store is clean and organized. with that being said, they are extremely slow. there's generally a long line in the drive thru and it doesn't move very quickly. all in all, it's starbucks and if you're willing to wait a bit then swing by.

if i could give this location 0 stars i would. i keep giving them a chance but this is definitely my last they are ridiculous with drive thru time we are waiting sometimes at least 5 min! i used to work in fastfood and we would always try to get our food out in under 2min but not this location like today i waited 5 min for a tall green tea frapp i usually wouldnt even bother to review but today was ridiculous and i'm livid. anyhow there are better starbucks and dds to go to other than this one.

the absolute worst location! i wish this wasn't the location closest to me or i would never go again, but it is on my way to grab it, so that is the only reason i go back. first of all, the drive thru line constantly takes so long to the point where it gets me late. like i'm talking i've waited for 30+ minutes and you get stuck in the line, so i can't get out at that point. it should not be taking that long ever with 5 or so cars in front of me. it has happened on many occasions. also, my coffee order is almost never made right like i'm very clear with my order and they still manage to mess it up. the food is always burnt too, which is a big reason why i rarely get it anymore. i used to go daily and now i go maybe once a week because i'm sick of wasting money on something that is either burnt or not even made right and i'm also sick of being late for work because of how slow they are. it's ridiculous and this location needs to be managed much better because it makes starbucks as a corporation look terrible.
great just like any starbucks, just loses a star because if the amount of beggars out in the street in front of it and that come inside and harass the people

this was my first time to this location, and let me tell you how cute it is inside. located near a college campus, this is the perfect pace to come study and enjoy some coffee. there are so many tables to sit at, and it's super spacious inside. i ordered a semi- complicated drink, the barista was wonderful, and my drink was delicious.

service was fast and friendly enough. but we ordered five drinks - four simple iced coffees - that were all not filled up (about a half inch or more below the lid). the iced cloud macchiato was like 3 inches down and barely cold within a couple minutes. the picture i posted has not been drunken out of. the foam didn't last at all. that was a $6 drink. i've ordered these drinks before and they aren't like this. the cloud macchiato does have the foam go down, but only after you start drinking. not so fast on it's own. both of these issues aren't normal so i wasn't impressed.

picture it: it's the middle of winter. the current temperature is dipping below zero. all you want is a steaming hot, caffeinated beverage. but, you're short on time and really? who wants to get out of the car it took so long to warm up? the seat is finally toasty warm and your feet are finally beginning to thaw and besides all that, every surface of pavement everywhere is covered in ice (which, we all know, isn't the least bit fun to attempt to traverse on foot--unless, of course, those feet have ice skates on them). enter starbucks drive-thru. yahtzee! not only does this location offer drive-thru service. but, the service is fast and friendly. the barista didn't seem the least inconvenienced to be leaning out into sub-arctic temperatures to hand me a steaming cuppa fabulous and even smiled while doing so. now, that's service. get to it!

feels very "alberta" here - long, communal wood tables, plush, overstuffed club chairs...rustic is used in the other reviews often and it's an apt description. staff are friendly and my coffee was good. can't complain!
good customer service. decent coffee, messed up order twice but fixed it both times. espresso sometimes tastes burnt but i think that's just the bean they use. overall good

safely covid friendly ... in store - app or drive thru friendly! i do come here daily & drinks have always been made accurately. overall highly satisfied & would recommend. i love love the brownie here btw.
always a great place to go. the starbucks near saint joseph's hospital particularly was pleasant for a drink & a snack.
quaint, quiet, good coffee but no cushy seating and some scattered trash on the floor.. my tall red eye is super good though! :)

i come to this at starbucks drive thru pretty religiously since it's close to work and they have the friendliest baristas. i usually see the same guy and thought man he's really friendly, but then i've had a few other people mixed in where i drove away delighted at how friendly and outgoing they are.

i love this starbucks they have the best baristas ever at this location. i'm a regular here my mom works nights and am a college student so she'll get coffee for me in the morning and i'll do the same at night time we always go through the drivethru. 2 of our favorite baristas i want to give a shoutout/shine spotlight on 1) carrie- she's there almost every morning nicest most cheerful person especially for being there so early 2) andrea- fastest, perfectionist that always makes the drinks spot on and makes sure yur coffee is wiped off and lids on correctly which is always a plus all the baristas are amazing these 2 are just constants that we see and talk to on a regular bases that know us by name

i've been coming here for years. they have never messed up an order , been rude , or done anything worth less then 5 stars. it's a starbucks. it's good. it's overpriced but you know that pulling in. they do a great job!
order mobile, got there 10 mins later, stood there waiting for 20 more mins waiting for my drink. please do not offer mobile ordering if you can not keep up with in house and mobile at the same time.

i was just there and ordered an iced coffee. i always use honey as a sweetener and asked that they add a little hot coffee to the honey while making my ice coffee so the honey mixes better with the cold coffee. apparently this is a very difficult task because when i picked up my coffee there was a big clump of honey at the bottom. the girl at the window said they would remake it but was so rude, i was embarrassed for starbucks. i received my coffee but it tastes rancid. i drink starbucks 5-6x a week and order my coffee the same way every time so i know how it should taste. it's obvious they did something to my coffee, and they were all laughing while re-making it. what a shame. the people working at this place are a joke. bad rep starbucks.
typical starbucks, coffee is always good and i enjoy the vanilla scones. a nice treat for a morning sweet fix.
new location, same drive thru problems. i always have to double check my order even after it's read back. it's also slow.

i would not recommend having babies here. the owners were very displeased, everyone kept giving me judgmental looks. and they weren't supportive at all, in this time of great stress. if i could rate 0 stars, i most definitely would. when i asked for assistance they told me i was too young and banned me from all starbucks franchises. don't have babies here!

i went with some co-workers to use the coupons on retailmenot for free caramel or hazelnut macchiatos from 3/1/13-3/31-13. retailmenot is a reputable site from which i have successfully gotten several other coupons or coupon codes before. the coupon has no limit aside from not using more than one in the same transaction. i used the coupon from retailmenot at this location and other locations the week prior with no problems. today, a few co-workers and i went in to use the coupons and there were two employees at the front register. when we told them that we had coupons, the male said, "oh, we're not taking those but i don't know why, talk to my co-worker." we went to speak with the co-worker and she insisted that they were a scam. the first week the coupons were available they were accepted but then all of a sudden they were a scam? especially considering the first employee "didn't know" why they were no longer accepting them, it seemed to us as if too many people used them so they didn't want to lose out on more money so began telling people they were not valid. i doubt starbucks is hurting for money, however, if they can't afford to have a month long special, they probably shouldn't offer free items with a coupon for a long period of time.
i love the service i get from this starbucks i like the management ,& workers are excellent especially the morning staff could not be happier
they are pretty good about making your coffee right. i have had some issues with quality but the take care of it right away. overall good experience
