quite possibly the worse starbucks i have ever been to. the 3 ladies serving on 9/26/19 at 11:14am were completely checked out. my order took forever to complete. not because they were busy, but busy talking amongst themselves. i got my food and ate it before they even started with my coffee. and it was the only one in line. no others to make. one of the workers used a long cutting knife to unplug the sink and then just set it aside to use again. the girl making my coffee wiped her nose before picking up my cup. this place is a sad reflection of what is typically a well run, clean establishment. i was at a conference and have no intentions of being back in that location even if it's the only one close by. very disappointing. these three girls could careless about their customers or the cleanliness of their work environment.

second time here during no high traffic time (around 2:30pm) and this is the slowest starbucks i ever been too. i hope they improve because it is a convenient location with easy parking and easy to get to.

i ordered a iced starbucks double shot today at the starbucks on davis montana afb store. i asked if i could get a straw with my drink and was informed that it is starbucks new policy that straws will not be given with any of the new lids. even after requesting a straw the sales person said no straws with that lid. i am sorry but if i request a straw no matter what lid is on my drink i should be able to get a simple straw. i'm sorry but this store is on a u.s. military base.
nice location. friendly and knowledgeable staff. i ordered a venti mocha frapp with two shots!
y'all lit as a bih drinks are bus employees are dumb lit except for lindsay and brandy but other than that keep doing y'all
there has simply got to be a management problem at this place...i have never been to a place where the employees are consistently joyless seem to sort of care, but with not much enthusiasm.
i come here a lot. always fast and courteous. i always think the tables could be a little bit cleaner, but there's a lot of space for seating and the bathroom is always clean.

i like this starbucks so much it pains me to have to share it with any other customers. dogs love the pup cups and get excited every time we visit. working on a way to take this whole starbucks to my house. i think there is room in my back yard.

i've been here multiple times and what i've noticed is that there's always limited seating. i was there for over an hour and there was a large table with four seats next to us that had one open laptop and a large stack of files- unattended! i never saw anyone sit there the whole time i was there. this is a very small starbucks and if you wanted to sit inside to relax or get things done it can get quite loud and chaotic. as a warning there are always lots of middle schoolers after school hours because they can walk to this one. i've never had a problem with my drinks here but the seating arrangement and policy could be improved.

this is the best coffee shop in nashville. the crew here knows what they are doing; they'll take good care of you. they are fast, courteous, and service-oriented. they make good coffee, always fresh, always hot. it is worth your while to make your way over here for the best coffee in town. it shares parking with several shops, so just grab a spot in the garage behind the building, and come in the back door.

i usually love starbucks but this location was sooo slow!! i am not exaggerating when i say it took more than 20 minutes for me to get my drink. and there wasn't a lot of people when i got there. the employees were taking their sweet time, laughing and having conversations while the clients waited. very upsetting because i know starbucks stores and i never had this experience before.
this location has no drive-thru which seems to result in faster counter service and makes it a pretty quick stop on the way into the office!

service is slow but that's because its always crowded, so i don't hold that against them. but every time i come in, there is only (the awful) pikes place blend on tap, never the bold or even decaf, and they will not brew the other 2 if you ask them too. i assume the manager only wants the cheapest blend offered there, and with the business they get there's probably no reason to change.

if i could, i would give this location a a big fat zero for customer service. i figured the last time i went was fluke maybe the representative was in a bad mood. then last week two of my coworkers and i visited this location because it was close to home-goods. upon entering this location i had my phone and order ready i walked to the counter where two representatives were helping the person in front of me. the representatives walked away one went to play on her cell phone and the other make the drink. so i waited, another representative came from the back and took the person next to me order, whom had just walked in. not once did the representative ask me if i had been helped . ok tori, stay calm, the one that went to check her phone finally walked up and in her most annoyed tone asked me "how can i help you" i gave her my order and she advised me that what i wanted wasn't on the menu. ok, but can you make it . she paused and said yes. she seemed annoyed that people were walking in. starbucks are everywhere, i will not be coming back to this one.

virginia, who was working the drive through today, is probably the best employee that they could ever ask for on the customer service end. unbelievably sweet. on top of that, she knew the name of the person in front of me and knew what they would be ordering before they ordered it. she just had it ready. never let go of her.
got the new triple caramel frappuccino and it was amazing! so tasty and even had a caramel type whipped cream. great service and tasted delicious! must try!

i've been here several times, my experience has always been pleasant. staff are friendly, welcoming and they make sure you like your drink. cute store, nice size so there are lots of seats which is a plus (you won't be standing shoulder to shoulder waiting in line, or looking clueless trying to find a seat). i needed somewhere to work this morning. this is the perfect place! my drink was good, very refreshing. the barista definitely gets two thumbs up this morning. if you're in the area and in need of starbucks you won't he disappointed!

every starbucks is the same bland, boring, sterile yuppie infested, "i'm in a rush, get my latte quick" type place. awful coffee, lousy rude service, terrible sandwiches, overpriced everything. i avoid it always unless i get a free gift card.

i frequent this starbucks location often for my favorite chai beverage. located in the midst of a shopping plaza, this location can get busy any time of day. often, i find myself waiting in long drive-thru lines to place my order. unfortunately, this location is very hit or miss depending on who makes your beverage. i haven't gone enough to be a known customer, so every time i pull up i pray that they make my drink correctly. tonight, i am happy to report that my drink was made correctly and there was only one person in the drive through line. i just wish for a little more consistency with this location. i've brought my drink back multiple times for significant order mess-ups and i'm typically not one to make a big stink about a slight mistake. but, messing up my chai often is a little unacceptable.

first and last time i'll go to this location. i opted to go in instead of going through the drive thru. it took 16 min to get my coffee and there was only one person in front of me. they clearly prioritize drive thru and mobile orders!
this starbucks is convenient if you're in manayunk. staff have always been nice, and i've had my fair share of coffee drinks here. for you drivers out there, street parking can be a royal pain.

local starbucks coffee shop on jefferson hwy. there's not a lot of seating; a few tables, a couple of recliners, and a sofa. standard starbucks free wifi, no need for a pass code. the coffee shop is clean. the service is fast. lots of starbucks accessories for sale, as well as the coffee and pastries. basically what you would expect from the national chain.

starbucks has such high quality control that reviewing one is pretty much a silly exercise. however this starbucks should be avoided unless you have lots of time to spend. case in point today is a sunday. hit the drive through at 1pm. spent 16 minutes waiting at the drive through window with no cars in front waiting for one normal drink. in two trips here over the past month, both were the slowest starbucks trips ever.

i used to love to go here but whenever i go to this location, i regret it. last time i got a smoothie here it was just ice and orange juice and mostly ice. today, my sandwich was still frozen. we also got a danish and it was stale and my caramel machiatto had so much caramel syrup that the straw was clogged from top to bottom. it was too sweet for me to drink (and i've been known to eat sugar). i agree with other posters that say the staff here look miserable and behave discourteously. if you're in the area of this starbucks, i recommend driving further down the street to the 5th street location or heading over to the purple bean.
